text: Bought the Pro version two days ago, today the app isn’t working all day. There’s no system status page, and there hasn’t been any notification about when it will be fixed.

Great app BUT.... FLAWED! I really liked this app, until I tried adding my own homemade recipes. It kept duplicating ingredients no matter what I did. I tried recreating the recipes multiple times, but every time something was wrong. And what adds to the frustration is you can't delete nor change a recipe after you've created it!! What a pain. Everything else was just OK. The database of products leaves something to be desired, I had to make compromises there all the time!

I know this is a minor gripe, but PLEASE change the name of the app on the phone to match the app store. I instinctively always look for the zwift companion app and it's simply called "companion" which is a nuisance. 😞

Better than S Health. Lots more food choices in database, easy to add new things by scanning bar code. Tracks my sugar intake (S health doesn't,actually hides it so I can't keep track).The exercise features are fine, gives me a good idea if I am balancing my calories consumed versus used(though I know calories used actually varies by individual). I do wonder why they branded as " Fat Secret" though. A better name would help market the app. The app itself is great, and does what it says it will

there is nothing free to use in this app. only a free trial. it's like to at least try some free ones for a while before I decide to pay for a service. nothing calm about trying to figure out how to test it without a free trial I'll forget to cancel and give me more anxiety than I already have.

I used Endomondo for years and it was far superior until Under Armor bought it and shut it down. Map My Ride lacks the weekly totals and other cumulative things that Endomondo had. It also lacks the desk top view for viewing all of your stored work outs. I guess it's better than nothing. In one word, underwhelmed. When they try to sell you the upgrade you literally can't access your account. They hold YOUR information hostage!

The new format for exercise is avsolute dogshit. It was great before. Start a circuit, it showed you z timer and a video of the exercise. You could easily go back and forth between exercises. Now the circuits are nothing but a glorified youtube video. They've just bunched up all the tiny exercise video into one big one, removing the ease of use. Why.

Could be better... Everything seems a little complicated to me. Some connections don't seem to update. The nutrition tracker should be more easy to use. Like QR codes on food packages or downloading recipes. There's so many easier apps. And yet why use this if others are easier. Exercise tracker included. It's more work than necessary, looking up EVERY activity and the calories it burns. Need to add more activity options.

for over a month the app and web version no longer sync. the problem is that all apps that use MFP data (like fitbit) pull from the web data. so now you have to enter everything on the web version which is a horrible UI and just not convenient. this is not a small temporary hiccup. it's been over a month!! their response? "we're working on it." they promise the next update will fix it. it doesn't. radio silence again.

Like many other users, I'm changing my review from 5 stars as the app is moving to a subscription model and I will lose my access to the features I paid for, while also trying to shove a more "customised" experience down my throat. I really enjoyed how the app was a clean way to track a simple workout plan, and I think for the price I paid it's very reasonable I should expect to keep those features. Only keeping 2 stars as it was so easy to extract my data and put it on a spreadsheet.

it just does everything I need from an alarm clock and more. bought a long time ago, one of the very few apps I actually spent money on, though now the price might have increased. the free version is still great, that's the reason why I spent money on it to begin with - out of respect for the free version. the customer support was nice when I needed help too
Cluttered and confusing to navigate... I can't advance to the 2nd day in the mindfulness for beginners. Also the lessons are so short? What's the hurry?
Crayola colors are cringe and childish. Also, the HR data for the same workout is different on different pages, and also conflicts with what I saw on my wrist during the workout. Trash
This is a totally lame application, there is only diet and weight tracking. What I want is an app like on the iPhone, where I can track my workouts. I am canceling my premium subscription today.

Used the app for a long time now (10 years or so off and on). Works well for tracking a lot of basics in the free version. However as time has passed more pay walls are added for features that used to be free, pop ups appear almost every time you open the app, and this most recent update made the visual navigation in my opinion far less user-friendly which is what's finally pushed me to write a review. This used to be a five star app, 3 because it still functions for basics or it'd be lower.
I really like this app but for some reason it logs me out constantly and when attempting to log me back in, it stagnates.

Good online site for food supplements.When I order an item, I have to pay customs duty in my home country India.But myprotein from UK has recently come out with new method which the cost of items are inclusive of customs duty. So no need to pay any duty at the time of receiving the package. It will be grateful if you make such modifications in your site.
Was able to scan a barcode but the record of what I ate disappeared. Searched for "tomato" and it found nothing. Useless app.

Insufficient exception management. When I try to book a class I get the message "something whent wrong, please try again soon". This information has now be present for a day. I wish that more time was spent on a proper exception message so I as a user would get better feedback about how to resolve the issue. Otherwise I would have rated this app 4 points.

hey there's a new bug that's annoying me to the point of avoiding the app. when I type something in like "chicken", the app automatically searches after I type "chi" and then refreshes the app and puts the cursor at the front of the word I just typed, so I end up typing "ckenchi" and I get an error because that's not a food item. It's extremely annoying.

2026 review (4 stars): The app has been more stable since my last review, and while the number of localized badges haven't improved, I still enjoy using this app. 2025 Review(3 stars) : since my 2023 review, the app stability has improved but country based badges is still lacking inclusion, but I like finding races option.
For anyone who struggles to get out of bed in the morning, those who turn the alarm off and roll back over this is the way. Using the NFC captcha is a game changer. highly recommend

I love the workouts, the tracking, and the guidance. Only reason its not 5 stars is because it keeps glitching! Ill do one of their workouts, and then it wont add it to my activity. and the only way to add it, for the entire time i did it for, is if i just keep it open playing on my phone. Also, when I add an outside-the-app activity, it glitches when inputting the details, and I have to restart the app. If these glitches were fixed itd be 5 stars
I can't even get past the sign in page 😭 the app glitches everytime I sign in just takes me right back to the sign in page...

I love that this app is free and I use it whenever I run. My frustration is that it often fails to save my runs. I sync them, and then it has some error and loses the data completely. Restarting my phone or the app does nothing, I haven't figured out a way to recover the lost data. It happens so often I've taken to screenshotting the data from my runs before I press sync because there's about a 30% chance I won't see it again otherwise.

I was very surprised at the amount of content that this app provides, from learning how to meditate, tackling anxiety and stress, and even providing you with calm and peaceful music for hours and hours to help you concentrate and all on your own time, you make time for your courses. Taking these courses have provided me with help to build up mental defenses against my panic and anxiety, and with each morning I look making time for the next course.

I usually do not do reviews. But this app, hands down, deserves a 10 star out of 5. It took me 2 hours to find it, but it was so worth it. I wanted an app on my phone, but where I could get on my PC to edit and add. It was the only one with a free option that is worth anything. Add recipes on the PC is super easy once you get the hang of it. You can share recipes with whoever. You can view all your recipes of your daily planner, which helps since I travel a lot.

Interesting approach. Typical Google, add yet another duplicate app. Then once the Fitbit Air was set up, it prompted me to set up the Fitbit Air, again. Once that was done, it prompted me to set up the Fitbit Air AGAIN. How many times does it need to be set up??
Latest version doesn't load anything. Unusable

as others have stated,I, too, have wondered why this app keeps opening in full screen mode recently. turned off notifications yet it still pops up. going to uninstall as I don't use it that much anyway, but it is nice for browsing their selection over mobile

Fitbit used to be good. Since the change, it's awful. Firstly, the sleep is terribly inaccurate. It used to be really helpful and accurate, now it keeps saying that I'm getting out of bed at 3am or that I am awake from 2am to 6am. Also, its layout just isn't intuitive. I still can't figure out how to see my active zone minutes total for the week. I have to add all the days up myself. I truly hate it the new app. Considering switching when my fitbit dies.
I'm a snowbird and do a lot of bike riding in Florida when we're down here. I use the app often. it works great.

The concept is what I was really looking for, but it fell at the very first hurdle on day one. I bought a sausage barm from a Spar, and I went to log it, only to find that the app wanted the weight in grams for a sausage. The app seems to be for people who are already acutely precise about their food. Which isn't me. I wanted to start exploring what I was eating and what changes I should make, but this app doesn't make it easy for beginners.
Easy to order, fast shipping, often discount codes offered, supplies always available. Overall great experience. I highly recommend 👌

Much like a few other users, the app will not allow me to log into my account (I even tried uninstalling and reinstalling in, and reset my password). Every time I try to log in, it tells me my password is incorrect. However, when logging in using a web browser, my credentials work. Clearly the app has issues. Called hotline and was placed on hold for over an hour. Poor service for the amount of money that merchants pay for this platform.

Depending on your watch, this app does NOT support Running Plans (you just won't see them) -- Pixel Watch 4 for example, doesn't support it. The app is fine for manual runs, or using one of the listed Guided Runs, but you won't be able to use the Running Plans. You will either have to use the phone, or run using the phone AND watch at the same time, but ditch the phone-recorded run at the end.

Strap is limited, I loved it before the last firmware update. I have to spend at least an hour playing with the band letting it shut off (take it off, and set it on the table) then wake it up then tickle it until it's in pairing mode so my phone whoop app can say "catching up." Nope don't have that kind of time. I invested in a next level sleep tracker that also uses HRV to help you identify the days to blow it out or workout softly.... It's cheaper to get a Oura ring.
After moving from the iPhone version, I was surprised that this version was missing some of the key features, such as being able to view my stats in a graph.

Great app!! I use this app almost daily to track my walks and bike rides. I also use it to log my swimming distances and times. I use it in conjunction with the "My Fitness Pal" app to keep track of caloric output and intake. I've logged in for 350 consecutive days and lost close to 40 pounds. It's a great motivator for me. I originally wrote this review about 5 years ago, and I'm still using the app. Last year my physical therapist told me that he also uses it and thinks its great.

Recently Fenix 7X Pro Saph Solar became useless for me. I spent hundreds of GBP and Garmin decided to change the software affecting features most important for me. I cannot anymore import gpx without internet. Why is that? Why Garmin decided make the watch useless? This watch is for hikers as well. They often are on fells with no internet. Is it because Garmin wants to push it to cloud so badly for every price not respecting customers who already paid money. Why the data cannot be synced later?

I like the UA "Map My ..." aps, but have had no luck getting them to interact with each other. They all say they're connected, but there's no interaction (i.e., workout on map my fitness does not download to my fitness pal), requiring me to manual insert the info. My Garmin Connect dataa would automatically show up in MFP. Very disappointing.

SO BAD. When it works it's great, which is why this has two stars - but it rarely works on my phone. For a company that has so many studios who pay for the business plan, they should be able to optimize this app better. Half the time when I try to open the app it just shows the loading bar for half an hour until I give up, when I search local businesses that I both know exist near me and are on the app it says that there are no businesses that match that name. Come on, do better.

Found a food item from a well known chain that had calories under reported in the app as compared to the corporate website of the chain. Emailed the app people to notify them of the issue. Their response was they dont control the database and I can just edit my entries. I have a paid version, I'm paying not to have to do my own research. Refund please.

Nothing like the app for iPhone . Not nearly as many options . Please fix and allow to track weight , show your limits and minimums on carbs and  others rather than just calories . See iPhone app and you will see what this is lacking . Very disappointing .

Overall, this is a pretty good app! However, here are a few things i realized 1. Make sure sleep tracking is running in order for the optimal wake up phase alarm to work 2. Make sure to set a notification sound for a wake-up check in system settings.. 3. There aren't that many alarm options that come with it, but you can use any sound you have on your device. I haven't used sleep tracking yet. Thank you to the one who gave me this info

I was scammed. payment was successful, but they want me to pay again. 60€ for an annual subscription and you have to use the features you will get for free. can't cancel it. I was looking for an app to count calories easily but got AI slop, it's even better/faster to use Excel sheets to count your calories. scam. don't try it.
The app is more difficult to find items. I prefer the website. It would be a nice feature if I could import my shopping cart from the website. It would give me the ability to use both sites.

Used for a few weeks. Better than others used. Had Fitpal for years, got tired of constant format changes and hassles. This is much better overall. Free app fantastic, thinking about paid version bc getting out of ads are pain sometimes. But paid is super great price. Awesome app. Thank you Love it

20 Years and Still Using VitaCost! I've always loved VitaCost for their discounts AND the extensive variety they offer, the extreme care they take in packaging, and the quick turnaround on delivery. Sometimes I get frustrated with "maneuvering" through choosing the BEST Promo Codes! Having said that, I always choose VitaCost over those BIG Companies that have run the little companies out of town.

What has happened to the Strava connection? The recent update seems to have affected the automatic run sharing. Seems to be working fine now. other than a few data sharing glitches here and there, I really like the app. It is easy to use and gives detailed information in many different areas.
works seamlessly and well designed user interface. has every sport you can play and track, and it provides all key health matrix

I have never been able to figure out an easy way to menu plan and hate the process. Since using this app, it has been so much easier and Iove that you can use recipes from the app or save your own recipes from the web. Also love that it creates a shopping list for you based on the recipes in your menu plan.

In general terms it is awesome and you get to enjoy the Zwift experience much more with the companion. Sometimes it has issues syncing with the game itself (maybe something to look upon on future updates). But I do enjoy this app a lot! As an athlete and coach.

Vitacost & their product selections are great. Prices are competitive or better than I find on other sites. My 3 star rating is for this APP. It takes too much time to get to products or never gets there at all. Search is slow. Order item count wants to update with every increase instead of waiting til I update cart. I never got to review my order before charge went thru either. I will use on-line order next time instead of APP.
As soon as I interact with the screen it crashes.

The main reason I got this app was to record my sleep talking and snoring or other noises, but the recordings come in so scratchy and inaudible it is impossible to make out what the noises are. My phone recorder itself works perfectly when used with my phone camera and other apps so the issue isn't on my end. There are some nice features, like the music to help you fall asleep and an alarm, but given that I was using it for the night recordings I was disappointed.
No shipping available to India... oro kalla thaayolikale

Purchased app and then they pull the rug out and change to subscription model. Should at least be grandfathered in to a basic version. This type of business practice should be illegal and the developers should be ashamed of themselves.

As a former MFP user of 9 years (daily user for 5 years), this app is incredible. The first day of the free version impressed me so much that I invested in myself by purchasing a 1 year subscription & am amazed by the nutritional insight I get from my logs. I also love how it includes data from my Garmin watch and has fasting built in, helping me replace multiple apps & streamline my health monitoring. I appreciate the care the team puts into making sure nutritional info is accurate too.

I used to love this app, it helped me create and keep track of my experiments in the kitchen. But since the last few updates, every recipe I open makes the app crash. Deleting cache, reinstalling the app, nothing solved it. It's a shame, because this was a really great app.

Excellent program, maps your treks, gives you ball park figures on mileage, calories burned, pace/minutes per mile and much more. Great for keeping track of your activities, it keeps a log of your activities linked to your sign in account. Many activity settings, I generally only use walk, general hike or walk/power-walk though. I recommend this app for anyone who likes to be active and/or fit. Note to developer, add kayak to activity choices, if possible. Map my Kayak?

Love the app overall, but the activity entry screen UX is so incredibly frustrating. It needs to be a full screen view, otherwise how it is now I always accidentally dismiss the activity entry when cycling through the start or end time entry. Will update to 5 stars if the activity entry screen is fixed.

I used to genuinely like this app. Not anymore. Any foods you log get some AI slop "Tips" The can NOT be turned off, they are ALWAYS negative, no matter if you just had a piece of chocolate or a more fatty cut of meat. ("Try switching out fish sticks for grilled fish for less calories!") No. I sometimes wanna eat something unhealthy! LEAVE ME BE. Another thing: The stupid logo/mascot. I liked the apple icon, it was nice and simple. The weird monster in there now? Annoying, invokes rage.

I began to use the NTC app in March 2020, and since then I've been enjoying how much it has to offer. Honestly, I consider it as a very complete app in terms of quality and quantity. It would definitely be a good option if you not only want to crush some abbs, but also build a healthy lifestyle. It is evolving constantly. Therefore, I would recommend to improve the programs' section by adding meaningful variables to its structure. Variables such as: weight, height, sex, workout's objective, etc.

Does what I want. Gives me speed, average speed, distance and duration and does it well. I don't need all the extras that come with the app or the cost. I am a 76 yr old that is a recreational rider that likes to try challenge myself.5/26/22 Still working good and meeting my needs. 8/30/22 Update: App still doing for what i need. I am not a competitor or road race, jut an old bicycle rider who likes to know speed, time & distance.

Many of the useful features of the FitBit app have been needlessly removed. Losing the custom food creation/logging has been a huge setback. Takes so long now. 2/3 of the homepage is now just an AI feed pushing duplicate data at you. You can squish a few useful bits of data into the remaining third, but you can't drag-and-drop tiles, which makes customizing your page clunky. I'll give it a little time to see if they improve it, but I'll probably be switching to a better fit-tracking company.
I bought paid version many years ago and now they blocked entire functionality and want subscription. Crazy
Whoop has been an excellent tool to help me stop drinking alcohol and improve my sleep, training, and recovery

Greedy monetization finally killed my love for this app. The basic option I had been using for years was stealthily removed and I was forced into a payment only option the next time I logged in. Uninstalled.

This is my favorite sleep tracking app I've found, but I'm having some issues with it in the last update. I haven't been using it for awhile to actually track my sleep, just as an alarm. For some reason now, when I shut off my alarm it decides to start up the sleep tracking. I don't usually notice until I'm walking out the door or until I get to work, whenever I pull out my phone for the first time after I turn off the alarm. Today I was very careful not to press any buttons other than the big red dismiss button then i let the screen time out on it's own, after my shower i checked it and sure enough the sleep tracking had started. It's not a huge problem, but it does need to be addressed.

Too many notifications. The toggle options are nonsensical to me. I can't figure out which ones to toggle off so I turned them all off. I'm a busy person. Stop spamming me with dozens of notifications. If I want whoop info that is not immediately important (like for example if my battery is almost dead) then I will open the app when I have time.

Interface is easy to use and I love vitacost, but the app itself took quite a bit of time between screens, especially at the payment section... I waited a good 5 seconds with a blank white screen after clicking the submit order button... The order did go through, but you gotta be patient... Maybe a simple notifier or something to show that something is happening...

Google wants more data from you. connect more applications and let us use your phone's accelerometer to watch your motion. They removed testing oxygen saturation from the sleep portion of the app. if you're using a footnotes device with O2 saturation, you have to go to the health section to see that. it tabulates daily, and doesn't show you a representation over time as you sleep. A little disappointed at this point. The Fitbit app analyzed sleep better.

Amazing app! It's the only thing I've used for an alarm the last 5 years and I always have it with me. It tracks all of my sleep patterns, sleep debt, if I'm snoring, time spent in deep sleep etc after I bought the add-on which is 100% worth it. I can even set challenges when the alarm goes off so that I have to do math or solve a quick puzzle before the alarm will shut off to prevent me from unconsciously turning it off and falling back asleep. I don't know what more you could ask for!
The iPhone version tracks workouts, too bad this one doesn't.

Excellent for those of us who are serious about losing weight, or just generally improving health (as of 02/16/2017). However, of late, I have noticed a significant issue with this app. It used to auto-sync my workout logs from MapMyWalk and MapMyFitness (including importing my step count from my walk logs); but no longer does so. Correct this immediately, please.
Waste of time. There's so much you can do on the website that you can't do here. No entering exercises or body measurements. Just use website, not this app.

At least, I was able to do one order with the App. The reason I give it 3 starts is because. It is very difficult to find a product. That I know it is sold in Vitacost. But, the when I did a search with the name of the product. It did not appear. I wish that searching for things were easier. When I was trying to look by category. I pushed one and it stayed thinking forever. Never pulled any foods in that specific category. I tried a different category and same. It is difficult.

This app used to be really good. I've used it for over six years and have had very few problems with it until the last 2-3 months. Since then it frequently stops tracking the first 3 or 4 minutes of my walk or bike ride. The timer starts, but the GPS won't track. I rode yesterday, and the GPS didn't start tracking until I was a mile down the road, and the ride before that (appx. 9 miles) didn't track at all. I usually lose the first .2 - . 3 miles of my walks. Sometimes, it will draw a straight line from my house to the point where it finally starts tracking, which means that it shows me cutting through buildings and other obstacles. They have an Android GPS Troubleshooting article on their website. I've tried all of the options on there, including reinstalling the app. That didn't fix it. I've tried turning off the power saving option, but then the app just kept crashing after my workout and I ended up losing that one. Their website says that it has to do with the screen shutting off,

Really good app to support Zwift app on a PC. A really useful addition would be the ability to use this app to toggle between perspective/"camera" on the PC screen during a Game session.. as when on the bike I've no easy access to the PC keyboard..

Gives me a good idea of how many calories I go through and definitely helps me keep track of my exercise. One thing that annoys me, though, is that tracking my walks results in variable results even though I follow the same route to the extent that one day will be marked as 6.5 miles while the next is 9, even though it's the very same route. A tool to edit these anomalies would be a plus.

Ever since this app updated, it doesn't recognize my sign in and tells me my credentials are invalid, but I can sign in just fine on the website. Please fix this problem because this app used to be great.

What's the point of this app if the tracking doesn't work? In the last month, 5 of my workouts paused themselves just after starting, and because I use the timer, I didn't notice until I got back home, except that, I run into quite a lot of GPS issues, mainly incorrect/missing tracking, often the map of my workout is completely wrong and includes straight line that are impossible to ride. Sucks.... Maybe something is wrong with my settings, let me know if you have an idea🤔

I love the Garmin watch. I believe it's the most accurate of all fitness watches. (I have some experience in research) I love the app allows you to connect with others and join so many challenges. the one drawback that prevented me from giving a perfect rating is that it doesn't do the fitness rings like most fitness apps. you have to go into multiple different sections to see. I would love to see that be integrated to close rings. I would probably never use Samsung health ever again if they did
Workout tab appears empty, without any filtering options, so you have to select across 600+ options, super inconvenient. it's a severe regression dating back from roughly feb'26. fix it!

Absurdly overpriced. Plenty of free resources exist that do similar things and this provides no added value apart from a couple things read by famous people. What they advertise as "bedtime stories for grownups" are actually just bedtime stories for children packaged slightly differently, which is not what I was looking for. Several of the narrators have a style that many people must life, but I find really annoying (I don't find a really slow, exaggerated "sleepy time voice" to be calming). Biggest issue is that they do everything they can to trick you into a paid subscription. I tried to cancel in the app, which you can't do for no apparent reason...tried to cancel through the Play store, but this was not working on my phone... since the free trial window is so short, I ended up wasting $60 on a thing I never wanted in the first place.

It doesn't sync to anything anymore. Google Fit is now blocking apps I want to connect to and have previously connected to. Data is inaccurate because of the lack of syncing, even when shown as connectedness. If you try disconnecting and reconnecting the applications, Google Fit will deny the connection, and there is no way to override. Google Fit is Junk!
This is my first Garmin. The experience linking the app with the watch is awesome. I'm made aware of my health data, which I really like.

I love the free content but I do not want to be charged a $60 fee after the trial. if this were in $5 per month installments like most apps, I would pay for it because it seems like a very useful app with a lot of detail. the price scares people. even if you did quarterly $20 charge instead of full 60 I'm sure way more people would buy it. I'll wait until maybe they come out with more $$ options.

It's a good way to log your food. They have several foods in the database and toy can save meals so you can quickly put them in. The only thing I wish they had is the ability to scan an item if you can't find it in the database. If you purchase the premium package you have access to many recipes and plans to try, ie: balanced, keto or low carb. I'm satisfied with this app for logging my food!

I like how it will tell MyFitnessPal when you started, how many calories you burned and how long you worked out. I wish it gave a little more detail, but for a free app, what more do you need? Not all of the activities will track on a map, but it's a free app. What more do you want? Thing 2: estimates of calories burned can be way off the mark, so don't eat back all those "burned" calories. E.g. it says I burned more calories coasting down a hill on my bike than climbing back up the hill. Strava says the obvious opposite. Auto-pause does re-start when you get going again.

The app is a good platform to find classes and studies around your location. Somehow I did not manage to pay the class fee without agreeing to save my credit card information in the app. Hope the develope would resolve this as this is very sensitive information nowadays with the constant threat of spams.

I love this app! I'm over 10kg down and it actually feels healthy and sustainable. However, a few things I would like to see: - A way to directly access my created meals/recipes through a separate tab at the bottom instead of going through adding them to a meal - Changing ingredients in recipes shouldn't retroactively change calories on old entries of said recipe. Just because I now use different cream doesn't mean I did last month. - a way to turn off the new meal tips - the old logo back🙈

Almost perfect I live in Australia so would love if I could send my shopping list to Woolworths or Coles as we don't have Walmart here. Would also love a quick option to convert weights and temperature to metric/Celsius

This app has helped me easily keep track of my calorie carb intake. it's helpful to keep track 👣 of everything I'm eating along with a log of my daily exercises. helepd me lose 30 lbs 3 times in a decade. My problem is getting off track. gaining lots of weight then working hard to lose it again. that is all within my fault. the losing weight can definitely be credited to this app through the years. easy to use. tried and true.
pay to use app, they should lead with that instead of have you go through signing up and filling out all the details just to tell you you can't use it without paying first

Nice app to track calories. Lots of food in their database with complete information. However I have the premium version and some things are just lacking or not working well. Ex: We need a list of favorite foods. There are recent and frequent lists, but I have items that I know I use quite a bit and are in neither. I need a list of favorites i can search in. Also my steps dont sync well with Health Connect despite many refreshes everywhere, and I can't input steps for previous days manually...

I enjoying the ability to interact with others on Zwift using the companion app. I can control most of the things using my phone. However, i still think there needs to be better interface between the companion App and zwift. Specifically when pairing device.

I love this app except for one small thing. Sometimes when I open it, it takes a long time to load. Just hangs on logo screen for an unteasonable amount of time. Happens on multiple devices. Otherwise it's the best totally free workout app I've tried. No ads and works well.

Been using since 2016, and this app is amazing. Insomnia since I was seven. These stats are so helpful. So many great features, but not enough room to write them all! 6/5 stars, at least, from me. I appreciate your work.

It's a miracle. I used to be late for everything because nothing could get me out of bed. But this app changed my life. I know this sounds tacky or an exaggeration but with this app and some self control you can maybe also force yourself out of bed. Since posting my last review of the app, I've changed the brand of bathroom scales that I use, but although the app they use does sync with Fit, only my weight is shared between the two apps, just like the last one did. None of the other measurements are. Again.

I have had this app for years at this point and it is easy to use, though sometimes updates make it forget settings and you have to re-enter them. I am hopeful that the data that is currently-and has been for years- free in the app remains free, as I do not want to pay for their very expensive monthly pay wall for features their already expensive products have as standard. I have had two Garmin devices and this app has been great for both of them the last 7 years.

Amazing. Love every workout! Love how you can pick the type/amount of equipment, which muscles you want to target, duration etc. Definitely has really changed my overall fitness for the better! Use this app all the time, cannot recommend it enough! And I love how if you save and download, you can use it while away on holiday !

I severely dislike the new design and how light it is to start, but I hate how you can no longer log custom food in the calories section. I make a lot of homemade recipes and primarily only buy whole foods. No, I don't want to select "Grand Traverse Pie Company" that has 600+ calories and my own has 200 less. Its also very difficult to navigate through the app now and some of my data was lost. I had been logging my periods so I could be prepared for my wedding in September— thats all gone.

Love this app! Super straight forward and easy to use. Premium is honestly pretty cheap compared to other apps, and I love the steps and water tracker. The barcode scanner is super convenient, and the smart scan is decently accurate depending on how visible different components are. I do wish the history log for food and drinks were separate, but that's just a potential feature I'd like, I have no actual cons for this app.

The app itself is ok, tracks calories and recipies are ok. The annoing thing is that after each update the basic functions are made more complicated; i used to be able to log my lunch in 5 seconds, now it takes 10 seconds, because of a more complicated user interface. Please make the interface simple and user friendly again. Good app development would be that i use less time, not more, in the app.

Was able to track every bite no matter where I ate or what I was eating ! Great app :-) (2014 ) I've used this app for over 6 yrs now , I've found with recent updates, many scanned items are a no show , and very many more are incorrect on the calories ( chobani and other common foods ) causing me to have to correct many items, thankfully I'm great at math . May be back, but going to try something new!
Great. Thanks for this. I'll be avoiding all Apple products, apple pie, apple strudel, apple crumble!!! I hate apple and am in huge support of your venture .

Was 5 stars for my 'lifetime' subscription way back in the beginning, when the app was buggy as hell.. now $75 per year for a bunch of extra programs I don't want or need. Hey Mehdi, I don't want your 20% discount on features I won't use. I just want the OG 5x5 tracker. So I unlocked the trial just so I can get to my weight history so I can move to a different app.

When I paid for this app years ago I had pro version then they made it free and was pointless buying it and now its been taking over by under armour its gone even more buggy and barely works. Wouldnt give it 1 star but thats the lowest rating. Dont use this non productive app its ridiculously frustrating. Cant even login it says network error but on pc logs in fine, useless app. Server overloads frequently and is so slow, it never used to be like that.
been consistent throughout the years. very simple options

I think this is the best free calories tracking app on Play. I have tried the other higher ranking app, those have very limited function if you are not paying the expensive subscription. FatSecret has everything you needed, even you are not paying for the premium, while it's not too expensive to pay to support the developers. Although some features (from the web version) is not included in the app, I would advice to use their website together the app. Totally recommended.
Do u hv any branch in india or u wil send it directly from usa?
Wow what a useful, wonderful app - and since this small company is being bullied by Apple I want to do my part to support them! You should too!

For the last month or so, (about since the Feb update) my A14 5G Samsung will not go into Game mode when I start a ride. This worked previously; I just started Zwift and Companion and when I started a ride Game started automatically. I have tried settings and reconnecting and restarting both phone and PC to no avail. Help.

i have used this app for well OVER a year. it helped me with my calorie goals and building muscle, but they lost me now. there is WAY too many ads, the pro version "limited time deals" are a scam, and now, the most BASIC feature like tracking a meal ahead of time is locked behind a paywall. i would've paid for it, given how much i use it, but i am sick of having to subscribe to every freaking thing. uninstalling now.

The app needs more polishing for Android users. the IOS version seems to be less buggy. During the ride Bluetooth will constantly drop out then you have to forceclose the app, turn off Bluetooth, wait 2 mins then restart the app. I will be opening a support ticket with Zwift to try and get some answers. For now seems like Android users are an afterthought for Zwift.

Alarms are turning off automatically after only ringing very quietly for just a few seconds. So I've been hours late to work multiple times because of this failure. This app alarmed reliably for years until now.
The watch vibrates even when the vibration is disabled. The worst feeling ever! Every software update triggers that, and there is no way to disable it.

Very inaccurate distance vs other apps and even itself between runs and devices. Also, the webpage shows only parts of path. eg. my last 4.9 mile activity (according to the app at least) on the the web page showed 0.3mi for last route. But if I open it up it shows the whole thing. Gonna stick with My Tracks.

update: the new update is absolutely trash and it's worse now than it ever was. I love the concept of this app. I've used it for years. I had the free premium trial because I did enjoy the barcode scanner... but now almost everything I've scanned has had incorrect information. Why pay for something that doesn't work even half the time? I will continue using the free app for now, but I can't justify the premium price for the barcode scanner if all the nutritional info rarely matches.

loved the app but there are way too many bugs now. it keeps prompting for login. and closing the app when hitting the create a new recipe button and requiring new login. this was not the same experience as earlier this year.
This app is a life saver and great tool to use.

Terrible application. Most of the time the current day does not even load, making it so you cannot track your calories. I purchased the pro version. The developers do not seem to care that their app has a substantial flaw as they will not issue me a refund. On top of that issue, when it does work, you have to pick a single unit to measure in. Either grams or ounces. I measure food in grams, but milk? Can't do one or the other. Also lacks a data base and can't enter food when the app doesn't load.

Terrible Support! I have the Premium version. Things synced for a week or so and then suddenly stopped. Nothing is syncing to MFP. Reached out to support after trying all the uninstall/reinstall, disconnect/reconnect things only to find out they only have support by email and only email every few days. It's been over a week since it broke and support is still clueless about why their app broke. DO NOT RECOMMEND.
I have tried multiple times to shop on your app but never been able to complete purchase, have stopped trying now. Please fix your payment process for international customers. Thanks.

Randomly updated out of the blue to where it was so confusing/impossible to use. Majority of the time I would add recipes and meals and it would track as 0 calories. So I refreshed the app thinking something was wrong and was rn I reopened the app, it had deleted all of my food and meals I had planned for the last two years straight. 2 years and 100lbs of data lost because they are trying to squeeze a few bucks out of my wallet to get premium, with this update. Disgusting.

Don't like the update. The new layout is overwhelming with metrics. At some point you start to question if this amount of optimization and tracking is really necessary. I only used the previous fitbit app for tracking HR for a medical condition, and calorie counting for occasional diets. The new app does not let you input individual calorie numbers. Did you eat something not in their database? Too bad, you can't input it! I'm going to back to pen, paper, and a calculator. Honestly, a relief.
Downloaded the app to be able to sign in to my Vitacost account so I could easily reorder what I previously purchased... And the Sign In doesn't work! Nothing in the MORE section works. UGH!

DO NOT USE WITH ANDROID!!! This isn't mentioned anywhere, but there is a major flaw that the app doesn't sync if you use with an android based phone. I logged for 8 months with my phone, only to lose 3/4 of my data when they did an update. Apparently, the developers are "aware" of this and working on it and they recommend NOT using the app, but rather the website instead. Completely ridiculous, unprofessional, and a terrible response.

Reply to support. I reported that everytime I try to run the APP it freezes my phone and I can't get past the opening title screen. It is with some amusement that I read the Developers suggestion. I can't get as far as any menu . I suspect it was a generic response rather than actually reading and responding to my feedback! Pity it was a great App, which I have had to delete.

Worst app, It automatically started resetting my steps count in few minutes after completion of the excercise. please fix this issue. This app is pathetic. Also it cannot sync properly with my Samsung Health application and Galaxy watch. Count and Activity data always be seen differ from the Samsung health application. Please somebody fix it.
Used to be good app now after the January 17th update I am unable to open.

Amazing app for an amazing beginner strength program. The app is super easy to use and comes with handy tools like built in rest timers and instructional videos and more. I've been using the app for about 6 months and my squat has gone up from 30kg to 135kg. I just wish there was Mad Cow support so I could continue using it when I transition to the next program in a few weeks!
It works well, and the features are as described. Doing math to turn off the alarm is a feature I'll rely on for the rest of my professional life.

love this app. easy to find specific food to add to your food diary, if it's not listed, just add it yourself. I really like the nutrients breakdown also, since i struggle to get the proper amounts of iron and protein. I've been using it regularly since December 24', and it has helped me keep focused on my goal. I've lost almost 30lb.
Lacks some really simple features like setting vibrations for hr zones and common activities like motorcycling, or even support for logging custom activities at all.

The new update is terrible. Everything I liked about the app has been removed or made more complicated. You can no longer add foods in the order you intend to eat them, you can no longer see your day at a glance easily and you have to jump through hoops to add foods to each meal.

If your device gets knocked out of position even slightly it will log your max heart rate. In my case I didn't notice for 2 hours which was enough time to ruin all my data. Customer service refuses to correct the data. This is my second time interacting with customer service and they pretty much just gaslight you until you go away. The data is awesome, but these days you can get the same analytics from companies with better data capture methods and without a subscription

This app is horrible. It is actively impairing my ability to track my fitness. I am 100% on the Google ecosystem and swore never to go back to Apple, and this is really making me question that choice. I need to be able to log a number of calories. Going back to not counting exercise after the fact is a huge loss. Extremely unhappy. Do not recommend.

Interface terrible, especially when logging activities. It's too easy to swipe-exit the logging-activity screen when swiping up and down to set the time or select the activity, resulting in having to start over again. The auto detection for activities is spotty. The analysis is nice, but want more assertive coaching, guidance as to what to do with my numbers, especially around sleep and recovery.

I love this app! So I was skeptical at 1st (there are so many apps out there), so I just did the free version initially. But I couldnt track everything I wanted, so I got a trial version of Cronometer Gold. Wow!! You can track everything! Water, bowel movements, all your food, your vitamins, you can add notes and pictures, etc. I love the barcode scanner and the quick adjustments I can make if I eat only a partial serving. I love all the charts and bargraphs, too. It's really an awesome tool!!

Love the program and the app makes it so easy to just get the work out done. Plate math and warm ups are all laid out for you. Just set up and lift. The one thing that I miss from older versions, is that the app was more clear about when it would deload for you. It would present the information upfront with clear copy, but now it just does it in the background. Very minor but I thought it was nice to know.

Overall really good app, with just a few minor nuisances. I've used the 10K and 1/2 marathon training plans with solid success. They have a nice variety of workouts, to keep training from getting stale. Primary gripe is that about once a month, a workout log gets wiped out/doesn't record. Minor gripe is that I find the audio coaching on the guided runs to be too chatty for my tastes. I'd prefer an option with just the start & stop times for intervals, without the "rah-rah" stuff.
I paid for lifetime access and now I can't use the app without a subscription. I don't care how long I've had the app for, not honoring the original deal is gutless.

The workouts are very challenging and there is a good variety of workouts to choose from.Unfortunately, the app either freezes up once opened and then crashes, or the workouts start to play but then I get the grey screen and nothing else. I tried on two different devices, a tablet and android phone with one of the results I mentioned above. I keep trying to get this to work, but I am ready to stop trying at this point. It's frustrating. My low rating is based solely on the functionality. The trainers and workouts are great, if I can only get to do them!

mind body is sadly full of bugs. I like that I can book sessions in different studios, but the days don't load proper times most of the time (for example it will show Sunday sessions for Monday). After the last update, calendar is missing a whole week of January and it's impossible to book.

If you are really wanting to explore sleep stats, this is the app for you. However, for me, there are just way too many settings. Took forever to get it set the way I wanted it. Buggy. No matter what I tried I could not get it to play the correct Spotify playlist. Garmin watch integration is really bad. It throws 3 to 4 notifications while tracking sleep, drains my watch battery like crazy, and it is really difficult to get it to sync up to go to sleep. Too much stress before I try to go to bed.
I'm a casual bike rider. It does everything I need. Good info from your phone. Even better online with a PC.

Absolutely wonderful! I needed a low-effort but precise app for calorie and protein counting, and I got one. Happily paid for premium. Barcode scanner and AI are beyond helpful, and recipes are great! Keep up the great work! P.S. Almost forgot to add - looove the little suggestions I get after logging the meal!

I've been using this app for years and it is usually pretty accurate at tracking my runs/walks. Within the last week the gps has stopped working. It is anywhere from .2 to 3 miles off. After tracking, the app also freezes and I have to force the app to stop and then open it back up. I have uninstalled and re-installed several times but that didn't help. I contacted their support team via email and was basically told, thanks for letting us know but we aren't going to look into the issue.

Any app that requires me to create an account before I can see what it offers gets an immediate one- star review and deleted of my phone. If you're going to profit by selling my personal info, I want to know what I'm getting in return first. Plenty of other apps that forever you to sign up before you can see what they do.

Unfortunately we use this app at the salon I work at. We have had numerous challenges with it, not limited to losing clients data, deleting appointments and crashing. As a stylist it is Handy to be able to check my schedule when not at work, however it likes to lock me out of my account on a regular basis and then it has to be reset by the salon owner. Sometimes that takes numerous tries. We have also looked at other apps however many of them are just another versions of the same company.
Bring back the old format, like previous purchases

I am a non athletic, not very strong individual. I have been using the free version for about 6 months. I workout 2-3 times a week. Actually noticing modest size gain, considerable improvement in posture and well-being, as well as doubling my strength numbers on all the lifts. I enjoy seeing the numbers go up :) I have only used the free version and honestly, it's enough for my needs. I will buy it when I feel the need to customize or want to directly support the developers.

Seemed to work well at first, but now it doesn't work. It will open and just freeze. I uninstalled it to try and fix the problem, but now it won't even reinstall on my Android phone. Back to Strava for me I guess. At least Strava continues to work, and the ads aren't as intrusive.
Not counting steps. if it does track them it will say a certain amount then when I look at the end of the day its taken them off so only says a couple of hundred steps

Up until recently I had no issues with the app. I love the design and how different workouts are broken down into different categories. However, the issue I have is with the app freezing during "class" formats. You could be in the middle of a yoga workout and the app will just freeze. I wish there a download option. That way I could download the video and not have to worry about it freezing during workouts 😅

I can't complain about anything overall.. it's easy to use and quick for sudden rides. It even track down my ride almost accurately. This is my first app to track my ride and I didn't expect to be this good.. great work!!
I just joined and I go to log in and I'm to incorrect email password. Google save it ten min prior. I've reset password 3 times. can't use your app

This app is great, kind and supportive but also motivational. I'm very happy with Garmin! I think it would be useful to be able to tell it that we are unwell, (if we are happy to share that) for context for HRV etc. This would also give us context for our run etc stats and peaks/troughs. Just a tick box 'I'm not well today' would do it with a text box to write in if you like.

Nice way to interact with the game. Still missing options to actually start a ride from the app. That would be useful when the laptop is connected to the TV and out of reach while cycling. It's a lot easier to have access to the phone in the bike when compared to the laptop.

i like the app. it is easy to track food there and new ai tips are helpful. but i have major issues with the app 1. after i've tracked the food i meet like 3 extremely annoying unskipable screens with animation to "commit to my streak" which i have to go through every day to see how much calories i've tracked. 2. i can't use grams to indicate how much of the recepiece i've ate. it's inconvenient and causes inaccuracies in tracking

The app suddenly stopped working...I uninstall and reinstalled...but for the last few days it keeps saying "something went wrong, please try again later". UPDATE 5th Dec.: App still NOT working...very annoying as it is my yoga studios preffered booking platform ....

This app used to be so good, but it has gotten terrible. Previously purchased items don't show, and there's no detail available for orders. I just got a double shipment that I was charged for. Now I have to wait for them to respond for a refund. Workers are not being careful putting orders together at all. Service has gotten so bad I'm starting to consider doing business elsewhere. Please fix your app. Thank you Irene

Coming back to this app after two years and it seems they've done a good job working on it. Edit: I've hit the same problem as before. I'm going to finish my 8weeks routine and will have to move to something else. I would be willing to make one off payment, but a subscription is not for me

Below review was when I first paid. When they renewed me in 2024, they Removed all my gold perks app is almost worthless. Still works for what I want/need, been using it for a while though I may started back up.

Great app other than when adding card details they didn't show as available to select to pay for my class.
