My partner had the same experience, other than that its great.
It is very user friendly and easy to monitor and track your booked scheduled events.
I Just placed my order.

Update below: New to the app. I've been looking for an app just like this for so long. The free version is definitely A step above. Most weightlifting trackers I've tried in the paid version is even better. I hope to update my review after a few weeks but my initial take? This app is the real deal! 🏋🏼‍♀️ Over the last year, I've taken up distance running. I just completed my third half marathon. The strong lifts light program was a perfect fit for my limited recovery training schedule!

Poor new update! Unfortunately with the new update you are no longer able to download videos. This means you have to stream it live. It then keeps getting stuck on buffering whilst trying to workout, and this morning it wouldn't load for me at all. They have also taken away being able to see the exercises for the session, which I used to look at in advance before my session would begin to ensure I knew each exercise. It also makes it more difficult as it's all chunked together into one session!

A straight forward interface that's easy to use. The searches are great and I like the aspect of keeping track of what you browsed. It also saves your purchase items so you can order them again. It does has a subscription option though. Overall e it's an excellent app.

Word of caution for anyone thinking of using the app: it is near to impossible to get your running data off of nike if you upload it there. I have spent over a year trying to get my data sent to me and their maze of a system makes it near to impossible. They do not want to give you the data and are happy to breach GDPR regulations. There are many better alternatives to this app that freely allow you to export your data. Garmin, Strava, and many more.

The new update is horrible. Garmin fitness watch app no longer links to it, adding calories burned for exercise never stays, the extra steps for adding food just made the app worse & viewing daily macros automatically goes too weakly, so you have to manually swap to see how much you consumed on a single day..... i have been using this application for years. It didn't need to be updated

This is a genuinely great app for calorie/macro/nutrient tracking. I've used a few apps and this is hands down the best one, even just with the free version. Ads are not too excessive or long. The amount of features you get on the free version are higher than some paid apps. I'm still learning the finer points of the UI but it was easy to jump in and get started. I am very happy with Cronometer and will most likely upgrade to the paid version at some point purely to support this app.

i am disapponted in premium version. Photo recognition and text input do not work at all. Besides that premium version does not differ from version for free. So do not recommend anyone to buy premium, waste of money.
Disappointing. Some great features but this is not easy app for users with food allergies. Was recommended to choose veggie diet although my only allergy is egg.

great alarm, all of the essential features function in the free version, which is pretty hard to come by! I don't find the stats useful, and I'm not convinced they are accurate. they might be much better if I had a smartwatch or something. it can't really tell if I'm awake or asleep, but it wakes me up every morning and I'm down from 5 alarms to one.

Has definitely gotten worse over the years but is still probably the best app of this type, with the most detailed tracking. Full screen ads with a tiny untappable 'x' in the corner, random crashes and slowdowns, the usual software enshittification. I can now recommend this app to my friends and colleagues only reluctantly. It didn't used to be like this.
This app was amazing until about a month ago when it barely started up anymore. I hope they fix this issue because I loved this app!

From the tracking apps is this the best I used. Quite exact, allowing users to adjust metrics to track even in basic and tracking can be paused also when the screen is locked. On the other side, application keeps promoting premium very agressively, everytime when I'm online it asks me if I want to buy premium and also shows me an ad. I was very dissapointed when it played the commercial loudly when I had active silent mode in class - that was just stupid.

UPDATE: 2-star --> 4-star ... Tracking has been fixed but sometimes posts will not load. (4/20) This used to be a great app but within the past few months the tracking is off; my workouts don't save; the activity feed disappears; and I cannot upload workout photos. Unfortunately, it's time for me to find a new app!

Have used this to build a healthy workout habit a couple of times. Doing it alone, having the Endomondo app itself is like keeping great company. Very happy with the spread of key measures on every workout, even in the free version of the app. It inspires to keep it and not quit. Thanks folks.

This app might be the most frustrating app I've ever used. First, their website was horrible and the way they manage user logins in a confusing way, especially when you want to sign up for multiple classes from  multiple providers. I finally broke down and installed the app.  I tried searching for the place I want to go to tomorrow morning, and typed it in about 10 different ways with no successful results. Anyway, their website is confusing, and this app is just as bad.  Complete fail for trying to register for tomorrow's class.

The app works fine and is easy to actually use but there is no way to modify a training schedule. It is set up on a Monday start date, even if that doesn't work for you. If you miss a a workout there is no way to redo it, you just get pushed through to the next week with longer and harder runs. It would be nice if it was a 'number of days' program that didn't progress unless you did instead of a rigid weekly program that doesn't allow for life.

I've been using StrongLifts for years now. I recommend this app as it's great for allowing you to adjust workouts (sets & weight), timing your sets, and recording workout history so you can look back and see your progress.
Great for the most part but sometimes the device disconnects without any warning

I've used this app for nearly ten years (paid for the full version long ago) and it worked really well.. but it's finally kicked the bucket. As others have said, GPS no longer works after a minute or two. I thought it was my phone's fault but I'm so glad I read these reviews before I factory reset it. RIP Edomondo.

Not user friendly at all. It wouldn't let me add/change my credit card info (from app or computer) I wrote them they gave THE most useless generic response 2 days later "did you try to turn your phone off then back on" 🙃 Do you think your users are stupid and wouldn't try basic stuff like that? I ended up figuring it out myself before said response. If your address in the app doesn't match the billing address on your credit card, you cant add said card. There, did your tech support for you.

I don't like this big bubble circle map of my workouts...of my steps. I want the previous version's record of days weeks or months compared in that list form it had. I could easily scroll down and see, for the past two years, which months or weeks or days I was most active. I used them as benchmarks to beat. And now where is all that data? Is it non-transferable to this new version? I'm very disappointed in my inability transfer back to the old version and in the lack of historical data in this.

I've been using this app for a number of years and i love it. i've watched it evolve and grow, always an improvement. i will say though, having a smartwatch or fitness tracker to connect to it is ideal. i find it has a hard time with using just my phone next to my pillow, after my second smartwatch broke. theres a lot of useful feedback you get from your sleep data in charts summaries. the "lullabys" have a decent variety of sounds to fall asleep, theres an add on for more options.

Downloaded so I could use my Kinetic Road Machine Smart Control trainer with Zwift on Windows PC. A little quirky but usable. You need the Android app and PC program up and running both using bluetooth or ANT+ to work. You get a few remote options using the phone app while you're on your bike but it needs a way to be able to work the BT pairing via phone app when you are troubleshooting on your own. The PC program recently went through a mandatory update (you dont get a choice) and now the program will connect to my trainer but will say it's not getting a signal from the trainer within 5-6 seconds rendering it USELESS. Hopefully will be fixed as I know it's not the trainer (everything works in Rouvy). Maybe the upcoming Android version of Zwift will be better. If only BKool supported my trainer. Zwift has great fitness programs and I really want to use it and pay for it but of course will not if they can't remedy soon.

I've been using this app all summer to track workouts and all of a sudden this week it's stopped tracking accurately. It'll say I've gone half a mile and then the tracker will immediately go back down to 0.22 miles walked. How does that even make sense? Using something else now because I don't have time for an app that doesn't do the only thing it's intended to do.
apps are good for the most part. but when you use GPS tracking with a whoop and connected device on the walk, the map is VERY hard to zoom in and out of or even move around in. horrible interface

They're about making money per month! to see a detailed graph of your heart, and workouts you have to get premium!! Then you have to install MyFitnessPal for nutrition! with Samsung health app all that comes with my Samsung Galaxy phones for the past 8 years ( has everything I just mentioned with no ads FREE ) the interface is easier, it has more of everything. You even get free workouts and again don't have to pay!! Was just bored trying new apps.. what a disappointment!!! Samsung rules!!!

Hate the new layout since migrating from FB to Google Health... it's just too wildly different and unnecessarily complicated. It's so much easier to just scroll down to see the info I want to track versus all these various screens and swiping. The app is not tracking my naps automatically. The hydration fluid amounts are... weird? The food log is now one long list. The resting heart rate is not the one I want to see... too much AI...

I've always been a little sceptical about such apps, but I guess I'm a stereotypical dude who decided to try it out and got nicely surprised. So far, I managed to lose 4 kg (8.8 lbs) in about 2 months. Of course, the app requires consequence. You need to be committed. Additionally, I run on the treadmill every other day. Still, the app is of great help. What I would personally improve is the possibility of editing the weight of meals scanned with the help of AI, not just the nutrients.
Great product and very in depth app with excellent AI. At this price point though it ought to be!
changed my review from 5 to 1 star. no I do not want to subscribe for AI garbage. I have my workout and I don't want to do anything else and give you a thing monthly to track something so basic.
The app was never improved, and the past three orders, I have had issues. My Mom was a dedicated customer as well until she started having issues with her orders.

Not working any more. Cannot sign in despite multiple password resets. I wanted to find a business I know is near me and it doesn't appear, nor do the classes and it just keeps kicking me out and telling me I'm a guest. Rubbish! It used to work brilliantly.

Terrible, so much worse than I feared. Forced onto Google Fit it today from Fitbit. So many functions lost completely: hourly movement, for example, which has been clinically proven to be an important metric of health. The home screen is awful. Presumably I have to buy the premium service to have more than a few tiny tiles on display. This has convinced me to get an Oura device, even if it means losing 11 years of Fitbit data. Cheap and nasty looking app.

New FatSecret user. I'm pretty satisfied with the app but is there a way to customize nutritional content? I'm making cannellini beans and I'm adding no salt at all. But the database in the app shows that dry cannellini beans have 300+ mg of sodium. Is there a work around? The app is a little clunky and not intuitive. Such as, I want to edit my nutritional goals. I'd expect that to be easy to find, like a main menu choice. Instead go to Reports and click the target icon.

Dear Fit Developers, I love your app I use it everyday and keeps me motivated to exercise daily! I have a complaint and a suggestion to make, however. - I think that the app could be better designed in terms of what are buttons that one can use to access more information (like there are no affordances to tell you that you can touch the distance/calories/steps numbers in the homepage). - I would love it if one could change the calorie count showed to Net calories burned instead of gross calories.

I really like this app, but I'm having trouble syncing my run data from Samsung health connect to Nike Run challenges, I'm not sure what the reason is for this failure. Sometimes it works and sometimes it doesn't... It's frustrating when it doesn't count towards challenges when you manually input the information when it didn't sync with Health Connect.

Horrible. You have to purchase extras for the app in order to bookmark your places, and often times even then it won't work properly. The location feature is a huge hindrance if you're searching by name, and it's impossible to search without the location setting, because it'll just start listing everything near you so you can't find what you're looking for. The interface isn't very user friendly and the app overall is very inconsistent. What a let down.

I didnt mind the ads before they were rarely bothersome. However within the last couple months ive become so annoyed with the increase in obnoxious ads. They pop up right as im trying to search something. Literally right in the middle of me typing! Drives me absolutely nuts! Ive deleted the app and gone back to just using Fitbit.. but I find myself missing Cronometer. I understand ads when you are using a free app I do. Just the timing of them is soo annoying I hesitate to go back :(

Love Map My Fitness!! I set it at the beginning of my workout and do not have to worry about counting steps or miles. At the end of my workout it has done everything for me! Great app especially when synced with Google Fit! The 2 apps together take away all the guess work in staying active and fit.
The app looks good, I don't cook often but I'd love to see you win the case against apple so that's why I am here, **CK APPLE.

The latest update does not allow color to be assigned to a specific activity and I really miss that option. Color is a great way of distinguishing differing activities quickly. Update... color is back! The the choice of an octogon shape for the widget is strange. It looks unrefined. The widget does update better than it used to, but it's not perfect.

I'm not sure if it's just me, but even the beginner programs are too advanced compared to other programs out there. I don't think their programs (even premium) are inclusive and appropriate for all body types. Also, I wish I could add my music through Spotify. But thanks for letting people use premium for free during quarantine.

Update: still glitchy, still wonky n slow, but somewhat improved. i think this may be the worst app I've ever used. it's really super fun picking out a bunch of items to buy, and adding them to your cart, only to see that your cart remains empty and you've wasted a bunch of time. figured by now, y'all would have fixed this abomination of programming. I can't even imagine how much this EXTREME FAIL (glitch is too kind a word) is vitacosting this company.

Edit 8/26/19: I cannot get this to connect to My Fitness Pal, made by the same company. Pretty ridiculous-- please fix! Just did my first street run, and i felt so motivated when i could hear my progress and sew my course as I ran. First time using app, so i need to rest it further, but good so far. Counts calories, too.

Path mapping appears a little inaccurate but a wide variety of stats maintained and lots of options. Audio updates are nice too. I find the map details dont update while riding so sometimes I have to stop just to get the map to update. I have been using for a while and saving my stats and routes for comparison and the more I use the app the more I like it. Options to turn off the screen and save battery is great to.

Worst UX ever... You cannot sleep at night, turn on the app, your eyes are half open... You try to listen to something, it doesn't work, another one, it doesn't work... Until you decide to read the message asking you to pay something in the middle of the night! Very clever guy!!! If all the musics are not free, so make them free, and make a purchase app...

I like VitaCost, but there are several things that make their Regular & Mobile websites very frustrating to navigate. Therefore, I will go to Amazon, Target, or Wal-Mart's website first and will only go to Vista cost if I have to. Which is too bad. I did try the app; however, the elements that caused frustration on their websites still existed on their app. With that said, I am impressed with how Vitacost packs their products.
it keeps track of everything even if you want to put notes in it and pictures.

So far so good! This was the first, free, bi-weekly available alarm that worked the best for me on Google Play. I reccomend this app to other people like me, who have crazy schedules and need something to rely on to get their days correct. One thing I wish the app had is a calendar to set specific day by day alarms. That way every other day, every other month, and daily alarms could be active at the same time in a more organized way.

latest update has totally messed up entire app. I had a prepaid, year membership at a studio. never has it shown me that there's a fee for classes but after update, it's showing a charge. I've checked my membership, which is still active, checked with the studio and they've changed nothing. it's 💯 the app update.

All of Vitacost's products are completely natural, affordable and very easy to re/order. I've been a customer for over 5 years and wouldn't buy my vitamins/health supplements anywhere else! Their organic foods and snacks are the tastiest around!

I love all of the free content. There truly is a workout for every need someone may have. However, I am giving it 3 stars because it isn't saving any of my workouts I have done within programs, and my Activity is saying I haven't done a workout since August 2024 despite having done quite a few since then. I have even tried ending programs and restarting them within the app to see if it would start tracking them properly, but it hasn't. This is such a shame because everything else is so great.

Free trial was great, could see current game ride, are other riders, chat, give thumbs up all that. Once you pay you lose all those features. I restarted phone, I uninstalled and reinstalled still can't do what I did with the free trial. Weird you pay and lose features

I have been a dedicated user of the premium version for 8 years. While I wanted to love the recent updates to the interface because it looks fresh - the functionality has suffered. It's difficult/impossible to see an entire day at a glance. Copying meals from day to day isn't seamless and I always end up with things entered on, or copied to, the wrong day. The app feels cumbersome with extra steps needed to log food and meals.

This app was at a 5 for me for a long time, but recently they updated the app and user interface and a lot of the things I used to enjoy I can no longer access easily. Browsing by category mostly hasn't changed, but they removed the feature where you can search workouts by trainer. I had workouts that I really enjoyed by specific trainers and now that entire tab is gone. The programs tab is just a really long scroll. Still amazing workouts, but harder to use app.
Took my money and ran. I originally purchased the Power Pack because I was happy to support the developer. Now they've decided to remove the features I paid for. Don't support this robbery.

I'm joining the chorus of people complaining about the new interface. If you want to see what you ate, you now have to access each meal individually (or access the list with one extra, useless tap) because all it shows is one item + x more instead of the full list like before. No one asked for this. It's just making things more tedious for no reason. I don't mind the other changes so much but PLEASE, let me see the full list at a glance.

it's very disappointing because some of my runs did not add to my previous runs in the challenge, I've worked hard to achieve that kilometers but it did not add to my challenge. it was added to the activity but it did not add to the challenge how disappointing😞😞😞😞😞

Super frustrating app, so many boxes and questions with screens you can't skip. just let me add my food, I don't want to answer a million questions. going back to other apps which link with my watch and don't patronise me or ask me 20 questions. I remember why I uninstalled this junk.

I was excited to download this app and have been using it regularly for several months. The problem is that their Daily Calm does not really help you meditate. There's not enough silence to allow you to go deep. I have suggested that they produce a short version (10 minutes) and a 20-minute version, simply lengthening the silence. i have rated each session "more silence" but apparently they are not listening. Yes, i could pause the recording to allow for more silence but that requires thought.

Absolutely awful, as soon as you pay for it you realise what a huge waste of money this app is. You get nothing from paying from it, it's a broken app which makes you find all the items yourself and figure out everything yourself, it literally does nothing that you can't do yourself with a notepage, calculator and the most basic of nutrition theory. Don't fall for its bs claims it offers nothing at all and just makes it harder to track your weightloss journey making it harder to track.

Update: Checked this morning and app still hadn't synced data from my phone since 11/24. Rebooted the device, then left app open to allow data to catch up. Data finally synced with server but discovered that, despite wearing 4.0 strap 24/7 and charged for the last 3 days, absolutely no data from that time was recorded. Have a few months left on my membership, but will cancel if this doesn't improve.
This app helps me to prioritize self care! I love that it shows me all of the current deals studios offer. It allows me to explore a variety of classes.
You can add new foods not found in the database on the website, but you can't in the app. Please add this feature.
every time I get over 6 miles this app crashes. I have to uninstall and redownload for it to function for another run. What is wrong with this app?

This is a good app and I see a LOT of potential for the app and the tracking. The major drawback I see (and will soon likely change) is that if there is food or a bar code that isn't in their database, there is no way to enter it and there is no way to quickly enter at least the calorie count if you are serious about tracking that.

Didn't think this app was that bad to start, but starting to slowly have more and more GPS issues. Running the same path every couple of days and actually ran longer in the first 5 minutes than normal but instead of being longer than the normal .52 it tells me I only went .48. Time to look into another app.

Burns a lot of battery with Garmin venu (a lot less when I was using wearos). The graphs and such can be quite small and hard to read without an option to enlarge sections or scroll around. I also find that with a Garmin watch, the "system" keeps running even when you stop and save the sleep which means the alarm keeps going off, often until I reboot!

Was a long time Whoop user - starting in 2017. Really unimpressed with the 4.0. I reached out to cancel my recurring membership while I had more than a month left on my membership. They transferred me among the departments for a week, then the final customer service person turned the whoop off while I still had time left on the account. All in all, these things are a waste of money. You pay a ton each month and get nothing. Whoop doesn't even honor the time you've already purchased. Do not recommend. Anything this does Garmin does better and you actually own that watch.
I love how efficient this app is! Shows what classes are offered at many locations plus instructors!!

I like the km times when I'm running and the map of where I have been. Love the new UI , well done Nike. Thank you for fixing the shoes list. You need to bring out t-shirts 👕 to celebrate the run levels, that would be great reward for sticking with your app.

relax practice not work without network connection, whoop simultaneously disconnected from app, activity auto-detection worx bad, stupid time input interface when filling out the journal — it's impossible to get to the right time (previous review magically deleted from store)

I used to love this app. I downloaded it over 5 years ago. It offered a lot more free content for a few years and it even had a rain and ocean sound for free which helped me sleep. It got me through a lot back then with the walkthroughs and sounds and for that I am definitely grateful. Now I am extremely discouraged that all the sounds and all but one walkthrough are behind a paywall. I am going to look for a different app as this one wants you to subscribe instead of a one and done payment.
Every time I turn off push notifications, the app automatically turns them back on. I don't need to hear about a 10% off sale on items I don't use every 2 hours.

If there's Bluetooth required for sending my activities to my phone why do I need the Internet to see them on my phone. this app should function offline. It's useless in low signal areas as the other functions like groups would not even load.
Totally useless. "Track your workouts" link just redirects you to the website and doesn't actually track your workout.
Well I used to like this app, but they've played with it so much, if you can actually get to something you want, you still cannot add it to the cart. So you can't buy anything if you wanted to.

I love the idea of this app but the user experience is absolutely awful. I can never actually book through the app and it constantly forgets me as a user so I end up logging into the app three separate times when I open it and that is if it managed not to crash in the 5-10 minutes I have it open.
This app is barely useable. Buttons overlap so I can't place an order, product images don't come up. It's just not functional.

What in the world has this app come to. Am I remembering wrong that they didn't used to shove the subscription down your throat. BHive is an awesome studio!! Every class I have tried, I have absolutely loved!

Aweful experience, I order many things 20 days ago, when it came to taking the money they took it instantly but they didnt ship the order yet and even worst no1 is replying to my E-mails. Dont buy from them they are a scam.
Terrible! Each run it says to hold stop to end/save a run and the only option I get is ti discard a run. 3 runs not recorded. I've tried troubleshooting and reinstalling the app. Useless

I like the apps. I even pay for the premium because of it. It also has my country food options in there. It's not perfect, but good enough, I guess. I just hope you add a fibre bar to the main screen as well, like protein, fat, and carbs.

So far, so good. Came from another app that became user unfriendly. Been using for past six months and have reduced my weight by 20 lbs. Thanks for this tool. My only suggestion at this time would be to consider adding a confirmation prompt when backing out of a menu where you've been adding items. It's too easy to accidentally back all the way out and lose all that you've entered when you only meant to delete one thing. This also applies when accidentally swiping across the screen.

This is a great app, and I've used it for years. I've used it to track different types of diets, calorie, keto, Mediterranean, CKD and fasting. Just set your targets and you're off. I also have it syncing with my Fitbit, KetoMojo monitor and iHealth BP monitor. I love being able to build custom recipes, and their food database is extensive, so it's easy to find your favorites. As you can tell, I really appreciate this app!

I've used this app for the last 8 years. It had a limited feature list that was convenient in 2014. They've expanded that list to a lottery of buttons and features that might work (if you're lucky.) I'm locked to my account through a Facebook sign in, the app doesn't work when signing in any other way. The final straw is now losing all of my purchased class passes, and saved method of payments. I'm just tired of the inconsistency. I will now be making a bigger effort to avoid this service.
Will not even open
Excellent! Fast setup, easy to use, maximum workout time... Keep up the good work!

Missing some pretty basic functionality. I was hoping for a one stop fitness data hub. Unfortunately it doesn't import weight biometrics or nutrition data from Health Connect. The data is there, my other apps share it. So far Google fit is still the best at having the most complete summary. I do like how Fitbit presents the data it does handle, but I'm absolutely not paying for premium insights if they can't even import basic data.
I'm just a beginner and the app worked well for me. I will use it every ride.

This App has helped me track my eating habits and curve most of my bad eating choices. By logging everything I consume, it allows me to see my caloric intake as well as the percentage ofcarbs, protien and fats. If you commit to keeping a daily log and keeping track of all you consume the app can really help to hold yourself accountable and make better eating choices. It helps set realistic weight loss goals. Only issue is the food data base, as I have had trouble finding some of my meals.

I signed up for the free 7-day trial for the premium plan. Even scheduled a text reminder for 6 days later to remind me to access if I liked the features enough to commit or cancel. Well, I was shocked to see next day I had been charged. I submitted request through Play store for a refund and they denied me. I'm going back to using Lose It app and disputing this charge with my bank to get my money back smh. Not a good experience and I don't recommend based off the customer service I received.

So well designed! So easy to use and I feel confident in the numbers and nutritional data, 98 percent of the time-- which is a lot more than other apps I've tried. I just need to figure out how to see the ingredients and amounts, AFTER I've added things to a recipe, so I can duplicate it next time without guessing or forgetting what I did the time before. I'm using it with the Lumen app, and I can make target templates and apply the right one to the current day. I'm very happy with this app!

A great app for following 5x5 Stronglifts; Exactly what anyone following the program needs! This app is great if youre coming from a previous routine, or if you're starting out completely fresh. It easily tracks exactly what you need to do, and directs you to their website if you're unsure on how to do it. Far and away the easiest way to track this particular routine, as there seems to be confusion that people think it's a general workout app? You walk into gym, use app, and walk out. 10/10

It's great when it actually works...I use the app for alarms, I like forcing myself to do a math problem to turn it off so I don't fall back asleep. However. Sometimes the alarms don't go off...They are not set for another day. They are set for that exact date and time. Most of the time the app works as it should. But I can't risk my alarms not going off... The default alarm volume has also changed on its own a few times, always changing the sound to be much quieter...something I did NOT do.
Visibly stutters when using the keyboard and constantly crashes. A hot mess of an app.
I can't fully review this app since I can't even login. Every time I correctly enter my username and password, it says it is incorrect.

To do anything meaningful, you need to log in, but it's such a pain. doesn't use biometrics, only the embedded browser, which doesn't work well with my password manager. having to log in most times because I'm not in it every day is the opposite of the experience i want and expect.

I've been a Stronglifts user for a long time - paid apps and all. But the app no longer works reliably. Doesn't reliably log workouts, randomly skips. Sometimes the log is correct, other times it's empty. This app is BROKEN. It will work for a week and then suddenly it tells you your last workout was weeks ago, and your next is the wrong one. USELESS.

The app nicely presents trends in calories, steps, duration, etc., but it falls short in some surprising areas. For example, the app will show a route overview of a hike, but the app doesn't let you zoom or pan on the map, so I can't see how accurate the track was, or precisely where I started or stopped, or even what's nearby. Another issue is the lack of any logic to detect when a workout has finished. The app will continue tracking for days until it's manually stopped.

I like this app a lot. Well, I used to. Then a lot more bugs started happening. I finished a half marathon training and now it won't let me set up any more training / race preps. Every time I enter my data, it says an error occurred. And now about one in ever 5 runs isn't recorded and is just time with no gps distance tracked. Very frustrating! I've started just running without it and entering my data post run.

Glitches all the time! I have only had the band for 3 weeks and have already had to unistall/reinstall the app 4 times. Anytime I have to put my phone on airplane mode and turn my phone back on, the app locks up and crashes repeatedly and won't connect. The only thing to fix is uninstall. Other than connecting issues, I like the analytics.

data insights are good, but under free trial, I hate how aggressive you are in trying to upsell me. I noticed the navigation is so confusing, especially the "more" section because the whole top page is covered by modules to upsell me. I have to scroll much lower to actually find what I was looking for like "journal". I thought I couldn't find it

great device and great app connection. It syncs basically in real time and it's nice to track my health stats more detailed in the app. There is no glitching or crashing. The app was easy to connect to the Garmin watch.
love the app, however for some reason it interferes with the volume of HeadSpace and lowers it to zero over say 20 minutes. Highly annoying.

There are three types of cycling: indoors, sport, transport; and some scuba diving which I don't think an apt activity to track on phone. But there is no unicycling, which is very frustrating to me as a unicyclist, although there have been many requests to add unicycling. My running tempo has not been adjusted after a speed test. And that's not dor the first time now. Very upsetting.

Along with Headspace, Calm is my favorite method to enjoy guided meditation! The app has lots of variety and guided playlists for what you'd like to focus on. It keeps track of time meditated, sessions completed, and how many days (streaks!) you meditate in a row. The option to switch between female and male narration is a neat feature, and for $60 it's hard to beat for a year subscription! I'll continue to use this as my platform for guided meditation, and recommend it for daily use and your meditation/mindfulness goals :)

this app honestly is life changing for me. I've always struggled to wake up in the morning and stay awake. I can turn alarm clocks off in my sleep without waking up (my partner has watched me do it for years) after years of constantly running late in the mornings and having to scramble to get out the door I can confidently say the barcode scanner alarm clock this app offers has changed my mornings. I can wake up gradually to the building alarm tone and get momentum getting out of bed. 10/10

update: the app has been fixed and is fantastic to use once again! I've loved this app, but not since the last update. The app navigation buttons are directly under my phone's navigation buttons. In fact I can even select "log in" instead of "create an acct" unless I split/ share my screen so the navigation buttons aren't on top of each other. If I split/share my screen and adjust it at all then it shuts down entirely and I'm back to logging in. Right now it's unusable on my phone.

I use it everytime I ride my bike & it seems to work everytime. Shows the area on a map that you've riden. Has a space you can make notes of temperature, wind, & any unusual things you may encounter. Good app. 👍😉

very scammy vibes tbh. the app uses psychological marketing tricks to make the customer believe, they won some huge deal (spinning a wheel, getting a scripted 85% discount). also, if you wanna upgrade your member status to pro, you are basically forced to take the annual deal, since monthly pricing for 3 months is at 20€/month (60€ total) while the annual deal costs you 7€ per month (84€ total). most features unique to this app are locked behind paywalls.

Way too many ads. after putting in each meal, you are greeted with an ad that requires you to click on it and takes you to the play store, then the ad will go away. then, if you havent paid, you have to click x on 3 different pages asking you to pay for a no ad experience. doing this multiple times a day is not worth it

Dates and calendar behaving very strangely, unable to see details of bookings for classes such as wait list or availability, clicking on next calendar date scrolls a week ahead instead of a day ahead for the studio. Frustrating update December 2025.

I have loved this app...until I downloaded the most recent version onto my new phone this past weekend. I have used it twice and now it won't open. I have uninstalled and reinstalled 3 times and it doesn't fix the issue. I hope it gets fixed soon as i'm starting c25k training next week.

About 85% of the time this app is fine - nothing special, but has some good features like sleep tracking and puzzles to turn off alarms. However, the alarm itself has more and more frequently just not been going off in the morning. I have a daily alarm set and I find if I open the app to change the time or just check that it is on, it won't go off. I simply cannot use an app in which the key feature isn't reliable.

App has good content, but it performs poorly. If you leave playing in the background, you sometimes can't pause it using the notification controls. If you don't leave it running in the background, sometimes it'll start playing on its own anyway, and silently drain your battery. I will unfortunately have to uninstall to avoid the unexpected battery drains.

Why would I ever pay for a premium membership on this app if the free version is this buggy already. Friend requests feature is malfunctioning. Meaning each time I logout of the app, all progress is lost. No use in re-entering data, still won't sync. Support says to use the desktop version for now, completely defeats the purpose of having an app.

I've been using this app since 2010. The macros aren't even correct lol. Yet another company that probably has record profits and qualitative decline to the point of their product being unusable. Like I'm at sub 100g carbs today, and it keeps showing me over my allotment. Does using this app add calories now? The UI, like so many products, is downright unfriendly and nonsensical. This app used to be decent but since the latest update it's been horrible. It also crashes and restarts at any seemingly simple interaction like loading maps etc. Unfortunate since I used to actually find new classes and studios though the app but now it's near impossible.
Nice app but scanner doesn't work... Or else the app is good.
always nice to see a map of your ride and all the important stats during and after the ride. I could be happier with this tool.

Fitbit was already in decline over the past year as far as its sleep tracking was concerned, but since it was rebranded as Google Health, it is absolutely disastrous. It has me going to bed an hour later than I did, or when I edit my sleep to reflect my actual start or end time, it adjusts all of the other sleep areas in ways that are flat out wrong. The scores are basically useless now. Edit: talk about missing the plot with your response. I know how the sleep tracking works. It just sucks now

This app is absolutely fantastic! It allows me to track my sleep progress and address the issues I'm having. I use it daily and the results have been great. It's simple to use and easy to follow. Great App!

I used to love the nike app. In the last year it has been a erking nerves .Some days it works fine some days it doesn't work correctly. Some days it doesn't calculate my miles properly . Someday it calculates properly but it does not add my to my challenge. Someday my mile show up and then they disappear, As if I never walked that day. I am very frustrated with this app. I am tired of calling customer service about this app.

The app is easy to use and looks nice! Lots of data! Nonetheless, there a few issues I'd like to see fixed: - taking naps won't add to your sleep-time or recovery (I work nights three times a week and this confuses whoop since I occasionally only get a few hours of sleep but catch up with naps) - Sometimes when recording martial arts or swimming, Whoop is not able to determine calories (map tracking was on and messed everything up) or time spent on the activity (noticed in my Strava-feed)

I’ve had ongoing issues where I need to uninstall and reinstall the app about once a week for it to work properly. Syncing stops to other apps, and I lose several days of data that I have to re-enter. The smiley face on the home screen often stops updating, showing it isn’t syncing with other apps. I contacted support a month ago and was told a ticket was submitted, but the issue still isn’t resolved. It is frustrating, but the app is useful when it works.

BY FAR THE BEST tracking app that exists!! SO easy to scan a food and add it by grams, so easy to add a food that's not in their database yet (just 2 quick pics of the food and the nutrition label) the nutrition info IS ALL ACTUALLY ACCURATE. I love that the bar for each vitamin,etc. turns red if you go over the upper allowance, because I used to get stressed when certain things would say like 283%. This app could not be better I love it so much ❤️
Great app. It's like having a health coach by your side, guiding you with the most accurate and up-to-date information.

Free version review only: I have not tried the Pro version, but the free version works great. The interface is simple and easy to use and the easy access to short gifs with instructions allows for beginners to be sure that they avoid injuries and get the most from their workouts. As a workout, it is one of the best at utilizing compound movements to improve overall strength in a short period of time. Fully recommend.

There's not enough explanation for pose like "world's greatest stretch" and "runner lunges" if you've never done them. Beside, it' s a pretty hard pose. Having "t-push up" in a beginner course feels discouraging. I feel the difficulty is uneven. Some things are pretty accessible and others not much.

MFP really helps with mindful eating. At the start, it takes some time to get used to tracking your intake, but the habit builds healthy intuitive eating. Love it and highly recommend! I also got the premium version, and the barcode scanner is such a game changer. Suggestion to Devs: Create the option to customize the nutrition the user wants to track. E.g. tracking magnesium/vitamin B etc.

Just hit my 90 day mark and I'm down 16.7 pounds! I absolutely LOVE this app, it's been a very important tool in my health journey helping you make sure you're hitting protein goals and staying under calories. I highly recommend this to anyone trying to eat healthier or lose weight to become healthier.

The app is great I have been using it for 2 years already, even had subscription. But something happened to the radio stations, I probably will come back as a subscriber if the radio improves. This is the feature I really wanted. The generic non descript music more often than not wakes me up annoyed with how bad it is. I don't care about Spotify and don't want to put my own music in it. Just don't want to wake up to a dude moaning in my ear... please add a proper rock/metal radio.

Absolutely hate the navigation in the new update (4/2/26). Things that used to take 1-2 clicks now take twice as many. The edit time > use last time function only shows the current time and I have to go into each food to update it properly. What happened to the home button? Why is there a "Plan" button now? I already subscribe, so that button should take me to information about me, but instead it's just an advertisement for the premium+ tier. It's so frustrating that I will not be renewing.

Edit: Since the last update the app keeps changing battery usage to unrestricted, despite me changing it to optimized on my Samsung s23+ and runs my battery down. I have had a Garmin Vivofit 2 for over 7 years now and it's perfect for me but the app interface is absolutely horrible. I like the way the old interface used to be in the app there's just too much space going on, I liked it when everything was more compact and concise.

I love this app. I use it to track both cycling and walking. Setup for a workout is quick and easy. It keeps track of all the important indicators and it maps my location with other useful information during my workout. I love the overview calendar and the ability to edit details of the workout after the workout is saved.

The build your own recipe part is hard to use and time consuming. It would be a lot easier to scan bulk ingredients but you can't. Also it only allows about two ingredients then, you cant see what you typing. once you make the recipe and save it, you cant edit it or see what the ingredients are, so later if you forget you can use it, its very poorly made. I love the rest of the app, but I wont renew unless they change that part I will look for one with better ability to build your own recipes.
I can't log in. Tried to change pass but I got stock on loading.

This device has been very helpful in learning my sleep deficiencies. I can see at a glance how long I was awake, in deep sleep, REM and light sleep. This singular thing has helped me feel better. I can also see progress in my stamina and it tells me when I need to have a light day because I'm pushing myself too hard. It has really helped me to take better care of myself!

After using the free version for years and taking a break thus past festive season, I opened the app to find an unskippable questionnaire and unasked for "personalised" workout in place of the wonderfully functional, core feature packed, and easy to use workout logger it used to be. The friendly community, and freely available basic plans were what got me into exercising regularly. I wish you all the best for your new business model Mr. Medhi, but I won't be using or recommendi this app anymore.

I love not having to think. I'll be interested to see once I start failing reps, how it handles the subsequent exercises. My only recommendation would be to introduce a mechanism in the app to increase rep/sets for one workout to the next for body weight exercises(pull-ups, push-ups, Ab wheel, etc) instead of adding weight (I don't own a weight belt).

I love the guided runs on this app. Some of them feel really meditative. I used this to train for my first 5k and my first 10k. So many good runs! My biggest issue: I desperately wish there was a way to sort or filter guided runs. It is a bit frustrating to spend so long just scrolling to find a run to fit what I need. I would love to be able to sort by time or distance or themes. I also don't think the run tracking part is fantastic. I rely on my Garmin for doing that.

Toddlers should not need a calorie counting app, but the new design heavily implies that it's target audience lays with kids under 5 yo. Please, offer a switch button for users to be able to disable your "motivational" features. Such a downgrade. Edit to add: omg it's only getting uglier and less usable

I've used the app for both iOS and Android, and found it nearly flawless. I've been on Swift since Nov 17, Tacx Flux, 8yr old inexpensive laptop with the ant+ antenna and I've only had 1 problem, when a ride didn't save. Apparently 'world swap' isn't too far away, but as long as we have very little of Richmond, I'm happy. Only negative on the app is the inability to 'start' a ride through it

Great app for cycling, especially in far off places where there is no data or signal. Most other apps do not work then. I've been using this app for 4 years now and switched phones twice in that time without losing any of my training history. It's got a few quirks like how to manually enter a previous day's workout, but once you've worked your way around it, it's easy as. Well done Endomondo Devs!

I love this app. It's got a huge database of foods and also has the ability to adjust sizes (for example, instead of 1 serving, you can put .5 for half.) It's very easy to use and I love how it also tracks carbs, protein and fat for me. I need to get better at grams, tho. LOL. Oh, and I also like that you can save meals for easy adding to your diary. For instance, I typically have a bran muffin with either yogurt or a variety of fruits so I save each and can just click on one when I have it.

why they switched from the fit app to Google I'll never know but I DO know the switch was stupid. none of the data is accurate now. my fitbit says i walked a certain amount of steps but it doesn't show that on the app. I keep track of steps at work and the app says I only walked 5 miles for the whole week. I walk that in the first hour in one day. this switch is a disaster. time to go buy another watch to keep track of everything.

Excellant app. Have been using it for years. Recently experienced what could be a bug in the app. If i change my endo password on say, a PC and then open endo on my phone (which has already been logged in with old password), the whole app goes into an infinite loop of on and off and only phone restart will stop the looping. Please correct this issue. My phone is Samsung S9 running Android 9.

Really useful if you already know what business you'll be using, super glitchy for more general searches. When scrolling I'll suddenly get the "no search results" screen and have to refresh to get back to the list. If I don't refresh completely, it will show "no results" for entire days at a time. So frustrating.

I seriously loved this app. It was a great catch-all for me because I hike, cycle, and horseback ride. But now that they're discontinuing it, the whole thing is going downhill. I have no idea why they decided to kill this thing; it was reliable, easy to use, and would sync nicely will other apps. Super disappointed because all of the other fitness apps look like buggy, expensive messes. PLEASE Google I BEG YOU to keep this app alive.

Already Vitacost customer, installed app to get offered 10% discount but the code did not work. When signing in it was almost like I was a new customer as some of my information was missing. So I uninstalled the app.

Couldn't get it to connect to my power meter or the PC. On the PC app I was also having the greyed out Bluetooth options problem others are having. Found myself subconsciously singing Adam Sandler's POS car while trying to set it up. Sufferfest here I come!
It keeps closing out on me and brings back to the app store. This is a great app idea, I love love this app!

My Personal Favorite. I love this app. I can't believe it's all FREE! I tried Strava but found the app too "busy" for my liking. From the guided runs to the plans - all at your fingertips and free. Unbelievable value for $0. You don't even have to use any of those. Coach Bennett is just the icing on the cake. He is the absolute best. A gem. I find his knowledge and wisdom to be inspiring and motivating. He's fun, too. A little dry humor never hurt anyone; and it's my favorite kind of humor.
It keeps telling me I have am incorrect username or password. I've tried everything from a new account to changing the password to make sure I'm writing it correctly.

I especially like the recipe import function of this app. It is great! The app also allows for a lot of user customization. I'm still tweaking things to figure out the best way to display my daily inputs. I do love that it will count in grams, ounces, cups and tablespoons. That saves me a lot of calculating. The only thing this is missing for me is reminders to take vitamins, but that is a small complaint and easy to do in other ways.

Cronometer is the best racking app with MyFitnessPal now ruined by private equity greed pay walls + awful Ai and UI changes. Cronometer's device integration (Garmin, HealthConnect) is top notch. Only request is better search. When searching for foods, recently-added foods matching search parameters should pop up first. E.g., if I recently logged "Dole 100% grape juice," when searching "grape" results should prioritize the Dole grape juice just logged. Not other grape-related items/brands.

Not a bad app for organising meals and recipe colections, however there are 2 very annoying bugs, when you add a recipe any ingredient that starts with a "g" eg Garlic the app takes the g to mean grams and thinks the ingredient is the rest of the word, I need 1 green pepper not 1gram of reen pepper and it can't get recipes from the bbc

I do NOT like the new layout! I want to see at a glance what the meal was, not have to click to see the items! Bad design. Not happy!!! It is also too easy to hit the 'add previous days meal'. Then you have to laboriously delete each item singly (why can't you swipe to delete? You should at least be able to delete multiple items in one go. It is also to easy to find yourself deleting your history, not meal items.

Worked great for a long time, then suddenly the alarms stopped going off. Same alarms and settings I used for years. I have the simple math setup to shut off the alarm, so I doubt I was making the right calculations in my sleep or pressing the right answer by chance. Woke up earlier to see if it would go off. Time came and went and the alarm shut off without a sound. If you can't wake me the app is no good. Uninstalled. I gave 3 stars to be fair, It was a good app for several years.

I this app whenever I go on a weightloss journey. It really helps to ensure that my carbohydrate intake stays low as well as helping to restrict calories. I now have become aware just how much calories I used to consume before. I feel motivated to stay on diet by noting meals and weightloss. The food and brand options is really great and helps you to make better choices. This app keeps me on track, brilliant app.

APP CRASHING WHEN I CLICK ON EVENTS... First issue is that when in the companion app whilst on zwift, my heart rate is displayed over 2 lines. I.e. if my heart rate is 135, then I have 13then the heart symbol on one line then 5 under the 3. I have a screen shot to show. This is very annoying...... 2nd is the lack of a function to select a ride from the zwift companion app. Need to be able to start a new ride within the app.

I'd give it 5 stars but you have to pay for damn near everything and then if you listen to a certain sound after so many times they lock it and you have to pay for it they should have FREE sounds where you can listen to it as many times as you want

I'm editing my review. I've been using this app for about 2 weeks and I've been impressed with how much they have in their food data base. It be great if they had different options for premium . As of now it's 19.99 monthly or 79.99 yearly. both seem a little steep for a trial run . 29.99 for 3 months would be nice doable median . even if it was only allowed once. More people could try it and perhaps get more future user's. because as of now ,20 bucks is just too much money to try something

Great app to accompany Zwift. Wish it was intuitively easier to give a thumbs up to someone who gave you a thumbs up. You have to search through the list of closest riders and remember what their last name was.

This app helps me to remember to take just a moment during my day, whenever I feel stressed or anxious, and simply breathe, relax, and recenter my mind in the moment, on myself. I have a tendency to take on too much and then get overwhelmed. This small, intentional gesture reminds me that if I try to do too much at once, or think about too much at once, all things I attempt will be impacted negatively.

Two stars for the UI. That's it. To begin, this app logs you out every single time you open it. Which is obnoxious after the second time. The GPS on treadmill runs is FUBAR. As much as I enjoy a gratuitous 3:00/mi less on my average pace, this app has been around far too long to have so many issues with pace, yes, but also accurate mileage tracking. More recently, guided run audio has been dropping out. Love the guided runs, so this sucks the most. Fix your app, Nike.
the new update is really bad. the interface is not convenient,and I have a feeling that a lot of barcodes that were available before, are missing now.

Wow, I was having an issue with the app and emailed the contact us email in the app. They fixed it and got back to me within 2 hours. Insane customer service to match an incredible app. I've never recommended an app to someone so hard. By far one of the best apps I've used

Nice 👍 thx for adding the option to skip the food tips after scanning !! Logging makes way more fun now again. It's cool that YAZIO actually really changed this feature, I take therefore back my bad review! Great app
a beginner lifter, this app has kept me going and progressing. It's easy to use and implement for constant tracking and progression in training. Definitely recommend it for all.
I love the app, have hardly any complainants, just have a suggestion to make it Tablet friendly by putting it on a horizontal mode when I want to look at the recipes propped up on my counter.
The constant tips, streak notifications, etc. are spammy and annoying and can't be turned off. Otherwise it's a decent app, I'll change my review once the annoying parts are gone.

So far very helpful for meal planning and grocery list. I was also hoping that I'd be able to add prices next to the items in my shopping list so I could also budget at the same time. Otherwise it's perfect!

Why.... Why would you turn most of the workouts into videos? So now I can't use my favorite workouts while listening to music, this app used to be amazing but now it just isn't. It turns my music way down to the point that there is no use listening to it. I used to love it when it wasn't videos. Probably the worst decision you guys made for this app. This really dissapointed me and is very annoying. Please bring it back to the old version where you don't need to watch videos for workouts.

updated 2026 Feb, whoop MG. still poor connection with the phone and the wearable. AI is amazing. The following happened about every two months, now several times a month, the mobile app and the wristband cannot be connected and I need to uninstall before I can use it again. It takes me 10 mins each time to get the connection.
coming from Fitbit to Garmin, this is what Fitbit should have been by now if Google didn't take them over and ruin it. no problems so far, amazing app with tons of options in still discovering.

So it's a nice concept, but I'm not convinced the smart alarm actually works. It goes off half an hour before my set alarm every day. I have been woken up out of deep sleep too many times, and yes, I do position in correctly. I already tried trouble shooting this as human error. For something I pay a monthly fee for, it's just not working for the one thing I wanted from it. I'm going to cancel my subscription and try a different app.

App used to be good. The new version is horrible. Super laggy and slow, user interface is low-res, unintuitive design, etc. How could they mess up so badly? It's so bad that I no longer wish to buy from them after 10+ years of being a loyal customer.

the app connection between my fenix 6 pro and s26 ultra fails immediately on connection. I've tried all online advice and nothing works. It seems many others are having the same issue, the app needs patched by Garmin to make it work.

This app has gone downhill. I have used it for over a decade. Then came the pay walls inside the app. Then the stupid ads when you turn off your alarm. Then alarms started going off without any kind of notification showing up to easily open the app to turn off the alarm. Finally, this morning, my phone went off and I couldn't turn off the alarm. The app didn't even show one being active. I had to uninstall after rebooting didn't work. All sleep data from 10 years, gone.

New UI is a horrible mess. I will NOT renew my Premium membership. Have used MFP 13 years. I want to see all meals at once with my entries, NOT suggestions (that are too easily entered unintentionally), & show blank meals too so I know what I've entered & what I still need to enter. Every day with the new UI is a total headache & way more work. Hate it. Also, entries are being doubled up. And I keep getting "offline" errors when I try to use barcode scan despite being fully online!

Not sure what's up, but for the last 3 days I am not able to add any photos to my workouts. I do outside workouts and like to include photos of the scenery I see. I uninstalled and reinstalled just to make sure that it wasn't an update that needed to be done. Not sure what's up but I will give it another couple days and try again.

I hope this reaches to anyone buying product in India, please dont. Devices are faulty, customer service is bad. The issues such as battery pack not working give more stress than what the device can help you with. And there are multiple threads with the same issues all over the internet but Whoop does nothing about it

started using Connect+ because of the new nutrition feature. The food database is not wide enough, 99% of things have only one possible portion/measure "100g". The AI recognition feature fails to identify very basic stuff about 80% of the time (complete fail) from the times it "works", it's only about 50% accurate
I liked everything until I wanted an online recipe and couldn't post it to my calender. if it had that option it would be what I was ideally looking for.
The only feature on this version is track what you eat. The iPhone version has a profile, exercise tracker, and work out plan.

No adds for the 1st week, then adds start popping up at annoying times (like while entering a food it pops up and interrupts so I can't just quickly add what I need to). That said, I've used a lot of calorie tracking apps and this one is by far my favorite. The free version lets you scan food items, which is usually a paid feature on other apps. Also it breaks down nutrients in foods in a very informative and helpful way. Love the app, just hate the timing of the adds.

I've been using this App since I was deployed in June 2018. I loved everything about it; however, the whiteboard set up on the new premium option is annoying. Obviously if you don't choose the premium service then there isn't a whiteboard format, but for those of us who paid for it it's a bit disappointing. While the regular exercise format shows the movement and allows you to review it during the workout, the whiteboard set up requires you to pause and watch a video outside of the workout.

Switching to video-based workouts has rendered this app usesless. Even connected to wifi, the videos load the first 30 seconds and then fail to buffer. I've made many Nike customer service attempts. They are phone-only during working hours. Each time, they attempt to connect me to "the right department" and the phone path routes me back to general customer service. They try to connect me again in a never-ending loop. Last attempt ended in a promise to call me back, which never happened.

why is Google ruining one of my favorite apps to use for tracking my calories. it's literally a downgrade from what we previously had. I just want something simple to add my calories in so I can just keep track but for me to even put in just calories I have to go through this AI coach which I don't care for. yes they have a barcode scanner and that's a reason why I liked Fitbit but they also had quick calories for the times that I can't find what I'm looking for and I did the math myself
love this ao. perfect and does not try to do too much does what it does and does it perfectly!

love the layout of the app and how well it supports my understanding of my workouts and sleep patterns. would love to have a feature that allows me to indicate when I'm struggling (e.g. sickness, family events... etc.) so that it better understands when I under perform on a run or can adjust the training demands

Vitacost is a great company. They have excellent customer service, unique products, and the app is easy to use. I have been more than happy using Vitacost products. It's one of the better companies out there.

I thought the new automatic Device Image on my News feed might be fun, but I just took a look and I have a bunch of images of my watch with faked up "Calories burned" screens 1/ I removed the calorie screens from my watch - it would not display that 2/ There is no way for me to delete or change the device images on my feed 3/ It is a BS metric 4/ I have friends and family who I don't want to see calorie counts Let me choose the stat, or *post the actual screenshots I take during the activities*
Some good stuff but way too many ads on here. Obviously the paid version will not have that but I can't recall a free version of an app with such an annoying amount of ads.

i paid supscritption, app is great but too much, first make a button to turn off meal tips, i dont need those but they always pop up, its hell. Every time i log a meal it moves me to current date. If you wish to tweak prefrences its a mess, you want to set reminders and those reminders are not like alarm they are just a notification, you want to change macro ratio its in last aka pro tab. why is it seperated from all other srttings idk. dev i hope you make this much more convinient for future.
hot yoga classes are amazing on this app and they make it very simple to sign up. you get to discover places to go to hot yoga or pilates even to get facials!!! love this app

Latest update = unusable app The latest update wrecked the app. Data is missing from several days of entries, and the new UI is terrible. It's clunky and frustrating. To try to correct the missing data, I uninstalled and reinstalled. Now it won't even load. Been using this app for over 15 years. It used to be great, but over time, more and more functions have been taken away (like bar code scan) and the overall functionality has greatly diminished - and now it's unusable.

I've been using this app for many years, but the last few weeks it has not been recording my route properly (missing entire sections) or it freezes so that I either can't start tracking, or I can't stop it at the end - distance is stopped but not time. Thinking about unstalling.

I love this app!! It works great, keep track of my ride as I'm going I can pause it when I stop to rest and then I can save it and track my progress from day-to-day or week-to-week no matter how you use it it's a great app!! Update: 5/30/22 I feel absolutely love this app!! It works so well and is very accurate and I like how it announces when you hit mile marker & time. Then when you're through you see the map of the route that you traveled and you can save your workout. I wouldn't use any app.
Instacart upgrades impressive. Is there a way to get a MONTHLY subscription? Over 100 is frankly a lot to plunk down at once, however worth it. 🤷🏻‍♀️

I've been using vita cost for the last few years without issues. This morning I opened the app & I'm signed out, which never happened before. I try to login to the app & says something went wrong. I go to the website & I login just fine there. You need to fix your app. Thank you.

very great app. so many features. I can see my runs that I recorded on my forerunner 165. it shows every stat I need and more. I like how there are challenges for my area and for the month. overall it is a great app with even more features than I really need!

1/18/2020: Five years later and still my favorite fitness app. Only one function I would change... when logging an activity after the fact -entering distance is done easily on a keypad but entering duration is done on a clunky scroll for hours/minutes/seconds. Would be much more user friendly if duration was entered with a keypad. 5/09/2015: Works everywhere I've needed it from the Olympic Peninsula to Miami, with or without internet. Tracks my fitness and steers me straight when I get lost.

I've had this app for about 10 years and this is probably the worst update I've ever seen. Other updates that took away features I've used were mild annoyances that I learned to deal with, but I was still able to utilize the basic features of NTC that always made it so great. Now that I need an internet connection to access every workout, my ability to do workouts wherever I go is severely limited. Thanks to all the trainers and staff who made this app what it was, but this is it for me. Peace.

I love Nike but this app blows. I have stuck with it for years now and I don't know why. it constantly freezes, stops tracking distance, but still counts time, won't let you end run, and therefore keeps very inaccurate statistics. All the things that are important to a runner. I'm out. Officially used for last time. Hello Strava...

Good UI. Can scan barcodes to get all the nutrition facts logged easy, and can easily add new entries if the barcode is unrecognized. Recent update made it so ads are no longer a constant barrage (supposedly was unintended) but they are still allowing ads to put fake x-out/skip buttons which route you to an external site. Dishonest practice at best to increase clickrate, shady practice to put cookies on a user's device and scrape data off us at worst. If I stop seeing that I'll fix my rating.

УБЕРИТЕ УДАЛЕНИЕ БЛЮДА ИЗ ЗАПИСИ СВАЙПОМ! Очень часто происходит миссклик и удаляешь что записывал. Remove the delete of the records by swiping! Too often you missclick (swipe) and delete the important food you ate.

The GPS is not working properly. I biked for 2:30, you can see on the map of the app that the distance was very long, but at the end the app showed only 7 km. This was not the only time happening. At other occasions showed 0 even better. Big dislike
Great app if it was not a greedy money hungry app wanting to make you pay just to listen to sounds. Just use U-Tube instead, its free. I can see to pay to listen. to podcast but not just noise.
Excellent app. Love being able to pull recipes from the web. Everything about this app is exactly what I need for meal planning.
I recently switched to Whisk and I love it! If you are looking for another grocery list/menu planner that is a great app to look at.

Loved the app a year ago, but I don't like the new version as much. In the old version going back in the calendar and flipping the phone would show that day's heartrate graph, but now it only shows today's graph no matter what. This makes it difficult to add an activity after the fact, and, let's be honest, sometimes we all forget to log it in the moment. The last two days I can't even get the device to connect to the app, even though it's paired to the phone's BT, and I can't update the alarm.
what is going on with the app? it's used to be 5 star.

Seems to be working so far but wish I didnt have to pay to see the cooking directions in-app.... it shows ingredients but to see the cooking instructions you have to click a link to the blog post the recipe is from or pay for subscription to go into "cook mode" and see it in-app...

i would love to love this app. From what little I've been able to do it seems like it would be perfect for me. However, it crashes every time I try to use it, which obviously makes it unusable. I tried to contact the help@prepear, with no response. Very disappointed.

I love the simplicity, and that it immediately connects to my schedule, but there's a few things I wish it also could do: - Id like to personalize my home screen, so if the "favorites" list on the main page could expand and I could hide more of the "explore" options since I'm only using the app for 2-3 gyms - an option to review my history/schedule in a monthly format rather than just in a list, it's easier to grasp my patterns that way - Link options to more apps/fitness tracker
I love this app, because my recipes are all in one place. I use to just save them to my home screen, but after I lost them all with a reset of my phone I found this app, thank-you Prepare.

The absolute best food tracker out there...period. Plus so much more! There are so many features with this app that make it easy to use. I'm sorry I didn't find this app sooner. I've tried a few other trackers but if it becomes too tedious to use, I'm out. Chronometer makes it very easy. The nutrition, macronutrient details and other information is extensive and quite helpful. I've done my research into Keto and can tell you, this team know what they're doing. I could go on and on. Awesome!

I particularly love the sleep stories, however, as I listen to one every night I find I have to almost always play one I've heard many times before. I would love it if you were to present us with new stories more frequently. I'm becoming very bored with all the mindfulness and meditation stuff that there is so much of.
Very basic recipe app with clunky design. No ability to customize including changing the font size which is far to be small to easily read when standing in a kitchen with the phone on the counter.

It's pretty good once you get used to it. Not everything is intuitive though. Best to go to search Google or YouTube to learn the features. Would be good to be able to see list of who has given you ride-ons whilst you're still riding rather than at the end.
Very slow, plus when I wanna check my order history it's says that I've got nothing ordered. Something is wrong with this app, to bad...

Best app out there for fitness and general wellbeing. I've tried Apple, Samsung, Polar, Casio and Tag Heuer, Garmin Connect is miles ahead. Plenty of good data. Configurable dashboard. Hassle free watch syncing. Can't recommend it highly enough.

I love zwift and the companion app but currently i can't use the app on multiple devises because the update that came out on the 6th can't push to most devices. I would have thought that zwift would have fixed the problem by now but clearly not. I have been using zwift without a companion for months now. Please can zwift fix the problem or come back with a reason why the update won't work that would be great.
Fresh in the Garmin ecosystem after Google ruined Fitbit and delighted with this app. Does everything I need it to simply and with a minimum of fuss, and the food logging is top notch.

Great companion app to Zwift. One thing which could be better is how to give people around you a "ride on". Too many clicks are needed (especially when you're running). What about putting a "ride on" button next to each name in the list or allow swiping over the name. Far easier to do

The log in problem has been fixed. I like the app and how it can track stuff even if you're not using the premium version. The only actual critique I can give is if only there was a way to implement measurements in grams for ALL food types. I like the different measurement options but I feel like to be precise and have some sort of credibility, there should always be an option to use grams (or ounces) as a measurement.

Update after 3 weeks use. The strength activity is basically broken. Thinks I'm doing tricep extensions while squatting. It doesn't have manual entry for missing exercises (there is a ton of basic ones missing from the list ie, Decline bench press) Rep counter doesn't allow for manual entry on watch when the auto Rep counter is off. This app could Be SO good. But I'll stick to notepad since this is a buggy mess.

I do a lot of walking at work but because I'm in the same building all day Pokémon GO's distance walked stats don't do much and I've relied on Adventure Sync to hatch eggs via Fit stats.

They dumbed the app down and now it sucks. Now it relies on heart points which are based solely on time. Do if I walk farther but faster I will accumulate fewer heart points than if I walk a shorter distance but walk slower and take a longer time to do so. They've also taken away stats for distance. So if I want to know the total distance I've walked in a day, week, or month it's simply not there. All in all just a terrible app now and I'm looking for another one.

I'm using the app to connect my trainer and 8 times on 10 I have pairing issues, lost of connection during the activities, even with my cardio. I gave up and bought a Bluetooth dongle for my pc, stop using the app for the connection. Same issues on android and IOS both.
Crashing issue is fixed. Really like the easy access & have with me to get groceries or check what I need to pack for tomorrow. Great tool for weight

I use the free app and it works great for me. I don't care about sharing my rides on social media. App talks to me during ride; tells me total distance and average mph so far. I save my ride and app will sum total distance for the week, the month, and total ever. A lot of stats for free! I have only noticed an ad one time in six weeks of using app.

was a great app but then years of recipes I saved and used regularly got deleted and my account had nothing in it. I emailed customer service several times with no response. I have been looking for an app that does it all from save recipes, add ingredients to my grocery list, and plan my meals for a long, LONG time. So glad I found Prepear! This makes life so much easier.

WHY does this DUCK have "garmin pay" when NOWHERE IN THE WORLD it does work !!! (tested in note than 100 countries across the globe and over 30 different cards and banks and NOT A SINGLE ONE "supports" this ... for over 5 years!!!

Can't rearrange metrics, attempting to removes them, forcing you to add them back. they then display in the order you added them only.
