This has been a great app to help me track what I'm eating and set goals for my nutrition! It helps you set a goal weight and determine the amount of calories I need. It's easy to use and I can see where I'm at on my goals daily with protein, fiber, etc. It also is easy to scan product bar codes so I dont always have to type in what I'm looking for.

Pretty decent app. I wish I could go full screen since swiping left or right changes view anyways. I'm a big map guy and not being able to maximize it is a bit frustrating. Also being able to hide my ride metrics since they are already displayed on my regular zwift app. Full screen map and ability to hide metrics please!!!
Buying supplements, protein powder and other in vitacost for more than 4 years and never disappointed. Customer care is awesome and really helpful. App is wonderful and most features available.

It's a good way of keeping track of when and what I'm eat. It has made me rethink my eating habits. I like that it has a good amount of knowledge about food from various shops. The only thing that's a bit difficult is when I've cooked a trial recipes or something which isn't on the list as you can't get a proper calorie count. I have tried to break it down by the ingredients I've used which I think can be very inaccurate. But I'm still enjoying the journey.

Zwift is a great product, with a good ethos around continuous improvement to meet customer needs. But this hasn't yet reached the companion app, which needs better functionality to remotely control and review settings such as FTP, route selection, garage, badges/achievements.

Generally good, some room for improvement and added functionality around the workout list, it would be nice to be able to favourite workouts so that they don't disappear from your list once they're completed. Occasional issues with the Companion app not recognising a workout in progress on my apple tv.

Edit: 05/05/24 I have been using this app for a while now. It has some session related issues. It doesn't log me out automatically when. I dont use it for a couple days. I have been using this app everyday for about 2 weeks. I have my first bicycle since I was a kid, literally. Anyway my friend recommended this to help track my new routine. Thus far I am only using the free portions and it is absolutely flawless.

I don't have a fit bit or anything like it, because map my ride does everything I want, I use it for walking, hiking, and biking. I just had a hip replacement, and I plan to use it to track my progress. It is very flexible while staying unobtrusive, and it has all of the tracking I want and is quite accurate.

Worked great and easy to use. However, now when I tried to connect it says I need to update. When I click update it takes me to the app open screen which when pressed takes me back to the screen asking me to update. This is also happening on my ipad. What's up Zwift.

Having the same problem as everyone else cant log on. Changed password and still cannot log on. I'll have to use another supplement store to order stuff today since I need to make an order now. I cant sit around for days waiting on the app to be fixed so have to order from elsewhere

while it sometimes works well, its still broken. notifications wont show on my venu 3s no matter what. going through all troubleshooting steps, factory resets, permissions, settings, customer support, still nothing. the notifications will work for a day, and then stop working for several months. same issue on Galaxy S21 FE and on S24 Ultra.

I don't understand the reason you'd have mapping and pace stats but no way of applying that to adjusting calories burned. I absolutely love Vitacost, but the app needs some major work. When you click on a promo and start scrolling 1. there are no pictures 2. The app is good to price compare items with vitacost vs a store but that's really about it. App details says bugs were fixed February 8th, but they weren't.

Fantastic app for anyone wants to lift/build strength. The workouts are really simple and the app does the calculations for you. Lifting heavy can be scary because of the risk of injury, but the form videos/descriptions are comprehensive yet easy to understand. Definitely one of the best strength building apps out there, especially after the V.3 update. No ads I believe, but I paid for the upgraded version ($20/yr). The warmup calculator is a huge help when you start lifting heavier imo.

Best free app I've ever used! I love being able to see and track micronutrients in addition to the macros that most other apps let you do without the subscription. The food library is HUGE and you can create recipes and meals with the free version. Once in a while I see an ad if I've had the app open for a significant time, but that's totally fine. I've used LifeSum, MyFitnessPal, Noom, and several others but this is my far my favorite. Couldn't be happier!

Not sure any thought has been given in designing charts. For example, considering 80/120 is considered normal blood pressure, I would expect the horizontal marker lines to be at every tens (e.g., 60, 70, 80, 90, 100, 110, 120, 130, 140, 150, 160) and not at some random (e.g. 75, 105, 135). A thoughtfully designed graph can help users whether they are in healthy range overtime (and if not, how far they are).

As someone who just wanted to dip their toes before spending money, I am really happy with the variety of features Cronometer lets you use for free. I will likely buy a subscription soon but you can definitely get by without spending money. Ads aren't very common either.

A great app when working out and from what I've read, the program is great too. I'd like the app to be able to include custom exercises though not just the pre-set ones. I'd like to be able to add 5x5 lateral raises at the end and be able to use the timer for them as well as the weight increments. unfortunately you can't

Like many others here, I've used this app for years and loved it. The recent update has ruined it completely, almost unusable. Doesn't save completed workouts in my activity, the demonstration videos don't load... Agh. Bummer. I guess it was good while it lasted I'll have to find something else.

This is the new fitbit app. Is it the same? No! The main page looks weirdly organized and you can't properly track your food anymore. The old app allowed you to add specialized food with the correct nutrition value, not this app though. I am due to get a new fitbit soon, but seeing how this app is run, I have to look into other options. If you are considering fitbit, look elsewhere.
I'm changing my previous review, as the sign in problem was promptly fixed. Thanks so much Prepear! So happy with this app!

Compared to Strava and Wahoo it starts better, that is, just one touch and it starts. It is more reliable than Strava that stops after 5 minutes due to Oppo battery management. The cycling metrics aren't quite up to the level of the other two but shows average speed over 1km segments, elevation, speed and workout summaries, time, distance, average speed, elevation gain and calories. The app is slowly getting better with incremental app features but for a company as big as Garmin they need to put more focus on the software side, they are getting demolished by other cardio activity apps because of user interface, and most importantly as a paying garmin connect+ customer, make a seperate Garmin strength app the same way you have the Garmin golf app. the current live activity feature is so bad compared to what else is available. Used this app for years to track my weight and food intake, I came back to it again recently to start being more serious about tracking my food again and they have taken away the scan bar code on the free version and the yearly subscription is a rip off just so I can scan bar codes. I'm going over to the Crono app instead as they have scan and are much cheaper. Sad that these apps are just turning into money grabbers all of a sudden when we are in a cost of living crisis.

I switched to Cronometer after seeing a recommendation for it in a reddit comment. I was getting really sick of MyFitnessPal being painfully slow so I decided to give it a shot. Cronometer blows all other nutrition trackers out of the water. It's super fast, snappy, has tons of customization and granular detail to your nutrition. Most importantly, the food databases they draw from are phenomenal, and the user submitted foods are curated by staff for accuracy. Give it a try, you won't regret it

Generally, the best app I know for tracking running or cycling (never used it for anything else). Good tracking, simple UK, easy to use and still gives good insights on the workout. On the negative side, I couldn't use it properly without unlocking my phone, and lately it stopped working all together on Xiaomi mi a2 lite (changed to Samsung galaxy and it works again). Could be better if it could interact with cheap fitness bands, or have *some* workout plans on the free version.
Good-ish calorie tracking app with support for the Fitbit app through Health Link. First week is okay but then ads every 7 minutes.

I have used the other popular app, but Cronometer far exceeds the rest! The interface is great; I rarely have any issues. I love the colors, the simplicity, and how much I can modify my food categories. The food data they have is extensive as well. Tracking can be such a headache, but this app keeps me accountable! Now, I have to convince my nutritionist to download so she can have access to my account! Adding a "current day" button instead of scrolling forward through each day would be nice.
App crashes when I'm looking at my grocery list.
Yes u r the part of this.. Its gud n always do your best..

Fat secret has amazingly comprehensive database of foods with nuutrients & calories. It includes many brand name foods, as well as generic foods. In many cases, you can find the portion size you find most convenient, whether that is ounces, grams, milliliters, each, serving, piece, etc. This makes it easy to make entries in your food journal.
It's been great till this year. And now has a bug in the sleep portion of the software. It won't accumulate or download the correct sleep data. And will not allow me to correct sleep data.

Usually a great app with decent coupons and very fast shipping. Sometimes there are errors in the app and calling customer service has proven to be a 'meh' experience for me. I like the company so far and would recommend them for most needs.

The most versatile and battle-tested Fitness app around. Meaningful information given, but I wish the graphs were made in such a way as to entice you to never take your eyes off them--like Fitbit's for example. Endomondo is my "go to" Exercise app, MyFitnessPal is my diet app, and Fitbit is my everything else. When you get all three apps to talk to each other, it's a beautiful thing to behold 😁

As I get older, I realize how important lifting is to maintaining my mobility and flexibility. Stronglifts was a great motivator for me by helping me make incremental progress toward my goals. After some time away for sickness and injury, I came back to the app to find that I now have to subscribe even to get to the data I have already in my account, much less use the app. Thanks but no thanks. I'll find another way.

Very disappointing to be paying for a service that does not do what is promised.

I love the fact the can not only monitor my body but can see results from my healthy suggestions from the WHOOP (Short for DUB, and my health coach the AI both together C-Dub) have assisted me in losing 10lbs in the very short time we've been together. LOVE IT!!!

Very good tracking for the most part. I would have given it 5 stars but the first 400 meters of my jogging today weren't noticed by the app. I checked if my location was on and it was. But this hasn't happened before so I'm nit complaining a lot. Keep up the good work.

It appears that the latest update has broke bluetooth connection for me. Prior to this update I could pair my trainer and HRM through the companion app. Now Zwift cannot find any devices when connected to the app.
Works well with cycling. It integrates my chest monitor automatically into ride data. Best of all it's not annoying like some other fitness apps
Confusing at checkout ,,, looks like only Paypal is accepted. Took me 3 times to figure out and had to come back twice before accepted. Time wasting!!

Very effective. love this app. is it possible to create a workout program for total body fitness using kettlebell and basic equipment. concentrating on strength, cardio, power. earlier it had difficulty level option please bring back that option. create more programs. thank you.

The routes world schedule always in delay, I can't know which route is open in which date. Second thing is that the uncompleted routes counter doesn't count well, for example I have 8 routes to finish in watopia but I see in the app 43/49 ... Which means I have 6 to complete. Third, the routes details of distance and height are wrong and doesn't fit to the Zwift app details.

They have done it again! This latest update has made the app more useless. You now have to go through a maze to see your full day, and you don't even see calorie totals by meal anymore. A key part of managing your health (what this app USED to be about) is consistency; making wholesale changes that disrupt how people do that is absurd. The app has taken another step towards garbage. After nearly 10 years, I will be looking for a less confusing app to track my calories.

Absolutely excellent. First and foremost, excepting user error, the alarm ALWAYS goes off when it should. I have yet to have an issue other than where I forgot to set an alarm back, and with it sending that optional bedtime notification, that is rare. I'd be more worried about an issue with an analog alarm. After the all important alarm, the app is packed with features. Excellent sleep tracking and integration with all kinds of wearables. The paid version of this app really was a steal.

No ability to simply add calorie intake without jumping through a million hoops adding every single ingredient and amount used. I'm not doing this EVERY TIME I make a meal, then attempting to figure out how to calculate from this what amount of each ingredient I had when I make a MEAL FOR A FAMILY. Not to mention THEY'VE REMOVED WORK AROUNDS!

Miraculous! I actually get out of bed. This alarm system is stimulating enough that it gives my rational-self time to make a stand against the idea that staying in bed is the best thing for me to do. The "captcha" options are abundant, including math, zombie walk, bar code scanning, and so many more - with different difficulty levels! There's not enough room to brag, and I'm on the FREE version! A big thank you to the developer. If I don't become tolerant, I'll feel guilty if I don't buy it.

Fitbit app good, Google Health dropped best features just before I received my preordered Air. No more dynamic caloric deficit (just flat caloric intake range like every other free app), no more custom meals (now you have to enter each item of a complex meal, so much for meal prepping because they do not always stay in the recent list), and no way to delete or rename synced workouts. The dynamic caloric deficit was the ONLY REASON I decided to go back to a Fitbit.

Easy to see and book with; reminders and schedule piece also reall easy to use! Really wish we could see how many other people/bookings are signed up for a class - so could choose diff class times where maybe there are 3 instead of 11 people, but just see available(room)/waitlist(full)/cancelled.

​I have been using YAZIO for a long time, but a few things are becoming very annoying: ​Animations: There is no way to disable them. Had hoped when I downloaded this app again was to use the "plan" tool to combine the running app and the workout app like I did a couple of years ago. This app was forced upon me once Google decided they no longer wanted to support the Fitbit app. This app is super busy compared to the Fitbit app, and information is hard to find and read. Finding what you want requires clicking through several pages. Information is also much harder to compare to previous days, making it difficult to monitor your progress. Quit selling out to Google. The design is a mess: half old, half new. It’s a lazy hack job. What’s up with the May 2026 update? Nothing loads, I can’t log data! Why does it need internet 24/7 just to log food? Support is non-existent - nowhere to report bugs. And let us disable that mascot and streak animations! They waste my time. I DON’T WANT TO WATCH THIS, I JUST WANT TO LOG DATA AND GET OUT! Build the app for users, not for show. You did great work before, but right now it’s a total disaster.

it's good but needs a little work. The ux is a little clunky and setting up connection to sneakers or other heart monitors is a little hard to do or if not supported. I have a Fitbit Blaze and it's not supported I have to use fitbit's app and sync it with this app but it's not that well-integrated. it would be better if the heart monitor was just supported. and for some reason my connection to my Under Armour shoes keeps dropping.
Feature wise it is good, but lags so much. Most of the time the app is unusable and has to be killed and restarted many times to make it work.

Complete garbage. The option to change measurement units is either very well hidden or doesn't exist at all. That's as far as I got. Update: after some messing around got the app set up, along with data sharing permissions. Coach Bennett is the best 😊 always hated running, but really love his guided runs and now I just don't like running 😄 EDIT Jun2025: However, lately the app has a real problem with tracking my route (like 300 m missing etc.) and messing up the pace because of it. GPS on my phone cannot be the problem, because I also have another app for wearable and the route is always there correctly. Hope someone fixes it, it makes comparing runs impossible.

I like this better than other popular calorie and macro trackers. I paid for the gold version on discount and it is awesomene. It's very user friendly and has lots of options to help you on your fitness journey goals.

This is without a doubt the best free running app on the market. It'll guide you toward your goals in a way that feels personal, and will motivate you to get across the finish line without all the pressure. I'm a relatively new runner, and this app has been tremendous in not only helping me navigate how I should be training as a new runner, but it also focuses on training safely for longevity, which I really appreciate.
Good information, easy to use
great app for riding my e bike. set it and forget it until your done riding!

Seamless integration between recipes, shopping lists, and then ordering those shopping lists from Walmart! I love this app! From finding recipes I like to getting the stuff I need to cook it. Very easy to use, including in different countries, such as Japan.

Pros: It lets me customize what nutrition info I care most and easily viewed at many levels. I like very much the recipe entry, as I almost eat exclusively home cooked food. I used to love this app, but I can no longer find free stories to help me sleep. Went thru updating all recommened apps and Note 9 is current. Very frustrating, will need to another app.

So far, so good. Battery drain is a concern but it was to be expected. Overall happy. Please note if you want to use another activity app (like Squad Easy), you may need to enable sharing data in Health Connect app.

So far, an incredible app! It isn't pushy about things, it doesn't request that I do anything dangerous, and actually seems invested in helping people do what they want with it. One flaw is that it is not incredibly effective at tracking water intake, but to my understanding that is a new feature. It tracjed the water from the onion I had with breakfast, but not the Sprite Zero I drank through the day. so that part definitely needs some fine tuning.

5/2023: Adding the new world has been great and the additional levels (yet I'm only on 30) but I specifically had a shed in my backyard built for working out and Zwift. I sort of stumbled across this app because of its alarm features. I've been using it for many years now to track my sleeping and I don't think I'll ever stop!

I'm frustrated with how it calculates sleep. If you're someone who stays in bed for any length of time after you wake up, the accuracy is incredibly bad. I have to go in and manually fix it literally 5 or 6 days per week. Very frustrating, especially since sleep tracking is the primary reason I use it (improving my sleep helps me manage my autoimmune disease better). If it calculates that you got more sleep than you actually did, none of your stats are reliable. Unreliable = expensive waste.

No one wants constant popup tips, and the automatic opening of meals is honestly frustrating.I often log food, then realize it went into Snacks instead of Lunch and have to redo everything.Just let users choose the meal category first. After getting past all of the annoying questions, the app is ok. I want to like this app, but it's annoying when I'm manually entering a run or walk, knowing how long and how far I went.... but when I add my distance first, then my time, the app decides to recalculate my distance based on whatever. Likewise when I enter time first then distance, time is recalculated. If I'm entering data, don't recalculate for me please. Otherwise, it tracks my run well (although sometimes I don't know if the app or my GPS signal is week).

WAS great. Some peon decided that an update meant going backwards in functionality... in that, plans are gone, replaced with collections and "options"... you know those links on Pinterest that say, "here's links to videos come back click on it and here's optional stuff to do after"... rather than reminding you and all that. I just don't get why you'd complicate things. not overly complicated but this is basically no better than a YouTube channel at this point.
unable to login

This app used to be the best app for weight loss, gain, just tracking your nutrition goals in general for anyone. Decided to go to calAI. Once my year sub ended, came back to MFP, and it's even worse. Can't even track macros correctly. Now the new layout is awful. This app fell apart. It was perfect. No more

App consistently logs me out and never able to log back in with ease. This time, I can't get back in for anything keeps asking my me to log in, I do. Info is blank and it says to log in if I have an account, but I just did. Need help, can't sign up for a class if I can't log in!

For some reason this app didn't work for me. To the developers, I have emailed screenshots of the error message I get.
Awesome! Love the bar code scanner. Just wish I could change date format. USA format confusing

This is a consistently frustrating app. Every update brings new problems (I had uninstalled the app for quite a while and only recently installed it again). The current version logs you out constantly.It's not worth the hassle- I'll just use the website. Vitacost itself is a great store with great products - I wonder if having a functional app might drive more business?
Used to be a great app that I used 2 or 3 times a week. Unfortunately it now crashes regularly and is unusable. Very disappointed.

I paid to have lifetime power pack years ago. Opened the app recently to find those same services behind a subscription service. Frustrating. Luckily similar apps on the store do what the OG Stronglifts used to do. I must uninstall this betrayal.

Google's takeover of Fitbit is an abject failure. It's full of AI slop and the UI is now so confusing and is nothing similar to what it was before. It also doesn't work very well. I can't change my active hours for example. Logging food is a mess now too. Just an awful app experience now and if it doesn't change to what it was before, I'll be cancelling. Btw Google, "Fitbit" was a superior name and was easy to say in all the world's languages. You're supposed to be a GLOBAL company.

I've used Myfitness Pal for years. It was always so easy to use. This latest update is horrible. It feels like using a complete new app. You can't see your whole day of meals without clicking on each individual meal. I'm seriously considering switching entirely to a different app. I understand the need to update, but why not keep some of the same features that users like and are used to.

This app is pretty good for logging what you eat through out the day and you can log your weight. You can see custom graphs of the process you've made. I wish there was a way to log exercise and calories burned. UI could use an update

90% of the time, this app is completely useless. It gets out if sync with the main game and/or freezes completely every few minutes. So the only thing it would potentially do to improve the experience does not work. The companion app actively makes your experience in zwift worse when using it. Please fix this!
I only used it 1 time but it worked great for the one time I used it check back after I got 10 days behind me ...

No more casting to Chromecast I am so disappointed - I have used the Nike Training Club app on and off over the years. I love the workouts available, and the ability to cast to the tv has always been a big drawcard. I just recommended the app to my son who was looking for ways to exercise more without forking out for a gym membership. I figured I'd take my own advice and get in the wagon again too, only to find the cast icon has disappeared and no matter what I try I can't cast to the TV anymore

This app could be so much better. Just completed my first run and it did not save the Run. In fact in order to get out of the app I had to discard the run there was no option to save. I used the coaching feature expecting Milestones to be announced specifically the halfway point. But that was not announced. So my outing took longer than expected. Fortunately today I had the extra time but some days I do not, so I need that halfway point so I know to turn and head back. I do not run on treadmills

For the developers of this application there was a small issue with your sign up link. The links location was under the login section, but the link was half exposed with the bottom navigation bar within the app. I am a new customer and was attempting to sign up through your application. However, due to the above issue I was not able to sign up as the link would not work as it was not able to be clicked. Hope this feed back aids in the further development of your app.

Was my go-to app for tracking workouts. A few updates ago, they set it up so that after you were done with the workout, it can take almost an hour before you can actually save the workout. If you forget to actually come back to the app an hour later and hit save, then you cannot start a new workout until you wait the hour before you can hit save, or you have to discard your workout and lose everything. Very frustrating that you can't easily save a workout without keeping the app open for hours!

Good but not quite 100%. Very versatile and well rounded although I've found setting parameters a little clunky and buggy. I'd really like to be able to change rest periods and also when I set my increments, I'd like too see that the top down increments fall within parameters. I.e my machines are 5kg, dumbbells are 2kg increments but it still calculates 0.5s etc All around though very good and I'll continue using it.

I like most of the features of the free app. I wish the exercise search would allow you to detail your form of activity a little more personalized. Overall, it's the best free app that I've tried using to help with weight loss

I love it as it counts NET CARBS..It was a bit of learning to get it set to where I wanted it. but I did figure it out to be close. I'm still learning, but it's easy to add my foods. plus it has a barcode reader...which yes no is awesome. AND I was able to add new foods that were not listed, simply by following the directions to take a photo of the front and back of the package, .

A rarity amongst apps. Most health apps are charging subscriptions, while this one does have the option, you can still get all of the benefits you need. FOR ONCE ITS HEALTH ABOVE PROFITS! It integrates with Google fit very easily. Set up was easy without the heavy bragging of some of the other paid for apps. I recommend this for people who can't afford to do subscriptions and want to self improve. I recommend this to those who want to pay for the enhanced services because this app is wonderful

I am changing my 4-star rating to 1-star. I paid for the full version many years ago when the app was still new. And now, I no longer have access since they want to move to a subscription model. This is super disrespectful to their long-time customers and shows how little they care about their users compared to making money. Very disappointed and I do NOT recommend them.

used to love this app, but ever since the new updated look, it doesn't sync properly with my watch. if U don't check constantly then your info may not save at all. Sometimes it takes powering off my watch just to get the two to speak to each other. What shows in my watch, doesn't show in the app. I've lost PB info thinking it was being stored. Was never an issue before.

I like how it stores my schedule so I can see what my plans are easily, but booking classes lags. I'm afraid I've paid for a class twice sometimes. Also, the search feature is terrible. Unless you get the name exactly right, it's a wild goose chase, and I mean the difference between '&' and 'and'. Even with a name typed correctly, I get results further away in location then the actual venue I'm trying to find.

Updated a couple days ago and now the app doesn't track my distance! It tracks time and calories, but not the route or distance. I saw so many others say the same thing in their review. I will uninstall and find a new app if this continues. And don't tell me to submit a ticket. I see how well that has worked for others too!

Don't like that they changed the weight chart to only a year now and you can't see previous years for comparison. It is also very hard to find food items and to change portions sizes. I do not like the new menstruation feature it can be a bit confusing. I feel it is trying too hard to be a fitness coach and not a personal fitness tracker. I would prefer the old app over this one.

Why can't I report existing food for edit or block it so it does not confuse me in the future? For example, someone has put White Rice food with a gram measurement but for the COOKED rice. How does it make sense to have cooked ingredient value by default? How does one measure cooked rice in prepared meal? Deducting one more star now: food I've used before is no longer in search results. Why is it so difficult to add hake fish to my diary? Why does food never default to grams?

I love the add your own recipe section in this app. You enter the ingredients, and it calculates the weight of everything so that you don't have to figure it out. (You can add cooked weight later if you want, also.) Then, later, all you have to do is put your food on your plate on the scale and measure the weight of your food, and it automatically calculates the macros for you for that serving. ❤️ You can now make that casserole or meatloaf without stressing!

Have never used the whoop 3.0 but was turned on to whoop from a family friend. Awesome concept and for most of the time awesome execution. However I have to agree with most comments who have posted within the last year. On Android, the whoop is a buggy messy. The biggest issue I have is with bluetooth connectivity, which can often get into endless reconnects and the error handler in this area of the app probably needs to be significantly refactored. We need to have these bugs prioritized.

I bought the premium plan to try it out. I scanned a 170g yogurt that already has all the nutrition details on the package, but when I use the AI camera, the values it shows are way higher than the label. I tried again and got the same result. I tried other foods too and they're all wrong. Is this a new way to scam people?" I'd like to request a refund,

Unreliable very often sluggish, its fine when working, edit another excercise missed because it could not got GPS fix I've stood on the bloody beach ffs!
