4/25/26 I can no longer use the app to track my foods, because after the latest update, it has a non-user friendly interface, and no longer displays the data necessary to plan my meals for the day. All I can think, is that people who don't use the app, and have never seen the prior app's layout created something out of fever dream, without consulting any of the paying users.

The app is very useful and much more accurate when tracking foods compared to others I've tried. While I don't mind the banner ads, the full screen video ads that pop up when hitting the "log food" or similar buttons that can't be backed out of and essentially lock the app until you fully watch it or close the app from the carousel are absolutely heinous. It doesn't make me want to pay for premium, it makes me want to stop using the app entirely.

App stopped working after android update in February 2020. Finally they fixed app so it would open but it removed all of my settings. Since then the gps will not track and the app is worthless I have lost 4 work outs to the gps not tracking. The app does not work when screen is locked. When you unlock the screen it will try and update your position but you lose all statistical information. I used the app for 5 years with no problems now it is complete garbage.

It would be Negative 5 if I could . . . who had the brilliant idea to cover the ENTIRE Map with our chosen Stats? I LIKED having everything visible as I Rode . . . it isn't safe to ride this way anymore! I'm shopping around for an App that works the way this one USED to . . . Update 11-11-17: Much improved recently . . . Meant to increase Stars a long time ago.

I've been a premium member for many years and love the app, but the only thing I dont like is that I can no longer edit the meal times. I used to plan and enter my meals into Myfitnesspal the day before so I know exactly what and how much to eat each meal to meet my macros, and then I would edit those meal times to "current" as I ate them. I cant do that anymore.

For five plus years, I used this app religiously. In the last few months, it has become worthless. It is inconsistent at best when trying to track a run. In the quick start, I have to pick a goal (distance, speed, time) when I used to be able to just run and let it track my run. I'm super disappointed with the changes you made that ruined this app. Please fix this! Galaxy S9

Amazing. Detailed metrics and a simple interface make this my favorite nutrition app by far. I'm learning a lot about the foods I put in my body, beyond their macronutrient profile, so that I can better understand how to make healthy choices. The food suggestion feature is genius as well, when you don't have a great idea of how to meet the remaining nutrient goals for the day.

Have the paid version and a new Galaxy Watch 5, the app doesn't seem to want to track SpO2 while sleeping, or even very accurately track my sleep with my watch. This was one of the major reasons to get a watch with all these fancy sensors. Is to track my health and sleep is part of that, as well as my overnight O2 levels as i need to keep an eye on that for my own piece of mind and medical records. (I know this isn't a professional medical device but it is still nice to have a base line.)

The alarm sound is nice (starts out quiet and gets louder over time), and the app has a good number of features. It's a bit easy to accidentally disable the alarm (manually delaying the alarm when you're within the early wake-up threshold seems to cancels the alarm, but there are other ways to delay your wake-up time if you wake up too early or need to rest for a bit longer).  EDIT: The app support team contacted me and responded to the complaint I had - all is good (sorry for the scare).
total garbage. made impossible by some idiots. I tried again on 5-4-26 with same results. Someone went out of their way to make this app user unfriendly.

It's a good workout app, with lots of different workouts. I do wish they had more beginner-friendly workouts, however. Additionally, there's a glitch where, if a phone notification goes off when the app lady is speaking, it erroneously permanently turns down the volume. Also, they really need to add native Chromecast support!
Lacks everything I liked about the app on iPhone. No way to change goals or track exercise. A little user unfriendly.
I've been using this app for about a week. So far it's really easy to use. Super easy to total my food and exercise for the day.
love it ! integrates with everything. great sleep tracking and support. you can see the devs want to do more as they expand their product line to include more apps. great work team !

I have been so incredibly happy with Zwift to the point where I am literally telling it up to all my friends. My life is busy and I rarely get a chance for real life rides. But Zwift is a great stand-in and training tool. And when i do get the chance to get a ride in outside I'm literally dropping PRs like a pigeon drops hot turds. The companion app makes it much easier to manage your ride and skip the use of a keyboard. Two thumbs up.

I used to use this app around a year or two ago, and it was great! So,i thought I'm getting back into fitness, I'll get it again! It doesn't load, just sits at the opening screen saying "preparing your data" and has gone unbelievably slow. Maybe I had used a different one before, but this just simply doesn't work for me. (and this is after I paid for it!)

Haven't had any issues. Works like a remote for the zwift program running on my pc. Use it for chat, turns on route, ride ons, screenshots, +/- on the ftp difficulty. Pretty useful. Wish you could see the entire training plan, not just individual workouts.
Super product! Easy to use & import recipes from the web. Love the grocery list as well.
Great way to track your walks, runs, jogs, bike rides

I love that the app has a shopping list automatically created by recipes and makes meal planning so much easier. It is also easy to add new recipes and adjust the serving size for recipe ingredients. The one major thing I do not love is that my boyfriend and I cannot have individual accounts and share the same meal plan for the week. He can view my meal plan but not add or edit it. This app is not meant if you are co-planning meals with someone.

The app is great. Good design, variety has been added to exercises - Yoga, boxing. The variety is good in keeping one motivated. Plus you can still do the classic workouts. Sign-in! Added: During repetition based workouts, when there is a timed exercise, the timer doesn't allow for switching and getting ready. Some 5 seconds would do. Fix it.
Nothing on the site is available in 'free' status. before paying such a high subscription, would have been nice to see something to hint to its potential value.
App is not nearly as good as the website, plus I got an email that said I would get 20% off if I downloaded the app, but not so! So that was disappointing as well.

I love how easily you can find a workout in the search menu. The categories are useful too! There are workouts for modifications, stages in pregnancy, and full programs! Now I wish I can display this on my tv, like an app on your laptop. You can cast on Google devices! Some tips: Reintroduce download features so you can workout anywhere. The ability to restart a workout without marking it as completed. I went into a wrong one and exited and marked as I completed it when i didn't.
A great app until they charged to use it even for the most basic program. Can't even see my records without paying them. Don't download

I have used it for years, and it's still the best sleep application for me, but I can't fix the automatic start of sleep tracking. It works spontaneously at any mode. Some nights it starts automatically. Or not. It could be caused by battery modes, but the morning alarm works properly every day

I haven't lifted in over a decade. This is a great way for me to get back to exercising. Easy to record workouts, easy to adjust weights on exercises that aren't progressing as fast as the default in the app. Timer is helpful in completing workouts in a timely manner, while not rushing. A few weeks in, and I feel stronger and am making gains.

Whoop is the best fitness band I have seen. I personally love that the band does not have a screen. It doesn't make me want to constantly look at my wrist for odd reasons, it's just there and doing its job. The sleep tracking is unbelievable, the stages of sleep and the things that effect my sleep are easily understood and it has drastically changed my habits. My favorite part is that it DOESN'T CARE ABOUT STEPS. it tracks your fitness in strain levels monitored by your heart rate. Amazing
Launches by itself each morning. My wife experiences the same thing. Yesterday it launched by itself while i was watching YouTube. It's very strange. This issue alone is cause for one star.

Lots of great metrics to follow! It took me some time to get used to the activities mode, learning what order to tap for reps and sets, etc. Super handy for keeping track of my workouts, weights I used, sets, reps and duration. Great app, great product.

The app is terrible! I like Vitacost, but hate the app. Update 04/2022 The app is 100% unusable!!! I just lost my entire shopping cart & I was logged in! Nothing more annoying than searching all the things you want, finally getting ready to place the order and nothing is in the cart! 😫

Edit: After being a long time user (years), I've given up. I had too many runs on beautiful days, in open areas, where my run wasn't tracked or looked like a scribble, and the mileage was way off. --- I've been using this app for years and it works for me. I appreciate the live tracking so my honey can have a clue where I am. I rarely use the premium features, but like no ads and the few extras I do use.

Lately App doesn't work at all. Doesn't sync with Garmin; doesn't want to open challenge overview, etc. On top of that, latest update was released on March 2019. I used to use Strava in parallel for about a year, but now I have decided to switch completely to Strava, because it is made very nicely and syncs within seconds after my activity.

The UI is overwhelming and there are too many options. for people new to meditation, it feels like drinking out of a firehose. I was gifted a free month of the premium version, and I wont be renewing. I shouldn’t feel decision fatigue when I'm trying to unwind. If someone hadn't told me what she listens to as a starting point, I would have just let the subscription run out.

I have really been enjoying Cronometer! It makes my calorie counting so much easier. Their database of foods is robust and adding food they don't have registered is made quick and simple with image recognition. Their widgets are simple and cute and their support team is quick to respond to issues. Overall my favorite app for calorie counting so far!
Helpful insights but I had an issue after I gifted my old whoop which damaged the brand for me as they didn't resolve it

I absolutely love this app. I had tried other apps, but this one, by far, supercedes them all. You get to track your macros, nutrients, your calorie counting, exercise tracking, etc. This app really has it all. I have actually had my friends and family switch to this app. It has made me rethink my food choices and brought me back on track to switching to a sensible and healthier diet. For now, I am on the free version, but down the road, I will definitely go premium for the added benefits.

Great device to have if you want encouragement to exercise and see progress. However the most recent changes to it have made my phone constantly disconnect from the strap and it claims it's because my phone isn't new enough to support changes to the bluetooth. Maybe set it to where you can revert to the old bluetooth firmware if your phone is older so I can still use this product without having to buy a new phone. I mean I already pay a decent amount each month for this, so this shouldn't happen

My app stopped working from last 2 days. I got this message "Fit is having trouble with Google Play Services.Please try again." I uninstalled in and reinstalled the app. I did the same for Google Play Services. I cleareded the cache on both apps but still the same. I switched off and on the phone a few times but nothing changed. All my other ups are working perfectly.

I just took away away one star. This app was working perfectly but now it keeps crashing. Please fix it! I want my beloved app back 2025 update: i stopped using this app because, at $120 per year its alot to pay

This app provides great motivation during the run, and even after. As you are running, the app can give you updates along segments of the run (completely customizable). The updates let you know you're progress without making you look at your phone! After the run, you can view your new achievements and review all the details of the run. The app overall is very easy to use, and the display while on a run contains all the information you need while being very easy to read. Great running app!

UPDATE MAY 23 2026: So the change from Fitbit to Google Health happened, and despite several messages to Google over the last few weeks while using Public Preview, they STILL haven't fixed the food tracker. No ability to add custom foods or new foods not in the database already, and you can't even add Quick Calories. Super Frustrating that they couldn't figure this out BEFORE taking away the ability to switch back to the old app to do this. DISAPPOINTING!!

I first received my Whoop about a month ago. For the first week, it was great. After that, when I went to charge it for the first time, my battery pack was defective. Customer support was great and issued me a new one along with time compensated for the loss of use given how long it took to process the shipment. Now I've had the strap again for about 3 days on my new Pixel 4. It constantly disconnects as others have also experienced. Very inconvenient considering we are paying $30/month.

Would be nice if it synced. Otherwise fine to use for calorie counting, but not much else at all. Haven't been able to sync with my Oura ring for 2 days despite uninstalling and reinstalling, disconnecting and reconnecting. Only constant ads and requests to upgrade. Maybe I would upgrade if the app was reliable?
This app seemed rock solid have been using it for years and I don't have any problems.

The browser/desktop should be improved as most of the features are hard to find and use in the mobile app. Adding exercises is very annoying and if I modify an already created routine b/c I adapted my workout for the day I loose all the changes and have to add them back in the next workout. Also, I can never find where to modify my existing workouts. Also, you could track locations in order to better identify when I'm leaving home / going to the gym. I'm beginning to regret my membership.

Has worked well for me so far, it kindda fit well with my routine so far. Since I take naps I love the nap alarm type so I dont have to make calculations in my head when I'm trying to hit the hay. Also the gradual + delayed alarm feature. So sick of waking up in a panic. Almost a week of use so far and I most of the time wake up to the brightening light of the soundless vibrateless minute of the alarm. Three times out of the total of about ~15 sleeps this week I had to be woken up by the sound
This does everything I need. It is awesome...when it actually works and doesn't keep stopping. Some days I can't even open it. It's very frustrating because I love it

Terrible. NOV 7, glitching and freezing a lot at the start. So not starting well at all, hence minutes to start tracking and then stops and doesn't track. Plus adverts getting worse again. OVERALL, improved, some ofthe previous annoyance have gone thankfully, ADVERTS, however for the past few versions the app is very slow to start and to save. generally it is still a heavily featured app.
i was able to log in once and never again! i tried uninstalling, clearing my tasks, rebooting my phone and reinstalling and i still cant get in, this needs to be fixed asap for Samsung Galaxy S2

It's an easy to use nutrition/ calorie tracker and includes a "social media" style blog where you can offer and receive support. There are presets on the calendar for most major "diets" such as Mediterranean, Keto, and Weight Watchers. Users can track weight, exercise, and nutritional intake. You can also share photos, journal, and food calendar with others, but it is not a requirement.

Last year i had no issues. Now, the app is horrible. It does not display the game mode. It happens in random. I tried to debugg it by various way describied on zwift support. No approach worked. As a result i cannot use companion app during some rides as it decides not to connect.

Just deleted the app. Idk what's happened with it, but during the past week and last few runs, it logs me out of the app/ doesn't track my run or stops & starts periodically during the run missing entire stretches. Then, when it ends and tor logs you out, it loses the activity...What's the point of running with a tracking app if it doesn't consistently track. So frustrating. Goodbye

The 3.0 update has Incredibly detailed feedback compared to previous versions. It now gives detailed breakdowns for HR, Power, cadence etc. Its also a great accessory for my handlebars during a workout with all the metrics from the main screen, condensed into a smaller screen on the phone, so does help when you're in the pain cave! Great to see the company taking so much user feedback and using it productively.

Easy to use and has a lot of workouts and food covered. But it needs a more granular subscription model, to purchase upgrades a la carte. edit: I knocked a star off because it USED to have the ability to find nearby routes from other people but that's no longer available on the app. You have to bookmark routes on the website in order to have it show up on your phone.

This is the very worst version of anything Google have done since buying Fitbit. I have gone through the trouble shooting process multiple times, uninstalled, reinstalled, checked permissions, etc. Consistently received "something went wrong" messages. You have now made my Fitbit fitness tracker into an expensive paperweight. Your responses to the issues raised by customers are inadequate. I am convinced that I will need to leave it behind and completely switch to another company.

Overall the app is pretty amazing. It records plenty of useful info and easy to sent to your pc to see it better. Only one slight issue is that its readings for elevation (or height above sea level) is way off. It tells me that I'm at an elevation of 64m when I'm cycling at along the coast at less than 1m above sea level. Otherwise all good so far.

Terrible update. All the programs/plans are class format so I can't workout offline. The videos are constantly buffering. - I can't see which exercises are in the workout so I can decide beforehand if I want to do it. Just terrible all around. - I am constantly listening to some instructor talk so I can't listen to music properly Suggestion: - Add a 'whiteboard' option for all the 'class format' workouts or provide similar programs/workouts in 'whiteboard' format.
Very calming, and soothing. Defiantly recommend this app for relaxing meditation techniques. Thank you very much Calm!
Cannot open app. Get error code page. Deleted and installed several times, made other changes with no success. The only app on my phone that does not work.

I like the app, helps me track my workout. However, I wish you could just purchase a base app that was essentially the "pro" version, at least in terms of tools, then have the yearly subscription if you wanted more of what's available on the site, with programs and whatnot.

Terrible user interface. Every time you complete listening to something, there's a ridiculous pop-up "congrats" type message. Now, there's a pop-up tracking how many days a week you completed something. The app doesn't even let you do basic things, like keep the story on repeat or queue up a few. Huge hassle in the middle of the night. Love the stories and sounds and would gladly keep the subscription, but this is clearly not designed with the user in mind. Will definitely be canceling.

I can't imagine the person who green-lit this update uses the app, because absolutely ruined its functionality. Figuring out what I've logged is profoundly cumbersome now that it requires a separate click to see each meal. I routinely have 10+ entries in a meal, and the inability to alter their order of appearance makes it more difficult to confirm I've entered everything correctly and haven't double-counted. Glad I found a way to revert to an older version, because the new one is garbage.

I have been lifting most of my life, off and on. I am in my 60th year and I've tried and done a lot of lifting programs, fitness programs and this makes everything so easy peezy. I've gone back to the basics and instead of writing everything down which I've done since forever, I now have something keeping track of what I do and I can make notes. This app keeps me on track!

I use this app all the time. I use it for all my workouts Update - After the app update, the calculations are inaccurate. Please fix Update :. I updated and still having problems. I even uninstalled the app and reinstalled and still having problems. When I log in a workout it doesn't show up. Yesterday I couldn't log on even with the correct email and password. Please fix! 4/24/20: Can't open up the app. Keeps crashing. Thinking about going to Strava

An excellent range of free workouts. My only issue is that it is hard to find the ones that you want without scrolling through hundreds of workouts. The app would be massively improved by a text search facility or the ability to select multiple categories at once to narrow down the selections, e.g. be able to select short strength workouts using basic equipment in whiteboard format.

The Nike Training Club app is a game-changer. Firstly, it's completely free—no hidden costs or subscriptions. The range of options is nearly limitless, allowing you to start at an easy level and progress, or dive into challenging body progression exercises that will enhance your core strength and stability. Having tried several paid apps, I can confidently say that NTC surpasses them by two to three levels in terms of quality and effectiveness.
works really well one little knock, the speed has a bit of a delay... besides that great app & a lot of options for the free version.

Update: I used to love this app, but lately it has been leaving off main ingredients from my shopping list (appears to not want me to buy zucchini). Really frustrating when you don't realize it until you are trying to cook. Tried to let developer know through the app, but no luck.
App will not let me sign in using my website user name and password.   So frustrating!

Part 2: zwift fails again.... App says to update so I update, hit the open app but the app fails. Instead its an endless loop telling me to update the already updated app. I try uninstall/newinstall with the same results. Zwift had to fail pretty bad to pull that one off. I guess i have no companion app for now.

Great easy checkout and access to my list and promo codes. Only problem was that i always order from my list first, then logor new items, and every time I added an item from my list, it went back to the top of my list and I have to scroll down and up and down and attempt to find where I left off on the list. It took much longer, and I doubled ordered things and had to fix the cart several times. I ran out of time and had to time to search for a new thing I was wanting.

Great for doing 5x5s. I love how it times the workout, with 3 minute breaks. Listening to a podcast, the notification to do the next set causes a light "Ding!" that doesn't interrupt my podcast. There's a small bug with Overhead Dumbbell Press and Deadlift: on my app, it increases ODP by 2.5lbs each workout (should be 5lbs), and Deadlift by 5lbs (should be 10lbs). Fix that and it's a 5-star app.

I've used map my walk before this, but then ventured into more diverse exercise, SO I switched. both have worked quite reliable, with only occasional drawbacks in my long distance trainings, namely having to turn off the power saving mode for accuracy, creating the need to carry an additional power bank, and occasionally when I am out of service, parts of my training were lost - however, this has occurred a handful of times with sometimes daily use over many years.

This app should include stairs stepping/running. Stairs Stepping is important to many runners. App performance has improved recently. More stable, no more sudden crashes. My old review below - App used to be better, now buggy, stalls and reloads forever. Useless if you use only this app because then your run data is lost. Sometimes can't get it started and if it per chance starts, it frustratingly stalls when your run ends. Very bad experience.

I absolutely fell in love with the app. There are so many workouts to suit anybody's taste: short and long, easy and hard, yoga and strength. Coaches are very engaging and genuine and they go a bit beyond just a workout, trying to create a human connection. Even though we've never met with any of Nike trainers, I feel like I know them and we have a bond, after sweating together for a few month. Thank you to all the people behind the app and community for creating a life-changing experience.

Love this app, great for monitoring everything from my sleep patterns to my morning commute. I especially love the ability to personalise both the in-app homescreen and what you see on your smartwatch via the Garmin Connect app

Garmin Connect has completely changed the way I track my fitness and daily health. The app is incredibly easy to use, and I love how customizable everything is. From creating workout screens to organizing the data I care about most, it feels personalized to exactly what I need for running, CrossFit, recovery, and everyday wellness. The layout is clean, easy to navigate, and gives detailed insights without feeling overwhelming. One of my favorite features is how seamlessly everything syncs and h

False advertisement is bad for business. Ordered vitamins & received an email saying my order was shipoed with USPS tracking ....Order was NOT shipped. They re-sent order but sale priced item is out of stock now. Had to call 5xs & call also to receive my money back cause that dont happen automatically when your order is canceled. Liars dont last long in my world nor do they get my money.

Really good app. I've been using it for years. Rarely doesn't log a route, usually very reliable. Good variety of different type of workouts to choose from. I only use the free version as that's good enough for my needs. I can see distance, how long it's taken, add up to 3 photos, add notes when saving a route. After saved a can see speed and distance done on any point of route, but can only do this via laptop or computer and logging on to the site.

Edit: I am deducting one star for the canned response from MFP. The app now receives a one star rating. The April changes to the UI are bad. I don't want to see macros. They are forced visible. I always fail the macros on low carb but I eat 1500 calories a day and I'm doing alright. Data entry per meal is more cumbersome. Seeing one day of diet information is not the default. Everything seems to take more clicks to accomplish. Everything else everyone else mentioned.

As of the most recent update at the beginning of May, the app no longer tracks my steps through any of the options I have available (Google Fit, Samsung Health, or Android Health Connect). I've tried disconnecting and reconnecting them all, separately, multiple times. Including clearing the caches. This is incredibly frustrating as now MFP doesn't allow me to properly track my calories, and does not allow any manual input of step counts. Please fix this, or allow us to input steps manually.

Decent simple app that helps me keep track of 5x5 workouts. Pretty specific but good for this workout. There are a lot of basic elements that are held off before a subscription, such as changing the prescribed weights on the routine, it might become an issue later, but right now it's going well. Essentially a useful little timer app which calculates your next workouts weights for you.

It is so easy to add recipes from the web or by cut and paste. This app instantly formats the recipe for you! I love that feature. Making a shopping list is super easy and it will even omit ingredients from my shopping list that I have deemed pantry items. I use both the app on my phone and on my laptop. It keeps your device from going to sleep while you are preparing a recipe so you don't have to keep touching your dirty device. I love this appb and have recommended it to all my friends.

I like the real-world choices for exercises. Getting even a little credit for sitting at my desk, glued to a computer, gives me a little hope! 3 years later I'm using it again, plotting every bite of food. It's laborious at first, but grows easier as the habit builds. This time I'm down nearly 30 pounds and I'm very motivated to not only keep going, but to keep it off. A big change is that this time I'm in a weight reduction class through my insurance company.
good app but pop up timed adverts on free is just horrible. they also don't kick in till you have used it for a few days. makes it unusable unless you pay pretty quickly

It was a very bad experience I tried them once and i ordered some supplements on nov 10 I supposeed to receive my order in nov 23 But i didn't receive it till now And every time i contact them they tell me it's in the customs and we can't do anything Also my friend have the same problem

I have used Endomondo for three years. Reliable and accurate with great statistics if you sign up for the premium. Definitely to be recommended. Recently it has had trouble picking up GPS and the voices change randomly. Not sure it is being maintained so well these days.. Down a star.
Password won't work from regular account
I have been using this app for several years. I like how it keeps my data. I like my garmin watch too. I'm really happy with both and just bought a new Garmin.

Lots I love about the Whoop, but the thing I don't love is the constant Bluetooth connection issues I've had, I have had to update all the settings on my phone just for this app to 'almost' work properly, but you still have to remember to have the app open all the time and to try and keep your phone with you all the time! Even so and following everything recommended by support I still get almost daily connectivity issues, especially if it's disconnected overnight and then the data has to catch up too which takes a while! If you lose connectivity you have to forget the device, take off the strap off your wrist and turn the phone off and on again to completely clear it from your Bluetooth settings so that you can reconnect again, having to do this nearly daily is a PITA, my friend has it on Apple and has had ZERO issues. In comparison my Fitbit app syncs my sleep in seconds and I never have connection issues, this morning I had to fully reconnect Whoop as it had lost connection 5 hours b
Great app. Easy food entry and comprehensive database. Essential features are free. Give it a whirl for your tracking needs.

I haven't used my bedside alarm clock in years, not even as a backup. The program is reliable and wakes me gently in the morning. I had to disable the vibrate on alarm function (optional), as the onset of the vibrations would immediately wake me. The gradual audible alarm is much better without it. Well worth the purchase.

Horrible app. =/ 1. It doesn't accurately track footsteps. I forgot my fitbit at home and relied on this whole running errands all day. In the end Google registered two walks. One for seven minutes and the other for 11 mins. Sigh. 2. When selecting an activity you have to manually scroll to select the type. Booo, this can easily be updated to make searching more efficient, come on Google! 3. It is sometimes nonexistant to edit a workout. Overall a horrible interface.

I see that these are uploads of recipes others make, I am very doubtful of the accuracy of the nutritional content. The recipes should be standardized in descriptions of instructions and everything checked. Looks cool but not trusting it

Just terrible as a sleep tracker. * Most importantly: You can't see if you need more sleep when you wake up, because it won't tell you sleep duration until you end tracking. Useless for insomnia/sleep disorders. * Misses most naps and won't let you manually add anything over 2h. * Doesn't seem to add multiple sleeps to your stats/charts properly.

I appreciate the fact that the premium workouts are free, as, like most of you, I'm currently working out in my basement. Ab workouts are good, the rest are just ok, but hey, free. Main gripe is the app has fits if anything else happens on your phone during a workout. Text messages, push notifications, anything else, cause the app to lose volume- and effect the volume of the music playing in the background. Touch the screen at that point, work out over.

I have been using it for years. Recently a new outdoor running season started and I used the app. GPS signal was getting lost as soon as the app was running in the background, so my track was shown as straight line between two points. And there are no audio settings, so I can't turn off the coach. Very disappointing, switching to another app

The app is long due for an update. Tracking your workout works fine but trying to see your workout history or feed takes minutes sometimes with the app trying to load it forever. Making changes in workout (like adding kms where you forgot to turn the app on) is jot very intuitive either. There's also an option to connect to MyFitnessPal which kind of works but not really.

customer support really really bad, my device is showing inaccurate reading, all numbers are wrong! whoop support doesn't care, ignoring me totally giving unacceptable solutions! they refused to change the device! and refused to refund my subscription! unfortunately whoop now is only money driving, they show you how supportive they are one you do the purchase everything is changed after! they are not like how they use to be!

App is basically unusable. If I click on 'previously ordered items' nothing appears. I put stuff in my cart and then clicked 'save for later'. Now I click on my cart and it says that it's empty. You can't access your 'saved for later' items if your cart is empty. Added stuff to my cart and cannot complete the checkout process. After my address, the next screen never loads. If you click on an item that is sold out, the screen just goes blank. Very frustrating.

Does not work. App never worked since I first downloaded, and not even after update. Waste of my time. Uninstalling. My phone is a Samsung S10. I will not email developer, so don't ask. I will check back in a few months. Please fix it. UPDATE: So, I got a new phone, Samsung S22, and thought I'd try again to use the app. Still can't log into my account. Do I have to have a different username and password on the app and not use the same credentials as on my laptop? I really want to use the app!
I enjoy using the app works good for me and like how it give u split speed when riding

While I understand that change can be hard to get used to, I hope that Google Health/Fitbit listens to these reviews. Before the change, the layout of the home page was easy to navigate & relevant info was there at your fingertips. I could easily scroll to other days to compare them. Now? Not even close. You have to click into things to see your history. The home page isn't even that customizable. I don't care about cardio load, steps are more important! When I open the app, I want to see them.

An excellent workout app that requires a bench, barbell and plates (space to do dips and pull ups too if you go for the assistance exercises) while still offering a full body workout, excellent for any level of fitness from total beginners to fit people who don't have access to/simply don't want to have to use a dozen different machines, feedback on your results and guidance during training is very helpful. One of the few apps where i found premium features to be worth the money too.
Paid for original app but can't even use it anymore without a separate subscription fee. Not worth it, plenty of similar apps for a flat fee or free.

The run tracker is quite inaccurate when running on indoor track. It tells me I am running much further than I actually am.. When running outside, it often freezes the tracker and I lose all my info mid-run. But I absolutely love the training plans and guided runs. It has helped me fall back in love with running.
The Garmin connect is great. Using my solar 2 instinct to track my gravel bike activity and viewing all the stats on connect is awsome.
Recently logged in and all my recipes and meal plans were gone! Will be looking for a different platform to use.

Easy to use, shop, filter and find promos and coupons. Big variety and good info on each item. I buy 95% of my supplements and now use autoship which helps me avoid the OS! moment when I notice I'm nearly out.

I'm a premium user & really enjoyed this app when I started using it in 2013. Simple, everything on the home screen & easy to view, especially foods eaten & weight (the two most important things). There have been some updates over the years, but the most recent one was disappointing. Can't view all foods eaten w/o multiple clicks, weight is hidden way at bottom, overall much less user friendly. Feels like they're moving away from its original purpose. Not sure I'll keep using this app.

Terrible app with almost no functionality. Not sure if it is a phone issue (I use a Google Pixel) or an app issue but I will be uninstalling this app shortly. In the More screen of options, I cannot get to my account or order info. In fact the only click option that took me anywhere on that page was Feedback. I placed an order and I could not tell if it was competed or if I was logged in. I will stick with the browser version of this website.

Google pixel 6, is there something with this combination that means the app constantly crashing/failing, being unresponsive when in the game mode. Won't send messages, no ride ons, no bell, nothing. Close the app, restart the app & nothing. Works for searching for events but the problems occur when trying to interact with riders during an event. Pointless having it, totally unstable on my phone.
Unreliable. Sometimes the app stops working for me. I can't sign in and searches return no results. It seems random and hss been happening for over a year.

Until a few days ago, this was my favorite alarm app with captchas that would reliably get me up out of bed. However, a few days prior to writing this review, this app started showing me full screen ads. I get the dev needs money, and if they want to incorporate banner ads, fine. But I draw the line at full screen ads. Last thing I need to see when I wake up is having a full screen video ad shoved down my throat. If this is the new norm for this app, then I will find a new alarm app.

An absolutely fantastic app! It has become an invaluable tool for me to stay healthy. I credit it with being a huge part of my 70 lb weight loss. I'm absolutely amazed everyday at the size and depth of their database. There has not been one food, grocery store, restaurant that it doesn't have listed. The free version is absolutely amazing by itself but the options you get with a paid subscription is even better.

I've been using the app for the last 2+ months and have been loving it. BUT today's update has been less than ideal. the UI feels clunkier and less intuitive. The push on the meal planning is a big detractor for me. I may want to check it out, but I don't want to be pushed. I paid a premium so I didn't get the ads. Now on to the positives, the barcode scanner and wealth of preloaded items is fantastic for busy working dad of 3. I can usually jump in and find items quickly.

App is nice and easy to use, the exercise function is just not accurate. I logged 50 minutes of gym, 15 minutes of walking and 8 hours of working at a desk, the rest it allocated to sleep and rest and it came out to 4000+ calories. there is just no way you burn that much in a day so easily
Absolutely no way to use this offline for free like I have been able to for the last 6 years. Time to move on.

This app sucks. I dont know why they would switch over to an app that doesn't do half of what it used to. Can not manually add in your protein. The food bar code doesn't scan 90% of items. It is very clunky and it seems nothing works like it should. It take so much time to try and not be successful in this app when it only took seconds before when it was the fitbit app. Just do not understand why they would change so much and for the worse.

I have been using Map My Ride for over a year now, mostly for cycling plus a few runs and walks. It is perfect. It works well on my Pixel 4a phone. While riding the app announces mile splits, maps the route accurately, and gives elevation statistics among others. I definitely recommend it and don't want to ride without it now. Also, you can save rides if you choose.
Worked well until mid-April, then can start a workout, but immediately asks to save workouts and is unable to save. Android 16.

3/28/19 UPDATE: I've been using this app since i find the Groupon discount. this part November they also had a deal for a lifetime subscription - I bought that. This app is really great. - I found a discount through Groupon so I could buy a full year subscription. The "free" app doesn't really allow for much so you do have to pay to get the most out of the app. Since experiencing the paid content I highly recommend this app. I keep telling everyone about it.
Go online and open a free account.

Stuck with a 1-year sub, but 2 weeks in there are major pitfalls. First, you can't exclude specific foods/allergies so they don't show up in recipes at all. The recipe selection is also poor. Second, the AI picture tool is cool but guesses calories based on a few ingredients, causing you to under-eat or over-eat. Neither is good. Finally, the feedback URL is broken, and giving a discount to a friend who didn't buy immediately feels like a scam. I paid €50, he could for €21.

This has to be the best of ever calorie counter/planner I have ever used, trust me tried many only to be frustrated and underwhelmed. Its so user friendly plus a great supportive group. There are recipes, photos and many keep their journals open so you can read success stories and struggles and what they eat. One great point for any issue you can contact them and omg they reply. Of course their is a slight learning curve. overall 5+stars☆☆☆☆☆☆+

Integration of this app with zwift leaves a lot to be desired. I'm hooked on Zwift, and the app has some nice features, but too basic. Whish I could not just check my training schedule, but also develop the schedule, overrule (select alternative workout/ride), select world etc. Giving thumbs up via app is a real pain. Rider lists is too dynamic, so easily wrong selection, plus too many buttons to press. Especially when the ride gets harder. Prefer to eg give 1 ride on to all nearby riders.
This is a fun app. Add your own recipes or look up new ones. Love the meal planning. It helps keep the "what's 4 dinner" question at bay.

Very poor user interface. clunky operation and, in some cases, just does not operate. takes a stupidly long time to input food/meals, which it shouldn't as the app is based on being hassle free. saving meals is potluck if it will actually save them. The vast majority of nutrition information is incorrect, and even when you correct it, it still reverts to the incorrect option. dont bother going premium, as I did, and only use as a very rough guide of calorie intake as it's usually wrong.

I've had this app on one phone or another for the last few years. Calm went from being able to choose among soundscapes and thematic backgrounds for free to charging for almost 80% of content, to ultimately being a FULL-PAY app. I wouldn't mind paying $60/year if I wanted to take advantage of all the narrations and music, but I was only here for the crickets and campfire. Sorry to see it go from my phone, but it can never be worth the price of admission for me.

This is by far the best calorie-tracking app. I have tried about a dozen and I can say that this one is better because of the following: - clean and pleasant UI. - easier set up without super invasive surveys in the beginning. - ads on the free version are annoying but manageable (I get that I am using a free version and these ads help to pay for the app maintenance and development). It's still annoying but not as bad as any of the other apps. - a fair amount of free features.

I can't write on feedback. Shows the curser moving but no letters appear. I wanted to know about items that would be back in stock and some other items, I can no longer find and if they are coming back. The site lags, and takes too long to load.
Have used 2 days now. Love the app gives me the info I want for the riding I do. So very happy 😊 Have been using the app for 3 years, and I'm still happy with it
Nice app, loses 4 stars because of connectivity drops and no way to reconnect to the game

In the most recent update, the save workout option has got even worse. When I finish and save, if I have no data connection I cannot start a new workout without discarding the current one. Not well thought out. (Update - this seems to apply to a 'general hike' and not a mtb - why different??) Previously after a workout usually when I tapped to 'save', I got a 'failed' message. I am often biking in forest where there is no wifi or cell signal. So the newer behavior is even worse.

I have problems with pairing my Garmin 540 all the time- it stucks, doesn't sync correctly, I needed to repair and it doesn't work at all, freezing on the pairing screen - sometimes randomly it works. even simple feature like menstrual cycle calendar doesn't work - it's ridiculous. I set menstrual cycle length to 28, standard, but it shows the forecasted period one week before, after 21 days 🤣

Absolute garbage compared to the Fitbit app. It's not intuitive to find anything I need. I used to see everything for the day on one page that I would scroll up and down on, and to see the previous day's data you just swipe or press an arrow. Now I have to click into each individual tiny tile to find it, and even then the RHR takes extra clicks from what I can tell. I Half the page is taken up by "notifications" that I don't care about. It's clunky and unattractive.

App was working fine until last update. IDK if I'm missing something or in fact it was the last update that messed things up. Everytime I open the app now my overview info is not up to date. It constantly says data catching up and it's usually about 8 to 9 hours it has to catch up. Fix this ASAP or help me to correct it if I'm doing something wrong otherwise I'll take the sad decision to discontinue this amazing subscription

Functionality is solid. In-app small ads are fine if you don't pay for a subscription, but full-screen pop up video ads while I'm using the app is unacceptable. It'd be nice to have a one time payment option, but they'll only take your money by fleecing you with an overpriced monthly subscription.

I personally love this App, from the daily features to the ease of use during workout sessions, if there is one thing I could ask to add is the ability to link/sync the data captured in this App to my Samsung Health account, the health account is great but the tracking of the workout in my opinion is terrible and redundant when i already track it on this account
I like it very much, but after less than 6 months of use, the battery only lasts 2 days.

I bought this app years ago and loved it. Now it has the typical subscribe monthly trash and an interface that throws that in your face. Gym keeper is far superior and a only one time fee, worth a look if you haven't checked it out. Update 2 years later: everytime you open this app even if you paid for the power pack it prompts you to buy the subscription service. Ever. Fn. Time. Don't give them your money.

I have been using this app for a number of years and it's usually been great! However, since the update a few days ago, it won't track distance any more. It seems to lose gps lock and distance is zero! Please fix this as soon as possible. I love keeping record of my walks!

Love this app! It's very useful and easy to use. I like how you can add ingredients from your weekly meals into your grocery list. I like the pantry section for things that you always have on hand (salt, oil, butter, etc). Nice and simple to enter recipes from your own collection, from a website or discover new recipes.

Went to look at MY adapted recipie and it wouldn't let me see the instructions! Instead forced me to use a blogger's website for the directions, which does not include my adaptations! Since i have no more free cook mode views this week, and am not willing to pay $60 to view MY recipe, there is no way to the directions. Update: now we have lost a different recipe because the site it was on has shut down.
works. useful for sleep and strain tracking. 3 years and counting.

I've been using this app for 2 years now. I love it. In fact, I upgraded to Gold because it's such a great help to me. When I remember to use it, we eat properly. I don't default to buying pizza or having another night of boxed nuggets and fries! My favourite thing is using the premade plans. That way, I honestly don't have to figure out what do put on the calendar. Does every meal work for our family? Nope. But at least it gives me a base to work from - like if there is a noodle dish that won't work for us, I know that I can make a noodle meal that night, etc. I am thankful that I can drag recipes around on the plan so if something needs more time and I know I won't have time that day, I can shift it around. Or I can delete and replace a recipe if it's a total "no" for us. I appreciate the cook view because then I don't have to scroll up and down with messy fingers to remember how much of something to add. I've discovered and tried foods I never would have made before (like cornbread!

I'm new to fitness trackers. My wife and kids would categorize me as anti-non-critical tech. I've always been healthy and have no chronic problems as others my age do. I got this Garmin Vivoactiv 6 to track training and recovery. I'm readying myself for the JFK50 and so far, I really appreciate some of the nice to haves, like playing music without having to take my phone. The training stats are helpful if you're micro analyzing running. The body battery may be a bit off for very active people...

all the resources and money you have at Google and this is the best you can come up with Random steps logged telling me I've burnt 3500 calories in a day when I've barely moved that day!? My watch tells me I've been on the treadmill for 20 minutes which is approx 200 calories, but when Fitbit sends this data to Nutracheck it says I've burnt 963 calories. It's so disjointed, very disappointing from such a huge company

The app has a feature to track average heartrate during a workout. Well, the number is was displaying didn't seem right to me, so I calculated my average manually. Their number was nowhere close. If the programmers couldn't even calculate a simple average corrwctly, what else did they f' up?

It was very pretty before but this new update made the graphics so ugly. update from 2 to 1 star: WHY DOES THIS APP KEEP GETTING WORSE AND WORSE EVERY UPDATE? I regret paying for the premium, before it was such a good app, now it looks scammy and has so many bugs

Been using this app recently, good to keep track of my walks .. but just recently while recording, the app itself seems to stop tracking my process along the way, and need to start over again halfway throughout my process of walks. Have there been any issues arising on this similar case with anyone using this app.

Don't get me wrong, it's a great thing to have access to the premium workouts and programs for free. I really enjoy using the app, but it doesn't support Chromecast. So it's super hard to workout in my garage when trying to keep up with the trainer on my tiny phone. Also, when I tried screen mirroring, it's like the app knew and would completely stop working. I've tried on both Apple and Android. No luck.

It's ok. Could be better. Should allow you to share and compete with friends and family. UI can also be more intuitive. I've also experienced the app removing a few heart points for no reason. Should also add a calendar view where you could see all the rings and if you closed them or not. Like what apple has for the apple watch. It's nice to look at your progress that way.

Great app, coming from Samsung Health app, this is so much more informative. also appreciate the indicators, body battery, VO2 max, required rest,... all help keep this 40+ body injury free while moving in the right direction!

Forced to upgrade from the Fitbit app. This app is very incomplete when it comes to heart monitoring during daily activities, the main reason I bought a Fitbit 2 years ago. Ok if you only care about daily summaries and you want to "compare" yourself to others based only on age and gender (a weird form of body shaming, not for me).

Was great for a few years, but within the past month it has started shutting itself off for no discernable reason. I get the GO prompt and tuck it away. Half a mile later I check it and it's off. Maybe recorded a few minutes, maybe nothing at all. Too annoying to futz with anymore. I'll just use my backpacking map app and ditch this.

Long time user: This new update's UI makes nearly every aspect of the display and logging worse. You can't see all the logged items at once unless you click on the day specifically. Can't swipe to different days anymore. Annoyingly located pop-up under meals to copy meals from different days, which when accidentally clicked, has to be deleted individually. The summary dashboard is less easily digestible. I recommend finding an alternative food tracker at this point.

As a fitness app tracker it is okay. App has plenty of activities to log which is great. What it doesn't seem to do is track heart rate to calculate calories burned for the exercise. You think with an app focused on exercising it would be informative to know that but hey you can buy their shoes though. Wear support is hit or miss but usually a miss unfortunately. Never works right.

The app has a pretty good picture feature, but the overall usability falls short of expectations. I paid for the Pro version, yet I can’t use it reliably—about half the time I run into error messages, which seem like server issues. The streak feature is especially frustrating; the constant pop-ups are unnecessary and intrusive. The same goes for the meal tips—they add little value and can’t even be turned off. It lists out all the vitamins, minerals, etc in my meals, and if I'm low on one, I can click on it and see which foods are full of that particular vitamin. Pretty cool.

It is hard to believe an update could make an app so much worse in multiple ways. Not sure what the product team was doing here. You can longer easily jump to different parts of a work out, and if you're playing music in the background it is now significantly muted. If you turn it up, then the trainer is screaming in your ear. Can I use the older version?

The latest update is terrible. Is it possible to revert the changes? Previous format with the exersise preview was great, I could chose the workout based on the exercises. Now it is not possible to see what is included into workout. Also witeboard workouts are useless, previously we could mark every exercises as completed in the app, now i need to track the progress on paper, which is ridiculous. Please revert to prevoius version.

Best digital fitness experience available. As a vet, I find it hard to find an affordable gym or fitness program, but this app allows me to stay healthy through fitness and health training. I highly recommend giving it a try, no equipment or experience required, just ambitious and an open mind to learn. The trainers give me a laugh during work outs cause they will say exactly what I'm thinking.
I love this app. I would love it even more if I could copy and paste my recipes in from my recipe app, rather than having to enter each ingredient one at a time.

This app is great from a functionality standpoint. I prefer it to the prior one I was using to track my food. I would have given it 5 stars but the ads in the app pop up too frequently and are a bit invasive. I don't mind ads to avoid paying but this app is worse than most.

Many things are good about this app, but there are Few issues: Everyday the alarm is setting a different delay, making the alarm go off early, 3:15 this morning, for example. Reset the delay to 0 and the issue happens again the next day; delete the alarm and set a new one: it comes back the next day. Also, it randomly ignores "skip alarm" option. I click to skip the next alarm, it confirms, I click OK, and it indicates it will be skipped, but then, sometimes, does not actually skip!

Since Google and the latest updates, everything is broken. The new period tracker is awful and inaccurate. Sleep is constantly logging while awake. Can't edit exercises, so I guess I did 3 hours of yoga and slept 12 hours. It has been a while, and nothing is fixed. Clearly, they don't care. After a decade, I'm moving to something else.

Everything was fine until they asked me to update my information. Then everything defaulted. There was no way of updating any of my statuses. So I am unable to know any of my previous weights or anything. I'm very very frustrated. I was in the middle of a workout program. I have asked for a refund for one month and have not heard anything back. They do not have a phone number to call anymore. So everything has to be done through email.
GARBAGE APP. Great resources on the website, but the app is literally just a shell that for every action just redirects you to a web link. 0/10 stars.

I dont understand what happened? was there an update? because I had been logging food just fine for years and now it can't find a single food I type in.. kind of rendering the app useless. guess I'll be looking for another app to use to track. update: the issue has been fixed. changed my rating but still not 5 star. when I first started using this app I was able to scan barcodes. it sucks to watch greed takeover and see apps make what used to be free features something you now pay for.

I typically don't write reviews but you need to fix this app. Since the last update 50% of the time I cannot log on to book my fitness classes. This is so frustrating especially when these classes are limited number of attendees. I wish the businesses had their own site so I don't have to rely on mindbody. Use to be a reliable app now its a nuisance!!!

Downloaded a week ago. I cannot book an appointment for the franchise that I currently have a membership with. I get the wheel of death after I select "confirm" on the dialog box asking me to confirm if I want to book the appointment. It never errors out. It just keeps spinning. I have signed out and back in to the app, deleted the app and reinstalled it - it just never works.

This app is not made for exact distances. Or sprinters. It calls out your time for each mile, but the time it calls is wrong. After my workout, I look at the times for my miles, and they are less than the times the app called. In addition, the app typically will not land on exact miles. For example, it goes to 3.01 miles instead of exactly 3.00 miles. And for sprinting short distances, it is very inaccurate, both in time and distance. This app is usable, but could be better

Don't pay for this. I was lucky to earn 3 months of the paid version at no cost through another subscription. It ends this week, and I won't renew even at 40% off. You can find meditations and sounds on paid AND free versions of Spotify. The sound quality is higher, and there are options for no ads for brown noise there, even on the free version. This is overpriced for what they offer, and I find the meditations annoying and kids' stories are not appropriate and NOT sorted well.

I like the different options to listen to for learning healthy habits. However, if I paused it partway and came back after 5 min, I needed to restart the speaker. Sometimes, I had to completely close the app before I could come back to listen. I also liked the option to listen to soft music to go to sleep and set a timer for it to stop.

Thanks to this app. I have improved my health, lost over 40 lbs, lowered my BP, (off BP meds.). The app is instrumental in tracking my calories to stay in a caloric deficit, and track all my nutrients, especially sodium and protein. I also record all my workout classes on the App. Lastly, the App is very easy to navigate, it is very user friendly!
edit: somehow, I get stuck on the subscription screen when it pops up, and there isn't a continue or X button. I have to close the app and open it again to go back to the original screen.

It's unfortunate, but this does very little to track the fitness level and goals of someone who is tracking with a watch. I don't take my phone on long runs, I leave it on the bench when I play sports, and its too much hassle to manually input everything. If it would connect with Garmin or Strava and add those steps, that would be outstanding! In the mean time, it does not really help. Good thought though!

Google Fit has lost its purpose. It shows fragmented, shallow data without meaningful insights or context, making it hard to get real value from the app. Google has the leverage to enforce standards and require developers to provide richer, more consistent data, turning Fit into a true health hub for all connected apps. It should clearly show which apps provide specific data like workouts, sleep, or heart rate, and how that information is used. Instead, everything feels disconnected, useless.
Easy to use, no annoyances so far. Makes my life easier!
I am locked out of Premium after paying and confirming my email. Chatbot useless, no helpful links. Is this a scam? The free access materials are good.

I have been using Cronometer for about a week now and find it easy to log my daily intake. My main criteria for chosing the app was to ensure I was getting adequate calcium, protein, magnesium, and vitamin C for my bone health. I obtain D3 and K2 with supplements but strive to get all the rest of my nutrients from food. Cronometer keeps me on track.

Great program no matter the age. I like that I can see the proper form without searching for anything. all the info you need in one package. Edit. The free app became pay to use. You get 7 days free but have to provide card details. -4 stars for providing a shitier experience for an app that was free to use. I will switch to writing things down and do math instead.

This app is literal trash compared to the Fitbit app. I wish we could've had a choice in this. I can't even log my calories appropriately because you can't enter anything below 0. if I had half a serving and there's only one serving size option it's either all or nothing. It also doesn't even have the calorie to serving size correct, an issue I hardly had on the Fitbit app.It completely wiped my goals and some aren't even possible to have now. nothing like the beta app they attempted. Be better

I love using this app for booking classes at Polefit Carolinas. It is easy to find the classes I want and add them to my schedule with a few taps. Wait list functionality works well also. The studio has used the booking info to notify students via FB of classes with extra room, or send reminders to drop if you can't attend full classes. Very handy scheduling tool. I like that it shows other classes at other studios in my area, so it's easy to try new things at new places!

Just that fast, it goes from a 4 star to a 1. Runs are not syncing with challenges, very disappointing. Never used to have this issue and now it seems to be ongoing. It's cheating and all bugged out.

You can tell when there's an app update because your fitbit struggles to sync and won't show any notifications from your phone. I've been a fitbit user since 2015 and it's been inconsistent lately. Not sure if it's because of Google buy-out or what. But, frustrated that things aren't working properly because when they do, it's awesome!

Great app! East to track what you eat (input) and how much you burn (output). Allows you to lose weight and maintain the loss. Easy to find foods. Selecting from recently eaten is great if you follow routines. It would be helpful if they carried a longer history for those who vary their routine more. Saved meals are huge time savers. I lost 118 pounds while using the app. I'm approaching maintenance mode now; accordingly, I've lost only 5 or so pounds in the last few months.
I use this app to track how far I ride and how long it it the best app that I found and it works great

Lots of questions asked, about 50 to start. Then not all UI buttons are working properly. After asking 50 questions, asked me to pay without me even trying it first. there is problem on the app i can't login still give me incorrect password, i try from the browser the password is correct,!!!

This is a great app because it always works and makes scheduling classes and appointments so easy! I have been using it for years and never had any problems. It also syncs with Google calendar. Thankful that I don't have to call and write down appointments anymore!

I like all the selections Vitacost offers and the ease of shopping on their site. There always seems to be a promo card I can use for a discount and sometimes special sales for me. I purchased endomondo pro a long time ago, when it was a one time fee for the pro app. I still have access to "features" from premium that my pro access gave from 8 years ago, however, I have no way of verifying what features I should have and there is no way of looking up what I do have. I've shopped at Vitacost for years and love it. However this app doesn't work. I tried clicking on the Add button numerous time but the item would not be in the shopping cart. Other glitches as well. Back to the website to order!

Downrating after Whoop betrayed its principle of not including a step counter. So long to quality battery life! Screens up next with the 5.0 probably. Whoop is the best for focusing on recovery & sleep and allows me to customize what I see or make it easy to skip past what I don't need. Thanks for not being just a dieting app like everyone else!!

my recent activities like walking is not captured for last few days. it was working before but recently it's not captured but it works when I open the app. please fix the bug asap.

Calm is the PERFECT tool for busy minds. If you are looking for a quick release, deeper sleep, or the perfect break to refocus, you can't go wrong here. Everyone needs a tool to help manage the stressors of life AND this is it! I'm using it to teach middle schoolers how to focus and be more mindful in their daily life (I start EVERY class with it!) and personally it helps me quiet my busy mind and sleep better. I wouldn't be without this app on every device I own.
Your app ain't working correctly, you guys need to fix the problem with your log-in..

I've been using this app almost daily for 10 years, but this new app update has ruined this app. I'm now actively searching for a replacement. This program meets all of my needs for tracking my riding practices and when I am at shows. I also like the option to be in challenges. It is very much worth using. An update in January 2020. What happened to the Blood pressure tracking im Whoop app? Woke up one morning and it simply had disappeared. Jury is still out on whoop. Works well and a solid running app that's free and also offers free training programs with variation and audio feedback.

I'm no professional Runner or anything, but how can so many Runners get 50, 60, up to 100+ miles in 18 hours in the 1st day of the month??? Tell me how that's possible!! Truly disappointed with the newest update, makes everything complicated, food searches take forever to load, and the days are all wacky, some days won't come up, some are repeated. When looking at your diary you have to go into the meals to view whats in them whereas before they were simply displayed on the page. I can't believe I didn't come across this app in any articles about best food tracking! It's the only one I found that truly has free unlimited scanning, and it has a great interface, excellent data tracking, all manner of unexpected positives. Seems likely I'll switch to the paid version at some point juts to support how awesome the app is!

Where do I start? Used to be a really spot on app, distances measured fine, even the calorie spend matched up broadly with Fitness Pal. Add in an "unexplained error" when I use the phone camera which kills the app, and I'm sorely disappointed. Have uninstalled/reinstalled, but if it doesn't improve its no use to me. Moto G5/Oreo

Great way to track your progress. It would be even better if the app can connect directly to a device without opening another app to read the device. An example would be a body strap heart rate monitor, i dont want to run 2 or more apps just to read my heart rate on one app then moved the data to this app.

There is not too much advertising, which you would expect from a free app. Gives great motivation to keep you moving and even lets you choose challenges. Also, the app differs human only walking and dog walking so both of us get our own dedicated log. However, they seem to promote controversial ideology rather than what they should push, fitness and training. Developers, leave your personal opinion out of your product.

Good app. Overall the interface is well designed, easy to navigate and easy on the eyes. Great that it can sync with other fitness trackers. A few complaints. They way the app deals with servings/units is confusing resulting in many erroneous food entries in their database. I am having to reference nutritional info on my food packaging to make sure the macro nutrients per serving are correct which takes a lot of time. It would be nice to be able to copy entries from previous days' food diary.

Two times i've used it and two times it didn't work correctly. While cycling, if you put it on pause, and start after- tine is running, but the rest like miles and calories- not. So i realised that when didn't hear from voice assistant for a while, that something is wrong. Even samsung's built-in health app works much better. Don't waste your time for this.

I have used this app for a lot of years. In the past, I have been very happy with it. In the last several weeks, more times than not, it will not load. It sits on the main screen as if it is trying to load, but never actually does. It is very frustrating. The home screen also used to be a lot more user-friendly. After the last update, everything is gone. I had a collection of recipes from various blogs. All of the selections that I was following are gone as well. I have uninstalled the app.

I was at 3/5 before. Useful metrics, but expensive compared to other options. No option to purchase the device with a minimal membership, only full cost no matter what. Now changing to 1/5. Since the switch over from fitbit the battery on my device has been draining very quickly, especially overnight to the point that sometimes it's turned off when I wake up because the battery is so low despite the fact that it was at 60% when I went to bed. I have been using it for two years and a half. Works quite well, especially sending the qorkuloit data to MyFitnessPal. However, yesterday it stopped sending the data and now I have to enter the workout data manually, which is a pain. Will this glitch be fixed any time soon?

annoying app, voice wouldn't shut up and talked garbage over my music couldn't see how to get him to go away. couldn't see how to add intervals assuming not a feature on this app, no warm up and told me I started my run at 0.7 miles moving at a good running pace when I was still walking. I don't know what the "head starts" are and without that knowledge I will be assuming they are the unwanted voice's way of saying I'm just going to kill the mood with unhelpful insights now. 1 star ⭐

This has been the best of about 5 apps like it that I have tried, 4 of which are considered " best", but come with too many ads or connections to 3rd party businesses. The free version of this app does a great job of allowing you to track progress and keep up with your diet log. Being able to set goals, adjust nutrients, and add to the library of foods is also easy. I usually give apps like this a 3 month trial, this is still month 1, and it is standing strong.

So time consuming looking for food choices, sometimes not finding some choices at all after an exhausting search for 20 to 30 minutes of searching only to come up empty. Should alphabetize choices so that they can be quickly found and if choice is not on the list would know right away without searching farther to input manually because it's not on the list Hope that makes sense.

Known bugs of custom meal duplicating still exist after years (and no way to delete it). Feature parity againts iOS version (missing upload to scan feature which is a dealbreaker for me as I cannot log airplane meals where there is no internet connection). Data syncing issue with health connect (steps are not getting synced, activities are duplicated). wonderful experience while running✨️ the voice feature every 1 km helps to push during runs 🙌; definitely worthiness!!

Update: Ad quantity is reasonable, and the ads mostly seem to be muted again (or I'm getting less obnoxious ads :) - either way it's more feasible to use now! Re Sleep Detection is horrible. You wake up 5mins at night and then back to sleep for 4hours. Whoop won't detect the additional 4hours. It is super frustrating, my cheap Fitbit was better than this. It's showing me barbell exercises even when I chose only dumbbell as equipment available. User interface needs to be better. Been using this for years. I find loopholes in other alarms when I'm tired and this was the only app that could get me up in the morning for school, and now it continues to wake me up years later for work. So many features and conveniences with this that I believe it's unmatched. Need a quick, standard alarm? ☑️ Need an alarm that will not give up until you're actually awake? ☑️ If there wasn't a character limit, I'd write a book for a review. Please, for all our sakes, don't get rid of this.

For most runs, this app is very useful. It tracks distance and cadence pretty well. I need to restart my phone just to get the app to work normally again. Also, every once in a while after giving an audio update at a mile marker, it won't return the volume in my music app to its original level, so it's near silent for the next mile.

Brilliant app for any business with bookabke services. Quick, easy, compact and the management information that is gleamed from the data collated is superb. Up until last week this was a great app. Now whenever I get the notifications for my check in or the daily calm quote and I click to open the app the only thing there is an offer for a discounted plan (which I cannot afford so I use the free version) and then a page of things that are locked. Well reseachered training routine that gets you from zero to hero in no time! Edit! They got greedy and I am out of here, that was a disgusting move. I do like this app it has helped me be more mindful of what im eating but I not convinced that it calculates the calories correct I can scan a few things to add to my journal and I know for a fact its wrong so scan again and it comes up with different numbers that is also wrong, not wrong by much but sometimes that little bit over a few meals can put you over what your goal

Usually pretty good but yesterday was annoying. Is there an Android update coming? I have Samsung Galaxy 8s and for some reason, last night's run was way off from my treadmill's stats. Waaay off. I have been using NRC & NTC since April 2015. This is THE best running and Coaching application available which is absolutely FREE. I just love this App. Thank you NIKE for the Wonderful app.
