Great app - user interface is smooth and reliable. Dashboards up front are configurable. Lots of ways to track workouts, health metrics, etc. And the best part is there are no annoying ads to look at. Only reason I didnt rate as a 5 star is because you only get 1 year of data tracking on the app (I'd really like to see performance across multiple years to show total progress) and the food logging doesn't seem to synch with My Fitness Pal easily. I still like this better than Stravia.

There is nothing I don't like about this app. Fantastic training aid and motivation tool. I haven't been in the gym for over nine years until now, the app helps me watch my steady progress back to fitness and takes the thinking out of warm ups and what weights to put on the bar. I paid for extra features and I think it's worth it. Thanks :-)

Been using it to wake me up for years. With the right settings, the alarm will only play when you're coming out of a deep sleep cycle, meaning you'll wake up less groggy. It's also fun to set the alarm disarm to 'Sleep Journal'. Forces you to write something to turn off the alarm, and results in a log of some pretty amusing messages.

Loved the app after using for several years. After the recent app update, the app toolbar is behind my Samsung toolbar. Am considering canceling my subscription if their update containing bugs isn't fixed within the next few weeks.
This app is sooo helpfull..i liked it very much....the logo is not similar to anyone..

App is good as a timer and mapping, however there is something wrong with how it calculates distance. I ride the same course and it gives me a different distance every time...sometimes by hundreds of meters. Also wish it had another voice option...the current one sounds like she is bored with the whole thing. Still, I like it better than others I have used..

Used to be good but then they decided to try to make the GPS distances more accurate. It now underestimates distances by 10% when compared with a car odometer for drives on flat terrain and in straight lines. Worse, it now quits tracking during runs and loses GPS altogether in places where it worked fine before. I'll uninstall pretty soon.

Was using it regularly but now unable to log in to the app. It says username or password incorrect. Tried everything. Can log into the website through the browser with the same credentials. Tried installing it in other phones as well but the result is the same. Unable to login. Hence uninstalling

App keeps getting stuck in endless sync and won't finish syncing. restart app and app is stuck on startup splash screen. watch seems to be connected to Bluetooth and I even get notifications but app won't leave splash screen.

AI picture analysis makes easier to load the information, but I don't understand why when we are focusing on the picture it shows a square frame to fit the dish inside, but the picture is a long narrow strip that ignores 2/3 of the dish content?

I gave it all the permissions it requested. I've now used it for a week & it missed so many KMs on my rides. I ride on average 14- 18km per day. The most this thing recorded was 3.48! Today, 24-04-26, I rode 14.40 km, it tracked 2.24! Definately a waste of my time effort & battery & I installing after this.

The title should reflect this is Zwift - just shows up on my device as 'Mobile Link'. Also, I am no longer getting notifications when favorites are riding. Finally, it would be great if you could change jerseys, bikes, etc. from the mobile app.

Decent app that does a fine job of tracking activities. I have used MMF since it's inception years ago. I no longer subscribe to the premium features as I just didn't see the value in the extra cost when my fitness tracker software does the same thing for free. I mainly use MMF ow to track workouts for personal challenge tracking.

Worked great for the first few months then the disconnection problems started. I have a Samsung Note 20 Ultra, a flagship device, and the Whoop 3.0 will not stay connected for more than an hour. If you so much as walk out of the room to use the bathroom, when you come back it will be disconnected and will not re-connect. You will have to go through pairing process manually over and over again, 20 times a day. Never had this kind of an issue with Fitbit or any other wearable before.
App is decent, the heart rate tracker and YEARLY $200 SUBSCRIPTION is an overpriced piece of garbage that can't track heart rate properly.

This app claims to be free and have the premium if you want more. Hardly nothing is free, nothing! There was only 2 audios that were 25 minutes free and will want you to sign up for the "free" trial. Do not sign up for the trial, I didn't thank god since I hear they will still charge you even if you cancel before the 7 days are up. No point in keeping this free might as well cut that out and just make it an app you would have to pay to begin with, so yeah dont waste your time with it.

incredibly annoying to actually log your calories in a simple way. I don't want to have to click through screens of an ai lecturing me (what they call "tips") every time I log. I don't want to have to click through the streaks stuff every time I log. just let me quickly and simply log calories without any feedback.

I'm mid 40s and new to weight lifting. The app simplifies everything and programs my progress. The only thing that isn't in the app are tips on how to perform the movements properly but you can get that on the site or from other sources. I'm going into my fourth week and feel stronger and motivated. The workouts are fun and I finish looking forward to the next session!
very disappointed with band keeps coming off, accuracy of readings and information display, compared to Garmin which is free.

Generally it's great but every now and then the Microsoft tablet version locks up and you loose what you have done. I think that was really a problem with the Surface Pro and not the phone app. It's really great when it all works because you can see exactly what you have done in terms of distance, power and heart rate afterwards. The new routes in Watopia are really great and the Tour of Watopia is always fun and good for your fitness!

Went for run, somehow app lost GPS location and couldnt record any more data (other app somehow didnt lose this info). Glitchy, random long loading times. Want to delete account but somehow i dont have internet connection? Pretty disappointed and looks like I am not the only one with this problem.

Really good app for tracking calories. Huge database of different products. If you can't find something, you can scan bar code and search by it, and if you don't find it like that, you can add on your own easily with 2 pictures of product, one should be nutrition table of product. You can even add your own recipes so it calculates for your whole meal

The workouts and trainers are pretty great, but the app is getting awful. It keeps deleting my program progress/workouts. The workout shows as logged and completed, then the next day when I come back it's gone. Also have to tediously log in every couple days for some reason. I love some of the trainers but it's about time to give up on this glitchy app. Also if a notification pings during a workout, it makes the workout volume turn all the way down which is weird.

Second edit: now the audio stops randomly even when I have internet connection. I'll be cancelling my subscription. This is gut wrenching - I have insomnia and have depended on the sleep stories. Editing my review. I've been using this app for years, and the technical issues just keep getting worse. Downloaded audio now routinely stops in the middle of the track, so I can only listen when I have a stable internet connection. So no airplane mode, no camping, no backpacking. For $70.

Biggest and most important complaint... any time you change goals and/or macros it's ALWAYS retroactive. So if you hit your weight loss goal and change to maintenance, all the past entries are changed to show the new calorie targets. One would think it would be easier, especially from a programming point of view, for the default to be "from this day forward". I can't even make a separate profile to use for my now maintenance numbers.

BOOM! update: 2026, Been a long time since I used this app (Last was 2014, then 2016). I am glad to be back on it. it's a no nonsense app for men and women that understand what lifting is for. Now at nearly 60, it makes me feel good to do these amazing compound moves and see the needle move up on my strength 💪🏽 🙌🏾

Keeps freezing whenever I try to add ingredients to the grocery list. The recipes look good, but I'm better off using Pinterest for ideas with how ridiculously slow this app runs. This would be worth it if y'all optimized your app a little more, but until then, pass. Also, the subscription price is ridiculous, and not worth 120$. For the cost of ad free Hulu for a year, this app will LET you read a recipe. Disgusting. There has to be better apps out there that aren't trying to bleed you dry.

Paid the subscription, still some animations take way too long, I need yo quickly track the food and go, the motivational screen animations take way too long, skip meal tips every time, etc. Since today, you can't log foods, search does not work, not even the settings work. It says there is a problems with the connection. Obviously there is a server issue from what I see, but locking you out of everything? Not even allowing some offline tracking or past data??? if this keeps up, why did I pay?

bit skeptical to begin with but there is peace of mind that you can log food manually if AI isn't accurate. Best app I've used so far in terms of UX. Would like to see some more widgets though, including a water tracking one that has a running total and a + button to log.

OMG, the new update app is so irritating! Changed my raiting because they took away the barcode scan option I've used free for years and added it to the premium pay option. Looks like a greedy move to me. Obviously they weren't getting enough people to purchase the premium option as it was, so they take away what was the most used feature (that was free) and move it to paid only option. Extremely disappointed in My Fitness Pal! I think it might be time to find another food logging app.

This app is great as a concept, but charging $10 month for the most critical feature is outrageous. The app used to be my go-to recipe source, but the developers cobbled the app in an attempt to squeeze as much money out of their user base as possible. I will take my business and attention elsewhere.

App was already too expensive, now the price is going up even more. Also, the app constantly runs in the background unless manually stopped. Its annoying, expensive for what you receive, and not really all that helpful. All the money subscribers pay is apparently used to pay celebrities to read bedtime stories to adults. Not helpful at all to me.
Can't even register, showing unknown error, please try again

Good functionality but ads are super annoying. I subscribe to this app. There was a bit of a learning curve to figure out the full functionality, but overall this is a really good app and I'm happy with it making calorie counting easy. I only gave it 3 (4) stars, however, because of the annoying ads in the free version I use for my husband. Every single time I want to log something I have to sit through an ad first. Basically the free version is unusable for that reason. Needs a family version.

I really like this app. I like that it doesn't have half dressed women pictured within it. It's just simple what you need to do your workout. Clean sharp design. I like that it adds reduces weigh automatically. I like graphics that picture the weights so you don't have to math it out at the gym which plates and how many on each side of the bar. I like that you can add other lifts to the routines. Keep up the good work.

1 week in with mixed reviews - I was very happy until it stopped syncing with Fitbit and continually asks to reauthenticate to no avail. The app for some reason doesn't have write capabilities to Health Connect either so I'm stuck without food tracking in any of my other apps now AND I've already paid for PRO... update - still has sync issues and now is also leaning heavy into gamification. Paired with down time and no ability to make updates to AI nutrition suggestions, I'll be moving on.
Don't buy product from this app. I had to pay additional 10 dollars while receiving this product from India more than delivery charges stated in this website

I liked it more when I first started using it years ago. I don't use all the special features. Mostly just a running tracker. Picked it over other apps because of the auto pause feature. Now it's just bloated and inconsistent, sometimes it will give mile and pace updates, sometimes not, auto pause sometimes happens when running full speed or doesn't pause when stopped. Has trouble syncing after runs so run history doesn't show up.

I'm not great with a computer or apps but this 1 was easy to figure out. I don't like having to tell it when I'm starting and finishing and when I'm taking breaks though. Other than that it works well and was easy enough for me to figure out.

Just 30 days so far and really enjoy the back and forth dialog with the AI assistant. 67 years old and I play singles picklball three times a week along with training 2-3 times to maintain my physical ability to perform playing a sport I enjoy. Whoop helps with my recovery advising me on several points along the way. I also play in tournaments and its helpful in my preparation in day's leading up to it. Diet, sleep, training intensity, recovery, etc.
The widget is borked. The information goes off the right side of the screen, and is useless. Also, there appears to be no way to view my fitbit in the app.

Unfortunately this is becoming more and more glitchy and I'm using the paid version. Coupled with only running training programs I won't be renewing as I'd prefer walking or body weight work outs. I've used this app since I had a blackberry before smartphones and it's always been amazing. Such a pity.

A great app, but some improvements I would suggest: Everytime I missclick on a diary item name (eg: "Consumed dairy?") , it opens the entry page, but when I return to the main page, it goes back to the top of the page. Kinda annoying that. Would also love to edit the names and delete (or hide) the conversations with the AI coach.

I loved this app when it showed the workouts with time based segments. It seems they changed all my saved workouts to a class based format. I was out for a few days and opened up to this disappointment. I liked being able to see the moves that would come up in the workouts before starting and prefer time-based workouts. Serious downgrade from 5 stars to 2.

Used to love this app, but it's become useless in the past few weeks. Heart points keep disappearing, data changes after being recorded, the journal never knows what day it is. For a few days some weeks ago, it wasn't showing any data at all. Eventually it came back, but it's been touch and go since then. Switched to Samsung Health, and it's been great.

God, I hate this app. When it works, it's great. But most of the time I end up in a enter-your-password loop after it's kicked me out for the 3rd time in a week. I have to use it for a business I frequent. I waste so much time trying to use mindbody. I would rather sign up for classes using quill and scroll instead of this buggy, frustrating app.

Been using this app every day to track my calories for the past few years and had no issues, it used to be the best free one of its kind around. But within the past year it's gotten significantly worse in every way. Redundant pop-ups and features you can't turn off, one ad after the other, UI changes that hurt the eyes. Now that you can no longer track your food ahead of time without paying I'm uninstalling. Ridiculous to lock such basic features that used to be free (!) behind a Paywall.
Great diet helper. barcode scanner is a really useful feature and the app can be used for free I haven't felt too much is locked behind a pay wall

If I could give a zero star review I would. This app offered me a 7 day free trial, and so I accepted the offer, and they charged me the free for the yearly subscription anyway. I clicked the offer for a 7 DAY FREE TRIAL and was IMMEDIATELY charged over $80. This app is a god damn scam.

stuck in a cycle of pressing join/sign in, entering my account info, pressing continue, and being sent back to the join/sign in screen. tried re-installing the app and making an account with a different email to no avail. nike you are testing my patience

A really great app for booking classes at favourite studios. However, a couple points of feedback for the developers: 1) the search / filter feature requires work (e.g. studios I know should appear in the search results often don't); 2) there doesn't seem to be any benefit in adding teachers / instructors as favourites as the app doesn't show upcoming classes in their profile - please do fix this as it'd be great to know the schedule of my favourite teachers!

**MUST HAVE** A must have app for zwifters as this makes it easy to join events and give out ride ons quick and easily and make profile adjustments. Also great to add friends on Zwift - if your app don't load after the latest update just uninstall the app and reinstall it works fine after that 👍🏼
Since the application has been updated and it's the worth application I've ever used too slow too heavy and always i need to change the country manually

I have contacted the developer several times over the years about one issue that is blatantly incorrect. On the users workout page there is a section called ELEVATION (Ft), the elevation that is shown is in METERS, not FEET. Either change the label to meters or convert the values to feet (multiply by 3.28) Everytime I see this page it irks the hell out of me, I've brought this up before when feedback was requested, and nothing was ever done. Please fix this undisputable error! Thanks

I absolutely loathe the transfer from the FitBit app. My watch doesn't seem to actually sync, track sleep, steps, calories and is not user friendly. I may toss my fitbit and swap to something else soon if things don't change.

I've used this app for nearly 10 years. It no longer works after the last few updates. The alarm doesn't go off anymore, which made me late to work a few times. I've stopped using it and switched to Alexa since I can't trust it. I white listed it in the background and it STILL doesn't work. Very sad, because I always loved the app and trusted it completely. No more.
I love how easy it is to use. I can easily search for recipes, create meal plans and generate a shopping list. And its FREE.

get an amazfit band instead - $100 one time cost, no subscription fee. this review is for the service generally, not app specifically. 1 - f any service that makes it so damn impossible to cancel! I HATE that, always the worst sign. 2 - I liked it generally, but for the whole 1-2 years I had it, thought "man this is so overpriced...why aren't there competitors?". Well now there are, so just swap off this! A lifetime with most competitors is less than a year with whoop, with little diff.
Whoop is amazing, love the sleep data and daily stress data. Really helps me understand what's impacting my life!
Every time I try to make an account it puts a green arrow next to the password, username, and email but when I click create an account it says there was an error and I should check my typing.

A lot has changed since my last review, when the app was constantly interrupted by popups for summaries, streaks, etc.. It improved but still needs work though. The streak feature still cannot be turned off, but it now only appears once a day for the first meal input. And the AI suggestions are not useful and intrusive, just another popup to click away. Please make stuff like that a setting. Otherwise, lot's of other useful feature, a good database and good overviews.
This app is helpful to measure the distance I travelled and it is very well in doing it's work.

I have been using this app for several years, and recently it has nothing but giving me problems. Need to fix the issues. The Grams or Oz's wont show up on the food you pick have to go back 2 -3 times for it to acknowledge it. Second issue the schedule calories for certain days doesn't work. Have have 1600 on Monday, Wednesday and Friday. Other calories for the rest of the week, yet it doesn't acknowledge that either ? Maybe time to look for a new app. To back i liked this one.
The app is good, but I'm really sad because of the apple's lawsuit, your logo does not even look like an apple, leaving a 5 star review because I support small apps and businesses

I began using MFP last August. I loved how easy it was to use and the interface, tabs, ect. were perfect in my opinion. This new update is far from that. Why "fix" what isn't broken? To add, it DOUBLE logs my workouts that are tracked/linked with my Samsung Health app. So when I track a walk on my Samsung Health app, it logs the workout TWICE. I had to go back each day for the last week to delete these additional logs. It's quite clear that EVERYONE dislikes the new update. Listen and fix it.
too slow to open from home screen on 128kbps connection and my email address in the first text box did not open an account.

Do not recommend. signed up for the annual plan for 3 people, not user friendly at all! They used to have great customer service but that disappeared & was replaced by AI. cant cancel, they keep billing you, When I signed us up for annual plans the upgrades were "supposed" to be included but that was not what happened. Loved the product and app but everything else was not worth the hassle. Looking forward to the fitbit air.

It's great. It tracks well and it has various activities to track, like running, cycling, kayaking, swimming etc. The only sad part is that you need premium to get coaching exercise plans. Other than that it's great!

Oof, not a great start. Spent all day yesterday getting used to the new interface, and then today the "Today" tab is showing my stats from yesterday. It's only when I click into the tiles that I see my current info. I've tried refreshing, etc., nothing works. What's going on? So many redundant stats, too, within the tabs. And how can I get rid of the Running tile? I'm not a runner and don't need that. Google, please listen to the people on this one. There are a lot of legitimate issues.

Love the discounts and promo's. Some products are same price, or higher than other stores. I don't like when I'm doing my checkout and I want more than 1 of something, it brings me always back to the beginning. So, if I want 5 of something at checkout, it's best to go back to the shopping to update.

Great at the beginning really informative insights and data. However the strap no longer automatically connects and in order to sync data you have to keep the app open on your screen (no longer updates in the background). Especially frustrating in the mornings and a waste of time. In my opinion, you should stick with your Garmin until this is further developed and all bugs are fixed.

I've used this app several years & loved it. I'm a type 1 diabetic and use it to keep track of calories consumed but more importantly to figure the number of carbs consumed in order to calculate my insulin boluses. I always thought it was a great app. However, I'm not happy with it since the last update. I miss the lists of food categories, restaurants and brands. It takes too long to enter each item. (My son says it hurts to watch me) Bring back the lists, please. I wish I could un-update it.
I can't quite believe how bad this is compared to it's previous incarnation (Fitbit) - what on earth were they thinking. All I want to do is track my runs. Dreadful.

This app isn't special in any way. It is just simply good. The price for premium account is way too high and isn't worth it. I would not pay more than 40$ per year or probably 3.99$ a month. I enjoyed a few guided meditations. Not all narrators are good. My favorite one was with LeBron James, but the series was VERY short. The app itself is laggy and lacks perfection, it is hard to find the specific meditation you need.

i dont like that it makes me feel guilty if I havent logged for a couple of days. what changed it asks. I want to log when I can...even 4 days a week will see where Im failing. each time it starts from scratch. also the ai is not great.

I want to like the app but wish i could do a training plan that will auto adjust. I hear that used to be a feature, I'm not sure why it was removed. App either freezes up and have to force close it or, if you go to another app (i.e. a music player) and come back in the middle of a workout, it ends the workout and you have to start all over again.

Pretty good, still wish that there was a better more thorough explanation of the data collected because after using it for over a year I have some interesting charts, but little context for them. Also, quit trying to get me to install another pay by the year app for cloud storage. I'm not doing it as I've already paid for the app once

I downloaded the app cause I was told I needed it to be able to book a yoga class through it. When I search for the studio it doesn't even show up. The location is not even more than 50kms away yet it is not even on there(and no it's not spelled wrong). I have tried different approaches but no success.

Absolutely loved this app when I first installed it but the latest update is FULL of bugs! I can't update my saved workout routines because it doesn't allow more than 1 character entry in the weight field. And I can't create new routines due to the same problem. Please fix this!

The app itself is very nice. My aunt bought me a Calm puzzle that included a free month subscription, and it has lots of different things to listen to. I love listening to rain soundscapes when it's raining. But, the biggest downside is the ridiculous paywall. It's 60 dollars for this paywall to disappear (I can't remember if it's monthly or yearly) and if you have the free version, you can't access anything at all.

App worked great for about 6 weeks, but I've had nothing but problems the past 3 weeks. Runs not being mapped. Very inaccurate distances. It crashed at the end of my long run today, no data saved, nothing. This app has become incredibly frustrating. It's whole point is to track runs and it can't do that right now. Hopefully an update fixes everything soon or I'm going to switch to a different app that actually works consistently.
Great app, keeps me on track and focused on my health/fitness goals. Since getting the app I have seen improvements in my body composition and eating habits.

Suddenly stopped tracking my step no matter what i do. Before this function perfectly but now no longer working. My built-in apps work perfectly fine but unfortunately cannot sync with othwr apps that i need to used.

After the update drowned in errors to the point of uselessness. Recents disappeared. Everything takes a few seconds to load. Logging in takes absurd amount of time. "TimeoutException: Future not completed" and other errors. No, it's not "my slow internet connection", actually. But hey, you added more intrusive ads, so usability doesn't matter. I was thinking about subscribing, but these practices tell me that things will only get worse for everyone. It was a great app, RIP.

Dont waste your time! This app was incredible. Until it wasn't. I used it for a bit over a year & it worked perfectly. Loved how much data you get from each workout, it cheers you on, the guided runs & advice to make you better. Then it stopped tracking. Gets stuck on 0:08 mins & nothing fixes it. Uninstalled/reinstalled, logged out/in, customer support is clueless. I'm sorely disappointed.

I love the app, I track my Calories and it helps tons. But my only problem is that it doesnt seem to have IN N OUT in their catalog. And personally I love IN N OUT TONS, and have it pretty frequently and not being able to track it makes its pretty difficult for me to stay on track with my goals #AddINNOUT

It has an extensive selection of activities, and a search option; which makes it easy to find your sport. Too bad it gets worse with every update. During my bike ride, after around 500 calories, whenever I slow down, it starts counting calories backwards; meaning, it subtracts calories.

I have used this app for years but the new update that occurred mid April 2026 is terrible to the point where I'm deleting it. It's not "oh it's new and I have to get used to it." No, they've made substantial changes that make it so unfriendly to use. I am now clicking more & more just to add my food & water intake. They use grey font on black background, it's hard to see your net calories total due to this. They separated breakfast, lunch, etc- you don't have access to all the info on one page.

This app is not good if you forget to save your workout. Endamondo was so much better as when you finished your workout it automatically saved it. Also not the easiest of apps to use when trying to find out the correct workout option! Again endamondo was so much better and easy to use. Also doesn't sync with my smart watch not enough options for all the new devices.

Get a good armband for your phone and your set. I've been tracking workouts (anything from mountain biking in the Rockies to Running on a beach in South America) for years. You can take pictures and add them to your workout as well. So it's nice to look back and remember things from years past I keep this as my workout and vacation diary. To address some other users... ive used this with a Samsung Galaxy S5, 6, 7, 8, 9 and now 10. It's worked well with every phone.

User unfriendly and riddled with bugs. Main issues: when in the middle of a route out exercising, all i see on the map is a blue dot on a white or green background, no other detail. Useless without the red line to indicate where you've come from so far. Such a sloooow app: everything takes ages. Opening up, changing activity, trying to save when finished. The app seems to have to think twice about everything. Weekly totals don't add up correctly. Not rocket science to get that right. Uninstall.

I used this years ago before getting a fitbit & switching to their app. I loved it back then. Just re-downloaded due to Google rolling out their half finished Health app to replace fitbit. After seeing that the barcode scanner is no longer available in the free version, I have deleted this without logging a thing. It is all but useless without being able to scan. Both of these apps I used to use and love have eliminated so many features I might as well long with pen and paper.
Very convenient to shop, and delivery tends to be fast. I know it was more difficult during the recent lockdown, but it's pretty much back to it's past performance pre-COVID.

I can see how this may be a good app for some cases, but, unfortunately, for me it did not solve my main problem: simplifying input, despite many options available. Almost every product from my region has no convenient serving size, only values per 100g. Textual input (this is where I had high hopes) is a gamble. It didn't work out for me, but I believe the app has potential. Especially if free text input becomes more reliable.
The AI can be a bit confused with time and date which give inaccurate recommendations. lots of correcting.

Ohhh mann. New interface on phone is unnecessary complicated. I am using endomondo for 5-6 years for running and cycling. Maybe new ui is useful for some sports that I am not practicing? Anyway I really don't like hybrid of map and workout information. If I need map, sliding to other screen was perfectly fine, but more often when I need orientation I switch to some other app with better maps(like OSM). I really miss average speed when I am running and stop button should be there(it is good idea to stop workout after holding button for couple seconds). Also I can still accidental pause workout, so it didn't solve much. Awesome UI/UX on the phone was something that used to distinguish endomondo from its competitors, I hope you will get again on track. And maybe you should focus more on improving web interface?

loved stronglifts and bought the power pack years ago. just started training again, and find it's now subscription only. things change I know, I don't mind purchasing but like many will never pay a subscription. such a shame. goodbye strong lifts, let me know if you can ever purchase it outright again. from one of your first customers

I want to like this app i really do. It does not consistently track simple workouts. It often stops mid workout and registers nothing. I have been through the trouble shooting guide. it is not GPS, i have high accuracy GPS on 100% of the time, it is not power saver, it is not battery, What it is, is inconsistent and frustrating, Now i remember why i had deleted this app a few years ago.

App no longer works without all these permissions: contacts, calendar, phone logs, text messages, location, camera, microphone, nearby devices, other accounts on phone. This was not the case when I bought my Garmin. You really don't *have to* mine and sell customer data. You really *could* just make great products. Sleep tracking is the worst on market even with great sensors. Thinks I've slept 158 hours straight although I've logged many activities during same timeframe. Misc sync issues.
it gives me all the data I want. the only downside is it can be a bit of a battery drain.

Website and products are great, app stinks. But be prepared for email marketing ad nauseam if you want the discounts. You cant choose anything except ALL or NONE. Sometimes as many as four or more per day. I have complained about this several times for several years but they never change the options.
Forced Gamification, it's got a really patronizing, non skippable streak and hint pages. A calorie tracker has to be frictionless, not annoy the user in an attempt to increase engagement

all I had to do was click dismiss, and the challenge I was supposed to have to do never timed out. It just stayed on the screen for hours as I slept in. This app is awesome in so many ways. I love it! It has no problems save one: it will not run on my Samsung Tab4 Tablet. Very disappointed as it would be easier to see on my Tablet than my iPhone.

This app is greatly improved with the addition of Ryan Flaherty's strength program. I am repeating his six week program because I can't find anything else as good. It's challenging, I feel stronger, I'm not bored, and it feels curated and intentional. I would pay for more programs like that. My only note is that some of the circuits aren't practical in a public gym because you can't monopolize more than one machine/bench at a time. Nonetheless, 10/0, give me more. Also fix your Spotify plugin.

Former 5 star app, now 1 star that's locked behind paywall. Like others have mentioned recently, this used to be a great app for tracking workouts and lifts. Disappointed that the lifetime Power Pack was disabled after only a few years of support. Appreciate the program, but time to move on. Pen and paper work just as well.
I can't access to my account through the app, many tries, changed password, uninstall the reinstall and still nothing. If I want use my account have to go website

Overall having a really good experience with this. Wish I could go back and look at all the other training options instead of being restricted to the program I'm currently pursuing without going into the Zwift app.

I agree with the last review I just read you can't get nothing done it was fine I should have just left it alone it sucks now bottom line all the app does is aggravate you so I'm not buying anything from that app

I love the live stream classes offered by Alkalign! It has really helped me deal with all the work/family/pandemic stress now that we are all cooped up. I also love that if I can't make the live class I signed up for, the studio will send me a link to the recorded version of the class and I can do the workout anytime later in the week. It's super convenient and allows me to never miss a workout! I also like trying the fantastic, supportive and different instructors from all over the country.

I have had this app installed for about a week. It is not counting my steps correctly, it is missing easily half of my daily steps compared to Samsung fitness app. I have now uninstall edit this app as it is unreliable.

relatively useless for training. no logging for weights? thousands of videos of preset workouts but no functional intuitive way to log your numbers.

Brilliant app, love Garmin kit and have used for nearly 20 years now. This app works almost seamlessly with watches and scales that we have, and makes tracking my fitness training and health very easy. I love the livetrack feature, excellent for solo runs in the mountains.

This app used to be one of the best out there. It literally got me through covid and was my go to place for exercise routines. Easy, to work with and understand. Detail analysis of workout with recommendations. Love this App.

I HATE waking up in the morning. "Simple math" setting actually requires my brain to function to shut up my alarm. It definitely works, and it's making me better at math. I've even set a 3x snooze function. I haven't even tried the other settings, but there's tons.

Easy to use. Automatically adds workouts on My Fitness Pal, which saves time and effort logging from a different device, like Fitbit. Shows lower calorie burn than Fitbit when tracking same workout, but may be due to no HR monitor. However, seems more accurate with distance when running or walking than Fitbit. Overall will keep using it.

i believe i would enjoy it but everytime i go to do something the app closed out on me. idk if its the phone or the app but had to take it off

been using for 2yrs now with annual subscription. I've never had any issues with the app having ads or anything like that. I can plan my run ahead of time if I want or just do whatever I want. It is easy to use and fun! Love it!!!!

update. I was connected to an actual customer support rep and they were responsive and gave me suggestions. this should be the dog wotists, But for all the amazing watches they make, the app is lacking. to track nutrition you have to pay, I did and it is the worst tracker I've used and went back to lose it. Excellent app! So easy to use and the free version has everything I need to track my eating habits and lose weight. Easy to install, were easier to setup. Only had to follow the prompts and I was ready to go. I'm learning as I go but I'm surely impressed with the different reports generated.

Great app to follow on your activity. Especially like functionality of creating your own routes in advance so you can easily track once on the way and save on time as no need to check maps. Not only are there banner ads, now ads will take up the whole screen, in the middle of you typing in a food. And it forces you to wai to skip. Haven't used for a while, but I don't remember that being a thing. And 5$ a month to turn off ads? LOL I'll be looking for a less greedy alternative.

update: the app delivers nice tracking incl a lot of insights and metrics! I loved the app instantly and bought it. Huge thanks Petr for fixing the issues with Garmin integration so quickly. It works perfectly now. Definitely five stars again!

I do this workout 3 months before going on holiday and it reaps fantastic results everytime. The app is easy to use, and definitely helps you learn a healthier way to eat. Don't go on a diet, change your diet. And this app helps with that if you're willing to use it consistently.

New-ish issue. When tracking my calorie intake, the food section does not update with my calories out and just says I'm over budget (0 expended), even though I'm not. I've tried force stopping the app and clearing the cache to no avail. Could we add a refresh button here or just fix the auto update functionality? When it works, the calorie tracking is much better than other apps I've tried. (Note I'm on Android) Thanks!

The app displays useful information, but the user interface needs a lot of work. It could be more intuitive, cleaner, and easier to navigate. Adding AI integration would make the app much more valuable by helping users understand their data, explaining trends, and recommending useful actions based on their activity and health metrics.

This daily nutrition tracker is good at one of the two things it does. It's very good at finding food and very easily logging your daily intake. As far a monitoring food and caloric intake goes, I love it for being super smooth, fast and intuitive (better than Noom and LoseIt!) but for tracking weight and body fat it's so basic - it borders on non-existent. It also should track exercise - which it     doesn't do at all. Looking forward to future enhancements.

Love the app but on some days it crashes too often. Also struggles to load when the internet connection is slow. Other than that, a very good and useful app delivering tremendous value and deserving 4-5*. It would be very handy if a repeat item could be created by swiping the item right (same way as copy item) instead having to go to the dedicated menu.
Will not let me log in. Keeps saying incorrect username and password.

Google ruined Fitbit. After it forced me to "upgrade," it took away the most valuable part of the app: the calorie deficit calculator. That one feature made it so easy to track how many calories you had left to eat in a day, without having to look up your calories burned/consumed and subtract your target deficit. Now they overwhelm and clutter the screen with lists of macro percentages and repetitive "hints " that you can't disable. Stupid. Fix it.

It's very convenient to use amd I like the workout details it provides. I do sometimes forget to turn it on or off. I wish there was a way to have it on and automatically detect the workout and its details whenever I workout and if the phone is on me.

Good app of it worked. Everything is fine minus the fact that it wont save runs which is the main point. Im new to the app and over several runs it just didnt save any of them but does all the other tracking. When trying to manually add runs, it doesn't save them. The other features are great but don't mean much if progress isn't tracking.

This app opens by itself each morning. Yesterday I made sure that I turned off notifications and other permissions. This morning the app was open when I turned on my tablet. This concerns me, as it seems to be a bit shady. So I must delete the app.
Definitely needs more of the web based options!
Just use the basic features, no coaching or equipment. I don't look at the ads. Glitches once in a long time. Overall it's enough.

The app starts off alright but then I find that it disconnects from your in game experience. Would also be better if you had a specific place to see your friend list, Change you bikes and wheels without entering the game and also a DIY route builder with out having to steer at every turn.

Been working great, however, recently in my last couple of group/team rides some chat has been missing...what I mean is that I can see most chat, but I can also see replies to chat that doesn't show on my group chat screen. Is this a fault that needs fixed or is there an update I have missed? Please help. Love the companion app for the ride ons!

Been a loyal Endomondo user for years but I can't anymore. Recently the GPS has stopped working EVERY single time, I've reinstalled the app, tried everything. Every other app knows my location immediately and doesn't drop, Endomondo has messed something up and if it isn't fixed pronto they're going to lose customers, there's too many other options out there. Glad to see this is a problem many people have, they need to act on this fast. Got a generic email basically saying it's my fault!!

App is unreliable and frankly useless. Came to read the reviews and so glad I'm not alone. I tried the app on 2 different phones, uninstalled and reinstalled, restarted both phones... and still get the same problem: it won't sync/recognize the pass I paid for so I can schedule classes. Have had to book directly through the fitness company via phone which completely defeats the purpose of this app. Already reached out to the customer service/ support email. So frustrating!
this app is lagging, twice now it didn't calculate my run, it always signs me out whenever i am done and when i log in back my activity is not logged, use at your own fustrated risk

Definitely one of my favourite fitness apps. I love the variety of exercises. I recommend people starting a fitness journey to sample different exercises and decide what works for them. And you get all this for free! However, lately I can't sign into my account. The 8 digit code that is supposed to be sent to your email when you've loggged out for a while never makes it to my inbox. I have tried several times and I check my spam too. Nothing. I'd appreciate some help.

For an app rated and priced the way this one is, I expect a much better UI. I want the app to check and make sure I'm awake a few times after I do the captcha to turn off the alarm. If there is a way to do this, I wouldn't know because none of the options under "wake-up check" are explained very well. I used Alarmy before this. I can set up an alarm within three minutes with Alarmy. With Sleep as Android, it takes a minimum of 10. Otherwise, a very good alarm with many options to customize.
I would really like to use this app... but I can't sign in. My username and password works on my Xbox and the actual daily burn app on my phone. This is the only place it doesn't work.

I have enjoyed using this app for years, I purchased the lifetime power pack and now when I attempt to use the app it appears that the lifetime power pack is being converted to a subscription. I liked the features I had, and that's what I paid for. I understand wanting more revenue, however there's no reason to stop customers who have already paid from accessing the functionality they purchased. I see dev saying it has been 12 years, but it was advertised as a lifetime purchase, not 12 years.

Pretty great app!! I love using the noise recording feature, as I sleep talk a lot, and there have been some very funny moments that I have caught. The only thing is that when I start the sleep tracking, there is a high pitched buzzing/shrieking sound that gives me a mild headache. I'm sure there's a way to fix it that I just can't find haha. Otherwise love using the app!!!
Very ugly app, disappointing it can go over a year with these problems and still no update for all of 2012

New app is awful! They removed a lot of useful features. I can't even look my old stats for a day at one time anymore I have to do it individually now which is a pain. They also took away the BMI feature from when you log your weight and now I can't input just the number of calories I ate it has to be a product. I don't understand why they decided to badly change a perfectly good app. I've had a fitbit for 5+ years and this definitely makes me feel like switching from fitbit.

I've been using this app for many years and find that any GPS glitches are more likely due to issues with the phone or the way I have it set up, eg: battery save mode. Only wish there was a way I could click on a point in time, elevation point, etc., and see that point on the map.

I've used this app for years to lose or maintain weight so it must be good. Lots of features with no notable ads all for free. If you're a developer, they've also got an API for free and you can use that to download all your data. If I've had one complaint it's that I don't like that the calendar begins with Monday but that's grasping for straws. Nonetheless I (still) highly recommend this app! I'm still using this app years more later. I love it! I can't recommend it enough!

This app is simply AWFUL and makes me not want book any classes! It's confusing, cumbersome, makes no sense at all. The website is also horrible! You need to test these apps on real users before releasing them! When you do book a class, and "add to calendar" it defaults to the San Diego time zone, adding incorrect information to the schedule (or the evening before here in Australia). I could go on but there isn't enough room here.

I am used to using Strava, but find this app to be inaccurate compared to Strava, especially in regard to elevation gained data. The app will overide elevation data recorded by my garmin device and sometimes provide an elevation gained that is over twice what it should be. The Garmin is correct based on eatablished elevations and this app incorrect.

not happy with this new merge. All my data that was collected with fitbit all this time did not transfer to Google, whatever its name is. Everything I was tracking simply got lost, especially my cycle health and sleep health. all that is missing. Like honestly 😒 this isn't working for me. If you make something because you say it is better, at least ensure that all data collected over time does not get lost. there is a reason why we are using this watch... to track certain health concerns.

I was using this app to train for 5k. It was recording that I was running 4k and I thought I was ready for 5K social club in my community. This app was indicating 3.89k on a 2.60k mark. I was disappointed with that. It means all the time I was running about 3k instead of 4k tracking on the app. Not accurate at all .

I don't usually leave reviews, but I would like to state this update absolutely bugged my calories (UI prior was fine too). It was perfectly fine this morning and now every day I've logged has 5,000 calories in surplus. I've tried reinstalling but that didn't work-- it's permanently logged multiple entries that weren't there before. I understand I'm one frustrated out of many, many long-term users who may be fine with this, but I'm not. I'm moving onwards; good luck, MFP.
It works amazingly! It helps me keep track of my calories and make sure I dont go over the limit. But the only flaw is that you have to upgrade when you want workout systems.  😊

it mostly works well, but having app notifications enabled by default means that every time I install a new app that sends notifications, if I don't want to receive them on my watch, I must manually find and disable them in the app. It's a convoluted enough process, that I often have to Google it to remember how.

Whoop has much more robust data than other fitness trackers I've used (Fitbit, Garmin, and Samsung). For a product where being data-oriented is the main value prop, it is surprisingly hard to just... View your raw data. I get that it takes time and consistent logging to generate insights, but the way the UI is organized is too centered on individual days. It's also insane to me that I can't log anything in my journal until the following day. Why wouldn't users be encouraged to log as we go?!
Glitches a lot and doesn't work correctly and accurately. Whoop need to investigate the app.

App is really good. Easy to use, has everything I need to simply track my calorie intake. My only problem sometimes some of my foods doubles. Like I put I had 2 snacks and after sometime app shows 4. Also would be nice if there was option for homemade food, I put all ingredients, how many servings in that dish and app counts how many calories in 1 serving.

I can't believe how terrible all calorie counter apps have become. Overcomplicated, handholding. I know my food, I know what to enter. And this app doesn't let me manually add an entry with calories and macros. It also doesn't let me adjust existing entries. If you want a custom entry, you have to create a whole recipe. I am losing my mind. Refunded.

Good tracker, with a few quibbles. Band is comfy, I do tend to switch which wrist it's on each time I shower though to prevent any issues. Data tracking for HR is mostly solid. I've had a few bizarre readings three or four times over 6 months where it's showing me at crazy high hr for 10mins or so. Annoyingly these can't be deleted properly so they throw out your daily stats. Sleep/recovery data is the main useful aspect for me. Good to see if I'm over training etc.

Great when it works but I seem to get random disconnects, especially when doing training sessions. I have disabled smart network etc. I will try airplane mode next time to see if it helps. This isn't constant but sometimes worse than others

I gave three stars because the app is good. Constant notices to upgrade to family plans or share it with someone else are annoying. But now I see that they have created a whole new app, and of course, a whole new subscription dedicated to sleep. That's half of the reason I got this app in the first place and now they want to charge for an additional subscription. I hope it is worth it to them; they just lost a paying customer due to trying to squeeze out more money from dedicated customers.

would be nice if you had more options for the weight lifting workouts, no belt squat, only one option for hack squat, no bench press machine, no adductor machine, no abductor machine as an example. also no real way to sub mit requests in app.

I've been using this app for years. I enjoy having something to track how many hours I sleep. All the other features aren't available with the free version. Also I can't link it to my Google fit app because Google keeps blocking it for trying to access sensitive data. Overall not impressed and might switch to another sleep app.
Keeps losing connection to the watch so I have to repair it (Fenix 5s) I would not mind this, if it didnt ask me to set it up like a new device every. single. time.

I hate the experience of not being able to pair my watch to other phones...this for sure is a software issue that needs update...I tried to remove the device from the app, cleared app data, cleared cache, reset the watch, reinstalled all the garmin apps, forget bluetooth connections, restarted my phone, restarted the watch...i tried literally everything watched multiple youtube tutorials but got no success pairing the watch with my phone...This by far is the most awful from all watches I own...
Fantastic!! But i can't sign up in your app
This new update is horrible!!! Takes forever to load. It's more like a webpage now and not an app. There was nothing wrong with the old setup.

This app has worked great for me. I love the level of customization available for the alarms. Summer storm is my personal favorite. The gradual onset alarm really does make waking up easier. I don't actually use the sleep tracking much, but the few times I have, it seemed accurate enough. Overall, an amazing quality app that's also free! I know I'm not taking advantage of all the features, but I've had it for over a year (I think) and I always keep it on my homepage for easy accessibility.👍👍❤❤

Excellent guided runs and curated training plans for all distances. Great variety of everyday easy/recovery runs, short runs, long runs, speed runs and everything inbetween. This app and the Nike run coaches have helped me to reconnect with my love of running.

Worked perfect before updating. Now half way through a ride it stops showing the mileage and once saved the miles aren't there. Very annoying and I'm looking at other apps that will record the mileage like yours once did. Also like to add I don't have an option for horse riding anymore so I have to record it as mountain biking. Such a shame as the app was great before.
Love this app! Easy to use, great recommendations and I love the meal planning.

I think it will be an OK food diary. My complaint is that it's frustrating to add your own recipes since almost all the food is measured in single serving amounts instead of giving the choice for large amounts (that is without a lot of calculations - like how many teaspoons in 3 cups) for a full recipe for a family. If you don't want to add your own family recipes it would probably be fine.
No way to custom add a food or item... really is a must for moat of us
Been using Sleep by Urban Droid since it launched. When it works, it works. It often flatlines even though I set my phone to never interrupt it's activity.

This app (now Google Health) does not accurately measure sleep length and other indicators. When I wake up in the middle of the night, and fall back asleep, the app will register that incident as my wake-up time, giving me incorrect sleep quality scores. Editing my wake-up time manually does not adjust this. Having accurate sleep information has been important for my health and wellness. I hope this glitch gets fixed.
Less options and promos than before. Not as user friendly as it was. Being a member kinda only pays off for shipping and tax.

I've found this app works great for me, at home in Seattle and awesome travelling in Massachusetts and Hawaii for work. I use the app for finding yoga studios mostly. I find attending classes in different places to be enriching, and the classes populate directly to my calendar. Love it, if it's on my calendar I'm far more disciplined!

It has been a good app as I search inner peace and balance in my life. I bought the premium version and enjoyed it quite a bit untill I found Insight Timer. A free app with same intention but a lot better. You can take classes, join groups, choose voices, languages and keep track of your progress. Simply perfect. I did not mind paying for Calm and its premium bennefits, I simply found a better app with a lot more to offer. I dont plan on requesting a refund as I have been a member for a few months.

The app works great for tracking the route, distance, and calories burned and lays it out in an easy to read way with a map and elevation change chart. Also, it pairs seamlessly with the MyFitnessPal app for exercise tracking. I like that it can also be used for machine workouts such as treadmills and ellipticals too. I also love that there is a website where you can see you monthly progress.

So very broken. Glitchy, confusing, and the developers are constantly revamping the app. (I think they're still trying to figure out how to monitize.) Your shopping list is unreliable, with items disappearing and reappearing randomly. The backend servers seem sluggish and unresponsive, because sometimes the app won't load your lists or recipes. It also has a walled garden model, so you can't share/download any, except with other people with the same app. Though you can print from their website.

The website and the app are not user friendly Tried shopping from my phone and it jumps around. Went to the laptop, there I have to scroll through everything, can't really get down to a customized list to look at. So I download the app, it's worse! Type in gluten-free I get a blank screen, click on gluten-free I get a blank screen.

love the food logging feature. makes it faster. scanner works great, creating custom foods recipes very easy. connects to my fitbit, now google health app awful now for loggingfoods, seamlessly. I see everything I need to see.

They completely updated and changed the fitbit app to whatever this is. It is very confusing. There is no way to see overall metrics for a single day. It shows by week Sunday to Saturday and there is no apparent way to change it. You have to set a goal even if you don't use the app for goal setting. The app automatically sets workout type and is usually wrong. It worked fine before. Why fix something that isn't broken?? Updated to 1-star after getting a generic AI slop response.

Edit: welp, they've removed support for power pack purchases so I guess I'll have to move on. I'd rather code my own app than pay a subscription indefinitely tbh. 😒 ............ Old review: Been using this for years. I have the lifetime upgrade from a while back. It's excellent, everything you'd need for starting strength training.

WARNING: App will recommend introductory offers but does not specify when there is an expiration date to use the class passes. And MindBody will not provide refunds (full or partial) because they are only an intermediary between you and the business providing the class. Otherwise it's a good app. But that's a pretty inexcusable design flaw that is effectively false advertising.

I've never had a particularly great opinion about Nike. As a runner, their gear's never felt great. As I'm getting older though, I've found myself needing to do more strength training to keep up, and I found this app incredibly easy to navigate and the workouts are structured incredibly! It's pretty scary knowing where to start, and this was insanely helpful. It's probably time to reevaluate some Nike gear 😮‍💨

Doesn't work very well. It crashes often when I lock my phone even though I setup the phone not to stop this app when locked, and it only uses the GPS when it's in high accuracy mode - but it doesn't say that. If you're not on high accuracy mode it'll just say there is no GPS connectivity.

If you're in New Zealand, this is the Calorie Counter App (and so much more) for you. Go Pro, it's worth the annual fee. I've tried other apps including a very popular W W one and this blows it out out the water. Add foods they do not have easily. Add regular foods to easy to capture meals. Has a basic link to your fitness tracker. Easy to use. Highly Recommended!!!

I use the free version and enjoy it a lot. a lot of foods are on here and if you want to just copy a meal from one day to the next you can (I wish there was a way to change it once copied in case one ingredient is different but, as far as I can tell, if one is different you need to put each ingredient in separately again and save it under a different meal). I also wish the free version let you track water but i believe you need to pay for that.

it is okay. it is useful when travelling and you are looking to book something in. A recent update has removed the ability to see how many people are in class once booked, this is a terrible idea. if classes are busy it is nice to know in advance and mentally prepare for that, or be able to book into a quieter one. I imagine businesses would appreciate that too as spreading the load is helpful for them and their instructors.

Use this for tracking walking, it is easy to use and was accurate, but no longer. I still love the app design and associated website. However the accuracy seems to have gone to pot recently. So I am now using another app which seems to give much more consistent and reliable results.

do not buy. it can't keep connected to your phone .. pretty sure this is just a scam just got one with a 1 year subscription off the site and they knew that on my log in but they still require a credit card. dark patterns aren't customer friendly. shows weak product lead growth if they assume their product doesn't have the kind of stickiness it needs to earn my credit card. now I can't log in because they stored my user name... low quality app here

4.23.26 - The new update is awful. You can't easily see your macros at a glance anymore to balance them out, adding meals is tedious, there's no easy way to visually track your status through the day or plan ahead. I'm seriously considering disputing the fee for my membership because I'm not going to use this anymore The website offers minimal features. The premium subscription is not worth the money at all. Everything is so difficult to find and tedious to create.
App updated on 6/1/2023 and now it will not allow me to log in. Reset password and still nothing.

so far they're amazing! I just wish that after you create a custom meal, you'd be able to alter the quantity of the meal you had depending on the day instead of altering the ingredients. when it's all together idk how many grams of each veggie or such I'm having, I just know I'm having 200g of the recipe. and then other days I wanna have 150g, others days 300g. Edit: there is an option for that! I was just doing custom meal every time instead of custom recipe. thanks for the reply!! 5 stars now

I found the new update has taken an app that was easy to use for logging my meals and made it far more difficult. I often wait to log my last meal until the next day and I'm finding it more difficult and confusing to do that now. I understand there is always a learning curve when changes happen but it's been awhile and am still finding myself feeling frustrated after the update. I have to constantly change the data to show "today" when checking my nutrients and macros because it defaults toweek.
great idea but impossible to use. doesn't allow you to check out and finish your order. tried a few times and just ended up using the website instead.
I love building workouts but I wish there was an option to pick either per hand weight or combined weight for dumbell exercises.

This is one of the worst applications I worked with. Updates of sport activities take a lot time to be displayed and challenges for which I installed this app just don't work. They have never loaded without problems, 50% of time don't load at all and for other 50 it takes around a minute to load.updating challenge leaderboard after adding activity takes about 30 minutes, so there's no rivaling. The ladder board shows just top few players and few around you so you can't even see how others do.

Used to be Great app for diy fitness at home, but the app makes you "continue with your email" every time and the process is a little glichy so it takes about 30 seconds to sign in everytime. It used to not be like this so hopefully they change it back. Now they change the workout to class format which makes talks all the time and you can't see what workout is next. I liked it way better when you could preview the workout and see all the moves

So why after benefitting from playing the Post Malone Circles song on a loop, can I no longer find that song anywhere in your app? I've loved your app, and this song happened to be one of the few things as a lifelong insomniac, that could help lull me to sleep. Now when I go to play it, not only can I not find it available, but it doesn't even show up in my favorites or any search history, how can that be? A heads up would've been nice for what I pay yearly.
I'm going back to Map My Fitness after trying Strava. Both are good but I prefer the simplicity and it does what I need it to do.

As an instructor, it would be nice when a change is made on the business app that it was appropriately updated on the client app. Too often on the business app will I update to a new instructor for a class and it still shows the previous instructor teaching the class. It's a small gripe, but the students need to be able to see who the new instructor is and not expect someone else when they show up for class

In general a good tracking app. But why change things that are good to things that demotivate you. The Meal Tips are just not very helpful. Why try to find just anything negative if the meal is already well balanced. If you try to correct based on the tips. It just finds something else where you can "improve" it does not help at all if your meals are already quite healthy. It just makes you feel bad for eating anything remotely "unhealthy"

Good interface but searching could be easier. Bar code scanning is awesome but I wish that recent foods had a search, and wasn't broken down by meal. I have a lot of the same thing for lunch or dinner so it's annoying to have to re search everything for dinner when I just had it for lunch. Also, I would like to be able to search all the food I've already had rather than just scrolling.

Vitacost carries a ton of products I've used for years. Their customer service is fantastic. Anytime I've had an issue, it was resovled quickly & I was happy with the outcome. Also, having worked in an Herb Shop I can tell you the prices are discounted. I used to pull the app up & compare what was on the shelf! The suggested retail that Vitacost lists is the same as those I would price in store. So when Vitacost says something is 40% off the retail you can trust it.

I love this app! It is the fastest, simplest I have used and it gives me detailed information that makes it easy to see where I need to make adjustments in my nutrition, habits, and exercise. Typically, the time it takes to track what I eat daily makes me quit after a few weeka, but this is fast and gives me so much information that it is worth it. I am losing more weight, much more quickly than I have with many other apps and programs.

After a little over a week and really wanting to like it, I've given up. Sonar is picked up by recording, ruining any audio, and battery is KILLED on Samsung Gear Sport, so would have to sleep with phone in bed, which ruins best part. UI is very clunky and cluttered. Starts becoming overwhelming and very difficult to organize after a few nights. Could/should be so much better.

MapMyFitness is a great app which I have been using to track walking, running and biking for 3 or so years. I like the notes feature in the app which I use to log which shoes I wore for a particular activity. I switch between shoes to even out the wear and to log how many miles are on them to make future purchasing decisions. MapMyFitness syncs with Vitality, Google Fit and Fitbit which helps bring all my data together! Great app! 1/29/2020 - MapMyFitness greatly improved its GPS tracking.
I like this app. the only problem is they need more foods added. it's really hard to find things sometimes
this version of the app and the previous version will NOT open on my Google Pixel 10 Fold and I get popups all day saying it's crashing.

Overall Great app, love the amount of useful information provided, I do not have another app to compare it to, but it does everything I want/need. As I get more used to the app I may upgrade to test the other features that seem legit. Update: I've been using the app for almost 3 months now and still a great app where you can join challenges. I'm using the free app but may upgrade in the near future

When it doesn't bug or glitch, this is a great app. It's a very friendly user interface and does exactly what it's supposed to do. However, in the last several months the app now has so many glitches from randomly logging me out of my profile/app frequently, not recording my runs, and randomly failing to record an accurate distance. I usually pause the app while I wait at stop lights, but sometimes the distance will freeze but the time keeps going which makes it horribly frustrating.

Great app easy to use and a good selection of stats. Consistent measurement of distances too. Too add to this. I had a problem where the app stopped tracking my progress. After many attempts at fixing reinstalling etc l stumbled on why it was happening. When starting a walk or activity do not have the full map on your display, just show the stats no map at all. If you display the map once you phones screen locks it stops tracking.

I would consider buying if you could set the maximin volume for the alarm. I need to wake up slowly, and I'd love to have music play for 15 minutes before I get up. But it "slowly" (maybe over a minute at most) goes to the loudest volume my phone puts out, while sitting right next to my head. The wakeup seems to be geared only to people with trouble waking up, not those of us looking to wake in a more peaceful, natural way.

Awful , random ads pop-ups showing up after each booking ! And those are not even yoga related !! I used to love this app, it now became one that I truly hate ! This is a new feature ( and I really don't understand how they could come up with this idea from a UX perspective! ) but everytime you book a yoga class, you get an automatic pop-up that offers you some discount on... wine ( yes, wine!!!! ) or food boxes.... truly nerve wrecking! 😤 I can't find any option to deactivate this behaviour!

I just knocked out my first run and the coach was amazing. I really felt like he was there watching me and I was able to keep pushing. I'm already a fan and I'm definitely going to recommend this app to my friends.

The new Google health app update is has one issue after another, from not having dynamic calorie tracking anymore, not being able to add foods that don't exist in the system, the layout being terrible, and hard to read, can't track your body fat, phantom counting both calories and steps, you just have to keep on fighting with the coach, every time I ask it where some settings Fitbit used to have, it says it doesn't exist and there's a workaround for it, but the workarounds for the Fitbit app!🙄

I've tried a lot of different workout apps and this is by far the best. They have plans that can be adjusted to your schedule and can incorporate runs into your plan that also use Nikes run club app which is nice. The workouts themselves are excellent and use great instructions for the movements. They are also very encouraging during the workout. I'm very impressed with this app. Great job!

Have used this app since 2015 and it was my fitness app of choice enabling me to look at the workouts and exercises in them ahead of time and gauge what I would be able to do given my knee injuries. Switching all of the workouts to class format has ruined what was once a great app. Now you can't see what a class will entail when the title is "quarter master" but have to watch the entire video which is slow and takes up a ton of data. I can't imagine who signed off on this update!
Perfect overall fitness tracker!

it's nice , but the problem not supporting the Arabic language, and we spoke with them more than 1 years and they promised to work on it !! it's major markets , and the ai do all the work for translation the apo easy, why not do it ?

Really used to enjoy this app but the March 2022 update was probably the worst one yet. The formats, class or whiteboard, are not easy to use or navigate. You can't go back to an exercise or skip to another one. You can't see some of the workouts without actually having to start the exercise. Can we please just go to the previous versions, those were fantastic. Glad to see that workout plans are back though

A great app for keeping track of your nutrient intake and controlling portion sizes! Forces Push Notifications! I have tried to turn off the notifications within the app multiple times, but the settings do not save. I will have to delete the app if this is not updated.

Loading feed or history often lasts for ages. So if you want to quickly check how was your workout or get back to data from i.e. last week, forget about. Great app for planning and tracking weight training workouts. The app has a large library of barbell, dumbbell, and body weight exercises you can add in, with short videos demonstrating the movement. It's intended for alternating full body workouts, but could easily be adapted for alternating between upper and lower days. Pro version is worth it IMO.

Edit: the issue seems to be resolved. The app is easy to use and has a nice user experience. The AI feature around analysing photos of food is interesting.
