I can sync my music and take off. I am just a routine jogger/walker, bike person who enjoys being able to still move at 71. I definitely for years enjoy the Armour compression hot/cold gear it is the best at least for me. So I'll be staying with this app and its products. For now I am not into purchasing premium levels.

I haven't had a subscription since December and everything except some music is locked. I remember long before I got the sub that I was able to use the app - even a simple body scan was immensely helpful in college. I loved the other content, but due to financial strain, I couldn't afford it anymore. I was bummed but thought that I'd have a couple of things. Literally everything's locked. Why bother listing a free version? There really isn't. I'm disappointed in the direction this app has taken.

I've used this app for years now with multiple different watches (Garmin has the best customer support I've ever had) occasional issues pairing (can take a couple attempts) but once it's done it always works just fine. lots of functionality in the app

I use this on my smartwatch mostly, and on my phone to see results, set goals, etc. There is lots of room for improvement in both interfaces. The watch doesn't give accurate step counts at the end of a work out, and the interface is generally clunky. On the phone, I'd like to be able to compare different workouts side by side, edit a workout (oops, i forgot to hit end!), and see more information compiled at a glance. Integration with MindBody also has lots of room for improvement.

Classic company overchargin for products that have quite poor UX for no reason. Like nothing requires me billing address to use my credit card on my phone yet garmin thinks it's a requirement. Not so simple/fast to use for basic suff you need to click through couple of menus for basic things and whatnot. But I guess it's not broken so 3 stars for just working properly
Good but occasional errors!

Maybe this is starting to look a bit dated. I don't get the hype for MFP and others. This tracks calories and macros far better (by food, meal, day, etc) than others for FREE. Way more obvious and useful. That is a pay option on other popular tracking apps if they even have it. Could use some new life. Barcode reader not always spot on.

I love these watches, and this app is pretty great. My biggest pet peeve with it, though, is that it doesn't count hiking as walking in the challenges. There are absolutely no hiking challenges, so I have to resort to the walking ones. Hiking gives me the right stats but I can't use it if I'm in challenges.

I love the macro counting and the amount of food choices and the nutrition but I ABSOLUTELY HATE the new update. It is not easy to toggle back and forth. It is much harder to find and update your food goals. I've been using MFP for many years and this is the first update I have not been happy with. Other than that, they have lots of food items in the database making tracking easy.

Downgrading from 5 to 3 stars bc ads. I have used this app for years and loved it! Recently the sleep tracking started only working every other day on the free version - ok, fair; I get holding back some features for the paid version. Now ads have started playing after I turn off an alarm. That's a hard NO. I was considering upgrading to lifetime premium, but I honestly find the insertion of full page ads after an alarm so gross that I'll probably just stop using the app. Rethink your choices.

I am definitely not one for diets but need to lose a bit of weight for my hols. This app lets me control what I eat and track my calories easily. Easy to use and tracks daily progress to my goal. Highly recommend.

Do not opt for free trial. If you accidently forget to unsubscribe even if you don't use the app, you will be charged (I requested refund within 24 hours). Any other app this has happened with has refunded my money. I was half asleep when I signed up trying to put something on to help me go back to sleep, I didn't like it, and forgot about it. Then $60 later I'm SOL. I paid 60 dollars for something I used for just a few minutes. Thanks "CALM".

Love it. My only suggestion though would be to be able to automatically convert ounces to grams for products for people living countries where units of measure are different to the website they are taking the recipe from. I love that feature btw.

By far the best app I have used, the ads are not intrusive, you can add custom recipes and common meals that include your custom recipes, the bar code scanner always works, and I can track my iron and vitamin intake! Also it picks up my weight, and workouts automatically from ringconn (via health connect) and adjusts my calorie goal for the day! I'd buy the premium if it was cheaper or a flat one time fee.

My previously useful Fitbit app no longer works, and I am unable to get my device to link to this POS app. I knew it was just a matter of time when Google bought Fitbit until the whole thing imploded.

I've used this app for almost ten years, and I've slowly watched it deteriorate into garbage. I dealt with not being able to scan food anymore. I've also dealt with what seems to be an issue with steps/calories getting logged incorrectly from Samsung Health. But now with the new, terrible UI update, and with what appears to be a bug that's causing sync issues and has wiped my food logs, I don't think I can deal with it anymore. It's way too much of a hassle and not worth the headache.

Welcome April. First this app used to show walking steps but from 2024 ending onwards no walking steps, only kcal are showing. Is anyone facing the same problem. I don't have a sports watch in which I can count. As noise Watch I had it got spoil i restart and sync that also not working, other watches are little expensive. Please Suggest different walking app.

Takes the thinking out of lifting. Calculates weight increases for me. Really love that it gives vibrated alarms after the right rest period so I know when to start my next set without watching a clock. Beautful simple and efficient. Only drawback is that I wish the descriptions for each lift were in the app so I don't have to go to the website to double check my form.

Don't get me wrong... It's a great interface probably the best one for my uses... I just wish that I didn't have to pay some rediculous amount to just see my heart rate in graph form.. if there was anything better for tracking I'd use it instead. Hey Underarmor: No I would not like to have someone coaching me, no I do not need workout plans, I just need to see what I feel is necessary.

For beginners and advanced runners alike. It has all kinds of ways to run, whether it be just setting your own run based on distance or time, or playing one of the hundreds of coached runs recorded by more than a handful of Nike coaches and seasoned professional runners. I always enjoy picking out my guided run each time. And when it comes time to perform, I've set my distance, and I get distance, time, and pace updates throughout my run. Love this app.

Chronometer is an excellent app to track your macros. I am new to the app, and I will sadly admit that I have been tracking through another similar app, but I have switched to Chronometer based on many Reddit users saying how great it is. I have only been using it for a few days and I can say that it is amazing so far. I love so many features in the free version (too many to list), please don't change a thing and THANK YOU!

Superb riding App if you wanna be social and interact with other riders. Tried a few different interactive apps but this is by far the best for ease of use. Built a whole new group of friends through this, who in turn came along and helped me finish a 600k ride for charity. Would have been 5 stars but keep getting intermittent drop outs of other riders which is disappointing.

It uses to much battery because it always searching for location for weather service, if the setting is to allow it all the time. I know that is I nice functionality, I know that it needs location for this, but a timeout for trying is an obvious must. I will disable it until is solved.

This app has EVERYTHING! Could not be happier switching to this app. First of all, it's Canadian made! 👏 At first I had trouble trying to find how to get meals to display total calorie count, but I finally found the setting. There is SO MUCH functionality in this app it's ridiculous. Tracking macros AND micronutrients is invaluable for me. I actually found that some of my supplements had me way over certain micronutrient tolerable UL, thanks to this app. Well worth the cost of the subscription.

I really like the app as it's free and easy to use, but it keeps logging me out and then not letting me log back in for several hours, even if I uninstall and reinstall it. It says there's a "handshake error" and "certificate verify failed." It's very annoying.

Mixed experience for an expensive subscription. The stats have been great. The heart rate monitoring is unreliable which makes me question said stats. Today was prime example. During my row the monitor read ~100bps. I paused midway and cleaned the sensor and my wrist. Continued rowing. Minimal change. I was pushing so hard I nearly threw up

App use to work fine, after the last update the app no longer pulls up any information it just opens, freezes and goes nowhere. I've un-installed the app, cleared cache, and when I try to log out of my account to see if re-logging in helps it just pulls up a loading screen and does nothing. I downloaded the app on my Apple product though and everything works perfectly fine on the Apple device but not my Samsung. I know Nike is partnered with Apple, but I'm not taking my iPad on a run.

worked when it was free. after paying to upgrade the app crashes constantly. I can't use it at all and before that it would load black screens when opened (as if it was trying to load an add and didn't). I can't even email support from within the app because it freezes and closes too quickly. if you use it don't pay for it, otherwise you risk it failing on you.
Not tracking walk properly. It is calculating steps 24 hours every day, which is practically impossible. None of the process mentioned in the app is helpful.

app is constantly asking for location permission so it can update the weather and push it to my vivo fit 4. i keep saying "DON'T ASK AGAIN " but it keep insisting on my gps antenna turned on and location permission. i do not want it. please correct this bug. thank you!

Too restrictive. trying to log the left over vegetables from a salad kit I had for dinner and cannot without all kit ingredients. Tried logging eact vegetable, shredded broccoli then shredded carrot but always brings up an established product as a best match. It seems to me the nutrient profile will be inaccurate so not a great match for me.
Great app! Now I'm can practice mental math while half asleep - a guaranteed recipe to wake up. The gui is a little confusing (even after several years) but I can figure out everything I need.

very useful to track the calories, no hidden money plans. strongly recommends to track calories by selecting your food. even the app has the option to select bands of the food items we are eating. it shows the exact nutrition details same as the package.

I used to use UA Record, but was forced to switch to this app when Under Armor pulled the plug. I have an account, but can't log in. Changed my password, still can't log in. Tried to open a new account, it says I already have an account. Still can't log in. Thanks for nothing.

I enjoy using the Zwift trainer but the apps, both Zwift and the Companion, are not easy to navigate. I also have to log in to the app and bypass it by starting a session to be able to view progress or upcoming workouts.
Works extremely well, free, and accurate! Had a great time using it+music on the app! 🔥🔥🔥
Great app, but on my samsung galaxy s21 plus, you can't add an image to recipe from the gallery. No images show when you select camera roll.

Fun App but unfortunately AI is so far off! If comparing nutrients from AI pictures with manually adding items in Yazio OR if comparing with results from just Gemini or Chatty you can find Yazio almost always being 1/3 under. So no good if relying on the app for various reasons. Cancelled subscription after just 2 weeks... 35€ down the drain 😟

App has a lot of features. I paid for the subscription but over the course of a week, I was constantly getting notifications that the app was crashing in the background. It would also cause my phone to freeze up and make the touchscreen unresponsive, to where I would have to reboot my phone for it to work. I deleted the app and no longer having the issues on my phone. please refund.
Poor attempt. Coming from iphone they had an awesome app. You think they could design the same thing. Every other developer does

They have worked on and really improved this app! I use it all the time now with no problems. I removed one star because it is slow -- just takes a long time to load and search, etc. Prev rev You can't scroll down on any pages to see important information (like reviews, and deals). Clicking on order history brings up nothing (and I am logged in and I have a ton of past orders). Crashes and quits. Totally useless. Was better several months ago. I'm going back to my laptop for vitacost.
Is this app being maintained? Its been awhile sincr the last upate?

Disappointed - false advertising. The ads running on FB and Instagram imply that they will give a specific plan, as opposed to what you actually get, which is a couple of binaural tracks and then a bunch of celebrity sleep podcast experiences. I've asked for my money back and received no reply.

by chance I got a discounted rate for 1 year. I wasn't so sure this app was for me, but now that I am able to try some of the premium features, this app is super helpful when I just need a moment to release some stress, help maintaining my peace throughout the day, or something slightly different than my norm to help me wind down and ready my mind for sleeping.
Super easy to use, and has so many businesses!

I first used the app & it had stopped tracking my ride after an hour or so, it timed it but recorded no other data after the hour. It only gave me the notification that something went wrong after I ended my workout. I followed all of the help guides & changed settings accordingly. Second ride? App only worked while I had the app open and the phone unlocked. It was already draining battery pretty good but that takes the cake. Uninstalled, plenty of other apps that work out there.

That didn't go well, I did some simple laps of my home area and the app failed to map the distance, it also lost GPS signal. My Strava and Google apps had no problems both saying the same distance. Looks like it will be just good for indoor static use if it can't manage this simple task.

This app tracks your daily meals, exercise and weight and provides detailed reports. The food database is quite comprehensive. While there are similar apps, this one doesn't require excessive permissions. However, two flaws detract from an otherwise very good app. The database search function is very literal and cannot find items that are even slightly spelled differently. It is too easy to delete an entry by accidentally swiping left due to the lack of a "confirm delete" function.

I moved to (was forced to) this app after using endomondo for years. I like how it has a lot of self-contained health tools but it still leaves a lot to be desired. For some reason it doesn't provide feedback to any precision. Only says three point... kilometres and time four minutes.. per kilometre. Really annoying when I'm willing myself along and its flooring my times! The Intervals suck when compared to endomondo. I used to set intervals that were different after every 3 rounds in a cycle.

App not for me. Been trying to cancel for past hour +, but Play Store will not let me cancel subscription as promised in app page. If I can't cancel before my free trial ends, I will be charged $70 for a year long subscription. What a great way to generate a revenue stream! Sell an app. Tell customer they can cancel without penalty if done by a certain date, then disallow it. SCAM!!
Everytime I go to check out it goes to log in. I log in and then it clears my cart. App always has issues.

The app works fine, but now that Android 11 tells you when an app gets your location in the background, I can see that it routinely reads my location throughout the day when I'm not using the app. There's no valid reason for that. For now, I can deny location permission entirely when not using the app, but it's annoying to toggle. I will be looking for a replacement.

I love the Calm app. I have been using it for years. BUT some things that need to be fixed are the random notifications that won't go away without "force stop" when you haven't even opened the app in days. Also, when I turn on Bluetooth in my car, the app starts playing music without me having the app open. I can't play Pandora without the music from Calm playing too. I have to "force stop." I'm going to wreck my car trying to go to settings. Please fix!

The different categories of workouts are wide-ranging and cover almost everything I would look for in a workout program; however, the app would go to 5 stars for me if there was a section for workouts that are entirely safe for diastasis recti patients. I cannot do the majority of the workouts because of this and really have to pick and choose which I am able to do or modify workouts. I would love to get on, go to a diastasis recti safe category, and just pick a workout.

Can't tell if it's the software or hardware but after only 3 days of this watch and high battery % it no longer pairs or works at all. For a subscription service with a steep price if expect better than a $20 cheapy tracker you could get at a sports store. Before it died like that the automatic sleep tracking was great, I like the fact it focuses more on actual data that would require a device vs random stuff you don't need a watch for and forums (like my old fitbit) but needs to work first!

This app helps me a lot identifying issues with my sleep and the AI functionality is actually nice, summarizing my sleep for the night and putting it into context. It also gives me some leads on how to improve my sleep

It's a great calorie tracker. I like that you can also add exercises and your own foods to it. It doesn't take forever to add foods either since this has a bar code scanner. I'd give it five stars if certain things weren't locked behind a pay wall (such as time stamps) but it's great for a free app!

My favorite workout app, I really appreciate Nike for prividing it for free! I've been using it for many years now. I love the variety of workouts (endurance, strength, yoga, mobility) and that they come in different lengths and difficulties. Overall great quality and easy to use app with videos and tips during the exercises!
25 minutes of unskippable questions. I ended up deleting the app after finishing the questions just because of how frustrated i ended up. Not worth installing

Fitbit sold out. So many of my favorite features are gone. I can no longer create custom foods to log if it doesn't recognize the barcode. I enjoyed the progress data charts so I could see overall trends but that's gone too. I also can't customize the watch face anymore. It's way oversimplified and not useful or enjoyable since Google took over it.

As someone who is new to running, I love the variation of runs. I used to just go out and run the same slow pace. But the speed runs really challenge me, and have made the other running much easier! Reason for 4 stars is the app can be glitchy. It also does not track well AT ALL indoors or on a treadmill. So it makes you look like you ran way less miles than you really did.

The app is great when the GPS works, the last month or so I've had so many issues with the GPS failing during walks the same walk I always do no difference the weather conditions might be all that is different but didn't realize that would cause the gps to stop working within first few mins of my more than 30 mins walk, I did all the troubleshooting steps asked even deleted and reinstalled I'm hoping it doesn't continue to happen or I'll just delete all of my fitness trackers and I'm using 2.

unfortunately, one of the glitchiest apps ive used. ive used this app for over 10 years and always loved it. in the last year it gets worse and worse. randomly deleting weeks or months worth of logs, duplicating weeks worth of entries so it looks like I went way over my goal, or even randomly adding things to my days that I never even logged, ruining months of data. if you are serious about your calorie deficit and like to look at past trends to interpret data from, this is NOT the app for you.

I've been using the Whoop fitness tracker for a while, and while it's great with an active subscription for in-depth metrics like recovery scores and sleep analysis, I was disappointed to learn what happens once it expires. Essentially, the device turns into little more than a basic live heart rate broadcaster—you can still get real-time HR data via Bluetooth, but that's about it. No new data tracking, no analytics, and the app becomes useless.

I have used this app for three runs. Each run is about 1.3 miles. The app says I ran 0.1 miles. I turned off auto pause and it still doesn't work. I clicked on the Contact Us button and it took me to a chat. The guy in the chat said this isn't the right spot for app troubleshooting and gave me a phone number.

I was annoyed with MFP because I feel like their barcode scan feature got worse after they started charging for it, but the newest update is garbage. I miss having a separate diary, it's harder to navigate between days and individual food items logged are now in giant bubbles of their own that take up way too much space and means you have to scroll instead of seeing it all at once. Also hate that you have to click around to find some features that used to be separately labeled in the diary.

I've found it on par if not better than any 2nd party routing app for cycling. never experienced anything abnormal or crashing. initiative and easy to master. informative and functional. had Strava, komoot and ditched them for Garmin.

Used to have zero issues with this app while using it to bluetooth all my sensors, but since the last update, it's complete garbage. Won't find the sensors. If it does it crashes 1 minute into the ride and won't reconnect. Try fixing your fundamentals before adding new bikes and gimmicky costumes next time eh Zwift?
Updated the app, and now it won't allow me past the terms and conditions screen.

There are some obvious errors in the app. For example, I walked today for 54 mins and it showed only 2.67 kms and steps over 5600. Now I know that around 1350 to 1400 steps make one km, so 5600 steps amount to 4 kms approx. It might be slightly less but never 2.67 km.
Great as a carb counter for atkins diet! Huge food database, easy to edit & select foods, even includes vitamins! Just what I needed to track my diet.

I don't want to try the 7 days because you have to put your payment in and get charged $70 if you don't cancel in time and that kind of thing gives me some anxiety, which is the opposite of what this is supposed to do, so i tried the free version and what a rip off... You get almost nothing. At the least i wanted to hear some music or listen to some sounds to see if it helped me sleep better but it's locked behind a subscription. This app is all about money, not calmness.

Used to have Pro version. Regular is fine & I get no ads so developers followed through on promise to eliminate ads for Pro purchasers. I do wish there were some way the app would start automatically... I hate getting into workout & realizing I forgot to start it -- again. Update Jan 2020: Still a good app, easy to use with a lot of options. Seems to be accurate & provide good feedback with minimal fluff.

Remaining hopeful, but the software seems to be very buggy. As an Android user not sure I'm experiencing all the benefits of an IOs user. Calorie count seems to be way out of line compared to other devices, leading me to wonder if all the other metrics are also inaccurate. Unlike others, I have not had connectivity issues and do like the long battery life. Have had my device almost 30 days and have yet to take off. Wish I could be more positive but I'm a little skeptical. Time will tell.

This app is amazing. I have some trouble getting out of bed in time. The captchas are helping me a lot. The UI looks great, the performance is good and the statistics give you a great overview of your sleep quality. I am using the pro version because I really want to support the devs behind the app. But the experience you get in the free version is really all you need. But if you like the app, please consider supporting the devs. Its worth it. UPDATE 2026: Still a happy user and a great app!
A ridiculous number of questions to answer before finding out you CAN NOT use the app without a paid subscription! It is listed as a free app, which is a lie.

At the suggestion of tech support, I switched from map my walk to map my fitness. It worked, until it updated. Now I have the same problem as I was having with map my walk. Really disappointed. And tech support came through. It's working again. I just wish they'd stop breaking the app! I can't imagine it's making the tech support job any easier.

it's not tracking my walking and it's affecting other apps that supposed to track my walking and earn points for it I'm connected to it's a problem. so like having problems with walk counter I've done everything and it still lagging and stops completely. like been walking for a hour counter saying around 1,000 like something is clearly wrong hear. it's affecting the other walking apps

I don't want to sound too negative but Google did a horrible job with the new UI. Fitbit had a few issues but at least you had control of what you put into the app for the most part. Update: I just use Grok to track my food and workouts since Google Health isn't as useful with custom foods and workouts. I shared the Calories Burned, Sleep Data, and other useful information with Grok and he did the rest. Grok also has a better UI and ease of use. It would be cool if they had a wearable.

the time pickers are nightmare. the add activity bottom sheet can be too easily swiped down. it should be a separate full screen view. also the latest (or favorite) types of activity should be directly in the screen applicable with one tap instead of going to the combo list. I'm missing landscape for stress graph and other metrics. i cannot check at what times exactly were my wake up events. i wanna be able to set alarm from the main screen. there are so many improvements to be done!
I like their selection of items but the app is glitchy and can take time to load. Sometimes it will tell me I'm denied access after I've already signed in.

Love that the basic version (including UPC scanning) is free with ads that aren't too intrusive. There's a subscription version for all features, but the free version has way more than what's available in other apps.

I don't like the new updated version of My Fitness Pal. If you close the app and open it again, it goes to whatever day you were last looking at. So you can accidentally log food on the wrong day. When you open the today tab, you see your calories on top and have to scroll to the bottom to see exercise. There's no way to take a screenshot that includes both at the same time. The old version was easier to use.

Edit: I came back after a break and found that there's no more free tier or way to see where I left off without setting up payment. Awful change. Makes workouts incredibly simple and makes it easier to get out and do the work.

I don't think it's accurate at all. the numbers are all over the place, example, recovery numbers on days when I had extremely restful sleep and feel great are low, and days when I sleep terrible it tells me everything is great. The step counts are way off from my Garmin Fenix, which is very accurate, and I've literally counted steps and compared, whoop is roughly 15 to 20% off. overpriced, don't see the value in the plan cost. regrettable purchase.

I've been using it for quite a while, but recently the app stops recording activity in the middle of the walk/run and it's really frustrating. It would be nice if the app had the capability to track my running shoes milage, something that competitor apps have had for a long time, easy to access.

Bad experience! What was once a fantastic app, accurate, precise and very consistent, is now STALLING particularly after 2.3k. Totally not recommended. Been like this since I reinstalled it recently. After the race you have only a small part recorded. Interestingly, the timer doesn't stop, just the distance recording stops. Nope. Am done.

The new update brought me to review the app with a one-star rating. I see others feel the same way. Please, please, please go back to the old design. I liked the circle that showed how much exercise impacted my "calories left" for the day. I liked that calories consumed, calories burned, and calories left were all at the top and easy to see. The app doesn't seem to be syncing with GoogleFit anymore - I'm used to a lag, but now it's not registering or converting them to calories burned.
Why is this app suddenly full of ads? I'm booking a yoga session and it's trying to sell me a hair drier. I'm bald...

Cool but hard to navigate. missing a bunch of common exercises. UI is bad as editing exercises afterwards is tedious and frustrating. There is a ton of info between the watch and the app but finding things can be difficult. An example is max heart rate and heart rate zones. my watch has it under settings>heart rate> heart rate and power. Instead of being under the heart rate settings on the app, it's under your device profile. Now try to find the 1 rep max screen on this app. It exists.

I use the free version, and it does what I need. Occasionally, there is a glitch, such as not recording the distance. However, that is rare and not inconvenient overall. I enjoy the challenges. The free version used to keep track of mileage on your shoes, but it doesn't anymore. However, the free version can connect to my brand of heartbeat monitor now, so that's an improvement.
Easy to use app. No adds. Helpful recommendations. Love the savings!! The vitacost brand of nuts, almond butter, dairy free chocolate chips, almond oil etc are excellent!

Not worth it. There are much better options than Calm that are either free or offer a much better product for a better price that take up less storage on your device. YouTube's free content was on par/better than anything I found using Calm. Balance will let you try premium on their app for free for 6 MONTHS if you choose. That shows that they truly believe in their product and put customers first. Well designed and easier to navigate. Endel is an incredible app, I highly recommend them too.

App has improved, but there are still issues with the recording of some guided runs, particularly the speed runs. if you forget to press pause for the stretching part, it won't record your intervals, it will stay at the "rest" step, you won't be able to save the run at all, the app will stay stuck trying to save and you'll have no choice but to discard the run. Quite frustrating. I love the guided runs on this app though. Fix your bugs and I'll rate it 5 stars.
Poorest functionality I've ever witnessed. I tried for 30 minutes to place an order. I've used the app for years, I simply changed phones. Pixel 6 and your app do not get along at all...

Not sure what's happened in the last few weeks, but the application won't open on my phone anymore. Stays frozen on the loading screen. I've uninstalled and reinstalled the application multiple times, restarted my phone, and checked for OS updates on my device with no progress. Would gladly rate it 5 stars if it worked for me again!

great app for logging runs- I love it. Developers PLEASE fix the issue where it keeps logging you out of the app. Sometimes even if I use it consecutive days it forces me to log back in - it's extremely annoying when you want to go for a run and have to go through this gate keeping. I understand security but this is excessive - thanks!

Never works. I can get it open and search for items, but if I add them to my cart, it won't let me adjust quantities or delete. I can never pull up order history or even feedback to let them know how bad their app sucks!

This app really helped me slow my thoughts at night, and really relax before going to bed. The bedtime stories gave my mind something else to focus on instead of racing thoughts, and I am able to fall asleep better. The breathing exercises have helped me to self-soothe/ calm down when I am faced with anxious situations. It's sad though that it requires one to subscribe to get the full usage out of the app, but I understand that people have to make money.

Not syncing properly. I hit my steps target yesterday and I checked it had synced to the app. Today it shows I didn't hit target which has ended a streak of over 1200days. Looking at the data it appears to have wiped everything recorded between 11pm yesterday and 1am today. Presumably because of the clock change. This is not acceptable.

This should be ZERO STARS. I liked my Fitbit app. I could find the data I needed. My biggest irritation was it wasn't stable and I had to reinstall periodically. They switched us without choice into the Health app. IT IS THE WORST APP I HAVE. I had to spend 30 minutes getting the information I want to display somewhere in the app -- but now I have to scroll through several pages to find it. In addition, I have to individually look at metrics to get a previous day's data. NOT HAPPY!

New UI is honestly just hideous Can't scale graphs now so heart rate variations less than 20bpm get squashed so much that you can barely see them App is now pumped full of so much AI slop it's actually gross
Wouldnt allow me to register which is upsetting because it looks like a great app.

Difficult to understand, not user friendly. Update May.24.26- The ratios for each widget cannot be adapted to for my needs. In the FitBit app I was able to view Carbs-Fats-Proteins together for my needs...I do not have that ability, and it is difficult to decipher the % given for my specific dietary needs. "You are dreaming" if you can't find a useful feature of Sleep as Android. I tried it for free at first for the smart alarm wake up in the morning. Now, I have tons of settings to help guide me thru a full and restful sleep.

I appreciate booking on 1 platform for multiple buisnees and easily linking to my calendar. A feature i would appreciate is if you could have "friends" and share calenders. I also wish I could just look and see what I did, not only x amount of cardio x amount barre ect. thanks! Great app!
Please stop asking all the time to share additional data with you. You keep bothering me with it every time I open the app or even worse during the usage. If one do not give a consent, respect that.

Very buggy. Casting to my Google Nest speaker is pretty bad, and as of right now, just completely not working. The app is nice, all good but, when it gives you 10sec for hiding your phone to the pocket for example only after you hide it it notify you about battery saving mode, so i ended up running for 35min and nothing was recorded, only time, not even distance! Bit annoying.

Keeps disconnecting from my vivoactive 4 each second week. Tired to use such - Google doesn't allow to say what it is - as Garmin. Now each time I reconnect my watch it sends me email with congrats on new device.

I have been using Fat secret on and off for several years. My most favorite feature is the calendar. Very unintuitive app and payments need to take so many tries before going through. the stripe 3ds system constantly times out, tried different cards (visa and MasterCard) absolutely the same thing. usually I need to try a few hours later for it to work but the class might have sold out by then. Ive been using the app for years, since the first iteration before they were bought by UA. The app has a good UI and the gps screwed up only once in like 7 years of tracking. This app has the potential to be great. It has an amazing layout and features. The downside is that unlike most of my other apps, it just randomly decides when it's going to track my steps. I've also encountered the app tracking my steps, and then the results aren't viewable later. So it's really hit or miss with how accurate your info is. Usually it tracks me all day at work, but then my night jogging never tracks itself at all. Sometimes it completely stops tracking after 8pm.

The WHOOP seems Iike an amazing idea. After hearing about it on various podcasts I was excited to give it a try. I have tried connecting to the WHOOP band for the last 12 hours and have had 0 luck. instructions on support impossible to follow. Nothing matches the descriptions or instructions .Can't add your own amounts or change or delete foods or food amounts. Horrible app! Very unhappy. Very buggy, never fixed, same issue has been there for years, but this is what my studio uses so little choice. Pretty good, used it relentlessly since April 2018 couple of crashes/freezing, but apart from that it does a good job. Used on free rides and workouts. Like the power dial in workouts, find that really helpful. Would be nice if more profile info was available like badges & garage.

This app helps to give the user a good idea of how far they've ran and how their training is progressing. I love the effectiveness of this app. very high quality for a free app. Nike's are my current set of running shoes but I appreciate that it allows you to add shoes of any brand to keep track of how far you've ran per set of shoes!

a very effective app with good choices for alarm sounds. i love that it starts softly then gets vety loud. Due to a snoring husband, i must wear earplugs! If i am not in a deep sleep, i can catch it at a lower volume, otherwise I NEED the loud sound. The app navigation is not intuitive when trying to get the stats. i have a paid subscription. I have to use a second app to get better stats; like snoring. Watch app doesn't get snoring, no sync w/ phone!!!

Hardly anything available for free to even try it out. One thing that keeps me from subscribing is the primary narrator voices are very annoying. Jay feels like he's shouting at me, it's grating. And the main woman - I forget her name - reads with a sort of condescending vocal fry affectation, like she is reading to children. I find it condescending and annoying. Was a fantastic app but now the app just seems to be a cash grab It has taken away the Lifetime purchase option which I was wanting to upgrade too so I let my yearly subscription end to do this. I resubscribed for a monthly option just to get my records Also the app offers nothing new for the huge price jump. After this month I will never use again
super easy to use and so far it's free
loved this app for years, but now they are requiring a 60$/y subscription to even access the app, while before the basic 5x5 was free.
Easy to use. I've been a Vitacost customer for years but have just recently started using the smartphone app. It has worked flawlessly for reordering items. Very easy to use.

This is a great app to record daily food intake. I really like how I can get a break down of sugars and fiber. What I wish could improve is to be able to add exercise. Sometimes I need to charge my smart watch and there isn't any way to add my exercise. I would consider purchasing the extra benefits, I wish it was a better yearly deal.

I couldn't remember why I had removed the app, then I started reading recent reviews. it is the fact the app opens automatically when ever I turn my device on. very annoying. I will not reinstall the app.
okay, but makes you curse when it doesn't save your exercise. save on watch, nothing in app, then it's gone from watch. Nice

It's good if you are self motivated. App tracts distance speed including average speed so you can try and improve/push yourself each run. You do however, have to pay extra if you want a program. Discards my runs. I complete a guided run and it only gives an option to discard the run. App only saves 1 in 5 runs. App can only be used for guided run and not for tracking any stats. I'm so disappointed

LOVE the app but every few weeks, usually on a Sunday, it crashes and I have to uninstall and reinstall it. Very frustrating.

Simple to use, just follow the program. It tells you what to do, even how to load the bar. Good videos on proper form, warm ups you just follow. App no longer finds the foods being searched for - even though they have been in my foods list for several months. Good app. I use it for my occasional running. A bit iffy on the metric versus imperial measures. Great app! The trainers are great and very motivational. Helps you not only with exercise, but nutrition and mindfulness as well. You will not only become a stronger athlete, but you will also have overall improved well being. I really like the new class format that they've added. There are many different types of workouts offered. Through this app, I've developed a new love for yoga. I really like the workout programs!

Sleep cycles seems to be fake. Recorded myself for multiple nights and the data it spat out was typical sleep cycles. I even checked on the phone and it was tracking my movements, but in the morning it still just showed great sleep patterns. The alarm function is fine, but there are better options out there.

Worked fine for about a year and a half. Then the gym I use disappeared from the app, indeed all gyms in our area disappeared from the app. Sometimes they appear again but with no reason why, only to disappear again. I drove past the gym and lo and behold it is still there so it isn't the gym. The app lists my gym membership pass so why not the gym? Mindbody has been a frustrating experience.

I really like this app but it is just confusing to use sometimes. I like to sleep reminder and I like how it can record you when you're sleeping and make the maps of snoring and such. I wish it was a little bit more user-friendly because I don't have time to research and learn some of the things that I think could be beneficial to know.

This has gotten extremely bad at accurately tracking when I walk and my GPS. Aargh! So frustrating! This app had a brief, shining moment of convenience and usefulness. Then, absolute uselessness and worse. Probably the easiest to use out of the calorie trackers I've used, and NO ADS in the free version. Worked fine, then one day it stopped letting me use my passes. I uninstalled and reinstalled and reset my password. I love this app, I use it only for running but it doesn't work sometimes, which is frustrating. today it froze and didn't respond. WASTE OF TIME! You can't do anything with this app unless you agree to become a paying subscriber. I first tried Stronglifts, aged 50. Back then we stored our workouts on the website, and personally in my log book. No more watching the clock, and forgetting what time I finished my last set. No more wondering if I'm adding the correct amount of weight. I can now just concentrate on form. Thank you Medhi 🙏

After the most recent update I can't listen to anything longer than 1 minute. Tried uninstalling and reinstalling, no help. New update is garbage
Using the same credentials that I log into account on pc and ipad. Says it's wrong.

I have the free version and its super easy to use to track my biking. It keeps times distance and even has metrics for tracking improvements. You can set weekly or daily or even monthly goals for tim, distance, calories etc. All that and its still simple to use. Theres sometimes a little glitch saving a picture but its not a problem. You just have to retrieve the pic from the gallery. Love this app and its kept me constantly riding for over a month.

Not happy being forced to switch to Google health from fitbit. I used to be able to manually add a food that wasn't found with scan bar. Now unless I pay for premium I'm not able to. Terrible I'm switching watches now. If I could give negative stars I would. I've had the fitbit for a long time and its been my favorite watch and tracker. This used to be one of my fav apps & the newer version isn't it. I loved that I could blast some music and get it! Now most are a "class format" and you can't really do that. Taking away a lifetime one-time purchase and forcing everyone to a monthly subscription under the guise of being "too valuable" not to charge for is extremely pathetic. Add new subscription features if you want, but don't touch what I've already paid for.

Fantastic app that does above and beyond what I thought it would do. I love that it shows me how many calories I burn throughout the day, even when I'm sleeping, which is unheard from any other device on the market! I love that it tracks my sleep and shows me the four different stages of sleep I was in and for how long I was in each stage. The best feature is that it captures 100 pieces of biometric data per second, that's unheard of in the market. It's extremely accurate! I LOVE this device!

Updated review. I updated to Gold so I could track a wider array of nutrients. it looks like the app is not correctly counting choline content in soy milk and soy nuts (and possibly other foods). Developers, can you please fix asap? there may be other similar issues that I have not discovered yet. Generally an amazing app so far, and I have not yet dived into all the features.

I’m extremely disappointed with this app. I paid almost $53 expecting a quality experience, but it does not deliver on what it promises.The AI feature is especially frustrating. it feels inaccurate, unhelpful, and honestly poorly developed. it completely misses the mark. I asked for a refund to Google and it wasn't given, and I still want a refund back. For an app at this price point, the refund policy should be more customer-friendly. Will not recommend.

This is the app that replaced the Fitbit app. It's a major downgrade. One of my most used features is the navigation ability to move back and forth between days and it is no longer easy to do this. What happened to auto-detecting exercises and allowing me to confirm them? The ugly panels that turn using the app into a chore instead of being able to view your data need to go away. I don't want the "Coach" speech bubble anywhere that I can accidentally touch it. Very disappointed.

I have had a Fitbit Zip for 10 years and this new app is not properly recording my active minutes. I can be sitting for hours and take a few steps to the fridge and it's tracked at 64 minutes. I also do not l like the layout. Too many unnecessary things to track. Now what do I do?

Whoop is suing Bevel while at the same time copying from them, which just feels off. It says a lot about the company and its culture. The Android app feels like a copy too - it’s not really native, and the UI/UX keeps getting worse. On top of that, the pricing is crazy for data that isn’t even reliable. I’m going to cancel my subscription and won’t support this company anymore.
Love the guided runs, but the app is really buggy. It will often just stop guiding mid-run, cancel a run, or crash while trying to save a run.

I got a premium version with a Samsung phone & liked it as it easily connected with all my ant+ sensors. I still like it & use it as a backup to a Garmin, had it still supported the sensors I'd most likely purchased the premium version rather than buy a new Garmin.

I just got my first workout done on this app, I had so far a good, no great experience with it, I mean premium workouts for free, you can't turn that down. I am an athlete, and this training club is not absolutely perfect to which I won't find any other app that is better, but it's free and effective.
Very helpful for visualizing and implementing lifestyle changes. A key part of my health journey, which includes exercise, weight training, nutrition, and medication.

Latest Update (1/26/2026) is giving me notifications of comments from connections but then comments aren't fully visible when I click on them from notifications tab. I've been using Garmin for several years now, so hopefully this is just a phase as they roll out routine updates. But still frustrating.

This app is now garbage for so many reasons. It freezes up during and after runs, signs me out incessantly, and has now failed to save my runs on a number of occasions. Update: Even the "Delete Account" function is going in a loop and failing to process (just like the rest of the app). I hope this isn't some sort of ploy to keep my account information. I love being able to order my favorite health foods that are no longer available at my local stores! There are ample selections and varieties to choose from! And they are always offering special discounts and free shipping!!!

I've used this app for years from everything from fasting to now tracking my food while I'm using wegovy. I like that it tracks my weight and my food so I get an idea of what kind of balance I have on my nutrition as well as my daily calorie intake so I know what days I've gone over my goal and what caused it. I also like the fact that it doesn't badge me about upgrading. if I want to upgrade and get those features. it's available with just a simple click of a button,

Great all in one app if you workout at several gyms. Only gripe is the search class feature for studios you have passes at is not user friendly. Also, the deals you see for classes and studios via email are not always in the app, but for some reason they're available when you login via web browser. Minor-ish things taking into account how convenient this app is.

Slowly getting worse. The subscription price increased but the game performance is deteriorating. 3 times tonight during a group ride the game disconnected from the servers, and it happened twice yesterday. It wasn't my own Internet connection because I was able to access other sites. Please.fix your IT infra problems because I don't use your service to get irritated.

I used to really like this app! Easy to use and clearly tracked data. This update it terrible though. Every time I add something, there is no update to my overall goal for that activity for the week. I don't get it. I hope the next update isn't as terrible, or I'm back to finding something that's actually useful. And, I don't care about "heart points". Just let me track my workouts for the week!

The GPS and distance tracking is some of the worst I've ever seen. I watched my distance tonight jump from 1.5 miles to 1.2 miles, while the number of steps increased. The same walk earlier this week was tracked as .4 miles total, and 3 miles total. This makes the app virtually useless, which is a shame, because the app has some truly great features.
Great app easy to use like that i can make a shopping list for the recipes i want to make

Best app for sleep sounds, sleep tracking, and easier wake ups I've ever found. The paywall is kinda annoying as it seems the app only functions at full capacity(so the way it's advertised) every other night. Meaning unless you pay the price you cannot rely on it every night. However, you can purchase a lifetime subscription, and with all of the updates they have to make to work with the different OS and software updates, the price is well worth it for a subscription or the lifetime plan.

This app used to work great, but the new speed runs never work correctly. After the warm up, the guidance will say something like, "Our first interval is 1 min, starting in 3...2....1". Then, I'll run for what feels like a minute, check the screen, and the rest timer will have been on for over a minute. About 30 seconds later, the voice coach will say, "First interval ending in 3...2...1". Additionally, when I go to stop a run, the "stop" button doesn't work.

Just like other fitness apps. Most features are locked behind a paywall, you could also use my fitnesspal instead of this, easier to navigate in my opinion. Also please add Zepp flow as a step sync app

Vitacost carries a ton of products I've used for years. Their customer service is fantastic. Anytime I've had an issue, it was resovled quickly & I was happy with the outcome. Nice easy app to record rides. Good web site to plan courses. transfers easily to different devices.

so many core pieces of the fitbit app just totally removed. I'm cool with implementing AI but why get rid of core features? 😤

I like the concept of this app, but it's not executed very well. I just manually added two recipes and have no idea where they saved even though I filed them under my Breakfast category. Not to mention,every time I try to add a recipe from the web, it nearly always fails.

Very good app to track your progress on running / jogging/walking. I have been using the app since last eight years and all my past data is still available. Unsat.

So useful to be able to track my calories earned - a major contribution to my weight loss - a dedicated "payment system" for all those treats I love to eat 😍. In general, I'm very happy. I'm new to the Map my Ride app but I find it funny that the plans and the progress goals relates to running and not cycling. Two small irritations (but not deal breakers): I can't change my location. Even though the app is set up in the metric system, it seems to measure my progress according to the imperial system.
i guess this app has been abandoned. help page doesn't exist anymore. on Android, the menu is blocked now by the bottom bar of my phone and there's no way to fix it.

The synchronisation is quite off between fit and the Mi Fitness app: unless it's an activity, the steps are hardly get maybe 10% of what the Mi Fitness measures, and I know that the Mi Fitness is accurate, because before the synchronization Google used to have the correct number. Shame. The other issue is that "body measurements" in the app means height, weight and body fat%. You should add other measurements too, like arms, chest, waist, hips, thights, etc.

it's true, a lot of content seems to be locked. What is available is very helpful for relaxing. I love the scheduling and tracker to see where I've been. My only real issue comes from the fee, a $59.99 yearly charge after the first seven days. Now as far as pricing goes, that's only $5 a month, but my hang up and there reason it got three stars instead of five, is that there are no other billing options for say just a month. My suggestion would be to give a monthly option. Otherwise wonderful.

not only is this app seemingly built by 4th graders going by how many times it doesnt work as intended, if you choose a guided run, don't even spending any time choosing music to listen to because 90% of these coaches who just love the sound of their own voice too much just spend the entire run yapping so you wont be able to hear your music anyway.

Had been using this app for about a year and it was great. Was able to favorite some songs I liked for meditation, calming anxiety or falling asleep. There was plenty to listen to for free. Disappointed because theres some songs I really like in this app, but there are better apps out there with way more content for free. Gotta uninstall. 😕

Great app to track your workouts. However, I've noticed inaccuracies when it tracks distance ran/walked. Otherwise, it works great.

3 days in, and I've had major connectivity issues 2 of those 3 days. As others have noted, the system isn't exactly stable on Android. Considering the cost of subscription this is simply unacceptable - if they came out and admitted the Android version was an open beta and cut the cost in half, it would be fine, but this product is supposed to be ready for market, and it's clearly not. I have no desire to spend 10 minutes every morning trying to get the strap to connect, and if this behaviour continues I'm going to cancel my subscription and ask for my money back.

I love my fitbit and use it to keep me healthy. The premium fee was expensive, but I thought it might be worth it. I can't see hourly exercise or log workouts. I HATE the AI and can't turn it off. Considering Amazfit or Garmin.

Beginning in March the app started losing my GPS. Same phone I've had since November 2019 and I've always had an Android. Ever since then the mileage tracker and GPS has been spotty. I have Verizon which has great GPS. Not sure if this an Android problem or the apps issue. I tried uninstalling and reinstalling the app. It has helped but hasn't eliminated the issue. Otherwise it use to be my favorite app. Now Im considering trying a different one.
the app has a fine design and has some good recommendations for the marathon training but the gps tracking measured in kms is wrong ( they mixed up conversion from miles to kms) . do not trust it.
Love the app most of the time. However, sometimes it doesn't "connect" when I'm riding, and I can't use the ride controls, etc. And frequently power ups don't show up in the app.

The forced update from the FitBit app made almost everything worse. The new UI is a mess that displays information very poorly. Shows snapshots or tiny tiles of things you should see more detailed at a glance with no way to change it. excellent tool for tracking your miles and progress.

I've had this app for probably 10 years on iPhone, Samsung, and a few cheap Chinese phones and I've never really had any issues. The GPS syncs quickly for me. I mostly just use this app to track runs (used to do bike rides too, no issues there either) and it gives me accurate lap times. I opened this app after some time and I'm at a loss for words. I am very, very disappointed, all you had to do was leave it alone. Luckily there is the adidas app that is what this used to be before they decided to functionally murder it.

The big one for me, you can't check achievements, ongoing challenges and edit your cyclist or bike in the companion app. It should all be done there! Potentially great app.

Updated to Android 16 and now Fit keeps insisting I turn on permissions for bio sensors. There is no permission for this. It does not exist. It won't work unless it has these permissions. I was really enjoying it, but since approx. New Years my app. has been causing my phone to randomly restart. Now the app won't work at all... won't even open. My phone keeps telling me the app won't respond.... paid for this through like July or August..... not cool.

Just awesome 👏🏻 Update: not so awesome anymore.. imho its time to build a new app from scratch. Everytime I open the app I have to login again and change the settings again. update: sept '25 and still the same =( update2: dec '25 problem still exists.. april '26: same...

Not ideal, but very good. You have to shop for prices, dosages and complexes. They are good providing labeling, so shopping is easier. Some of the less stable products require freezing or refrigeration.

When I was in Britain, I'd start recording when I left my hotel, and it gave me a good route to follow when I was heading back to the hotel in the evening. Thank you MMR.

this app, which used to be good, is now useless at tracking cycling routes and distances. completely trashed the app. can no longer manually enter food. they want you to pay for the subscription or get another app in addition to this one. you can't even submit anything to be added in to their system anymore. instead of just keeping the feature that was already available. can no longer see an overview for past days, only the current day, otherwise you have to click each feature individually to look at other days.

Google has managed to ruin a perfectly good app (used to be the Fitbit app). They have scattered all the available information from your device across four different screens. Charts have become less detailed. Information I purchased the device for has simply gone away. Very disappointing.

I LOVE using this app and have recommended it to countless people, however there has been a few occasions where the app doesn't track things properly. For example, after working out this morning and inputting the values, I check my phone a bit later and the values skyrocketed to over 100 move minutes and more than double that in heart points, and continued to add more and more all day at times when I wasn't doing anything. Also has done the opposite after working out, where the values dropped.

The May update ruined the food diary. It's a nightmare to navigate now. I'm copying, adding and deleting foods from/to the wrong day so it's mangling my diary history. The only thing the old diary needed was a way to search your diary history for a specific food. Instead, I pay for Premium, and you've made it HARDER to use. WHY?

A counterintuitive app causing you to spend a lot more time than you think you would having to enter the majority of healthy foods and their nutritional values yourself. If you live in a fast food orientated area like the USA, then this app would probably be easier to use. Nutritional values of the foods are mostly either very inaccurate or missing. I might as well have written my own database and entered the nutritional values myself. A bit of a waste of money.

I really like the app. It gives me enough granularity to understand my activities and sleep. I only wish I could log a nap as an "activity" as there is no way to log any rest during the day. I also haven't seen a category for racket sports. Every time I play a racket sport it thinks ive done swimming. But it's a great free app and I can look at the stats going back a long time too.

Since switching over to the new google health app tracking calories accurately is almost impossible. Even after scanning the barcode the difference is anywhere from 50 calories under to 50 over. Also the app no longer let's me add my own custom recipes. If you want to use it just for steps, it's fine but using it for food is horrible. Use a different app to track your calories.

The Apple Watch app is utterly useless!! It is permanently loading, and conveniently everytime I want to start a workout. It's only reads my heart rate half the time, struggles to register activity and sometimes shuts down half way through a workout. Worst version of the app I have used to date.

4/25/26 edit.......why are the Garmin and Nike apps not talking to each other? Very frustrating! There used to be a feature to manually apply a run to a training program. Because I didn't complete a run through the app using a guided run, the training program shows them as uncomplete. There used to be a way to manually apply a run to the training programs. I wish that feature would return. The guided runs have changed my view on running for the better.

In light of UA's Map My apo collection, this one is redundant. It's nice that it tracks stationary workouts, but Record did that, & much more. With some tweaks, would be a great consolidation of Map My Ride, Run, & Walk, now rendered moot. As a replacement for Record, it is sorely lacking. The elimination of that app leaves a gaping hole for those serious about tracking our health, which is about so much more than merely diet & working out. Limited challenges too. Would love to participate.

This app has managed to keep me on track for two months now. It's not always easy to log your food, especially if it's something homemade and you basically have to add all the ingredients. And having the water drinking tracker as a premium upgrade seems a little strange. But overall, for a free app it's been perfectly sufficient for what I need.

Recently stopped GPS. Took three attempts over three days to reinstall. Like most similar apps, still heavy on the nutrition and cardio emphasis, was awkward to log weight training. Only use ot because a few friends and I track each other's progress, and some good challenges.
No issues with the app and all the meals ive tried have been fantastic. I love that i can add recipes from fhe web SUPER easily too.

I've used this app for years and I like it a lot. My only issue is the program tracking feature. Even though I finish the program workouts and choose the difficulty and place of workout at the end, the app only records completion of some workouts. it'd be nice if we could mark workouts in a program complete ourselves since the app doesn't record the progress properly.

I mostly got this app for the "skip next" feature which I use frequently as my work schedule changes a lot. It's a pretty intuitive alarm app and I like it. But what the hell is the alarm that goes off telling you its bedtime?! Took me forever to figure out how to turn it off and it woke me up several times while was just getting to sleep. What a bizarre feature to be the default.

Used this on iphone years ago & when getting an android phone I installed it there. Pro:  With free account you can transfer all details between iphone & android Con: Android app has no flow chart of weight & body fat loss / gain.

The activity sharing is bad. Lacks design and options. Would recommend others use Strava instead. Garmin needs to start getting creative. On the other hand, I really hope that we would be able to see the average stats between two timestamps in charts instead of the difference between the two points. It's weird for an app like this to not be able to do that.

Help I have used this APP for several years now as part of tracking my journey so it has a lot of history on it. The last time I used it I had trouble with it recording my workout. Now it will not open up. I am thinking if I uninstall it I will loose all my history. Any suggestions? I really don't want to loose my tracking:(
app allows me to map out places where I usually drive. Great to look at local business too.

I downloaded this app to see what options it gave compared to my fitness pal. Very disappointing to see it has no way to track a workout despite the fact there are instructions for it in the help section. I could find no "workout page" as the help section suggested.

Prepear is awesome! I am able to save the recipes I find on different sites that work for my family. I can make notes on the recipe of changes that I made so I can make the recipe over and over. And my original recipes can be saved with pictures. All my family favorites are in one convenient place. No more searching in multiple cookbooks or websites to find that one recipe. If my family likes it I enter the recipe in the app and it's ready for the next time I want to use it. I would recommend this app to anyone who loves to cook and stay organized! Easy peasy bright and breezy app!

This is the fourth time in a row that the app has not behaved as expected. It paused itself at the three and a half mile mark and did not resume for the last mile. I have the settings to auto pause and as such feel it unnecessary to have to ensure it auto resumes. Also it keeps elongating my pace. I start off with the normal and then as I progress through my run, it adds on double the amount so the end of my five mile run, which is around 58 minutes, ends up saying it's taking me 4 hours.

The full discounts don't display properly using the phone app until you're on the final order screen. At times it actually adds the discount rather than subtracting it until the final screen where it shows the discount properly. Other than that, I love the app and how easy it is to make an order.

Not working and keeps going into pause frequently. And holding to finish workout and it's stuck, won't do anything~! It is stuck in "hold to finish" and "resume"~! It messed my workout now up and many previous ones~! It's updated, so that's not the problem, but app is a problem~!

I have had a pretty good experience with this application. It lets me use all of my fitness watch features and tracks activities. Sometimes, I run into editing issues with the tracker. I wish it would beep when you get back to the starting point so I can have the chance to turn it off as I forget to end activities or pause them when I go from one hiking spot to another one.

It's pretty good. I especially like the barcode scanner because it works in Canada. I really wish it displayed calories and nutrient breakdown by meal not just the entire day, but it's not a deal breaker. (Edit: thank you for such a quick response and advice. It's now displaying calories by meal. Excellent product and support!) I've been using a different app for 15 years and only recently moved on to Cronometer so it takes time to adjust.

I have the latest version as auto update is on. Lately, the app has been inconsistent in how it tracks my ride. Most days it's fine. Today it tracked my time for about 7 or 8 minutes, and showed calories burning, but the distance stayed at zero. Distance finally started tracking when I had ridden about a mile and a half. Because the time had already been running, the average speed when the app tracked 2 miles was 3.7 mph, when I know I was riding around 8 mph the whole time. Please fix this!

Absolute nightmare. I've been a subscriber of Whoop since 4.0 first came out, but the latest combination of software and firmware updates has turned this into a useless expensive bracelet. Constantly losing connection to my phone, I have to uninstall, reinstall and repair the device at least twice a week. Now the one thing that that's kept me trying- Bluetooth HR connection to my Concept2 erg- has become uselessly unreliable since the last firmware update. I'm done waiting. Time to move on.

Love this app. Customizing is great. Only complaint is that setting up exercise is a little tedious. You have to go to settings etc. Wish there was just a + button to immediately add a new workout, with the previous one being the default. Instead it takes some poking to get where you want. Other than that, the type, duration, audio coach, all that is amazing. Love UA. Pro America. Unlike Nike.

Fitbit - Google Health, What a complete mess up of an upgrade (downgrade) removing the useful parts like custom calorie entry. changing the whole Interface and not giving customers the option to remain as they are. This has messed up my mental health and I will be removing it as my mental health is more important than your upgrade and metrics Bring back the old format and I might reinstall the app .

It used to be great but now it's a HUGE disappointment! I had calm a year ago and I loved it....I mostly used it for the relaxing sounds. I just downloaded it onto a new device and it asks you to either do a 7-day free trial or pay around $70 to have access. I didn't want to pay such a huge amount when I had used it for free before....but sadly you can't use 95% of the options unless you pay. I'm 100% disappointed!. :(

absolute junk. heart rate tracking is trash and strength straining on this is an absolute chore with you needing to start and stop each set on the phone no way to tap the damn device and do that. already cancelled the sub and will check out Bevel instead.

I like that this app is so closely integrated into the Android OS and that it works well with wearables such as Wear OS. It also covers all the basics that you would expect from a fitness app (steps, active minutes, calories, heart rate, etc...) and connects well with 3rd party health apps. What it doesn't have is workout detection and sleep tracking that many of the top fitness ecosystems have.

After 15 minutes of questions I finally managed to get into the app, I went into recipes to see if I could save my own recipes and guess what? More questions! I just want to use the app 20 min in I'm still answering questions and spinning wheels

I have not tried to use yet . But first off map my ride .is misleading . I wanted to quickly and easily see the routes I have been driving . Not much to it. Yes you can browse, you can shop but you cannot view you set & save. What kind of app does not allow you to manage your set & save?

I've used the Nike Run Club app for a long time and I am very appreciative that it has avoided the trappings of so many other platforms. No ads. No subscriptions. Does exactly what you need and no unneeded extras or paywalls. Setting goals and tracking progress is simple. I have data in the app from as far back as 2011 and it's really fun to see the ebbs and flows in my running habits and pace as someone who runs for fun with occasional training for a race here and there over the years.

Latest update brought "enhancements" that now prevent you from previewing the exercises. Now it's just one big video for the whole thing. Can't see the progression bar per exercises any longer. You can no longer save the workouts. If you go international, you better have a good data plan... Filters equipment search is a joke... look for nothing, "basic equipment" or "full gym".

AI trash Tipps that are often just simply wrong. It has goten much more expensive for this reason. Aslo now in the latest update ingame adverts for their ADDITIONAL SUBSCRIPTION. Go **** a Duck Yatrashio!

Especially since I'm trying to get back on the training horse 💪 I love the workouts but it won't load and display after day 2. I like checking to see if I'm doing it right or knowing when to switch to the next exercise.

I've used this app for nearly 10 years, and recently, it went to a paid model. The paid features the app initially offered a few years ago were of no use to me, so I ignored them. Now, the 5 star app I've been using to track everything has unexpectedly gone to a fully paid model, and there's no way to access my old information or anything unless I pay the fee. Love the app, love the workouts, but you have to fix the sound bugs.. sometime the sound just lowers down and never comes back unless I close and reopen the app. Additionally, I recently got a new phone and the app suddenly lowers the volume of the entire phone (including the trainer's) once the trainer is talking, I can't hear the instructions and can't workout this way. No matter how much I increase the volume it will lower back down on the next instruction. The app is unusable to me this way.

The app used to be excellent and provided different formats for exercises. Time to move to another app
what did y'all do to this app? I was asked to download a different version, lost all my historical data, and then the new app can't even load my 'today sleep's' widget without crashing. Abismal

I have only used the free portion of the app and it's awesome! Very user friendly to enter and track meals. The best lifting app out there. Not only does it track better than any other, but the 5x5 program instills the training fundamentals in beginners. With so much misinformation out there stronglifts is a massive breath of fresh air. Medhi is a great guy. Also highly recommend the email list 👍 Great info for free on all aspects if training.
Great! Can calculate all kind of food and connect with your smartwatch by healthconnect. Calories count is very accurated AI would have to edit nutrition and amont.

I'm in my 70s, so learning new apps is a bit of a challenge, due to lack of confidence. I did not enjoy the add that pops up with no x to delete it so I can get on with what I was looking at the app in the first place.

I bought the lifetime plan and was able to use it for a while. Now, with a recent update, a paid monthly subscription is required in addition to the lifetime access I already paid for. This app is a money grabbing scam. Avoid it if you can. Very disappointing, because it was a nice app.

Quite why Map My Fitness expects me to wait for an email (that never arrived) to verify my email address when I have been using the app for months when I am about to go for a run I really don't know. Annoying American adverts too. Looking for an alternative.

This USED to be my favorite app. Pretty much the only app I'd use on my watch except for the actual clock. Now it's no longer available in the store for Galaxy watch. App looks great. A couple of suggestions 1) need to be able to add items from your recipies to your shopping list instead of typing and 2) be able to scan items from your pantry. Other than that I am happy with this app. Can't wait to try some of these great recipes.
I can't often get into the app...I have the latest version... I've tried uninstalling. Not sure what other options I have. Quite frustrating.
Every time have to refresh it to country, & shopped cart becomes empty.... Give some extra discount & free shipping to international customers, as here in india we have to pay high duty 👎👎 sad part

I couldn't find the phone icon for Garmin Connect because it's called "Connect". Crazy, that could be anything since there is no Garmin reference. I have about 100 apps and Connect was buried among them. I had to Google to find out the real name.

Excelente lugar, todos los instructores son buenísimo! Es un lugar que tiene buena vibra y todo el que asiste lleva su mejor energia/An Excellent place, all the instructors are fantastic! it has a great vibe, and everyone who attends brings their best energy.

good app for a good price but it's starting to bug out and has stopped working for my routine. I use this to book with my local gym classes. It's super easy to use and entices me to explore what else is in my area.

I've had no problems booking for myself. my issue is the fact that they let you list family members but there doesn't seem to be any functional benefit to having this option. Looks like my review disappeared. Love the improvements from the first time it came out. Although buggy and crashes often on my samsung10. Just got it. Edit: 1 month in and my lifts are shooting up at a rate of knots. Baby gains right?! This method is solid for a novice seeking to get strong and make gains and feel great about progress. Not recommended. This app leads you through a lengthy questionnaire to start with and then insists on you buying either an overpriced 3-month plan or a full year at almost 100 bucks! This app is unusable on a Samsung Galaxy s22 ultra. The app opens to a blank white screen and does not load any features. I attend multiple studios who use this service. I wish they didn't. Mind body-- both desktop and mobile app--make it impossible to sign up for classes.

These guys are the best! Great variety, value and speedy shipping. Also trustworthy when handling any issues. Have been a customer for several years. Love them!!! Well 2024 begins a new year but I trust that vitacost remains simply awesome!
This app pretty much sucks. The search mode is terrible, and the website seriously needs improved as well. But if you are going to order use the website, this app is a pain to use.

Excellent at managing workouts and has GPS mapping. I like it for reading-out stats every mile, too. I think I can save my maps, will have to investigate that soon. I'm running out on an LG V20 phone and using a Scosche Rhythm+ 2.0 fitness tracker on my upper arm.

Wast of time. Put things in my cart then look at cart it's empty. Does not work for me at all. This time. Will use my computer. Now three stars.
