Great app for keeping track of your workouts, logging time, distance, calories burned etc. 💪🏃‍♀️👍

I'm new to this whole food allergy thing and I'm still working out the details on exactly which food is the trigger, so my opinion on this app doesn't hold much value at the moment. It's pretty user friendly though, I love that there is a 'how to use app' option in the menu, a lot of apps don't have that option, surprisingly. I'll update my review later when I'm more familiar with keeping track of your food intake. The dev team has done a really good job!

Definitely a lot of options. Personally I prefer really tough workouts. While most of them are challenging. Even the longest advanced ones don't leave you fully burned out. That's my personal preference though. However there are enough workouts and quick ones that make it easy to slip in a workout everyday if you want. The trainers are engaging if a bit over the top, but they do a good job of explaining things.

I downloaded this app as well as mapmywalk to record my walking and riding distance. I do not need the completely unnecessary notifications so I have them turned off. Now everytime I open Mapmyride I get a message saying notifications are off. If I don't want the notifications I also don't want messages about them. Oh and I switched back to an older phone and I could see my progress by pulling down the menu bar So after a few updates that is gone. Why? I am doing other things with my phone.

4.5 stars - It's easy to use and has a number of configurable features that I like including voice updates and coaching, stats, etc. Docked half a star as there isn't a way to set the voice volume directly - sometimes it blasts my ears and sometimes I can barely hear it.

I have been using ( and paying for )this app for about two years. I really do enjoy it, but it lacks some basic features to make it 5 🌟. First, they claim you can download things to listen offline, but it does not work. at least it does not work on a Samsung S24. Second, you can't sort stories by narrator. Adding this would make the app much more user friendly.

I feel its over all easy to use. for being the most recommended food log app im surprised at how inconsistent it is. the food calories are all over the bored. I have to good first. and then find the one closest. the my recipe tab is not what I like. you cant edit recipes once finished. In this crazy new world Zwift provides a fun and motivating way to work on my fitness. A nice free app that let's you plan your daily meals. You can add in recipies from the app, add your own recipe, or link one from the internet. *You have to add a recipe though; you can't just say something like "cereal".

Has been super helpful for me to lose weight! I've been using it since 2015 and it has probably saved me so much money that I could've spent on a gym. I have not found this app to work well on my desktop or mobile. I struggle to get it to do anything I need it to do from logging in to my account to adding a recipe from a website. I doubt I'll continue to use it.

Between the fact the connection (as a bridge for my sensors) regularly drop off and tank my training session right at the surges, and the fact I can't even login anymore!! I clearly can't recommend this app more than that... I'd really want to but it's really just a pain in the a**!

I like the concept of this app. I would gladly give 5 stars if it were a bit more intuitive/user friendly. For example, I accidentally added a recipe to the wrong day and could not figure out how to remove it. I also was not able to delete a recipe I imported from another website to my cookbook.

I generally like the app - it's got some great workouts and the plans are a great way to keep on top of your fitness.  Like others have said, it can be quite buggy. I often don't get videos loading randomly for exercises, certain exercises will have the wrong video mapped to them, etc.  It would be great to get WearOS functionality. There's a lot of apps out there, but if you could do all your exercise and stream music without your phone, I think it would push this app to next level.

Speaking strictly about the app and not the strength training program, it is easy to use and straightforward. I paid the small fee for premium and appreciate the extra features. It really has everything I need so I can focus on actually lifting. Auto records the weight, auto timer with audible sound when it's time to lift, plate calculator for when my brain is stupid at 5am. I like it.

Some functions on the app are acceptable; none are great. In particular, the sleep quality app is useless, completely. It continually rates sleep as "poor," apparently because there is a "stress" metric that drags down everything else. And it is impossible to disable this stress metric. I do not need to have my stress level applied to the algorithm (it isn't even a "metric" that returns a value!). I do need the other values (which do return values) to be calculated as useful sleep score.

Annoying ads that you can't skip, ok app made in Replit by AI. Could be better with less ads and without occasional lag. Also hiding tracking apps behind a paywall is scummy, my steps won't work with the default app because Xiaomi doesn't have a good one that works.

This is WAY better than dealing with online recipes. this is a great app and you get a lot of features with the free version. the paid version is not crazy expensive and it really helps with meal planning. im glad I found this app because it has helped me stay on track in many ways.

I can't believe that something so comprehensive and varied is actually FREE. This running application offers a comprehensive suite of features to support your fitness journey. It thoughtfully tracks your runs, providing valuable insights into your progress over time. The user interface is quite intuitive, making it easy to navigate and set your personal goals. Many users find the motivational prompts to be a helpful addition for staying on track. Overall, it presents itself as a reliable companion for runners of all levels.

They could definitely add more in terms of features. As a companion app I have no issues joining events, messaging other Zwifters, using power ups and getting a general overview of stats for my ride in progress. I've had to uninstall the app and reinstall it twice. Also, it doesn't always capture accurate mileage when you're running indoors as opposed to my fitness watch.

There's a couple of really annoying things about this app; the calories burnt goes down when I'm putting most effort in cycling uphill and they go up very fast when going downhill, putting very little effort in. the average speed is also off a lot and after a few miles I can be going much faster than my average for those first few miles for over the next mile, but the average speed goes down and there's been a difference of about 20 percent in the distance it shows for the exact same route.

Some hashtag items have a more consistent sleep percentage than others, so it would be neat to have some kind of number to reflect that. app is not accurate to the tic watch fit app. the fit app on the watch will show 1500 steps during a walk while the app on the phone will say 0 or 100. this number can change during the say but will never be the correct amount. this is not limited to steps. it can be the distance, or calories burned as well. this is very frustrating and would love it to be corrected. oh and if you just log the activity to the phone application instead, the problem will still continue to happen.

Calm is a great concept, but know in advance there's some major limitations. For instance, sometimes it takes me a while to fall asleep. The Sleep Stories are wonderful, but when a story ends, there's no transition to another story. Without a continuous playlist or a timer, I have to pick up my device at the end of each story, get blinded by the light, and activate another story. Understandably, the light and the movement wake me up again. I wrote the company, and their response was a template thanking me for my "thoughtful feedback." The two longest stories are 45 minutes in length, but most run about 30-ish minutes, with others lasting 20, 19, 15, or even a mere 12 minutes. Those people who can consistently fall asleep in 12 minutes probably wouldn't need this feature. But what about the rest of us?  If this app had a playlist or a timer feature for Sleep Stories, I'd surely give it 5 stars!

Update has created new issues. The app is no longer syncing my walks with My Fitness Pal. When I try to view my activity in the Map My fitness app. The entire app freezes. This is a first for me. The app usually never gives me problems. This is a poor update.

this app us very helpful and useful in my daily workout at the same time it reminds you of how many kms you have done without looking at your phone.

They have in the past year put some key features (Barcode scanning) which I fell in love with behind the paywall but it's still a really good and reliable app. Update: this latest update has removed the edit/check box selection feature making deleting multiple items from my phone impossible. I don't like it. Please restore.

I love that the app has a mix of precise logging for when I'm home as well as AI guesstimations for when I'm out and about. -1 star because the "helpful" reccomendations after you log food are the most annoying thing ever and cannot be permanently turned off. They're constantly nitpicking, even when you've picked an objectively good choice for your macros/calorie goals. Also don't love that you can't adjust meal calorie goals for weekdays. It should be more customizable for the price.
Can't log in. Contacted customer support and they were no help. Was informed to use the website.🙁

Unfortunately im not interested in this App, but i downloaded and rate to support you guys against the ridiculous Apple suit against you, stay strong guys!! 💪 Edit: i try it and find really good, now is the favorite app of my mom. And compliments on how you make really easy to increase/reduce the portion. Good Job.

Why did the product team or whoever call the shots change this app? The new format is clunky. The long videos do not stream smoothly (it's not an Internet problem); the video format itself is inferior to the previous version that had much more control and better guided training. Besides, my preferred workouts have gone, I couldn't find them in my historicals. I've been using this app since 2017. Uninstalling now.

I used to use this app all the time, then it started crashing to point I had to move on, couldn't get through 5 minutes without a playback error. Tried everything to get it working, very disappointed. When the app worked it's great, but it's not worth the frustration dealing with a faulty product, there's plenty of other apps out there.

I really like this app when it's working. It's motivating to see how many heart points I've earned for my different workouts. However, it often fails to show the correct number of points. One day it'll show 20 points for a workout, and the next day those points will disappear. I have screenshots that show different number of heart points on the home page and on the journal page. It's very DEmotivating when this happens.

Only works about half the time I open it. Sometimes it doesn't open at all, other times I'll sign in but it won't recognize it, or I get "Whoops. Something went wrong..." I've reported to support, and both times was told to uninstall/reinstall, but when that works, it's only for one or two uses. It's just become too frustrating. I HAVE to use Mindbody to sign up for classes at my Y, else there's no way I'd keep trying to use it. Now I usually wait to be on a PC and go that route.

The update is awful for people who like to plan out meals and adjust portions/ selections based on calorie totals. Ex: the 'diary view' only shows that breakfast is 465 calories (each item is hidden, only 1 shows) - so you have to swipe over to 'full view' to see each ingredient & its calories ... but there's no total calories in that view. So, if my goal was to have 400 calories for breakfast, I have to adjust items in the full view page THEN go back to the diary to check the total cals.

Great app for tracking food. I've been using this app for 2 months. it's very useful and easy compared to an other famous one. I don't use the AI advices and it's a little friction in the usage. would be nice if we could deactivate it. edit : the friction is when I add something to a meal. then i come back to the total cal / carb / prot. there is always a screen with 3 tips for the meal. I never wait for them to pop and I have to select "skip" to go to the main screen.

This has been my go-to fitness app since 2011. I LOVED IT!!!!! I would always recommend it to friends and family. But after the update, all the things I loved about it, are gone. Being able to download the workouts, seeing the workout routine before downloading, playing music during the workout, having rest times in between, and tracking progress. I hate that after 12 years, I am thinking about finding a different app. 😕

Most diet apps are calorie counters which doesn't help its all about the carbs and protein people! Low carb and high protein will get you the best body you can have! That's why I love this app, it tracks what I truly care to see.

New version barely functional and is visually unappealing. Takes forever to open up. Plate calculator is visual instead of written out, which is annoying. Automatically deloads without asking or telling if it has been a few days. Hard to see where you were last time. Does not let you mark sets complete from the notification - you have to go into the app. I miss this the most from the old one. Alerts are inconsistent - sometimes it dings, sometimes nothing. The old one was awesome.

I haven't decided to upgrade, yet, but the free version is quite usable and has great info. Some reports are a little awkward, but still useful. Ads aren't too intrusive. I've tried several trackers, and this is the first I've considered paying for. A free trial might put me over the edge so I could see what I'd pay for and if it's worth it.

Since the "refresh" the app has become more cumbersome to use. No favorites, or custom food for calorie tracking. Even using the recent when you add food you would think it would ask you when you ate it, nope just dumps it all into the Other category. The interface aesthetically looks nice, but again not streamlined in the least. Overall, needed more time in the oven!

Good product, bad customer service. I was on the fence about buying Whoop due to reported connectivity issues but I've had no issues at all. I find the data to be pretty accurate and useful for tracking my sleep and fitness. However, the customer service is really lacking. I wanted to return the extra band I bought but you have to email support to do so and it took 9 emails from me to finally get a label to return it. A shame that a good product can be tarnished by poor service.

I have been using this app for almost 10 years now. This app is super intuitive and easy to use. The bluetooth connect works like a charm and has never given my Android or Garmin a problem. I love all of the data that is available for tracking your activities. However, I am sad to see that Garmin has started charging a fee for their "premium" content. The cost of the watch is so high already. I unfortunately can not justify paying another $70 for content that used to be free.

The app itself works pretty well. Pretty easy to add activities and weight. I like the widget for tracking how I'm doing. One major complaint: it's too hard to get a useful overview of my data. Mobile isn't the best form factor for that, so I would like to view it on my desktop. But for some inexplicable reason, Google shut down their Fit website. Please offer a way to see my data on my computer!

I've been using the app for years, and it serves its purpose. I don't like that my progress doesn't save sometimes, so workouts show as incomplete. I also don't like that I can't connect it to my Samsung Health app. There also used to be a feature that allowed for notifications when it was time to workout. Update: Just finished a program, but the workouts aren't marked as complete so I have to give a negative review of the program to end it manually..but I liked it.

I've never heard of this product. I was gifted this device for my birthday and I absolutely love it. The battery life is amazing and I love how it tracks my sleep, recovery, strain, stress and body temp, it helps keep me accountable and informed of whats happening with me. The device is expensive but it does have a lifetime warranty. I love the simplicity. no screen just a band. However it does need more band selection, maybe something a little more cute and fashionable to complement my outfits.

After the switch to Google Health, the AI assistant is now a mandatory central feature that can't be turned off. There's a toggle for it in settings, but it doesn't do anything. To make it worse, the AI is wrong more often than not. It falsely logs exercises I didn't do, fails to log exercises I did do, and won't accept corrections, making the entire point of logging activity pointless.

Worked great for a couple of years, until last weekend when it shut off in the middle of a 50-mile ride. Now it crashes whenever I try to start it. I cycled on/off my blu tooth and GPS. Brand new phone, 10Se. Uninstalled and then re-installed but didn't fix the problem. Two years of training records gone. Lesson learned. Nothing beats a pencil and a notepad. Good riddance.

All of a sudden I couldn't connect my Garmin Edge 530 to Garmin Connect (both iPhone and Android). Tried resetting both phone and bike comp, uninstalling Garmin Connect app but no luck. Fixed it and I'll give 5 stars.

Works great, has a variety of captchas/challenges to help you really wake up, as well as other really used features like checking if you really woke up, bedtime reminders and a host of other useful settings and features. Reliable and consistently updated.

What a major disappointment from what the Fitbit app used to be. I was looking forward to this update because I believed it would have more data tracking, or the things like the food tracker would be both easier to use while also have a bigger food database. Instead this new version of the app is just another way to sell ad space without caring about the user experience. where once I was able to see all my data in a quick glance, now it is just ad space. Looks like I need a new watch. 😔

For such a high priced service, app has some flaws, like trying to add an activity such as morning workout, but forgot to manually turn sleep off. Rather then automatically end sleep at workout time, it forces you to go back to home screen and you can't edit sleep because it just says "pending" Also, on android, the buttons to submit or move forward are over the navigation is buttons, so you can't press them without exiting the app.

I downloaded this app way back in 2017, I supported them by purchasing the full version which was maybe $8 at the time. I've used it sporadically since then up until now. I went to log a workout and everything had been reset and was told my version of the app was no longer available and must pay a subscription. I purchased your app in its infancy and you take it away so you could make more money. Don't even waste your time on these money grabbers, find a better app like I will be doing

Not all Sleep Stories are created equal. Some are perfectly dreamy, but, sometimes, the reading is less than professional or the vocabulary is narrow. Calm's idea of A-list artists differs from mine, but, discovering new voices can be good. I've only just seen the rating feature, but, as the goal is to be asleep by the end of the session, it will probably go unused. If there is a timer, I haven't found it. I fell asleep to train sounds and was awakened by same, several times in the night. (ب_ب)

Krogger bought Vitacost, and I suspect they have retired this app. I can't even search I vitamin C and bring up results. It's like it's no longer connected to a database. It looks like the website is working for now.

the Google heath app sucks. the layout of the fitbit app was way better! I had a bar that showed calories in by a bar that had calories burned and I could see it for long periods of time like that! now if I want to compare the two I have to jump back and forth from different section on the app! the least you could do is give us the option to go back to the old app

The new "improvements" are inaccurate. Plus you can no longer add fitness goals and if you erase old goals you can't get them back. Good for have an idea of how much you've walked but it's glitchy and will change your stats for no reason. Example today i walked .48 miles around a store but now it says "0" miles but it still shows the time. Also fails to register all of your steps. The old design was much more accurate.

Amazing device with highest accuracy compare to any available devices in market. keep track of your overall health and provide deep statistics of your body. Been using from last 2 years, must recommend.
Are you sure that is an application???I think that is a third quilty browser...

Really liked the app but the recent update was a step backwards for UX. I can no longer see all of my food in my diary without clicking on each meal separately, making it hard to verify if I've actually tracked everything I meant to for the day. On my diary, I wish my total calories for the day stayed visible at the top even as I scrolled down through the meals.
I really like this app, it's easy to use, clear to read, everything you would want for no fuss tracking.

My trackers are no longer what I set most of them to, some information has become completely inaccurate. I don't know what they did recently when switching to Google health, but almost all of the settings I made/adjusted have either been changed without notice or the information that's supposed to be based on input is completely unrelated. The app seems to be showing input from someone else's account and mine.

The nutritional side of this app needs a lot of work. I can't put in custom foods or use Gemini to provide an estimate from a picture, which is important for when you are in a restaurant. Also, the total amount of calories "allowed" needs to adjust per activity like Garmin does it with their connect+ subscription. There needs to be a widget for total miles cycled per week just like for running. I was hoping this could eventually be my go-to fitness app but it isn't there yet.

It's getting worse over time. I have a membership for unlimited classes in two studios. I used to be able to have my default search for fitness classes to filter to those two studios, show me all classes available, sorted by time. This was very convenient. Now it only shows me a bunch of locations, and when I go to classes they are sorted in random times, I need to manually apply the studio filters.

Used to be great. Now even things that were free are behind a paywall. I'd be fine with paying, but I need the option to unlock specific sounds. The whole time I had this app, I only ever used the crickets soundscape, so it doesn't make sense for me to be paying for all those other options. For $70 a year, I'll just go record my own crickets when I go back home to the countryside. For $3-5 per sound, however,I'd be down. Devs- maybe something to think about? I'm sure I'm not the only one.

Great app that does everything it claims to, AND which you can get ALL features of by paying for it once! one time!! that is so insanely rare these days, I've used it daily, and i paid for this several years ago, i super recommend it for that reason alone! i also use the integration in the app so it controls my bedroom lights etc., which is my main use case together with the 'soft wake' using said lights, and it works super well and makes me feel less tired when i wake up.

Such a shame! It was my go to fitness app. You guys have RUINED something which didn't need any fixing.First, the "plans" feature was removed. Then,it was the downloadable workouts. Now, we need a very fast and stable internet connection for the workouts to run smoothly, and even then, there is constant buffering during the workouts.Imaging being halfway through a tough workout phase and it BUFFERS! On top of all that, customer service doesn't even respond.
Good app. Creating a course could be better. But creating workouts is great!

A Wearable for Data Science nerds. This is a complex app collecting data almost endlessly, and you are paying not only for that, but it to compile that data; make connections about your results; and give you tips on performing your best. I've been with whoop almost two years and it's been a game changer for getting my health back in line. I can print the weekly and monthly reports and take them to my primary care doctor and go over how to improve. Love it.
still can't listen to music while using the app. the app will randomly change volume levels of the music, the app, and notifications. sadly, they really don't care about Android users.

decent app and I've used it for a long time. but I just got a FitBit and have it connected. when I go to sleep at night, it'll say "connecting to FitBit" and have a progress bar. when I wake up, the "connecting to FitBit" notification is still there. also, it would be nice if it could use the FitBit motion sensor so I don't have to leave my phone on the bed at night. so many options and settings can be overwhelming but I like the customization possibilities.

I would die without this app! It is the only reason I'm able to sleep at night. I saw an advertisement once that was 25 minutes long, but decided I'd watch the whole thing and give it a try. I apparently zonked out within the first FIVE MINUTES (it was narrated by Stephen Fry, and he is incredibly perfect for this and should definitely do more!). After that I bought it on the spot and use it almost every night. I have several favorites that downloaded offline for plane rides even.

The new food log is awful! Sometimes the food I ate is not in the database, so I have to log something comparable, but I can no longer look at the macros before deciding to log a food item. This has made the process time consuming and a hassle. No one WANTS to really log their food, and making it a hassle is a great reason why people give up on it. Fitbit's wasn't perfect, but it was SO much better than what there is now. Please fix this.

this is not a bad app...but it doesn't do a good job with integration from other apps. for example, I use a Garmin to track my sleep and that doesn't get uploaded no matter how many times I've "reconnected/resynched" the apps . I'm looking for a one-stop shop (nutrition , exercise, sleep) and this doesn't offer it. for a calorie counter/tracking though...its great;

has potential but so far pretty poor. I was impressed by how easily it syncs my Garmin data, but despite it having heart rate data, it doesn't count toward cardio load in the app. also the training plan is not super helpful. it just regurgitates what I told it I want to do. it might be okay for a beginner, but for someone who wants to see a full plan in the calendar, it would be nice to have the AI produce that and be able to edit it over time using the coach. it's also missing a lot of metrics.

What happened to this app? It used to work so well. Now, it seems like half the app doesn't work anymore. Not even deleting and redownloading it, fixes anything. My progress bar is gone, the calculation for how much weight I'd lose is busted, if I click on my progress picture sometimes it just disappears and I lose it forever. It used to not be this bad. It's sad, because it's one of the best calorie counting apps. Plz fix things, these are all issues I've dealt with for a long time.
Login needs to be fixed. Cannot use as it repeatedly tells me that my username/password is incorrect. App is useless until fixed.
This company is awesome 2 day shipping cheap and everything works as advertised thank you so much

The concept is amazing. The multi tier access membership is the done thing these days. I dislike it but understand it. Ticketsatwork had a promotion for a 1 year subscription. I purchased 2 of them because it seemed like a good value. I just activated one of them and realized it is a lower tier subscription from what allows me access to what I want but I can pay additional for premium access. Yea ok...if that's the case, I'd have bought it...

why can't I disable the stupid notification telling me I can locate my edge device?!? I KNOW I CAN LOCATE IT!!! You know what I rarely need to do, though? use that feature! Once in 5 years is not a reason to give me a notification every single time I use the device.

I purchased the lifetime plan for the app years ago. Now they moved to a subscription only service and lock you out of using any of the old features I love. Disappointing and will never use this again. disgusting behavior from an app creator to suddenly lie about purchases, and remove something you paid for.
love it! I also love the training programs for running. I only wish I could find something similar on the app but for strength training.

so far, the app has been great and easy to use. I only wish they had an AI scanner as some of the other apps out there. Their food catalog seems to be extensive, however, sometimes its tricky to find the item you're looking for, so you need to chose the closest thing to it, and the portion size/measurements could also use a bit of a broader range, as the two to four options it gives you, don't always reflect what you've actually eaten. Do keep in mind, that I am using the free version.

I'm woman of a certain age & did not work out for over 10 years. I used to love the gym, spin classes, body pump etc. I was very conscious of not only my body shape but my physical health. This class has not only given my confidence back but a bit of my body shape. I'm building my 'gran bod' for my future health & stability

Worst Running App by far i've encountered the running app feezing up for countless amount of times which means they are not tracking my mileag and i tried closing the app, turning off my GPS,Data and switched on airplane mode, i even tried the "Stop" button in app and it keeps on loading, only way is to restart my phone,many times i was halfway through a run and it froze, have to restart my phone which spoils my whole running rhythm, i have wrote in feedback many times to no replies....

Bring back the Fitbit app. Everything I needed to see was conveniently on one page. Google took all of those stats, threw them in a hat, and have placed them in random locations across 4 tabs. Google cannot even properly reverse engineer an app they bought several years ago. What a piece of junk. What is worse is that I purchased their pieces of junk. Well, fool me once. New users of the app that never used Fitbit probably will not even notice and sadly that is probably their strategy.

Overall, this is a great app! I find that when I actually enable sleep tracking (especially with my watch) I tend to wake up feeling more well rested (even if I didn't let myself sleep very long). That said, setting the alarm/nap time can be a little frustrating at times when you tap on the time. It pulls out the keyboard which is rarely necessary for scrolling through a list. Developers are very responsive to any sporadic issues

The latest update is dissapointing. I liked being able to preview workouts. Now I have to commit to a 30 minute workout without knowing what's in it. That doesn't make sense to me. A personal trainer would definitely outline a workout before starting it. Now the workouts are just long videos that probably use a ton of data. The app also logs you put frequently and the Chromecast function is gone.

After most recent update I can no longer log in. It's just a constant circle of entering log in details to be taken back to log in page. From reviews this seems to be happening to everyone. With current covid 19 restrictions on class sizes it's important to be able to book classes asap

Pretty decent features for not having to purchase anything. I wish you could adjust the speed and pitch of the voice that tells you when you reach every mile. Also I'm using this app to track my routes on mountain bike trails, some grades get real steep so my pace slows way down and the app thinks I've stopped. So adjusting the "auto paused" threshold would be superb.

my go to app when things bo bad on the scale. @developer any option to add the ability to jump to a specific week/date in the nutrition calendar plz? would be great to be able to go to eg last year's food without the need of clicking week by week.

I love Vitacost. The App is only OK compared to their awesome website. The website has a great filter. The filter on the app is limited. Its impossible to filter out everything your not looking for forcing you to page through hundreds of products your not interested in. There is no sorting by low to high price and visa versa.

The old FitBit app was better. In the calorie tracker, I wasn't able to enter/add items not in the database. When I tried to change a quantity to something less than 1 (i.e., .5 or .25), I got an error message saying the quantity had to be more than zero. Very frustrating "upgrade" when the old app was far more flexible/robust.

Used to be 5 star and I'd recommend it to everyone. Now I can't get the in workout music to turn off and it plays over my music. Which makes it unuseable for me. Need to listen to my music to do the cardio. Also the video workouts that used to be white board workouts are useless. Previous review: Great trainer led classes as well as routines to follow. Love the programs and they keep adding content. Great for working out at home. Best of all it's free! Something good to come out of the pandemic.
It kept telling me my user name and password were incorrect, even though they work on the website. Uninstalled.

i like this app but i have a couple of issues which is why I'm only giving it 3 stars. when I'm hiking and lose Data, the app will freeze and sometimes crash. it won't always record all the data. also you are unable to start tracking a workout if there's no data. last issue is that it's unable to sync with my Garmin watch. the Samsung fitness app on my old s7 had none of these issues.

Finally not oversleeping! I haven't used every feature, but what I've used is fantastic. The app music isn't very effective, but you can use any sound from your phone. And it's able to memorize any barcode, which you can use as the off switch and put on the other side of house. Until you scan it, it won't turn off, and you can't change volume. EDIT: Omg, there's even the option to prevent phone shutdown. I was freaking out because I developed the habit of restarting my phone in my sleep haze!
Great App! Works effectively and efficiently. Many options to set your workout. Accurate and supported well.

I just switched to this app from Fitbit, so change is always hard. I really don't like that I cannot rearrange the tiles on my dashboard. I can add or remove tiles, but the ones I removed still have the blank box where the tile was removed from. The remaining tiles should move up to fill the gaps, at minimum. Ideally, I could rearrange them while in edit mode so I can put them in order of importance to me. Hopefully that upgrade will come soon. Otherwise the app is fine.

Insanely buggy. App opens itself randomly while playing games in other apps. Constantly disconnects from zwift, often wont reconnect. Useless. Stop asking me to re-review it in app. Its still a buggy mess. One screen in the app says I have a powerup while the other screen in the same app says I dont. The coffee button works while its grayed out. Ive never seen an app so bad.

Frequently resets to a loading circle screen when trying to navigate through the app and gives me error messages that something went wrong or there's no connection when all other apps have wifi. Very frustrating as it takes 2-3x as long to book classes often because of the resets and errors.
maps ride in real time. you can see progress as you do it.

Great content but can't download program for offline use. The standalone workouts can be downloaded but if you want class/coached programs there is no way to download a video so u can use somewhere without wifi (outside, gym w/out internet). Cant leave the app midworkout to grab equipment after description, to start music or troubleshoot a connection. Hit pause, leave the app it marks as complete. No way to resume. I'm loving the free content just hard to take with. I'd pay to download programs!

Bluetooth isn't working with latest update. My Wahoo kickr and Tickr Heart rate sync and disconnect constantly (every few seconds). Unable to train please fix immediately. 05/03/2021 Update: Was able to train but still dropping issues. Up until Feb 2021 I wasnt having issues like this, but now it has become a serious issue. What is going on?

I'm trying to delete an old account of mine but it literally will not let me. I've tried following the directions THEY give but after selecting "Data Preferences" the site that it redirects to literally doesn't exist. I've tried emailing support, finding a support form, and using their terrible chatbot that just parrots the same directions (THAT DON'T WORK) at me. Be warned that if you sign up for an account you're apparently signing up forever so they can steal your data forever.

Hasn't functioned properly for weeks. I keep oversleeping because of it. Plus, when you set a second alarm to keep from oversleeping the first, it disables the second alarm when you snooze the first. I used to love this app. I used to recommend it to everybody, not anymore. -- to developers. my battery optimization software has exceptions for the app. and the snooze DISABLES all other alarms, not silence them for the duration of the snooze. your suggestions are a cop-out.

Been using it for several years and has never let me down. I highly recommend it. Programmable wake up alarms with a rotating series of tones (so I don't get used to one... or end up hating a song), go to sleep notifications, sleep tracking, and more. I can't imagine my daily routine going back to an old alarm clock. Ever.
The only free calorie calculator app to let you scan barcodes. Smaller food label sample pool but you can make do.

Love the app. My only issue is logging of my food items. I try and I keep getting blocked with a message that reads... Oops something went wrong. Error Code: 412. Customer Support... what is this and how can I fix it or are you guys honing in on the problem with a fix soon??? Thank You. Will update my rating as I navigate the app extensively throughout usage.
A wonderful place to get your gear...when it works. Logging into the app comes with a message error now and it's starting to lag. Otherwise, a great deal of products harder to find, even on GNC.

Woud gi e this app 10 stars if possible. Love seeing where I have been and how far I have come toward goals. Great app. Love it. Would give more stars if I could. Just an all-around great (and accurate) app. Will recommend to anyone.
I like it. I use this in my training an when I go to the gym an it helps me to go harder and push my limits chur

Very unstable and full of bugs. For example: parts of the UI go missing randomly; the map sometimes fails to completely load and cannot be moved; Bluetooth connection often fails, especially while running; loses connection to server every now and then. All this could be averted of those lazy folks at Zwoft would add support for Windows+Bluetooth, just like the people behind the Tacx Trainer application (for Windows 10) have.

Bluetooth nightmare. Constantly asks me to connect to a Bluetooth device that doesn't have anything to do with me when trying to start a Zwift session and connecting Bluetooth through the Companion App. Just connect to the 3 devices I ALWAYS use. When it does connect completely random "No signal" errors.

Best free calorie tracker right now. Pros: easy food entry interface, extensive catalog of food options, good graphs. Cons: the app name, and the recipe entry system. It requires you to publish your recipe to everyone. I can't just enter that I made 6 servings worth of whatever-was-in-my-fridge soup to get accurate portion entries for me for the next few days, it has to be a permanent formal recipe for everyone to see

I've been using this app for a while and it's an amazing workout. Some of the reviews say it's only a beginners routine, I completely disagree. I switch up my workouts to maintain mussel growth but this is my core routine that I always come back to. The app works very well and I recommend getting the pro version it's well worth the money. Just remember you have to do the workouts, just having the app on your phone doesn't get you pumped moving the weights does. And eat, eat, and eat some more!
I enjoy using the Whoop app. It provides a lot of detail in a good format.

This is a great exercise app for working out at home. I love the variety of workouts and the different levels of challenge. The personal trainers that I have experienced so far have been great. They are energetic and positive. I find them to be motivating. I love how they have videos of how to do exercises with proper form. One feature I love is being able to bookmark workouts that you like set, you can go back to them. I'm looking forward tomorrow workouts on the nike training club app.

I've been using this app for about 6 years now and all is fine, but an age old problem which has never been addressed is the LOST GPS SIGNAL on long duration usage. endomondo has given a list of trouble shooting tricks for this problem (that means they know there is a problem with this app) but none of these seem to work. may be its for us to upgrade to a better alternative??

I paid for a lifetime upgrade previously. Now the app has moved to subscription only after I changed my new phone. I couldn't restore my purchase. App did not provide lifetime service as promised. Integrity check.
Money grabbing scum; use other websites, for supplements and plans
Really user friendly app with so much choice of various activities to suit your needs. Graphics are also great!

I really want to like this app, but for me it can be very unreliable. Sometimes it just doesn't load, sometimes it shows gyms as not having classes and I have to go via the instructors to book a class. There are loads of good features, but that doesn't really matter when the most basic functions aren't consistently working
