I am in the middle of completing the 30 days to meditation for 10 minutes A-day as I wake up. It is just the right amount of time at the start of a busy day to slow down and focus and be mindful. I think this is ideal for beginners and I use many of the techniques with my students at school. The sleep stories put me out like a light! Be warned that some of the voices of the guides may be irritating to you. Fortunately, there is a large library of available Meditations led by varied voices.

It's really disruptive to my ride when Companion loses the ride I'm currently doing. I have to close the app and re-open it to get back into the dashboard for the ride, and I lose all of the messages from my group. I ride Zwift for the community, and this issue ruins that element. Zwift Companion also doesn't recognize my power-ups, so I have to reach for my laptop to use them

Scanning barcodes, using AI, etc. are on one side, what's the point if I can't even enter a meal's macros manually (not just calories, but the rest)? The free version is useful only if someone else has already added your meal to their database. I like the app, but I deleted it after seeing this greediness.
Gives you access to dailyburns food db, but interface is clunky. Cant edit entries or track exercise.

Update, with headphones you hear the cues fine Great app except for the audio cues, they are not loud enough, esp for slow down one, I miss the cue bc it is so low. When I realize it, it is time to run fast again. The app I had before you could hear the cues clearly, but the accuracy of the cues were waaay off so I'm trying endomondo again

I had a knee replacement, then 6 months later the other one was done. Before the first and after, and again after the 2nd op I have cycled so I could build muscle up. This app has been great at letting me know where I've been, how hard the ride was (elevation and such), and the distance. All I need to know, it does more but I don't use that as it's not relevant to my needs.

Ive only been using the app for a couple days now. I like it so far but my only issue is that its constantly trying to get me to do the paid version. Everything I do I get the pop up and I cant immediately exit or say no. I have to let it do its thing and hit continue to be able to exit it and move on. Id rather have ads.

Bought the pro version some time ago. Could still use it the way I wanted when the premium subscription became a thing, but they have started to disable features I used since then, and are gradually giving me less and forcing me to pay, if I want to keep using basic stats like "lap speed". Meanwhile the app that came with my Jabra ear buds gives me all of that and way... WAY more without a monthly sub. So yeah.. uninstalled (no affiliation with Jabra, just fed up with endomundo)
Compared to the apple version this is not worth the attempt.

This is the only app you need. Practical and uncluttered I have been using it for nearly 3 years now and reccomend the Pro version. As a middle aged man who travels a lot the visual display of the weights I need to select plus the ability to change the inventory based on what is available makes it worth every penny.

There doesn't seem to be a way to schedule a class that you've already paid for, they always ask for payment even though you purchased a package.... It doesn't seem to connect with your acct accurately. Annoying!!! You have to call to schedule due to the lack of communication between the app and your acct.

After using this app for 4739 days, the latest "NEW MYFITNESSPAL" is a terrible change. Each item you enter doesn't stay in the order you enter it. Meal item times take on the entry time, and if you are altering the meal time, you have to edit the time for each item. It's not rocket science to know that all meal items will have been eaten at the same time. There is so much more I could complain about, but time and characters are running out. It is simply a terrible mess to a good app.

I like the app and the option to add custom foods and drinks, however the ads are getting insane. Every time I add something that I've eaten, and ad pops up after. I'm not talking after adding a meal, I'm talking after adding each individual food. I ate carrots- ad. I had a glass of milk- ad. It is making it difficult to use the app because of how often it pops up, instead of quickly logging my meal, I'm sitting there for 10 minutes trying to add my calories accurately
Locked everything behind a paywall due to adding features I don't need or use. Now I'm losing 7 years of history and switching to another option.

Fraudulent, so I open the app to start using it. 1st off it wants your Facebook or email to create a account. Then 2nd they only give you a 7-day trial and after that its 69.99 per year. I thought this was a free app. My mistake if I missed that disclosure somewhere, but you got to give someone more than 7 days to feel this out if you want my 70 bucks a year. I think that is so deceitful you get my info so you can bug me, when your app didn't do anything.

This alarm app does everything I need it to. I can keep multiple regularly-scheduled alarms, as well as alarms that aren't scheduled and I can turn on when needed. I like the sleep tracking, but I don't really use it much anymore except to monitor my sleep deficit. A feature I really like is the ability to craft different length snoozes; my first snooze is 5 minutes, then 2 minutes, then 1, then none. What I really don't like is that when I use the postpone feature, the "skip next" button is right next to it, where one might expect an "accept" button. I'm pretty sure I've been late to work a number of times because of this button's horrible placement.

Fell off lifting for a few years and got way out of shape, this program and app have been great for getting back into it. It's a great program and easy to follow especially if you're just getting into lifting or getting back to it. My only regret is not getting the premium version right away, it makes it so much easier

Generally get good data about my rides on this and I like using it while on a ride. But sometimes things don't work, especially u-turns which never seems to work. Sadly today I can't open the app as it says it is out of date, but following the link to the play store there's no update available so for the moment it's unusable.
This app is very useful in tracking rides and progress. It keeps track of all of the things for you so you can focus on riding.

used to use Map my walk, but it would stop and discard my walk if the screen timed out or if I would use another app. Got Uninstalled pretty quick. this one so far...No problems. not with stopping on me (or any other issues, for that matter.) plus GPS is very accurate. Only reason 4 instead of 5 stars, is I haven't used it for very long yet. easy to navigate, but I need to explore the app a little more. see what I might be missing. also, I haven't even noticed any ads. are they there?

everything remotely useful is behind paywalls and it's a pain to navigate. all I'm looking for is white noise type stuff to sleep to, not all the rest of this BS. good luck finding that. the ui is shiny but needlessly complicated. deleting immediately

I love this app because not only does it allow me to track my runs but also it helps me hold myself accountable by viewing how much more I need to train to meet my running goals. Recommend to everyone! Bonus: it let's you add your friends so your able to cheer them on when they go for a run which is great bc everyone could always use extra motivation!

I've been a long time Fitbit user, mostly because it had some of the most accurate sleep tracking. Since being forced to switch to Google Health, the sleep tracking has become hot garbage. It's now incredibly inaccurate. There are times that I know I was awake, up, and doing things that it will say I was fast asleep. Unless Google reverts back to the accurate sleep tracking or somehow fixes this, I'll be in the market for a new device.

Useful functionality that works most of the time. Sometimes the companion on an android phone does not sync to the activity on an iOS/tvOS device. The game/map display could benefit from showing the road gradient. Other than that it's nice and helpful.

Went from my best app to my worst. On Premium but for some reason I can no longer log in. So it's useless for me. Tried everything but keep on saying "Network error occured. Please try again in a moment " But it's been weeks now so don't know how long your moment is. Decided to try Strava since I also get no assistance from you. Mails just return saying they are experiencing problems and are working on it.

I bought a Pro license and generally like the app, but the lack of an option to access my data for further analysis, whether through an API or a file export, is really disappointing. Not having this feature is becoming a deal-breaker, and I would not renew my paid license without it. For now, I’m giving it 2 stars; once this feature is added, I’ll change it to 5 stars immediately.

so far I'm enjoying the app, it's easy to use, tracks everything perfectly, and for those curious it works well with fitdoc. However one thing I wish it could do was tell you the amount of calories you're about to consume if you take a photo of the food you're about to eat by using the camera in the phone a nice alternative for those who don't have a smartwatch.

Great tracking, but the sonar mode messes with the wireless charging my phone. When I wake up, my phone isn't charging for some reason. Happens every night so far since I started using this app. Otherwise, this app has some really cool features! Im bummed out I need to find something else. Maybe if I get a cheap sleep tracker instead of a watch I'd be down to use this again.

UPDATE FROM BELOW : IT SEEMS LIKE THERE WAS A POSSIBLE SYNCH ISSUE BETWEEN SOURCE AND CACHED LOCATIONS FOLLOWING THAT UPDATE ? OR MAYBE A FIX WAS PUSHED- ANYWAY FOR PAST 30 MINUTES EVERYTHING IS WORKING AS NORMAL AGAIN - THANKS Worked perfectly until this latest update - now crashing in loops. Tried the obvious : reinstalling, changing password, emptying cache- nothing doing. Desktop version still working so seems to confirm issue with app version. Phone Samsung Galaxy Note 10+, Android 10.

I haven't had it for long but am SUPER impressed with it so far. I have wanted to get onto meal planning for a while and haven't but this app is making it very easy. Just felt like I should leave a review so more people use it!
It's a good app, but with these kinds of purchases I prefer to use the full-screen'd website version. Which, in no way takes away from my experience with VitaCost. I LOVE this company!!
Really like the idea. Creates shopping lists. not super android friendly. tech support is amazing though

I'm having the same issues. Won't let me record sleep. Doesnt notice when I wake up and records sleep all day long. Yesterday I felt fine and was at 15% recovery with wacko numbers, today 81%, day prior 78%. Can't delete a workout that somehow got entered when I hadn't worked out. The notes section is gone altogether. I'm uninstalling and reinstalling to see if that helps but paying as much as this product costs every month this thing should not be having these issues.

Works well. Keeps accurate distance on the phones I've used it with. Had the premium for a while. I'm broke so I got rid of it but I loved it. Voice coach was especially cool Bought premium again and now it crashes on startup. Using Pixel 4 and can't even open the app
Only tried the free version for a day. Everything I clicked on was behind the paywall, with no way of sorting/finding free content? This was confusing and frustrating. Not calming.

Love this wearable tech. It gives great insight on what's happening with my body and let's me know where I'm falling off from my fitness journey and how to get back on track. I personally recommend this to athletes, people who used to be athletes in their Prime, and other people who are just trying to improve the quality of their life. If you're just looking for a tool to count your steps, this isn't it. This is definitely a lot more!

Really like to ease of the app, I am able to add my own receipes and recipes from the internet. Then I can put my weeks menu together and it is supposed to create a grocery list. I only gave it three stars because i could not add any more recipes from the internet. For some reason it keeps giving me an error and takes me back to the last recipe I saved.

More and more disappointing. For more than seven months, I haven't been able to add new products to the library. The error message:"Oops, something went wrong, error code: 412." I contacted support and they were completely useless and did not provide any solution except to wait for it to be fixed. Same situation on different devices. I have canceled my subscription due to this issue.

I write this review mostly in hope that an old feature returns. Overall a very useful app from bodyweight to some weight training. There's also always new workouts added frequently. However the one feature that set them apart(The one most of us want back) was the workout plans. The plans were great because they updated weekly based on your personal effort and progress. It's been replaced with a feature called collection which I personally do not use.

This app used to be my favorite, but for the past three days I haven't been able to sync my Fitbit, which has been very frustrating. Does anyone know how to remedy this issue? My Fitbit is perfectly synced with every other app so I'm not sure what the issue is.

The app is ruined. The AI stuff on the Today tab is completely useless and takes up too much space. The charts under Health tab be there instead, really that tab should be the first page you land on. You can't track portions that aren't full portions (no decimals allowed atm.) You can't see your calorie burn vs calorie intake overlaid, anymore. Focus on UX instead of AI. Updated my review from a 1 to a 3, being able to use a photo to track calories is a nice addition. Still needs tons of work.
BEST APP EVER!!!! Thank you Apple, you petty idiots, for bringing this app to our attention! Rot in hell Apple! ;)

With every update it becomes worse. It rebranded as an AI company but their AI adds zero value. The AI powered tips look like a kid trying to tell you how to track your calories. It's just one more thing I need to close or ignore between me and what I actually want to do. update: now I actively have to close the app and restart it every time I want to log something because of unskippable animations and useless stuff. I don't want to deal with this. will cancel my sub because it's not worth it.

This app has single handily fixed my sleep. I was always oversleeping or forgetting to set the correct alarms for the correct days / weeks — no problem with this app. Additionally the support is very friendly, helpful and patient. I have recommended this app to friends and family and the people who chose to use it, are just as satisfied as I am.

Click V2 randomly stopped working, which seems to be a regular bug for a lot of folks. Can't seem to get it working again consistently, so I've paid for something that doesn't even work without constant workarounds to reconnect!

I have been using this app for years and love it! I used the paid version for a long time and wish I could still afford it but the free version is great too. I add my own recipes and ones I find from websites plus they have tons available from many contributors. Recently, I am having issues with logging in on the app where I have to reset my password every time but hopefully that gets fixed soon

Used to be fantastic, but keeps crashing relentlessly. Hardly get a minute's worth of use before it freezes up and kicks you out. Also likes to log you out for no reason and not let you log back in. Really disappointed.

Does not get any better learning about yourself than using one of these I have the mid-tier subscription with a 4.0 and completely satisfied with it. I've had it for about 4 years, I use a bicep band cuz I hate things around my wrists and definitely can't sleep with one on. If you're going to go bicep band my recommendation after years is get a second band so once a week when you charge the whoop swap bands and arms. Keeps smelling nice and manages your whoop tan line. 🤣

Fun to have by my side while Zwifting. Have connected with some amazing people from all over the world! Technically, one must practice navigating gently. Its a great app. Didnt give 5 just because there is always room for improvement. Thx Companion!

Vitacost is a great store, but the app is glitchy and slow. I can't sign in any more unless I change my password first; then after my session expires, it doesn't recognize my new password and I have to change it again. Searching items is slow and tapping on the result often ends up in a broken page. I can't access my cart, either. I'll just stick to the store's website for now.

I'm very disappointed with the new Google health app. Especially with the changes with the food tracking feature. They completely got rid of the custom food feature, limiting how you track homemade meals (where you can't manually enter calories or macros). How you were able to track your food was much better on the old app.

Great app on most ways. Suggestion: Searching for events several days in the future is torture, slowly advancing one day at a time. Please add a feature where I click on the date to get to a calendar and choose my date or date range.

Overall brilliant but steer clear of Apple TV as you need an iphone if you want to connect more than one BLE sensor, so there's no cost saving over an ipad. I can't get companion to pair apple tv to android. It used to, even if it crashed during training. I stick to an iPad.

It's hard because when the app works it's wonderful. But for some reason, today my alarms didn't go off. And I didn't do anything different! I hate when the trust gets broken because how do I know it won't happen again tomorrow?? 😪 Edit: I've made sure every permission, and 'deep sleep' settings are correct. STILL randomly goes off hours after alarm, or not at all. It doesn't say 'missed alarm' so who knows if it even went off.
I suggest don't buy from here they don't cear customs and don't pay  back

ever since the last update the app has been almost unusable. I have reached out to support as I can't open any of the different things in the app I can only see my home screen but have not heard from them as of yet. For example I went for a walk and cannot even open the walk statistics. I hope they come out with a new update very very soon to correct the bugs that they created with the last one. Until then I'm not sure how usable it is.
The new lay out is terrible

Been really helpful to track my diet. However recently it doesn't seem to sync with Google fit very well. It's never been amazing, you need to make sure you visit Google fit app and MFP at the end of the day for it to sync properly but it was fine, now it doesn't seem to sync at all at the moment which is a shame

As of today, when I tap the + to add food items to a meal, it assigns the food to the previous meal instead (ie. breakfast instead of lunch). I then have to move the food to a specific time of day to get it into the correct meal. The last app update was several days ago, so I don't know why it just started happening. Very annoying, since I track everything I eat.

I love my watch - I use it for health tracking, mainly sleep, oxygen, heart rate, HRV, and stress. Lately, I'm mostly tracking sleep. I think it's extremely annoying that you cannot delete naps in the Garmin Connect app. My Garmin device frequently falsely detects naps. You can edit the nap times, but cannot delete them! It also sometimes doesn't detect sleep properly, and even after editing sleep times, will still give false info about the sleep I got, like saying it was short when it wasn't.

Great app and plan. App works flawlessly and simplifies my time in the gym. I love that it shows me what plates to use to save time doing it in my head. Two months into the routine with a few days missed here and there and I'm down almost 20 lbs and have increased my lifts by over 100% (started at bare minimum to focus on form). Feel better and more confident than I have in years and my wife and others have noticed the physical and mental changes.

Brilliant app, great for training, however tendancy to crash and lose hr and power data prior zwift upload. This happens every other month, has occured with a laptop, android and ios device, contact support everytime with no resolutions! Happened again today, 66miles ride, hr and power data gone...

This App has been great! Easy to track the workout, built in timer, guides on the fly, this App is AMAZING! The only thing I wish they had was a some kind of way to connect to my watch. That and a way to see your potential lifting weight in the future if you stay on track would be a great motivator. Still, a perfect App to get you going and keep you in the game! Great job to Medhi and the developers.

There's a lot that I love about this app and I've used it for years, but it seems to end up with different glitches coming up all the time. The current one(s) is when i click the Walmart button to shop it just brings me to the Walmart home page and doesn't actually search my items in my list, so i have to manually search each item. I've tried to contact prepear, but it requires a login on my website and I can't login
Great functions, good software, just the community feature has a bug that didn't get fixed for me yet so I can't see the chat.

Honestly this app would be really great if almost everything on it didnt have to be bought. I was trying to connect this app to my Samsung health app on my phone which is free and i had to unlock the "pro" icon on the app which i needed to pay for. Even to look at recipes or anything. Kinda annoying. I like the very specific details this app has allows you to pick and chooses specific diet plans. I would have enjoyed this more if it gave you more information as to why you should or shouldn't eat a high fat diet or how much protein we should intake. It was just vague settings that of course i could adjust myself. But what about those who dont know how much they should intake? Overall it needs some work. Its a like and dislike.

I have many rare and overlapping conditions. I'm not the elite athlete this device is built for, which can be frustrating. But the quality of the data and the focus on training/rest have been really helpful in getting me from an HRV in the teens to the high 30s, and to average strains in the 9s to the 13s. I could also compare the gains from 1 yr physical therapy (10x) to that from 1 yr LDN (for pain) with PT (1000x). If I could customize and track activities that suit me, it would be perfect.

Good, but needs a few kinks ironed out. The "Stop" button is just not registering. At first, I thought maybe my hand was too sweaty but, even after toweling everything off, it still won't register. The other functions do. To stop a ride though, I have to dismount an do it from my computer (of fumble with my wireless keyboard). Hopefully, will be fixed soon. The other "Would like to have" would be to select and start a ride from the companion app.

The recent heart points tracking has been trash. I'll get awarded a bunch, then later in the day they're cut in half, and by the next day they're almost entirely gone. Very frustrating, particularly considering how dominant they are in the design of the application. old review: I love that it passively picks up on both walking and cycling activity, without putting too much burden on the battery. Looking forward to more integrations.
Seems easy to use. Wish it had a sodium info/tracker as part of it.
Had some issues when first setting up, but ever since then, no troubles and excellent, time-relevent information pertaining to the quality of my sleep, workouts, and recovery times.

Love my fitness pal! Been using it for years but unfortunately I cannot recommend this without the ability to barcode scan! Ridiculous this is part of the premium package that you have to pay for now! For someone like me who has ADHD, the ability to scan barcodes and add food quickly has been a huge plus. I don't have time to search and log food now. Gutted to be saying bye to this app! Hope this is revised in the future

Great company, always delivers easy transactions and great selection at fantastic prices that rival Amazon! Wow serious bugs in this update. Not functional. Cannot navigate at all. Please restore older version while fixing. There are too many competitors with decent apps. Don't risk losing your business to them.
Nothing work in the settings side of the app. I can't see my order history, track, lists, etc.

I would give 5 stars until this year. The app simply doesn't track my run due to lost of gps signal and then they say it's because of battery saving. I've checked more than once the battery set up for this app and it has all permissions. It's just frustrating I run for a goal when actually there's no tracking. I think I'm going to look for another app which is unfortunate because this was my running app of choice for years!
The app works great. Just wish there was an option for automatic pause. But otherwise all good for me!

It's a good app, the only one I've seen that lets you select your country to give you food options from the stores here. But meal planning is impossible. The moment you exit out of one of the menus, it pelts you with stats, ads, premium subscription sales and "You've earned a streak freeze". It takes 6 taps to exit one menu. It's become so annoying, it's better to just close the app and reopen it.

Helps me understand myself very well. After using for 2 years, I'm sold that I'll keep with Whoop. The annual membership isnt the worst, but if it came partnered with other applications and deals it could be an all in one suite. MyFitnessPal would be a great member to import data over. Much better than FitBit. I'm not sure what else is there to explore. Things were better before the March 2023 update, but im sure tha public acklash will be taken and heard.

For years, I have used my fitbit to track my sleep. Now, I am forced to use the new Google app, which includes more metrics related to sleep quality. Consequently, my sleep scores are systematically lower and thus inconsistent with previous data. For example, I'm penalized for getting up to use the bathroom more than once. It's not taking into account that this is normal for an 81 year old female. TMI (clutter) on the home page. I'm very unhappy!

I've had this app for about 5 years. It's easy to use, has plenty of functions and that's not even having the premium package. it's great to track daily eating habits as well as keeping you (and only you) accountable to reach your goals whatever they are. The notifications are great for times when you forget to track. Easy setup with phones/watches so logs your workouts, walks, hikes, bikes whatever and adds to your daily calories/daily expenditure. awesome for beginners all the way to advanced

I don't see tech support in the app. It would be nice if users could tag nutritional values that may be incorrect so that somebody could review them. I just noticed a discrepancy with McDonald's vanilla iced coffee, with different values per oz between small medium and large. set all three to 32 oz and the large is high in fiber while the small and medium contain no fiber. The app includes no option to report or correct this.

I really like how this app works, unfortunately I have no interest in paying such a large amount of money each month so that I can save my own meal plans. I consider that my own content as I created it.

No longer happy with this app unfortunately, used for years on iPhone and everything worked fine. Moved over to android Huawei phone and now it gives the incorrect distance. I had a voice notification to tell me the distance and time after 1km, about 5 minutes in, then I only ran a further .2km in the following 30 minutes when I actually ran 6km. I had done the run before on my iPhone so had the route saved so managed to edit but should not have to do this everytime. Sort it out please.

Wow this app has changed since Dec 2018. That's the last time I used it, then I became a mom and all working out came to a halt. This app kept me in shape during my pregnancy. Now you can no longer start a program. They're "collections" now but starting one workout in the collection doesn't create a schedule like it used to. This app used to be SOOO HANDY AND ALL FREE. Now it's just a collection of workouts and banners for the Premium version. Ugh, what happened?

I've been using this for over 10 years now and still love it. My only issue is that an update or two ago, it became much more difficult to edit a playlist made of music on my phone. One of my music files in a 12 song playlist is broken, and I can't just edit it to remove it. EDIT: No, using the File icon under Playlist will only let me choose 1 song, even if I long-press to try to choose multiple, and then shows it under Song not Playlist.

I am beyond sick and tired of the app telling me there are pairing issues and asking me to go into settings to 'forget the strap' and pair it again. This happens EVERYDAY, and unless I'm opening the app every 2 hours it will inevitably happen. Now mind you, I looked all over on WHOOP's support pages for help and all I could find was advice not to close the app and leave it running in the background. Even went into android settings and made sure it was always allowed to run in the background.

Holy friction, batman. The long and boring unskippable questionnaire in the beginning made me uninstall halfway through. I wanted to try out the app, not attend an interview. Luckily there are quicker apps to start using.

It looked really promissing - all the features that I was looking for, but once I installed it and wanted to start by adding my own recipe, there was a major issue. No matter how many times (and different phots) I tried, a photo for the recipe couldn't be uploaded. I also tried saving the recipe without a photo, but it simply didn't work. I hit the green button Save and then I am pushed back to the main recipe window where I can't click Save and if I click Back, the recipe isn't saved.

I had this app for months and it was fine, now suddenly EVERY time I open it, it glitches. The main page comes up, and then refreshes, and then does this for a total of 3 times before I have to sign in again. Everytime I close and open the app! This has been so frustrating, and there is no way around it because this is the only way I can book my gym classes. Please fix this.

GPS dropping, not acceptable...I must have had this app for a good 10 years on and off. Never had a problem. Last used it October. Installed it 2 weeks ago and all runs and walks have had GPS issues since. I've just ran a 10k and it only tracked 3 miles of it. The voice coach isn't telling me when I hit each mile any more, that was a major feature. I'm going to have to switch to another app, which is a shame as all my history is on this one, albeit the past 2 weeks are inaccurate! Please fix!

I have been using Fitbit premium for 3 years. It's always been really easy to see already how much you've eaten a day compared to how much you have burned. It's been easy to find things and add food. This switch to Google health is purely made it seems to push Google Gemini, which I refuse to use, and it makes the user experience worse. I'm going to give it a little bit longer, but most likely we'll stop paying for the premium, and try to find a app that is actually user friendly.

I think the stronglifts program is excellent for developing a strong base for strength training. The app itself is amazing, it does all the work for you. The best part is if you have any bugs to report, you get a response within 24 hours in my case, it was from Mehdi himself. I find all this to be amazing and well worth what I paid for the app.
rubbish. I want a refund on my Fitbit premium - this app barely works, inconsistent, often an hour or more behind it it's readings. please give the option of using Fitbit app until you sort this out!

Normally a good app, but since the update, I've the same issues as everybody else: it doesn't recognise my favourites, and I'm unable to book any classes. Edit: Thought it was a problem with my phone, so I uninstalled and reinstalled the app, and now I can't even log in.

Edit: 5 star -> 2 star. most of the workouts are there, but the workout programs (that I consistently used) was removed and they're clearly shifting to a system where you can pay for your plan. Side note to Nike: $15/month makes me cringe. I don't even want to try the free trial for fear I'll lose out on $15 if I forget to cancel it.

I have tried at least 5 free pedometer apps, and I like this one best. Very accurate for not counting driving in car as steps. At first I didn't like the amount of emphasis on Move Min and Heart Points, but I have come to appreciate it. I have tried most of the calories eaten/burned tracking apps such as MyFitnessPal, MyNetDiary, Lose It, Fat Secret, etc. Google Fit syncs to more of them than any other free pedometer app. Only thing I dont like is the widget updates only about every 30min

This started off pretty good with coach Bennet. Now I realize it's never going to work right with my Samsung watch, and the other coaches talk too much. Also, basic things like the fact the app is always requiring me to log in like ita the first time I have used it... If I just wanted motivational speeches, I could download a podcast. This had a lot of potential but missed on all points.

I've been meaning to download this app for years, but never remembered. Tonight I took the plunge as I saw an ad. Downloaded and loaded her up....just to find nearly every single item was locked for premium content... I understand there's a 7-day free trial, but not without agreeing to their auto-bill subscription. What a joke. If Calm truly cared about making people's lives better, they wouldn't monetize the entirety or their application. It's just a damn fluffy scam to get your $80.
It's ok but not the best...it should include what exercise you do as well ad the food....

for nutrition it would be extremely helpful if fiber was calculated and show for each day as this should be tracked. it would also be helpful if users could add food to the database that isn't in there and submit it for review/approval like other apps do to verify accuracy or make changes.

Great app to track my food and easy to save favorite meals. Quick entry & quick glance to see my fat, calories, etc. It has every food on the planet! Haven't put in workouts yet. Thats next! Update ** i have been intermittent fasting for 3 weeks. This app is so easy i don't get tired of it. Using it for workouts too!

I've been using this app for 5 years, so that alone speaks to the benefits, but it's getting so buggy I may have to switch to something paid. Pros: Free, I love the main trainers and original basic programs, positive and healthy messaging about caring for your body and performing to a level that is right for you Cons: doesn't save workouts half the time which makes programs pointless, keep adding niche content but need to refresh their intermediate dumbbell and bodyweight programs
The developers aren't entitled to give you free stuff overall a great app free version is fine (it's the free version what do you expect) overall clean design

I enjoy this app. It allows me to input my meals that I make at home, ingredient by ingredient and save it for future use. I can also input/add to the list of options if what I ate isn't in the app. Helps me set a goal weight and decide which calorie intake I would like to use and shows me the expected date I'd reach said goal. I use the free version and it has so many features I didn't expect to be able to use.

I have 2 problems with this app: although I paid for the pro version years ago, they changed my membership to the free version when they updated the pro version. I contacted customer service and they indicated that I would need to pay again if I wanted to keep the pro membership and get the new features. Secondly, the app had been accessing my location data in the background even though the permissions are set to allow access only when the app is running. Good bye Endomondo. Uninstalling.

Most of the time, it works great. The coaching has been fantastic. Edit: Runs have not been logging lately. The app has been very buggy. Works while running but then crashes with no apparent fix after the run. I want to use the app but it's getting harder and harder to justify using it over others. Would like to give 5 stars but this is an annoying issue. Android User.

Same problem as others since the update, drops Bluetooth device connections relentlessly, lucky to get 30 seconds riding before it drops. Also completely crashes half of the time when it drops. Was flawless before the update on pixel xl and Galaxy S9, now unusable on both. Tried uninstall and reinstall, still useless.
Used this app while preparing for boot camp. Found all the tools and features very useful. Then I went on a cruise and washed out. oops. 😂
Awful app better on iTunes

Love this app. Best app to track your runs that's out. No other program compares. Was wondering if you could add a feature I loved from the old Noom Cardio Trainer app. It would take the total miles from all of your runs and show a circle around your current position on the map. The radius to the circumference was your total miles. I would make goals to eventually "run to Chicago" or one "get to Dallas" based on my total number of miles run. It really motivated me and was a unique feature. Thank you!

As usual, an over eager LLM creates a frustrating experience that costs me more time to fix than it does helping me. In the one day since I've been forced to use it, it's hallucinated its own workout, won't let me save a standard workout, and mis-logged time without allowing me to make updates. Can't wait to see how it helps me next!

I've been using this app for almost 2 years. It is extremely laggy, it has also logged me out countless times while I'm tracking my food, and it will add a food to my meal multiple times instead of the one time I clicked on it. It's a very difficult app to use at times, and the recent update made it much more confusing to navigate.

It's a great app with great workouts and amazing and motivating trainers! Whether your a beginner, an athlete, or anything in between, this app has everything you need to workout, and it has some great nutrition advice, too. It has helped me become a better athlete both on and off the field, I would definitely recommend this app to anyone thinking about getting it. The only problem I experience is that sometimes the music doesnt play over the workout like it's supposed to.

Occasionally crashes, gets sluggish if you're looking through a long list, and there's no night mode. On the other hand, it's easy to navigate, they have the best deals, and it was really easy to use.

I did the sleep tracking for one night and it didn't actually track anything. There's no information for rem, deep sleep, and time awake (I even know I woke up a couple times), snoring, or even when I fell asleep. It just tells me the duration is when I turned on the sleep tracking, until the alarm went off. There is no useful information. I also read that this app can wake you up when you're during light sleep but the only option I can find is a regular alarm.

Missing basic features It's a great idea to offer a database full of foods but when there is no option to correct nutritional values, it undermines the entire system. No one expects every food to be correct, that would be a neverending task, so where is the functionality to easily edit the data? e.g. fresh orange juice allegedly has 0% vitamin...

Unbelievably convenient app. I am not sure it can be stretched to regular gym programs and stay that perfect, but specifically for 5x5 it is as convenient and thoughtful as no other gym apps for their uses. I had doubts purchasing a single-program app, but I never regret that decision. Worth every dollar. Many thanks for separate weights/body units - I am a "metric" guy who attends "imperial" gym. The most important feature, though, is plates calculator - cannot live without it.

I just started using this app specifically because it tracked minutes of activity rather than steps. It was the perfect alternative to Fitbit for someone recovering from a foot injury who can't walk as much. But now they changed it to track steps instead! No way to choose if you'd prefer minutes or steps. So it's now basically a pared down Fitbit. "Heart points" are insufficient to keep me using it.
it's easy to use and free of non-stop ads. I love the breakdown of macro nutrients.

was a 5 star review for a decade then he removed my powerpack and made it a subscription. it's ridiculous. I don't want anything beyond what I had. just scroll the reviews you'll see everyones annoyed. also have you ever seen an onboarding flow with an unskippable "add widget to your homescreen" step. since when do I need to allow you my homescreen space in order to use this app. it's stupid and it's a perfect example of the entitlement and attitudes that the app is pushing.

the barcode scanner sucks now, I cant log my food manually. I do not like anything about it. Why try and fix something thats not broken. its all confusing to understand now. the fitbit app worked just fine and was easier to understand. And you could manually type into your food log to get exactly what your calories were per serving. You can't even do that, so its not even accurate because you can not search for exactly what product you have. Makes me not even want to buy a new fitbit watch.

says you can remove sharing of personal data. doesn't say that's only if you delete the account. app is advertised with interval timers but there's no way to set them. you have to let the program create them. i wanted to set toners as in walk 1 min run 5 min x 5 sers. app wants to know how far I want to run. I don't know. my plan is for time! useless for my purposes. going back to ironman watch.

I am upping my review to five Stars. I've had this thing on my phone since 2017, now in 2023 it continues to work for me. I use it for a bicycling, walking, hiking, and--through a connection to my Garmin watch--for stand up paddling. It's all in this app. And I am cheap and have never paid a thing for it. So there you go.

This is to warn others about the app being rather unreliable, especially for payment processing and online class bookings. The app charged me twice for a booking although there was no indication on the app interface about the payment being successful (app was just stuck on the page to confirm payment) and no upcoming classes reflected in my profile even after the two "unprocessed" payments. Had to go to the orignal website of the company whose class I wanted to book, and request a refund.

Hardware and software are not good for the price paid. If you use GSam battery monitor app, you can see this app will wake your device perpetually and drain your battery more than most or all apps. Whoop can be good for first few months as you gain insights, but it doesn't last and you can buy better trackers that measure things like sleep more accurately compared to gold standard tests (e.g., Fitbit inspire 3). My advice -- sell your device and subscription to someone else.

Program must have installed a fix. It works okay for bicycling, but shortcut jumping across points on streets and shorting me mileage. It is recently doing better. The street layout has changed over the last three years and it needs a map update here in Northern Florida. Other than that I am generally pleased with the program.
All of a sudden, this app won't recognize my login credentials. Keeps saying I reached my maximum login attempts. Can login via website just fine...

love the app! makes losing weight a whole lot easier for me. The only downside is that it keeps recommending adding nuts to my diet. seeing I'm allergic I'd love to have a way so it recommends alternatives?
Doesn't notify you if your over your fat, carbs, protein limit. Doest allow for tracking exercise. App is a good start but needs a lot of work.

Love the app, but love it a little less now. Love the set programmes as an option, but still think you should bring back the personalised ones too. Otherwise things get too monotonous and unappealing. Also an option to build your own programme (playlist of workouts) would be good. But that also was a feature of the precious personalised programmes - one could swap out things that didn't really work, this could also allow for someone to add more though.
why don't the app descriptions tell you the costs? you need to go through all of the set up before you see the costs, to compare costs.

Yazio has many features that I expect for a food tracker app. Unlike FitnessPal, i like that this app helps me stay consistent. The worst part about this app is the user interface. The weight logging is just a counter, like a settings config, rather than a daily log form. When I enter a new food, the labels for servings is confusing. It always gets the metrics wrong every time and it wont let you edit it. Only custom recipes can be edited, if you can recall what you named them.

I have not been able to use fitbit on my phone since it changed to google health. I've Uninstalled and reinstalled and restarted my phone and nothing works. all I get is a message on the app that it switched to google health.

The Nike Run Club is ok as a very basic running app. But it is not a "club" there is no sense of community at all on the app unless you add your friends, but still you can't see what recent runs your friends have taken or their pace, etc. Elevation is not recorded without a connected device. Tracking is quite accurate. What sucks is sometimes I get home, auto connect to the Wi-Fi and as I end my run the app logs me out. I've lost multiple runs this way and it is extremely infuriating! ❗ 5/10❗

I've been using Garmin devices for as long as I can remember. navigation on Antarctica: Garmin. Health & Fitness: Garmin. Driving in traffic: Garmin... But the GUI keeps growing more difficult to use on this app. It requires more and more scrolling because the info tiles are getting bigger and bigger. WHY is this necessary???? Please give us a summary overview from which I can zoom in on what I want to see. I don't need big, mostly empty tiles that require more and more scrolling...

Very strong without a subscription, a strong, accurate, full-featured food diary that's easy to use, input and review. 11/10 with one. Feels like it is a bit beyond, with helpful reporting, stronger macro-nutrient management and micro-nutrient tracking, and the food diary becomes even easier to use, input and review. TLDR: great without sub, doesn't feel like you're forced to pay for solid use; beyond great with it.
The apple app allows you to track workouts and change your daily targets this app is bare minimum but still handy
Worst app every workout is paid stop buying form there store they just want money every plan is paid

New update is half baked. Some things are "still being worked on" (Delete Sleep Session). Some setting menus are completely inaccessible (One about permissions or something, I only saw "Steps" on it. I pressed back and cannot return to it through any of the settings). Some buttons do not work (Learn More). Classic Google Incompetence. They want health data, but turned the device into a paper weight. Encrudification practices.

Since an update was released this app is using up a lot of data in the background. It seems to be using up to 1GB a day - not efficient on mobile network. I also suspect this is draining the phones battery too.

While I have been using the app since 2013, the latest update has ruined the functionality of the app. Previously, I could type a brand name into the search and pull up recent items. Now I have to type in a keyword from the item description. Bring back the ability to search by brand! It also deleted all my recent foods and I now have to re-enter them. An update should make the app better, not break previous functionality!

This is a sophisticated app that doesn't miss a single detail. I have two devices. I have a wearable and a cycling computer which communicate flawlessly with each other and with the app. Also, the communication between Connect and other fitness apps makes life very convenient. I highly recommend it.
No fitness tracking? That's the only reason I signed up.

Very reliable app. Tracks my workouts and afternoon strolls. It's nice to see where I've been, how many miles, how long I've been out, etc. Works well with my music app playing in the background. Definitely give it a try! I've noticed some reviewers mentioning the GPS portion of the app cutting out but I have not experienced any problems with it.

The transfer from Fitbit to Google Health hasn't been a pleasant experience. I loathe the new GUI! Instead of gaining features we've lost them; e.g. can't enter new foods into the database, can't enter a specific calorie amount, etc. Instead, you're limited to bar code scanning and what's in the DB. That's it! Had I known they were going to transfer to this horrendous app, I would've started looking for a different device. The layout of the interface is clunky; just awful.

The old version "Fitbit" was wonderful. It tracked everything well, gave helpful resources, and helped me track and stay motivated. The new version is honestly revolting just to look at, which kills so much of my experience. I'm hoping they give me an option to return to the old version that wasn't broken. If not, I might move on to something else and get rid of my premium membership.

It's got a plethora of options to pick from. Meditations, even some low key exercises, sleep stories, soothing sounds and music. There's almost too much to be honest. Especially when im feeling in between two of the available options. Or even an "other" option where I could type out the specifics. Otherwise I like this app
Its a very good company, would like to purchase from it if it was in India.(or branch) where i can claim if i have defect

UI has improved. PayPal interface tends to fail which is frustrating (did I get charged? feelings). If I wasn't such a lex luthor (Jeff bezos) hater, Amazon would be loads easier where I can order in seconds.

The redesign is not ready for prime time. activities and minutes keep disappearing. Calorie counts are wildly inconsistent. There is a lot of promise with the addition of the heart points but, until it works it's just taking up space on the phone. Update: still broken! Now it's changing the distances of tracked workouts half a day later. And I can not edit them to fix the errors! Fail!

I don't use the built in routines but this is my favorite workout tracking app. Very easy interface. Been using it since I did stronglifts nearly a decade ago. I bought multiple "lifetime" upgrades. Any time you switch phones or get signed out though the app WILL NOT let you use it without signing up for the free trial.

IT'S COMPLETELY FREE! Can't expect too much but it does have a good library of workouts. Wish I could search by upper/lower body, push/pull, etc, instead of scrolling through all of them. Can search by strength, cardio, Hiit, etc but search features could be set up a little better. Will use this app until I feel the need for AI to take control of me workouts as I am poor and cannot afford expensive workout apps or personal trainers, so this app suits me well.

I used to love this app! But now they've intertwined all of the paid stuff with the free stuff, and the free stuff is constantly changing. I know they want you to pay, but be a little user friendly. Will get 5 stars if this feature is implemented. The simplest most flawless workout app I have ever used. If you're a beginner like me and are bothering with anything more complicated than this there's almost no point. Machines in general are a waste of time, there is a general consensus among the sports scientists that compound lifts with progressive overload are the most efficient way to build strength.

Quite a fun experience! And so many details one can choose to add... (Also: I suppose it can help a lot for those who need community support--because it looks like there's some of that, too.) I'm really impressed with this, after having thought, "I'm not getting a calorie-counting app. What's the point? I'll just Google everything and use my calculator..." And then a YouTube star mentioned it once and piqued my interest. And, well, I'll be! It's a little confusing... but DELIGHTFUL!
Sucks that it is going away.

rlly only downloaded this app so i could track my cals / fasts , meanwhile the fasts thing is incredibly broken and not useable. can't even tell it when you've started/ended without it changing the times. also the weight keeps resetting to my starting one. rlly wouldn't recommend

This app is by far one of the better GPS tracking and excercise tracker apps. Works fluid like in the background, very compatible with other excercise apps and music streaming services. i am not able to save the run. When i long press after completing the run, the app asks to discard the run. i have reinstalled 3 times now. No change. please fix it soon.
terribly long onboarding process only to be then asked to subscribe and cannot progress further until you commit to a subscription. yes you can cancel, but I don't want to subscribe in advance.

Nike name is enough for people to download this app. It has various good workouts. But the only problem with this app is its interface. It's not user-friendly at all. Horrible app , never trust them , they will take ur money and send u the stuff u bought out of stuck wait or cancel ur order and never returning ur shipping money back

I've used this app 11+ years and it's been my go-to b/c it was that good. The latest update changed it so much, I'm going to go to something else. If I wanted a class format workout, I'd go to the gym. I want to see the exercises for each workout before starting because I have injuries and can't do certain things. I want the workouts back that list each drill and have the progress bar and voice-over notes. It was perfect, they've ruined it for me.

It's not intuitive. I have 2 children I try to manage their accounts. It shouldn't take me more than 30 seconds to be able to find what I need. This app should be easier to use. it's a schedule tool and they can't figure that out

Favorite app. Great for logging, easy to look up items including both food and suppliments from giant DB. Good high level dashboard and can mine into low level details on macros, vits/minerals/omegas. Can add custom recipies, reads fitness and biometrics from Garmin seemlessly. Can also tailor goals and set up dashboard to specific ways of eating eg Galveston. 10/10.

This was my first experience tracking and controlling calories, and it really helped me understand how much I’m actually eating. The app gives an accurate calorie count, and it’s easy to adjust grams and quantities as needed. I’ve been using it for three weeks now, and I’ve already lost weight by strictly following my diet. I would definitely recommend this to anyone who wants to manage their diet through calorie tracking—it’s the right way to go.

I had used this app for years. The June 29 update broke it. It wasn't tracking anywhere from 0.3 miles to 7.0 miles on rides. I ran Strava at the same time just make sure it wasn't my new phone. Strava was accurate, but I don't really care for Strava. The latest July 27 update helped some but still loses tracking sometimes. I hope the developers apply a fix soon. Great app, issue was a Google OS update.

The commercials imply that you can listen to many different things without a subscription. No subscription is even mentioned in the commercials. You used to be able to listen to maybe 5 different things for free, but not anymore. Now it's literally just crickets and everything else is an expensive yearly subscription for what other apps give for free. What a waste, especially during a stressful time like a pandemic with a lot more unemployed people who can't afford to relax.

Ads for pro version are INSANE!!! It may mean that your product is not robust enough in the free version that you need to NAG the user into purchasing the pro version. Maybe don't even offer the free version. It's also misleading in the beginning to pop-up the options with the intention of showing there was no truly free version. But there is, it just sucks. UDAP violation for sure.

As everyone else is experiencing, the last couple updates have made the app mostly useless. Been using for two years and it has always been great It no longer is able to get accurate GPS and doesn't display a map after. My 4 mile runs are usually +/- 1 mile. Same phone, same route as before the updates. I've moved to another app this week, which is a shame because I really loved having all my historic stats and PRs (some of which it's ruined since it thinks I'm running may faster than I am).

I used to use this app and it seemed to work ok with my garmin watch a long time ago, but for a number of years now have just been using it as an alarm. But that may not be short lived. The user interface is nice and fairly easy to use. Main complaint is that there is no way to manually into food and must be in the lookup database... Edit: updated rating as the app creators reached out and provided helpful feedback. I feel that add food option could be more intuitive and easier to locate.

As a person who doesn't really like to work out, 5x5 lets you forget about the math and forget about keeping track, and just do the deed. Very helpful. Five star. EDIT: The weather is nice now so I went out to the garage to get started again and get my brain correct after a cold winter. Stronglifts is no longer honoring our agreement and is demanding that I pay a subscription fee to continue. I am not happy and awaiting a response. Hopefully we can resolve this issue.

I used this app for a few years, and it's always working fine. Since a week ago, it would automatically stop after started within 1 or 2 minutes without any warning or notification. I couldn't fix it by changing any settings of this app. After I uninstalled and reinstall it yesterday, the auto stop issue was totally resolved, and is working well again now.
Overall pretty good, able to reorder previously ordered items. It wigs out from time to time.
Allows me to seamlessly track my workout routine and everything I eat. Nutrition section breaks down macro groups and helps with goal setting. Email reminders help me stay on track.
It needs a sodium tracker.

loved this app but tried to get my partner on to it and found he couldn't without a subscription. I did a deep dive into the comments with people who liked me bought lifetime access having that rescinded. I honestly don't know if I can recommend an app that I'm waiting to see if I lose access to after purchasing lifetime membership.

Update: erroneous tracking doesn't seem to be an issue anymore. However as far as weight tracking, it's still not very useful as Fit no longer has a web dashboard and the app is very limited in how it allows you to view data. For example the 3 month view for a January date shows January through March when November through January (last 3 months) would be more useful. Completely unable to slide the time-frames when viewing logs.

Has always worked very well. But after years of using it I've finally reduced its star rating. I am blown away that you still have to "spin a dial" to enter in your heart rate after a workout. Make it so we can just enter the number with our keyboard. It is a silly waste of time and bad design.

The App updated and has been terrible! I have classes scheduled 3x a week at my gym and my schedule shows my 1st appointment is in March of 2020. Hoping my gym selects a different scheduling app soon!
Food is disappearing from options. Items I have logged before, I cannot find anymore.

It's almost unheard of that I give a full 5-star rating, but this earned it! This app is amazing! It does things that I didn't even know I wanted it to do, like provide a gentle reminder that it's bed time (based on how long I want to sleep and what time my alarm is set for the next day) and a whole boatload of statistics that are fun and useful. I was perfectly happy with the free version, but just bought the unlocker app just to support these great developers!

I like the app, but been having trouble with the tracking feature ( the main function) is unavailable for long stretches of the day. the default "servings" of many food items in the app are inaccurate so you have to double check what you're putting in.

I quite enjoy using the app, very detailed data comes out of it. The only thing that really bothers me about it is the layout of the journal: Firstly I think some of the loggings involving time (e.g. When did you stop...?) Need the slider replaced, I've found them fiddly and it would be nice to change it to just typing in your custom time. Secondly, the "Hydrate Sufficiently?" Tab - can there be an option to input litres or ounces instead of "glasses" because it feels a little abstract

The keyboard auto minimizes itself when you try to click on a text field for the last few versions. I've found that version 4.48.4 resolves this issue for me. The ability to scan your food for a new entry and the AI will auto put in the macros for you is amazing. Thank you for a great service!

It used to be so much more functional. For the last year or more After some update...new favorites marked won't save. Removing older favorites won't save. Locks up. Desktop is good y To search clases anywhere, but Cumbersome b/c you can't save any favorites.

This is the most frustrating app I've ever had to deal with. Not only is it inaccurate with step counting (by the thousands), but it consistently deletes steps as well. I have no choice but to use this if I want to participate in the money back program through my insurance, but it's so awful that I may just give up on the program.

I've been using this app for years! it has plenty of options for integration with other apps. it's the only alarm app I found with webhooks which I use with home assistant to automate lights when the alarm is dismissed or tracking started. there are specific blueprints for home assistant for use with this app. Top work Devs. I applaud you.

I used to love this app, but it's so buggy now. It's starting to track workouts I haven't even done, like walks. And when I delete a workout, it mysteriously shows back up. this garbage app just cost me $20 because it decided to download gigabytes of data on 5g for no apparent reason. it wasn't even running. I hadn't opened the app in days. I'm extremely pissed off about this

The search doesn't work properly, really difficult to find the business by name, the search doesn't use the location you enter it will only take your current location. I am very happy with this app for it's scanning capability alone. It would be nice if it tracked exercise to. The menus and settings are limited.

I love what it is supposed to do for you but it is terribly confusing. It seems to get stuck or hung up. The amount in my cart doesn't change no matter how I try. At this point I m not sure how to rate this app.

Love this app! It's so easy to use. With the free version, you can track your weight progress and your meals. I switched from my last app because you had to pay to scan barcodes.
