The Garmin Connect app is an adequate fitness app. I'm still not a fan of the current interface, as it looks amateurish but it does what I need it to. The older interface was far more intuitive. Perhaps a happy compromise would be customizable tiles. Having some reports buried whereas other, less critical reports front and center is unhelpful and cumbersome.
Wonderful program Easy to use. Packed with great information. How to make it a picture of not only my diet but my nutrition

Love your app, but I wish there will be a check mark beside the trainings I have done. I like trying new ones but it so hard to track easily the one I have done and the one I have not done as the only way to find out is to go back to my history. I just want a check mark. I also agree with the other comments about the new changes. I prefer the old app because I could preview the list of exercises. I also don't like the hyper enthousiam coach. I also prefered being able to download in advance.
What trash. They "updated"the app. But it's only just the mobile web version. It looks a runs worse then the old app. Switch it back.

At the time of this writing, 3/26, the ads are minimal, the available nutrient tracking options are extensive with good explanations of their functions & sources, & the features are quite nice. In particular, the barcode scanner works even on the free version as well as scanning & adding labels for foods not already in the apps database. Sole complaint is that if there is a way to view trends over longer than a week, I don't know how to find it.

Best tracker app by far. Counts calories like all the others but adds so many layers ontop from vitamins and micronutrients, to phytonutrients that might disrupt absorption. You can choose not to count undigestable fibre as part of your carb count and you can even set the app to deduct the thermic effect of food from your total daily calories! And unlike many other apps, you can have more than 3 meals per day which makes things much easier when your a bodybuilder. Excellent app!

I really like this app! I bought the full version, I can't remember why because it was awhile ago, lol, but I'm very happy with it! I snore like a hog, and I love that it vibrates to try to stop me lol -- however, it doesn't seem to help me, as you can hear me just keep snoring in the sounds log haha. I have no issues running this app all night on my OnePlus 6T. If you have a phone with low RAM and a slower preocessor, it might get glitchy.

overall great app but a glitch in the Breathwork on pixel 10a will not allow me to open the activity details and see graphs on the app itself. I have to open my activity from the web version to do this. can this get fixed please?

*** I've been trying to update my calorie goals it keeps saying that it failed to save for over 2 weeks****Thank you for this free resource. In over a year worth of use I've only ran into maybe three items that were not already listed. Has been a helpful tool to make me aware of my eating habits & choices. Would be nice if the export feature allowed you to sellect more than just one day at a time.

I have used several apps for macros/calorie counting and unfortunately I paid for this one so am stuck with it. The biggest problem I have is that so many common eateries/restaurants are not in the app, so you have to put a bunch of work into manually entering food and beverages. Also, the exercises are fairly generic. Why would YOU (yes, the app developers not put chain restaurant foods into your app????). My spouse has the other popular app and I often have to ask her for info.

Great app. There are plenty of workouts even if you don't use the premium. I actually prefer those more as you get a list of the workouts and equipment needed. I don't always want to look at a screen and prefer the audio prompts. It's move to have the options though. (Downrated as I have been having frequent freezing in the app workouts pause without touching anything and I can't resume. Can only end workout and try to pick up where I left off)
This app constantly signs me out for no reason then when I try to sign in it sends me verification codes that don't work and when they do work it loads and then immediately signs me out again.

I've used this app almost daily for 11 yrs. It has been very useful for me. Which means I spend a lot of time entering things. This new update is awful I wish I could take back the update. I spent more time trying to figure out where & how to enter stuff than I care to admit. I liked seeing all my food for the day & "add" right all on the same page & nutrition icon. Now you have to hit "view all" or "log" (which is very small) & More.Why? So I spend more time on the app? Sorry I might jump ship.

I have been using this for years now. NFC to turn off is the only thing that can get me to wake up with one alarm. I set the phone far enough away that I have to sit up. NFC tag outside the room and the alarm won't turn off without it. I used to set ten alarms and would still wake up hours after with no recollection of disabling a single alarm. Even other apps with puzzles or math problems didn't work. Love this app.

It's a good and detailed app but it can be hard to navigate round and get used to as it's not directly evident how to find certain stats without googling. I would like the journal Notes textarea to have to option to expand so that more text can be seen when writing, as it's currently too small and awkward on the page.

Great app! Love the interaction with other riders and options available. My only complaint would be that there are not enough 'real' worlds to ride in and the routes in the current worlds are a bit limited. Would be great to have longer rides available without doing loops. Hopefully that will come.
Requires an account. Not signing up for that.

The iPhone version has much better tracking to show you your daily intake relative to your individual macro goals.  It also shows the graph of several body tracking stats while this one only shows a list of data points.  Many features in general from the site are missing as well. Other than that it's ok, the features that ARE implemented are mostly done well.  But on an Android phone you might as well just go to the website outside of the bar code scanning ability.

App tells me my username or password is incorrect. I use the exact username and password on my laptop and it works like it always has. I would love to be able to use the app but it doesn't work. UPDATE 11/28/21 After Developer response of working on my issue I decided to be patient. It's been 5+ months and it still doesn't work. Seems no one cares.

The worst of the bunch! It is only good for uploading your activities to another service. The interface is cramped with too many, arranged in an ugly way, and atop of this Garmin launched a subscription service for stuff that isn't worth a cent. The connect apps are worthless too. When they have updates, they stop displaying data until you update them. It also has a nearly inexistent community. I am just about to shift to another platform.
I love this app. It makes meal planning and shopping for ingredients a breeze.

Version 4.0 appears to be a disappointment. First of all, some functionality appears and disappears without a clear reason as to why. For example, seeing my heartbeat live is typically possible by going to device settings. Now it is mostly empty. More annoying is the alarm setting - the menu for changing the time seems to only be available around 9 or 10pm, other times it is not there. So if you wake up night and want to disable alarm - good luck. Data loss issues became much more frequent.

Works great for everything I do! Tracking is very detailed and I definitely learned alot about my tendencies and I have been able to improve my overall fitness and health. They are always looking to improve the app and fix any bugs that occur(which over the past 5 years i think i can recall maybe 1). Overall the app is great and pairs well with whatever piece of Garmin equipment you use. Garmin is a great company, I have always been satisfied with the level of customer support I receive.

This app is worth having just for the "smart alarm" which wakes you gently at the correct point in your sleep cycle. Adding more features, which is optional, makes it even better. Once you get the hang of reading your sleep data, you will want to keep your numbers in the groove. That, in turn, leads to a happier, healthier life. 20210512: Update. normally when apps push to me for another review I ignore it. But I really use this app. It is my alarm clock and helps me to keep my sleep sorted.
Way too expensive! The device is extremely uncomfortable to wear constantly and breaks down your skin. Keeping up with the Journaling is very time consuming and required... I don't like that either

I'm NOT one to write reviews, that being said, when I noticed this company only got 3.5 stars, I had to say something. I have used this company for years. I love how each item is individually packaged in bags and extra cardboard for safe delivery. Only once did I have an item messed up and 2 days later my replacement was at the door with little hassle. I feel the rates for my specialty diet are lower than grocery stores and health stores. I always use the free shipping and surf for a coupon!

Started using it 2 days ago and so far this app can't go a day without something breaking. The AI camera feature was loading for minutes before I got fed up and just searched up something close to the meal I had and today I can't have dinner because it refuses to load my data. Will change my review if this thing can be reliable for a week straight.

Used to love this app. Simple, great content. It's now subscription based, which I wouldn't mind, except it's also bloated with so many different things that just aren't interesting to me and I haven't been able to find the types of meditations I used love. Being overwhelmed with content and struggling to find what I want is not a great experience when popping in to find those few quiet moments of solace amidst the chaos. I've let my subscription expire and have no interest in renewing.

this is the best app (I'll never buy) fantastic way to track nutrition. easy to use, formative and well designed. however, the pricing. This is priced as a super premium app. the subscription costs about half the cost of a Netflix sub. when you consider the work Netflix do for my money compared to this app, it's just not worth the money they ask. As a one off amount, sure I'd buy it in a heartbeat. but as an annual subscription? no thanks , poor value.

I've had this app for 3 years and have been a premium member. it was great. Today they changed the layout and it is clunky and borderline unusable. I suggest you try another app unless you wanna take 30 minutes to log a meal and fight to see what you've logged for a meal. what a shame. Update: I raised this from a 1 to a 3 star because while the app is now a headache to use, it still provides the good information it always has if you are willing to dig for it.

The app tracks fitness and steps really well. the ability to select a run distance and get help to achieve it is brilliant. my big downside is it poor sleep tracking. I use this a lot due to health conditions and it frequently hasn't recorded any data, at least 2 or 3 times a week. I've tried all of their suggestions but it still won't track. I have to use my old Fitbit just for my sleep recording.

Fit eats my miles. This bike ride was at least 8 miles round-trip. Halfway through, it said 4.28 miles and actually had a little extra track. By the time I'm back to where I started, it says 2 miles. There's some overlap on the route, bit this is so far off, it becomes worthless as a fitness tracker. You guys need to improve tracking. Location services and data are all enabled. The track on the map is mostly accurate. But the total miles are only a quarter of what I rode.

I love using this app! Have had it on a few different phones off and on for 2-3 years. The greatest thing is that it has saved every workout (Mtn Biking) to my Google acct since the beginning. I can look back and see all the details for every ride; avg speed, total distance including the route I took, duration, etc. There are settings for many different types of workouts and it can be used offline (by GPS?). It then computes the data when next online. I definitely recommend Endomondo!
Very bare bones. Would be really nice if you could track exercise. If the actual website wasn't so nice, I'd switch to another app. There are better ones out there.

This app used to be great, but it's really gone downhill. It doesnt track my run accurately, my guided run stopped working 20 minutes in, and now it thinks I run 7 minute miles so I know it's not tracking me right. bummer. I have no idea what happened. I know phone gps isn't super accurate but the readings used to be consistent with each other. Now, they're useless.

App has suddenly stopped working properly. I've uninstalled and reinstalled multiple times but no fix. The first problem was it suddenly started signing me out constantly. Currently it stays signed in but it forgets all my favorites, etc. Fantastic app when it works properly. Please fix!

In the past I Ioved this app. I was effective and simple to use. Somewhere down the line though Google changed the way permissions worked and now this app only has location permission "when active". Which means in the past when I pressed "start workout", turned the screen off, put my phone in my pocket and went for a run. It would still accurately track. Now though the app still runs, if you turn the screen off and 20 min later on you get a straight line from point A to B. Need all time access.

Been a cronometer user for over a decade. Unskippable video ads after every ingredient I log killed this app for me. Entering everything you eat is a tedious process to begin with, the added friction means I'll probably end up moving to one of the many other alternatives that do the same thing. Would be nice to see a silver tier or one time payment that just removes ads, hard to stomach paying so much for gold for a bunch of features I don't need just to make logging bearable again.
I just started using the app, and I love all the categories and how personalized you can make this. I just which there was a keto category as well.

Overall good, just extremely weird that when viewing a screenshot, the download and share buttons in the top right are barely visible, since it's white on almost-white. Seems like an unintended design decision.

This is the life/health journal I've been looking for. Yes it records your sleep, activity and nutrition chronologically but it also allows you to enter track symptoms, and how healthy you are feeling. You can log when you have a headache, brain fog, or when you're feeling energetic and since it's in chronological you can show patterns related to activities, sleep, and nutrition. It connects to Health Connect so you can import data from your activity/sleep fitness device.

I really like the simplicity of the interface in Endomondo. It is consistent in both mileage and tracking, except when the GPS occasionally shows a shorter route than you would expect (which might be the fault of the phone hardware). UPDATE 2/2020: Still enjoy the simplicity of the app. It has been my go to tracking app for over five years. I have not gone premium but for those that need the extras it is there.

This app is failing to record my runs accurately, as I see is common with other posters.  The pacer announces once or twice then quits.  My runs since the middle of July are an inconsistent mess.   Then there is the truly annoying cheer at the end.  Especially annoying when time has been recorded, and the miles have not.  Hurrah, I just did an 18 minute mile.  :(

There's definitely room for improvement in Zwift but they've done a great job with the companion app making it simple to plan rides, see previous activities, find and add friends and creating meetups. I use it every ride while zwifting to use power ups and give ride ons to fellow zwifters

It's wasn't the most accurate by itself, so it's only real use was to act as a go-between to synchronize data from my fitness watch to my diet tracking app. Well, it didn't do that consistently either, and now that they've done some sort of update or migration with Health Connect, it doesn't even serve that purpose anymore. What a waste of phone space - do not bother with this app, it's completely worthless now, and I have to seek out another app to somehow get my data to my diet tracker.

Love the app BUT!!! I'm using the app for a very long time and love tracking my running and chasing the challenges. Lately my app mismatches my treadmill for approx 10%. It is especially frustrating when you are running a high intense or long ones. I happily reached 4 miles on my treadmill and realized that I have almost half mile to go on my app, which tracks me much slower. Tried different treadmills, same issue. Similar thing happens outdoor, where I run known path andapp showing shorter

Looks great but I can't get past the sign up/sign in screens (I used my phone to create an account). I'm using an old tablet, but this app is supposedly compatible with Android 4.4 and up, so it shouldn't be a problem.

At first the app let me play some of the music for free, but now it won't. You have to pay. The music was ok, pretty relaxing when you wanted to just chill, but it's not so serious as to make me wanted to pay a subscription for it. And listening to people talk slowly is annoying. I rather just stay with YouTube. You find everything there that you can find here. Don't waste your money.

I was so hesitant to switch to Garmin from Fitbit (even after it had been engoogified), mainly because I assumed Fitbit had better fitness sensors and because I just didn't like the round and "diver watch" look of most Garmin's I saw. Then I saw the Venu X1 and made the switch and I am so happy I did. The watch is amazing, the sensors seem equal if not better to Fitbit, and now I'm even more away from Google. Yay. Big win all around and so happy Garmin exists and makes quality products.
Great app, helps me organize my recipes and grocery list. It makes it super easy

Had to move to Google health from Fitbit. Google health is far worse! The food tracking portion is clunky. There is no place to enter in custom foods. The old fitbit allowed one to see a calories burned vs calorie intake daily/weekly histogram, which this doesn't allow. Also the design is annoyingly big and bubbly. ugh. Can we please have an app that provides the data we need and not a bunch of oversized bubbly graphics? I'm also not a fan of the fitness tracking display.
Can't even adjust calorie goals or see pie chart of nutritional intake on android app.

I've been using this app for a long time without problems. Now it tells me Endomondo keeps stopping & I cannot get the app to work at all. Sent feedback through app itself with no reply. I'm at a loss as to what to do. As of 30 Apr I can open the app but it doesnt seem to be continiously synching with GPS as my routings are all shown as straight lines from point to point on Endomondo map.

It seems like it used to work, because I would get notifications about hitting my step target for the day. Now it's not tracking any activity at all, despite my settings being the same. Very confused. Nothing quite like getting to the end of a full and exhausting day on your feet and opening the app to see "0 steps today"!

Works brilliantly on my galaxy S10e. The GPS shows my cycle route on google maps, tells me how fast im going, calories burnt. The voice notification every 5 mins how far ive gone etc so i dont need to look on the screen, if i can hear it. I save my workout when finished then every week i get an email with weeks totals. One annoying thing is sometimes my phone will switch on in my backpack and accidentally 'hold to save' then there is no way back to continue that workout. Recommend overall.

I really love this app, I use it to track calories and macros every day. However, the prevalence of the ads is unbearably annoying. I'm all for supporting developers but I don't need all the extra features of the paid tier for the amount of money it costs. If there was a lower cost paid tier that only removed ads, I'd pay it in an instant.
Very good app, it has been an eye-opener, I realized that my "normal diet" turned out to be a significant undereating.

It's got some good workouts and I like that I can cast them to my Chromecast. However, they could use more whiteboard-style workouts. Also, I miss being able to interact with my friends. What's the point in adding your contacts if you can't see their activity or share workouts with them (no screenshots allowed)??

This is such an easy way to track my intake. I had no idea what I was really eating before and now it's so clear. things are broken down so I can see where I am taking in a lot more fats and carbs than I expected. I think this will be a big part of reaching my fitness goals. the free version includes a lot so it is definitely worth trying it out.

The app works very well. It is very useful for managing my ride whilst on the bike. At first the meetup tool was confusing to use, but after getting the hang of it it helps me create meetups with my friends, and lately they have been updating this letting you specify whether to ride together or each one at their own pace. Overall, I recommend this app for enhancing the zwifting experience.

“Free” Trial That Isn’t Really Free (In My Experience) My experience with the “free” trial was not what I expected. When returning the device, there are shipping charges, and additional fees may apply that effectively offset the value of the free period. Also, if the return shipment is delayed, extra costs can be added, which in some cases may exceed $150. This is only my personal experience, but in my view the term “free” can be misleading for some users, as you may later discover costs

It's a low "meh". Don' get me wrong, the soundscapes are high quality. But there was already very little in the free version and since I first got it you've moved even MORE stuff to premium. Speaking of payments, why isn't it an option to just permanently buy individual selections for a small one-time fee? I'd gladly pay for PERMANENT ACCESS to an individual item but since its premium or nothing, I've just gotta do without, which means you don't get my money. Dont you want my money? Needs work.

i havent been able to use it to its full extent as the gym program i signed up for asked me to download the app. Still really handy, the only thing is i have the notifications turned on and i have yet to receive one reminding me of my classes. unfortunately i missed a yoga class i booked because i forgot about it and if i had that notification i dont think i would have missed it. Otherwise i love that its not specified to one gym or even just gyms it has a bit of everything in one place.

App is good, but nutrition tracking is atrociously bad. If you live in Ireland - don't bother. When you choose Ireland as a region then it won't scan British products, if you change the region to the UK then the issue will be fixed... until you decide to scan an Irish product... a very inefficient way to add food - takes multiple taps and minutes of your time to do it. Better try nutracheck - effortless data input for the fraction of the price. The other parts of the app are all good.

Doesn't want to connect to my powerlabs heart rate monitor. My phones Bluetooth picks up the heart rate monitor but when I try and connect it to the app it doesn't show. tried uninstalling and such and nothing. Can't say I've seen much in customer service either. Useless for now. Sad, heard such good things about this app. Oh well, on to the next app! 🙂

I love my Garmin venue watch and the Garmin connect app. The Garmin app provides just the right balance of health matrix and tracking without attempting to get overly complicated. I also love that there is no required subscription and features are not locked to the type of phone you use. I would strongly recommend any Garmin watch and this app.

Used to be a great app, but now with new design changes it takes me longer to log meals, which I don't like at all. Now to find created meals I have to go through two drop down menus, not convenient at all.

I really like this app; it makes food logging simple. Dailyburn has a huge database of foods, and the interface is easy to use. My one complaint is that the total calorie display can be inconsistent (the goal number may not be right sometimes), but aside from that, it works well.

It used to track weekly miles walked. That panel has now disappeared and replaced with "Energy Expended" (calories I guess) which I don't care about. In fact I don't see any way of editing my goals anymore. There used to be a way of doing this. Maybe it's right in front of my face and I'm missing it. If I figure it out I'll edit this review.

Snooze after timeout doesn't work. Just turns into a timeout notification (emergency is set to silent for 1 min) EDIT: Is there a way to make it just snooze after timeout w/o the emergency alarm (I don't see an option to turn it off so its only timeout>snooze. currently forced to timeout> silent emergency at 1 min (which is apparently10 mins)>then snooze)
It will not allow me to log in for some reason. I can clog in just fine via the internet.
No graphs,  no real info.  Just a  barcode scanner.

I love the meal planner and automatic grocery list, saves me from forgetting ingredients for recipes and keeps me from picking up unneccesary items at the grocery store. Great to have all my recipes - from the web as well as my own, in one place. Nutrition info and scaling the recipe (e.g. if you want to do 3 servings instead of 4) a good bonus. You will need to invest some time to key in your own recipes, but the effort is worth it IMO. Very rich in features.

Dissapointing, Only gives you 7 free session options [include short stories and meditation] but it only allows you to play them 3 times each, like what the hell. I've had the app for 21 days so I'm nearing the end of all my free sessions. It's good for awhile but it's not going to stay on my phone for much longer. Very upset as it has potential.

recent update last week has bricked the activity interface for me, have reinstalled and it crashes everytime. I cannot click on an activity to open it only view it from the feed. if you have thr choice, don't update until they fix it.
First use today and I loved it. the app performed better then expected and surprisingly didn't kill my battery.
Good, but missing exercise tracker

It works most of the time, but is very buggy. I expected more for a product that costs so much. Bugs found so far: 1. Trying to check for firmware updates on the 2.0 strap will always result in the app showing the strap disconnected. 2. Sleep recommendations vary wildly from expected. One late night oncall will have the app telling me I require 3 hours of sleep a night. 3. If sleep is still processing the user input data is not saved. The app will just keep prompting you for the same info.

Great device, terrible customer support and warranty policy. If it was just reviewing the device or software it would be 5 stars, but since this is a subscription that requires hardware my experience takes it down to 2 stars and it's only 2 because when it was working the entire package was the real deal. First the good. The device when it worked, really helped me understand my sleep patterns allowing me to maximize my mountain bike rides because I was fully recovered. The level of data and AI was nothing short of amazing. Now the bad, for a company that bases it's revenue stream off of a subscription fee the customer support and warranty process is terrible. My device died, as in went from a topped off charge to dead and unable to accept a charge. I went to the website expecting to enter in some basic info and get a new device, I mean I was paying them a monthly fee with an 18 month commitment. Unfortunately, I was sadly mistaken. The only support I could get was through a generic ema

Like the voice updates aling the way, but can take a while for the gps signal to come up. Also have to start a new session where the signal stops unexpectedly. Update 10/5: Endomondo app has stopped working as it should. Synchronisation isn't happening. It takes hours for the synchronisation to complete, sometimes does not complete. The app performance is worsening over recent days.
great app. it misses two features. voice mode. and a feature to add a product nutritional facts by taking a photo of the nutritional facts of the product.

So far amazing!!! Yes, I have recommend. And I love how the app syncs with my food intake app, really helps me see how many calories I am actually burning. And love the GPS tracking, even works out on trails, have yet to get gps to work on an indoor track though). With the live tracker can totally see routs and distances outdoors, save and review, and even select for entering a session If I missed turning on live tracking. Thanks UA!
At first it was frustrating, used Fitbit for years, now I really enjoy the Garmin, the app is also a pleasure.

App constantly drops my gym out of my favourites. I'm often on the waitlist for classes but the app doesn't send you notifications when your status changes you have to check manually. Its sporadic in its calendar sync. I'm not sure if gym's pay for this because its the only option or if its just cheap, but I'd probably say its not worth it!

I've been using this app for years now and I have never had any major issues with it. It's my go to exercise app and I've been using it even more since the pandemic started. However, after the latest update, the instructions and timer sounds started behaving erratically for all workouts. They cut off after 2 mins, and briefly come back after 5 mins, cut off again, until the end of the workout. I hope Nike fixes this glitch soon as many have also been experiencing this issue.
The worst app ever

I have used older version of app and even paid for power pack and was very happy with its easy to use functions. I came back to app and there was a new pro version. I paid the $99 for lifetime membership because it clearly stated create and log own programs and create reps and sets. Upon trying to do this (unless I'm missing something) it looks as if you can only have one program active and are limited to a max of 5 sets. That is not very custom. Guess I'm going back to strong app and out $99.

I love the app. It does have a lot of great meditation content, the sleep stories are great, even the music. They seem to add content on a regular basis. It was glitchy when I first installed it. But it sends to be much better. It's the first time I paid so much for an app, but I do enjoy it. If they lower the price a bit I'd certainly renew.

I like my device but switching from using a Fitbit, I am finding the app is not user friendly. My biggest annoyance is the period tracker it assumes your period has started when it has not! No way to edit or track your cycle . I wish the layout and ease of use was similar to other fitness trackers. I will use this one until it dies and just buy a cheap Amazon tracker next time

Functionality is great, map cache not so much. It would be nice to be able to clear map cache or manually download maps. Most areas of Watopia are a few thin strips of map. A few other maps are the same. Clearing cache does not help and clearing data only works for a little bit. Multiple Android devices, similar map issues.

not too much to say. It got the time correct, the speed correct, the distance was close enough, but for some reason it thinks I cycled in a perfectly straight line. Not sure why, because I did basically the opposite.

Easy to use fitness booking app, looks good, and it has improved a fair bit over the last 6-9 months (a few bugs and annoyances I've had, have been addressed). It's still glitchy with sometimes showing the wrong day's classes for days ahead of the current day, but a refresh (pull down on the page) fixes that.

I really love this app. It has just about every function and tracking I could ask for. Please add the ability to manually add body composition information(body fat percentage, etc.) without first having to buy the Garmin scale. Thank you.

I had to search for a product I needed but never found. I like Vitacost. But like everywhere prices have gone up. I have had trouble with the app. It will not checkout with PayPal. I have items until recently I couldn't find any elsewhere. Now, I can but it's much more expensive, so I always come back to Vitacost. The app has given me problems almost everytime I shop. I wish I would have shopped online instead. They have a wide variety, informative blogs & recipes, quick delivery.
It took a few days to master the app that is to realize what you can potentially do with it. Just don't give up to quickly and you'll begin to love it.
Great start, personally i'd like a widget to show how many calories etc. If scan is not found quick add facility.

Tracking, even for walking and with precision location allowed, is substandard. Even a straight road walk in loops is tracked with loop ends much short of the actual turn. In 9/10 times the distance measured is short by at least 20%. This renders the app useless for actual activity tracking.

Although I appreciate that my fitbit device is always currently synced with Google Health, I am completely dissatisfied with the calorie log. I want to have the option to manually enter my total calories at the end of the day. I don't want to be forced to choose from a recently used log. It would be wonderful if the calorie tile were similar to the hydration tile. The hydration tile allows me to enter the date and the number of ounces. Super simple! Can that option be added to the calorie tile?

I received a free trial for 30 days and I will say that this app has helped me a lot over the past month. ALTHOUGH....on day 29 I canceled the free trial to keep from being charged, it showed being canceled. Day 30 it shows being canceled for 2027. YES!!! I was charged for a year subscription. And they have no REFUND policy! Needless to say I am very upset with Google play store! Be careful with the free trials not just from CALM but ALL. Lesson learned for myself!
Customer service is non existent. Says that they are available 24/7 but when you call the number that is listed on the website they says that they are unavailable

Pretty good on the whole. Would help if it was easier to give ride-ons during a ride and to easily return ride-ons via this app too. Switching from Fitbit and tossing mine. Fun fact - it doesn't actually need your location information to sync. Rather, they made it necessary specifically so that they can - per their privacy policy - selI your location information. They will claim they need that permission to sync data - as a developer, I can assure you this is untrue. They also cleverly made it so that in order to do basic things, like setting alarms, you have to use the app, then sync... And thus give them location access. 👎
Excellent app, easy to use and really convenient.

After the most recent update this app went from incredible to total garbage. They deleted a ton of workouts, so if you had saved workouts or benchmarks you were screwed. Additionally, they removed some of their best workout formats for seemingly no reason. Honestly so disappointed by this drastic and sudden change, guess it's back to hunting for another workout app.

App keeps crashing on signing in or continue as guest. I'm an android user using Samsung ultra S22 . Just downloaded the app last night. It was fine until after I purchase classes. Today when I tried to reopen, it keeps crashing no matter how many times I have closed and reopen the app.

five minutes of inane questions (such as which clothes i'd like to wear after i've lost weight) after which there's three different 'pay for premium!' screens and an unskippable wheel of fortune to reduce the price. just to set up. uninstalled without ever completing signup, i'm going back to myfitnesspal.

Great app, but It would be nice if the zwift companion app can show your stats, what level you on and how far till the next level, how many drops you have, all the routes that's available, if you have done them or not, like it shows on Zwift. Perfect for those that are just beginning or those that haven't worked out for over a year. Updated Jan 19. 2026: life got busy, so the work outs stopped for a few years. far worse than fitbit app, which itself seemed to be getting worse steadily. probably need to go Garmin, though some free apps also seem to be catching up to google's paid fitbit experience. Great shop and great app. Easy to use and more convenient than the website from my mobile device. Vitacost is my source for all my natural products, supplements and even things like fitness products, tea and gum. I'd always rather support them than Amazon.

Do NOT get this app! And I'm gonna tell you why. EVERYTHING in this app, from meal plans to cookbooks to recipes the app designers charge you for. UPDATED: I deleted the app and just my studio's website. I hate interacting with this company! Nope. Extremely inaccurate. One morning I woke up to find my phone on the bedside had done 8000+ steps on the night. Apparently without me. Other users have all raised similar, and all other issues with this app. Apparently it used to be better, but I have never seen this for myself.

Terrible app. Mixes up my home country when I'm selecting products. Often times products I've selected and then deleted still show up in my cart. It's very easy and quick the exhaust the free portion of the app. I especially didn't like the sales push you get WITHIN some of the free meditations. Really annoying. Also, the "birds and crickets" in the background were a little too much for me. Otherwise, the functionality of the app is fine. Very simple and straightforward. And no need to download.

The new UI update is AWFUL. It is not intuitive to be able to log food and exercise. It visually is also a lot more difficult to view the stats for the day. And to see everything you used to is more clicks, which makes it extremely unfriendly for neurodivergent users. You can't swipe between days. Just awful. Absolutely amazing classes, I have arthritis in my knees and shoulders, and find this class easier due to the heat....it's a must try!!! love it x

The Fitbit app had its issues, but after using it for around six years I found it almost charming. Google Health has ruined a perfectly fine app.

The app is great however it would be nice if you had the option to put group riders in alphabetical order because it hard to give kudos back with all the names bouncing around as riders change positions.

Great app with a variety of exercises for all areas of your life. Unfortunately, a 7-day free trial isn't long enough to determine if this app is right for you. The premium version is a bit expensive for what I've seen even with 40% off. You can find other apps with all the premium features for free with the same leaders. Was great, but the latest update seems to have broken something. I can no longer find many food items that I previously normally logged.

Overall, I've had a good experience doing the training plans available on the app. It makes running a lot less unbearable with someone keeping you going. Distance tracking is pretty good and you can see how far your friends in your club go as well. Pretty cool to see yearly, monthly and weekly summaries. After the most recent update I am absolutely pissed because suddenly a feature that was always free is now tied behind a pro subscription which is just unbelievably anti consumer. Great food tracker, but buried with annoying gimmicks. Database is solid, barcode scanning works well, but daily streaks, AI recommendations, diamonds? A minimal mode would make this 5 stars for users who want simplicity over gamification. The annoyances only motivate users to move to a competitor app.

the app is good iv been using it daily for the past 2 weeks, what i dont like: doesnt have a weekly estimate the calorie defecit for progress and adjusment porpuses, it doesnt update all vitamins just the basics( protien carbs fats and sometimes fiber) .it doesnt let you set a fast that started a day befor if u forgot to update so im using a different app. i purchased a year worth of sub in advance and then i cancled the sub in case i no longer need the service but it keeps the pro tap red

not the most accurate of this type of app. also keeps crashing my android phone even after I followed the advice in the FAQ. it is easy to use and good for short (less than an hour) workouts I am looking for something more accurate and reliable, one that lets me use my phone in an emergency if I have to.

pretty good so far on the free version. connects to heart rate monitor. seems off on the calorie counter but on the whole. good vibes so far

Ruined my health app. There is no longer any data you as the user can benefit from. Tracking is incorrect or not there at all. Inaccurate time, distance and route tracking. The route it tracks me on will regularly miss entire sections of the run (cutting me straight across long curves, tracking my return route as through peoples yards and other Inaccuracies) making my pace seem very slow because 25% of my run is just missing. I was a big fan until the most recent update. All I want is to track calories. The most effective way to accomplish this goal is to see the breakdown of each item and meal quickly. I want to see the sum for the day, sum for each meal, and total for each item on the same page. This separated MFP from other calorie tracking apps. there's a lot of great features. Had a great experience with the app until just a few months ago. Just like many others, I had problems with the app resetting the time my workouts would last if my screen was off. I love using this app... when I am actually able to use it! Consistently over the last several months the app simply won't open no matter how often I click the button. Now I can't even get the website to open.

The app isn't free. It isn't even freemium. It's a week trial for a subscription service. What shady tactics. I would use extreme caution installing this app on your device. if they're willing to be sneaky like this, who knows what kind of shady code is running in this app. Stay clear of this one.

theres no way to really see you sleep on the free plan. the app was incredibly bad about understanding when I went to sleep, and would try to say I'm asleep every couple hours when I was still at my keyboard working hard. it gave me no information about when it would trigger the alarm, just sometime before it needed to, with no information. app did not help me get a better model of my sleep, just did things. uninstalled.

considering you're not paying for the app, it's great, but I would expect more from the sleep band, it's data tracking is poor. if the band falls off or you take it off middle of the night and then you pull it back on then you go back to sleep. it does not track any more sleep. it doesn't pick up naps either during the day. This cycling tracking app is simple and easy to use. It tracks your rides and distance well without being confusing, which makes it great for quick use. The layout is clean and straightforward, so you can start riding without messing with settings. It covers the basics, but I couldn’t find a calories burned feature. Overall, it’s a solid app if you want something simple and reliable for tracking your bike rides.

The Game feature does not connect on a consistent basis. In fact, most of the time it does not engage. I can't give Ride Ons, take photos, change POV or turn. Mind you, I'm on your pro plan, and you could've at least made an option to turn it off, but no. I'll change the app once the paid plan is over.

1 star because Yazio doesn't provide the calories to health connect. Otherwise, I like the ai camera but often have to fix the suggestions. I also find the dark pattern onboarding sequence egregious. I paid 11 euros for the year so please don't accept any price above that.

the absolute worst step tracker. doesn't sync properly and even forcing sync doesn't work most times. I hate this app so much. The selling point of this app is that the alarm will wake you up during a 30min window during the lightest part of your sleep cycle, as opposed to when you're deeply asleep. Like most sleep trackers, this is determined by movement. Well, it took my cats less than a week to figure out that if they walk around between me and the phone in the morning, the alarm goes off sooner. So now every day, it goes off as soon as the 30 minute window starts. 🙄 It's a pretty good alarm without the sleep tracking, but doesn't do anything my old one didn't do.

The free version is OK. However, I'd only consider purchasing the app if and only if it (1) offered the same feature set for Android devices as it offers for iPhones, (2) I could update information directly from my smartphone instead of having to update info online and then uninstall and reinstall the app for the changes to sync, and (3) the cost of the app was brought (way) down. MyFitnessPal offers more features without obtrusive ads for free.
It won't let me log in . Everything else is logged in but this.

3.5. I really like the concept of this app. However, after installing it I've found it pretty buggy - definitely not Google's usual standard. The workouts are good, but the UI change was a significant step backwards. It's no longer possible to easily check and properly learn exercises before starting the workout. Strap not connecting to app. Went through instructions sent via email. I also enjoy being able to see other's strain/recovery as a form of external motivation through competition.

Entered name and password, tried to add my address but... "We are having technical difficulties. Please try again or check back with us shortly." 15 May 26 Still the same issue despite working on different troubleshooting steps. Disappointing that an expensive watch requires a lot of researching from users for it to hopefully work. 14 May 26 My venu3 shows as incompatible and won't upload a custom training I created right away.

I enjoyed the older Fitbit App. This current Health app is an absolute failure! I used to love Fitbit, but Google ruined it. I have used this app for years and loved it but this new update is horrible! it's hard to glance and see what you ate for each meal and the total calorie for the day is harder because it also leaves your calories burned still in the total for calories ate...it just seems like it went 10 years backward in the clean simple interface. Perfect for tracking colories with some issues. too many pop ads and every time it asks for premium version it's annoying. also many of the foods are lost in their database I also couldn't find a way to add my new food.

you can keep login traditional optional unless and until user feels that the interface is quite good easy to use and this app will make any sense in their life. let them use it first and if they will feel that this is good enough then user can use their traditionals to create their account. otherwise it is none of used to create their account if you use a doesn't use that in future. I love Whoop. I can see how different behaviours - getting enough sleep, caffeine, alcohol or eating a late meal affects how my body reacts. I can track many things & see their impact over time. I love Whoop coach that interprets the data on my behalf & I can ask it questions. A 60 year old woman & not sporty at all but I have found Whoop so valuable for monitoring what impacts me. I installed it based on a commercial I saw because I was having trouble falling asleep. The advertised feature (sleep stories) is pay only and I had to sign up for a free trial. Then it didn't help at all. I listened to two of them (over an hour of listening) and was still awake and exhausted. Maybe it works for some people, just not me. Keeps track of your workouts, mileage, and your GPS route. I just lowered the rating because, just as I was starting a ride... It took way too long to start. Not bad, but there are better apps out there with nicer interface and more data

I've tried a couple calorie counting apps and this one has been by far my favorite! The app is well laid out and intuitive to use, there are lots of recipes, the food database is huge, there are no annoying ads, the daily graphs tracking weight, calories, and macros are really helpful. There is also a paid version for a very reasonable fee which has wonderful nutritional plans. I highly recommend this app.
this is a good food tracker. it has a lot of foods. it's pretty easy to use. and it's cute. The problems I originally had with it have now been fixed.

None of these "Map My..." apps are working. Worthless! They don't keep tracking distance, they crash constantly, don't pause or finish when selected (defeats the purpose of timing a workout) and the biggest complaint I have is the apps are not uploading a cover photo. Zwift has decided to force their users to have to deal with a useless Fitness function and now an "AI" driven training plan, since they decided they want to compete with real Fitness apps such as Training Peaks. Just pure hard-selling and the recommendations just don't match your goals. I entered "improve fitness" as a goal and it gave me a protein target of 1g per kg body weight... Reason why 2 stars still: really liked the fresh design of the app. But don't let the design fool you, steer clear.

Great app! I really like all the data that whoop is able to collect and how they "dumb" it down, so I can understand it. Having said that, the one complaint I have, is that when it tells me how much sleep I should get, it is usually an hour or two after I should be in bed. For example, I normally get up at 4:30 on work days. So, to get 8 hours of sleep I need to go to bed at 8:30, but the app doesn't tell me that until 9:00-10:00ish.

Am not saying it's bad, but you can make the experience a lot better with all the data you provide. The Whoop UI is far better and one of the perfect apps though the data they provide is comparitively lesser than garmin. If you can customize the UI like that, connect app will be awesome.

I was initially happy with this app, but then they added lots of new paid features that get in the way of a simple workout. App doesn't work as well as the original Fitbit app Info doesn't transfer to the phone on a Regular basis

App is good for keeping me motivated. I have all the ""Map My" Apps. You can keep in contact with friends. Motivate each other. 1 negative...I believe calories burned total is off. Treadmill lists about 100 calories less. Try all apps for a variety. I subscribed too.

For example, every morning it tells me to swap brown sugar for fruit, but what i count actually is the brown sugar from my coffee. I won't get rid of the sugar in my coffee, no sir. A friend recommended this to me to book classes at my cycle gym in London (I normally book through the web schedule powered by MindBody). So not easier - I'm leaving this unhelpful app behind and it's back to the web version for me.

I now use Endomondo to record my rides, sync using Tapiriik, and Strava to tracky achievements and share with friends.

Works well. Disappointed that many features that used to be free require subscription that is too costly for the limited use I would make of it. I would be willing to pay $1/month for route details as any given point--speed, altitude, distance--and the route itself.

I am ridiculously impressed by this app and totally geeked out about how much detail it tracks. The fact that it is HIGHLY customizable is pure genius. A lot of thought clearly went into it. I am currently focused on protein, fiber, and water. Boom. Its right there. I love that I can integrate notes into my diary too! I rarely pay for apps but this one is worth its weight in gold. I have the app for android and also the web browser for my desktop. seamless integration. Bravo. Truly amazing.

The app is working well now. I haven't lost a run in awhile. As long as I can record all my runs in the app, without losing them, then I'm happy. Please bring more trophies & awards, they motivate me to achieve them.

Edit: unreliable and useless app. Unable to pay for classes easily as I've had to enter in my credit card, then leave the page and come back and then it mysteriously will take my money! I like vitacost, but the app is a bit tricky to navigate for me. It took some time to figure out how to move around and search for items.
it's a good app, but not amazing. not totally intuitive, some activities require several steps. sometimes difficult to navigate.

The app is great to use, after a ride you can look back over the route you have taken and assess your ride. Occasionally the app fails to log all the ride, could be a breakdown in the GPS signal, but this doesn't happen very often. All in all this is an excellent app for logging my rides and gives me a history of my 'workouts' so that I can see how I progress over a period of time.

If you enjoy riding but want to be able to do so even when outside conditions are not conducive, then Zwift is an excellent choice. It can be a touch glitchy however. Tracks easily once you get the hang of it. as more people use it the more items will be available.

It's good that it's free because the issues with casting to other devices is one of the most anger-inducing moments of my morning routine. It didn't used to be this bad. I would happily pay for this app if the casting feature actually just worked.

Just starting out but seems darn good, and with reasonable widgets. This is by far the best experience I've had with a fitness app, the amount of data available for free is fantastic! The health tracking tools offered outside of needing a watch is a nice touch. I enjoy the accuracy and library of food items available. I enjoy building/inputting my own recipes into the system. My PCP/Nutritionist can see my intake and critique as need be to tweak for my long term sense. Since using Cronometer this past March, I have lost 75 lbs and much of that is due to learning about the quality of food (or not) in out food supply. I've learned to use macro's as my path(s) to eat healthier and actually enjoy whole, non-processed foods. Well worth the value!!!

Greatest sleep monitoring, logging sleep details app, and honestly there's just so many features that I can't pick just three to highlight. I'm a bit of a tech minded person with health issues, so I use a lot of the features that allow me granular control over logging my sleep as it ties into my overall health picture. I do think it's an amazing app and very customizable. 15/10 Get it.

This app has got every thing to make your sleep so much healthier! I use the smart period option that picks the best time to wake you up. Wakes you up when you're moving the most instead of deep sleep, basically. Then I set up a QR code on my fridge so the app forces me to stand up and go to the kitchen to scan it for the alarm to stop. You can lower the alarm for a bit so that it isn't annoying while you're walking. Really makes sure I am awake and keeps me disciplined!

you would just improve your Android app. I get a crash message every time I open the app, I can't track workouts through my phone, the calories don't display correctly, it's ugly. Lots of data available, especially if you opt for premium. All your CV fitness history, in one place such that you can monitor routes and your times. Will probably appeal to runners and cyclists rather than gym users. Personally I haven't tried other apps, but this suits my needs very well.

User Interface is not intuative. When a ride on button does not give a ride on and you have to Google how to give a ride on it means it's not a good Interface. I love the app so much, which is why I've also decided to go Premium. However, I don't enjoy the fact that my trainings from the training plan aren't saved if I don't have internet nor the fact that I can't add them through my training plan directly from the app. Having to log into my account through a browser is such a bummer, not to mention the fact that my account in the browser and the app aren't in complete sync and sometimes it takes hours for a workout to show in the browser.

Good way to keep track of daily intake of food. It's a bit of a pain the first two weeks because you have to input your food's nutritional info, but once thats done it's fine. One thing, it has never been able to sync with Samsung Health so you won't know your real calorie surplus/deficit as their calorie count default isn't accurate to you (and you can't input the correct count off of watch/app). Hopefully this will change soon.

I'm not a morning person, but having calm Cello music slowly wake me up before vibrating, and eventually turning into a more demanding Alarm if I don't get up is great. Then pairing that typically less stressful wake up, with scanning a code on the coffee machine as I press the button, is a much better way to wake up. Compared to desperately hitting snooze button to make the sound stop until I have to get up stressed out, and already running late. Amazing customization, that helps me get more sleep, reduce my snoring that caused headaches, and wake up easier!

Great App, when it works as it should! I love the guided runs. Coach Bennett is awesome. But: intermittent logging out is annoying. Gone down hill. I loved this app. Spent many hours uploading my recipes because it was a simplistic app. Gave what I need. But lately it isn't saving my planner. Constantly glitching out. redoing my review from 3 to 1 stars. I find it hilarious that google has the audacity to want to charge you $80 a year for premium on an app that doesn't even work 70% of the time. Easy and intuitive to use. Enables me to set a calorie target and see how my eating meets (or exceeds!) that by simple tracking of my food. I also appreciate the macronutrient breakdown - carbon vs. protein vs. fat. A great tool.

I lost over 100 pounds in less than one year using Cronometer to track my food intake. Now, I'm continuing to use it to track macros as I build back lean muscle mass. It's easy to use and has become such a habit that I don't even think about it anymore.

have been using this app forever. unfortunately out of nowhere alarms started not going off pretty randomly. I am still unsure what the issue is but I can no longer risk being late to work for this app

I've used this for 5 years, mostly happily, but it has become buggy and unpredictable. For example, it suddenly stopped reporting snoring %, and the main screen reorganized itself. Changing Settings to customize and fix the display has always been a disorganized, unintuitive mess, and is now just not worth the effort. The user interface needs a complete overhaul by someone with expertise in usability, and I don't expect that will ever happen.

I've been using the free version for a few years now, and decided to buy the full version. However, what I love about this app is the free version is still a great alarm app. It doesn't force you to use the dashboard view showing all the features you have to pay to use, you can just set it to show your alarms and that's basically it. It's as simple or as complicated as you want it to be, and you don't have to open your wallet to eliminate any reminders of features you don't want or need.

This app used to be wonderful, now it's terrible!! The dashboard used to display all of my stats easily, now I have to search for them. I no longer get my resting heart rate, just the daily range and I can no longer add new food items in the tracker, I have to pick the closest thing to what I'm eating. DO NOT BUY A FITBIT! Google ruined something that was so wonderful.

I love this app. Scanning food items by bar code is genius. Counting calories makes you hyper aware of what you are putting into your body. There are people from all over the world using this thing. If the premium version had a better user interface and translation API that would be wild. The community behind the simple tracking is what makes this app magic. That is where the value-added skyrockets. Make connecting with others easier and more fluid and if you go public, I'll buy.

The best free sleep app, period. I have been using it for a long dang time. it has the best alarm "Are you up?" implementation ive ever seen. It helped me keep my job that I've been at for 12 years. The tracking versatility is top notch with the tracking itself being above average. I bought the upgrade a while back and have used it since.

GPS tracking is perfect to the metre. Very easy app to use and the data read outs are spot on. Perfect to record distance, calories, average speed, all speed information and elevation. The short cut notification that appears on my phone's sleep mode screen is also a great feature. One touch and I can see distance and time totals without unlocking my phone. No glitching and seems to be ad free. Bravo app developers, Bravo!

The website itself is much more helpful when it comes to counting calories and macros. I can Google search the exact ounces or grams of a food item and the appropriate macros will show up. On the app it just shows "medium sized banana", which is not accurate enough for monitoring your food consumption. Furthermore, the app tells me I need to consume 3100 calories per day to lose weight, when that is in fact 600 calories OVER my MAINTENANCE level. Needs some fine tuning in my opinion, big time.

Love it, very easy to track my intake, even when I cook my meals from scratch. Upgrade to gold so I can import receipts which makes it even faster. I used to love this app and considered subscribing but now the ads are ridiculous. multiple popping up before you can even log a entire meal and some you can't even close without restarting the app. If you are trying to annoy people into subscribing it isn't working on me. just makes me want to find a new app. I don't mind ads but put them as soon as I open the app or after I log something not in the middle of typing and trying to search and make sure they can be closed.
not a good app, reloads too often, too slow, clunky navigation.

the previous Fitbit app was great it told me about my sleep style and how many total miles I've walked gave me badges and made me proud to get to certain milestones. now it say I had great sleep when I tossed and turned and woke up multiple times in the night, there doesn't seem to be any milestones for anything. I don't want to log my dinner I'm not trying to count calories and it's the first thing that pops up on my home page.

The product and ordering process are fine. Unfortunately, along the way I had to concent to marketing emails. Way too many and apparently no way to reduce the 'spam' without losing my order history. Draconian.
First time occurrence. App kicked me out, won't let me login with my Facebook or Gmail account. Won't let me get past login page on phone browser or desktop

Health data has been beyond insightful since getting the Garmin Venu 3! It has been so much better since the updates that connect to other apps as well! Helps keep everything in one place! Only update I'm hoping will eventually come out is for blood pressure readings being able to be imported from MedM so then you get a complete view.

The app would be good if there'd be an option to turn off chests, streaks and the mascot thing. I hate the chest and streak feature and the creepy mascot as well. Please don't be Duolingo. Be a good, functioning calorie tracking app. Simplicity is not bad!!! Could be a premium feature. I'd really appreciate it if something like that would be available...

bait and switch. the emdr advertised us not what you get. operating the app is unnecessarily complex...too many steos to get to what you want. if there is any interruption, you have to restart completely and then cannot simply return to where you were. advertised cost is not what is actually charged, and cost to value is untenable.
Love it. The easiest app I've used to save, organize and shop from my recipes. Also love how the recipe creators have numerous ways to get paid for what they've created. Wins all around.

I like the app. I don't have the premium version. I know you get full access to content with premium, but I definitely wish I had access to more sleep stories. Even 2 or 3 more would be nice. I can turn a sleep story on, while not feeling tired, and then find myself drifting off. I'd be happy to give more stars if free app users got access to a couple more sleep stories.

Switched from My Fitness Pal. Cronometer is a far better app. I suggest subscribing to the Gold for ad free for a seamless experience. Price is right and the upgraded options are fast and very user friendly. One glitch when subscribing signing up through Google Play Store may cause an error message to cancel your subscription as GPS requests you open the app to confirm subscription, even after you've already done so. GPS ended up canceling my subscription, but I immediately re-subscribed.
Have used Garmin products and app for many years, no complaints always helpful and improving!

As with every app I've tried of this kind, the auto pause/resume doesn't happen snappily enough, but this could be a GPS thing as much as the app. The one thing it will no longer do, at all, is share via Facebook. Why? Galaxy S5. Still not sharing - Galaxy Note 3. And still the same...S7 Edge. All the above comments still apply, but generally more reliable as time passes. Still the tracking app I use, after all, so must be doing something right! 1 star added.
App won't launch. Just says to check internet connection while connected to the internet. Love the store, but the app is terrible.

The app is good works and easy to use. The issue is the new 4.0 is garbage. I have had it now a week and it has only work the first day. The following days it does not stay on and does not track your heart rate. Out of 24 hours it works 3 hours out of the day. If you have a 3.0 use it don't upgrade. Customer service works with you until there are no more options on how to fix it, then you never hear from them again. And how on earth can we not have the time on the damn thing. Fit bit is the way.

April 2024 version of the app has reduced the data shown on the home screen and instead insists on filling the screen with a couple of graphics, I want a quick glance at the numbers, not a bunch of pretty pictures. Previous version was much more useful. I now have to go hunting for useful data. It's a shame as the hardware is good. It would appear that the Devs ignored the beta testers and listened to their marketing team instead. Not a clever move. 2026 Update: This review is still relevant.

Every so often my favourites are deleted which is frustrating. I opened the app this morning, and usually it gives you a list of classes for the same day. But because today's a public holiday it was a list of tomorrow's classes. It's not obvious at all, so I booked a class on the wrong day and then the studio was shut. They really need to say 'no classes today' instead of automatically skipping ahead to the next day.

this is the worst fitness app I've ever used. I track my bike rides through wahoo, which then sends the data to fit through health connect, I routinely lose 5+km each ride through this process. Google fit also reads data from my Fitbit despite me having fitbit set to read only on healthconnect. It also takes at least 15 minutes to load my exercises from health connect. It also changes my avg speed in impossible ways, how could I go 17kmh in the graph for 20 minutes yet only go .13km???

Slow startup with adguard (ad blocking software) running. Thought was an issue with the app, but this is a new issue. Brilliant app but shouldnt rely on an Internet connection to load up. Should load up first and then show connectivity with server etc. Also why is it being flagged by adblock software - is there reporting to ad providers?

I really enjoy using the app (although not using it as frequent as I should) but recently the sign in process has kept me stuck on the waiting screen (first off I don't see the need why I need to sign in everyday, It feels like an unnecessary extra step). I was forced to do cardio today because of this 🫠🫠 Edit: they've fixed the sign in process, so that's better. Also, I would like a search/filter function for finding programmes. It'll really streamline the process.

This app doesn't show you the fat macro percentage. Only: Protein/ carbs/ "RDI" and no fat!! You have to dig into each individual meal and food item to get the number and then add them up yourself. So frustrating because it's otherwise a decent app!! Going back to Samsung health because though it sucks, at least I can get all my numbers totaled on the main screen.

This app seems promising. The recipe builder is straightforward, though the measurements aren't always UK friendly. Unlike other meal planning apps out there I can't see a way of inviting someone to the meal plan, so we have a shared view of the meals for that week. Ideally this would work like a shared calendar, like Timetree.

Wow this might be the most demoralizing piece of tech I've ever owned. Air temps drop by 50 degrees and suddenly my fitness is -5 on the next run. Pee break at 4am? You couldn't possibly have slept prior to that, with only 2.5hrs of sleep, you need a rest day! I've owned multiple forerunners and they've gotten more underwhelming with each new release. The app itself crashes regularly and settings are buried in random menu's 5 clicks deep which is a hassle. Might be time to move on from Garmin.
Won't allow me to sign up on Facebook or by email. Try to go to the log in page and try that way but still doesn't work. Sad.

Amazing for meal prepping This app is ready to use and VERY handy for planning or meals too easy during the week. I love the grocery list feature as well. Lets you scale it to the amount of servings you intend on making, and you can modify the list too account for what you already have as you add things. Very well thought out. This app has won me over and changed the way I eat forever!
App won't let me log in anymore even though I change the password. However, I can log in through Chrome browser. I like the convenience of the app but it's pretty unreliable

I thought it'd be a good app judging by the score but I guess standards have just got pretty low for apps. The basic functionality is fine, presentation could use a little work, but all the interesting features are of course locked behind a subscription. Problem is they charge a stupid amount for it and it's subscription only so it'd be a never ending fire of money. when did twenty dollars monthly become the default? this does not offer that kind of value, that's closer to a one time cost.

I love Map My Ride! Is a motivator for me. It is fun to keep track of my miles and find new trails and routes. The only thing I don't like about the app, is trying to discard a workout like if I forgot to pause or finish a workout. It is nearly impossible without uninstalling the app and reinstalling it. Very frustrating and it has happened to me several times.

I have been using this app for years, and it has been an absolute gift! Since the latest update, however, the app will stop registering my workout at random intervals, dropping the GPS on routes I have cycled many times before. I'm sure it will be fixed, but it's not really very useful right this moment.

The latest May 2026 update broke timestamps, at least for premium users. 'Use last time' no longer recognizes the last time. Why can't we just set the time of the meal? How many people actively separate the time they ate each food in a meal anyway? There are other issues with the update, but that's the one that made me drop a star.

They should tell you the actual cost if the app before you download and register for the product. After the 7 day trial you will be charged $59.99 for the year. Can't pay monthly, you can't pay for individual products, etc. In the description it states in-app charges $.99-$399.99 or something to that regard. Be upfront about the cost of the subscription in the description, not everyone has $60 to pay at one time, especially with Covid but could benefit from the app. Give other options!!

The Monthly & Yearly headings used to have sections with stats like "Average Distance per Run", "Average Time per Run" & "Total Elevation Gain", but they seem to have recently been removed. I am very disappointed in that since it gave me extra metrics to base my running goals on. It would be great if they were put back. I really liked having those additional insights into my stats. After all, the app is about running & should include as many personal metrics as possible. The interface is good, but as far as providing additional motivation it is the stats that pack the punch for me; the little digital badges don't do it. The app occasionally crashes on my longer runs when I am trying to end it & I lose everything from that run, but it isn't often anymore. Had that happen at the Grand Canyon a couple months ago which was a massive bummer. Used to be a big problem. I think the tracking is close enough.

Works exactly how I wanted it to. It tracks my sleep and helps me want to have a good sleeping schedule, it gives me small, useful bits of advice, and the "shake it" feature for the alarm actually helps me get up in the morning (major struggle for me). Bonus; I use the sound recording every night and it catches the sound of my cat purring, and it makes me happy.

does what i need it to do, might go to plus some day we will see. definitely replaced MFP Update: Started a gold trial, so far so good. I import recipes from the meal prep manual site, which is far easier than having to enter things manually. I basically meal prep everything, so it works out. If you are serious about counting calories and knowing what you're eating, cronometer is worth it. The secret to gaining/losing weight is manipulating your calorie intake.

This is an awesome meal planning app and the only one I use now. You can search for special diets, including gluten-free. Each recipe has the option of generating a shopping list for you, so you have everything you need when you go to the store. I've got about 11 cookbooks saved in here, and the recipes have been interesting, easy and kid-friendly. Highly recommended.

Hmmm - not great really. The app itself works okay but the GPS functionality is mostly awful. When (if!) it works the app is really good, but the GPS constantly dropping out and missing whole sections of runs / walks / workouts really spoils it, almost to the point its useless as an activity tracker because the GPS is so unreliable. 22/06/2021 - reduced the rating to one Star and deleted the app. There are just too many bugs, glitches, poor functionality and the GPS keeps dropping out.

5/10/19: Still not fixed. 4/15/19: What is going on with this app??? I have been using Map My Ride for four years. Within the last two months, the ability of the app to accurately track any of my workouts is WAY off - a half mile or more short each time. The routes it's tracking are a series of jagged lines that aren't even close to the actual route. I install all updates.

I can no longer do strength training, even 'free' without it automatically tracking sets reps and rest. Sets and Auto are TURNED OFF in settings. It drives me CRAZY - it doesn't even recognise when I'm ACTUALLY starting a new set or resting. It's completely random! It's disappointing if I need to stop recording workouts as Strength and use another activity instead.

Smooth Running App. I'm not a high mileage person, but I still enjoy the challenges. I use them to motivate me when I don't want to get up and get moving. I don't use the auto stop feature because I end up losing a good quarter mile. Other wise an overall good tracker. (Update) The updated dashboard gives good info. I'm not training for a race, but I can see how the stats on pace for each mile would come in handy. (Update 2) The new tracker gives a more accurate reading of distance.

The App has some good features, i.e. data sharing with Fitbit👌! But it has two major gaps,🙄 on the food scanning: 1. It doesn't allow you, to store a scanned and revised dish, as a meal, for repeated usage 2. It's not prepared for Latin/Spanish eating out behavior - Tapas Sharing of dishes, since you can't scan a dish and divide it into equal portions (same for pizza sharing).🤞

Best free sleep app I've ever tried! Option to play sounds to fall asleep. Tracks your movements and can even record all sounds in your sleep(listed to track snoring). I kept waking up feeling like I hadn't rested at all. After a week of seeing that I was waking multiple times during the night, I started recording the sounds too. I reviewed everything one morning and learned that it was my husband crawling into and out of bed late at night that was waking me out of deep sleep 5-6 times a night.
good, easy and clear to use, only have free version. but no adds. have treid other apps this this has been the best for keeping me on track

Good app. Connected effortlessly to my smart trainer. I enjoy the routes, training programs, sponsored challenges, pro riders, races, zwift community of users. I believe the app is worth the monthly price. Also there's no initiation fees or monthly contract commitments. Charges are monthly and drop without penalties. I recommend this app!

Hate the new format. Just changed today. Harder to log and find my stats for the day. May have to find another app for this. Not happy at all. Loved it for years. Not anymore. It's awful. Takes longer to log exercise and meals. Old format was easier. Now I have to turn the screen to see macros, doesn't fit on my screen. This is not progress at all.
This is a bada** app. Very easy to use

I know you can't make eyeryone happy and I undertand that . But I do not like the new view. I was part of beta testing and I wish there was an option to keep the old view. As someone with Autism I find this new setup to be too much and less user friendly. I miss having a homescreen with the stats there. Maybe under settings there could be an option to switch back, that might be too costly. But right now it feels too much, too much AI, too much happening on the page. I miss the simplicity.

I loved this when I first got it a few months ago, now it's just frustrating. I like to keep my checked items so i can easily add things to my grocery list that I buy regularly. The grocery list started unchecking all of my checked items, and it always happened at the store, which was super inconvenient. Now I have to clear out all of my checked items at the end of each trip. Also, in the last couple of weeks, it says it can't import any web recipe I try to save.

Used to be an awesome app, but I can't log in anymore. So disappointing, because I have tons of favorite recipes saved.
