Requires me to use a Facebook login because that was how I originally signed up, but now it says Facebook is not supported on the app when you click the login button. I've reached out to support and no one has ever responded.

Finally... I can stop guessing and make informed decisions based on real quality metrics. The sleep data alone is invaluable. Whoop... you guys nailed it. I can feel the difference in my day to day living. The app is going downhill fast. Doesn't measure my strain, recovery is all aver the map lately etc... looking at other recent reviews its not just me. I used to recommend whoop to everyone. Not any more.

decent app especially on the free version, the paid version doesn't really give you much more I've found...could do with more options around linking your fitness apps they're all Google heavy, Garmin and Fitbit...it automatically gives you 700+ calories used on a spinning session when mine says 240. be good if you can just add the meal as a whole calorific value instead of adding all the recipe one by one which is a massive ball ache. nice easy app, it would be awesome if it expanded more
Daily burn Tracker

It is a shame you cant do more in the companion app. It would be nice to have the ability to change your avatar and bikes without being "in game". Also if you could check out the different routes or workouts that would be beneficial.

Fabulous App! Definitely an unfair advantage in your training. Train any time of the day or night, at your own level, loads of group activities, and excellent training programmes. I love the extended fun events like the Tour de Zwift too. Also rides like the Andy Schleck Cappucino Ride are really cool. Love it! (The profile of the rides is a little small to see when you are riding).

It works well for a free app. It seems like it used to autostart, which wasn't great because it wouldn't always autostart. At a stop sign for instance. Now I just forget to stop it when I take a break which messes up the data. I love the data and readouts and it makes a nice little route map. Elevation gain is very helpful.
Horrible, cant book my virtual classes, so I restalled it and wont sign me in instead ask me to create a new account.

I need to reconnect/pair my Fenix 6 Pro at least once a day (mostly more often) with Garmin Connect running on my Samsung Galaxy zfold 4. Device and Android are updated. It is a common problem I learnt on the Web. Why is GARMIN not dealing with this? None of the fixes described on the Internet worked for me. Funny enough, I get after each pairing another Congratulations email from Garmin: Congrats on your new device! It is annoying to be pushed to pair/ reconnect that often. Probably Garmin ...

Google Health just took over the Fitbit app. I am still working on finding my data. I use it primarily for sleep data, and since I am trying to lose weight, I want to be able to compare calorie intake to energy output. This was so easy with the Fitbit app. I also eat food that I calculate the calories for myself. I am no longer able to do quick add under calories. This is essential. Almost no one eats food from prepackaged products. And we don't always eat a full serving size. Please fix this.

the app is so easy to use and has a great interface that doesn't look cluttered. they have a wide selection of foods in the database and it's very easy to add your own recipes. I had never found an app in the past that made it easy to track home cooked meals like this. the nutrient tracking is helpful too and i found adding exercise to be easy as well. it's refreshing to see a free app that doesn't bombard you with ads. keep up the great work!!

Switched from MFP and this app is so much better. The barcode scanner alone is extremely helpful! Few things I think would help. 1. If after completing the day, there could be a projection of where you'll be after so long if you stay doing this day with activity, food, water, etc. 2. When selecting foods, you could select multiple at once instead of going in and out of the menu options. 3. If you could add progress pictures in with your weight and then do a comparison after so many months.

New Google Health is difficult to navigate and got rid of some essential functions of the fitbit app. The ability to see a side by side of calorie output/input at various timescales is gone. The calories consumed page that tracks where you fall (below, above, on track) is no longer based on actual output for that day but instead is based on a large window of your average caloric needs (unclear how that is determined). Custom food gone. Nutri. info of food item only visible after logging.
great app!!! easy to use. very intuitive and organized. extremely smart and helpful.

Throughout the ride a pleasant female voice is periodicly saying total distance, split times, total time, etc. Love it. Added note:Two issues : 1 is heart rate logging is only on certain days. I log all the info in Spreadsheet. So I wish that was more consistent. 2 the elevation tracking has to be wrong. It can't be negative where I'm at. I'd rather have heart rate all the time and shut off elevation.

Once you play around with the app a little bit, you'll definitely get the hang of it. I thoroughly enjoy how you can create your own exercises if they're not in the pre-listed workouts already. One thing I would suggest is adding variations of specific workouts (e.g. seated row, cable row). The ability to create your own exercises compensates for it though. I'm also terrible at giving myself rest time so the app ensures you're taking at least 3 minutes of rest between sets. I highly recommend.

I like this app a lot. I've been using it for maybe a year or two? I've always loved it, but three most recent update has created a problem for me. I use a Samsung, not an iPhone (oh the horror...) but have found that the toolbar at the bottom of the screen is now behind my phone's toolbar. It used to adjust up above, but is now smack behind it. I can't get to the toolbar in the app unless I adjust the settings on my phone EVERY. SINGLE. TIME. Annoying. Hoping they fix it soon!
Works Great for tracking what we do. We use it for walking and recording our exploration drives around town and beyond.

The golden age of fitness apps is over. The location accuracy of this app is horrible. It will come up close to 0.25 miles short on a 4 mile run. Most features are MVP ($) only. Like every other app that's been aquired by UnderArmor, the quality has gone down and everything is an upsell.
Randomly started charging without a heads up.
Absolute trash, don't buy a whoop, such garbage. Locking people into a 1 year subscription is super shady too.
Used this app for at least a year now 3 to 4 times per week, weather permitting. It has never failed. Very happy with it.
Not as intuitive or user friendly as I hoped but you get the hang of it at some point. I think the stats it keeps are really neat and helpful.

I LOVE this app. It's been my alarm app for years, always very reliable. Love the customization options, love how postponing my alarm for one day (or making it earlier) is so easy. I'm updating my review now with something even better: their support is amazing. I noticed a minor bug. Sent an email literally saying "you don't have to respond to this email." Got a response within 40 minutes and the bug was fixed within a day! And it's literally the only issue I've ever had. 100% recommend

Want it to sync to my Gear S3 Frontier and S-Health but seems to connect and then doesn't work and is unconnected again. Also tried the Endomondo app on the watch and again doesn't really support training plans so have to take my phone with me to start and manage the plan, would be better if the plan synced to the watch and worked from there with coaching prompts. Good idea and used to work for my biking but not really sure with running, even the other UA apps on Gear don't seem to sync either very well or at all in the case of Record.

I would give this app a Zero. It doesn't count distance accurately compared to my wife's fitbit. The journal hasn't updated in 5 days. The map of my route looks like an abstract drawing. I checked my steps and distance halfway, when I finished my steps increased but the distance was less. Go figure The measurement of my pace is way off. I wish I could find an app that works without intrusive advertising.

I would like to give this app 5 stars because it used to work just fine and I had no complaints. However, it hasn't tracked any distances since June 14th. I've checked app permissions, reinstalled the app, turned on enhanced location, and nothing seems to work. I have 33,000 steps logged but only a distance of less than a mile? That's not at all accurate. Really hoping that Google can fix this.

Used to be a better app. I like to fall asleep to a podcast, and now the app will record that sound in its entirety as "sleep noise". Also the sleep tracking screen doesn't come up when I unlock my phone in the morning, so I forget it's even running. I wish this formerly excellent app would become excellent again. I even sprung for the paid version, it used to be so good. Would not pay for the app in its current state.

ad increase after a week of usage nice tactic to buy premium, i wouldve bought it if the ad spam wasnt as bad. Im never buying premium now just to spite you. good app though. edit reply: tbh the app is awesome, one of the best trackers however it was the lulling/false security of feeling like it was ad-free that initially got me heated. I actually wouldn't mind getting spammed with ads from the beginning, i feel like I would've bought premium then.

New UI is bad, not that the old UI was good, but the new one is genuinely terrible. I can no longer swipe between days, I need to scroll back to the top and tap the exact day I want or use a dropdown menu and pick a day from the calendar if I want to quickly see or edit what I ate yesterday or the day before that. The new UI also forces you to constantly need to scroll back to the top to see how many calories you have left, because of the new rounded look combined with the multiple ad spots. BOO

I found it difficult to use. I want a brief "calm down" practice, not a 30 minute story, but couldn't figure out how to get that. But most significantly, if I am paying for a subscription, there should be no ads or pop-ups. (If there are ads, the app should be free.) I refuse to both pay a high subscription cost for a phone app AND get inundated with ads. Will be canceling the free trial (hopefully it will really cancel 🤞) and will uninstall.

This was good until today when I wanted to start a session and the app puts me at a different location than where I am. I checked my regular map app and it has my location right. Worse, I can't find a help choice in the app. I have no choice but to uninstall it and probably loose all of my data. I don't have Premium and, given this problem, won't ever buy it. This is a basic function.

Have used this app for years but in the last few months it is pretty useless. I can't rely on it to track my walk without it stopping mid way through. If I take a photograph or receive a message it stops but sometimes it stops anyway. Very annoying. Have always relied on it to track my walks.

Unusably buggy! Plan almost never loads at start and takes a random amount of time to finally realize there is a plan. Tracking is on and off. Some workout only gets recorded as a fraction of the actual length. I've used both the iOS and the Android version. Both are buggy in different ways. The Android version is worse. Tried Oct 16 update. Same problem persisted.

This app has came a long way in accuracy. Even when cellular and data connections is off all you need is the GPS turned on to use the app. Along with that I love the coaching feature that helps me train for marathons. The training has improved my pace and my distance :-) However, I do like how other apps allows for more of a social platform and finding/interacting with other runners.

My previous review was 4 star, but I downgraded it because of recent changes. It used to be so convenient to open app, log meal, close app. Now you have to filter through multiple screens to get to where you can log meals... and I'm a Premium Member! Sorry Yazio, but I pay for convenience, and this app no longer qualifies. So long, and thanks for all the fish. I'm out of here.

[UPDATE July 29,2025] It's rare to find an app that's well maintained 7 years later, but here we are! Great app, great support, great features! Less expensive than the cheapest Walmart alarm clock. [Dec 21, 2018] This is the perfect sleep tracking and alarm app for light and heavy sleepers. Great features, well maintained app. I often fall back asleep, so the alarm challenges and awake check are perfect for me. (It's a notification 5 min after alarm, dismiss or get another round of alarm)
Dailyburn killed off their API so they could release this? Libra is one of those apps that got cut off from this, and the best weighin app. Not cool.

Overall great: it's a shame that they don't read the info from Google's "health connect". Clearly the company doesn't want smart scales or watches from other companies to be used - which simply limits the user. I will no longer be able to use Garmin, since I have a smart watch from another brand now, and wanted to use it together with my Garmin scale. They are punishing you for not choosing exclusively Garmin products. I will just switch ecosystems.
Very easy to navigate and book or cancel classes.

Previously this was a 5 star app. Lately however it's become very frustrating, specifically with programs and tracking. I'll do a workout in a set program, enter difficulty and where I did the workout and everything seems great. Next day when I login the app doesn't recognize I did the workout. This forces me to play the workout again, wait for it to finish, and then move into the next workout/stage in the program. Pretty frustrating and reading other reviews - common issue.

I have the same problem with some users here. It was working fine till Feb, and then i can never login via the app. I can easily do it on a browser though, which means there's no issue with my email/password. I have highlighted this to Vitacost and was informed a team will look into it since last wk, but i still hv not heard back. Note: i hv uninstalled, reinstalled, cleared data & cache etc to no avail.

Absolutely riddled with glitches that would be embarrassing from a C list app, let alone Nike. Logs me out after every use. Guided runs are never synchronized to the time of the run due to buffering (says you have seven minutes to go three minutes from end of run, etcétera). Does not work with Spotify at all despite claiming cross compatibility. Fixes don't work, apps pause each other. When it works it's great. Good luck making that happen.

Ruined a perfectly good app. A very important part of my fitness journey has been tracking nutrition. Before the update, you could enter customized food/nutrition information. As someone who does not consume a lot of products that come from packages and rarely uses the barcode feature, this has rendered the app nearly useless to me. I have used this app religiously for 6 year. Looks like I will be going to an old fashioned paper and pen food journal.

I love this app, I can track my fitness and it suggests new workouts that follow what I've been doing. It makes working out easy and helps keep me focused on my over all fitness goals. Update, it has changed its workout formats so I can't get rid of the guidance which can be frustrating when I'm trying to listen to my music while working out :c even when I've selected that option it carries on, it also doesn't list the specifics of each workout any more so it's a bit of a guessing game !

I hate that it doesn't give you an extra calorie allowance when you exercise. If I'm doing 1-2 hours of exercise in a day, the number of calories I was given before exercise just isn't going to be enough, and it's really frustrating and demoralizing to be told I'm eating too much when I know I'm not. Even giving me back half of the calories I burned during exercise would be enough Other than that, the interface is fine. I'm still gonna shop around for something else because of this issue, though

Since Vitacost has been bought by iHerb, there's no more vitacist brands or simple truth brand items. it appears they will no longer offer sitewide promotions like they used to. not sure why anyone would buy from Vitacost now because these prices are ridiculous.

For the same trail it is giving me different distances. I won't talk about elevation, because of course they can't have mapped the whole mountains, so elevation is way off and also changing with each ride. But at least distance I thought I could trust, but it is different few km's on the aame trail. Caloriws burned have to be off too. So it is just noce to see numbers but can't take it seriously...

Update 230119: I've been using this app continuously on a variety of bikes and rides. Works well for me with minimal intrusiveness. Auto pause works pretty well, and battery life on my phone is good. Update 181029. Using this for a while now, with Strava. Neither app meets 100% of what I want, so I use them together, hence 4 stars instead of 5. (Earlier) Worked well on my first ride. Didn't seem too bad on the battery, but I didn't leave the display on continuously.

This app is a mystery to me. I can't imagine you would purposely design it so poorly. So then I wonder if the poor studios you sell it to have that little choice. It is the backend of a booking/paying system where (I think) studios can put their own "front" on it so it looks individual. Nothing but trouble. Impossible to go between studios. I just paid online via the mindbody interface and I can't even find the business on the app let alone my purchase.

Used to love it . . . but lately it has not recorded my walk correctly. I will return from walking two or more miles, but it shows me only walking the first mile. It doesn't do this every time, and I can't figure out how to fix the issue. Other than that frustration, I do enjoy using this app. I would give it five stars if it actually tracked me for my entire walk consistently.

Been using Map My Fitness for a while and, I must say, changes which came about a few months ago have been great! Able to keep track of my walks and changes in speed. Makes sharing very easy with screenshots. With that, I would say that a next step would be to make sharing on social media easier and dynamic. Have the options on what points to share would be really cool.

Amazing App, The best one I've found out there. Only thing that never works for is when in diary & click today to check report, if always takes you to this week you got to keep going back to diary & click today then it eventually comes up with today's report.
best app to buy original protien guys
Can't login on this app. Pointless
This is the best diet tracker I have used, it syncs nicely with the website. Interface isn't as nice as some others.

I always love, love Vitacost! Their prices are always fair and everything that I need, I always find. I also find that their App is very simple and easy to use. Never have problems with it. Excellent company!!!

Once one of the best fitness trackers, I feel like it's lagging behind some of the others. I've been a premium subscriber for 3+ years and the extra features offered are very limited. Stuff like group challenges (not public), slightly more customizable training plans. It still is a good app but with the current development pace not worth the premium. Update, 2 years after this review nothing has changed. No support for additional hardware, no new features. Development seems to be dead.

Update: This issue is fixed. I love this app more than any I have tried before. It's not a calorie tracker. It is a health tracker. I love the way they show the vitamins, minerals, amino acids, etc. I can see if I am getting my immune health support daily and correct by adding food or supplements. Also, connected my watch, Withings scale, and BP. I can see it all in one place: my sleep, activity, weight, food, BP, etc. It can't seem to log me in. It was great. Now I can't use it.

Nice app, the look and feel is a bit dated, but it does the job. I am actually amazed at the amount of food I can eat to make up my daily allowance! Most days I don't eat all the calories - it is just too much food.. shows that healthier choices do not equal starving yourself 😀

Don't install the new version ( version 5+). Garmin has dumbed down the app to suit amaturers. If you are a serious user you won't like it. If you record an activity where there is no network connection you cannot see the activity on your phone untill a network connection exists. The weight charts are not as good as they used to be. The one year chart used to group data by months (now uses week). The one month chart used to group data by week (now uses day). can't delete errone

AI Camera requires MONEY. Not worth it. Edit: Thank you for your reply. I have since found 3 apps that has AI Calorie Camera feature. Therefore, I won't be needing your app. Thank you for understanding.

This app has serious issues with the battery. I run a competing product with very similar features enabled and the competitor's app burns through less battery. In a one-hour ride over the same course, the competing app uses 30-35% of the battery. This app, however, uses almost twice that amount (65-70% of the battery). One thing that might help would be a "dark mode" to reduce battery consumption. I would love to run this app to link with MyFitnessPal, but the battery thing is a deal-breaker.

Lately the devs have been on fire, the app gets better and better. They recently added support for Android Wear and together with the LG G R it works marvelously. Low battery use, good tracking, no bt connection loss etc. Wonderful! edit 2026: still using it after more than a decade with sleep logs for all that time. It's great!
I love daily burn, and really wish this app worked so I could pair the two.  I can't get past the log in page,  I know it's correct but like all the other reviews say it still won't let me in.

Great app. I wish there was a batter way to connect your GCM and a few more features on the free version or lower the cost on the paid version. More people need access to apps like this. I wish the recently used list/favorites etc was alphabetical. It sucks searching. other than that, I love this app.

The last major update makes the app so much less functional - and many of the new workouts are just ridiculous, with niche exercises no one has ever heard of. You can't preview any of the old workouts like you used to be able to, so I couldn't avoid, say, plyo drills that I can't do (I'm a runner so my knees and hips take enough of a beating as is). So many other complaints, but I'm running out of characters. Nike really dropped the ball on this one. So disappointing.

So far, so good... Literally just downloaded and got set up... I needed an app that allows me to log food calories taken in AND workout calories burned, without having to PAY for this feature... Why would any fitness tracker app not offer both options in a free version, since they are equally important when tracking health?! Anyway, this app allows for this, and I am thrilled! Thank you for letting us track food AND exercise in one app...

This app was recommended to me and I absolutely love it! My favorite part is that the alarm volume gradually increases as opposed to regular alarms that yell at you and freak you out in the morning. If you like a constant volume alarm, I'm sure this option is available within this app too - it's pretty easy and straightforward to navigate.

They changed everything i liked about the app. Made exercises into classes instead of time/rep based checklists. Turned all program into class format (just a video for you to follow along). And that's the only option. No way to roll back to lists. The new format is very annoying with instructor talking all the time and no way to know the next and previous exercise. Very disappinted after 6 years of use, I'm ditching it. I hope nike rolls this back.

Great app for beginning or even intermediate weight lifters. Sticks to the basic but very functional and full body movements with a heavy barbell. I like that you can add additional exercises. Begins easy but not for the faint of heart once the progressive overload gets up there. Be careful, take your time, and ONLY progress when you have proper/good form. It's ok to have form issues while you go up as no set will be "perfect". But if you really break form don't go up or you risk injury.

I would give this 0 stars if I could. Google has ruined this app. They rearranged everything, and the look and feel are totally different. And the look is RIDICULOUS. They went with that stupid bubbly style, but stripped out the color so it is somehow both cartoony and boring to look at. It is also suddenly more complicated to use. I absolutely HATE IT and when this fitbit breaks I am going to look at other options. I have been using fitbits for a decade, but it may be time to move on.

App seems fine, but from my experience 95% of the content is locked behind their paid subscription, which was I was asked to use a "free trial" not once, but twice, and that was even before I had even got to the main menu. You're only given a very select few "options", heck even the "personalized recommendations" tab was mostly filled with locked content. Perhaps the paid version is better, but I would like to see the free version improved first.

It's a decent app, but I lost interest when they removed the "Plans" and replaced them with the "Programs," which are fine, but they're not for me. I use it mostly for yoga and stretching now. I really think they should bring back the "Plans" how it was years ago and not something overhauled. Sometimes, you don't need to fix something that's already good.

I initially bought my fitbit specifically to track my heartrate for health reasons, so having it only have the option to show my heartrate in a tiny slot now is annoying at best. It also reset my settings (I use the heartrate speed alerts for a reason) when it updated, which I wouldn't have noticed if I didn't go to make this review. tldr: Unnecessary rebranding and less user friendly interface make me less likely to buy any fitbit products going forward.

Third class app. Reduces km walked by end of the day by half. Never accurate. Forget accurate, it is nowhere close to mediocre. When it has to fetch old data, it crashes, restarts, dies again, restart, dies again. This is the wonder that Google makes. It never count the steps when I am walking and a phone call is also going on. Useless app.

App is pretty good overall still but I do not like it's syncing with Google fit. It's on even one of the secure sync apps, which is disappointing as there are fare more generic ones that off that. Im not a fast runner and will stop to walk at times but the run app gets logged as a walk on Google fit.. annoying. That said I do like the variety of runs you can do, though it can be hard to navigate at times. Why can't it sync music with Spotify? Would like an obvious area for interval running.

So much better than the others. Everything works as expected. it provides all the right data, charts and history. After one month of use it is clear that Garmin is not a toy and not a bling watch. Garmin is serious about providing great data in a great interface both on the watch and on your phone.

Fat secret is an easy way to track calories and keep track of exercise and nutrition. It also tracks the macros that you eat and helps you balance your food. The badges earned for weighing in and tracking food are nice rewards to show how far you have come. The app doesn't feel judgy and doesn't send annoying reminders all day. Noom became insufferable and the recent update took away exercise tracking.

Fantastic app with lots of insight. BUT... The first few days you use it it will be good with only a small banner ad that is not too intrusive. But after a few days you will encounter FULL SCREEN ADS that completely pause your experience for a few seconds about 2-3 times a day. This is incredibly frustrating and the biggest thing holding the app back. A subscription purchase will remove these apparently. if you can look past the ads then it's a good option.

You can't upload a gpx from phone to watch without an internet connection. This is a major limitation - I've been on multiple runs out in the wilderness where I had the gpx on my phone, yet could not get it onto the watch as the process doesn't work without internet connectivity (which should NOT be a requirement).

Good free workout app. I haven't tried (and don't plan to try) the extra-cost features, since the free version gets the job done. I notice that the app shows 1 to 2% shorter distances than my bike computer, probably just not logging quite as frequently. The only really bad shortfall came when my phone ran low on battery halfway through the ride. In battery-saving mode it seems to have woken up every 5 or 10 minutes, and the saved route showed a LOT fewer turns than reality. Otherwise works fine.

works well. I can track my progress. I wish that if I update a strength workout it would sync up. maybe I am doing something wrong . seems like I need to delete workout on my watch and then add updated workout .

Starting about 2 days ago. The app will crash anytime I open it. I get a system error saying it is having issues running. Do you want to kill it or wait. I tried restarting my phone, stopping and starting the application. Up until then it has been a great app. Has there been an update pushed that I can roll back to a previous version?
fantastic program! i have used it for over 5 years now. If you sleep through alarms, not anymore with the captcha function.
I've really enjoyed using this app! I haven't used it in a few months, but coming back expecting it to be better!! I have High Hopes!!

Very glitchy app. About every fifth run it will lose it or freeze in the middle. When you manually correct, it won't count. And there is no option to report a bug or an error. Veryuch need a fix for the app. Edit: Two years later and it is worse. They still haven't fixed the old errors and now there are new. It will just randomly pause your run for no reason.

love this app, simple but can do what I need. one issue though, when I'm pushing baby in a stroller and phone is inside the stroller, it registers the activity as cycling. that would be an extremely slow bike ride! then when I'm changing it to walking, all the stats stay the same, calories and heart points. That is surely wrong.

guys, stop improving ui. the recent one is inconvenient. it is aweful. i don't care about steaks. i can go on holiday, i can be ill. i don't need this reinder. fonts you changed some time ago are aweful too. i have been with the app for many years, and i dont like what you did with it recently. if I could go to the older version , I would do

This is an amazing app and I've been using it for the past 6 years. You get to choose from a wide range of workouts from quick 10 mins to a whole 45 mins session. You can do HIIT, Yoga, workouts with minimal equipment as well as full fledged gym workouts. The user interface is really good and it is very easy to browse through the app. It tracks progress and badges you earn at certain milestones are a really motivating. All of this for free. One couldn't ask for more. I highly recommend the app.

Google Fit burned through 8.5 GB of mobile data entirely in the background on my Pixel. I barely even opened the app — foreground usage was only a few hundred KB. Something in this app is clearly stuck in a sync loop or runaway background process. It consumed almost my entire monthly data allotment without warning. This is unacceptable behavior for a fitness app. Background data is now disabled and I’m uninstalling it. Google needs to explain what the app was doing and fix this immediately.

I want the FitBit app back. This one told me it took me less than 10 minutes to fall asleep when I tossed and turned for 2 hours and even got up at one point. Fitbit app would be able to tell when my workouts end and this waits nearly an hour longer to notice. Since the forced switch, my steps increased, workouts increased, and sleep improved despite NOTHING actually changing. The data is so unreliable, I will be looking for a new option. This is practically useless.

This app has a few features I need that some others don't, but that advantage is ruined by its poor useability and reliability. Inefficient UI. Lots of garbage in the database. I have to restart the app several times a day when it starts getting slow to respond. The water intake log often forgets entries after initially appearing to accept them. Lately it's been forgetting food entries, too, or placing them on the wrong date. Stops showing my food history in search results. The list goes on.

Ai tracking is ok. Im currently using foodvisor which has a better ai tracking and has a quick add feature which is very handy. i paid 24 dollars for 1 year subscription for this app and i wish it was cheaper 😅 . oh well lesson learned.
I had no problems until the recent update. Now I can't load anything, see my passes or my studios.

It's okay, I guess. It works well enough, but it feels more like a running app with cycling tacked on as an afterthought than an app built for cyclists. I had hoped for more from this since Under Armour acquired and shut down Endomondo, but I don't see any actual improvement over that. Not sure there's really any reason for a standalone ride app except to pull in cyclists as a target demographic to serve more UA ads.

The fitbit app worked ok, then when it was hijacked by Google, it didn't work quite as well, but at least it worked. This new app is terrible. Impossible to track anything useful, doesn't sync with the phone most of the time, the most useful features of the fitbit app are gone. Totally useless now. They should re-release the old app so we have something to use until they can straighten out this mess.

New update completely ruined the food tracking. Fitbit was doing great at calculating; if a serving size was one cup but I only ate half that, I could enter .5 and get an accurate calculation. It no longer allows decimals. Navigating the calorie tracking is also more complicated. You're telling me you crammed a bunch of deeply unwelcome AI garbage I didn't want and didn't ask for into an app which was working just fine, but made it WORSE at math? FOH.

Informative, practical, useful, easy to use. The guided runs are perfect for me. Downside is that training events seem to be completed only when GPS data is recorded. The app wont record any of my training events as completed when running on a treadmill, even if the training is time based. As a result, I have some training events not completed on my profile. Please add the ability to edit a training event and/or select treadmill running. 3 stars because of this

The free version of the app is very basic, so I upgraded fairly quickly to the paid version, which is pretty inexpensive. I like all of the things I can customize. I can set multiple alarms, do sleep tracking with or without am alarm, and change the alarm sound. I also like being able to use my own music to fall asleep, though it can impact the sleep tracking a bit. I wish I could sync the data in this app with my other health apps more easily, though i know that can be a programming challenge. Overall, I'm pretty pleased with this app, and it is pretty helpful for what I'm trying to do.
The free version is exactly what I need to keep track of my miles and MPH! I also like that I can connect it to Google fit. I do not use the social part of the app.

Great app, but can we please have all the details that we have on the zwift app as well, like the badges, challenges, and workout plans that's available. Also all the profile information that one can see on the zwift app, like FTP, Racing scores, what's your progress on challenges , badges, compeleted routes, etc etc, all the gear you have in your garage and what's available in the drop shop. Basically all stats, progresses and everything thats available

I started a running plan and I loved the guided run. BUT THEN I looked at my pace and realized it was way off. Instead of the 7min/km pace that I actually ran (tracked with a GPS watch) nike run app said it was a 5MIN PACE. I should be training for the OLYMPICS right now if that's my pace w/o any training!!! Could be motivating to think I'm actually that fast. Sadly I know I'm not there (yet). 🤣🤣 Love the features, but it does n'importe quoi with my GPS sadly.

It's a good app don't get me wrong, but there's too many locked features. If you really want to experience the app, you practically have to pay an arm and a leg for it. When I first found this app, used to be able to see the scenes completely in now there are two bars across the top page four features that are locked. Sure I can get a 7-Day trial, but then it's $60 a year after that. That's a little pricey. after the last few years of everything being so restricted, I am finally uninstalling this app!

App works very well, with current features. I would have given it 5 stars if the meet-up feature allowed me to set-up/share a group workout with people from my Tri club, who already use Zwift, so we can sweat together as a club.

Super frustrated right now. This app is the opposite of easy and straightforward. Finding pricing packages is not surfaced at the top. But my biggest bone to pick right now is that I am trying to pay for a fitness class (a 5 day pass) and the god dam checkout says the cark checkout has failed. My card is not the problem as I used it online yesterday. I dont have another card!!!!!! This is so frustrating.

I took the Training for the marathon and followed it as strictly as I could. After 18 weeks I ran my first marathon in 4:30 hours. I can only share my great experience and confirm that the training plans in the app actually work if you follow them accordingly. So whoever is thinking of running a half marathon or a marathon for the first time, use this app, you wont need anything else.

This app is amazingly free! I can get tailored workouts or select individual workouts to create a workout. The workouts vary in length: 7 minutes to 1 hour. They vary intensity and equipment. They have workouts without equipment and workouts that can utilize an entire gym! I have been using this app for about 7 years now, and there isn't anything else out there that can guarantee results with their trainers and celebrity trainers. They even collaborate with athletes on some of the workouts!

I really like the app, but requires an Internet connection to do anything. I should at least be able to log items offline and sync later. You could at least allow saving of notes when offline. Then I could write down what I had for a meal and look it up later.

Gave 5 stars 2 years ago and would give 6 now if I could with all the updates and improvements to the app. Well worth the yearly subscription fee as it comes out to less than 1 fancy Starbucks drink a month for motivating its users to accomplish steady strengths gains. Or you can be a cheapo and use the free version (which is still great). Highly recommend this app!!!

Works good enough on a smart phone, but would really be better on a tablet if I owned one. The messaging function needs to have a font size change feature, I can't be using my reading glasses while I am riding my smart trainer!

I've used MyFitnessPal for years and so far this app is far superior. Barcode scanning works without a subscription. Integrates with Health Connect. Far superior web browser experience. I wish I had switched sooner.

I have been using this app for almost 10 years and loved it until 6/14 when an update went through overnight. When my alarm went off, the sleeping sheep updated and was impossible. The only way I could turn the alarm off was to uninstall the app. I lost all of that history since I couldn't back it up, and discovered any backup I did have would require upgrading $$ to import. Very bummed user. I had to drop you from a 5 to a 4 star for that.

Overall a decent app, but there are a few annoying bugs. The main issue is that when I delete a food from my diary, sometimes it'll magically reappear a while later. When I add a food to my diary, sometimes it'll randomly add the food twice. The "add a recipe" tool is also broken. It won't let me manually input ingredients. I tried uninstalling and reinstalling the app, but that didn't help. Other than these issues, the app is a decent way to track your eating.

Pros: Fun to use, I like the user interface for the most part. It's great for cardio workout tracking. It auto detects activities. I like the daily tips for what to do during the day. I've been testing it with the oura ring and the data more or less matches. I'm a fan of the strap itself. Cons: Not helpful for strength activities like Pilates and yoga

This is by far my favorite food app. it's easy to use, precise, and full of options such as entering amounts in both grams or ounces, including fractions of amounts. I've never seen an app show all nutrient consumption of vitamins and minerals so clearly. It's also easy to import other daily app info such as sleep or exercise. I could go on and on about what I like and so far have found no cons. For me, hands-down, this is the best app I've ever used.
Plzz does not use this app they are not give all the information about duty charges and all which customer has to be suffer for there money

Yesterday I could see all my logged food without having to expand each meal & easily see how I was doing against my goals for my chosen macros. Today that has vanished; all I see is how many grams total and the percentage split of my macros, without doing the maths manually (which kinda defeats the object) I can't now see how many more grams of protein (for example) I need to hit my goals. Looks like a downgrade rather than a useful update, I'm on a free premium+ trial and now unlikely to pay

Love this app! I can easily save all of the web recipes that I find and can easily put them on my menu and my phone doesn't lock while I'm cooking :-) I have only been using the free part and it is awesome! Update: the part that takes you to the website instead of saving it is FIXED! You can now save recipes again. The meal plan part is awesome too. Seriously....this app will make your life MUCH easier! Try it!

Love this app! When I think of something I need to get, I go to the app and save it in the cart. If I decide to order from the website, it sync up nicely. When I check out from the app, it's super easy and quick.
The apps search function is absolutely useless!!! I've been a Vitacost customer for years, but I'm tired of fighting so hard to do business with them via a non-functioning app...

Why does it change my tracked activity run distance after 10+- minutes of saving? I finish my run and it correctly shows the map and distance (6.29 miles) but later the same map shows incorrect mile markers with a total distance of 7.09 miles. There's no logical reason that your app makes this erroneous change. It happens every time.

The app has become extremely unreliable since the last couple updates. Most of the time now it doesn't start tracking and logging movement. On the rare occasion that it does start tracking it suddenly stops at some point during the cycle. I have experience d this with two different Android phones I have, so it is NOT a hardware problem. It is an app software problem that needs to be fixed.

been using this app off and on for like 8 yrs. has alot of ads but i use the free version so no real complaints there. it was down today between breakfast and lunch and i almost just skipped lunch because when i bulk or trim i track everything. i checked an hr later and it was fixed. thanks crono team i was scared to look at other apps because I'm so locked into this one. its great.

this app replaced my fitbit app a few weeks ago. I've been using it for almost 2 weeks. I love it for tracking my progress and lots of motivation. There is one issue that I can't seem to resolve. the coach part of the app keeps bringing up a topic that I added in the health screening on the first day. I ask everyday for it to not bring up that topic again, yet everyday it gets brought up again. the AI coach keeps promising to not bring it up again, bet it still happens every day.
I have now uninstalled the app. Because it keeps launching itself when I turn on my phone in the morning. If it can't be fixed it's not easy to order items from so I may go elsewhere. Fix it ASAP!

The app works fine, but: - doing a re-scheduled training resulted on the wrong calendar data of the calendar section (correct in the news feed). Because the day is past, it's not possible to correct it. Also it is making me skip a training because of this (Garmin coach) - when rescheduling a training a warning message pops up to tell 3 trainings in a row are discouraged, even if there are no trainings the day before/after (Garmin coach) - HR zones visualisation in past activities is terrible

Doesn't give me option to permanently delete account. Constantly repeating :" check your connection" ! Thanks but one more privacy intrusive app constantly pushing for premium version purchase. I'm sick of this. Uninstalled immediately, but my details with account will stay locked on someone's cloud to share without my permission!!

a good, simple app which I find is the first one that actually encourages me to work out regularly. one small suggestion - the app won't allow you to add progression to the Plank with weight, only time. I prefer to keep the time the same but add weight.

Lists products as being "In Stock" but throws an error because the app lies about the product's status. A shameful presentation of a company's products and brand. Don't bother with a pithy response like an empty apology. Fix the app. That's apology enough. But since the app has not been updated since 2011, that is probably expecting too much.

I absolutely hate the rebranding as Google Health. The Fitbit app was far superior. The reorganization is bizarre and it is so annoying that I immediately began seeking a better tracker app/non-google/Fitbit device for the future. If you are going to make changes, at least have them make sense. If you needed to change the name, you should have just left it at that. You managed to take an intuitive app and render it so annoying it's essentially useless. Congrats, that's hard to do!

New annual subscriber. I really enjoy the app but with so much content available I wish there was a better way to search or sort the options. For example, if I select "meditate" or "music" then "all", I'd like to be able to sort the results by length, title, narrator, etc. Would give 4 or 5 stars otherwise.

Just getting started, but already disappointed that we can't create multiple "Grocery Lists" or scan the UPC of items into the list! I currently use a different app for this and love the ability to create a separate list for each store I typically shop at....

Updating to 4.5/5 as I have continued to use the app for many years now, and appreciate we get new workouts. One feature request is to provide an overview of the exercises (it can be in list format), since the video preview does not really showcase all the exercises. This is one feature I really miss from a previous version before all older workouts were turned into "class/video" format, as it allowed me to get a sense of the workout and if it is appropriate for where I am at (location/injuries)

I love the cycling simulation but the running feature is a love-hate relationship... In running, there are Issues on Pod connection, speed variances and limitation of support devices. I have to buy the run pod in a different country just to get one and use it. Only to find poor connectivity between devices and the pod.

Pretty neat app but it's just not for me. The anti-snore alerts work, but are annoying to me and my wife. The smart wake up system is an interesting novelty, but it really doesn't seem to help for me. Maybe it will for you though. The premium add ons such as lullabies and ringtones are not of interest to me so I cannot justify paying for the app. All I want is a simple alarm that gradually gets louder as it rings.

It is terrible at automatically knowing when I'm going to sleep and when I'm getting up. I have been unable to figure out how to edit what time I wake up. It's a small thing but it annoys me that I cannot just correct the time I got out of bed.
This app WAS amazing but since the recent update it doesn't import all of the ingredients from recipes in planner into the Grocery List which was the main reason I use this; to save time. :-(

I recently switched from paid version of MyFitnessPal to paid version of Cronometer. I miss the ability (on Android) to log food by voice, but otherwise I'm thrilled with the app's detail, accuracy and speed. Thanks to Cronometer's detailed nutrient analysis I found a serious dietary deficiency. The bar code reader is insanely fast. Recipe imports are far more accurate than FitnessPal.

Absolute garbage. Everything worked how it was supposed to the first time then after that nothing worked. The app that work on my phone nor did it work on my watch properly. I'm using it on a Galaxy s20 Ultra and I have it connected to a Samsung active 2 LTE version as well. Just stick with Samsung Health so you don't waste your time trying to get this garbage to work.. it will a hundred percent guarantee ruin your workout experience.

edit since getting New Google Pixel 10 I've had nothing but issues with the sound on the app. sounds like the audio is stuttering which makes it hard to meditate. have uninstalled and reinstalled with no luck. disappointed ☹️ Love all the different types of meditations. It's nice to have a variety of choices. I can do whatever I'm feeling at the time and it really helps me with my anxiety
A little annoying but it helps with those who snooze their alarms. Awesome alarm been using it for about 6 years now

Current Review Until They Fix It: I hate that I have to log into it every time! I have used this app consistently for over a year and they made it so inconvenient I'm going to have to leave. Initial Review: This app saves my sanity. It's an easy place where I store my meals. No more wondering what's for dinner.
App doesn't track or do anything on either Google pixel watch 2 or s26 ultra. Doesn't synchronize with other health apps even though it's supposed to. Went back to the Fitbit app.

overall app is an interesting way to keep track of various biomarkers. heart rate monitor during activities is unacceptable inaccurate (says i am at 140 when I am actually at 100, for example). can't log weightlifting exercises during the activity, so need to remember and log after the fact. does not import android health connect data reliably from Samsung health data and oxilife scale. actual ekg measures are correct and discern heart rhythms accurately. decent beginning, ready for improvement
have used this for several years. there are bugs with updates but they fix quickly. recommend

Consistently has been unable to track my runs correctly. I live in a fairly populated area in the Orlando area, yet still, after the first mile, it stops tracking distance, and it stops tracking my route, so even if it does start tracking again (which doesn't happen unless you run another 3-4 miles), it doesn't show the route I took. Essentially it only works as a timer with extra steps. Disappointing because I really enjoyed this app when I first got it. Can't recommend thisto friends anymore.

I have to hand it to these guys - I recently had a scare where it seemed my recipes had almost entirely disappeared, which was quite upsetting because I had saved some of my grandmother's and other family recipes in the app and I didn't have them backed up anywhere. I heard from the Prepear team within the hour offering support to get my recipes back. Sure enough, I was able to retrieve all my recipes and while I had planned to not trust the app again their response changed my mind. Well done!

There is no integration with the DailyBurn workout programs, no integration with monitoring programs, such as HRM or activity trackers, our at the least have their own.  Have to pay for features that are free on far superior apps.  Definitely behind other players like MyFitnessPal.

Love this app! Perfect for counting calories. It has popular resturant nutrition facts and it has everything I need. It also keeps track of fiber, fat and anything else you want to monitor. Also, there's a supportive community. I've had an account since before smart phones and apps were a thing. It used to just be a website. They kept up with the times.

I like the simple no nonsense interface that collates data from multiple apps and sensors without unnecessary bloat, gamification or ads. However, it's rather spoilt by random data loss and inconsistent syncing. It's annoying to watch your hard-earned heart points disappear in front of your eyes. Could be a great tool, but it feels like Google can't be bothered to sort out the long-standing bugs.

The app is okay. Gives you workouts to do. I like the videos to see what the exercises are if you're unfamiliar. The frustration is in trying to select the workout you want. There is a nice long list of programs to look at. You can edit those programs or delete them, but adding them to your workout schedule is a pain. I can't figure out how to simply select the one I want and add it to workouts. I am not the most tech-savvy person, however, I have clicked all the options. No add feature. lame.

The data this shows you is good enough, although pretty basic. You can enter your food macros but it doesn't do anything with it and that's a bit of a theme, you can add loads of data but most of it is never used. There is a big fault with the app though, nothing works of you don't have a signal. You can't even cancel your alarm of you don't have internet connection let alone record workouts.

The new Google Health UX is just comically bad. Two thirds of the main screen is blank (presumably reserved for future AI enslopification), despite the tiles at the top overflowing horizontally. The tiles themselves cannot be reordered or moved, leaving blank slots that you can only clear by removing and re-adding every. single. tile. one. by. one. I could go on (and on) but I only have 500 characters. Boy am I glad I wasn't paying for FitBit Premium (and there's no chance I will, now.)

if you're planning on getting the whoop just bare in mind the service team takes 1-2 days to get back to you, and within the first year in itself you app will start glitching, I am two months short of my first year and the app is glitching with no way to fix it whatsoever.
Crashed my phone instantly. Uninstalling!

Was looking to get a new fitbit sometime this year, but with the new Google app update I'm not so sure I'll stick with it. I love the metrics fitbit tracks, but the app is now not as user friendly. I also declined the coach option, but the ask coach bubble won't go away. I am not interested in gemini being involved with my fitbit. I miss the old apps layout and easy visibility without having to dig.

the app is "Ok it needs to sync to capture data from the wrist monitor so is not live it enables monitoring over time and the export activities function enables further analysis off line in a spreadsheet for example. Drained the battery really quickly on my Vivoactive 3. now upgraded to active 5 and it says not compatible 🤔

charging $22/month and hav in the companion app working 70% of the time is crazy. Doesn't matter what phone I use. I always update the program and the app before every ride. Either drop the price or fix the app from dropping every 5 seconds.

I've been trying to download this and set up an account multiple times and the app keeps glitching and will not allow me to set up an account. I've used the Map My Fitness apps before and really liked how they worked. It's unfortunate that it hasn't been working as I was looking forward to tracking my cycling workouts.

When it logs your workout, it is extremely accurate. Would be five stars, except about 10% of the time it seems to get stuck on a premium nagscreen before the workout starts (I use a 30s delay), leading to the workout never getting logged. For the price it's hard to complain, but I'd love to see that detail fixed.

I'm not sure why this app no longer works for bringing up the photos of items. I've updated many times, re-installed, unistalled, shut down , restart the app. Even got a new phone. Still doesn't bring up photos. Every once in awhile it will. Since I've had so much problems, I quit ordering and I was a regular customer.

Probably the worst health tracking app available. I think an Excel spreadsheet would be better. It fails at everything it says it's supposed to do. Randomly stops syncing with fitness trackers. They should have kept the web version of fit. At least you could edit your information. Now it's only mobile and the interface is terrible. Plus, there's no information about how they're calculating your points when it does work. Sleep tracking doesn't work at all either. -10 stars! Worst app!!
25-item limit on "my stuff" is too low. No "search" functionality for workouts. List views are poorly implemented and buggy. This app is really frustrating to use for scheduling a workout.
It don't work. Everytime i login, an error occured with popup "Server not working". Please check the app and fix it. We follow this site from past 6 years. Please fix it immediately.

$20 and super buggy. Too bad. I like the interface, ease of use, and simplicity, but the I'm having the same problems as others here, e.g. workouts changing on the middle of my workout from A to B or vice versa, weighs not tracking correctly day to day, and the next workout is always listed as tomorrow. When I wait for the day after the app says i skipped it. In the end, I wish I hadn't wasted $20.

Like many of the other reviewers have said, I purchased the power pack many years ago and have been a faithful user of this app since then. The app was amazing. However now the app requires a mandatory subscription in order to use it. My daughter wanted to start doing this program as well as a new year's resolution and I'm not paying this subscription for myself and I'm sure as hell not paying for it for two people. Very unfortunate. This WAS a great app, it's sad to see the state it's in now.

I've been using this app for 2 weeks now and absolutely love it! Not only are the recipes really easy and delicious, my kids love them too! I think the "Prepear mode" while cooking a recipe, to show on half the screen your ingredients and the other half the instructions, is genius. But I think my favorite part is the shopping feature and how it auto-populates the ingredients into a search. I haven't stepped foot in a grocery store in 2 weeks and it has been wonderful.

App doesn't work that great and some parts of the interface aren't intuitive (such as trying to scroll down, zoom, or pan on a sleep chart selects it to be deleted or something). If the phone in sonar mode gets moved the slightest bit it doesn't detect anything. Accelerometer isn't accurate. Sound recorder records every time I rustle throughout the night and claims it's a snore. Not a great app.

I seem to have nothing but trouble with this app. I used it for a long time without issue, but then I would get caught in a Save Workout loop. Even after selecting to discard the workout it would just take me back to the Save Workout window. The only way out is to uninstall and reinstall. More recently I'm getting to my destination only to find that it stopped tracking about halfway, making it pointless to record the rides. Today I'm back in save workout loop and have once again uninstalled.

New app is trash for tracking calories. You can't make your own entry if the database doesn't have it. Defeats the purpose of me using the app at all. I can handle the other changes but not this one. Edit to respond: I'm not paying for AI to enter my food or take a picture. Just change it back. I already paid a few hundred dollars for my watch. The LEAST your company could do is not screw up how people track calories.

I've been using this app for the longest time. I love its simplicity. However, lately the app hasn't been working properly AT ALL! It doesn't log me in, doesn't record my runs, and I'm afraid I might lose my records because I have a goal for the end of the year, and I'm tracking the miles on my shoes as well. I've tried uninstalling and everything and nothing works!

I had this as 5* but after the latest update, the app is completely unstable. I've reinstalled, cleared cache, changed password, and it just loads then crashes. It's practically unusable when in this loop, but managed to resend the verification email from settings and it is stable again. Pixel 3 in case anyone else has this issue.

Terrible. After reloading, still wouldn't login until it took over my other Under Armour apps that were working just fine. Now none of them track distance. Unfortunately, it is the only Under Armour product that has a link to my Sony watch. That means I can't use any of the apps with my watch. Poor design; worst execution. Disheartening how many people launch unproven products. 1st Review: Couldn't even login. ....
Always says my password is wrong, even after I reinstalled the app, reset my password, and made sure it was correct. I emailed support but they have not been helpful yet.

Used to be a 5 star app, but they updated the app which removed some features from free to paid subscription. Suggestion would be to have some things pay once for and packages for subscriptions. Edit to 1 star, same review applies.

My account is set up for 5 passes a week for my Renov8 account. but mindbody only lets me schedule 3 a week, then they want to charge me for the last 2 sessions. Another problem is I pay for 1 month at a time. that should be 30 days. But since they don't take the money out of my account until the 3rd of the month. I lose 3 days every month when I should be able to register for classes. .

Edit: This app regularly fails to track my sleep and recovery. I'll absolutely make sure I do not renew, and use other tools to track my fitness. (Original review) Whoop would be a 5 star app if every feature worked all the time. For over 200 dollars per year, I don't want to have to monitor the app for the first 10 minutes of every workout to make sure it's not having another glitch and failing to track strain, distance, heart rate, etc. Just buy a Garmin. it's cheaper, and it always works.

I have an app for my scale that tracks weight, an app that tracks exercise, an app that tracks fasting, and an app that tracks my food. All of them do those specific things very well. However, not all of them can be linked together. The ONLY reason I was using Google Fit was so I could share the weight data amongst all of the apps, which it in theory, says it should be doing. Unfortunately, early October the weight stopped updating. Super frustrating.
The step tracker is not always accurate and the app can be a little bare bones. it would be nice to be able to track other things like food or water consumption.
I enjoy this app I like how convenient it is when your trying to find something new to do in your area

Seems comprehensive, if the phases of skeep are indeed accurate - there's little way to know if it is, but it accurately identified me waking up both at 0200 and in the morning. However, running two apps simultaneously to compare, this one doesn't register snoring as well as other apps. (SnoreLab seems the best but expensive). I've just increased detection sensitivity and we'll see if that works better. If it does, this may be the one to keep.

Recent update(s?) have included a lot of bugs in the software where food items that you enter shows zero units and 0 calories. Sometimes items you enter look fine after you enter them, but if you go back a day or two later you'll find that they are corrupted and show zero calories. I've been using this app for well over 10 years and have always liked it, but this bug is a deal breaker for me. I'm holding on to the end of the rope hoping they will fix it quickly, otherwise I will move on.

Have been using this app for the last 5 yrs. Never had a problem. Now for some reason my app crashes. Doesnt record my runs. I have uninstalled/reinstalled the app at least 5 times in the last 2 weeks to get it to work properly. Still not working. Bug needs to be fixed asap before myself and a lot of NRC users delete the app! Very disappointing.

The application itself is pleasant to use. Streaming classes do not stream to the app but instead open a browser link. They require that you use the Chrome browser. Having browser requirements is something you'd expect in 2010 not 2020. Additionally, Chrome is not a privacy friendly browser, unlike Firefox. If they force users to use a less private browser, it makes me question how seriously they take privacy overall.

Love the new app design! But, can you let us press and drag the tiles to move them instead of just using the (-)(+) symbols? Also, can you bump up the widget's refresh rate? It's not updating right, showing some amounts as zero. Also, is there a way to go through questions on the app like the free version MyFitnessPal, to obtain recommended calories, protein etc... instead of having to input the limits ourselves? Thank You! Amazing 👏 work on the app.

This app was previously 5 stars from both myself and my spouse. We even upgraded to the premium subscription. When I opened the app today the format was completely changed and NOT for the better! It is almost completely unusable. So much so that if it isn't changed back we will cancel our subscriptions, delete the app, and search for a new one. Way to go, My Fitness Pal.
I use my Prepear app miltiple times a day and LOVE IT! However, I HATE that I no longer can see what each item is for under the grocery list with the new update!!!!! 😡
Very disappointed, I paid for ai tracking but the camera was not working, i complained about this issue so they r still trying to fix it.
Really poor layout which is not easily configurable. Irritatingly overloaded with meaningless data. I will be looking for a different app that can talk to my Fitbit.
One of the best apps for tracking calories, many recipes and products available, but PLEASE let me synchronize it with mi fitness 🙏🙏

Trash. Not sure why update an ruin a good app when the original version was good enough. Over thinking things usually end up a complete failure and this App is the perfect example of that. Well done 👏 next work on the government they need your help

I've been trying to find my friend's challenge for over a week. I've added her as a friend and can't see the challenge from her profile like she says I should be able to. I can't search for the challenge, it only gives me the option to filter by sport and type. It's a shame because I wanted to join my friend's challenge but I'm going to stick to Strava, it works much better.

What a piece of junk. I installed it, and every time I try to add a card, it says it worked but then the card doesn't show up. So I can't book any appointments. And the ingenious search feature -- I type a name of a business letter for letter, and it shows up like 20th in the search results.

I have been using this app for years. This app helped encourage me to start jogging when I was a beginner. At specific intervals during an activity a music stops and a voice tells you your stats (total time, pace ect.) It can be toggled on and off in settings. There are also some really awesome challenges like "you vs. The year. I highly recommend this app.

Great when it works, but not reliable. Updated to 3 stars from 2. After giving up on using ZC with zwift on a PC, and more using it with a phone, reliability is much better. I think the problem is with zwift on a PC, but that's not my problem!

Promo codes are not getting copied to the cart. There is so no add option when you do a search and the item comes up. You need to double click on it first. Very time consuming. I rather use the website... it is less time consuming.

The app has steadily improved over the last few years. My main complaint is the lack of a landscape mode, or ideally full screen rotation. My tablet for example has the USB port on the bottom/narrow edge. I cannot prop up my tablet while it's charging while using this app. It's not a deal breaker, but definitely an annoyance.

Gender required :( I couldn't finish setting up my account because it required gender. there was no way to skip or opt out. online it said it was due to algorithms but I don't want gender factored into those either. I'm returning the watch I just bought tomorrow because it's useless without putting in a binary gender (even though intersex people exist).
Awesome app! The Map My....apps for biking and running!
I see reviews from April about the app not opening. June update: still doesn't open

I really love tracking my workouts with Edomondo. I'm in a wheelchair most of the time and being able to adjust the length of my stride helps better gage how my workout really impacts me as an athlete. I've tried other apps that aren't geared for an athlete like myself and they've all left me disappointed. THANK YOU Endomondo for helping me get the most out of every workout! 💜 Rae S.

Using the app is unnecessarily time-consuming. Half of the time it lacks the default 100g option when inserting new food items, even though literally every food product in Europe only shows nutritional value per 100g. When I brought it up with customer service then they just told me to calculate it myself. Things like that would be easily fixable.

Easy to use, very intuitive and overall one of the greatest tracking apps! But, I have to admit that lots of features have unnecessarily become for pro users. I can't even plan ahead what I'm going to eat the next day because "it's a pro feature" now. A lot of people have very busy schedules and find the day before the only right time to log their food and keep their mind organized, like me.
Completely inaccurate. Doesn't track steps reliably. Have to constantly uninstall and reinstall to unfreeze it.

I have samsung fold and the app often needs me either to open the fold to resize or does not cooperate and I have to restart app to continue. I have updated the app many times, I do not believe development has created a fix for this in mobile view for either screen. very basic just like a website that is in full desktop browser layout being viewed on a smartphone(not in mobile version) it does not look right or operate to its full capacity.

Great Calorie Counter with a Terrible Name. Every item has the option to be entered in grams or in servings. It is easy to enter new food items. The interface is simple enough. It is easy to create flexible recipes as "meals" which can then be adjusted ingredient by ingredient if you need to. I wanted a calorie counter as good as Spark People. This is it.

Terrible app. I like to keep adding to my list over time and place an order about once a month. But lately it has been repeatedly deleting everything in my shopping list. Very annoying. Repeatedly getting nearly expired food is annoying, too. Time to switch to Thrive Market, I guess.

In theory it's a great app, but unfortunately it's attempt at health moderation is completely stifled by expensive subscriptions, processing errors and a faulty user interface. Although the application does incorporate unique features like "soundscapes" or "meditation" guides --as well as others-- none of these features allow you to even try them unless of course for the minimum subscription price of $69.99! That's right, 70 bucks for some stuff you can do for free anyway. 0 stars if I could :).

The new app is missing the minute by minute sleep reporting that I could use to pinpoint to the minute sleep events. also I can't get information about each hour in my calorie burn diagram. This new app is clunky and not intuitive and I have to spend longer in it than before, just to get the same information. the old app was much more streamlined. I don't see the benefits of this new app, to me anyway.

This app is amazing, and is really helping me stay on track with my work outs. Seriously considering getting the paid version. 2025 Update: I'm really disappointed that the "Lifetime Powerpack" purchase I made has been essentially cancelled for upgrades that I didn't want or ask for. The app was perfect, but here are with me switching to other apps to get the experience that I paid for with Stronglifts and has been cancelled on me.

Hoping custoner support can help, perhaps someone here can. My smartphone recognizes my wahoo cadence sensor and indicates connected. Otherwise, love the price and other features.
I like the meditations a lot. However, I'm noticing that the background nature sounds (rain and birds) are quite loud and drown out the speaker's voice.

The name isn't great. The app itself IS great, though. This is the best food tracker and calorie counter I've ever used. Easy to use, and with a deep "library" of all the popular restaurant chain menu items and common grocery brands, so your daily tracker entries can be really specific and accurate! And they have excellent resources and articles and you can opt to be part of a community to encourage and help each other.
This app will not allow me to purchase items. It'll keep prompting I've reached maximum number of logon attempts without trying to attempt prior.

I really enjoy using this app. It has been a great companion to help me achieve my fitness goals. However there's one feature that just doesn't seem to work. My friends and I compete with each other within a community but the messaging for the community is simply broken. I receive notifications from my friends' messages but I can never see their messages in the chat and any attempt to send a message simply fails.

I've been using the application for a couple of weeks and I like it a lot. it has all the functions that I need. Barcode scans. Custom food creation is a breeze, by taking a picture of the nutritional label, very accurate. Custom meals and recipes, so that logging your detailed consumption is fast and fun. The only issue is that in the free version that I use, ads become very insistent and frequent, which somewhat spoils the experience.

Vitacost send an email that says "App-only access 20% off your order". I click it but it just opens the Play Store to install the app, which is already installed. I don't see the 20% discount when I do open the app.

App doesn't allow feedback! The link goes to an empty page. Tracking numbers don't work, links go to a website that says the tracking number doesn't exist. Every few months the app says my login information isn't recognized and I have to reinstall the app. Frustrating for sure. But it's a good company for the most part, I just wish the app would improve.

For what it is, the app works, not 100% of the time, but maybe 80%. 2hich is enough I guess. I've lost maybe 2 runs due to the app force closing or whatever, which may be the apps fault or my phone's (OnePlus 3t). But besides that, it does what I want it to do, which is mainly track my runs and stats. That's about all I truly expect out of it and it does that well

*Found the rest now, they are in each tab now instead of all in one place. Watch and app work for what I need* Since the change to google health, I am no longer able to find all the mindfulness options. I can't find audios I had saved the way I could before, and there seems to be a lot of the mindfulness sessions just gone. There are about 5 that are available without the subscription, when there used to be about 15 to 20.

Something has changed in there last couple weeks. When I'm walking if I slow down just a little it pauses and says "pause my workout". Even if I don't pause. When I walk on the track at the gym it pauses constantly even if I don't slow down. I did more than a mile on the track but it said .6 miles. I don't like this change at all and will uninstall if it doesn't change back.

Complete rubbish. It gets worse every time you do a new upgrade. Disconnects from the phone and shows nonsense. Often doesn't count the floors or shows high stress level when I am not stressed and low when I feel stressed. The sleep tracking doesn't work well, I woke up and went to the bathroom at 2am and it didn't show that. I was tracking my period with it. I had set the cycle length to 28 days and it shows that I will have the next period in 40 days. What a complete waste of money and time!!

This is a great app. My only issue is that it will drain the phone's battery in about 2 hours if the screen is always on. Is there a "night mode" that would use less energy? (Solution: skip changing from Power Saving mode.) Question: Why does the calorie counter begin dropping as my speed drops? My effort is greater climbing enen though my speed is lower. Conversely, when I'm coasting down a hill, the calorie counter adds calories burned. This is a bug, not a feature.

Very nice app, very organized and a lot of useful features for a free app. Only issue I have is that I can't upload any photos, no profile picture and no recipe photos and I can't figure out why. When clicking on add photo, the file picker opens, I choose the picture, hit "done" and then I'm presented the rotating loading circles for an indefinite amount of time, without anything happening, not even after 10 minutes of waiting. Using Huawei Nova 5T on Android 10 and EMUI 11.
Is NRC keeps force stopping after the latest May 2026 update? I like the basic feature that works awesomely like the previous version

Stay away from this app. Used to be the best tracker for food, good price for PRO as well, now it's the best at annoying you and making it not want to use it anymore because you spend more time closing popups and seeing stuff you don't want to see.
