Worst fitness app it forgets to count sometimes, due to bloody app only I lost my discount from a Insurance company, in 2025 there is a step count mine is almost 15 lakhs , but suddenly it became zero
I enjoy shopping at Vitacost! They have quality merchandise and great prices. I would like to be able to click on items in my list so I can read their descriptions.

This app has turned into complete garbage. It used to be great, and it still tracks your steps. However it now does not track workouts correctly. When you start a workout to track it often just disappers after 5 or 10 minutes. You can then add it manually, however when you add a 2nd workout activity for the day the first one disappers. Virtually worthless for tracking actual workouts. Seems now their focus is just more accurate location based and step data to tie togwther for maps and ads. Sad.
Mindbody makes it super easy to to book and manage my self care schedule. Love it!

I have a love-hate relationship with this app. It's great for tracking running activity & for distance or time based runs. But the long runs won't really add structure to your longer workouts. It's more like listening to a podcast with an athlete--super annoying because they talk over your music.

Frustrating. Connection issues. I had about a year of minimal issues. And the last year I've had to restart my phone a few times every third ride or so until it go to the point where reinstalling the app was the most full proof way to fix the issue. Now I'm having issues every ride. Internet connection is fine and running a tacx2t neo. Same setup for last 2 yrs.

I love this app! Iv only been using it for a couple of days because i got a free trial, but it has really helped me alot. I have trouble falking asleeo and staying asleep and my schedule is always random and so my sleeping schedule is not a schedule at all. But this is helping me feel so rested, refreshed, and i am happier. Great App. There is alot of different meditations, such as types, who is the voice, and more so anyone can find something that works specifically for them.
Great app for recipes and meal planning. Love everything about it.

Always been 5star app but last few weeks constant issues with synchronizing workouts, feed refresh etc. Got so bad I cancelled prem subscription. For time being 2* is fair but once they sort problems out I'll review my rating. Thx. Above review from April, June tomorrow and nothing been fixed. Don't waste your money for premium stuff as you won't get one. 1star is fair.

Perfect, except... I need a way to export the graphs in "Charts > Graphs." I have Non 24 and would love to be able to export this data. (When I sleep on a graph with hours slept per day in a list of days). with Non-24, this pattern looks like a wave. The Graph for this in the app is odd because data goes out of bounds in the graph. This is not as helpful as it could be. I know a lot of Non-24 patients and I would be able to recommend it more if this feature was improved.
very good business app

I really want this app to work and it would be a total 5 star if it didn't constantly stop in the middle of a workout. Then I have to restart the workout and go back to the part where it lagged. This happens multiple times per work out. It's not related to my internet and it happens on every workout. Please fix this for Android and I'll totlaly update to 5 star.

Of all the actually free fitness apps I've tried so far, this one had by far the best guided home workouts. They were varied, intense, and had great video/audio to explain each exercise without an excess of annoying chatter or beeps. Unfortunately they removed the "plans" feature, crippling the app for me. Having a great workout library is only half of what I want from a fitness app. I also want to be held to a schedule, and measure my progress against it. Plans did that, but now they're gone.  So it was good for most of 2018, then they crippled it.  ------------- EDIT: Well, they sort of re-added plans, but now they (and a lot of their workout library) are locked behind "Premium". Sad day. I guess they need to make a buck somehow, but I've already moved on to alternatives and this gives me no reason to come back.

With the increases price they gotta have more guaranteed saving. I am still riding with messages from other players but cannot save. Lost a PR that was huge for me given my situation and weekly streak plus and hour messing around trying to get it to save. It is a clear design failure and no feedback to the user. The signal is saving the results is not a priority for zwift. super upsetting. using this for 4-5 years. I am pretty crushed.

I love the app for browsing. But I have two usability issues. 1. Cannot see previously ordered items. The shopping area loads fine , but when I need to see Information on past orders, I get a blank screen. 2. When adding items to the cart, it very frequently does not update your cart properly when I go to the computer to order. This makes the app really unusable for other than browsing.

Step counter doesn't work. For whatever reason the app doesn't allow you to enter your steps manually. You must sync with separate apps to count steps, but the sync never works. Search Google & you'll find this is a common problem. You must go through a complicated process of re-installing and re-syncing to add your steps, only for it to stop working again the next day. It's completely asinine that you can't just enter your steps in manually. 1 star until that is fixed. Also the UI is lacking.

One of the best apps I've ever used to track my calorie intake. It has all the metrics you'd expect and then some! Huge foods database (all you need to accurately track your food is scan the barcode), complete breakdown of the macro/nutrients, water tracking, syncing with health apps to import biometrics. The UI could use some improvements but that's only secondary. On the plus side, it's only showing limited ads (once or twice a day, I can live with it). Search no further!

The forced app "upgrade" from fitbit is awful. it is clunky and cartoonish. I'm an adult that likes to review information presented as if I'm over the age of 10. Other specific complaints - I can't scroll backward by days. Plus I cannot get fine details by the hour anymore. Is there anything I can do to get the old app format back? This is bad enough to make me abandon Fitbit (or Google fitness tech), even though I've been a loyal user for a decade.

It's great when it works. Since the last update I just get the blank black Connect screen and the app won't open about 50% of the time. I have to restart my phone multiple times a day to get it to work. Really inconvenient when I'm using this to track health things and pace due to chronic illness.

Review Update: This is the 3rd time I have uninstalled and reinstalled this app after it locks up. On the prior review I dropped it to 2 stars. It is starting to feel like 2 stars is too generous. It's very annoying when it randomly fails to save a run after I finish. Sometimes it works for weeks, then it dies. Disappointing.
Can't login always says password is incorrect. But it works on website. Terrible app

Forced from old Fitbit app to this nightmare. Stats are incorrect. Calculates cardio differently. Can't find basic, important data. Sets random, unwanted goals. Tracks sleep differently. It's a bloody mess, and they're now compiling all of your health info into your primary Google account. It's no longer separated by an app "wall". The fitbit version worked well. This is horrible. Had I known this massive change was coming, I wouldn't have bought a fitbit. What a shame.

I've been using WHOOP for over four years. I love the data and information it provides. But if you have tattoos, the device won't work or at least won't work well. Be aware of that. The Maim Reason for such a low rating is the absolute decline of customer service. They lean too heavily on AI and make it a pain to submit any help request. It takes on average 2-3 days for a response. If it keeps up, I will be terminating my subscription.

I don't have the shoes or accessories to sync to the app, but the feature that allows me to track my workout through gps is PERFECT. I can easily select my type of workout (walk, run, etc.), Start, pause, resume, or finish as needed, And I can play music from my other apps in the background without disturbing this app. Also, every mile a voice updates me that I've hit the mile marker and provides details on my pace and accumulated distance (while slightly dimming my music in the background).
I was hoping that this would connect to my daily burn app. I wanted to keep track of which workouts I have completed. sort of a letdown.
Even after so many years, why isn't there a way to edit workout times? 😕 Lot of people forget to end the sessions, and the graph looks horrible.

It's a good companion but spectating riders is very scuffed. It's tricky to spectate a rider and there's hardly an indicator which of the riders is the one you're watching. It's also hard to see where the race ends and how much longer they're racing for. It's just not designed for spectating but as someone who zwifts casually and her boyfriend races, I'd like to see some quality of life improvements for the spectating feature.

It's very buggy, often won't refresh, sometimes not showing anything, sometimes crashes, most notifications don't work, synchronization with sport watches is really delayed, even the website often doesn't work or gets stuck in a loop on login. It feels like an abandoned project now... It's time for everybody to start looking for alternatives
This is frustrating to use. When I tried to use the categories to shop the Vitacost Brand I get a blank screen. I will also get a blank screen if I hit the load more button.

Use to be a great app. Lots of data immediately available in easy to read format. Every update removes more of that. Words replaced with cryptic icons. Graphics so large you have to scroll and scroll to see stuff. Most data that was available on the homescreen has been buried in nested menu clicks. Tips filling the screen that you can't disable. Awful. As soon as I find a replacement with a decent food budget, this app is uninstalled. Only good thing left in the app. Period. Don't buy a Fitbit.
Easy to find what you need, great selection and prices, frequent promotions, quick delivery. App is very convenient and faster than using my laptop.

I have used fitbit for many years, and it used to be a great product. Not anymore. Since merging with Google, it has become a complete waste of time and money. Data used to be immediately available in an easy to read format. Now, every update removes more of that and buries the data in too many menu clicks. Useless tips filling the screen that you can not disable. Leave it to Google to screw up another great product and turn it into junk.

Amazing app! It detects our movements, and records any sound/talking/snoring we make as we sleep. It claims to be able to isolate sounds/movements from your spouse. I can't say as I sleep alone. I love that it doesn't record the white noise of my CPAP. 20 bucks gets you some advantages even over a home sleep study by recording your movement and sounds. We're so used to getting apps for free, $20 seems like a lot for an app, yet it's nothing given how important our sleep is.

Sleep monitoring is a joke, both with fenix 7x pro and with the index sleep band. Body battery is frequently out of sync not only between watch and home screen tile (even immediately after data sync) but also between the tile and the detail page you access through the tile.

The app keeps crashing & won't stay open. A friend introduced me to this app awhile back when I was trying to track my food & activity, but then I had stopped due to asurgery. I'm trying to get back into a routine of logging my food & activity, but the app keeps closing. It'll open then if you try to do something, it crashes & closes. Also, like the last review I also noticed that it was logging activity twice. It logs from the Fitbit & from Health Connect. Please fix! Thx!

Awful downloading recipes was not simple which made it pointless to me. Then it was awful to unsubscribe because apparently it was through Google play store so I got charge over $100 twice. WORST WASTE OF MONEY EVER.

What happened to this app?? You can't do anything without paying for it. Adding a recipe manually, it changes everything you write. Then allows you to edit and still it's wrong. If you give up and want to just add the macros at least to have something, it only allows you to add calories, no carb, no protein, no anything. This used to be a good app now, NO WAY. UPDATE: Please if you intend to respond to reviews then at least respond with an actual response, not some stock reply with a link.

Everything appears to be working well at the moment, this will be my 9th year with this app. One thing I found I had to do recently was delete the app data stored by my phone, there was over 2. 5gb of it and was slowing everything down, once that was done and the settings re entered things returned to normal. Seems to be working fine this summer until today when I was logged out? Tried everything but had to delete app then reinstall seemed to work again!?
Love this app! Meal planning and grocery shopping is so easy with this app. It keeps us on track with cooking and limits last minute choices to go out to eat.

TERRIBLE APP!!! One of the worst apps I've ever used. It's unfortunate that this crappy company became the default standard for beauty salons and spas. I guess they just have no competition. First, you need a separate password for each business that you deal with. So, I have a few massage therapists, two beauty salons and a few spas...some are synced but others require a different password even though I use the same email address. Also, they lock you out if you accidentally enter the wrong password, so you have to wait to log back in. The search is also terrible. I have a salon that I book on the web that uses MindBody for their bookings, but when I search in the app the salon doesn't show up--I can only book it on the web, and my bookings don't show up in the app. Finally, you're restricted to searching up to 50 miles from your CURRENT LOCATION! What if I'm on a business trip to NYC but I want to book a hair cut for when I'm back home in Boston? Tough luck!!! You can't search for that

Been using it for about 3-4 weeks now. Pretty easy to use, some of the food in the search may be off by a few calories but that's personally not a huge issue for me. There's an option to add your own if it doesn't match the label exactly though which is helpful. Good interface. I enjoy it. Update: I've lost over 60lbs thanks to being able to easily track my intake with this app. My only suggestion is to add a block button, it would be nice to block accounts that post irrelevant propaganda.

I love using this app for my training and the guided runs are great for motivation. I just wish there were more guided runs with run/walk intervals. I'm confident in my ability to run non-stop, but I would like more run/walks for light and easy days.

Do NOT Agree to the 'trial version'. Its extremely difficult to cancel the subscription, jumping through several hoops just to cancel a lame app that you can find elsewhere for free. I was charged $59.99 for a yearly subscription when I tried several times to cancel BEFORE the trial ended. Good luck finding a phone # to call customer service & speak to actual human being to help solve this issue. $60 bucks for a years worth of anxiety knowing I got ripped off. Yeah, that'll calm me down. SCAM!!!

I don't normally leave ratings or write reviews for apps. I've used this app for many years & didn't think much about it. Honestly, overall this is a must-have app for anybody into fitness looking to track your goals. This app for my particular use has been great, It's straightforward & very easy to function. I like to use this app mostly for biking and walking, I love the fact that it tracks your distance, speed, calories & also shows your route traveled through the map which is cool. NO ADS.

Def needs help. Constantly crashes. UI is buggy and it's really hard to figure out what's what. Not worth the monthly fee if it doesn't even work. Dropped to 1 star. Every morning I'm nagged to journal about COVID BS. EDIT: customer service is horrendous. No one seems to know what is going on over there. I've been over charged for months and no one can seem to tell me how or when the appropriate"service team" is going to contact me. This company is a joke.

What I like about this app is that it is simple to use, works flawlessly, and it really is free (with an upgrade option). The only issue I have is that I would love to be able to add an icon to food entries. I started out testing the MyNetDiary and Track apps but on my Samsung phone both had issues with logging in foods.

I'm compare it to lose it. way better app. main issue is the search tool and even after you add and used an entry if it passes 2 weeks I think it won't remember it. you have to search for it again and often can't find it. also can't make corrections. It happens often for me that it show the European nutrition and serving size and now I have to do weird fractions to figure out correct calories, but macros are wrong. Even though I'm paying for the service I have limited reports I can run

I have a love hate relationship with yazio, it's a good calorie tracker but the gamification became unbearable. I'm a paid user and there is no way to turn the tips and all around stupidity. I don't want to commit to a streak, I said no for the 6th times today just leave me use the app I pay for. And this mascot and overall interface? Is your target demographic kids? If this continue I'll just move to chatgpt at the end of the period. Edit: just don't bother replying if it's to post this ai bull

I'm new to fitness tracking apps and I'm trying to get back into shape. The app together with my phone's camera, measures heart rate and respiratory rate more accurately and with a far better display of those results, compared to pulse oximeters I've used. However. I personally don't rely on the app's measurements of the intensity of my activities, because I am less likely to be carrying my phone with the app open during my most intense physical activities.

This is for allowing impact app to record steps. this impact app is very fraudulent or defective or incorrectly programmed, when I start impact app it keeps on recording steps without actually walking and record the same on Google fit. Google fit data is 100% perfect. Glitch in the app. Active mode in walking mode remains on, activity of walking ends only after restarting mobile App is excellent & best app for health monitoring.

Useless. I got this app for the wellness program for my employer to get a reduction on my insurance. At first I just kept the phone in my pocket. Some days it registers the activity, most days it doesn't. Bought an arm band for the phone. The app registered nothing and I've got sweat running down my face. After 10 actual workouts the app registered 2. Absolutely useless.

Doesn't sync sleep correctly from my Garmin watch. Tried the Garmin Sleep experimental app and syncing via Health Connect but the imports were always incorrect, despite my tracked sleep showing up flawlessly in Garmin Connect, and in other Health Connect apps. Hard to navigate the graphs as it automatically zooms in on sequences when I try to scroll through my graph for the night. Smart alarm didn't work, despite testing the pairing of my watch nightly with Garmin Sleep plugin.

This new update is so clunky and difficult to use. Everything now takes at least 3 or 4 extra clicks, there's no way to see my full food diary in a single view anymore. Lunch is "mayo and 5 others"? How is that helpful? The nutrition summary is misleading since it now defaults to a weekly average. You've made food tracking so frustrating and annoying that it's no longer useful and I'm finally willing to give up my 15+ years of historical data and recipes to switch to a better app.

I've been using this app here and there, and it's a good app. It is especially useful if you're a beginner and you want to do the StrongLifts 5x5 program at the gym. Heads up - they've removed the export option a few years back (by accident it seems). So, if you decide to purchase a pro subscription, you still won't be able to export your data into a csv for a more thorough analysis. (Please add this option back 😄🙏)
Great app. I've been using it for almost a decade and still loving it.

Clean interface. Easy to use. Sometimes parts of run get cut off but it seems that only happens when you have another app running that uses your GPS. Overall the best run tracking app I've used. One annoying thing (that doesn't really matter, but annoying nonetheless). There are lots of people who cheat on the leaderboards. Like why?
Easy to use and does exactly what I need to get back into power walking progressing to running again.
I have had this app for a while and haven't really used it. I set it for a walk and it was nice hearing when I had walked a mile and the time it took me! I will be using this more often!👍

Uninstalled. Not sure why, but today the layout changed and it's terrible. I didn't update the app, nor do I have it set to download automatically. Before, I could view every item in breakfast, lunch and dinner on one page. Now, multiple items are grouped together and I have to do an extra step to go into each section to view all the items, including water/exercise. It's discombobluated. Can we PLEASE have the option to change it back? What was the point of messing with a perfectly good thing?
the free version now includes some od the worst ads and ad videos I've seen on an app. if youre willing to pay definitely do that. do not download the free version.

This is the best free app. There is lots of workouts available. Other apps have very little for free. To get work outs/routines you have to pay monthly fee. I love it because there is always a workout available depending on time constraints & what I want to do work at any particular time. The best is of will plan a work out routine for you depending on what you want to work on, work out availability & strength. No better app around!

Inaccurate and frustrating App. I have tried this many times over the last couple of years always thinking I'll get it to sync accurately but always disappointed. Steps taken and source data are unhelpful and not a true record. The Health Points never make sense to activities actually carried out. I will be deleting the app and my account for the last time.

So much for a lifetime purchase. They've decided it's over because they want more money. I don't expect access to new features, I expect access to the features I paid for. If you don't want to maintain them in a single app then make a separate one and stop giving us updates. If so few people were using their lifetime licenses why did you get so many 1 star reviews for this stunt?

Essential for Zwift. I use it for team rides and chatting with group. I can keep track of messages and conversation cross crossing the screen. I use it for running for the same reason as well. When in a training run/ride, the interval times and target power/pace is useful and has a nice range to focus on. Easy to use to find group runs and rides. Overall, app is polished and easy to use.

I loved the app, it helped me lose weight. I barely use it now because the streak system and the gamifacation. I hate it sooo much. I wish I could turn this awful feature off but can't. I'll look for another app when my subscription ends. Edit, 2026/03/23: streak system is still there and they've added a new annoying feature (meal tips after logging). I changed 3 stars to 1 star.

In general, it's pretty good, and I've used it for several years without problems. Today, not so much. I am run Zwift on a Lenovo Vantage tablet. The Zwift app works fine. I run Companion on a Google Pixel 9a phone. The phone was fully updated today, both system and apps. When I ran Companion, it seemed to start up fine, switched to the map, and showed me on it, and immediately put up a "Something went wrong" message, and exited. Rebooted the phone, tried Companion and again it quit on me.

Good for the most part. Easy to use and I like the voice advising times on the benchmark run . 2 things I do not like are that you cannot have more than 1 training existing. Once you start new training you have no info to look back at any prior. Second no voice on any runs except benchmark, so you have to look at phone to know when you have gone 400 meters every time (last time for me 7 times) or 800 meters or anything but benchmark. Makes it hard to do and I accidentally bump each time.

Been tracking everything diligently every day. After the latest update, everything takes forever to load, doesn't load most of the time, and my tracking timeline is shot (half of the days display as "nothing entered" in the calendar view even though things are entered in the individual day's view) leading to my 20+ week streak getting reset and my streak freezes being deleted. Also, tracking any new foods is basically impossible because of the endless loading and failures.

I did competitive powerlifting 30 years ago. I stopped through injury though I've still trained in gyms since, just not squatting or deadlifting. I started 12 weeks ago with just the bar and I'm amazed at where I'm at now. I've really focused on form and if I've missed a rep I've stayed at the weight until I could complete all reps and sets. The app is very flexible that way. Once weight doesn't go up every session it can be changed to every other or every week. Spot on app, thanks Mendhi.

It's absolutely horrible, and I'm tired of giving it more chances to have my correct info. It's hard to believe this app has me at an almost 24 min mile while I'm on the treadmill with the indoors setting on and at a pace of 10:54. I installed this app to be a part of a challenge group to help me get ready for the marathon, and this has demotivated me so much, and I'm tired of it. Now, I won't have a group to work with, but it's not worth keeping this app.

It's user friendly enough, not complicated although I literally only use it for walking the dog and using my elliptical. I will say recently it has let me down a bit- it will get frozen without me noticing so when I finish my exercise it shows I've done 12 seconds, or whatever it was. It then freezes and I have to wait til it's sorted itself. It's linked to my calorie counting app, MyFitnessPal, so this is annoying as I then have to make a broad guess at my calorie burn. But pretty good overall.

Great tech, but the pricing model is outdated and flat out unfair in EU region. I really like the product, but the subscription pricing isn't market-realistic for all of Europe. You can't just apply one "EU rate" across the board when there are such massive income gaps between regions. If you want to actually build and keep a subscriber base in the CSE area, you need to price more competitively for local markets. Other than the cost barrier, it’s a great product...

"an unexpected error occurred" at every step of the process. Uninstalling and reinstalling does nothing. It thinks my first name isn't valid?? Then when I skip that, put in my info, it asks for a screen name. It errors out, then tells me my name is already in use ad nauseum. It says I already have a login but nothing happens when I try to sign in, and if I try to reset my password, "an unexpected error occurred." Even after activating my account. Completely unusable.

Fix the GPS. Using Galaxy Note 20 Ultra, GPS works fine, but in this app it's like it is updating too frequently and doesn't remember orientation state so the map just changes direction several times per second. For a paid (expensive app) there's no excuse. Also, after so many years can you please make the HR zones a better feature if we have to pay for it.
It's show all products in dollars and its not a user friendly app

Pretty good app. I used it for about 8 years. The free tier pretty much covered all I needed from a gym tracker. Unfortunately they switched to a subscription only model. Their justification is that it is now personalized and therefore better. No thank you, I think I can personalize my trainings based on my own needs rather than having some "expert" algorithm do it for me. I'm giving 2 stars because it was good before they messed it up.

Very useful on the bike as often cast to a TV, which is not that stable, and use companion as back up. Would prefer a little more training plan visibility/ interface similar to the main app, but understand there probably has to be differences otherwise it's the main app. 😄

I've been using this app for over a month now. It's fantastic. I've lost 9lbs. The system works if you stick to it. If you're the kind of person that is motivated by visual tools and data, this is for you! A small food scale is helpful (Amazon for like $8). Foods that I had been eating previously were healthy, whole foods, but my portions were too large, and my "treats" were more calorie dense than I realized. This app raises awareness about what you're putting in your body. Highly recommend!
Great when it worked. Glitches and will not load on Samsung now.

Zwift has allowed to me exercise during these pressing times. The app and experience is great I just wish it had a better and more seamless integration with Windows but aside from that it's wonderful and great for training.

Edited after customer service helped me. Okay, crisis avoided. Brad from customer service helped me figure my issue out (recipes were missing) turns out I'm a dummy and was logging into a blank account. Thank you Brad for pointing this out to me! Now that I know my recipes are safe I can go back to being happy with this app. Honestly, the meal planning and shopping list really makes life easy so I'm glad nothing is missing!

Unable to go back to previous sounds! I started using this app due to severe sleep disturbances. As well as, unusual events happening in my house. I captured a male voice with a baritone sound, in the middle of the night. I just paid for the premium service in hopes of being able to recover. What is the algorithm that causes our sounds to be deleted? Specifically more so, when only a few nights/ sounds were recorded to begin with?

Used to be a great app, then they got greedy. used to be free, with an option for a one time payment to unlock the pro version (which I was actually thinking about about getting) then they made the pro version a "subscription based service", and I can't stand subscription based services. Then they got rid of the free version all together. A shame really, was a great app, but I refuse to pay monthly to use a simple app. I don't see the value in paying monthly for an app to do weight math for you

This is showing my completed orders as not yet shipped and previously ordered items comes up blank. Everything is fine on the Web, but this APP isn't pulling in the data. When you fix it you can have stars.

Years later still an amazing app. Extremely customizable with many alarm options including NFC. The Best alarm app I've ever used. Three possible improvements would be easier playlist integration. Having to click all of the songs is time consuming when you want it to shuffle through many. A "cancel all alarms until X time" would be great. Last would be decreasing snooze times. 5 min, 3 min, 1 min, etc. Still, highly recommended.

I have been logging in this app for a few weeks now. I tried it after getting back on an app that I used to use and finding many features that I used to use in the other app now behind a pay wall. Fatsecret provides everything that I need to easily track my macros, so that I can make the decisions that I need to for my health.
Unable to login

I love my Garmin watch; if I ever want to change it, I'll definitely buy another Garmin. However, I'm not sure what changes you've made to the app recently, but my watch keeps getting disconnected. Please fix it and keep your brand reliable, as it has been for many years.

Getting frustrated with latency. the app (premium paid version) is great when it connects and works but it has become very noticeable over the last few months how often sleep stories sit on three dots trying to upload (I am on a measured 260mbps connection). If you are not prepared to upscale capacity to make it a stress free experience, I will be cancelling my renewal when it is due. Score will drop the longer this continues
I used this app at fall to track my calories, then took a brake for several month and returned now, to see all the previous records were gone, however i did not uninstalled the app

I made an update as requested today by the app (I have PRO subscription), after update all tips from AI are in Latvian or Lithuanian language, witch I do not understand. my phone is in English, app is in English and database of the Food is Estonia, from where it is taking this language I do not use anywhere? Please fix it. UPDATE: Support was very quick. I have forced to use ENGLISH language in the app even I have English system language in my phone. It helped. Thanks a lot.

They changed the app and now its trash. I used to be able to track my calorie intake and expenditure easily for weight loss bc I would simply put in that I wanted a 500 calorie deficit every day of whatever my total calorie expenditure was and now I have to put in a target range. every day is different! A range is not helpful. Whoever made this has never had to experience weight loss or weight gain.

Won't show the current workout so no controls. Zwift constantly over promises and under delivers on basic functionality. Zwift owners, you've got a great product idea but this culture of deceiving your customers is building negative brand equity. I am the first one to sign up for new competitos like Trainerroad, at least they are honest.
Checkout/payment was glitchy in the past (especially with PayPal) but they must have resolved that! Easy smooth checkout through the app today! And an awesome 4th of July code! Woo!
Can no longer log in.. Used to use the app all the time. Haven't been able to log in for months. Uninstalled and reinstalled app with no luck. Password works fine on the browser.

Love the guided training plans for 5k etc! So so helpful to have a voice pushing you and keeping track for intervals etc. Biggest issue now is it CONSTANTLY logs you out and it's really annoying to log in every time you open the app.. Also sometimes it doesn't sync runs I've done to challenge plans... You used to be able to edit and add runs to plans and now you can't. 3.21.26 update - finished a run on my 10k plan today and went to look at the next run and now the plans are gone???

Just started using it since I got a code for Christmas. Constantly has weird sound artifacts when listening to meditations. Difficult to navigate and find topics, and most of the topics I found feel commercial or have very short meditations. Also, no ability to change the speed of the meditation, sometimes I just wanna listen at 1.25x speed because these people speak so slowly. Prefer Headspace over this by leaps and bounds so far
working good but the prize shows very expensive...

I love the barcode option for the alarm but lately it's been buggy. the alarm has gone off at weird times, sometimes an hour earlier than it's set for and once even 7 hours late! not sure if it's user error but that one made no sense to me. I sleep very deeply and need an alarm that doesn't let me just turn it off and go back to sleep. this was IT until recently.

app hasn't been updated since invention. 1. no new stickers 2. very old map and run stat mock ups 3. no map stiker to overlap on images . while this could have been a competition to Strava . Nike never didn't bother to at least keep up upto date with online , but simply abandoned the entire idea and eventually sply lost market share.

I am consistently experiencing freezing issues, and my Whoop device has not provided any readings for two weeks. The customer service experience has been disappointing, as I have followed the same troubleshooting steps with multiple representatives without a resolution. Android user

When I first made the switch from Fitbit to Google Health, I was big mad! I couldn't find my usual metrics, etc., But after exploring it for a bit, I gotta say.... I kinda like it! That being said, I DON'T like that I can't reorganize the tiles because there are specific tiles that I view/edit often (i.e., water & protein), therefore I'd like those tiles to be "Front and Center" when I open the app.

Love the app and have been using for years as someone who hated running growing up. The only thing to point out it regularly logs out the account and removes any saved and favourites, which is not a big thing, but still a bit annoying to need to re-add it every time. otherwise highly recommend especially for those new to running.

The user interface is slow where I need to back out and go back in. I also hate the new update where they removed the ability to see how many people are signed up for class after you have already booked the class. Hopefully they will add that feature back in. I found it useful as sometimes if I'm the only person signed up for a class I may cancel so the instructor is not having to come in just for me.

Works ok, needs some key improvements. For one, you can only add a recipe by visiting the website (not via the app). Another suggestion would be to allow you to easily copy one or more items between meals (or days). Maybe you can already do this, but if so, it's not obvious. My biggest complaint would be how serving sizes are specified. Example: if an item serving is 2tbsp and you used one only 1, you have enter .5. it's awkward & would be easier if sizes were a single unit where applicable.

I started using this app for the sleep tracking and smart alarm. The accuracy of the smart alarm is dubious, but I've continued using the app because I love the alarm customization (I use vibration under my pillow), the snooze options, and it is nice to see how long I've slept in any case. Don't rely on this app for insights about sleep cycles, REM, etc, but do use it if you want to be more conscious of your sleep habits.

App opens very slow on my phone possibly because of snooping into my phone. The app is very slow to bring up data. The daily workout data that the app stores takes up a large amount of storage space on the phone and there is no way to easily delete it all without uninstalling and reinstalling the app. Because of these problems I have stopped using the app for my daily hikes and I no longer recommend that for anybody.

Great app, works and connects and syncs with my watch daily so I don't have to. I would recommend it. Sometimes random glitches happen, but they are usually fixed pretty fast or you can find a solution with some digging. Very rare though!

I am changing my review score again, and hopefully for the last time.   This app does a good job of tracking and aggregating a lot of activity data. It syncs with devices and apps that I use to track nutrition/exercise metrics. And considering it is free, you really can't beat it.  After reconsidering overnight and thinking about what I want from this app I think it was unreasonable to change my review to a lower score.  It indeed does not display the metrics in the same was as it did before, but they're still there. I think it was more of a reaction to change than an actual complaint about functionality. And that's not how I like to do things.
On the whole works well but a couple of times it would not recognise my region.

simple to use, lots of recipes to pick from, a huge help for weekly meal planning. I love the grocery lists that are generated and the unique step by step prepear mode is great when making the recipe!

I need to back out of the app and refresh way too often.

Unreliable lately. Sometimes is pulls up my saved favorites, other times it does not. I type in the name of the a previously saved business and nothing. Try to add my CC info and the app says (paraphrasing) "having trouble please try again later". The app worked last night, now...not so much.
Intuitive to use. Amazing for finding classes. Easy to book and pay. Works as intended. Do recommend!

A really useful addition to Zwift; I always use the companion when I'm zwifting as it adds to the functionality. I have been using it for several years and it works well and I have not had any significant issues. But it could do so much more, which is why it didn't get 5*

Good at some things, others need improvement.  I like the GPS tracking that traces where I walk. That is, when it works.  There's a lot that could be improved however - I have lots of trouble importing info from my other tracking apps into Fit. I use a smart scale that also measures muscle mass, body fat, etc and it would be great to be able to add all that info to Google Fit as well. Fit doesn't import my entire weight history from my smart scale, which would also be nice.  It would also be great if there was alternate ways to track my walks without using GPS and using so much data - drains the battery on my phone way too fast.

Frozen Watts... I am using this app on a Galaxy z fold 7. It works perfectly fine on my wife's iPhone. My avatar doesn't respond either. I have done everything to try to remedy this but nothing seems to work. I think it is just a bug with my Android phone and this app. It still works fine for me otherwise.
When trying to edit something that you entered in the log Where you tap is not where the app thinks you tap, and it still hasn't been fixed.

The app no longer tracks distance. I don't know if it's because of the most recent update, but the timing seems to coincide. I went through the T-shooting ideas as recommended, but all the settings are already where they need to be. Your link to enter a trouble ticket only allows me to enter a question which gives me the same automated replay. ARGHHH!

Super clunky. Sometimes the search shows only 1 page and won't load any more items but it's not frozen. Just wont6 show you everything. Love the app but took a star off because it's been glitchy lately. This app has been a great resource for working out at home. I was initially bummed that the old programs were gone, but I love the video format of the premium programs and don't miss the old ones at all! But since the last update, the video glitches, causing the audio to be ahead of the video by 30 seconds by the end of a 15 min workout. It doesn't sound like a lot, but it makes a big difference when you're pushing thru a workout

Great app, don't bother with premium. 90% of recipes you won't like and the barcodes tracking rarely works. Also, daily diamonds don't always work if you have a late breakfast, say after 9, when it claims it works from 6-noon.

Was a really good app, but they changed it just to change it and now nothing is intuitive. You can accidentally add all the logged foods from a previous day to a meal with one tap, which you then have to delete 1 by 1. Calories remaining is so small, you have to squint to read. you can't see everything in 1 day anymore, you have to tap individual meals and then go back to see calories logged. so clunky now.
User interface is pathetic for such a big website.

After the update it isn't tracking things as well as it used to. It won't count my steps if I'm pushing something like a wheelchair, and when it comes to tracking my sleep, it's super off. Haven't even been able to log in and set up the strap. If I try logging in, it sends me to the "find the Strap serial number " page and does nothing else. EDIT: Thank you for the reply. It takes some getting used to. My country location makes it difficult to find matches for my food in the search but it works out. I'm happy to have something accessible that I can use to help me stay the course.
Paid for a lifetime access years ago. Now being forced to subscribe with no option to reaccess the app i paid for. Very disappointing and greedy move by the developers.
Easy to use and navigate.
I love this app, wish I knew about it sooner, I like how it kepts track of speed, distance and let's me know where I am. thanks again for a wonderful app.
Love this app!! Makes meal prep so much easier!

Could be great. But distance units don't update when selecting kms, and you can't sort search results to show closest first. Specifying to only show businesses less than 50km away does not work. I'm still seeing businesses 10,000mi from me. It's all pretty annoying but should be simple and straightforward.

I used the free version for a long time and loved it. Tried to use it Saturday and it said I had to upgrade. So I did the 7 day free trial. Good thing cause I couldn't even figure out how to use it to track my trip. Completely different interface than the free one I was using. Does not even have a "Paddle" option but just about everything else you could think of. Cancelled free trial the same day! Now I have to find another app to track my paddles.

The app is a waste of time. Continuously crashes, when works is super slow and frustrating. I hate to order from bodybuilding because takes such a long time to place an order. Normally takes at least an hour whe suppose to take 10 minutes.

Zwift continues to listen to user feedback and provide a great platform. Delighted screenshots now just save to my phone gallery, so I can share while riding. The companion app is pretty much essential when I'm training with Zwift on a tablet (...and/or projector to a garage door.) If I had to nitpick, the HUD font is smaller in the latest version (both companion and main app) - I have to squint if I don't have my glasses on (e.g. while training). HR is the thing I care about most.

There's a great variety in sleep stories, but the navigation is focused on repeating ones you've already listened to. The app also tends to crash when opening it for the first time each day, which makes it harder to choose content, which partially defeats the purpose of sleep meditations. But all in all, it's still a fairly useful sleep aid.

The main thing I'd want from this app is to be able to view and sign up to training plans, which isn't possible here. You can review a plan you're already signed up to, but can't see stress scores of upcoming workouts, can't mark them as complete, can't reorder them or plan when you're doing them. This is a very expensive subscription to a training app that fails to support you in planning your training.

I would LOVE this app... If it worked all of the time. But most of the time I click on my studio and it won't populate the schedule or let me see the classes I've signed up for. It's so frustrating and I've missed classes because I couldn't book them and they filled up before I could get there.
Hasn't worked properly in months. Doesn't even show some countries in the shipping options (United Kingdom missing... Really?).
I can't believe that DailyBurn would only provide this to Pro members. It took them FOREVER to even make an app, and now they are not providing to everyone. I'm very dissatisfied.

Wow! This app makes it much easier to calculate resistance and rests for each session! I did stronglifts in 2012, hit a plateau and quit! I won't have a problem this time! Once I've gone through the initial 12 wks I might upgrade to pro... Update Oct 30, 2023 Purchased pro! Great decision! Have more options and tools to help me reach my goal.

Previously a premium user, but only when on offer because at full price, its far too expensive. Recent updates have now made this awful to use so now looking for other alternatives. Lack of barcode scanning means that searching through the millions of entries for food is exhausting and nearly impossible to get right. UX is clunky and all exercises from samsung watch is duplicated. Calories left in allowance also changes randomly, which could lead to overeating against calorie goals.

bought it and now regret. Too much noise and distractions, unwanted tips and gamification. No option to turn it off or customise. I wanted a tracker app, and not another green owl. Every time getting lost in the UI, even if I simply want to input meal records. AI camera works relatively well. UPD: -1 star for the useless automatic reply.
The new update for this app is terrible! Slow, dosen't work correctly. I've been ordering from this company for years. Great company! But the app needs some attention asap

Edit: After heavy traffic of emails to Google Play and app's payment platform, I got refunded. Giving 1 star until my issue is resolved. been using this app for nearly 10 years, the detail and tracking is brilliant. looking forward to upgrading my watch to get even more out of the app

strap my phone in, start map my ride and it tracks everything! Love the map that shows the route. Although real time stats are always available; you get voice updates as well. 3/21/26 : first ride of 26! love map my ride...nothing better out there

NOBODY WANTS THIS. Absolutely awful UI update. Previously the app was streamlined, data readily visible, fast and efficient. Can't login through app it keeps saying my info is wrong but I can login on website

Make the app only as paid version. People download free apps because it's free. Looks nice but that's about it. It tracks while you drive. Can't enter seconds in manual workouts. Had to go back and make this 1 star. Would make it zero if I could. You are able to monitor your sleep and your deficit or surplus had throughout the week. You can also set your alarms to a song then have that song fade in to slowly wake you up. The UI is clean and simple, and the nap options are right there without having to navigate menus. Overall it's a good app to have for personalizing alarms and setting nap alarms.

The latest updates have ruined what used to be a great app. The ability to preview the exercises in a workout is gone, and I'm not willing to just blindly hope that a given workout has what I want. Since getting the 5.0 whoop I need to re-sync every morning which is frustrating. I have reached out to support a few times and none of their troubeshooting recommendations fixes the issue. Really disappointing as the 4.0 band never had this issue.
This app was great, now it sucks. Changed password 4 effing times, didn't make a difference. PLEASE fix this!!!

This app is so amazing! I love this app so much! Please make more apps just like this one!!!!!!!!!!!! Best app ever!!!!!!!!!! It literally never crashes!😍😍😍😍always works perfectly! Negatives: 1 - as many people have pointed out, the app is kind of hit-and-miss with regards to accurately counting steps, and allocating heart points appropriately. 2 - There really should be a cumulative total distance walked available for each day, rather than having to add up each "walk" for a total. 3 - On MANY occasions, the cumulative number of miles DOES NOT ADD UP TO what the app told my on the particular day AS THE DAY WAS PROGRESSING! Whenever you stop a run, it just stands and loads and never proceeds. I took a screenshot, closed the app and started it again and it ends up in the same location from the previous run still stuck loading. no way of starting a new run. Other than that a good app for that one run.
Coach Bennett is fantastic love the guided runs. The guided runs dont stay on the app so I have to download them every time i want to use one which is annoying.
Terrible app ! Lags on every click, not mobile friendly at all.
Better than MFP by miles, more intuitive UI and non-paywalled barcode scanning. Ads do increase after the 1st wk (full page ads shown but no 5s timer) but not unbearable. Easy to add custom foods.

There are so many bugs, it's completely unusable. First, I ran around a large block 3 times: Fit tracked it as a half a lap and then me teleporting through a building to get to my final location. I checked my phone just before the final lap and it said 1.25 miles, but when I ended the workout it said 0.8 miles?? Next, the weight tracking uses the absolute worst moving average curve I've ever seen. If you enter your weight 8 times in a day, the curve makes a sharp turn. That's just terrible math.
Terrible app. Slow, freezes, doesn't load. Takes forever to place an order
Reliable alarm with useful math captcha. That's all I care about. EDIT: Great, the captcha animation was made less intrusive

Omg. I have never had so many issues trying to hand over my money as to my yoga studio. It only works with some cards (not my Australian visa debit, which I have no probs with elsewhere). Super long-time user. Been using Nike Training for just under a decade. Ever since they switched their format to videos, I've been looking for an alternative. I mainly used it for yoga. I loved that the voice that came with most of the workouts was slow and soothing, and that you could play your own music with it. Now, each instructor sounds like a wannabe podcast host who speaks way too fast and too constantly for me to actually get into the mindset that helps me excel at yoga. So disappointed.
awful app. since it has updated and removed fit bit. I can not log my meals to keep track of my calories

Multi-year Premium subscription: I find this app much clunkier and user-unfriendly than it was previously. Even simple things like logging food are less visible, and making changes is harder. To add a food, there is a choice of private, friends only or public. It seems impossible to change this after setting it up. I chose private, as I wanted to test it out, but then I couldn't add in a recipe or meal... Nor could I change it to public. Definitely feel I am getting less for my money.

Switched to this after FitnessPal decided to charge for the use of the barcode scanner. I love that I can track my macros, and personalize them for me specifically. This app has helped me drop more weight and monitor my health much better than any other (using the free version). The paid version is amazing, but I don't need the extras at this point (financially).

I absolutely hate the new Google Health interface. I really wish there was an option to make it look like the original fitbit interface that I am used to. the only reason it gets a star at all is because it run. Edit to add: also realizing that you cannot manually log calories, create custom food options, or add anything to their database. The Worst It constantly shuts down/doesn't track my runs. It also makes me log in every single time. Sometimes I have to remove the app completely and add it back to even get it to let me sign in. I'd opt for a different running app.

Not as many features as I had become accustomed to with endomondo pro. If basic functionality like this is deficient it causes me to lack confidence in paying for the Pro version.

Free App but Limited Free Selections. So, I wanted to find a bedtime story (fiction) + there were ~ 100 selections, 6 of them were free and 2 of those were for children. You really do get what you pay for. Worth $70/yr? Not sure, but it's 1 more subscription that adds up. One positive note - Steven Fry reading "Blue Gold" was excellent though! So, I found 1 in 100 that I enjoyed so far.
First use, seems better than Bike map. update 2/20/26. This is better than Bike map, will continue to use it. I like that it announces the mileage, elapsed time and average speed every mile.

Only a four cause there is always room for improvement, otherwise it's a great tool already. This is an awesome app. Works very well for my meal planning. Love the feature to add recipes from web. Thank you and God bless ❣

The new update doesn't allow downloading the workouts prior to use it in the gym offline. It now works only on data if you are in the gym. Please allow to download the workouts before hand so as the workouts takes tons of data. And you can't even play music while the workout is going on , there used to be an option to mute the voice.

Used to be great, but is now dismal. First came the annoying ads. Then the consistently underestimated distances, and average speeds. Then the recent 'syncing' issues, and the 'show all data' tab refusing to show anything. The last straw was today, though, when having completed a 21 mile ride, the app crashed when I tried to save it, losing all the ride data. Now it won't even open, it just crashes immediately! Look elsewhere, as sadly they've ruined Map My Ride.

This is a wonderful little app. It helps you develop better sleep habits. First it gives you a bedtime reminder. It also aids by making noise while your snoring. It has a record option so you can listen to talking, snoring while sleeping. There are lullabies to fall asleep. It has an alarm to wake you in the morning and added math to make sure your awake. After a week it gives you suggestions for better sleep. There's so much to this app I've probably missed some things. Try it you won't be disappointed!
Need to be able to create your own food. Such as if I make a recipe that has nutritional facts I should be able to add that. Other than that it is awesome

For a long time, the app worked well. I enjoyed logging my meals and trying to keep them under the calorie counts. then recently the app stopped working. Regarding your response, I can't open the app so I won't be able to contact you through it. thank you.

The "Programmes" feature is what makes this app great. I almost gave up using it because I thought they only had individual, isolated workouts, rather than much longer term plans with specific goals. Also, achievement date/time, Mi Fitness integration, and a Windows app. And... why not list the outfits/equipment used by the instructors? Seems like a missed opportunity...

Fantastic app to find events, join and create group rides, interact with other users, and throw Ride On's around. If I could change anything, control over the icons at the bottom of the game page, to get rid of unused icons would be great, and the white ring recharging quicker to throw out more Ride On's would be great.
This app is a scam. You literally get nothing in the free tier. Literally nothing. So you can't even try the app before you purchase a subscription. Blatant false advertising.
Made me fall in love with running! I love the guided runs and the focus on effort based running. Great app for people who want to get started with running!
Easy to use. Love the detail on the sleep score. Great for recording walks and running. Still haven't used to fill potential yet but so far so good

If I could rate negative I would. Not a user friendly app. not useful as a free app, wouldn't allow a free trial, and if a product (such a pre made meal prep service) doesn't have a bar code you can spend 10 minutes searching for the meal to view every other meal in the database at least 4x. This new app is awful. The UI makes no sense, there is very little info on the main screen. Also, setting up an alarm is so hidden and requires so many taps, there should be a shortcut right on the home screen for that. please do better Google.

This is great for logging exercise and mileage. I also like that it pairs with MyFitnessPal for calorie tracking as well. However, it would be nice if the app sensed when I have to stop (ex: for traffic) and stopped the timer and restarted again when I move automatically.

I bought it to watch my strain/recovery, as I train many hours most days. So far really happy, even though I was always quite mindful of sleep, it makes me more vigilant, not just with sleep, but more so with restorative activities, as I want to try and be in green recivery as much as possible.

The recent interface update pushed me over the edge to cancel. Been using this for easily a decade or two? It was the gold standard and I referred friends and family to it. I can no longer see everything eaten in a day, have to click in meal by meal. Date and logging info are unclear. The only redeeming benefit is the large database. Not worth it.

I used to love this app but since the update I'm unable to book classes with my gym about 95% of the time. It just keeps going back to home page or asking me to sign into my account. Very frustrating. I am continually on waitlists and other times completely miss out on classes!

1.5 years now on Whoop. Love VitaCost, best deals, best products, usually prefer their brand-based on the ingredients used. App is super simple. It makes it almost too easy to shop. But don't worry you get 30min window for buyer's remorse to cancel orders no questions asked. But I usually prefer to keep the massive deals

Really very useful for athletes and health coaches. Also for normal fitness focused persons. More insight more accurate. Very helpful for programming your exercise and planning your sleep and recovery activities

I love this app! I never would have thought that the guided runs would be for me, but I LOVE them. I also use the custom training plans to train for races, and they are fantastic. The companion app could be more intuitive for functions like giving others a thumbs up. It also bothers me that the rider list on a group ride can't lock you in the center of the list. I end up hitting the find me button all the time.

After waiting so long for this it is a disappointment to find out you must be a pro user to use this. This company has an obvious bias towards iOS. I really like this app. I use the free version and even though the ads are becoming excessive it's manageable.
