Got a Garmin watch because it's listed as officially supported by Sleep as Android. But the Sleep app for Garmin is completely broken and unusable. Therefore money wasted on both a watch and premium subscription for the app.

Great way to find and book services. Nice variety of options. Geo Search helps find services and activities near me. Sometimes it sends several of the same event to my calendar. Sends great reminder emails as well. Solid little app.

the best calorie tracking app I've tried, the premium price is great compared to other apps which also makes you want to stick to it more. only thing I wish was different is that you log the supplements/medicine as breakfast lunch or dinner and there isn't a different similar section for them, which I guess isn't a big issue but sometimes I kind of want to check back to see if I took them having them in a tab would make it easier. it also doesn't connect to Xiaomi bands which is an inconvenience

New to running so found Endomondo a great, free, entry level product. I use it for mapping, timing and logging runs (and dog walks! ). With the option to upgrade at any time if I get more serious and need more detailed statistics.

For some odd reason I cannot download a profile picture, maybe because I'm using an android. This app seems like it's made for IOS only which is frustrating. Will not let me join certain groups, invite codes do not work, which makes me wonder what else I don't have access to. Also, makes me speculate if my strain, recovery, sleep efficient, etc is even accurate.
Love being able to scan foods instead of typing everything in. It works great for keeping up with food but have to log on computer for workouts.

The management of multisport activities is horrible (particularly the division slider + the fact you can't quickly divide based on manually entered laps). The calorie counter on multisport activities doesn't work (I just hiked 800 m ascent and ran the way down for a total of 15 km and after dividing the activity my calories spent number is 76). It'd be very convenient if this could be accessed via PC to make the editing process easier.

Been using Calm for 5yrs now. Superb app ....until last update. Now you cannot search for a sleep story narrator. I just want to search for the likes of Erik Braa or Alan Sklar and then pick a sleep story. Can't do that any more. The search option goes through meditations and recommends random items and four stories from the narrator. I want to see ALL their stories. Fix it, please, Calm.
keep trying to pay for a swim but app throws me out every time

Great app with solid tracking! I really love how reliably it syncs with my watch and tracks all my health metrics and daily activities. However, I feel like the user interface could use a more modern, fresh look, and I would love to see more structured workout ideas or training suggestions directly in the app.

overall good, but it keeps logging me out of the account which is really really really annoying when you need a weird password because it didn't accept the normal password you've used for years and years as if the app is some bank app

the app is great, very easy to understand and manage. I like all the info it provides for workouts. Especially strength workouts, which show you exactly which muscles you trained and how much intensity.
App has gone downhill with new updates. Cannot save wishlist and continuously logs out.
I have the free version. My reason for the app is to track distance, calories consumed, and generally to keep up with my regimen. It does it all very efficiently.
Garbage app Takes a decade to open

Love this app. Its so engaging and has made indoor training really interesting and challenging. Sure it has a few tech problems but nothing that detracts from my enjoying 99% of the time. Would be useful to be able to choose a training program from companion rather than just events.

I'm really very pleased with the functions of this app. I've been through quite a few, and this one gives me superior results all round. I'm sticking with map my ride. Using it for running and cycling. I get HRM support for my el cheapo chest belt. Good information. Cloud upload lets me review data on the big screen computer. Close links with Google fit, works well. Altitude, route and distance data is much more accurate than many other apps I've used. Overall clear easy operation.

The Android version is extremely buggy and the fact that the only way to cast a workout is just to cast your screen seems like a weird bootstrap for such a big company. The features for recording non-NTC workouts are extremely basic. Most annoying for me, though, is the fact that when you've finished a program, you can't go back and redo individual workouts in that program afterward, which would be helpful. Having said that, the workouts themselves are varied and high-quality.

It doesn't record activity. It's failed to record two of my runs this week and has done so multiple times in the past. The app says it's been "Finishing [my] run" for two days. It frequently crashes and fails to perform it's most basic function. If there was an alternative I would use it, but there isn't, at least not one that isn't also broken.

The biggest disappointement of my life. Order multiple packages. The ISO whey protein that was sent was a fake one and then i havd other 2 packages stuck for last 4 months for which I am not getting any updates or help in tracking.

How/Why did they change the app? Just re-name it. It doesn't track my "allowed" calories based on output/exercise & my desired weight loss goals. What is the point of even having a CICO part in the app if it gives no information?? I was told I have to link it to a food-tracking app. So, now I need MORE apps, to do what Fitbit did on its own?!? Plus, I lost my custom food. The watch, Sense 2, randomly changes my watch face, shows dashes instead of numbers on the watch. I HATE this app!!!

So long Endomondo, you've served me well over the last 9 years. You 've plotted my routes and performance, you've let my wife see where I was when out running alone. I'm moving to Runmeter which does everything Endomondo did, just a bit more clunkily (and has a yearly subscription to turn on audio coaching). So thanks Endomondo team, it's been a pleasure but I'm uninstalling it at last.
the app is so useless it can't connect Garmin Runner 225 and only Strava alone can connect and can't even be seen by all phones Bluetooth rubbish
Doesn't keep me logged in, and I get push notification advertisements even though I turn them off. No thanks. Deleted.
Love Vitacost! Been shopping here for over 10 years my favorite place to shop for all health related items. Having the app on homescreen makes it easy for me to re-order must have items.

I'm new to the app. it's a good app. my only issue is the gym workouts. you ask for duration, but that is not an accurate take on calories used. for example, if I'm doing 3 sets of 15 at 50 kg. I'm using more energy than someone who is doing the same but using less weight. no different than if I do a brisk walk for an hour vs. someone running for the same amount of time. more gym exercises added would be nice, too. if there is a way you can adjust this, this app would be perfect for me.

sinful downgrade from fitbit. where is my data? buried. where are the tiny details. gone. functionality? lol. this app is the equivalent of The Cheesecake Factory menu. full of nothing and left hungry. I have a better chance of finding a needle in a hay stack than this app that has been forced upon me actually being useful to my fitness lifestyle. oh but hey, it can really track your period. that button was easy to find.

I'm an avid sleep talker/yeller that occasionally acts out their dreams. While I have successfully captured recordings, I'm uncertain on how accurate it tracks your sleep cycles. Despite obviously being awake while turning on the tracker, getting up to use the bathroom, or waking up to an alarm, it doesn't track that and thinks I'm in a light sleep. Ideally I would've liked to know which stages of sleep I act out, but I'm still very pleased with the recording software.

I really like the majority of this app. The music and sleep stories have greatly helped keep my mind from racing at night. I love the timer feature to play until the set time then turn off. If you pick a time longer than the track you select it selects another track and when it's a track that's much louder its actually woken me up. Not cool. Other than that it's been a great addition to (pun intended) calm down.

Downloaded it just to say,, Apple doesn't have right to sue you for having a fruit logo with a leaf! It doesn't look identical, and Apple has no right to do that! I can't review the app rn, I have to discuss about getting an account. But the pictures seem very nice, and I LOVE how you can do your own recipe, discover, have a grocery list, etc. Thank you for this app.

Not a hoax, I have done so many calories counters. And all of them end in not free stuff you kinda want. Which is fine. It's a business..but the stuff I need on this is accurate and free. Plus all the micros, and it links to Samsung health and Google fit for free. In my opinion, lifesum and MFP. Whole not had apps. The basics you have to pay for. So not with this. Give it a shot 😊. I didn't get paid to make this comment. I just don't mind supporting something that works. Cheers

This is a good app but not near as good as fat secret. I really liked the favorites feature.  Negatives: bar code feature often produced different numbers than what were on the package. Also could not find a way to add my own food other than barcode, searched and searched but to no avail.  Might have missed it because I could not believe you could not manually enter food data.
Once you have your recipes in shopping is so much easier.

This app was my go to app for home workouts. But with the recent update, all past workouts have been replaced. No calorie counter is there for each excercise. Also in the video format, we don't the set of excercises that would come up which is not convenient. We can browse through the workout set to choose if that suits our needs. I have started looking for alternative apps now.

The most recent update has been very frustrating. 1)Can't see total calories by meal, 2)can no longer conveniently see the entire formula that computes remaining calories, 3)On the calories chart there is no option for 7 rolling days, 4)cannot delete more than one item at a time, 5)on the Today tab, it is visually difficult to see at a glance which meals I've logged as each meal has the option to copy prior meals.I've been on this app for over 5 years and it keeps getting worse with each update.

It's good, but missing so much! You can't look at your garage, change bike or jersey etc. This is surely a basic requirement. You can't see achievements or even alter your FTP. Again, pretty basic. If you choose "not now" to join a meetup that you're signed up to, that's it, opportunity lost. You might be 40 seconds from the top of the Alpe, but you can't join after that 30 second countdown. Why?

I like the app a lot, and currently pay for the pro version. It's quick and easy to use. I just really wish there was a way to manually set each day's calorie goal. If I change my calorie goal, it applies it to all existing days. As someone who uses calorie cycling, this means I have a lot more red days than I should. I could just ignore the calorie goal and cycle how I want, but that kind of defeats the purpose of a calorie counting app.

Great app and interface to track calories and meals. I like that you can create your own meals and recipes. The database is not as extensive as MyFitnessPal but you can submit requests for products you use frequently. I actually submitted request and they replied in a timely manner. I was able to scan my product using the bar code. Great customer service. The only downside is the intrusive ads you get when inputting data. It is very annoying but I understand they keep the product free.

Great health monitor, TERRIBLE GPS tracking while working out. I've been using my Samsung Galaxy watch to track my fitness activity because Whoop just won't track me 90% of the time. I can't tell you how many times I finish a workout, and go to check my distance only to see it says 0.1 miles after a 3-5 mile hike or run. The app itself is buggy a lot, it's updated pretty often though, so that's good. If you're looking for a health tracker, it's good, but the app could definitely be better.

I enjoy the app functionality. I had an issue with the recent patch where I kept receiving a message that my version was out of date. Customer support responded to my report and resolved the issue quickly. Thank you!

The app is useful for tracking your numbers, but only if you fact check beforehand. It commonly provides inaccurate or missleading information in most recipes and meal plans. For example, the "High Protein, Low Carb" meal plan has significantly more carbs than protein. Chickfila meals show calorie counts far higher than what they actually are, which is inexcusable considering how the nutritional information is readily available.

I used to rave about this app - but it has somehow lost all functionality and is completely useless. On every run, after the warmup/stretch, the app cannot properly track anything. The timer says you're resting when the audio says to be pushing on an interval and vice versa. So the interval lengths, paces, distances are wildly off. This app is so good in concept and got me through half marathon training last year. Nike, what happened?!?!

I can save my walks and view totals for the day, week, or month (but only on the cell phone; the PC won't show all 3). But the phone has been crashing on every one of our 4 daily dog walks for the last week. I have to delete the incomplete walks on the phone, then manually put them in on the PC so the full walks are recorded. This has happened many times & I keep having to delete the app and restart the phone .. but the phone has not had an update in months so that's not the reason.
Still ok, but all the tracking of how much weight went haywire. see how it goes, but probably back to own and paper, again...

This app is working with the new Fitbit/Google change over. Which is why I paid for a year without ads. It is very easy to use. I wish there was a larger database for basic food. Anything with a barcode is easily added. Tons of nutritional information! My reason for three stars is that it shuts down frequently. Sometimes I feel like I'm playing whack a mole when trying to enter a meal.
As of now,it's one of the best devices in the market. Would recommend adding the paste tracking tier.

After trying a couple different apps, I have used FatSecret for about 5 weeks and pretty happy with it. Like others, it lets you search and record your daily food/drink, the reports are helpful, etc. Minuses are that you can't track daily water drinking and you can't reset the default sleep or rest values. You can enter exercise but the app doesn't credit this against your calorie intake. The ease of use was the deciding factor in my choice.

New update was a massive failure. Instead of being able to see every workout it plays one long video. Also before you could control voice assistance, where you could choose if you want it to explain how to do the exercise and where to focus or make it not say anything. Now it never stops talking with the most useless information, I can't even listen to music while work out, because the video silences it. Don't think I'll be using this app anymore.

I feel like this is really accurate and helps reach my fitness goals compared to any other device I have used in the past. The one exception is the treadmill running workouts are way off either it doesn't track at all or my pace is too fast.

Update: I use the app every single day and I never plan to switch! Thank you for adding the day of the week to the date on the diary as well! I've been looking for something that would be an upgraded experience to what I find missing in Samsung Health for food tracking. This nails almost every aspect! (Only had a few days so far.) It will be a great fitness companion and make meal tracking souch easier! So excited to have found this! Thank you!
Log screen frequently locks up.

This app used to be really good, but it stopped syncing with other apps & the step counter is no longer accurate. Even when it counts steps, it will not total them correctly for the day. The email address listed under app support, is no longer active, so there is no way to contact anyone about this issue. Google really dropped the ball on this one.

Always like and preferred Garmin gear but kind of put up with the app but over the last 3 years it's really closed the gap on others, will always be the best for hard core athletes but lately starting to think of the everyday adverage users which is nice to see

Clicked on a contact, it says Invalid phone number. To my understanding, it works with phone numbers with the country code. The app should add the current country code to local numbers automatically. // As I have a incoming call, it shows the caller name correctly. But when I check the call history, it shows just numbers, which is pretty useless. Who remembers phone numbers these days? // My GTR3 Pro can answer Line calls, but my Venu4 can't. The watch is 3x the price and 5 years newer.

Great workout content and well-structured training plans, but progress tracking is unreliable. I complete workouts in a multi-week program, yet my progress often doesn’t save and I have to start over. This is very frustrating and kills motivation. Progress tracking is a core feature, and when it fails, the whole experience suffers. Hope this gets fixed soon.

I've been using this app for several years with few problems. However, since an update earlier this week, I can't get past the splash screen when launching. Clearing the app data cache doesn't help, either. From reading recent reviews, I see that I'm not alone in this. ETA: Developer's reply to my review asked me to open app to submit a support request. Unfortunately, this is not possible, because as previously stated, the app will not open at all.

It's a good app but I have noticed some discrepancies. I went out to eat and what I got was logged in already to the app but the numbers were all wrong according to the nutritional information the resturant has. I have noticed this with bar codes i have scanned too. The carbs , fat, and protein numbers dont always match what is on the label.

Garmin UI stinks. Too much garbage to wade through. App doesn't sync when I stop a ride on my Edge 850. When I get home and end a ride on my Wahoo it automatically connects to my home wifi and by the time I get to my back door my ride is on Strava. And that is without needing to connect my phone to the Wahoo. The 850 will give me non-stop notifications that the app is connected or disconnected but doesn't sync my rides.

Food logging is garbage. Especially the "recent" tab, I accidentally logged something I've never actually eaten before about a year ago. Immediately deleted the entry and never logged it again. It has been stuck at the top of my recent tab for months. The list also contains various other things I haven't eaten in years. Meanwhile, things I've logged every day for weeks aren't on there at all or are at the very bottom.

Awful..since the new update.. The app ain't opening.. Are you tech people sleeping.???It's been a week since the issue and no solution till now.. Never expected this from such a reputed store.. Boooo!!!!

There is no way to add the nutritional value of custom recipes now with the new app. This is something that is extremely helpful. Logging my food accurately is an important feature. I've been using Oura for a few years and came back to a device compatible with Fitbit solely for the fact that Oura doesn't track calories. Thankfully I'm still in the trial phase. Looks like I'll be switching to My Fitness Pal.

Very, very little content for free. And the little bit that is available is only intros to multi-part selections. So although there seemed to be a lot of content, I didn't even bother looking at it because I had to sort through so many "lock" icons overlapping everything you can pick. If you have money to spend, but 1 star for me, horrible first impression.

I overall really love the Garmin app. The features it offers, especially for sleep tracking, cycle tracking, and workouts is just unrivaled. I also love that it automatically adapts your step goals, depending on how much you walk, and your menstrual cycle predictions, depending on your past cycles. The only unintuitive thing, at first, is knowing what to change on the watch, the app, or in the browser. Other than that I'm super happy with it and would highly recommend it.
Would b better if u could track excercises done and calculated the calories burned from your daily intake. Link up with my fitness pal or something

I absolutely love this app and game,I quit smoking and lost 20lbs since subscribing.One improvement I would make in the companion app is access to routes,workouts and more information on Watopia routes already ridden.Oh and the ability to schedule a route ride from the app.You guys Rock!

love it easy to use, scan function works great, macros show in grams not just a percentage has features that other large for. down side is long commercials and hard to choose units of measurement meaning oz,cups,grams etc. I wish that can be improved. Still my favorite!

I downloaded this app (and paid the outrageous subscription fee) to help with sleep. I have it on both my pixel phone and Samsung tablet. This app randomly "freezes" during the night (about 30% of the time) so it goes silent. Nothing more frustrating than coming back to bed after bathroom and trying to decide if it's worth totally waking myself up to RESTART the sleep app I paid for. Very disappointed 😞. a year later and couldn't be more disappointed in this app. finally purchased a machine.

This app is just not as user friendly or visually appealing as MyFitness Pal unfortunately. I have been tracking macros for many years. I tried the Gold version of the app on the recommendation that this app is more accurate and all entries are verified. I liked the ease of entering a recipe. That function seems to be more efficient. However, the way the macros and calories are listed in your Diary are hard to understand. It's difficult to determine what macros you have left.

It used to work well, but then I upgraded my phone to a pixel 8 and it stopped working well. I have to force quit the app daily to get it to sync properly with my Fitbit inspire 2. I've also had to reset all the storage twice because it gets in a weird state where it has calories tracked into the future. It is still a decent logging app, and when the exercise tracking works (sometimes it gets stuck and counts walks as 0 distance) it works nicely. Edit: More recent versions are much more stable.

Dropped all functionality for the Heart Rate Monitors that are using the ANT+ protocol. This used to be included and they they decide to just drop it. They could have easily left the code in and then listed it as an unsupported feature.

it used to be so much better before under armor took over. All they seem to want to do is push you to pay for premium. Polar has features that I like. Such as heart rate graph I can see in real time what zone my cardio is at ect. But endomondo links with more apps so I'm torn to keep looking for the best fitness app.

Bad Customer Service. Calm charged me twice for an annual subscription, and despite several emails since January 15th when this happened, I have received no response other than a reply email on January 17th saying they"we are still looking into the issue". Clearly, no one is looking into the issue. I have sent a follow-up email every single week to try to recoup the $80 overcharge. Will never subscribe to this app ever again, and watch out if you ever need an issue resolved. No one will reply.

I've logged all my meals and exercises on MyFitnessPal for 3,850 days without missing a day. I used to love this app but the new app is terrible!!! they removed the ability to change which day is the week your weekly calories or macros tracker starts on and defaults to Sunday... that's so stupid. why change something so simple and not give people a way to change it. the new look is also terrible and takes more work to do simple things. don't download this unless they go back to the older version

Don't download if you prefer apps that: avoid reopening themselves to play ads, don't have to be force stopped because the ads won't let you close the app at all, don't trigger seizures because of the ads, don't make you forget what you were doing because of the ads, don't give you a headache/migraine because of the constant ads and the way they jump scare you, don't make you spend all day waiting for the ads to end so you can look after your health. The ads only get more frequent and intrusive.

The last update ruined this by making everything class format! You use to be able to see a review of the workout before start it and see every single exercise in it, during workout you could skip and/or come back exercises . I use to use the app regularly and it was my fav, but now it's boring. Why you change something that was great, couldn't you just had add the new classes and keep the other ones as they were...you would had variety without compromising the great work you done before!

Dropped from 5 to 4 stars. The app remains simple and useful as it has. The new features (7/29/2021) are welcome, but under utilized. Specifically the nutrition section under the Browse tab. Once you into nut. Info from what you are, you can't edit it. Also, it doesn't seem like the app actually takes the calories consumed and turns it into useful data. It just stores it as a log. I would like to see a simple breakdown of Macro % and cal burned vs cal consumed.

Would be Great if it Worked! Unfortunately, even in the Premium+ Subscription at its absolutely PREMIUM (EXPENSIVE!) price, does not work. The app consistently fails to save custom meal targets, defaulting to an even distribution. Website changes for these targets also do not transfer to the app. It does not retain links to third-party apps like Samsung Fitness, requiring daily re-linking for activity tracking. The app's calorie target remains inaccurately high compared to the web calculations.

Not everything is super intuitive and user friendly right away. It definitely took some playing with to find all the tools and info, but it makes booking classes so easy. I think the only thing it's missing is a permanent navigation bar so you can view how many classes you have left (re passes), and can quickly see to your own schedule.

The list of foods is quite complete. But the UI is VERY annoying, with all the gamification, useless slow animations and useless delay screens. There's an "Ideas & Feedback" page for Yazio that shows 1000s of users have had the same complaints for years, but Yazio is not taking it seriously. They even respond it's "by design". (Bad design!) But by far what is totally unacceptable is that Yazio counts steps double, thus skewing the results by up to 1000kcal. I'm switching to Lifesum today.
Been using it for nearly 12 years now almost daily and still love it. A really good and smart alarm app with lots of useful features, which helps you to follow your sleep statistics. Two thumbs up!
Great products, cheaper than a lot of other places, easy to use, quick shipping. I have used Vitacost for years and years. Highly recommend.

Companion has really helped me throughout my progress on Zwift. It allows me to see all of the people that I follow if they are active or not. You can also use Companion while zwifting so you can chat to people. I couldn't have gotten to where I am now if it wasn't for Zwift Companion! If you have Zwift, you NEED the Companion extension. There are many more things this app can do that I didn't mention.

I like the app. Really straightforward, easy to navigate, and looking up food and putting in macros is very simple. My only knit picky complaint is not having the ability to have a preferred unit of measurement when punching in food. I always weigh everything in grams, so it can be a tiny bit tedious having to swap cups to grams every time I put something in the food diary. Other than that, it's a solid app for nutrition and weight monitoring.
not the easiest calorie counter to use, but I'm starting to get use it and it's doing enough to suit my needs. I must point out this comment is for the free version
Totally waste of money.. they don't clear customs in india..
It wont let me sign up for this!

Love that it's easy to just log in, set a distance/time, and go for it. Super simple and quick, no weird hoops or bunch steps to just hit start and go. Def recommend to anyone trying to get into running!! :)

April 2017: I thought this app was great until 2 days ago. When I walk the timer and calorie counter works, but it is not recording my distance. Is anyone else having this problem? Please fix or tell me what I'm doing wrong. Update: It's 3 years later. The GPS STILL hasn't been fixed, and now all the settings seem to change with every step you take. I'm looking for a new app.
App used to be functional but now requires you to email a verification code to simply open it on the same phone it's always been used on

Very good for weight loss! You can save recipes for meals you make regularly, easy to find most items. I quit using this app for a while when I went off my diet plan and I recently downloaded some others and tried to use them. I ended up coming back to this app because I couldn't save recipes, couldn't find some of the regular food items I use, or the whole app seemed too complicated. I really like this app.

Way better than other fitness/calorie trackers. I've used other apps previously, but was tired of the limitations. With Cronometer, I have no issues adding new foods to my list. My family regularly consumes food from outside the US and it's so easy to add to the system. The scanning barcode feature works for most foods, but when the food is recognized I can easily just snap a couple of pics and instantly add all the nutritional information. I am so happy I was recommended this app!

This is the 2nd time I've installed the app and sadly the second time I'll uninstall it. I installed again because I love Mels Kitchen, but it crashes every time I try to scroll through anyone's recipes.

Great app, I've enjoyed using it. The customer service is great and it is a great way to get an idea of your daily intake. However, every other food app allows you to separate food by meals (breakfast, lunch, etc). Though the nutritional database is amazing, this keeps me from ranking the app any higher. I've maintained a gold membership for two years, but I can't say that it's worth it, as the only benefit I use is separating my meals.

If you thought the Fitbit app was bad, you'll really loathe this new Google version. The worst thing about this new version (and there are many of them) is the food tracking. Completely useless now. You can't add custom food, so unless you can scan the UPC, you're out of luck. I just tried to scan a protein powder that is a best selling item on Amazon, but it doesn't come up and I can't submit it or add as a custom, so I need to search other random items and find one that's close enough.

The app does as advertised but I won't give it more than 1 star until they fix the sync issue. New phone season is here and everyone who is upgrading will be stuck losing any unsynced data or, like me, be forced to use my old device to track calories until they fix the problem. It has been an issue for months now and it's honestly unacceptable. I'll be finding an alternative app if it isn't corrected soon.

It works, sometimes. Every day it asks me to log in, there's no "remember me" or anything like that. It's more of a pain to get the app running then it is to run myself. Sometimes the runs don't track, it'll stop tracking and get stuck at a distance, but it will keep time. And then often it freezes when it's "Completing my run" and I have to close the app all the way (log in again) and then the run might be there. I like the guided runs and the map, but I think it's time for a different app

The app is great. The features allow you to pool multiple exercises in one place to track your progress. However, sometimes the GPS is a little bit off. I have ridden side by side with another person using the app and our results have been different by as much as a half a mile on a 6 mile or higher bike ride. The same for running with the app. Or I can do the same ride several days in a row and get different distances on each ride.
Not happy with this... I had daily burn in the past on my old iPhone but wanted to start using it again on my S4... Just a bad experience.
The image does not appear when you click on the item you are trying to purchase. I want to see what I buying.. kind of pointless. Everything is always sold out ( rolls eyes).

The app is ok, but hasn't changed a whole lot. Inconsistent foods from people that don't fill in all info is a big problem. It feels overpriced when comparable options are out there. They had some sort of $59.99 offer that I declined when I downloaded and signed into the app but then I thought about it and thought I'd give it a try, but instead when I went to sub it kept pushing my towards the $99 plan. So now I'm giving Garmin Connect+ a try instead.
keeps u very aware of ur intake

Great app to help start running, encouraging guided runs by enthusiastic coaches. The app measures distance, pace and other metrics when combined with a wearable. I like that it's free and the large variety of different runs available. I used the guided runs when first starting out. This motivated me enough to keep going, once I was running more regularly and clocking more k's I would just do distance or timed runs and put on an audio book to zone out.

This app no longer works with Android. Used to be great, not anymore. I've done every type of trouble shooting to fix the GPS tracking to no avail. You know it's an issue when there are multiple pages in their FAQs addressing the issue. It was great, no longer.

October 2022 edit Still loving the app HOWEVER, I'm finding that when casting workouts to my TV they aren't saving in progress history. I just updated it, so hopefully, this issue doesn't persist. The app worked well until recently. It doesn't record my distance and trail correctly. No bad apples here. Great app and great functionality. I also got this as a F*** you to apple. I hope every cent my use generates help towards the legal costs in your trademark dispute with that bloodsucking cooperation.

Great customer service. Mehdi responded within 2 minutes to my message and fixed my problem. I like how streamlined the app is. Give us the old Fitbit app back! This switch to Google completely ruined all the Fitbit features I use literally every single day. I'm especially upset with the food log... Best workout app I've used. Easy, fast, pre programmed. If you don't want to pay for the app stronglufts has a 5x5 spreadsheet on their site you can open in excel and docs. love the device... but constantly have to force close the app and restart my phone. no other app on my phone has this issue.

Because of a sync loop bug, the app has used over 12 GB of mobile data in just a few days, completely exhausting my monthly allowance and costing me money. Background data for heavy syncing should be set to 'Wi-Fi only' by default during updates to prevent user data plan depletion. Please fix this bug.

I'm not a runner, but the app helps me track my workouts and approximate calorie burn. 2/16- had to reduce my rating. There are too many times that the app will not track my treadmill runs accurately. Like today. 1st mile was my usual around 13 min mile. Then, the app has me running 0.71 at over 22 min mile. How?! I never stopped! Fing irritating. Same issues with indoor runs as noted 2 years ago. Very frustrating when you change the app to indoors.

My experience of Zwift was absolutley awful, pairing devices was almost impossible, after initial contact i was told to use the companion app and put my phone to aeroplane mode as this work around would work but alas even this stopped working after 2 or 3 weeks. I have since moved back to a competitor indoor training tool which has paired to all devices with ease. 1 star as unable to give 0 stars
find this planner very useful, easy to search and a convenient way to keep my grocery list

I do like this app (I am a Pro user and pay for it) but it has a lot of room for improvement. When things are wrong in the database they don't make it easy to fix - you usually have to input the nutrition facts manually by 100 g (which is not always the serving size) which would require numerous manual calculations. Many other apps have the capability to just take photos of the nutrition facts where it pulls data. Recipes also should be shareable and add other serving units like cooked weight.

Loving the improvements I've seen over the years returning to this app. Now it's a daily habit. It's great for motivation. *UPDATE- 5 down to 3 stars. No longer as great. paywall and ads. Also used it for 10+ yrs and one thing that's always bothered me is that the weight progess graph isn't more user friendly. gimme a slider! *2ND UPDATE- 3 down to 2 stars. kept logging me out every 2 weeks, even when I would use it every three days or less.

The interface isn't as user friendly as the original fitbit app. I'm still trying to navigate through it. I want to track my sleeping; even though I wore it last night while I slept, it produced no data. I don't see where to activate this feature or do stuff like I used to, such as logging eating habits.

I have a Google phone (Pixel 3) and one would think it would yield very good results with Google fit. Not only is the map always off and doesn't track correctly, but the length is different (2.1 miles vs. 3.7 miles on the exact same path I walk every single day). The calorie counts are totally off even though I put my data in. Now it also started tracking a bike ride while I was at home and didn't touch my phone. SUPER disappointed in this :( will download a different ap

Interface designed by programmers, not users. Very difficult to navigate between yesterday and today (for those of use who don't enter all the data for dinner). Recent foods don't include ALL recent foods with no rhyme or reason. Recent foods are named differently from in the database [like Acme chicken breast vs. Chicken Breast (Acme)] which makes scanning for recent foods difficult. Can't enter my own foods and recipes. A clunker of a program like it was written 20 to 30 years ago.
I love this app. Tracking my exercise and sleep makes me feel in control of my health. I can't wait to sync my exercise watch daily.

Adding food manually for tracking is near impossible. The only way to do it (say if your food item is not listed in the database and there is no barcode to scan) is to verbally add calories and other macros without being able to designate them to a food or meal. Which i have still yet to figure out. You basically need a second food/macro tracking app to connect to Google Health. Disappointed. Wanted an all in one.

MFP has a great database of food. I appreciate the new UI updates with the cleaner and simpler UI. However I do find several tasks much harder to complete. The multi-select feature within meals is now gone, which makes any duplicate entries a pain to delete, since you need to select one by one. The option to copy single items within meals is also gone. The nutrient view when navigated to from a meal also defaults to a weekly view, which is odd, not sure if that's a bug.

This app is good for basic nutrition tracking, but it has a few flaws that prompted me to switch to MyFitnessPal. One of them is that sometimes a barcode is not recognized, which means I have to enter all the information manually, or the barcode scans but the item's calories are grossly overestimated. Secondly, I have not been able to intuitively find an easy way to add calories and skip the gaff.

This app did work, then yesterday(11/6/25) it wouldn't allow me to get out of the shopping list. I uninstalled and reinstalled the app, then realized its because the screen is placing the buttons for "home", "plans", etc... underneath my home and back button on my Android phone.

So tell me something.. why can my maps app track every activity.. walking.. kayaking.. etc.. to the minute.. but this useless fitness app can't track automatically when I'm walking or doing any other activity? Baffles me! Before you say anything.. yes, all settings are enabled just fine! Also, please bring back the ability to connect my HRM instead of using my watch!
App looks interesting but seems extremely unstable on my Nexus 7; constantly crashes

- Easy to use - Syncs to calendar - You can explore options around the area - You can add staff and places to favourites so the schedules are easily accessible - You can view deals and pricing and keep track of your passes and expirey dates - Keeps track of your history so you can rebook - You get notifications if there are any changes to your class - It's neat and tidy, makes you feel very organised. Love the app!

Haven't used it in a while and don't even recognize it now. It was so SIMPLE for the things I needed to do. Now it's visually VERY cluttered and the greed is on full display: a lot that was free is now "premium," and the upsell is more aggressive than a used car dealer!   What used to feel like it supported MY goals is much, MUCH more about supporting theirs. PRIVATE EQUITY RUINS EVERYTHING, and what a great and thorough example MyFitnessPal has become. Don't like it, wouldn't recommend it.

My wife has been using StrongLifts for about a year off and on. We decided to start lifting again and wanted to be on the same program. I downloaded this app, loaded my info and started. The biggest plus for me is the rest timer. In the past, I didn't rest long enough between reps. I also like the interface and on the fly editing.
it's very easy to use. it has an excellent listing of food. the secret is you need to want to lose and stay motivated.

lose it premium is half off for a year. process asks 80 questions. Just let people use your damn app. To the reply: I understand the point of onnboarding but there still needs to be an option that says "Let's stick to the basics" where you enter in age, height, weight and target weight you had so many questions I was just trying to answer the bottom one and skipping ahead to the point i almost signed up for premium. then you had me spin a wheel twice then we were finally at the login page.

It’s really good, simple to use for the most part. It really helps with my adhd. You track meals, snacks, calories, nutrition, AND daily water intake. You can create your own recipes which I enjoy I just wish it was simpler. EDIT: I KNOW....you have to manually type in the ingredient portions for custom recipes...the point is the way it’s designed is honestly stupid. All that extra work the user has to do when it could just automatically be a single serving measurement.

Decent app, but the battery drain was ridiculous. My phone and watch would be at 100% at night and in the morning they'd be in the red and the app wouldn't record correctly. Was hoping for a replacement for the sleep on SHealth, but this wasn't it. Edit: I don't use sound recording so this isn't it, and I reduced all the settings to maximize battery life, as per instructions. Still drained my watch and phone. Glad it works for others, but it didn't work for me. Edit 2: not a useful app for me.
I can't even buy stuff because it keeps saying, address is not the same as billing, which it's the same one I've used for a lot of websites, so a waste of money.

Gives you a "free" trial that converts I to a subscription without telling the price upfront. The only visible price is "0€". Also the "explore the app" option doesn't let you explore the app but tries to funnel you into an annual subscription without telling the user the price.

Did the free trial. Calm sent me an email for 40% off. Clicked, subscribed, was charged full price. Took forever to manage to send them an inquiry. They've been "looking into it" for 8 days now. After six days, I emailed again to cancel and get a refund. Received another generic email that they are experiencing significant delays in response times. So, obviously, they don't care about customer service at all. I guess I'm going to have to do a chargeback.

Was a happy paying customer until the horrible new user interface came about a month ago. I can't track my nutrients as easily anymore without having to click 3 buttons deep. Also, have to manually delete 1 food item at a time instead of being able to mass delete like before. Go back to the old user interface. Will unsubscribe soon if this keeps up.

I've been a Daily Burn member for a while now, but have never been able to log into this. It keeps telling me my password is wrong, but I'm able to log into the Fault Burn app. I thought maybe this was a seperate thing, so I tried to create an account.  I get a message advising me that I'm already registered.  So frustrating. Please fix this!
App isn't working properly. I click on the search tab and it takes a while before i can search for anything. Sometimes i can't get it to search.

I'm continually in the search for an app that can track my cycling workouts. I've tried most of them and this app is just the worst. Generally, I get to about 3 miles and it shuts down or doesn't track my miles. Before you say it's my phone, I've used the same app on at least two other Android's and had the same issue. For a company with so many really awesome products that I use daily, this one is just...plain...awful.

I've been using it for a week, and I'm really happy with the features and the UI. They don't have that many foods in Serbia, BUT you can create your own items - brilliant. You just need to snap 2 pics of the product, and it automatically populates the nutritional info, based on the pics, with surprising accuracy. CS is also great! The ads are not intrusive at all, and it's honestly a breath of fresh air. Which just makes me want to actually pay for it now! And I will subscribe.
I like it. But please update to show the cost per serving. It's a very convenient and money saving detail.
This app is like a cherry on top of Zwift. It's fun to browse other users, and send encouragement. I look forward to searching users. Typing messages can be a little difficult on the bike.
Slow to load anything. It took me 30 minutes to make an order for 3 items. You took a great app and destroyed it.

WHOOP will change your life and add tremendous discipline and routine. However, I feel that it overestimates things like calories, steps etc by a bit so you need to also mentally sync up with it and adjust accordingly.

Forced me to use this to keep my Endomondo stats. What a garbage app. Can't set a goal for a workout, can't view stats over time. I was excited for the workouts until I tried one and it was a guy just demonstrating a work out then going to the next exercise. Totally useless to me. Why kill a great app for this garbage?

It initially was ok, however lately I have noticed that it is totally unreliable. While I understand that it might have an issue counting steps if it is in a backbag obviously, it also doesn't count step while actively having Google maps open and also ignores the distance. Simultaneously it logs walking to the toilet as "afternoon walk" for 1 minute which baffles me and then ignores a 1km walk.

I've been using Yazio for 3+ years, and I've always loved it. I'm also a Premium user. But I'm writing a negative review cause I fully agree with everyone that the animations are way too much, specifically the yeti skiing. I'm personally a Yazio loyalist, so I'm not planning to switch for now, but please listen to the reviews that the animations are too much, take forever to skip over & I find myself spam tapping the screen to get over all the unnecessary pop ups. And no, they aren't motivating.

Food and drink log feature is garbage. There's no ability to add new items to log. The "scan barcode" feature typically finds the wrong item, or not at all. Unable to manually enter caloric intake or macros. All food or drink items already listed in the database are the only items available (and lacking). The other trackers are fine, I guess. I haven't used them much due to my frustration with trying to track my nutrition.

Love it . Highly accurate in comparison to its competition . To those saying they can noy find batteries for old devices that isn't true. They're quite easy to find. It's a waterproof device up to a certain point , and can long periods of time without charging. Impressed with how accurate it is in every aspect from tracking sleep, work outs, step etc . To me it's worth paying for.

This app is trash now. It was just the Fitbit app but now it has Google AI shoved into it and it got rid of a lot of features. The biggest one for me is quick calories being gone. It was a fast and easy way to enter calories into the food log without having to search for it. Helpful for small restaurants where you don't know the calories and they aren't in the system but you can estimate how many calories it was and enter it quickly with quick calories. Unnecessary changes make this a bad app.

App won't even let me log in. I claimed the 40% off offer on Instagram, but after I paid for it, it wouldnt let me attach it to an account. Now, I'm pretty sure I have a recurring subscription that I can't cancel because I was dumb and didn't go through the Play store. Their support is also useless and has not been able to help me in any way. Do not use this app. Don't use the site either, it's not any better.

This used to be my favorite diet/body measurements tracking app.  However, they've went too far trying to push users into their overpriced paid subscriptions.  When I first saw the TV ads after not using the app for a while, I was like "Cool, they're finally going to get some visibility and rightly make some more money."  I didn't know they also had plans to destroy the free version of the app, stripping away countless features and access to data previously recorded.  I hope this company crashes and burns.

This is by far the BEST Sleep Tracking and Alarm Clock App on the market. Not only is the sleep tracking extremely accurate, especially with using it in conjunction with my Galaxy S3 Watch (additional Galaxy Watch App install required to use with this App) but the wake up feature, in my opinion, is the best feature of the App. It wakes you up during an "optimal time" , making sure not to wake you during REM sleep so you wake up feeling refreshed and ready to go for the day. Would highly recommend.

Normally this is a great app, however the last couple of weeks it has not been working since there was an update. I have been having to order through the website which is not as user friendly on mobile. I have a Samsung Galaxy S21 Ultra.

I use this app often to schedule classes, and it works well. However, over the last few months the calendar scheduling is completely broken. There's something very wrong with the dates and the items that show up under that date. Sometimes they are correct, sometimes the listing is for a different date than the one selected. if I refresh the view while on a specific date, the options display different items each time, apparently cycling between multiple days.

Installed ok on my Samsung S7, but would not configure. Hang on "Step 1 of 5" when it tried to pick a few great workouts for me.  Phone reboot and reinstall of the app did not help.  Two weeks later and after an Android update I tried again. Now it seems to work very well. Today, for the first time, I used it and I didn't experience any problems.   Changing my score from 1 to 4.

At first it was tricky to set up the definitions to get data from other sources, but it started to work well. Now, it’s a mess. Capturing activity and exercise data takes ages, it deletes data for no reason, and it duplicates, triples, and even quadruples meal entries on the same day and on previous days. I’ve spent hours deleting duplicate entries across 67 days out of the 72 I’ve been using the app. If these errors aren’t fixed, I won’t be renewing the subscription.

I never used an app from a major supplier that is so disfunctional. The last time I used it in 2018 the tracking UI was terrible. l installed this version today and immediately receive an invalid UN and PW message. All permissions are enabled. Clearing data didn't help. Works fine on Website. I'm beyond frustrated and uninstalling the app. I'll use the Website. Very disappointed...again.

Love this app, it's brilliant. I have lost 3 kgs now in 3 weeks and the workouts are dynamic and quite challenging. Just one major flaw though, which is why I gave it 4 stars: when I tap on "End Workout" accidentally, unlike many other training apps, it took me straight to the end of the workout without asking for any confirmation and then I had to do the workout all over again. A lot of us would really appreciate it if the development team could look into this issue and resolve it asap.

I've only used the free version. It took a bit to find the settings that I needed to adjust but once that was done its super easy to use. The bar code scanner works well and if it's not already in the database its easy to add. Only down side is the widgets could use some work. Using the 5x6 icon home page on my phone the widgets get wonky and take up an extra row with the bottom one being completely blank. Also a +1 water widget would be very helpful.
This one app has been motivating me for years together. Recording and measuring your workouts is a great way to keep them consistent. Love this app. All thanks to app makers. wonderful app

It WAS decent, but not perfect (it always seriously underestimated my distance when I went for a run, but I have a Garmin for that). However, it has now stopped working properly. It displays my step count and heart points as zero, no matter how many steps I've actually walked/run. I've tried clearing the cache, clearing the data, uninstalling and reinstalling, rebooting my phone, all to no avail. Based on comments on the Google Community Help page, I'm not the only one with this issue. I'll change my review if/when this issue is fixed.
I found this app too be just ok. I wish that there were some more visuals and graphs like the ones you see on the daily burn website (which I love more than most things.)

This is not a complaint about the mobile app overall this about how this app has UI that actually works but the whole experienced Is ruined by the atrocious UI of the pc application of zwift. I'm running the pc app on current gen hard ware and I frequently lose Bluetooth connection. That's besides the truly breaking thing that it will just crash which is fine IF I COULD AT LEAST LOAD BACK INTO THE WORKOUT I WAS ON. Until they fix this I would not give them your money, or not do training plans.

I like it but needs some more organization. For example,I use this for myself I have no children but sometimes i have to sift through the kids stuff to find what i want. So I wish there was a way to divide the children content from the adult stuff. It would make it a bit more convient when I go to scroll through the sleep stories at night. Currently there is not a way to view all stories minus kid stories. What would work is a setting letting you exclude child content completely from the app.
app is ok and works, but for some reason if the watch is disconnected from phone app, then it's very painful to connect it back. there is no improvement from Garmin in this area

Enjoy the basic benefits of this app. Can set it for multiple different fitness options and different bikes. The auto pause function can be slow to pick up movement beginning again if you are doing lots of stop start like walking a dog but otherwise functional. That's possibly more a function of the phone's gps anyway

This app is really helpful with keeping of track of your weights so that you don't have to do it all yourself. The paid version will tell you what warmups to do as well but even the free version is super helpful for getting into the stronglifts 5x5. The videos on youtube have plenty of information on the warmups you should be doing.

This app is insanely disappointing. I have issues with it connecting to Bluetooth, when it does it doesn't sync. I absolutely hate the notebook feature. It always blocks me from actually doing what I want. There's lots of random errors in the data. This is clearly meant for more of an endurance athlete, not a weight training athlete.

I absolutely LOVE this app. It's been such a great tool to keep my family in healthy eating. I love browsing recipes, you know, if I have some chicken in the freezer I don't know what to do with, and being able to just add the ingredients to my shopping list... I've been pressuring my family members to use this, too, because I want my "What's Cookin'?" feed to be FULL, but they haven't jumped on this train yet. It's awesome to be able to search for low carb recipes that take 30 minutes or less in the french cuisine for dinner. The search parameters are sure to keep everyone pleased. The only thing I wish we could do, especially at this time of year, is create multiple shopping lists. Things pop into my head for gifts and I want to add them to a christmas shopping list. I have a specific event I'm cooking for, and I don't want it to mix with my home grocery list. You know, stuff like that. Anyway, MUCH LOVE to the Prepear team. You guys ROCK. Go hug a kitten for me. ;)

I love this app! I've used it for years and always come back when I try something new. However, I am no longer able to add photos to my recipes unless I take a new picture. I usually take pictures in advance, while making the recipe, and then manually enter the recipe in later when I'm not busy. It's a total hassle to have to enter in the recipe, not be able to use the pic I took and then have to wait until I make it again and hopefully remember to take another pic for the recipe.
Unusable crash bug On Pixel 8 Pro running Android 16, Cronometer crashes within a few seconds of opening. This problem has persisted for a while. Updating score when this bug is fixed.

I hate the new update/change to Google health (Fitbit). The old version was a much cleaner display and easier to see everything being tracked on the main page. Food tracking was a lot easier. Now for whatever reason I can't add percentages or decimals for serving sizes. It makes me round up to the nearest whole number. A one cup serving makes a big difference compared to a .5 cup serving. Unfortunately, now I need to find a new app for tracking and am debating upgrading my watch as well.

Please give us the ability to edit certain activity attributes. For example, if I walk or run on an indoor track, the mileage is way off. If I'm strength training and don't have the time to correct the exercise for each set, I can't correct it in the app later. Please also add a lap counter for walking/running around a track. Also sometimes the app will record a walk or run when i haven't done one, but it won't let me delete it.
great way to get running metrics (never used it for anything else). Keeps a log of all activities, you can run reports for all different metrics to see progress over time.
I'm a brand new beginner bike rider & this app keeps track of how far I've ridden, for how long, my speed & calories burned! This is the first time I've used it & I love it so much! 💕

Not user friendly. This app is difficult to navigate and I have to search for awhile to find what I'm looking for. I can't see expired passes so I don't know if they are used up or just not showing up in the app. I used to get notifications when passes were expiring but now I don't (though I was told this is a feature that has to be turned on by the vendor). Honestly, if my yoga studio didn't insist I use this app for payment, I would delete it.
Easy to understand. Great selection of products. Cost less. My go to for health products. I didn't notice any ads in the app at all.
This is a good app, it's very basic and does basic tracking. But the entire purpose of this app is defeated by Google owning Fitbit. Hopefully the merge between these apps happens soon!

Use Endomondo several years. But it often stop record unexpectedly on my current phone (Android 9) + no battery optimization with it. Cannot record a complete run while other tracking record APP works well at the same time. It's annoying. Please resolve it.
The app works horrible on android. No pictures, can't renew my cart, it doesn't let me go add things to cart. Or when it does items just dissappear in minutes. Horrible app experience
Won't accept my valid info for login. Makes it worthless. I will change rating if they can fix it
great app when it's working but it freezes at the end of every run. I know it's free but I expected better from Nike

I usually love this app, but in the last few days when I'm tracking a walk and trying to end it, it no longer shows as something that's active (not in my notifications or even in the app itself). It'll show it as an event that just completed. Hours later, I'll discover that it's still tracking, and my 45-minute walk has been going on for 16 hours.
Just started cycling. I love this app! It lets me know how far and how long I ride and if I could afford it, I'd pay for premium. Amazing app!
First time I apply the free gift but I thought I will change the gift. But when I removed that gift then no option came for free gift.

Unbelievably difficult to use! Every time I try to use this app, I get an error message or it freezes. I've tried to add info to wallet, Update address, order a class package. So far NOTHING has worked. Makes me want to NEVER use a business affiliated with this app. Absolute frustration!

It works, but glitchy. It does help me fall asleep faster. I use the Rain/ Nature sounds. However, when I first started using the app, the sound would play on a loop, which I like, now sometimes it loops or shuts off after about 5 min or so. I've set up timers hoping it would play the length of the timer, but it still stops. Bring a continuous play option. I use this to fall asleep, it's hard when I have to keep hitting play when it stops. Idk what why it just stops randomly.

Glad I joined. Of course I'd love for this platform to be cheaper, but it gives you a great variety of exercises and experiences. I'm training for several different events coming up and there is no doubt in my mind I'll have the fitness needed to complete them all. Thanks to the developers and all the pros that participate in zwift. It gives me the needed motivation to get into the A class of racers. My goal is to get to 5w/kg for an hour by next April aka shoot for the stars and hit the moon

Google UI is awful. I miss Fitbit's dev style already. Please stop pushing the "material you" look on everything. it's oversaturated and pretty much the first thing they teach you in design style courses. This "in-your-face" style makes it hard to pay attention to any of the stats. Cardio goals and sleep goals are also not tracking the same either, and have been moved so it's a PitA to find.

Vitacost is a great marketplace. But the app is horrible. I need to login every time I open the app. Don't you want to keep me engaged? And some searches just spin forever. You're going to leave me no choice but to find another retailer.

I honestly loved this app, until I got a new phone and suddenly my subscription is nowhere to be found. I've logged in with the original account and everything. Tried uninstalling and reinstalling with no luck. And then when I looked at the prices for the premium on both my old phone and the new phone, I see a $10 difference in the price. It's really upsetting that an app I paid so much for suddenly doesn't work just because I changed phones. It reset my account on both phones too...
I love dailyburn and have used it for years. I just don't like how this app only allows you to select from certain serving sizes instead of typing it

For Strength Training, your WearOS counterpart has more functionality than your actual phone app? I cant manually tell it what reps I did in the Phone App? Also, it keeps track of my reps, but I cant really do anything with that data. I cant look up easily what I did last time for a specific lift. I cannot do a 1 rep max calculation. It really isnt very helpful to have information with no ability to do anything with it, or search it.  I would like the ability to have a list of all my exercises, what I did last time, what my 1 rep max would be, and other information like 80%, 90%, etc. If you are going to add weight lifting tracking, you need to add helpful weight lifting statistics. Maybe a drop down for if it was too easy, too hard, just right, etc.

I have used several nutrition trackers in the past and Cronomoter is by far the best... Leaves others in the dust. The Daily page is perfect with dynamic stats at the top. The app is super easy to use, and search has yielded every item I've looked up. Tabs under search make easy switching from "All" to "Favorites." I would give it 10 stars if I could.

Very good stories and meditations. I used the free version for about a year before I decided to get the annual subscription. It's really worth every penny. I listen to their stories when I go to bed, their music when studying, or their meditations when I need to take a break at least once per day. I would suggest that they they bring more artists with lower timbre voices. Those do much better on sleep stories.

False milage and pace. pls update Did all the updates. miss my nike watch. This app is not accurate, the mileage is always off, the pace timing is off. Which makes for a frustrating app to use when training. I've updated the app all that jazz, still always off on my runs and times. I hate when I run and it won't save my run, spins and spins then it disappeared. Just ran the other day, 10 mile run finished it didn't load to my mileage really getting annoyed. I had even screen spotted my splits?

This has become my alarm app for about a year now. It has been pretty reliable. I've had only a few days the alarm didn't go off and I still do not know why (it has not been a problem for over a month now without me doing anything). I like the cockcrow ringtone. I just wish there were more variety of cockcrow ring tones. I really like rich feature/'detailed configurations' around alarm. Recent UI changes also made it much easier and more pleasant to use.

It became stable through the years but still lots missing. No way to see a list of the events you signed up, no form of is search for zwifters making it funny business to try to follow someone with a common name and other little things here and there.

Scan the barcode - app comes up with a wrong result - I fix it and enter correct food item details for that barcode, save it as a consumed item in the food diary. All is well, right? NO! Scan same barcode - app gives wrong result AGAIN - I search for the food item that I've literally just entered and saved for this barcode earlier - and it's not in the list! Newly entered food items don't get saved, corrections to nutrition tables for items don't get saved. Useless now, can't track properly.

I've been using this app for several years. love the overall attempt to modernize the interface. Few unexplained glitches like why you have to log in several times to get in. or why you can't use a photo from your gallery. when you select gallery, you don't see anything, it's just a blank screen. Sometimes you log in and your activities are missing. Can you address those issues? tx
I love Vitacost and the app. Wish barcode reader worked like it used to, seems like doesn't read everything like it did before but still great app! Easy to find, compare and reorder.

I've literally been using this app for years and recently it has stopped tracking my distance and route, only the duration. I have checked all the app settings and my phone settings to make sure sharing my location wasn't the issue and it's still not working properly. So frustrating. I've stopped using the app and have moved onto another because this is so annoying.

Last update sucked. The new format makes everything more confusing. It's gotten harder to go back and log things you've forgotten without getting the days mixed up. It's too easy to inadvertently log yesterday's meals on today's log. The counter for your calorie allotment was much easier to read at a glance. Seems like a huge format change that improved nothing.

Not worth. Big waste of money. Inaccurate data and faulty equipment. Business model is predatory, gets you guys locked in subscription with no option to opt out. I requested a new battery and the delay is over 7 weeks which will be a waste of monthly subscription. 1 star because customer service is friendly but not helpful. Item is low quality but price is high due to their marketing spend.

I am super disappointed with the most recent UI changes. I took a break from tracking for a few months and switched from Apple to Samsung during that time. It was such a pain setting up my new account with the forced data entry to start. Then I immediately re-subscribed to premium thinking the worst was over. I wish I didn't pay for a year in advance because the streak pop-up, goal setting, etc you have to click through is SO annoying. Please give the option to toggle those off for quick entries

Abysmal. What a waste of three hours. Could not get the app to connect to my Wattbike Atom or control the bike via the Wattbike hub whilst the Zwift companion app was open. There needs to be some clear guidance around the order in which things should be switched on. When my bike did show connected within the Zwift program there was no signal. All in all a truly depressing experience,.

I loved this app until the past few days. Like others have mentioned, the Bluetooth keeps dropping my hr monitor. And I can't view all of the meet ups past a few hours. The screens keep spinning. It's almost worthless now.

Paying user, the app fundamentally does its job, database top. But: -the app should automatically update the calorie goal weekly with the trend of the logged weight and other metrics (would be a much better use of the AI, instead of a very bad tracker) -the "deactivate tips for 2 weeks" is here finally, but the UI is annoying, I have to click "I'm committed" every single time I log in, even on the same day!! Using the widget helps, but guys, be serious!!! Because of the UI I'm looking elsewhere.

I have just purchased the pro version of the app. so there are few things that could be improved. when choosing what I ate, it would be great to see pictures of the food attached, it would be much easier. Fddb has it, you could take a look. also, what really irritates me, that Steps are counted maybe with the phone (?), but I usually leave it there laying and my steps are being calculated through Garmin watch. I connected the watch with the app, but it doesnt synchronise. such a shame..
I've been using Sleep as Android for more than a decade. It's reliable and helps me to understand how well I'm sleeping. Healthy sleep is a important as healthy nutrition.
You need to fix it.

Well designed, very intuitive. Great company with a vast inventory of excellent products at very competitive prices. This app produces incorrect searches. It doesn't sync well with the web site. It doesn't execute a REORDER the proper way. I should have just placed orders via the web site. This app has screwed up everything. Have to downgrade the Vitacost experience to just 3-stars. SORRY!

How difficult is it to have a landscape mode?! It was requested years ago. Also, why do I need the main app to get into character info? That should be in here along with the drop shop. Without needing to start a ride I'll then have to scrap. Let me setup things BEFORE choosing to ride.

I can't say enough good about this app. My boyfriend is blown away by the intelligence behind the functionality - there's not much it can't do. Now, just waiting on the upgrade so it can make me breakfast. 😜 Just kidding, but I do love the lullabies and being able to track my sleeping. I have insomnia and have trouble getting restful sleep. This has helped me in multiple ways keep track of where I am. I love the "get to bed" reminder so i have time for at least plenty of down time.

Great to keep track of mileage. Doing a 100 mile bike challenge and this is perfect along with my Fitbit. You will love it! Update 4/5/2022 Still a great app, love using it still 5 stars, however it would be nice if I could share my workouts with my Fitbit friends. Any chance of that happening? When I bring up "share" a lot of nonessential apps come up but not any fitness apps. Thanks!
app won't open just displays a black screen
Installed, uninstalled, and installed again but app keeps crashing as soon as I open it

If you could stop spamming me with unnecessary banners after I log my meal that'd be great. Just add an option where I can disable all those "motivating" full screen banners please. Also there's an extremely annoying bug where the app asks me every time I'm logging my meal in which country I'm shopping... Update: it's been almost a year and the country selection bug is still present - although less often than it used to be. I guess they added some sort of a limit to how often the user is asked.

I'm using the paid version so not OG 5x5 and I like this flexibility. Overall it's good. For some reason I can't lower exercises down to BW anymore. Some days I use weights and sometimes I just do bodyweight due to disability. But I'm unable to input 0 weight. Another thing is on calendar view you can only see one workout per day. I often combine different routines on the same day but it's not reflected on the calendar. Not too important but hopefully it could be fixed some day.

Update: Still slow, clunky, and overall sucks. This app is buggy, charges your card but does not place the order. Also pictures don't load with the product description. Please fix this app and make it more user-friendly. Be sure to include some way to contact customer support.
This is a monstrosity. Why would you replace the old app with this horrible, website looking thing. Bring back the old one

Loading errors for everything! Watch asks me in the morning, if I'm waking up. If I just wanted to check my sleepscore and go back to sleep, I am forced to choose 'yes' to see the stats. If I then go back to sleep 3min later, it will not be logged. I have to manually navigate all the way to my stats view, to find Naps and then tap it to start the nap. I just want a button to "continue sleeping", bec. the "nap" won't be counted as part of night sleep and I get a warning that my naps are too long.
I can't login to my account using the app

I used to love this app but the latest updates are not nice for the user experience. You can't see a preview of the workout, so I can figure out adjustments I need to make. Also not able to download workout anymore, makes it hard to workout offline or without data/wifi. The most frustrating part is when I finish a programme workout and it is logged, then the next time I log on to the app, my progress is gone.
LOVE the whoop band and app! reliable, informative and innovative. the AI integration is so genius and helpful giving me personalized insights daily!

I absolutely love this app. It's infinitely better than the site. Easy to use, doesn't drain my battery, runs smoothly, and haven't had it crash or anything. My only complaint is that the app freaks out after booking a class. It'll display bookable sessions for other days so you have to refresh the schedule after every single booking. It's annoying.
It helps to count my mile per minute, but the GPS track is wonky. It rarely shows the right route

I've found this app to cover pretty much everything you need, from focused workouts to more general ones, short and long, equipment or none, intense and easy. the only tweak I'd like is to be able to refine based on type of equipment, i.e. so you could find ones with kettlebell only, as i tend to use this when away from home and provision varies.

I like how this app is kiwi friendly, meaning the barcode scanner knows alot of the foods we find at the shops. It also gives you a reasonable amount of daily calories, some apps start you sooo low that you're never gonna stick with...

After being a fitbit customer for 10 years and having premium for the last few years, this app is absolutely terrible. The AI assistant is constantly wrong and dangerously suggests the wrong advice / exercises. It's so buggy it is unusable. And now all my run data has been changed to "workout", and I lost all my run related metrics. Absolutely unacceptable. Google can do better but refuses to do so.

Considering the number of people that use it....its not a very intuitive, user friendly app. Designers need to drag someone in off the street and watch them trying to figure it out. Actually, zwiftpower is waaay worse. Sorry to be so negative.

MapMyRide is much improved! MapMyRide has recently fixed a lot in the app. MapMyRide now pauses and unpauses quicker when using the Auto-Pause making calculations more accurate. The speedometer lag it had before seems to be fixed now! The only issue I still see now is when we finish and save the workout. It takes forever to sync if we rode a long ride. I only write one sentence and I have to keep checking to see if it saved my workout. I do recommend using MapMyRide!

For over a year I have not been able to save recipes I create. After entering all of my items, with a combination of scanning, searching the database or adding a new item...every single time the app gives an error. This is not a cheap app and this feature should work. A quick google or reddit search shows tons of people have this problem. Updated rating as recipes now work finally.
Hello I have had daily burn for 2 months now. It is awesome! But it will not let me log into the tracker app. PLEASE HELP
Clear easy logging of food. Macros and nutrition shown daily or weekly. Loads of foods on Barcode scanner and really easy to add if product is not on there ready for next time.

I like the app overall it helps me keep track of the time as I am doing it however for the deadlift portion of the workouts it is only supposed to be one set of deadlifts and it is set up for five sets so pretty much every workout you wind up failing because you don't do the other four sets which are not supposed to be there. If you can just update that this would be a five-star review.

It's nice to feel good. That's about it. Changed phones and no way to transfer my history to continue to track. Basically like starting completely over. Limited windows for you to review graphs.
