Bubble graph automatically for month. No month to month tracking. I could go on and on. Nice week visual that's about it in a nutshell.

First of all, I truly want to thank for making the app available in my country, even though it is not in the country listing. The application does deliver what it is intended for. One thing that I would love to add, is that some workouts don't have cool downs. I would truly appreciate it if all the workouts could end with brief cool down. Thank you & peace.

The app is easy to navigate and I've been ordering supplements from Vitacost for about 20 years. I work in Healthcare and refer patients to Vitacost weekly. I love having the app so I can place an order quickly wherever I am.

This free app and variety of workout programs help motivate and keep me accountable, especially in world of pandemic and lockdowns. Feeling more fit and healthy than a year ago at age 50! Great way to set fitness goals. Even use Nike Running app sometimes with coaches talking you through jogs. Excellent meal tips too. Thank you, Nike! Please don't leave me!
It's alright, but not what I want in a meal planner app. Too focused on buying other people's meal plans, rather than creating your own.

Came back to use this app again, and I love it even more. I only wish that there were a setting where you can say whether the exercise is on a machine or not - a machine doesn't have "plates" per se. Also incredible amount of exercises; but please add more over time, and videos as well!

Good app, like the barcode scanning, actual vs goals on macros/nutrients and others, weight tracking and not being tied to a large corporations with its ads, simple easy to use interface. Don't like that if you eat something for breakfast then you can't add it for dinner without searching for it again and wish the recipe entry was easier.

I've been using this app since 2011. I currently have a 2,327 day streak. I can understand making some features paid, like barcode scanning, net carbs and other things. Have to make some money. However, this recent user interface update is awful. Has completely ruined the app imo. You got rid of the diary button, replacing it with what looks to be some Ai slop paid feature. You can no longer edit meals, allowing you to copy to another day, or even save certain things. Just completely awful.

This is a good app with a lot of good workouts which I really enjoy. The premium workouts are great, even if there are not a lot of 'programs', I think you could get through most of them in a few months, considering even if you want to do all of them! There are major glitches in the app though. My app freezes (as others have mentioned), the sync to Google Fit has never worked properly, making me have to log workouts manually (which is annoying). The fact that you can't use casting to put the workouts onto a bigger screen is a huge misstep as well. They are doable from a phone/tablet, but I find myself having to reposition the phone based on what kind of movement is being done (on the floor vs. upright) to make sure I can follow and have proper form.

Works about 5% of the time. I REALLY wish it did, but it rarely stays connected. If it does stay connected then you freeze on the map or either your messages/ride ons never actually send or work. You can't use your power ups or anything. Don't even bother with it and just enjoy the riding. I think they would rather you pay real money for the controllers or their bike unfortunately. Zwift doesn't care about their customers or this would be fixed ASAP. Instead its been an ongoing issue.

Google’s transition from Fit/Fitbit to Google Health is a complete mess right now. Important health metrics like blood pressure and glucose feel half-finished. I can manually log glucose, but there’s no useful history view or trends screen. Blood pressure still requires awkward workarounds through older apps like Google Fit or third-party apps. Health Connect itself seems powerful, but the ecosystem is fragmented, confusing, and badly explained. Basic medical metrics should not be this difficul
I paid for the lifetime power pack years ago. Came back to start lifting again to find that apparently that doesn't count for anything any more. Total disappointment.

The Update ruined it. Previously, the app was a breeze to use, priced well, and very useful. Now the UI is uglier and everything takes more time to input. For example, you can no longer see the brand of foods you search for unless you click into each item that shows up. Finding a particular food item in the database now requires numerous more clicks and a lot more time. It's genuinely a pain to use now and I can't recommend it.

Unable to rely on calendar syncing for class registrations causes me to miss classes I signed up for weeks in advance . In addition, app has started to insert offers for other products when registering for classes. This is disruptive, not exactly a mind-body experience. Update 5/20/2025 - when booking classes, often shows the wrong day's class schedule. requires multiple refreshes to show correct schedule - logged me out and won't let me log in
It would be even greater if you could add random calorie values written on food packages. You can only make entry via search and some foods can not be found on the search.
I had to delete the app over winter and couldnt reset my password. big hassle, I used another email address...

Fantastic app, excellent way to keep me and the kids running. This phone doesn't have an Internet connection while we're running, the phone GPS does a great job of tracking our run, Pace (max & average), estimated Calories, Distance and some more advance breakdowns once we're back within WiFi range. Highly recommended for anyone new to running like ourselves.
No paypal, but option is there

I really wish I didn't need this app to connect my watch via Bluetooth. I have to reconnect my f6 pro every day just to get my workouts and stats synced. I can't reliably receive notifications either. as far as trouble shooting is concerned, I've factory reset my watch multiple times. Uninstalled the app. reset network and Bluetooth settings. uninstalled any additional apps for the watch. it doesn't matter, it has simply become unreliable. unless I'm hiking or working out, it isn't useful.

My first monrh was a trial, found it very interesting with all the offers and features. Now in my 2nd free month, love how it keeps track of EVERYTHING. only downfall they don't have fasting as an option.

The interface is somehow worse after a major overhaul - it lost a lot of actual customization and is now basically customizable according to their ideas only. Most importantly, they went entirely backwards in their food tracking. You can't add custom foods or quick info anymore (unless you pay premium for an AI feature apparently). The math also doesn't actually update in real time when you enter things, so you have to manually calculate things if you want info in the moment.

workouts seem pretty good, so 2 stars. Bugs: Cannot select a choice for audio in the workouts (full / basic / none) as the radio buttons never light up after you press it; Audio for your workouts sometimes start 20-30 seconds after the workout starts; audio in workouts is very laggy / chopped up when connected to Chromecast, "Sync Pending..." in activity log forever; Not syncing activity to Google Fit. I have used this app twice and found these bugs... the QA team needs to be involved as much as the dev team here

What Am I paying for, if every time I do a search for something I ate within the last day doesn't show up? This is annoying and time consuming. There really isn't much of an advantage to paying for a premium membership if I have to go through all the extra work to find the foods that I'm commonly eating anyway. Perhaps it's an algorithm change to get people to pay for the premium plus. Regardless, I likely will not be renewing my membership.

This app was splendid. It tracked how fast I was going on bike rides and walks, used minimal data, and linked up to MyFitnessPal effortlessly, which is part of why the rating is higher. I would have given it one star, but the app now constantly crashes on me, where I can't open the app, it is a disappointment. I've been using this app for a while, and it was awesome, but now that I can't even use it, it's a bummer. I am using an zte with Android 7, please fix this so I can have this app back.

terrible, slow, and can never complete a purchase on the app. dont waste your time. use a computer instead. it even canceled some of my auto ships when I managed to log in (after 15min of trying) and I didn't realize it until now when im out of literally everything. the service is a great idea... this app and its execution are the worst I've ever seen and not worth the time to try to use.

I am using the free version of this app, for now, as I've had some unforeseen expenses,BUT it has really changed my mind set on a higher level. it fascinates me to see how quickly calories actually add up amd I understand better why its so Important to keep track. I HIGHLY recommend this app to anyone who is struggling to lose weight and is not understanding why..... eating small amounts of food has nothing to do with losing weight, but counting your calories is the secret. Thank you fatsecret.

I was just very impressed with whoever created all of these apps, I honestly wouldn't care if they harvested every piece of my phone the ui is super intuitive and the functions are through the roof. and I don't just mean this app, I love twilight and power line.

No longer reliably working with my Samsung Gear Sport, so it's pretty useless. Emailed support a few weeks ago about trying to figure it out (because I do love it when it's working as intended) but the only thing I got back was an automated response to read the FAQ. Edit: lol, yet another bot response telling me to read the FAQ. I did respond to the initial automated email asking for more specific help, and that was the last I heard.

The Zwift companion app is an absolute must when using Zwift. More or less it's your control panel and remote control for the application. I use a PC to run the game and an Android phone to run the companion app. It's a must have.
Constantly crashes, no way to customize options. Would b a great app at least if it didn't crash EVERY DAY, and erase all my favorites.

No complaints so far, but I only use the app to book and cancel the classes. I have not tried other features. It would be great if you could link the app with other fitness watches/trackers as well as at the moment the choice is limited.

Since the latest update, the app starts, shows the map for a very brief time (no interaction possible) and immediately closes again. I simply can't use it anymore. What the hell is going on? Been using this apps for almost 10 years! To say I was happy is an overstatement, but it worked well enough. The last couple of weeks it's just dying.... Angry to see such a unprofessional behaviour when my money is involved... Tested on two devices (nokia 6.1 & pixel 3a) - same bug.
Won't accept my account credentials. Unusable.

All in all for the sole purpose of adequate tracking, it is a brilliant app. But seriously, I prefer it the old way. The new mascot and esp the meal tips are so annoying. It will always find something to moan about regardless of how balanced and healthy your meal was. Therefore it is not really helpful but i see it more as a danger to peeps that are at risk of eating disorders. Please make it a setting that can be turned on and off, so everyone can decide whether or not it's beneficial to them.

Missing one star for a couple of things in my opinion. The daily journal is great, but the sliders make it difficult to hit the correct time, quantity, whatever it's asking for. This needs to be either something other than a dial or offer a direct entry. The other gripe, minor though it is, would be for the "Other" category of workout to offer a Note section or description to say what the workout was. There are plenty of at-home workouts that don't fit other categories and would like to be able to note that it was just push-ups/sit-ups or some form of bodyweight exercises. Otherwise agree, this is the best fitness tracker on the market and doesn't bog the experience down with useless facts and metrics. This has improved my recovery and performance in the short time I've used it.
Its not usefull for health tracking. its just a tracking device for your movement. Can't even count your steps properly

Used to use my fitness pal, and it became way too buggy, bloated, ad-ridden and just a UI mess to navigate. I found this calorie counter, and while it doesn't have as good of a food finder (yet), it is by far a cleaner, more enjoyable experience. There are some improvements to be made, but this is my new accountability partner, and I recommend!

Great app! I got it to track my vitamin intake, and I love that you can track water intake and also modify to add specific nutrients like Beta carotene. There's a really thorough database of store-bought foods, though I sometimes question the nutritional values entered (or not entered) in the app. I'd also love to see more variety of exercises available for tracking. I practice Kundalini yoga which isn't listed in here, so I have to estimate. Aside from these minor things, I highly recommend it!
Easy to use and accurate mileage.

The new layout update is perfectly designed to allow for overeating. I won't recommend it for anyone who has trouble with sticking to a calorie deficit. All details which were previously very clear and concise to help understand your patterns and food weaknesses, now are hidden away and no longer visible for clarity. I don't believe the app is catered towards identifying meaningful lifestyle changes that can help in losing weight and keeping it off.

App is slower than usual lately to load workouts. Also, I felt it provided a better user experience back in the day when it was possible to see which exercises where in a workout session without having to load and watch the full session video. This new experience makes it difficult to evaluate whether a workout is suitable when I'm trying to seek out or avoid certain movements.
easy to use and navigate. Thanks!
Tips you can't turn off, long animations for streaks you can't turn off. I get some people benefit from the positivity, but I just want to track my calories.

Glitchy recently. I've been using this app for *years* to track hikes and loved it. Recently (maybe since an app update?), it has stopped tracking in the middle of multiple hikes, including a big 15-mile, 4500-foot elevation mtn peak hike; super upset! I'm now looking for a different gps activity tracking app to replace this one.

Almost perfect. One big thing missing is integration with other platforms (i.e. being able to open the app in a laptop or tablet running other OS) I wouldn't mind paying for the app if it was capable of that. Aside from that hiccup, this has been the best free app for workouts during the strange days of Covid. I think I'll keep on using it, even now that my gym is opening again. Cheers team!
great app! I just wish it had a feature to track calories & food by scanning barcodes like fitbit used to have
i've paid money for a piece of rubbish that uses all my data allowance and if I turn off its permission to do so it just turns it back on again. So now I have no data.

I really like how accurate the data is and find it very helpful. Been wearing it 8 months pretty much straight and find the insights and monthly summary helpful. The alarm setting isn't enough to wake me up but I wish there were more options to add unlimited alarms for various things. This would really help my time blindness and help me be more mindful throughout the day or where I should be or what I should be doing to stay on track with my goals.

Firstly, Power Saving mode needs to be disabled. Appreciate the activity tracking, including audible progress updates along the way, but these are set to every mile, regardless whether you're set to imperial or metric, and regardless of what activity you're doing. Auto pause and resume are a bit delayed, maybe affecting stats, and map history is a bit loose, especially for fast, winding sections. For improvement, please add history search by location or activity name.

I like the app, and use it daily. I only gave it four stars because it keeps bothering me about sleep tracking, when I am only interested in the alarm options at this time. Thank you for the response! I do use the alarm-only options, but it still gives me a box in the lower right corner asking me to start sleep tracking.

I was excited to try this app out, I love the ideas of music, sounds, and stories being available to listen to when looking to relax. Unfortunately, after the free download, I realized that nothing available unless I subscribe for an annual fee! I can do a free trial for 7 days; however, I don't like to sign up for trials all of the time and am really not interested in paying another subscription. Deleting the app, will find something else.

I still don't like all the things you have to agree to, it seems they have access to almost everything except your inside leg measurement. Data mining comes to mind. But the app itself seems to work extremely well and I particularly like the function where it automatically pauses if you stop for any significant time. Not at all sure how accurate the calorie count is though for the type of 'robust' bicycle that I use - lightweight road bike maybe, not city bike with paniers!

The loss of features with the latest big update is inexcusable. We used to be able to *choose* what we wanted to set as our goals and what we cared most about in terms of our health. Now we are forced to use the move minutes nonsense. I am actively trying to *gain* weight and I don't care at all about steps and cardio. I care about strength workouts and calories. Now I'm trying to figure out what alternatives exist aside from Google for fitness tracking because it's become completely useless.

Before it was amazing shop and application. Especially I liked flavor rating and wide choice of products, now nothing there and one of the worst app which I ever seen. Please return flavor rating. Thank you

I loved this app until they took away plans. Now I have to keep track of more on my own... making it less efficient than other workout apps. Update: for some reason, they keep making this app harder and harder to use and navigate. I miss when this app was easy to pick up and sort through. I mean, the programs tab doesn't work and you can't even participate in any. The app being free is the only benefit.

Overall would be a good app if not buggy. App randomly ceases to track GPS during workout (duration continues while speed/distance/route hangs), making the stats and itself useless. Edit: now that the bug is fixed, I can reccomend it. It is still a bit slow on location update, but overall very good, free app.

The app is helpful in tracking calories and macros, but I do find it frustrating sometimes to find the correct of closest food option to select from. Also, the paid version, in my opinion (was on a 1 year paid subscription), did not offer that much more value, so using the free version now, just for basic tracking, is fine for me.

There's only one thing I like better about the new app, but overall it's worse. The most frustrating thing is that 2 days ago I could see my Peloton workouts with the name of the workout, the instructor, all of the information. Now it just says "other". The accounts are synced, so that is not the problem. It's hard to find information, you have to click twice just to find your resting heart rate. Do they not test these things before releasing? I could write much more, but I'm out of characters.

Has Under Armour decided to get rid of you guys? I have been using your app for years and have been a premium member since 2013. Unfortunately, I have been facing a lot of issues like a lot of 500 errors from your nginx load balancers, infinite authentication redirection loops, inaccessible pages and so on for a couple of months now. Could you please decide if you would like to continue maintaining this application? If you won't maintain it, I do not want to pay you anymore!

Absolutely horrendous app on Android. I can never use my previously logged food when logging something new in my diary unless I manually change a setting every time I log. as suggested on the website, I reinstalled the app to see if it fixes the issue, but it erased almost all my history. weird thing is that it didn't lose everything - just a few bits and pieces from every meal, which is even more odd. There is no easy way to contact support either and super disappointed with the user experience

Starting using it ten years ago, the free version, loved it. Started cycling again recently, tried using it, had all my old data etc. Decided to use it a bit more before paying for the full app (almost tno features on the unpaid version), noticed some GPS issues, today was the worst, did a 10k ride and app only recorded 1k of it due to GPS signal loss issues. Checked my hardware and its functioning well on other GPS related apps.

App worked fine for 2-3 weeks. Was sick for a few days and didn't use it. Now every time I try to open the app it crashes immediately. Uninstalled -> re-installed several times to no avail. Will update review pending update to app that resolves the issue. EDIT: App received update today, 1/22. After installing the update, the app appears to be working normal again. Thanks to support for making a promise they could eventually deliver on.

It's one of my favorite apps that have assisted me fit. I still prefer the format of how the workouts were displayed before starting a workout. That helped me to set up my equipment and the needed size of the weights. With the new whiteboard and especially with this format, there is no need for you to play the whole workout so that you can how to set up your equipment. I still miss the ability to download offline workouts. None the less it is still a great to help one be fit.

I have been using this app every night for over a year. it has helped me get more regular sleep. I put my phone on the charger and then lay it next to my pillow. it tracks my sleep very well, except for when I do get up in the middle of the night. It doesn't mark that on the record. It's also supposed to work with my Garmin watch but running the app on my watch kills the watch every night (instead of it lasting 6+ days).

Used to be perfect. The May update ruined the database, so most of local poducts, food from the local restraunts have been wiped out. Barcode search is mostly useless. What used to take seconds now takes minutes. Hope they will fix that.
Works fine but on android it is only called "connect" so you can't find it by searching garmin in apps - annoying when you forget what the garmin app is called!
The slow increase of volume and vibration makes waking less upsetting and the math makes sure I'm awake. Other features are just icing. Have used this many years. I'd be lost without it.🤤

I used this app exclusively 6 or 7 years ago and it helped me get in great shape. Sadly, it has slowly declined in quality and value since. You use to be able choose your goal and set up a program and determine the days and it would notify you when it was time to work out. The interface was much better. It created a schedule for you. Now its just a handful of workouts and the option to "upgrade to premium" to get better variety of workouts. So so so disappointed with the change.

This app is really deceptive. They don't have any refund. I signed up for a flex membership on this app in January of this year. Tried it for free and then I canceled my membership, but they charged me every month after cancel. Also I applied for class 11 online at a RSC yoga studio. I bought passes, but it was no longer available, so I complained to the technical team Joe, but he answered to go to the bank and stop payment but they won't refund all charges. This app is a danger. Don't use this.

I have a hybrid bike that I ride to work sometimes, grocery shop and ride easy to moderate trails. This app is extremely accurate, easy and fun to use. It will pause and restart your workout when it detects that you've stopped for an extended period of time. It will read speed, time, distance, elevations, calories, and even map your trip. I like to rename my trips to remember where I went or compare times. I love this app over the others I've sampled.

When it's working, it's fine. Not as full of features as other tracking apps and not a real replacement for the old "my tracks" app. As of a recent update, the numbers portion of the main display for heart points and steps has stopped updating. The circles around the numbers are still working and you can see that the steps are generating the bar charts, but the numbers are showing 0.
It is easy to use and see what nutrients you have consumed. you can even log your own recipes and ingredients for better calculations.
used in two android phones the experience was same which is worst... pausing my run and resuming is not happening... app keeps crashing

MyFitnessPal refugee checking in here. Overall, the app seems pretty nice. I wish that it was easier to get to the barcode scanner (you have to go through the camera and then manually tap on the scanner) and sometimes I encounter a glitch after scanning where the app doesn't load up any nutritional info on the thing I just scanned, so I have to exit the app and re-enter. But it's definitely promising and it syncs to Google Fit unlike MFP!

I am really loving this app. I really like the idea of the new AI feature. I think it would be great though if you could add items you have on hand from your fridge and pantry then you can ask the AI to generate suggestions from that. Also, the AI gives ingredients but not directions. Most of the time it looks like the suggestions are pretty easy to figure out but not always. It would be great if you could see a recipe.

This app is amazing and is a great way to keep track of exercise. It has so many different types of activities to select from and even subgenres of that activity. This makes it very easy to customize the recording to your specific exercise. They have things like walking, running, hiking, and biking. They also have things like skiing, snowboarding, and just about any sport you can think of. I have an app that pays you gift cards for exercising and this app is a great way to earn points on there.

it's an amazing app to track calories and macros. I've used it for years to track my food weekly. however, with the last update, it's not allowed anymore to track food in advance and, for people like me that we meal prep and know what we are going to eat everyday, it has become a tedious task to have to daily input the same meals. paid version allows this...hopefully you'll amend this as it used to be great!

Good app. it will be great if this app could improve to continuously record workout for walking for 24 hours per cycle as long as my speed is within threshold range. For example I might be walking whole day with pace from 0 to 6 km per hour. My regular walking speed is 4 km per hour. Any speed above 4km/hr will be recorded. Then I dont have to keep watch out my walkout by pressing pause and resume.

Fun app adds to the overall positive Zwift experience. A few months ago, I suggested that route elevation profiles be made available in the ZWIFT App to complement the existing route maps. The developers listened and made it happen with 2 profile types: 1) an overall route profile that shows the whole route, and 2) pop-up profiles for each climb that includes gradients. This development deserves MAJOR KUDOS because this allows Zwifters to plan, gauge, and adjust efforts during routes!

Like many others I had purchased the life time pack years ago and now they have completely locked me out from using what I paid for. Users who paid previously should be grandfathered in for the basic features. Unacceptable! Changing to One star for this deception on what was sold as a life time purchase!

add an option to turn off the meal tips!! i loved the summary after logging, which is gone. that being said, this app helped me gain 5lb, so it can be used for weight gain as well. i used to find it fun and easy to log my meals and it has a great database of US foods! it also syncs with my Google fit data & accounts for those calories, which is awesome! paid for the yearly subscription. edit: unsatisfied with dev response, cancelled my sub, STILL got a dot & notification for pro. ridiculous.

update caused problems. since the update it continually disconnects from the watch and it wont sync the activities even though they show on the watch itself. it takes a lot of time and effort faffing around to get it to reconnect. its not as accurate as it once was barely reading heartrate when my other monitor shows a high rate during activity.

Great app to get access to zwift when your tablet is broadcasting to the tv. Easy to type a message, give kudos and change your route. My only complaint is that sometimes the game part of the app doesn't open and you have to try a few times to get the map/game to load up. Other than that it's very helpful.

I love this app. if you are dedicated and willing to put in the work, this app will help you keep track of your calories. I like it. The only complaint that I have is if you add something to the food menu and you make a mistake, there's no way to correct that, at least no way that I've seen. For instance I added a meal to the menu and I forgot to put the calories in but had all the other information correct, however I didn't know how to go back and either delete that entry or correct it.

The app shows lot of information. Most of which you don't understand or is of no use. I was surprised to see that even with top of the line membership, the first thing on Health tab is to pay for Advanced Labs. App is difficult to use. One time it logged my sleep as nap and nap later in the day as sleep. It messed up all the numbers. I had hard time discovering the issue. Customer support kept on giving different instructions to try and none of them worked. Never found the WHOOP Coach in the app

Voice feedback is set to an interval of 1 kilometer. But instead it gives me feedback at 1.61km, 3.22km etc. Clearly there is some internal setting stuck on miles, but all the settings I can find are in metric.

The app would be great but it's designed to be always-connected. Get to the trailhead where there's no signal? You may be SOL because it is demanding you log in to their server before you can do anything in the app! Come on guys, this is an obvious use case. Why is the app asking me to log in all the time anyway? Also, yesterday saved a long run, heard the message "Workout complete, time..." Later I looked and the run was gone. What? I went to manually add the run, and there it was, incomplete

No refunds offered. No full version of the other application offered. They took my money for a one year subscription recently, which means they knew when they took my money that their application was shutting down. Super unethical and I'd say it constitutes fraud.

Very intuitive once you learn. Literally only takes 1 read of the guide but it's a lot at first if you don't take a look. Would be cool if they offered a quick guide upon first use. Maybe I skipped it. Correct me if I'm wrong lol. Won't take a Star off because either way it works as intended. No issues first workout. Amazing app nonetheless. Worth whatever they offer in my opinion
awesome running ap to help you reach your goals with encouragement and the right amount of pressure. I love the variety of long, fast, and recovery runs

I bored to answer all the millions questions on the start of app, and I even not have the choice to skip. After millions questions you see subscription page, where you can't choice some free trial. You should start playing for app that you even not see at all, only what you saw it was millions questions that already push you to remove this app before finish answering.

Works great! I read somewhere that it's the best app to use in terms of the free side, and I think I agree. Tracking weight, workouts, calories, and percentage of macro intake have been super helpful in weight loss. Only problems are with some UI trouble. Deleting a saved meal is difficult or impossible, I haven't figured out how to do it. Clicking on things accidentally happens, as it can be finicky. Overall great experience!

Usable if you don't want to listen to music. Find some other app if you love music or podcasts during workouts: even after you disable all notifications in the Endomondo app and in Android settings, it will still notify you about progress audibly every km or mile. This completely kills your mood and workout experience.

I got the app because they promised a coupon code of 13%. It was never there. I called customer service and they were nice but thought the problem was me. Told me delete the app, reinstall and the code would be there. They didn't wait to verify it worked but ended the call. The coupon wasn't there. I wasted way too much time on this with nothing to show for it. The app is okay but it's just a shopping app and I would rather place my orders on a real computer with a real monitor.

The WHOOP 3.0 was great and my first introduction to the device. I hardly, if ever, had any issues. Was excited for and waited forever for the 4.0, and it's clear it wasn't ready. Currently dealing with the second instance of not having my device/app sync to the cloud, so I have 0 access to my metrics. Been stuck this way for 5 days, and no amount of resetting, unpairing, re-pairing, etc., is working, so the device is useless. Hoping they fix these issues before long.

The app works great. The gps seems accurate. I've recorded a little over 10 miles and the only problem I've encountered so far is it wouldn't let me "end" one of my runs, therefore, that 5k didn't get recorded. Other than that, no problems. 2/26/20 EDIT: Can't build run plans anymore. I've been trying for weeks. Keeps giving error message. Not happy at all about this. Was 4 stars, now 2.

Worthless. Do not get this app! All this is, is a fancy calorie counter and goal timer with some recipes. You have to pay to use any potentially useful features. I paid not knowing everything that was offered. Once I opened the paid version I was extremely disappointed in what was actually offered. My Fitness Pal offers almost all of the same stuff in their free version so WHY would anyone pay 20 bucks for 1 or 2 extra (pointless) features?! Also, they will NOT give you a refund once you pay.

Automatic activity tracking is broken at the moment. Used to have no problems while phone was in my pocket to pick up on walks (treadmill or outside). Hasn't been the case in a number of days now and no changes made to my phone. Edit: Still no help, Google. I did that and tried. Still nothing. It is a to E and set to automatically track. And again, over a week ago it did without issues. I haven't updated or changed any phone settings.

I am canceling the monthly subscription I just bought yesterday because I open the app, and it redirects me to get website after the take a deep breath loading screen. and it opens in the edge browser that isn't my default browser. fix it I wasted my money.
Been a user for 10 years, lately some free features like smart wakeup turned premium . Thats not a way to get my money , very disappointed , uninstalled.

The update is absolutely worthless. impossible to decipher, got rid of daily cardio load targets, which were the one feature helping me fix a debilitating health issue I've been having. now it's Monday morning, I did one walk, and I'm 200% over my "weekly target." No, I don't want to talk to a brainless AI that can't get anything right. give the option to be able to use the app in the way that it was actually useful. I see alot of people canceling and junking their devices. I may be following

I've tried every other app out there. This is the one. The one thing I wish, is that they would create a one-time purchase to buy the app features that don't require their servers (custom meals, meal planner, micronutrients, etc). I don't care for the subscription benefits (weekly spam, designed meals, AI stuff)

No problem selecting from prior purchases, just checked what I wanted today, added to cart. Record of address on file for shipping. Payment card on record, all I had to add was the csv. Any shipping or fulfillment probs in the past have been quickly reconciled to my satisfaction. First time using app, worked fine. Have relied on Vitacost for many years, both for quality selections and price.

Paid full prices years ago but they expect me to pay again. What happens when they update again in few years, will people have to pay again, scammers. If they don't make version available I paid for then that is classed has a scam, I don't care for a monthly subscription when I paid full price for lifetime years ago, sad thing is I did it to support team, seems they have become greedy. Guys Personal Training Coach is a great app, many many workouts programs on app, don't bother with SL app

I use this daily to track my commuting miles and speed. It is near perfect for this. I would give it 5 stars if it was feasible (or much easier) to mod bookmarked rides to friendlier names. Better compatibility with smart watches and other sensors would also be nice. 10 months later, still using regularly; still wish I could get it to read my Fitbit.

I have lost 30 lbs so far using this app. It has the flexibility and usability I need to quickly track calories. The food database is pretty good. I upgraded to the premium version to have more than just the three basic meals plus snack. It recommended 1500 calories per day instead of 1200, which made all the difference. I can stick to this plan and I don't stall loss because my calories are too low. Takes a few weeks to get the hang of it, but once you're familiar, it's very easy.

Excellent app that not only tracks your rides but can also track runs, hikes, skiing and most other sports as well. And it's free! You can also log into your account online to view past activities, see weekly and monthly totals as well as detailed breakdowns of workouts. And even that is free!

Almost a necessary companion app while riding if you want any kind of social interaction. Best for looking at activities. One downside is there seems to be a disconnect between developers of this app and the game. Why can't we see achievements in the app, and why can't we enter the garage/store and see/purchase gear? Seems like an easy add to the app.

Great app. Personally I find it better than the main competitor (MFP). There are many missing foods and sometimes I wish it was a little easier to quickly add a new/custom food. The water tracker is a bit annoying if you're not adding in consistent amounts. I also wish it was better to track creatine.

It is great tracking my ride in real time. The audio updates are useful - since my phone is tucked away in a pocket, it's helpful to know distance & time elapsed. I often take photos on my ride, so being able to add them to my stats makes for an awesome way to save those photos - they are in context with my stats & ride journal. I had trouble adding multiple photos to a ride that I logged manually, since I forgot to open this. Maybe that feature is not available? Or I didn't see how to do that.

Great help for tracking all I do especially for my fit bit record 2017 ...and still doing well. So far I do not see the advantage of the premium App as the free provides all that I need. Although I have used it at least 2 times a week since at least since 2013, the record is in my Fitbit app. Thanks

It works well, but would like to see more flexibility and customisation. It's baffling for example that gradient is displayed in Zwift, but not in Companion. It would also be helpful to be able to change settings in companion rather than having to log into Zwift and start a "Ride"

I really like that this isn't a weight loss app. You can calibrate it to be if you want, but I have a really unbalanced diet and just wanted something to make sure I'm eating enough and getting adequate protein and vitamins, etc. and this app is perfect for that. My only gripe is that scanning food seems to be very inaccurate sometimes. My protein shake showed up with 2.1 g of protein instead of 25 g so I had to manually input it anyway. Hope it doesn't end up being a major issue.

not happy that they removed functionality that was paid for. AND they raised the price. already killing the subscription The calorie tracker is also less useful. The bar code scanner works less often, you can't add your own food anymore, and previously saved food is no longer available. The old app just worked. I even paid for for the higher tier. now it costs more and does less. looking for something better now

Could you please add a feature that would temporarily switch the loud volume off when the phone is being moved? So if I pick up the phone to check something late at night, and and an in-app video ad starts up, it's not going to wake the whole house.
works really well tracking my walks and runs and links to my fitness pal which is great for helping to stay on track

They changed the design of the app. Half the good information is no longer shown. You have to click numerous things to see anything and it's just unappealing all around. Also, there was an update and now my app crashes constantly, double and triple logs my food daily. I have to manually go in and delete each of the extra items all the time. It is time consuming and I'm ready to look into a different nutritional tracker app.

Best app, and so disappointed that UA is discontinuing it. Awful decision. The other suggested apps are terrible, and not in any way a good substitute for Endomondo. The UI was one of the best, and the choice of activities was excellent. So many apps only offer run/walk/cycle. Also, the fact that it would sync with BOTH my TICKR chest strap (for accurate HR monitoring) and then with Fitbit, was awesome. Fitbit, like most wrist monitors, does a terrible job of picking up heart rate in the higher range. Please...bring back Endomondo!

I found that 'Caynax' is a very similar replacement: multisport, stats, voice announcements. Search for 'Endomondo export to excel' to find easy instructions on extracting your data to excel or csv. After finally fixing all the bugs, it's been flawless for years, but now it's dead. RIP. Underwhelming Under Armor.

The app is a bare minimum. It can't zoom in on e.g. heart rate, you get a small graph (that doesn't even scale) and that's it. There's no cursor that can be used to get more information as many other apps provide. Useless. The multi-day graphs are even worse. Lots of improvements possible guys, talk to your users!

switched from apple to Samsung so I needed to replace my apple watch got a Garmin and the tracking for working out compared to Apple is way better in my opinion especially being able to enter in individual exercises(aside from some time it does take to set all up) very happy I made the switch

I used to love Endomondo, now the app is just simply not worth using it. It crashes at the middle of a training session, so you miss all the tracking info. When it does not crash, the GPS readings are awful, it shows you "teleporting" from one point to other, and sometimes it stops tracking your movement at all. I am so disappointed

The app creates bogus log entries & you can't delete some of them. For example, I entered my weight for yesterday morning & later found two other (incorrect) entries logged for that afternoon. Then, my phone was sitting on my nightstand charging last night when the app created an entry saying I went for a 5-minute walk (???). It would be great if the app worked, but I can't use it in its current condition. Deleting.
the app allows quick access to great prices. Would give 5 stars if their promo code system was not so confusing. Excellent customer service !!!

When I first started using this app years ago, I opted to make the one-off payment rather than install the free version, so I could unlock things like interval and pyramid training. Since then, they introduced a subscription and moved that content onto the subscription platform, which makes me wonder why I paid in the first place. Very shady, but they won't be getting more of my money after that.

The app worked great until recently. Over the past few months, when I book certain days, it will either not book or it will book a different day. It's been frustrating, and I've lost time and money. It's been happening with two separate studios I attend regularly. After following basic protocols of uninstalling and updating the app, their response was, "I've exhausted all available resources related to your concern." Disappointing that they're taking no responsibility for their app problem.

Zwift keeps me going through winter months and bad weather days when outside isn't an option. Since i started with Zwift, I've seen much improvement in my ability in real life to tackle big hills as well as much improvement in my overall speed. Love that I can ride with those around the world who have the same passion for riding as I. Recommend a bike permanently set up on a direct drive smart trainer with a second bike for outdoor riding, so switching back and forth is a no-brainer.

The only actually free macro tracking app, I downloaded 4 other "free" ones but every time I reached that screen where you gotta pick your subscription but finally when I got cronometer, yes I still saw that screen, but I was still able to log my meals for the day and see protein, carbs, fats, and likely how much of a deficit or in the future, surplus that I am in.

I don't know what the bug is, but every few days, the app stops loading. I have cleared the cache, deleted data, uninstalled and reinstalled, nothing works. keeps asking me to log in, and when I do it just gets stuck on the loading page, every time! Really getting tired of this issue.

The app is usable, but its representation of health data is too rigid. Sleep Mode is frustrating; you can't manually override it if you wake up early without the system forcing it back on. Pregnancy Tracking is also poorly implemented. It calculated gestational age incorrectly, hid historical cycle data (still medically relevant), and failed to adjust health targets. For a company that markets itself on data precision, these are failures in reliability, not just a lack of flexibility.
Crashes constantly and just links up browser

I've been struggling with meal planning lately, discovering this app has been extremely helpful. Finding recipes and being able to just transfer the needed ingredients directly to my shopping list is great. You can also change the amount of servings and it will adjust the recipe for you, given I usually need to make recipes smaller as a single mom this is very useful.

App is pretty horrible to use, logs you out and says wrong password when trying to log back in, rider profiles hardly ever load when trying to view, on group rides it's hard to see riders behind to see if they need help. For the monthly price the companion app and the game in general have lots of faults. If it wasn't for the community I'd have left after the price hike.

Mostly good, any problems I've had have been easy to correct with plenty of online advice. Wish it would recognise Trail running as an actual activity not just a faster hike though and add canicross. Presently trying one of the training plans and finding it very good (as long as you tell the truth!😂) I also set workouts and find that incredibly useful. I use the 935, on my 3rd, it does what I want, I dont need bells or whistles and it suits me!

I had continued to type until I ran out of room about how much I love this app, theres so much good stuff, and im just on the free version right now! So, what I will say is, the free version is amazing, and I will definitely be upgrading to the full version. I'm also telling a lot of friends and family about this app! 10/10 highly recommend! P.S. Free version there's maybe 1 ad a day and you can close it about as quickly as it pops up.

This app is not it. It constantly asks me to login, freezes up often. I love the guided runs (although sometimes the yapping is a bit too much), but not all of them have the same qualities, and I constantly have to download them every time I run. The final straw is the fact that it doesn't even track properly in recent months. It messes up your pace and your distance.

I used this app for years - since before they were acquired by Under Armour - it was reasonably reliable and worked with my HR sensor, unlike the app I moved from, and allowed data export. Paid for the Pro version to get the training plans. Currently, though, it won't even start on a fully-patched Android 10 phone. Chances that that will be remedied seem slim in an app that hasn't been updated in more than a year. So long, old friend - you can be one in a million but on the Play Store there are ten just like you.

I'm only giving this app a one-star because there's a glitch that needs to be resolved. So developers if you're reading or listening this is for you when I try to sign into the app I tap login, it asks if it's me. I select that it is, and then I'm redirected to the login screen again. It's just an endless circle. If you're able to fix the glitch and send an update I would love to be able to sign into this app! I would also love to give it a better rating once I can get in.

Data is not syncing across my devices. I use my secondary phone while doing my runs and workouts and log my data every day after finishing. That data is now only available on my secondary phone and not syncing to my account to view on other devices. This used to work fine but now has just stopped. I don't know what to do, besides the app has become very heavy and laggy.
Won't let me register. Removed after 4th try.

I've been using this app for about six years now and recorded over three thousand miles on it. The last two weeks, the application no longer tracks distance or calories burned. I have repeatedly stopped and restarted both the app and my phone, as well as checking all my location and GPS settings. I'm trying one more time to reinstall this app. If it doesn't work, I'm changing to Strava. Well, I'm pleased to say, that today, 11-08-21 my app worked perfectly. Thanks to the team at MapMyRide!

User friendly app that has gotten better over the years. I use this almost exclusively for running in conjunction Map My Run to plan my runs, since I suspect the GPS on the watch is not 100% accurate. The additional stats are helpful but not to be depended on for health reasons- one note: the Incident Alert is surprisingly sensitive, which I like- it went off when I did a couple skips off a high curb. It is also SUPER easy to cancel the alert.
Great app, please put Michelle Lewin on it :)

EDIT: App not syncing my Exercise data. It's been working fine, now it's not. EDIT: I log my food every day, yet I get notifications each day that I haven't registered my meals. What gives? Plus, I set my macro percentages but the app fails to adjust to my settings. The daily calorie goal set by the app is way above maintenance for days where I'm less active. Sadly, there's no way to vary this number to coincide with my training schedule.

The app failed to capture GPS information during parts of many of my trips. For the missing parts, it just draws a straight line on the map over the unknown segments. That means the route, distance, and calories calculations are wrong. The only thing it tells me correctly is the elapsed time, which I can figure in my head. Almost useless! Post Script: I learned you have to make an adjustment to your phone to keep the app from hibernating. Once I did that, it worked fine.

Kristina is pretty cool. She challenges you and pays attention to yogis to be sure people are getting the moves correct and what to use to challenge the class. Don't think too much about everything you have going on and you'll be fine.

I've never been particularly impressed with this app or with garmin equipment. I have the Epix Pro and the Edge 1030. The watch is always one minute behind actual time. That in itself is the most ridiculous fact of all. The 1030 I haven't used for a couple of years. The best thing about the watch is the battery life. The rest is just okay.

Since the app was revised to "more accurately" reflect mileage, it consistently records significantly less than our group's odometers. While they are not as accurate as measuring with a rolling tape measure, this method was not done throughout the US. I will try other apps and if they are consistent with this one, I may consider returning.
Does exactly what I need. Even has an alarm setting with gradual volume increase over time to not startle you when waking you up. I use the nature sounds of birds chirping very subtly.

anybody having some consistent log in issues with the app these days? almost always it says the session has timed out and I need to log in. And now when I go to log in with Google, I end up in an error loop "OOPS". Also the app often shows the schedule for the wrong day. These issues are starting to outweigh the convenience of signing up for classes through the app. please advise.

Out of all of the similar apps out there, this is my favorite. I always have trouble with other apps and it's barcode scanning and, adding meals. This is the only app that I can actually add foods into a meal instead of logging each and every food separately. It also easily gives me suggestions and let's me search for foods. I also enjoy the mini challenges, such as going without chocolate for 21 days. It really keeps me motivated. It has a lot of features that other apps just don't have.

When I create my own food item, and then I go to create a recipe, there should be a separate menu option of just MY FOODS that I can search through. Instead I have to search through the database and *hope* that I find my food item. And no matter how creative I get with the naming I can never find my own items. MyFitnessPal has been around way too long for something like this to be a problem. It absolutely ruins the experience. 0/10 until y'all figure it out. :)
the app is garbage. I have cell data and wifi connection and the app keeps asking me to check my Internet connection. I have uninstalled and reinstalled multiple times as well as rebooted my phone.
every update makes this app slower and more frustrating to use.
Best app ever! And I like the san (with which you can look up any! item and it will automatically show you their nutrition) the most. And everything works perfect! Super easy to use :)

Stressful, needs improvement if items are in my list it won't let you tap on it you have to go to the begging look for it to get info ur looking for, at payout u use PayPal take you to type in ur card then it gets mixed up says u still have items in cart but payment did go through "I check my bank transaction" in ur cart sometimes it won't let u delete an item.

I have been on MFP since 2012. I have had great success trimmed my weight from 212 down to my goal 167.0 at 18%. However, MFP is limiting the "Free" app functions and making them only available through paid " Premium" subscription. It is just another example of "Billionaire Buddies" sticking it to us! Charge more to the advertising " Billionaire Buddies"!

This app helped me a lot on my journey to lose 30 kg of fat and get into a healthier and more fitness orientated lifestyle. I definitely recommend it to anyone who wants to get insights in his nutrition, it's database is huge and what isn't there, you just create. Simple and easy. If you've never tracked before, you'd be surprised what an impact certain foods have. Some in a good, some in a bad way.

Since switching from iPhone to Pixel, I've had a terrible experience with the Whoop app. Notifications are delayed, syncs and activity processing are slow, and there's no search option to start an activity. Plus, messages in the community groups often show up hours later. Disappointed to see how the Android version of the app is not as refined as IOS. Hope they fix it soon.
Doesn't meaningfully contribute to the ride or workout.

This was a really great app 10 years ago but has gone down since unfortunately. The latest update is worst, I don't know why you would ever take away the ability to choose what type of workout you are looking for (ie. yoga, strength, etc) without scrolling through all of the workout options. Probably going to delete after all these years. Workouts also aren't as potent as they used to be. Very repetitive, not much gain, but it is a good help if you need to follow a workout and watch examples.

This program is the best I have seen. It covers just about every nutrient one could want except potassium. and phosphrous content. I wish these could be added. These two nutrients are restricted and monitored in kidney failure. This would make this program perfect!!!! As is stands, this program is more inclusive and comprehensive than one I bought for my IPad. So many foods are included. The very best part is being able to shame pictures of foods, recipes, AND to follow individual progress.

The workouts and interface is nice but it's completely useless because it crashes every time I want to do the next workout in a program. I don't want to get dressed in my workout clothes, get out my mat and weights, only to have to find a different workout because the app won't work. This happened to me with Nike Run Club a year ago. Since both apps become unusable at random, I can't keep using them.

Review for Android app only bc I LOVE my Whoop! 3 stars only because I have the iOS version on my iPad and it is in fact much better. Also, there's a glitch that prompts you to enter information about how you felt when you woke up over and over again, whenever you click the recovery tab. Eventually I hit skip because it's really annoying. Also, they should add a feature for women to log their cycles, which can heavily affect athletic performance and recommended output.

Hate the new Update!! I can deal with most of it but I HATE the new way to log food. This part is the worst. It doesn't give you the list anymore. You also can't enter manually for items that is not on there. And you can't officially see how many total calories you burned from the day before because it only shows the active energy calories. HATE.

Its a good app for tracking a simple but effect workout. You dont have to have a lot of experience in the gym to figure this system out, or even any experience. 5 exercises, 5 sets, 5 reps. Easy. The only thing keeping this from a 5 star is the lack of warm up assitance without paying to unlock, but in all fairness that might just be me being a cheapass. Great app, great workout.

I absolutely love this app. I just wish it was a one time purchase and not a subscription. I would gladly support a one time purchase. just the subscription fatigue in 2026 is so bad. I will likely buy a couple months just to support the dev, this app is truly a life saver for me.

Excellent app to track my walks, biking and hiking. I even use it cruising the lake on the boat. It has all the good "useful" stats of your journey while in play, and even more stats to summarize the workout when done! Hands down - worth downloading and is my go to app for activities! Tracks distance, speed, pace, avg pace, elevation, calories, time and much more. Excellent mapping /GPS during your route.
App won't open, update has crashed this app.

I usually love calm, but lately my experience been bad. I started using the soundscapes with timer about 1 week ago to sleep at night.. It worked for the first couple of days. Now it randomly stops every half hour, despite an eight hour timer set, to give me the "well done" message. Not good, not good at all. Please fix it asap or I will need to cancel my subscription.

I really like the strong lifting app. It's simple and easy to use. The timer is helpful when knowing how long to rest and it's satisfying watching your lifts go up in weight in the graphs. The videos on form are helpful too. Only complaint is I'd like to add in another exercise to track but to do that you have to get the paid version (which is fair enough they need to make money somehow!) I just add extea exercises in the notes which is fine. All in all I'd recommend this app.

This application is inaccurate and lately has been offline more often than not. Quick update to the review, this application used to be at least reasonably convenient. However, with the latest update (April 2026) it has become more inconvenient and, more importantly, WILDLY INACCURATE. I would be extremely careful about trusting any data from this application for any purposes. The rating remains one star only because the system doesn't let me go any lower.
the policies suck I accidentally canceled my account because I was trying to upgrade, and they couldn't issue me a refund. I paid for the whole year and only used it for a little over a month

The workouts and plans are all amazing and I would have given this app 5 stars, but you can only do the workouts by downloading them, and you cannot remove the downloaded workouts without deleting all your other data as well, like your plans, workout and training history. So as a result, over the months the app has gotten laggy because of how much storage space it takes up. Update: Figured out how to clear up space on this app, so adding one star. But it's not super intuitive so not giving 5 yet

Absolutely terrible app. Unable to reset my password as I just get error messages saying it is unable to be reset at this time, or that there was an error but then no actual information on what I am supposed to do to fix this. The information on their website is useless as well. Genuinely appalling. This is basic functionality. Everyone involved in this should be ashamed of themselves. Would give a zero rating if possible. Edit: Customer support said they can't help either. A farce of a company

During the brief periods between glitches and malfunctions it is okay. However, there persists regular system glitches: saved foods and meals disappearing or getting multiplied 20+ times in the list. Currently deleting certain foods from saved meals list daily, not transferring calories for some foods, cannot enter new foods due to unit of measurement not working. These types of glitches are a regular part of using this app. The highest absurdity is that they expect you to pay for it.
Almost there, how about adding the ability to change worlds, the ability to see your achievements, stop a training plan. Not need to log into the tour de zwift every time when I'm already in the app.

This app is atrocious. The only feature that worked well was the food log. Now you cannot even log food for future dates so you cannot plan your meals even more. And editing workout events is now far more difficult. The app is not nearly as customizable as it previously was. I hate this app even more than the Fitbit app and just after one day of use. Well done Google.
App works very well, except I wish it would pause automatically when motion stops more than 10 seconds.

I've been using fitbit for a decade and have loved it. I use it to log my calorie intake as well as my cardio. My main reason for using it is that instead of having a daily calorie goal, my goal is a 500 calorie a day deficit, and it adjust throughout the day. The new merge with Google health doesn't seem to have this feature. The UI also is trash and I can't find what I need to. There are more steps to complete what I used to do in fitbit. I'll have to find a new app unfortunately.

Well thought out aid to training. Lots of options very customisable. Started with the original 5x5, however my recovery rate wasn't fast enough, I'm approaching 60. However the mini program is just fantastic for me. Works all the main muscle groups for functional strength without too much stress on joints etc. You can add accessories or modify as needed if you feel like it. It's brief and to the point, add in some cardio afterwards and all your exercise boxes are ticked. Great app.
I use the app still with my Garmin Instinct version 1 and I use it also too keep record of my blood pressure. (Need to add it manually). But it works great, no complaints.
needs more selection of products
I hit the sign in button and the app doesnt do anything. I cant even get in and I have 7day free trial that will be wasted
Tons of bugs (crashes, dropped appointments), and lots of annoying pop ups and demands for five star ratings. I can't wait until my gym switches scheduling apps.

This used to be a very stable app but now disconnects from device rather too frequently for no apparent reason. All permissions are enabled including unlimited battery. This could become annoying... Please no suggestions to forget/reconnect/switch off/on etc. as this really shouldn't be necessary.

Paid a fortune for signing up, and it still asks for more money when I want to see more recipe books. The healthy options are not actually very healthy. And they keep rotating the same recipes, not very variant. On the top of that now the app is not even stats anymore. Why did I pay for this??!

I liked the fitbit app for the most part. Forcing me to change to the Google health app is frustrating. The information that I was using is harder to find, and I was using the food tracking which is much more difficult in google health. For example, the food tracker won't allow me to put in an item in the future which is important when your weighing your food and need to record the weights, but it's for a future meal like lunch or dinner that's being prepared early.

This is the most buggy, slow, incredibly frustrating apps I've probably ever used. It takes upwards of 20 minutes to "complete my run". It logs me out all the time. The UI is not very user friendly I don't think. I feel like this app should be miles and miles better than what it is but it falls very short of that. I am on an android device so that might be my problem but why on earth should that mess with it? I hope it gets better soon or I'm looking elsewhere.

Love the audio coaching as well as the run planning of "My Coach". I just wished they synced up better. when I select the MyCoach scheduled run I don't get the audio (I'm sure some prefer that). When I select the audio, the speed runs are for different sets. the first few audio coach runs had great background music, but the later ones don't seem to have that, and I'd love it if there was an option to bring it back. I don't have playlists on my phone. All in all love it, but want more.

Good, but not enough silence. There are some great things in this app and I want to like it. The problem is they talk waayy too much during the meditations. I know they're supposed to be guiding you, but every time I would start to get my mind still, a voice would jump in and go on a minute long directive. I never finished a session without feel frustrated. I've tried many other meditation apps and this one offers the least amount of silence, which is a priority when learning to meditate.
App throws error screen after every action I take making it impossible to use the app and even after uninstalling and reinstalling again. Uninstalled for good. Using Android phone.

Hi! This app usually works great but hasn't been working for me lately. I've run an update and restarted my phone a few times. The app pops up when I click on it but doesn't start up or open, if that makes sense. All I see is the black Nike screen. Hopefully someone who runs the app sees this & can work on the issue. 🤞🤞When it works, it works great though. :)

I researched apps that will help me track food without the focus being on calories and this one delivers! I am even able to track added sugar in my diet. Highly recommend! I don't usually like having to pay for an app but there is a trial period and I fully plan on keeping the subscription

Great just wish it had a bit more. I use the free version and it allows me to do so much more than other apps. I have used this app on and off for around 6 years now or so if that says anything. I just wish they would add a way to set a zigzag diet as your goal (have your kcal goal for the day be different throughout the week) Overall would recommend, have lost just under 40lbs as of this review.

I don't have an Apple watch, and this app is amazing for tracking distances and paths. I highly recommend it. The best part is If you're walking around and unfamiliar location, you can retrace your steps to find where you came from. It's easy to lose your bearings walking in unfamiliar areas, I've used the map to find my way back numerous times

They fixed something that was not broken. If your main goal is food tracking, you need multiple steps just to get to that cumulative data on one screen. It's not the main page focus, unlike the previous versions. And if you are trying to track nutrients like sodium or saturated fat, you're out of luck. Too many steps to find them.

One of the most frustrating apps to use ever! 💢 Constantly logs me out every day and takes forever to load when trying to log back in. App also has lot of issues in showing available classes/passes and I wish it would show how many open spots are available so that I can coordinate to join classes with friends. The calendar skips around weird when scrolling and trying to find classes too. I literally wouldn't use this app if the dance studios I go to didn't use this system for booking.

Its great for keeping track of what you eat, finding protein counts, and the scanner feature is super convenient and easy to use. I think it needs an update of some sorts for an error message that keeps occuring, which states a food can not be added to favorites, which is another nice feature. But other than that, I haven't had any problems with it.

Zwift is great, this companion app doesn't really allow you to do anything different. It would be nice if you could select routes mid-ride or see your progress on your selected route, anything to add to the Zwift experience. It works well, just needs more to it.

Updating my 4 star review for a 5 star now, for I've been using this app for years and not only they've added more features, but haven't incorporated ads of any kind. For that only, you guys are a real g.

Just tried the app and was pleasantly surprised at how easy it was to use. I found everything I need and was able to find and apply existing codes for discounts. Super convenient I will definitely be reordering soon.
Keeps me on track for consistent sleep and exercising. Helping with my recovery from a broken leg by recommending different non-weigh bearing exercises

Hidden fees and required sign up. Hey try this free app.... but you can only use it free for a short time, and you're required to leave payment info..... seems a bit shifty to me. I don't see any mention on the download page about any of that. A little warning like "in app fees" like games put in their info would have been more honest. I can't afford fees during this pandemic.

This app used to be quite useful to track caloric intake, exercise, weight, etc. now it doesn't load beyond the startup splash screen unless I clear the app's storage, and even then I'm unable to add food to the dairy. this issue has been ongoing for a while now, and attempts to get help from support have been useless.

I like this application, but it will only let me save a few favorite foods. I eat a lot more than 15 ingredients throughout the week, and having to search for an item daily is annoying.  Great otherwise.

Zwift have made lots of improvements over the 4 years I have been using the app. But the one simple update would be to allow the companion app to go into landscape mode. I put this in my review almost 4 years ago and it's still not done. I have a lots of devices to use with the companion app but I can't use any of the decent holders I have because it only works in portrait mode so frustrating. Come on Zwift this can't be that difficult!
Have daily burn membership so this was a real let down. Couldnt register or log in. Can't use it if u can't get into it.

I've been using MFP for a decade, no issues. The new interface/update is terrible. please change it back or give us the option to use the old interface. It's not user friendly, it isn't logging or saving the food I log, and probably more, I just don't care to find out. None of it makes sense, and is a confusing mess. I'm exhausted after solving problems all day, I don't need more puzzles to solve. I'm deleting, and downloading a different tracking app until MFP reverts back.

Very useful and nice sometimes even when im not cooking i just find it fun to just look around and see what kind of food there is and what i can make with what i already have. Apple is dumb for thinking this is anything like their products it's better.

Unusable now. The app used to record the GPS accurately in the background. Now it does not record GPS at all unless the screen is open and unlocked. Which is useless for anyone running, cycling or playing music. Extremely disappointed, I used this app for years, now I have to find an app that actually works.

I paid for lifetime access to Power Pack many years ago, I didn't want any fancy features or app "improvements". I wanted a simple experience that incrementally increased the lifts I was doing. If I had known the purchase for lifetime access would be revoked once the company decided they wanted more money, I would not have purchased. I would seek a refund if I could.

I actually really love this app. the training plans are great the coaches doing the guided runs are awesome but I can never end the runs in the plans right. it always discards bc when I hold to end and save it asks if I want to discard and its an endless loop. its frustrating I really wanted to see my progress on the plan and it wont save right

The same login info I use on my PC and in the other DailyBurn app (the one to let you watch videos from my phone) refuses to accept my credentials, making this 100% worthless. Also I'm noticing the DB team hasn't responded to others who've made the same complaint so... Not impressed with that either.

It's a truly great app that's let down in one area. The app has outstanding depth of reports for your nutritional information. It is very localised to Australia and it's very easy to add new foods. The interrupting full screen ads are hyper disruptive, often starting when you are doing an entry. They last about 5-8 seconds. it's faster actually closing the app and reopening. Maybe you ask, why not super with premium, I don't like to do subscriptions and there's no life time one purchase.

I used the app when each workout showed the list of exercises it contained, separated, each exercise in it's own video. It was perfect. You could see the overall of the workout and choose which workout suited you. You could know which exercise is next and get ready for it, with breaks at certain steps. It was guided, user oriented, most amazing UX. Although the exercises are good nonetheless, it has become easier to exercise on youtube than here. I sooo miss that perfect version.

Very disappointed with Google Fit. I walk around 5,000–10,000 steps daily, but the app fails to track them properly. Many times, it doesn’t count steps at all or shows incorrect data. I’ve already checked permissions, battery settings, and updates—everything is in place, yet the issue continues. This makes the app unreliable and defeats its entire purpose. Please fix this ASAP. A fitness app that can’t track steps accurately is unacceptable.
Not sure what happened but this app is unusable now. Should've left it alone... Guess I'm using the mobile website.

Cant do the basics for runners. Very poor splits data and (more importantly) it freezes and looses gps points. I use the gps with other apps and it is flawless. It wouldn't be so bad if you could correct your data somehow but there is no way to do that. So without all of the gps points, the distances and all of the rest of the metrics are off. Very bummed because Underarmor has a great reputation in everything else. Even the socks i am currently wearing are UA. TLDR: good socks, bad apps.
most of the the discount which are exhibited in website are not showing in the app version.

Lots of clutter, constant ads, and pushing to buy premium. Getting worse over time, more and more pop up stuff. I don't need you to pop up a screen congratulating me for using the app every day. update: moved to one star. somehow they made it even worse. navigating through your food diary is way clunkier, added more steps and doesn't show all the food unless you go to a separate screen. no easy way to flip between days and see your diary on previous days. slower and more button pushes needed.

The app is pretty great overall, it's a really easy way to check for studios nearby and book classes. Unfortunately, for some reason it seems to sign me in to the class when I am standing outside of the locked studio and it is too late to go into the class, even if I dismiss the notification to sign in. It would be nice if they made the sign in process for booked classes more deliberate. Also, the categories tend to get pretty messy in the wellness and beauty sections.

1★ – Very disappointing. The step counter keeps resetting during the day. It starts counting correctly in the morning, but suddenly all steps disappear and the counter restarts from 1. This defeats the whole purpose of a fitness tracking app because the daily activity data becomes completely inaccurate. I am using a OnePlus Nord and even after checking permissions and battery settings the issue still happens. Please fix this serious bug.

Please remove the slide to indicate the time of last serving of caffeine. It is a struggle to select the correct half hour time interval. Please update to entering a time manually closer to the Android Clock app. The current setup is horrific to use for people with bigger fingers.
App needs work. Step count doesn't update in real time! There should be no reason that it can't keep track in real time. Like other reviews have stated. Not impressed at all.

I have been using this app for years and loved it. But within the last few months it has completely gone downhill. A few things: I walked the same trail a number of times and it recorded a different mileage each time. It didn't gps my correct location and had me zig zaging all over the place miles away. Has frozen and not come back up in the middle of workouts. Longed a few of my hikes 2.5 miles more than they actually were. Calories burned seem to go by time not considering miles or elevation.
Awesome measuring app. I can tell how long and far I've run since I started a couple of years back. It can be better by fixing a bug that prevents finishing runs sometimes.

Much better connection reliability these days and lots of functionality.
