It's still the most convenient way to browse and join events (even if a search facility would be useful) or look at results and in game is useful, even though I now have Zwift Play. For example the chat log in game is useful to refer to etc.

Came back to lifting after surgery and some life stuff, shocked to see that owners of the lifetime premium option are now forced to pay a predatory subscription fee. Not only that but there's no longer a free option as the dev has become money-hungry and couldn't care less about longstanding users. There are better free apps out there. I hope this app either fails or completely redesigns itself after this ridiculous step backwards.

since the Fitbit app changed to this my fitbit does not work. it dies quickly, the time is wrong, it will not sync, ntifications for calls and text do not work anymore, and it can be fully charged and dies within a few hours. it is very frustrating that it is not working correctly. I have been using my fitbit for years but this is making me want to change unless it starts working properly. also, I really do not like the new app features. my fitbit dies more quickly will I open the app.

Everything about this app is user-friendly. My only gripe, and it is miniscule. if you stop for a moment, you have to pull the phone and check it before getting back underway as it will stay paused. Very annoying when you find yourself 2/3 through your activity and it's not being recorded by the app. However, being a free app, who could possibly complain? Just keep aware.
App is too limited and has small ambitions, but it does what it says. The barcode scanner is pretty cool. More metric measurement from users would be nice, but it's not dev's fault.

There are some exercises you can use from the program. Personally I just use this app for is graph and keeping track of progress. It also connects to Health Connect to help you keep track of calories used, it's sometime takes a while for it show up on health connect app though. I have to open and close the apps until the other apps finally cycle so the way through.

Editing my review on April 4th 2026. I purchased the powerpack in July 2016. All I had ever wanted was tracking, auto-increase and automatic alternative training session app. Now, with the app being completely subscription-only, I am also deleting it from my devices since the app is riding the enshittification wave many others are more than happy to join. What a shame.

The total cost of ownership in insanely high. They change their terms of use without notification or possibility of refund. I use it only, because I can't find any alternative. It's a great device, but terrible company and value. The support is just some cheap AI.
Ads would be removed
great app! Good recipe ideas and good meals. I would like to see more healthy meals (more veggies and less cheese and pasta). I use this every week when I meal plan.

Good app and I recommend it anyone looking for an exercise tracking app. But why in the world does the swimming not include 'yards' and 'meters' as a distance? That's what the overwhelming majority of swimmers keep track of...not 'miles'.

Good app but inconsistent. I walk/run the exact same path everyday. But the app showed consistently different lengths for the path over the duration of use. Also noticed that when trying the premium version the measured distance was more than when using the free version. I am not sure if this is a problem of the GPS - even if it is I am sure that there is a way to calibrate the app to consider average of all the readings. In any case I hope the developers manage to make improvements to accuracy

The Google Health 'update' from FitBit is bad on a good day. It has a long lag when adding information or doesn't log calories/water into your total at all until you delete and re-enter the food/water. Good chance I will be changing from a Fitbit to a different watch when my current one is ready to be updated. * Developer response was to log out and back in - that was the second thing I did, after a power off/on and before I left the review.
Great app! I used to have issues with signing in but realized that I had a space after my username that was messing it up

I loved this app! It helped me so much with meditation and controlling my anxiety. I just wish it wasn't so expensive 🥺 or that there was financing options. I've tried so many other meditation apps and none of them compare. I love the narrator, the interface, the customizable scenes and background noises. But the thing I love the most about this app that I haven't found with others is the daily guided meditation. I found myself looking forward to it each morning. Please make more affordable ❤.

Update: - 2 stars. sometimes failes to track GPS data. Records first 200 meters and that's it. I still can go and check my route in Google maps. Good app for running and works well with Scoche Rhythm+ in premium version. -1 star for unreadable white font on a light map when sharing to social media.

Needs some work. Didn't the app get tested? After I typed in CC code and entered it, page went to Google play store to rate app instead of showing me my receipt. One item I'm getting 2 because it flat out refused to update the amount to 1. It's only 2.60 so I'll just get 2. I need the rest of the order.
High-quality soundscapes, wise tidbits of thought, and useful tools for sleep and wake. This is invaluable for anyone trying to keep their calm in a chaotic world.
the app suddenly pauses during workout. so my recordings are not effective. what a bad surprise when I check my screen with just bad metrics on it. please, tell me what is wrong

In the last month I have been having issues with whoop keeping the connection between the device and the phone. It is annoying because I have to resinc the device multiple time a day if I want to see my data. Contacted support, they sad is a bug they are aware and they will fit asap however a month sounds too much. And I see this is an issue impacting a lot of people paying for the service. Whoop, take care of your customers!

DON'T UPGRADE IF YOU CAN AVOID IT! The fitbit app had a lot of flaws. Google Health addresses none of them and is even less user friendly. There were so many things I could see at a glance that now require additional swiping, scrolling, and tapping. The nutrition info is particularly annoying, although I will say that when logging food, it does seem to bring up previously logged foods more quickly. Overall, just cluttered, unintuitive, and unnecessary.
After investing about an hour into building recipes, there seems to be a bug that the app is not pulling all ingredients to my shopping list.

Can no longer track sleep. I do not have sleep issues so I don't check the app often. The last 3 nights it's recorded only half of my sleep. The first two nights I was traveling and so I thought okay not too weird... No last night I crashed at 10:15 to make up for the lost sleep and the app says I didn't go to bed until 1:30?? So not sure if it's a calibration issue or what but I LOVED my fitbit and now it's back to being just a pedometer. If it's not broken, don't fix it. :(

Never seen an app as convoluted as Garmin Connect. Even basic stuff like checking steps etc takes forever clicks. I hate it each time I open it just like I hated Fitbit Charge 4 when I wanted to charge it (obviously charging failed first). With Garmin Connect the connection is something that fails every time and it is so by design. The watch is very nice.
Overly a decent app. Would be nice to download workouts to avoid them buffering when connectivity is horrible. And also if you could select what exact equipment you have when filtering workouts.

The User Manual and available support and info make this app a delight! The Android home screen widgets weren't working at first. Then, after a quick search, I saw a post by a support person mentioning you can utilize the long press icons similar to widgets. "Add Food" or "Scan Barcode" etc, options, can be placed as home screen shortcuts. Great work, from a fellow Canadian! 🍁

MapMyRide is one of the best bike computers you can use. If they have problems they solve them pretty fast. There's a coach that can give you various stats on your workouts out loud while you're riding. I really love that feature. It's totally adjustable as is everything else. You can add a photo to your workout which I like. I've been using them for 5 years and I can look back on any past ride I want. I use it to track and it's the best. I ride a Norco Rideau hybrid bike.
Used to be grt but is useless now, can't log in to the app. Please fix it!

Overall, I appreciate this app. I do think it is helping me stick to my goals. My only complaint so far is that it doesn't have a simple start/stop button for fasting, you have to select a timeframe. My schedule changes every day and sometimes I only fast for 15 hours and sometimes I fast for 18 or more. It would just be so much easier to have a start/stop for tracking rather than having to manually adjust the times.

I have used map my fitness for many years very happily, but the new ads with sound that pop up between screens may make me stop using it. They are jarring and attract the attention of surrounding people and you can't just hit the x to make them stop right away. I have found myself just quickly closing the app to make the noise stop. They definitely mess with my post-workout relaxed state. I hope this recent change gets re-thought.

When are we finally getting a better strength tracking option (or another Garmin app for strength/gym tracking)???? The current option is lacking, e.g. no custom or editable exercises, no improvement or comparison graphs, no search function to quickly look up weights for specific exercises,... With the current set-up, one can't even successfully export workouts to analyse them with other apps cause either metadata is lost or sync doesn't work at all. disappointing

Love using this app. It keeps track of my rides all season, I can compare my progress from season start to season end all on a free app. The ads are minimal, they do seem to be geared toward ladies products which I'm not sure if they are judging by the strength of my workouts? The data on the split times is also very useful. Highly recomend.

I have been using this app intermittently for over 8 years and I can safely say this is THE BEST app for beginner or non-competitive runners to just start learning running and track the basic stats and stay focused on the run.
Will not accept my password.. please fix this immediately! !!

Really user-friendly and it provides what you ask for. No constant notifications and reminders! You *can* ask for the reminders, but I love that it is a thing you select. It also has a good (and reliable, I've double-checked a few) crowd sourced set of foods and exercises, and it's very easy to add to.
the most accurate equipment and advises as you ride 😁

Useful but way too pushy with the AI stuff, as well as making us hold random buttons "to show our motivation". Make it optional or remove it. Also don't have streaks pop ups, your streak isn't synchronized across devices, so it always resets on one of them. The streak doesn't even track consistency, it tracks when you add food into the app, not the same. Some people have days where they just don't eat for personal reasons, that's not being inconsistent. Keep the UI simple.

What an outstanding app to follow. the calories calculated are comparable to other apps, but this app is a great tool to navigate, and I have lost 2.7 kg in 2 weeks. The motivation information is top-notch and the food scanner is easy to use. I rate the 5 star! a start to my weight loss journey. This app was recommended by a work colleague. He lost 4 stones and is still using the app for maintenance.

Thoroughly unimpressed by the latest update. Why break something that worked just fine? The colors are totally unnecessary and the design looks very unprofessional, considering what it's for and who uses it. The layout is clunky as well. I've had Fitbits for 6 years now, but I'll be looking at other options when my current one goes belly up. Good job of upending what worked well and looked just fine.

Overall great app. My only complaint is there's no auto detection if you forget to end the ride. If you're in a car going highway speeds it still tracks like you are riding. An option to toggle this or to help trim the ride after would be great.

I've been using this app off and on for years, it's always been reliable, until the new update. The update added so many extra steps, why when I got my nutrient and macro page does it automatically show weekly average? It makes no sense for me to have to change it to show just today, today should be default. The diary used to be very neat and easy but now there's so much extra scrolling involved. I will likely be switching to Lose It.

Like most I had trouble connecting it to Android. Once connected it frequently loses connection and has to resync. After wearing it for a day and nothing recorded I went to the website to start looking at returning it. I saw my data on the website. Everything is apparently getting uploaded but nothing is on the app which for a mobile fitness tracker defeats the purpose.

Too many bugs recently and Companion app on Samsung needs work. The stop button never worked fod me at least, have to jump off and end the ride on my laptop. Had it in my old Samsung and on current samsung Note10. Please can you also add average speed/HR/power to the companion app, really good feature to have to see during the app. Saves from connecting garmin in parallel just to see these eatures and deleting event afterwards fo avoid doublecounting workouts which messes with other apps

This app worked fairly well but recently it gives random numbers for heart points. I take a 3 mile hike every morning and get approximately 45 heart points for the hour long hike. Last week I got 168 heart points for the same workout and yesterday I got 256 points. I'm going to uninstall and reinstall to see if that fixes it. Edit...now this app doesn't track me very well. awful, had to restart phone as the sound would not stop playing despite pressing stop and closing the app the sound still played, not a very intuitive app annoyed I spent money on it!
Hit and miss. Difficult to view the schedule. Today is not working at all.

Best meal planning app. Just what I need to keep my meals organized. I love that I can either add a saved recipe or just type "tacos". I just wish I could move the quick notes to other days, like I can with the recipes.

It's a good app. I use a Fossil Gen 6 and while I use both apps Fit seems to work better. I like tracking my vitals along with my activity for the day. THAT SAID... I have a backlog of data and don't like weight entry set up like an "odometer" and the calendar is also tedious. Just let me type in the data please. Even better, you folks seem hard core on us talking to our phones, why not just me tell Fit, without typing/scrolling at all? And bring back desktop support like all your other apps!
It's good app only if you want to pay for receipts. It's not free, has many advantage such as organizing your daily plates but cost you money...

MMRide stopped GPS tracking!!! You've got to get the basics right first otherwise the app is useless! Using Samsung S7. About two weeks ago the app stopped being able to track a route. It's a pretty awful tracker. It very frequently does not record runs. UPDATE 12/27/24: Still works great on my Pixel 8a. Allows notes on each walk/run so I can add details. It works on my Pixel 3a. GPS distance seems to be accurate and consistent. There is no one ambient sound, relaxation music, anything at all that is not for a 7-day trial period and then a charge. Every app like this should have a sentence key: "per subscription" right under its name, so people not interested in it will not waste time downloading and deleting the app.

I don't like the new format. I specifically like to see the whole day all on one screen, the way it was before. I don't like that you have to click on each meal section. It's just too many extra steps. Just a personal preference but the font was better before as well. Also, the little emojis on the side are unnecessary and distracting. Simple is better.

Just not impressed. I experience constant disconnects while playing the game. It becomes really frustrating. Nothing else on my network disconnects so I'm not sure what the problem is. With a really high monthly fee to play the game I just don't feel this companion app cuts it.

please fix app to be more compatible with android power settings instead of turning off and not being accurate. Update: this seems to be working now. Overall, it's a very strong app: the design is a little overwhelming, but the workout trainers are solid and the tips and coaching within the workouts are great. when this went from Fitbit to Google, like half my data was gone and the interface is completely different, and worse. also, my Fitbit inspire 3 used to have my regular exercise types saved for easy access (something I could change in app) and now I have to scroll through every option to get down to "yoga". it's really annoying and I have a worse product now. switch it back.

UPDATE: 05/31/26. Versa 4, S23 Ultra. unpaired, reset, and paired again. This app is a downgrade. Easy to use, I can follow friends, there are options for cycling and walking, and I love that it keeps my stats. I also like that I can save each activity with a description and image!

My two wish-list items would be the ability to set an alarm for a duration starting when I fall asleep—when I set a duration alarm, it starts counting now, but what if I don't have to be up at a certain time and just want to make sure I get my 8 hours? Great app to get back in shape. 3 weeks in and I already feel much better. Very adaptive, well laid-out, and easy to use. A mat and some space and you're set ! When I first downloaded the app I got immediately into it. I usually didn't do exercises. I lost ALL my motivation because I either have to follow a rigorous and pre-set program or I have to chose from 260 different work outs. So now it's just more difficult to get into the exercise.

I'm assuming Under Armour no longer owns this app? I've been installing and uninstalling it for over a decade now. When I went to install it this last time, could not locate it under UA. Whoever took it over was smart enough to leave the 'Map My Fitness' part... I believe this app is by far, the best out of all the workout apps. It has a plethora of workout options, and is easy to navigate and use. This is THE ONLY app you will ever need!

This app used to be great, but now it constantly has issues. Every time I open the app the Workout tab is missing the categorized workout tabs. I use the app. It's insanely frustrating and not worth the effort. FIX THE APP.

when it works, this app is good. unfortunately it s usually glitching. for the past 2 months I've been unable to book classes via the app - I tried all troubleshooting and redownloaded multiple times, bit the issue persists. customer service is apparently non-existent. very disappointing.

I really like the app. It does what you need/want it to with a good, simple interface. No more, no less. However, I will NEVER buy a "subscription" to the pro version. Love the app so far, the only problem I've had is even when I add things into my pantry it still shows up on my shopping list. Other than that I'm in love with most of the features!

No clear purpose for this app. Never mind that it's got it's share if bugs, there's no clear purpose for this. You get the number of minutes you moved, and number of steps. That it. Even after you connect your workout and sleep apps, you don't see that relevant data reported. You still have to go to each individual app for analysis. How on earth is there no ANT+ support for Android? Most Android phones have ANT+ support build in, and supporting ANT+ would be a clear way to bring all ANT+ accessories into the ecosystem, especially for users who are using an iPad as a display. Given that ANT+ is a widely-used standard and many devices don't support BLE, this is a huge oversight. App function is otherwise pretty poor - seems like inputs are frequently ignored, link with main Zwift app is quite weak, data on the two devices rarely syncs up, and navigation isn't great.

I think it's ridiculous that this whole app doesn't work when my phone is offline. Why??? Why can't I change an alarm when I'm on a plane. I'll definitely end the subscription after the mandatory first year. It's just too annoying and the data way too far off. And don't get me started on the limited and overpriced bulky design options.

Give me back the old app. It's been a POS since the last update. Why do I have to log in every single time, even though I check the box that says "I'm on a personal device, keep me logged in." until the 4th month was good. after that it slowed down dramatically. every time you change a menu you have to wait 10 seconds to load. going to another app.

I spent most of my life struggling to control my sleep schedule, so this app was literally life-changing for me. Easily the best option I have found for an alarm clock. This application guarantees that I wake up on time. It is wildly customizable, letting you set a custom alarm tone that increases in volume to gradually wake you up, set wake-up tasks like simple addition or more complex puzzles before you can turn the alarm off, decide how much you are okay with waking up before the desired time, and much more. I can't recommend this app enough if you want the best from your alarm clock.

Do not like the changes from the fitbit app. the layout is not user friendly. Good app! I love that you can manage memberships and book different classes from one app. What I don't like is the interface. The app is generally good except when it comes to adding an activity after the fact, or updating the start and end times on an an activity it assumes. It is terrible with setting start and end times, particularly when crossing the noon hour.

First of all, it's so easy to use! All I have to do is open it, choose an activity and press start. This is an way to use app & I have recommended it to others.  Wish it: +items, subtract calories burned from exercise, track water intake.

Zwift do not care about their long term users. They only care about attracting new users and making as much money as possible. There is currently an issue where route badges are not being awarded. Trying to log homemade meals? Too bad. Want to see your macros when you log food? Nope, we only show calories now. Thanks, I uninstalled the app.

Newest update is AWFUL. You used to be able to see everything easily at a glance on one screen. It was simple to see if I had missed logging something. Now I have to click into each meal. By the time I am done clicking through snacks I have forgotten what the other tabs said 🤦‍♀️ I shouldn't need to create a seperate list in my notes app to keep organized. I have used mfp on and off for over a decade and this update is making me consider switching to a different app.

Rubbish. After a couple of days, the thing wouldn't let me add any more food. Don't waste your time with it. Rather use a spreadsheet! Just got it yesterday. Long time user of the app. The constant bombardment of ads for Pro when I already have Power Pack (which was a lifetime upgrade) is pretty annoying. Prior UI used to be more intuitive in my opinion. Items haven't received almost coming to 3 weeks. And my order is glitch. Can't contact live chat nor email hasn't been replied.

The app's behavior needs to be changed when the bracelet fails to capture a night of data properly! Too often, Whoop misses data collection in the middle of the night. I used to really like this app, but not a fan of the new changes. Bring back the feature where you could see all the exercises is advance! This was so helpful in setting up weights pre-workout. HELP. I really like this app. I love that I can choose to use downloaded tracks for wakeup alarms. The customization is awesome. I've used it for 5 yrs. BUT. What the hell is going on with resting calorie burn? This app is great! I knew when I first saw it that I would love it! Ive made so many healthy recipes now. The shopping list that combines all the ingredients I need is exactly what Ive been looking for, for several years! All I can say is thank you so much!!!

No issues w/app, but being an expensive device, it's silly they don't include abundant details on how to install/open/close the band clasp, they'll have you guess, you could potentially damage the device or break the band. update: I love my Whoop now

Great for seeing schedule of your favorites but search function for new studios absolutely sucks! It doesn't accept zip codes for location search (seriously?). Don't ask me to contact developer because you can see this is not a user problem; it's systematic.

The bait and switch pricing of this "calming" app made my blood boil. When you open the app is says 7 day free trial, then $47.99 per year. When you hit subscribe, the price changes to $69.99 per year. Stay away! Edit: Your response missed the point. I didn't get an unintended charge, I'm complaining about the $22 difference in advertised price vs. the price you attempted to charge. False advertising!

The app is great (when it works). I have only had the app for 2 weeks and already had to uninstall twice due to the app not saving my runs. I have just uninstalled and reinstalled for the third time now. Pretty disheartening when you're trying to train for a marathon!

I have used this app for 4ish years now, and it was always pretty reliable. But recently (2025) it keeps stopping mid-workout. Easily 4/5 of my bike rides end with me finding the app stopped miles ago. My phone hardware, software, and even my route hasnt changed. The app gives you a lot of great features, but sadly they are meaningless if the basic function fails. In its current state, i cannot recommencd if you are a user who wants a semi-accurate record.

New format is unusable. You can't preview workouts to see exercises or equipment. The instructors are beyond obnoxious and you can't mute, play music, or even have less talking without getting 0 info on exercises or even when to change from one exercise to another. It's just Youtube videos but worse, because the streaming is incredibly slow. On 1000/1000 upload/download internet I had the stream buffer 3 times in a 10 min workout. Absolutely destroyed this app.

App is exceptional for all the free features. Food logging is a breeze making is easy to stay on track. Not to mention, you get badges for logging food and weight, who doesn't like some extra motivation? Barcode scanner is part of the free version, how many apps can say that? I've tried the big name apps and I've used MFP, paid and free and honestly, Fatsecret is my favorite. I've been using it for over two months now and have lost 15 lbs.

I have the paid MVP version and the mileage tracking keeps stopping, so something is going on with this app. I saw 2 other reviews that had the problem I had in my last two rides in the last 4 days. The app tracks my distance and speed up to a point, then the distance tracker stops--first at 8.19, then at 8,82. The timer continues, but the speed and distance tracker stop. When I touch the screen, the mileage drops back to zero, so I have no record of my ride distance. This needs to be fixed or I will have to replace the app

This app really helps me lose weight.... when I take the time to use it! Calorie tracking is super easy, adding activity is also easy. You can add foods that are not already in the app (but you'd be surprised at what is already there). The activity choices could be a bit better (hot yoga, please), but I can usually find something that is similar to what I'm doing. Added bonus is a VERY supportive community of users, many of whom face similar challenges with fitness or weight loss, and reading their posts, along with the replies of support and suggestions is a nice place to go in this increasingly uncivil world.

Actually they charged me $18.35 on my first day. It said $16.99 (nothing about tax) and it did say I might be charged 2 days before the 7 day trial began. It's a scam and I want my money back immediately because that's how fast you took it. I would have understood a hold or a couple of dollars to see if the charge card was good, and most of Calm the stuff is on free apps. Great way to bring in long time first time customers...not. Then when I went to send an email to them it took 15 minutes.

I loved working out with the Nike training app, however. I have difficulties to see my workouts in landscape, not in portrait. The same thing with picture quality whatsoever. It hasn't been fixed. I usually used it on iOS version rather than Android. This is not what I want. Worst of all, my Google Fit used to track down my movements until it showed me smaller progress.

Creating challenges is very limited. I tried to create a pushup challenge and the only option that was relevant was, "gym workout." It didn't have an option for greatest volume or most reps. When choosing an activity to track it has several statistics that it will help you track over the course of a workout which is awesome, but when I choose "upper body," the stats on display are related to cardio. They can changed, but have to be reapplied if you move to a cardio workout. I love the feed.

It's a good way to track your calories and your exercise. your water intake. It does not talk to Google very well and it's very aggressive if you don't turn off it's notifications. it will harass you all day long until you put something into it. so make sure when you download it you turn off the notifications that way it's on your terms instead of on its terms. edit: 2 out of 5 stars is not a great review. did you even read this? sounds like you have a standard reply for everything.
drops page often, irratic navigation from products to cart, drops or adds products, will NOT accept my credit card...EVER!👎

two runs down after a 20 year hiatus from running...coach is so encouraging to the point its a bit annoying, but if you need that it is available. I actually do think he helps. you can always turn him off if you get too annoyed. Overall thoroughly enjoying the app so far. Thanks Nike even though you do use slave labor to build your shoes. which is why I don't buy your products, and to this point the app is free, so you don't have to feel conflicted.

updated review: If you are a strength and conditioning athlete, this is not for you. It is not good with how it applies your strength training to your strain nor is it effective at tracking your heart rate if you do anything other than walk, run, or row. I just finished a burpee HIIT session where it logged it as zone 0, which of course make zero sense. I paid too much money to only use a few of its features.

Incredible how this is free. My only real complaint is the hassle to add new brands. I live in Brazil and sometimes I have to use similar brands to count calories because the product I'm consuming is not on the database.

This app provides/records a fair amount of data; it is a shame it is being end-of-lifed. I bought the app a few years ago (after which they expired that app and encouraged people to pay again for functionality in the new app). The app has died a number of times without interaction (eg if taking a photo during the exercise), but I've looked at a couple of other apps that aren't as functional (albeit with more social-media options) as this. It is a shame Endomondo is ending 😞

I like how fatsecret offers stuff you would have to pay for with my fitness pal, but inputting food in the daily diary is not as easy. It doesn't automatically or quickly bring up the foods I commonly eat when I start typing so I have to search as though I never ate the food before. Also, there is less of a selection when scanning barcodes or typing in foods. So I ended up just not counting calories at all. Fatsecret should see what is different when doing searches compared to MFP.

love it! Discovered the app back in 2012/2013-ish and i used it consistently back then. I did take a long break from it but recently I rediscovered it and its as good as I remembered. There's a few things that could be fixed, for instance, sometimes when you skip an exercise on a workout it gets stuck and the BEEP to start again wont work and time is just running. Sometimes the app will get stuck for no reason and close on its own. But the actual workouts themselves are great.

Very disappointed, I was expecting to be able to download the app, put my tablet on the handle bars and away I go, like I can with bkool. Oh no the app doesn't has limited functionality which doesn't include connecting your turbo trainer. Seriously what's the point. Amateurs!!!!

The sleep score seemed a bit off so one night I tested it. I sat & bed & scrolled IG for around 3 hours but otherwise pretty still. The hand O scrolled with was the same my watch was on. In the morning, the watch gave me an 82 sleep score and claimed I was in "deep sleep" from around 11:30pm to 1am even though I was scrolling. I went to the bathroom at 1:15 which it recorded accurately as me being awake but the fact that it had me in deep sleep while I was wide awake shows how bogus this is.

Only major complaints are have to log in to the full app to see workouts and the compainion app wants my cadence to be 50 RPMs higher than the game! Doesn't sound like much but spinning at 95 and it wants 120... not doable for me.

Maps glitch when used on interior screen. App Notification should have a search box vs scrolling thru a long list of apps on the phone. Items are listed in a non-alphabetical order, a search box should be provided.
Simple to use. Lots of useful options. Good info. Whether I'm walking or riding my bike, great data tracking.

Really clumsy to buy a pass from my favorite yoga studio. Honestly it's so frustrating to try to figure this out every time. Also integration to Strava stopped working months ago. It's goofy, I know, but it pisses me off. I sign up for the yoga challenges, do the work, and then nothing. Guys, you've got to do better... Update! Response from developer includes an un-clickable link. Thanks! Also got that integration to Strava fixed yet? No? Grrrr.
It offers little value. Just a chart. Plus pop ups asking you link more accounts since Google would love to have more of your data. No benefits for us, just more data for them to steal from us.

This app was awesome until the latest upgrade, but now the ads are horrendous. They send you to ads online and in the play store, and getting back to the app isn't always easy. I didn't mind exiting out of the ads on the last version, but this is too much. It's not worth the $30 they're asking so I'm looking for an alternative. Edit: they've fixed the way ads work so I've upped my rating.

Only downside is a run will not stop or pause if phone dies while tracking. Result was a run logged at over an hour long (time continued to run after the phone charged and turned back on, only ending when I realized and manually stopped it). Phone died at end of run, so distance was correct, but duration doubled. Apart from this awesome app! Edit: 'My Coach' feature, does not sync properly with NTC app. Your NTC workouts will not be ✅'d off and count toward plan. Support couldn't fix bug.

I'm back, and yet nothing has improved. UI is not intuitive, blocky, wastes space, Bluetooth issues, it's riddled with bugs. Contacted the developers, rude and stated they will not be changing anything as "only a few people had negative experiences". Decent app. I happily paid for the pro version before it went subscription and it was great. When it switched to a subscription model I lost the ability to save my workouts to Google Drive. Bought a fitbit just before the update to Google health. It is missing so many basic features now that it's fairly useless. So I can't even rate the actual app in itself because it won't even let me login, I know my password is right because when I sign in on the main daily burn app, it works.
Tried using my dailyburn sign in to gain access, but it keeps telling me I have a wrong password. I sign in fine everywhere else though. Too bad.

I've been using this app on and off for the last years. I love that the app keeps me motivated by giving me badges when I am consistent. The coaching workouts are great too. There's also exclusive discounts you can get for being a nike member and participating in challenges. Over all a great appfor beginners and more experienced runners. Playlist r choice 2

great. but I dislike the handling of the journal enty. if you hit the wrong place and get to the description, when you go back to the journal you have to scroll and scroll down to item. also, the coach is very repetitive and you can't see previous "conversations".

I'm trying to be forgiving but I'm upset. No issues for a few years. The main reason for downloading was the only app thar plays through your headphones allowing your partner to not be disturbed. Can't cancel online, cancel page was not loading. I love this app 4 tracking all my workouts. I wish I could give it 5 stars. One major disappointment is that it seems to no be able to communicate with all the other "MapMy..." apps. Very bad app .. slow and not helpful at all ... Order from website is better

Love this app, but so so buggy on Android. Would love to see more upper body workouts. Many / most (?) seem oriented for women, which is fine.. but a few more for blokes would be lovely. For such a premium service, the amount of bugs I constantly encounter are unacceptable. - Haptic alarm frequently never goes off - Docs say to turn off battery optimization, but this means my phone is nearly dead by morning. - There's no way to start tracking an activity without a data connection. This used to work, but now only sleep and nap activities are available "offline". Easily reproducible by turning on airplane mode with Bluetooth on (to connect to the strap).

This app is inaccurate (steps counter) and has gross inconsistencies on distances. For example, it correctly tracked my 13k steps and initially told me I walked 5.5 miles (roughly accurate). WORST APP EVER IT IS TAKING 10MIN TO LOGON WITH FULL SPEED OF INTERNET SUCH PATHETIC APP😠🤬🤬🤬

Because the phone app doesn't display on the Watch I couldn't even look at this for pace guidance. No point in paying for Premium. App needs improving and ideally Watch connectivity.
First of all it makes you sign up. Secondly, I Can't even sign up, there's "a problem with the information" ?? Wow

The app is decent but still needs lots of work. doesn't show current calories burned during workouts. my biggest gripe is if you have an opposite schedule during the work week (graveyards or swings). despite manually putting in a nap it overwrites it and makes it a "sleep." it's frustrating when it automatically overwrites your manual inputs and replaces it with incorrect "auto detection."

I'm using the same login as on the computer and video app.  Says wrong pass or username.  I will change the review once this is fixed and I can actually see how the app is.  I am using a Galaxy Note 3.

HR accuracy is getting worse. Really want to like this. The concept is great, the information is awesome, but the hardware just can't make it happen. Was good initially but seemed to get worse with every firmware update. Really unfortunate. Truly hope it gets better. Will update if it does. Edit: reducing to 1 star. The data is getting worse and worse. The hardware is very finicky and erratic. Edit: Wish it would import gps data instead of needing to track itself. Causes large battery drain.

Essential if using Zwift. Works brilliantly. Only thing that could be better would be a button to go into and out of the current ride. Managing that is a bit fiddly, but when the legs are burning then that could be viewed as useful distraction ;-)
Use to be good now won't let me login keeps saying wrong password but on the regular site it let's me login no problem so definitely this app is the problem.

I've used this app for years, before UA decided to take over. Now I can't share on social media today after an update over a week ago? Can't upload photos. I used to use this app all the time - for years - to map my run, but in October 2019 they updated it with "better GPS" and it is terrible now. My routes are at least .1 to .5 miles off and my times are about 30 seconds slower. As a competitive runner who consistently runs 5Ks I know my routes and my times and this app is completely inaccurate now. Do not use this app if you want accuracy in your runs.
Used to be a great free app, with the option to buy a life time version. Now forces you to pay a subscription.

I'm not sure yet if I will keep it. I found it very inaccurate. The resting heart rate is good, but everything else is not. Sleep detection is disappointing, with a lot of false activity detected. Additionally, the app is currently experiencing issues with servers, even though it shows as "operational" in the status. Also, the Whoop is bigger than expected and not comfortable at all.

This app is more friendlier for at home workouts with limited equipment than most. I like to use for yoga and cardio exercises. I like the quick short hiit sessions as a finisher when i want to spice up my routine. I have seen changes in my body, stamina, and flexibility combining with my home workouts. Only glitch is that freezes up or want connect to my tv, or if it does, feeezes and crash.

The new UI isn't all bad. It's easier to find some sections now than in the old version, but I wish I could rearrange tiles without having to remove and re-add them in a specific order. I really dislike that the Coach button and occasional AI summaries are still there even when you opt out of using AI; it feels like I'm being badgered to use it. Downgraded from 5 to 1 because it's been completely unreliable and an hindrance to my runs. Timing, audio, all messed up. Either this app helps you time travel, in which case I give it 5/5, or they messed it up after the last update, which then justifies my 1/5.

This app has everything I need to track my food and supplements. I've been pretty impressed by the photo logging feature. Its accuracy is surprisingly good and when it makes a mistake I understand why and it's easy to fix. I use it mostly on mobile and find it convenient and easy to interact with. I'm using the paid version and in my opinion it's worth the price.

Absolutely HATE the new update. RIP MFP
The free version works great now; they got rid of the really annoying ads and now just have ads for the premium version which is fine. Works well, barcode feature is great.

Ruined the app with the latest update. It was fantastic before, worked great. You could see the contents of every workout to see if that's something you'd want to do. You could pause a workout at any time to get a preview of the current excersise to make sure you're doing it correctly. Now that's all thrown out the window. Now the app just plays a single long video per workout, it's no better than YouTube. A total regression in usability and quality.

Good way to track and keep "on track" my riding . Still working great at the end of 2018. Like weekly and yearly totals. Still working in 2019, great apt. Still working in 2020. Still working in 2023 still working in 2025. accurate mileage. working great in france 2026

Zwift companion is a excellent add on to the zwift platform, it makes the gameplay more engaging being able to interact with other riders and makes workouts much easier especially when you need to watch your power values. The companion app means I can connect my Bluetooth trainer when I'm not using my laptop via ant+. Nice work Zwift.

I love this app, so easy to use. It is free to check net carbs on this app. It is also better than my fitness pal because it doesn't add calories you can eat for the day when you exercise. There aren't as many products listed as MyFitnessPal, but I always just add my products in myself. I eat a lot of the same foods so I have gotten used to it.
I exercise with this every day. Thought I'd down load the tracker. It wont let me sign in.

Powerful aspect of my wellness program. Tracks and analyzes every aspect of my physiology with the option to pick from a variety of variables to fine tune its wellness impact. The monthly subscription made me tentative about trying out Whoop, but now that I've had it for several months, I never want to be without it for the rest of my life, not exaggerating. Especially considering that Whoop is persistent in improving its software and hardware. I do recommend it to family and friends already.

This is the best app I've found for tracking what I eat. It actually helps me PLAN my meals in advance while making adjustments for the reality checks that happen throughout the day- co-workers bring donuts, I miss a meal because of a meeting, etc. Also, because it's been a lround for a long time, it has a VERY robust food index/library- the biggest I've found. And if it's not there, it's really easy to find parts you're looking for and put them together in a meal! Thanks for the great work.

Truly this used to be a great app. Now upon reinstalling it I've come to find they've removed the barcode scanner for free users and you now have to pay. I refuse to pay for an app that does not work and the glitches this app has does not make me want to pay for it. The Fitbit steps sync up great for a few days and then they don't work anymore and no amount of uninstalling or reinstalling will change it. Customer service is so hard to get a hold off. I absolutely love Vitacost, but this app makes it so hard to shop. It is extremely glitchy and slow to respond. It has been that way for such a long time. I just hope they make improvements to the app, to make the experience complete.

Was ok for tracking outdoor runs or walks. Workouts was awful to use, kept changing everything over to kgs, I would change it back, do a set put in my weight I used and it changes back to kgs. AWESOME APP!  My friend and I just joined a fitness challenge and they are having us track our calories and protien intake.  This app allowed me to change the range our trainer wanted us to stay in between and has a huge database of food.  The scanner on the phone app works super fast unlike a weight watchers one I used to use.  So for I am impressed in 1 DAY!!!! AND IT'S FREE :)

I really enjoyed the workouts with generic voices and no chatter (e.g. no speed limit, 360 degrees stronger). Unfortunately I can't give more than 1 star because I can't log into my account on my tablet. On the sign in screen I can enter my email and password, but clicking the "sign in" button does nothing. My login information is correct, I use this app on my phone without any problems.

I originally gave it four stars now would give it zero. I've had the paid Android version for a few years. I was very pleased with it until a few months ago when it kept dropping GPS and misreporting times. I uninstalled it and reinstalled it and installed a GPS tracking app and kept the screen on during workouts. It worked very well until 5 days ago when again it kept dropping the GPS and keeping wrong times. There are no updates from the company. I'm tired of dealing with it. I'm moving on.

Where to start, first off it has the worst designed band I have ever used, the data it collects is wrong ( that is assuming it tracks data which most of the time it doesn't ) It doesn't track sleep most of the time, heart rate data is laughably off, then the app, not sure what to say here. I really don't mind the design but it can't sync most of the time. Terrible service. Save your money because there is no value in whoop !

Great organization; atrocious performance The apps ability to show a variety of classes from a large amount of different type of studios is amazing. I've been using this app for years. The background sounds help me focus by blocking audible distractions. The sleep stories help my son get right to sleep. Also, most meditations are ten minutes, follow a similar structure, and have you focus on the breath. More variety in lengths, structures, and objects of attention would be welcome.
Love this app! So easy to use. Love that you can scale up or down depending on the amount of servings. And it does the calculating for you.

It used to be a great app but since the last update I've been experiencing a lot of bugs, the programs section is frozen and when I go to Browse More Programs I'm unable to start any exercise there, already tried the End Program and to uninstall and reinstall but is not working. Smart wake-up feature no longer seems to work, the alarm always goes off at the end of the time, despite nothing changing about my phone or my sleeping situation. Possibly something Android has changed in a system update? Still fabulous for making me actually get out of bed to turn the alarm off.

Update 3/25/21:App does retain a lot of local data as, over time, it I increasingly uses more storage. Why not cloud storage older data? Also, my distances are quite different than other devices. Bring back the OG fitbit app. This one Su*ks!!!! 😡 Where is the function to add my own food items! I make a TON of my own food and need to log without having to scan a package!!
