I'd also like to see a way to add reps instead of weight once you complete a workout X times. I'm posting feature requests here because I can't figure out how to make the request in-app... I'm just given the option to copy a block of text when I click that link.
The calm app has been on my phone for years! I predominately use it for sleep, but it's great for writing when you need a certain ambiance as well.
I don't like the app i rather stick to website
App is too slow.it takes a lifetime to add each item and then you have to scroll through your entire list again to find the next item. Computer based version works much smoother.

Latest update made it harder to use the app. Removed the +/- weight button so now you have to manually type it in. No quick way to view what plates you need. Now you had to click through 3 sub selections to view a picture of what plates you need. And most annoying of all: when the chime timer goes off it turns down my music AND THEN LEAVES IT TURNED DOWN. So I have to stop and mess with the volume buttons a few times to get it back to where it was

This is a really excellent fitness programme. However, it is nigh on impossible to download the sessions. I just get "connection error" over and over, over wifi or 4G. Eventually I force stop the app, and that almost always fixes it for one time but now I'm fonding that that doesn't even work. It's very disappointing considering the workouts are excellent. Come on, Nike, you can fix this.

Using this product is so frustrating. Why doesn't heart rate broadcast work reliably? I've reached out to support but to be honest this just isn't worth the amount of time needed to troubleshoot. You charge a premium price and waste your users time with an unreliable product. Update: it stopped working again after just 10 days and a lengthy conversation with their support team. This device makes me wanna rip my hair out

Sleep and step syncing is perpetually delayed despite all troubleshooting. It's spotty enough that all my data is off and my whoop age is +2 yrs. Samsung health and Google fit only logged 1100 of the 26000 steps from Saturday. It's thrown off my carb and calorie tracker. I don't need to the net gains/losses days after I drop out of ketosis. Also, the app looks like it was organized by a hoarder with untreated Schizophrenia and ADHD. Convoluted, disorganized, and random. Make it allcustomizable!

The best tracking app available! If you're interested in your overall health beyond how many calories and macro nutrients you consume, this app if for you! It tracks the lion's share of important nutrients and minerals in addition to the macro nutrients. It tells you calories consumed vs calories burned (taking into account the calories you burn just by existing). The icing is I have yet to type in a food that wasn't listed, and only two items I've scanned were missing. LOVE this app!

update The paid version features have so many bugs. The free version you don't notice the errors because you can't adjust anything. However as soon as you make any adjustments the program gets very confused. It cannot keep track of your goals and reverts back and forth from defaults to adjusted numbers. It also continually loses your water intake. It changes your favorite to French so you can't read. it double counts exercise, It counts fiber as zero kcal which is wrong, it changes your target

The app features and playlist options wre good but the app itself is sooooo difficult to navigate, that Calm itself is ironically frustrating. I don't meed to get worked up and frustrated before bedtime trying to find my Favourites. And the settings options are pretty unhelpful. You need to have a photographic memory to remember "oh no, that setting isn't what all the other apps put in your account details or options, that 'sound off' setting is in the tiny icon top left, home screen".
The title says it all, I can't log in with my Daily Burn account. The android support for this stuff is lacking. (Nexus)
Vitacost is great, the app on Android not so much. Some features simply go to blank screens. Search and selection works fine. Trying to review previously ordered items has not worked for a long time.

Best recipe and meal planning app I've ever used! Great features, adding and categorizing recipes is a breeze compared to Paprika and others I've used in the past. The UI is beautiful too! Love that the experience is nearly the same on both my phone (Android) and on the iPad (iOS) as I often interchange the two when cooking. My only "criticism" would be that the nutrition section is fairly basic, but all of the main info is there. Would love to see some sort of integration with Fitbit or Lifesum in the future, but until then I'll continue using and loving Prepear!

The app uses intrusive ads now every time I sign up for a class. Which is most days. I didn't want green chef yesterday or the day before or today. I clicked the needs improvement button and it auto opened an email so I sent that feedback and today I got a server email saying my email bounced. I hate mindbody and I hope someone comes up with an alternative that my gym can use to sign up for classes

I don't like the new version of this app. First of all, I want to be able to see the overview of previous days like we could before. Now, you can only look at previous days' stats one at a time. Also, there's no quick calorie log option. Sometimes the food I'm eating isn't in the database and I just want to manually estimate the calories without logging a specific food. There's also no more favorites tab where you can save foods that you log frequently. Besides that, the new UI looks nice ig

Really helpful app, especially (but not exclusively) running in companionship with the actual workout (map, talking to other zwifters,...) , as well as accessing owns profile, finding other users or planning events. Would appreciate more access to settings as one has it internally in the game though (challenge, Avatar, gear support, loop badges and whatnot..) Besides this it's great! Easy to navigate the app.
Have been using it for years without any issues. Early wakeup and captcha options make this my go-to alarm. Would definitely recommend!

I hadn't used the app for a number of months. When I resumed, the "current speed" indicator had gone wonky. I can be heading down a hill, quickly gaining speed - with the "average speed" showing a small increase as well - but the "current speed" indicator shows a sustained and steady DECREASE in speed. Makes it impossible to monitor my speed and try to maintain a minimum. The rest of the app still works fine.

Horrible new interface. The Sleep information is not easy to read anymore. You can't look at a simple chart with all your types of sleep together anymore. The chart was the best part where you could compare your benchmark and your averages and this new interface shows less information in a worse way. It is a busy interface even on the home screen. Why would you take something that was great and ruin it. it makes me not even want to open the app and use it anymore. Sad because it was greatbefore

In terms of the services they provide, yes mindbody is great. The app however is super slow to load classes or pages, and frequently will give errors when I'm trying to pay. Since i only get insurance wellness credit if I book through the app, it's really a pain when it doesn't work. The search functionally and organization of the app is also confusing. Their search isn't very good. You can type the name of the thing exactly and it'll be #30 in the list. Needs better software support.

This latest update is horrible, it makes things look prettier at the expense of usability. I can't delete multiple items at once. I can't copy an item or multiple items from past days without selecting all. I can't swipe to look at the previous day. The Log previous meal box is too big and easily wrongly pressed causing me to have to delete every item one by one. The AI support is horrible.
I love this app, it has helped me out very much however lately I have been getting an error when trying to add things into my "favorite" food and i cant favorite anything...

Can't even count how many times this app bugged mid run and completely ruined my running data...stuck on the same kms while I'm still running, which makes pace go way higher and lose a lot of kms that were ran and not saved. I will never propose this app to anyone I know!

I'm on the free trial and I'm glad I haven't started paying. The app is trash. It gives me all these averages, but when I want to see something as simple as my heart rate throughout the day it isn't there. It collects so much data, but when I want to see it, there is no way to get to it. For how expensive the subscription is, I expected A LOT more. Canceling my trial before the month is over for sure.

Bring back downloadble workouts, please! Having everything in a class format burns up too much mobile data and the app crashes all the time, unless using WiFi. It's also super inconvient to not be able to see what moves are in any given workout ahead of time, or to easily repeat or skip ahead to a different part of a workout. Nike, just do it!

I used to love this app but it's gotten worse and worse. Now it won't even let me sign in on my phone, I'm having to use my computer. It's a shame because I don't want to have to move all my recipes somewhere else after 6 years of using this app to manage them, but I may need to.

The app has great data but it's not intuitive. It shows you data and graphs when it comes to deep sleep, REM, light sleep and a bunch of other stuff including sleep efficiency. There's no benchmarks though so it's not easy to see how well you're sleeping compared to the average. Fitbit and Samsung have much more intuitive interfaces and you don't have to dive into a bunch of FAQs to figure it out. The smart awake alarm clock is also not available to set on the watch and only the app.

no way to add custom exercises. the app will only put it in from a designated list. want to put in you stretched? it'll say foam roll. you did a barbell row? it'll say high row is closest they have. with everything being incorrect in name the coach is worthless as it really doesn't know what you've actually been doing edit: they responded but that's not what I'm asking. the manual workouts still need to be part of the pre existing list they have or you can't add it. no custom naming allowed

I just downloaded this app. It said it was for free , but everything I clicked on is not if I want any recipes for dieting , I have to subscribe and pay. if I want to track my weight I have to pay if I want to do voice recording I have to pay this said it was a free app without subscribing or paying. I'm deleting this ASAP and Deleting notifications and blocking this app. Nobody wants to help anymore It's all about money. it's a app it's not like you're a personal trainer in the gym. fs.
I paid full price for Stronglifts Pro and now I suddenly can't even use the app unless I pay them a monthly subscription. This is outright theft and fraud and merits a class-action lawsuit.

This used to be a great app, but the most recent update is a major step back. Meals you save and then use on another day used to put the exact foods, so you could easily customize amounts. Now the meal gets added as a single item Search algorithm got a huge downgrade. You used to be able to search by maker and no longer can. Searching Starbucks in my recents doesn't bring up my Starbucks drinks, I now need to type the specific drink

Love the app and the content available. One of the very few apps which have no requirements of payment of any kind. There's a workout for everyone in this app - whether you are post partum, don't have enough time, want strength training or no equipment at all, want a calming flow, there's something for everyone. And it's really easy to follow. Highly recommended as a pilates coach myself.

It's a free app with lots of professional coaches to do workouts with. I've been using it for more than year and I did more than 100 workouts with it. I feel very active and keep on working during the programs nike has since a real person is exercising with me. If you cast it on your TV, it feels more like being in a gym or a workout class.

The change over from Fitbit to the Goodle Health platform is terrible. Supposedly the new design is supposed to reduce the number of clicks, but I am having to click more to get to the previous days stats, whereas before I could just do one click to see all the data. Now I have to click on every item I want to look at from the previous day. Google can say this is more intuitive all they want, but honestly, it's awful. This change is simply an AI data grab. Bring back the old format.

Generally an excellent app I've been using this app for nearly 10yrs and I've never really had any major issues. As with other reviews I agree that when searching for a product there can be too many choices with very varied nutritional info which can take time to find the one that matches the info on the tin, you might say. Biggest bug I have found is that the progress has changed and instead of my most recent weight being at the top the oldest one is displayed first. Hopefully this get fixed

I listen to lofi to help me fall asleep, among other sleep sound apps. I was thrilled to find an app that combined the best of both worlds! Now, however, instead of setting a timer and having music play continuously in the background, this app stops playing after EVERY. SINGLE. SONG. Haven't used it in months and decided to give it another go, but to no avail. Still running into the same errors. Also, the volume is inconsistent between songs; some are louder than others at the same volume.

Latest update basically broke the app. poor UAT. 1/Displays under status/notification bar and navigation bar, so buttons in the app that usually are in those positions are now covered. 2/If you move the app to background while recording a workout for long enough, then go back to finish your workout, app crashes, disconnects from the Whoop device, you lose all workout metrics. Whoop is going from bad to worse quickly.

This has been a tremendously useful tool to help me out of a 6 month long panic loop. My fight or flight response has been stuck wide open, and my nervous system is wrecked. With the help of this app, I am slowly regaining my life back. I honestly can't say words to express my gratitude.
I have been using the web version because the app crashed every time. I am back years later and it appears they have fixed the bugs. My order went through and no crashes! Thank you.

I would like to give this app a much better rating. and guess what, it doesn't crash when you first started. it doesn't crash in the middle. it crashes in the very end right when you're finishing your workout. you go to click stop and the app crashes you can't get back into it it won't open it just sits there stuck on the screen like it is right now for me. how about making an app that actually works, so you can actually view what you did in your workout

I used this app for years and loved it. But i recently had to uninstall it because of a crazy surge in ads that made it unusable. Also, the recent pop ups that make it impossible to quickly and smoothly use the app. And for some reason, now if you miss one or two days it makes you go through a lengthy "welcome back" process that is actually just a convoluted membership ad. Im very disappointed, wont be coming back.

Misleading!!! "Calm is free to download and use. There are never any ads and some of the programs and features are free forever. Some content is only available through an optional paid subscription" This is a lie! I downloaded and everything is locked. There is not a free item to use at all! If your items are only free to use during a trial period then just say so up front. Honestly is appreciated by people. This should have said try it free for 7 days then continue with a subscription.

Generally one of the better fitness apps. It is able to track the length and duration of my walks and you can see the walk map. It allows for making notes after each walk (or run). DEVELOPERS: I'm elderly and would love to be able to send my whereabouts on my walk to my wife in real time in case I fall so she would be able to use Google maps to come get me. This would make the app very popular for elderly people!

My level of engagement has certainly up since the update. In the past to check status, would open the app, take a look and call it. Now, open it up, troubleshoot why my watch is not syncing. Now I'm just 8 short steps away from having that information. 3 dashboards and 2-3 clicks per dashboard and I have the same info as before. I'm definitely spending significantly more time with this app since the update.
I almost never oversleep now thanks to the great CAPTCHA challenges required to turn off the alarm... And the alarm I use is chirping birds, which doesn't wake my wife.

Just as in 2018 with my VivoActive 3, two weeks ago similar issues caused by firmware update to my Garmin First Avengers watch. Now it wil not connect to my Garmin heartrate strap, unless every time I remove it as a device and then ask the watch to search for new heart rate strap. Only then it finds it and connects. Very annoying.

The new Google Health app we were forced to switch to from fitbit is absolutely horrendous. There's way too much going on, I can't find the metrics I focus on easily, and the sleep is no longer accurate. It doesn't register me getting up to use the bathroom and my sleep scores are inflated compared to my average. My Peloton workouts used to show up in the fitbit app, now it just says other. The cardio fitness score is gone, total zone minutes is gone, my faith in fitbit is gone.

It's infuriating trying to find this app on my phone when all I have in my head is "zwift". Whoever decided that this app should install with a full name of "companion" should be fired. I don't need an app called "companion", I'm happily married. The app store entry says "zwift companion", but the "zwift" part of the name is lost at installation.

This is so much worse than the fitbit app used to be. To reorganize tiles, you have to delete them all and redo them in a new order. Data is no longer easily visible by day across categories. my favorite feature for weightloss, the calorie budget that compared input to output, has been removed which makes the calculations so much harder. This app is making me consider buying a different fitness tracker just to avoid dealing with this abomination.

app is crazy priced, does nothing a free app doesn't do. paid money for it once and they revoked my access in favour of a subscription model with no refund. doesn't really matter cause it's an outdated program with many better alternatives free on other apps.
Best tool that I have used for the past 15 months! Beyond recording the calories, it helps me know if I need a fiber supplement or not, and logging my exercise to balance out my calories or not.

Very well done. It does all tue thinking you want the app to do, but nothing you dont. You can fully customize each workout and it will calculate warm-up sets and weight increases but you are free to modify these or ignore at you will. You can add or subtract exercises. It syncs with google fit. Great app.

Very good. You can access premium for free by doing a code, which makes them lose money, but even without premium it is a very good app for meditation. Just wish it had yoga or stretching more in the app, rather than a 7 min stretch once a day, I find it a bit too easy! Overall, a lovely app helping me to fall asleep quickly.

Galaxy Phones (like my S9+) are currently not loading the recipe discovery menu. Effectively, you can search for things and find them but you can't just mindlessly scroll. You can type "tuna" and find a bunch of tuna related stuff, for instance. Going to the website + Desktop mode is a workaround for now. App is otherwise great so I haven't factored this bug into my rating. I'm stupid tired so won't talk about why I took a star off too much but the tldr is just that I haven't been able to use the app much due to this bug. I'll be actually using my PC instead to find and add my recipes and once that's done I'll use the app and have a full proper review. I don't want to mention perceived missing features only to find them. >.>
Easy to manoeuvre but experiencing teething problems as I was sent notification that I hadn't attended a booked class when in fact I had! I'm sure it'll run smoothly once established.

I like this app to keep track of my calories and meals but there are way too many pop ups, I just want to log my food and go about my day why do I have to watch a yeti turn into a meteor and wait 10 seconds for the meal tips to load everytime I log an item, please listen to your subscribers and give us an option to remove the pop ups

Update review: first night using the sleep recording was pretty cool. It's surprising how well it records even when my phone's four feet away. But, it's hard for me to figure out how to turn it off, lol. I went to check the recording at lunch and found that it recorded my whole morning routine after I was positive I turned it off.Then it recorded my workday, when I know I turned it off. It'd be great if instead of pause there was an "end sleep recording" button.Maybe I'll figure it out tonight..

Had the 3.0. Very inconsistent numbers. Waited over 2 months for the 4.0. It was obviously shipped out with no testing or quality control. Totally defective. Kept connecting and disconnecting every few seconds and killed the battery in a day and was never able to get any sleep or strain data. Customer service basically ignored me for about a week. When they eventually got back to me, they were no help and kept tooling around obviously knowing they shipped a totally useless device. Just awful.

2.5 years later, I still like this app more than other popular alternatives. I have all my recipes in here and also use it to keep track of what's in my freezer with the saved meals. Update: 5 years later I'm still using FatSecret. There's a premium version available now, and I'm glad they are offering this because I want them to be successful and continue to make improvements. I'm very happy with the results.

Used this app to train for a marathon. It's distance and pace tracking is way off. It will tell me I've run 20 miles when I've only run not much more than 12 and that my average pace was much quicker than it actually was. Unfortunately this wasn't apparent until the actual date of the race. I'll be using a more accurate tracker for future training.

Cant even view the app without making an account first, I don't want to be forced to jump through a bunch of hoops in order to determine if an app has the features I'm looking for, when my data could have just been saved locally. It's an unnecessary way to data mine and with so many reviews about the intrusive ads on top of that, I'm not going to bother wasting my time.

middle of the line app, mostly because it's to confusing to read the data. the user manual is ok but i have to access it from inside the app making it hard to view the squiggly lines and read about them at the same time. 3 day trials aren't anywhere near enough time to even get this thing set up to work with my GWU and my S24FE phone. all the research I had to do took all 3 days and then some.

For me, a woman, beginning strength training, helped to take guesswork out of where to begin and how to proceed. I don't add as frequently as suggested, maybe every week or 2. But I've seen great, steady progress in 10 months in use. I won't continue with the subscription model because I need a more full-body workout, not just 3 exercises 2 days a week. I think I've reached a point where I don't feel I need to get stronger anyhow, so I'm taking what I've learned and moving on. But 5 stars cause it does what I needed it to do. Videos are helpful. Really good app.
great plan editing, with rest timers, variety or regular/drop/pyramid sets, extensive exercise list with option for custom. all round great workout app. 10/10
Terrible update. Hard to navigate; why the change

I really do love this app. Makes sure I'm awake before turning off the alarm. At this point, I usually wake up right before my alarm without realizing what time it is. When I use sleep tracking, it realizes I've woken up and my alarm goes off before I really go back to sleep. I also love that the widget is dressed up for the appropriate time of the day. It's so cute!

Amazingly detailed information, with cited references, a good place to start, in tracking nutrients to address newly diagnosed, mild osteoporosis (so far) and general health, with suggested options to address nutritional shortfalls in a day. Seems educational, thorough, and reliable so far, fairly easy to figure out, but still exploring, first day in. Optimistic that I can DO this, with the help of this app!

Simple, yet feature rich. while youre pushing yourself in the gym, the last thing you want to do is plate math or fumble around with changing up numbers in an app. this is the easiest app ive found to automatically progressively overload your muscles with solid programming. the free version even lets you put in starting bar weight to tell you how much weight per side is needed to complete your next lift. if you skip too many days, the app knows how to program in a deload so as to void injuring u
So far so good, I like the app. it's efficient. it works well. Now I'm just waiting to see if when the points are collected/ it actually follows through

App won't open I have uninstalled and reinstalled this app but continue to have difficulty in getting the app to open. The reinstall made me need to reset everything as well which was a nuisance as my watch is sending me alerts for things that I had stopped and I can't get back in again today.
One of my favorite application

Slowest app on my phone. More than a minute to load, everytime. Lot of features but loading times are so annoying. I can't close the app and come back 5 minutes later, it will try to download maybe a full Blu-ray movie or something and I will have to wait minutes before it dares display something. Neither my internet connect or my phone are at fault here.

So, I downloaded this just to give a bad review for the advertising- I get them all the time and they always makes my anxiety worse. I have been professionally diagnosed with anxiety, used to take medication for it and everything. I thought maybe the app isn't as bad as the ads were. Well there's literally nothing you can do on the app for free. You have to pay for premium, which is way too expensive. So, the app has essentially nothing and it has terrible advertising. 0/5 stars.

the only sleep tracker out of the 5 that I have tried, that actually tracks my light sleep and awake phases accurately. I was so impressed that I paid the one off subscription fee (you read that right folks, just once!) Other apps I could PICK UP MY PHONE AND USE IT and they would still log me asleep. Definitely recommend!
Awesome service, great customer support. Loved the service.

I like that this calculates calories burned. That's what I'm trying to track but some apps only tell you how far you've walked or how many steps. App does not track distance while phone is locked anymore. Very frustrating to try to turn screen off to conserve battery during workout.
Won't allow me to log in keeps saying wrong username or password try back in 15 minutes. I use the same username and password on the web page and it lets me log in.

There are a few glitches I experience from time to time. Like calorie estimates are way off. I can enter a 45 min run and it'll just apply 8 calories. But overall I love it. It keeps me accountable. I also wish they would add Samsung smartwatches connectivity.

Calm has done as much for my mental health as months of intended therapy in healing the trauma from my past. That is no exaggeration. This app is worth every single penny they charge for it if you are looking for a tool to help you grow. Try the EMDR Playlist for sleep and you will see exactly what I'm talking about right there! Plus, the sleep stories are a fantastic way to gently fall asleep each night. Then, I start the day with ten minutes of meditation.

Marketed as having a free version, almost everything is locked. I needed a relaxation app, but you can't use soundscapes, stories, music, or narrations without paying. There is only, and I literally can't emphasize this enough, ONE MEDITATION THAT YOU CAN USE WITHOUT PAYING FOR PREMIUM. Let me give you an example of how frustrating this is, Netflix, which has no relation to this app, tells you that you can't use their app without a subscription, this pretends you don't need one. Very upset.
Not sure what they did, used to be a great app to find any supplies that I needed. Now it is terrible! The app feels more like a link to the website.

I'm sure this app is great should you choose to pay for a subscription. If you have no interest in paying, however, this is NOT a great fit. The app has dozens of options for a myriad of things from sleep issues to anxiety (so much so that the general organization is a bit dizzying), but these options are only available when you pay. Even simple things like the sound of rain or the ocean is barred off. Super disappointed with the lack of general availability.

App is extremely unreliable, constantly logs you out, then when you log in your saved places are gone. Always having to reinstall. Class sizes are limited due to covid which is understandable, but it will sign you in and send a text that you got in then you show up to class to find out your not. Very frustrating. Wouldn't recommend this app!!! I wish my gym would use a different app.

Been using this app almost a year. No problems. All of a sudden every time I start a workout it stops randomly, without even telling me. Has done this the last 4 times. Uninstalled and installed again, same thing. Battery not low, and location gps working. Has worked great up until now. What's going on??
works great - EXCEPT - when it doesn't. "If" i have the map app running and am playing music simultaneously, a phone call incoming, or text, or WhatsApp CAN accidentally end my workout.

I'd be lost without the Under Armour MapMy... apps. I used MapMyRide for years, but decided to switch to MapMyFitness recently. I don't know if the apps are different other than their name and the color of their icons, but both have been the cornerstone of my walking and bicycling workouts. I use the free version, which provides just what I need to track my workouts and progress.

Not new to barbell training by any means, but sometimes it's nice to get back to basics, and concentrate on the fundamentals. Great intro to strength training before moving onto more advanced programs. If you are fairly experienced already, going premium really isn't necessary as the extras it gives are very easy to do yourself. Good app.

After a total of one ride, I'm happy with what I see so far. Update: I have been tracking my walking and riding for over a month now. This app is reliable and shows me everything I need to know. Update: I have been using this app for years now. The only thing that confounds me is how my Fitbit watch and the app disagree about distance and calories. The Fitnit has no GPS, so presumably they both use my phone's GPS. Anyway, no biggie. Map My Ride is still great in my book.

Just rating for Pixel Watch 3 and even attempting to use generic device location or even Fitbit after paying for app. First, took 3 attempts to even DL and install to wearable in Play Store. Second, no device could be found except when going through Wear OS but even after setting up multiple times this app says it isn't installed even though I can test it. However it won't stay locked on despite WiFi+BT connections and doesn't even have a way to save setup, just a back button. Fix it please.

Editing the old review to reflect the change from Fitbit to Health. The new app is much more difficult to use and removes a lot of good things from Fitbit. Sleep tracking is less accurate, and it won't allow deletion of the wrong sessions the app detects. Also it no longer calculates total sleep time for the day, for someone who relies on short and frequent naps to refuel as a daily pattern, the app just keeps giving notifications that I don't sleep enough, which is very frustrating.

This app used to be the best, especially for at-home workouts. Their programs were excellent and the right fit for whatever you wanted, but now they made it so you have to pay for premium to get access. Even if it's free during the pandemic, why not go back to it being free all the time!? It became just like every other app out there. So upsetting!!
I use Prepear constantly to organize, search, and plan my recipes. I use the shopping list weekly. This is my most used app after email and texting.

The GPS tracking is accurate. I like that I can sync activity from my mapmyfitness app. Since I can't track sets and weights here doing it there is the alternative. However that activity is not showing up in my endomondo challenges. I wish under armour would add the ability to use a Fitbit to track heart rate. That's a negative.

Tried it out, then got a timed out error. App told me to login. So I tried and it kept telling me account didn't exist. I made a new account thinking maybe I goofed and forgot my password. Same issue! So I gave up.

The most recent update is horrible. I'm hoping they actually review the posts and revert back. It is no longer user friendly. You have to click so many more times to add food and then many times to go back to the main screen. It's much harder to see where you're at with your daily macros. I am planning to check if I can cancel my membership with money back and will be trying another app. It was worth paying for but I no longer feel that way.

Great app! I've been using it about a year after reading Why We Sleep. I really appreciate the charts that track deep sleep patterns and the alarm that wakes you up when it detects you're in light sleep. Initially, I used this app for 2 weeks and then Sleep Cycle for 2 weeks to compare. I think this one is the better deal. Sleep Cycle has a prettier UI and more audio options, but this app offers more detail/data for a far more reasonable price and I personally don't use audio to fall asleep.

Was my goto app until I got a amazfit bip for xmas. There is no support yet for this watch, so I no longer use. Look forward to the day when new support is offered.

Excellent for when riding in general and doing a workout. Some features I wish were there: easier to find and give ride ons to people around me, that you could choose your workout and such from your phone rather than on the main screen (allowing you to ride first, then workout, rather than beginning a workout right off the bat), and the ability to customize your person rather than having to do it with Zwift open.

Very engaging and interactive fitness app. Software bugs are typically addressed within 30 days. It takes some research and patience to get the various components communicating properly. The Zwift community is very welcoming and helpful. There are workouts, group rides, races and special events to help keep you engaged and achieve your goals.

Best app for managing recipes. Others have lots of limitations, ads or are too buggy and are hard to use. There are a couple minor issues that would make app perfect for my needs: 1. When a measure is in weight (like 4.2 gr), when changing number of portion, amount won't change. It seems problem is due to decimal value 2. App doesn't recognize some measurements like gr, kg or oz 3. Be able to add ingredients from a recipe without having to add recipe to a plan
Doesn't display or allow access to key functions on my Samsung Note 10.1 tablet.  Love it on my I-phone.  Hate it on Android.

The old App was amazing. They recently updated the App with some massive downgrades. They changed how they do websites to try to make more money for themselves, and it's almost impossible to use in terms of making an organized shipping list, editing to fit what you really do, or easy to follow directions when cooking. It's now just painful. If you aren't already sucked into this, I strongly suggest you shop around- this is no longer a top App for food organization.

I like the features and it's easy to use for simple stuff. Good for alarm clocks, smart alarm that wakes you up in your REM, sleep charts. All for free. Notes for improvement: Complex stuff like the charts are a bit hard to read. Also, you need to remember to press the button before you go to bed in order to chart your sleep. But for a free app, A+. This is more functional than some of the apps I've spent money on.

Great way to find what I need at the best price with minimum fiddling. Discount codes and free shipping are easy to get AND easy to actually use, on most orders. Store brand is very good consistent quality. Vitacost and the app are always a smooth experience I will readily repeat, and have many times.

Still 4 stars. Crashed the other day 90 minutes into a ride and it took several minutes after restart to get my Zwift One reconnected. still has periodic and serious issues connecting to Zwift One, which renders the whole apparatus useless because it won't perform the virtual shifting.
I used it before, but after I started to use it again, I had to realize: ads made it unusable and ruined the user experience. I understand the free to use model, but they pushed it too far.

Buyer beware. I was charged $80 immediately when I accepted the 1 week free trial, and have been unable to get a refund from either Google or the app developer (despite initiating a refund only one hour after I installed the app). I'm not a fan of the developer offering a free trial that they never intended to honor.

Similar to many of the other reviews here, I've encountered more technical problems than I would like over my last 2 years of use. Running 5 times a week I have encountered about 4 out of every 10 runs to have a technical problem. Either I find the app closed out and didn't record anything from the run, or at the very end of the run, after clicking "finish run", it gets stuck on a loading screen forever. Otherwise the app is great. The guided runs are fantastic and trainng plans are solid.

Zero miles for my 3 bike rides today. I earned extra points for one of these bike rides but the penalty for that was that I then could not change the zero miles to 2.8 miles, apparently because I received the extra points. I added miles for the other 2 bike rides. Somehow I got logged into my wrong Google account and cannot log out. I'm logged into the correct account on the device (Google Pixel 7 Pro) so not sure how that could happen but it should have asked me if I were sure before switching.

Was a 4 star before recent update. Specifically,  i dont like the new heart rate tracking.  Old version had a graph showing heart rate varitation throughout my mma work workouts. Now it just an average . Not very useful for tracking max heart rate, recovery, etc.. basically a calorie counter now and useless for me.

Great library of food items, many with brand names available. Connects to Samsung Fitness for exercise info, although for some reason it doesn't give you as much credit for calories burned. By tracking my calorie intake, I've lost 16lbs during quarantine, while most people have gained weight. It's the only method that works for real and is manageable because of an app like this one. Forget about the fad diets! I guarantee you are eating way more calories that you think! You really have no idea!

I love this app! the customization, exercise information, videos, all of it; incredible! my only feedback would be that if we customized a workout that we could copy and move those workouts through the three dots drop down on the right, to other programs so we don't have to rebuild them.

App regularly crashes, cannot view logged workouts on app (only on laptop). Sometimes freezes so you cannot finish your timed workout). GPS sometimes unreliable even when phone is connected. Lost a hike halfway through the other day and then app said I had been walking for 23 hours (was only at 1.5 hours) and couldn't stop and restart it. Have updated app and still happening.

I do so far enjoy this app. Pretty easy to log food and exercise. My main issue is the app doesn't distinguish between total and added sugars. It'd be additional help to see how much added sugar I'm consuming. Additionally, the graph would be even better to see the breakdown of macros and being able to choose what to see.

I am so grateful to finally find a food tracking app that accounts for pregnancy and breastfeeding. I can now safely count calories and nutrients and knowing my accurace goals and macros. Highly recommend for all users, especially breastfeeding moms who are looking to stay on target nutrition wise and lose weight. The barcode scanner is included in the free version of the app with is 5 star! Most apps charge extra for that. It does have some delays occasionally.

Hey NRC, i want to start by saying how grateful i am for the free guided runs, they are a big part of me succeeding at my health goals this year. My only issue came out when i started running in my gyms' treadmills post workouts. I dont own a fancy watch like apple or gamin and i found that the app wasnt able to take my speed and distance well when i run indoors. Finishing my runs and finding out that the app only picked 0.2 km from my 2.4km run was really disappointing.

Glitchy search engine, cannot find specific products, gets error messages. Products found by scrolling product group icons. Vitacost carries lots of products, being searched for, but search bar is not effective to finding them. Therefore, good luck finding specific products by scrolling. This error issue happens when using generic product terms, too. Deleted the app. Looks like with all the terrible app reviews, vitacost would fix its app or fire its app project managers.

I used this app on occasion for the ambient music and it worked great. The majority of content was locked by premium, but there were still free parts. Now everything (and I mean everything) is locked. Why is it a free app if nothing is available? It feels a disingenuous to have an app meant to calm people blocked entirely by paywalls. Having everything locked icon is stressful enough, and you'll have to scroll through hundreds. I would look elsewhere for more variety and less blatant paywalls.

Edited review: remaining 2 stars. I reached out to fatsecret and proved to them their RDI for maximum weight loss is literally my maintenance calories and they've told me they're not wrong. You will not lose weight using this app if you follow their RDI. I can use this app, but I know my BMR and what I should be eating.

Tracked my run perfectly. Good voice pronunciation of the current stats. However, the 4 stars is because sometime the app misses some of my run, usually at the beginning. I assume it is the app, but I guess it could be the phone dropping position. After many years of using the app, it performs to a very high standard and accuracy. Very pleased with the app and its functionality.

The companion is fine, although not really necessary. But overall, Zwift isn't committed to Android. The main Android app is absolutely terrible, unless you have a Samsung phone with Ant+. Zwift should invest even a fraction of their advertising budget in fixing their apps and Bluetooth implementation. Terrible.

I have the paid version. It's nice but not much different than the free. Always scan your food. I have found many inaccurate information. I make my own when They don't match up. I wish they would add a veggies tracker like the water tracker. I forget to eat my veggies still. I am dropping weight very well by doing that. Better than other apps I have used.

has become useless over the day. I usually log w/PC because it's easier, &then I check in on things via the app. It's always been buggy and I have to tell it "complete day" and then it populates. The new update apparently needs you to log something from in the app(annoying but whatevs) but now it doesn't even sync! I chose this because I know it's also popular in Japan and I hope to move there someday but time to break my 450+ day streak & go to loseit, I guess. I had the whole week planned.🫠

Hi! I love this app. I have kidney disease and use it to track certain nutrients. It is really great. Today though it will not open the diary? The circle just keeps spinning. I have tried a force stop but it still will not open. All my foods and recipes are still there, and the graphs. Just no diary. Help please?

All in all a quality app that allows you to track your runs. That being said, sometimes the app crashes randomly during my runs and doesn't record what I did for the day. Or at least it doesn't add it to my set plan. Another thing, the app keeps giving me a connection error message when attempting to create a new running plan. These issues need to be fixed ASAP.

App is clean, nice to look at, & easy to navigate. Purchasing (via PayPal) was a breeze. Much prefer the Vitacost website for intense browsing. For quick/repeat purchases, though, the app is more than capable. ✔️

Bad experience. App constantly underestimates the distance I have covered, e.g. I know I have been cycling ~ 8.5 km, whereas the app shows only 6.5. Google Maps shows the distance correctly, while Endomondo cannot approximate the distance. I am only using this app for our company charity challenge, when it ends I am switching to a different app.

Nice app. I use it often. So far mostly for the map it generates. Seems like it would be fun to use some other options like adding photos and notes. Stats are cool too like speed and elevation also duration and calories. One problem is the "start workout/pause" button is too low on the screen and I have inadvertently paused the workout when I hit the home screen or back button on my phone. Again nice app!

Why don't you tell me what features I'm letting you have access to my payment info for? Hell, why not let me use the app for free with its basic version and then highlight what the paid features look like? All I did was sign up and suddenly you want a subscription that auto-renews unless I cancel it, don't offer an actual free plan, don't point out how the subscription improves the app's function.... Since I couldn't even get to the app before being asked for money, I uninstalled it.
I was ok with the newest version for a few seconds until it started to pop up ads over the cooking instructions over and over again. Uninstall.

I installed the regular daily burn first and then got the tracker for free.  In the last screenshot in small print at the bottom it says Pro account needed for this.  They have problems with clear instructions as evidenced by rating comments.  Making untrue statements like "only tracker with scanner" ridiculous

Negligent BMI calculation. This app adds an extra calories to your Basal Metabolic rate for sleep. BMI already includes a full 24 hours. This is costing the users who don't know any better, time and effort because of over eating based on a rediculous calculation that should never have happened. Do the simplest of research and you will know that BMI has no time input in the calculations. Its set for a 24 hour period by default. How can you make a calorie counter app with an error that big.

I set a daily goal of 55 Heart Points. I completed it and the app showed my goal as achieved. However, the next day, my previous day’s points dropped to 54 and the checkmark was removed. This is confusing. If the goal was not actually completed, why does the app show it as done at the time? It seems like the app recalculates activity later, but the goal status is not updated properly. This creates inconsistency and reduces trust in tracking. Please improve accuracy and ensure that goal comple

Awesome app to follow all the hard work done on the trainer. Great to review stats after the ride. Only minor irritation I have found is that sometimes after finishing a workout and zwift reverts back to a normal ride when you end that the companion still thinks you are riding. I have found a way to get it to finish the ride so not a drama.

First time using this and its OK, but it managed to completely miss about half a km on my walk because it randomly stopped tracking my location, then about 5mins later tracked me about 500m away from where I actually was :/ So tracking isn't great, the user interface is a little clumsy, I gave up trying to add friends, and I can't find a way to change the units. All said and done I prefer to use the Huawei health app

I would rate this higher, since whats there is pretty good, but WAY too much is locked behind premium. I don't make in app purchases, and I literally can't access even like 1/8 of what is available. they give you like five things you. an access and none of the daily things are available. if you get access to a series, nope it's just the first episode. I would look somewhere else if you dont want to pay

Good app. . . when it works. Frequently loses track of my concurrent activity occurring alongside Zwift. I find myself having to kill the app and then relaunch to find my current map position. Some days there is hardly any problems, others or it is a frustrating "companion."

The app is nice and basic and looks good. But that's it. I have it linked to my device and it's syncing something, cut only about a quarter of my steps. It's very consistent about it. It's never matched anywhere near my actual step count. That's a bummer because my tracker is supposed to sync to Google, which will sync to my food tracker. Instead I'll just have to track things manually in the food app. Without that Fit is kinda useless. And yes, I left feedback through the app.
works well, tracks distance, cadence and speed very well

Edit: Fixed since today's update. Great! Since the last update I'm not able to choose any other language other than the system language. Before I was able to choose any language. Maybe you look into this. There is no other app on my device denying to change languages. Will update review when this works again.

It seems they have fixed the issue where it would drop the connection constantly. It was very frustrating. If your WiFi dropped for a second this app used to not reconnect like other apps. Usually I had to open and close it repeatedly and turn Wi-Fi off and on to get it to start working again.

I've used this app for years, the only issue I have experienced is when the app updates the alarm doesn't work till you manually restart it... solution is to ensure the app is running before I go to sleep.

I have mindbody accounts with three different businesses...which somehow means I have to have three different mindbody accounts with separate login/passwords, which my devices' autofill consistently get mixed up. Every time I need to book an appt/class I go down a rabbit hole of trying to match the right login to the right account? Why can't this be simpler???

Edit: I updated the app, and the issue remains. ----- for the past few days, every time I click on my dance studio, in place of the schedule page, the screen is blank that says "coming soon", "please call [studio] for more information" and a call button. nothing else visible.

This used to be a better app, but they have locked away almost everything behind the paywall. I don't know how it can be "calming" for someone to open up the app and find 99% of content with a lock symbol next to it. It's truly difficult to find the free content and I only use it occasionally because it's one of the few apps with Google fit integration. I wish some of the other apps had that and then I'd probably never come back to calm.

A few more cycling options would be great but even better, please add a night mode or allow the app to adapt to the phone's theme like Google Maps does. If you're cycling at night and want to keep track of your route and time, etc., a big white screen is really not helpful or safe for your vision in low light. Add that and it would be 5*!

Disappointed. Had to take a 3 month break following surgery. Logged in today excited to get started again and was hit with unskippable questions and learned that I must now pay for the app even though I bought the base version years ago. Didn't ask for new features, all I need is something to track my workouts. Now instead of lifting, I'm searching for a new app.

So far it's awesome. I paid for premium. The only 2 problems I have are: 1) Some of the sleep data is different than what my watch shows in its tracking. 2) I started a nap & went to view the data for it. Instead of logging it separately, all the data for my nap combined with my sleep from last night. I wanted to see the 2 individually. I don't want my nap during the day combining with my sleep from last night. That's very strange. 🫤

Companion is a must if you want to control Zwift from your phone and it works quite well for this. Personally, I think it could use a redesign. I can't figure out why there is such a clunky disconnect from the "Home" page and the "Game" interface.

Products are great, but the app is nearly useless. Order as a guest on chrome or whatever or just call them. It's an ordeal to order every time. I've had to re enter all my info each time. Never works the same way twice.

It would be great if it worked right.  Today, I took my 2-mile fitness walk.  Fit reported 1.08 miles, and the map of my "morning walk" was completely wrong.  This is on a Pixel 3, Google's "flagship" phone.   Fit used to work much better.  I don't know if it was the Android 9 update that broke it, but the app is useless if the collected data is junk.
it has been working great, it is just a little hard figuring out some of its functions and setup, but once you figure it out and can remember what you did it is even better!

Thank you!! Having kids with multiple food allergies makes meal planning so difficult sometimes. This app is EVERYTHING I've dreamed of in a meal planning/shopping/recipe app! I was considering even hiring a dietitian but this app takes the stress out of planning and preparing nutritious meals for my family.
Very difficult to sign in. Consistently says my sign in info is wrong even though it's been the same for years. I was able to sign a couple months ago, but no longer can.
so easy to follow and this gives me far more energy as it doesn't restrict foods. As long as I count them it works !!

I honestly love the app and the interface tons. But I have a huge problem with the fact that you cannot input any custom BMI values UNLESS you purchase and add the Garmin scale they sell. I paid to have a very accurate BMI measurement taken and I have nowhere to input it now. It's not a technology issue, it's the decision they actively made against us.

I like the way the app presents calories and options. It makes it easy to quickly track progress, exercise, etc. It only takes a few seconds to log every time I eat and saves frequent items and quantities.

The app will say my strap is disconnected after a work out, when it wasn't. and sometimes the app will close and open again before I can pause and stop to log a work out. Over all it is solid with all other biometrics.

Do not waste your money. The content is good but the app is a disaster. Hard to navigate and find things and constantly trying to get you to upgrade or share, so you have to click a bunch of times to get to a meditation. By then, my blood pressure is up, so it sort of defeats the calming. you can get EDMR on your music streaming, I wish I knew that. Will not be renewing this

I used this app for over a month. I gave it a really hard try. I wanted to like this app so much. i camt take it anymore tho. this is the worst calorie counter. do not bother with it. UI: terrible. Search Function limited to no findings and when you do find it. the micro/macro set is wrong. measuring methods are inconsistent, serving sizes very and dont match packaging. custom creations cant modify or look at ingredients. its piss poor design meets tragic data.

Was a great app that I've been using for years. I've had to adjust my rating of the app to reflect the fact that despite purchasing the paid version of the app (power pack) many years ago, I now have access to absolutely nothing unless I give them more money for updates I don't want. I chose to buy this app because I couldn't justify paying a subscription for the basic workout tracking features I want from an exercise app and a one-time simple purchase gave me peace of mind. Stupid me I guess.

I still can't believe this app is free. It has everything I've ever wanted in a running app and things I didn't know I wanted. I love the custom training programs. They really are the most customizable I've seen. You can choose how hard. Long and often you want to train. The mileage tracking is awesome. I get informative notifications during my run. The app also quickly pauses if I stop and starts back up. There are music options, ways to share your runs and great guided runs!!!

The best health tracker on the market. Resilient, accurate and a lot of fun to use. Once you give it some time to know your lifestyle, it becomes a crucial part of your fitness life. Tracking metrics begins to give you strong insights of what you need to change, balance or improve upon in your everyday life. The AI component really gives you helpful information on your lifestyle and provides workout tips, as well as nutrition advice.
How about we fix the app instead of letting it continue to not work.

Absolutely terrible app. Agree with the review written 7 April 2026. Difficult to log food. No simple way to adjust quantity and calories from the AI camera logging method. Incredibly annoying trying to log multiple items in quick succession as you have to deal with various Yazio splash screens, tips and motivational nonsense before you can add a second item. I honestly wonder if the developers have ever tracked their own calories. Requested a refund after a day and half of use.

Been using this app since 2016. I paid for a lifetime subscription which I guess was only 10 years not lifetime. Its a basic concept not hard to do yourself. The timer and tracking was nice. I won't be subscribing and will be deleting the app.

This app keeps malfunctioning. I have to log in through a browser instead and that is almost just as bad. I've uninstalled and reinstalled the app several times. It's just awful now. UPDATE 4/22: THE APP IS WORKING GREAT AGAIN! THANK YOU VITACOST!

I'm using Samsung watch with Samsung Health for tracking steps, that is linked through Health Connect with Google Fit. But for some absolutely ridiculous reason it only syncs data at 23:59 every day. What's the point... To sync another app and not to see the data here. The biggest problem is, in the settings I can clearly see the data gets accessed every time you open the app, so Fit has the data all the time. It just doesn't show them!!? This also breaks integration with other apps. Stupid
it sort of works but loses loads of data (eg it shows me how many steps/calories etc for today but when I click on the 7 day record they disappear) think it's got worse since last update

Great addition to my arsenal of run/workout tracking apps! My only issue is the community feed is not in any sort of chronological order. Its honestly a mess, posts are from all over the place and different times; 4 minutes, 4 hours, 4 days. Should be in chronilogical order! Also my public posts/runs are not showing up for some reason even though they're set ro public.

**Update** The Devs for Prepear contacted me and they were able to track down the bug that was causing the crashes. After a couple months of using the app sporadically, I'm using the app for meal planning and forming grocery lists. I've really enjoyed it. I think the app has some very useful tools and is mostly intuitive. Would love to see a calendar view in the planner! *Had high hopes. Tried to add the same recipe 3 times. It crashed at a different point each time and didn't save the recipe.

Received a gift sub from a friend. After deciding it's not for me, I cancelled on 2nd May (even checked Google subs) and got an email confirming this. On 13th May I get charged $79.99 USD. Turns out they bypassed all that and charged me through Paypal instead. Cust.sup. was great & they issued me a refund, but due to conversions I still got charged £2.76. 1 star, bc cancelling a sub should not be this difficult, nor should I be on guard if I'll be charged anyway. It's just not good practice.

this app has changed my life. I could not imagine my life without it . at first I was surprised by how many thing you can do other then just meditate and try to sleep ! the sleep stories are great you can even listen to them just for enjoyment rather then sleep aid as well as that . I never tried another meditation app but that's because I never needed too. calm has everything I needed to begin and continue my mental health wellness journey .

On ios there is more tracking options, such as the exercise tracking. There is no meal snap for android either. The app itself works and the database is great.  To bad though that you cant add a barcode to someone elses food item in the app.  Please DB, add exercises and meal snap to android

Overall, the app might be good, but it breaks with team conversations on the android version. it will stop loading any new messages. and I can see the notifications, but the messages don't show up. I've tried uninstalling and reinstalling, That works for a while, but then it immediately breaks again. I've also tried clearing the cache and clearing all stories. nothing works.

Everything work well for me, except running cadence. In other apps it measures in steps per minute (spm) and example values are 160-180. In endomondo it's rpm and I see values like 75-100. I don't understand the metric and can not use it, but I believe it's important for runners.
Too many ads. Trying to buy a 10-class pass and it just says "something went wrong, please try again soon". Boy, that's helpful

Ordered three items, was charged for them, never received the order. I opened a chat session and was told that I had cancelled the order. NOT TRUE. I was denied a refund. Avoid due to crooked business practices!
this app is great but overtime it doesnt automatically log my gym session as it used to. this throws all the data out

When the app works, it's great. But when it doesn't work, boy oh boy does it not work. Can't even log in, can't access my account in any way, won't track any of my information, and there seems to be 0 way to fix it until it randomly fixed itself. Have had this problem twice and there seems to be nothing I can do. Have sent reports to Google multiple times, still no fix. No excuse for this!
I love this ap for keeping me on track

I work so hard on intervals and then get this at the end!!!???(was trying to add a glitch video, but basically it freezes and wouldn't let me stop the run, ot looks like someone else already talked about it) and this isn't the first time happening!!! It is part of the half marathon training, but it would not count towards it. I just also really want to know my pace as I really really tried hard!!!!! so disappointing
Something has changed with this app. it no longer sounds an alarm at the appointed time. it just stopped working and now I'm late for work. womp.
I like the app and when it starts crashing when I open it there is usually an update for it ready to go

I've been using the app for guided runs. The content is great, very inspiring, informative and motivational. The app occasionally logged me out about every 2 weeks. A few days ago, I downloaded a training plan and now I cannot get the app to connect past the login screen. I've uninstalled and reinstalled the app multiple times to no avail.

Seemed pretty good but kept turning on an alarm when I hit start recording sleep. I couldn't find a way to disable it. I shut it off last night and the night before but today it started alarming with no notification and I couldn't stop the alarm during a work meeting. This is day #2 of using it and uninstalling it. Needs a "disable all alarms" option in settings.

This app is shockingly bad. It looks good on the surface, but it is so very half baked. Playlists don't actually play everything in the list. The curated lists are broken into 1 to 5 minute videos, but the app stops after each video, asking for a review. It is jarring to have to unlock my phone, click through prompts, and start a new session which also asks you to write a note and click tags (with no context at all). I just fall back to YouTube and regret paying a year for this. For celebrities
Needs a lot more options. It should at least allow you to track workouts and your progress. Does none of these things.

I used to love this app. It has helped me get better sleep and was the best alarm. Now, the graduated alarm snooze doesn't work, I only get 1 no matter what I do. The sleep phazer won't work properly, I've had 3 of them. I can sync my watch with it only if I don't use any other connected device. Everything was working great at first, but after an update, it all quit doing what made it such a great app.

App lagged mid-run and actually asked for an update while I was running. After that, all data was broken: cadence 25 with a 4:30 pace for 7 KM — which is physically impossible. This is unacceptable for a running app. Unreliable, frustrating, and a complete waste of effort. Uninstalled.

I sleep better. The app is very interesting in the way it records and analyzes your sleep patterns. I don't think I got a great analysis, since the husband may or may not be the one doing all the snoring.... But it makes sleeping into an interactive pursuit, almost a game. While using this app, I slept better. I stopped, and went back to bad patterns. I also like their business model. You can extend your trial by watching ads, or pay 5-6 bucks to get the full edition.
I have purchased gold 3 times in a week and my subscription wont stay. I have wasted $120 on 1 subscription!

Don't understand why Zwift pushes the companion app so much instead of just putting the functionality in to the game. It's frustrating and inconvenient to have to use both devices just to go for a ride. And besides that, the app isn't that good and often crashes or fails to operate correctly.
always reliable and counts accurately, easy to use

I have been using the Strong Lifts app for over six years now, and I have never been disappointed. Mehdi is continually expanding it and has added dozens of features over that time. He has always responded in a timely manner if I had any questions. I highly recommend Strong Lifts for anyone that wants a convenient and powerful workout tracking method.

I've used this app since I was a wee tot. Was that over a decade? Almost two? It's been my favorite. There's been problems here and there, including the random Captcha diffuclliculty scaling, and the consistency of the alarm gradual increase, but otherwise, I've always loved it. And now with this zombie walk? It's gonna be another fame changer.

What happened to the "Activity Tracker" in the pulldown menu? It's no longer there with this update. That's a primary area to keep track of workouts; I've been using it for two years! Do we suddenly need to upgrade just to see past workouts? I otherwise liked the app and will change my rating if that area is restored.

For months i can not load previous ordered items on app. I have to go to the website which completely negates having an app. If not fixed soon i will have to uninstall to keep from throwing phone across the room.

All I wanted this app for was to track distance and average speed. It has a lot of other features that are cool and very informative (elevation change is something I didn't know I needed, but now I can't live without) but it's so SLOW that I hardly use the other features. I've had to get into a habit of starting the app while I get my gear on, otherwise I'm waiting on my bike for it to load. I can no longer bring up previous workouts to see improvements or what trail I rode while in other towns.

I am a beginner runner and this app is best for beginners. It has guided run playlists and other types of runs. Best app for runners. If you can include what to eat and drink before and after the run, it will be beneficial to us.
This is still a preview release. It only has nutrition tracking and the health profile (without graphs). I hope for more to come soon so that it will be on par with the iPhone version.
so far the best apps to use even without subscription. but I think can be better if it show individual meal (Breakfast, lunch, dinner, snack) total intake and macros and not only overall

still getting to know it. I believe you need to enter pretty much everything it will know about you. steps take 10 minutes or longer to calculate consistency. so its super slow that way. not all that impressed at the moment. wish I would have opted for the trial. I may not own it.

.I’ve been using the Garmin watch and app for walking and navigation and overall I’m really impressed. I've used this for YEARS now. There are so many useful features in this app! It does everything it says it does, and it's easy to use. Definitely worth it. *I did download Dozzzer also (an app recommended within this one), to save battery when I can't plug in my phone. It works very well with it.

Bought the lifetime powerpack years ago and have been using the app since. It is so excellent. I should have switched years ago. Thank you! I am enjoying it and am excited to enter all my food and keep my protein up where it should be. This app makes it very easy to do. Is it a feature to ask everytime the country I am in now? ( I have opened Yazio once on a short trip to Germany and scanned something... since that moment I have to confirm the country I am in right now). P.S. The same "feature" wasn't observed on iOS, just asked once, exactly at the right moment.

This is a wonderful app for bedtime stories n unwinding for me, even for my kids. I use Smartbabytoon youtube channel also for free bedtime stories for my kids, they love it.

I have had this APP for years. My subscription was coming due. Thankfully, my credit card company declined the charge. When I checked where it came from it was from MFP. Very useful simple tool that helps you track your calorie intake and your weight-loss journey pretty well. Higher pay tier unlocks recipes and a water intake section to track your hydration. Though it is not needed in any regard, most info of that sort is available online for free. I use the app to keep portions in line and use my honesty to not go overboard.

I'm about to uninstall this app, but I'll ask for help in here first. The app can't track my location throughout my workout even though I have GPS settings fully open. Other apps like Zombies Run can track me just fine, so it isn't my phone. I used to love the app.

I got this specifically for the headphone output feature only. I've been using the "use phone volume" and it works fine, but sometimes too quiet. Guess Ill have to try out other alarms. Too bad, because the other features are nice.

I love the Jeffing workout program that is customizable but not the strength training workout. The app desperately needs custom exercise name input for strength training. The built-in exercise database is missing basic gym machines.

I signed up based on an ad for a particular course. Once paid up and in the app discover there is no such course. The customer service emails are just that tired trick of 'we're experiencing high volumes ' and have so far waited over a week with no response to my complaint and refund request.

Really love how easy it is to track everything, somewhat difficult at the start to find exactly what food to add and how, but once you understand it's amazing. If I see I go over my daily caloric intake or am about to I can reign myself in, and see the calories from everything is really astounding. This app really helped me lose weight by showing exactly the amount of calories in a dish and how much carbs I was consuming. As always, no matter what, it requires q willpower above all else.

I am terribly disappointed with the most recent "upgrade" and I will not be renewing my paid subscription. I was tracking my calories in My Fitness Pal, which is supposedly integrated with Fitbit. It isn't. Don't even bother. It was a major hassle. Nothing useful I chose vegan option and went to Indian cuisine. Only 34 results and almost all of them made of Chicken.

This is a great free calorie counter! I've tried a few of them, and they all had annoying pop ups or ads, and this is the first truly free calorie counter I've found without ads. There are other features that I really like and are included in the free version. The apps connectivity and syncing messes up especially if it is after business hours. It will not show readings for days that I wore my device or not sync until the next day, even though it states it is all caught up. I gave two stars because I am frustrated I spent so much $ for inconsistent sync
easy to use and syncs well with my other health apps

Erases credentials on login screen when switching between running apps, making it impossible to paste credentials from a password manager. I'll never understand why some app developers want to screw with the login process to add "features" that nobody wants.

I was looking for a free alternative to Noom and found this app. It's simple and easy to use, and helps me keep track of my eating. The only thing I would change is the name, as "Fat Secret" is a little blunt for a weight loss app. Otherwise, it's great!

I had Fitbit since 2015. Google has ruined the reasons for my Fitbit. I don't want AI to have my health records, I don't want AI to assist me in any way, shape, or form. REM Muscle atonia — almost none ⚠️ Often classified as "deep" The morning REM paradox: precisely when you are in the deepest stage of REM (7–8 a.m.), the algorithm may mistake this for deep NREM → either failing to wake you during the window, or waking you from REM (which is subjectively more difficult).

So I mainly use this because it has a feature where the alarm can only sound through your headphones, which I love. It worked fine when I used it the first few times. Terrible, terrible design. User interface is the most confusing, non-intuitive design imaginable. Also, wildly inconsistent calorie counts for food items. excellent running app. tracks my pace, route, and provides excellent plans all for free. I love the guided runs with a coach. Helpful overall. I think it turns off my wi-fi, so every time I turn it on I have to go over and turn my wi-fi back on so it doesn't use my data all night. I have placed 6 separate orders over maybe 3 months. Each time, it gets canceled. No reason given. I have spent hours on hold with them with no resolution.

Some time in the past I really enjoy map my ride. I start my ride walk or run and everything is tracked. I use out too the max
poor sync with Google health fitbit. I choose breakfast and it syncs as other. if i delete from this app it still stays in the Google health app . Sync does not work properly

And with the money-hungry decision to remove power pack support, I'm leaving the app for good. Chatgpt could give these personalised workouts for free. One time purchase meant one time purchase. every music that used to be free is now not free and this app wants me to pay JUST FOR listening to the music? this so unfair and this app is greedy-money , if I open it tomorrow nothing will be free, this app is the worst app ever , I used to hear alot of music for free now there only few tracks counted on fingers are available and free , and tomorrow there will be no track for free, this is NON SENSE and UNFAIR

The transfer to Health from Fitbit has been a mess. I've found this app easy to use, and very helpful. I've used the motion sensing sleep tracking feature, not the sound one, and I haven't had any problems with it draining my battery. Great app. 👍👍

ok for a rough guide its quite often very off especially using the camera ai and for some reason the camera takes pics in a very low resolution so the ai cant make out the nutritional information on packaging unlike the other apps ive used but this is by far the best price

Absolutely in love with this app! I use it for all my cooking needs and planning with it makes my life so much easier. I hope they continue development for a loooong time. Sometimes the calorie tracking is incorrect. The ai recogniser is pretty good but if youre in nz and showing it a product sometimes it won't know and end up substituting the cals incorrectly. This has given my workouts structure. I've never felt so strong overall, and while every workout day is a leg day, it has a purpose. One option I would like put in is to set a top limit for your lifts. I'm getting older, and I don't want to lift too heavy. I just want to keep my squat at 225, bench at 245 etc.

Every time an update happens, your device settings reset. Which, if you're broadcasting your heartrate to another fitness tracking device/app, means if you don't remember to go turn it back on every single time, you're going to wonder where your heartrate data is. Can't tell you how many times I've had this happen. Reached out a few times over the years to let them know. Even then, the support is terrible. You'd think not switching your user's settings off without them knowing would be UX 101.

This is nice to have, but really does so little! How about telling us how many experience points, Drops, etc. were earned in a session or event? Further improve the automatic naming to let me toggle off parts. Why do I have to open up Zwift itself to see how many xp I have, or view/buy things in the drop shop? How about a permanent "center on me" button instead of making me scroll kilometers away to make it pop up? I could go on & on about the app's unfulfilled potential.

Overall, it does what it's supposed to. I debate the accuracy, but my one real complaint at this point is that if I am looking through the data for previous days, there is no way to see the total distance traveled that day. You can add the distance for each recorded activity set, but that's an irritating waste of my time. I would like to be able to calculate average distance per day more easily.

I used to be able to use my kickrSnap with the ZC app but recently, when connecting, the app will recognize the trainer but won't actually connect via BT. With the new update I can't customize meals. I went through lots to make many customized meals, I knew how much protien and salt they had in them, it's all gone. Lots of things missing to make this 5* Zwift devs need to take a look at "Sauce for Zwift" and incorporate missing features. -- 4 star to 3. The Bluetooth keeps disconnecting after ever ride. This only happens with this application. 😡

Like the convenience and connection to studios around the world. But today you suck.
