I don't review apps often, but Cronometer is seriously the best. All of the functionality I need layed out in an intuitive, easy to use interface. I gladly pay the month subscription to track my metrics over time.

This app has been simplified to the point that it's barely even functional anymore. I've used Fitbit since 2015 and Google Health ruined it. UI is awful and calorie tracking is now useless. Can no longer track less than 1 as an amount (i.e. used to track .5 serving or .25 cup). They Ruined the Best App.... This app used to be my absolute favorite and ever go-to lifesaver. Nice app. Good for checking macro & micro (vitamins, etc) nutrients. it would be nice to have 1. anti-oxidents tracking ability. 2. family plan. I can't get it to properly link to healthlink to import exercise data.

did the free trial. tried to purchase the annual version (£27.50), the price that came out of my account was double (£54.99) I was stretching myself for the first one so the second is too far for me. looking to try again, the price has now jumped up to that second number. superb.keep it up

Clock is intermittent (other than hard pauses in workout) and the tracker is trash. I take the exact same route 5 days a week, and get 5 different distances every week. The calories burned isn't accurate either. Simple math will tell you how much you actually burn, app is routinely 75-125 off. Just mediocre.
I think the search engine could be better. and it would be helpful if search results were in a logical order so the shopper doesn't have to weed through great volumes of data.

I haven't tryed the upgrade (yet), but the free app function is simple. Instead of writing in a book and clock watching between sets this just keeps track for me. I am used to doing the other app elements in my head, however I have done 5x5 many times over the years and as you move up the weights you will find that the extra will be handy because yiu will want to focus 100% on form for safety the closer you get to your limit.

I have been using this app for quite a few years and it's great to look back to compare my activity. Encourages me to get moving 😊 4/25/20: all of a sudden the app wouldn't load, looked up some issues and had to clear the cache and data from my phone. Logged back in and seems to work fine now.

Whoop seems to base most of its criteria for recuperation on sleep. It wants an unrealistic amount of sleep, and many times it incorrectly concludes that you slept until 1AM or 4AM when in fact you went back to bed or were just sleeping. I would rather have all the dashboards on the Web site than in the app. Finally, I'm not sure that it really helps knowing how hard you can train in any given day. So lots of data I moderately care about, but not enough practical indication for an athlete.

Pretty solid companion app. No big complaints after 3 months of riding. Loving Zwift! Edit: not a complaint. But a wishlist item - it would be GREAT if you could access profile data (badges, garage, etc...) from the companion app. I hate having to pick a route before entering Zwift, and then having to back out and repeat because I already had that badge.

Their logic in going fully paid seems weak at best and potentially a poor business decision. I tried the free week of the app and the "personalized" workout plan. I wasn't impressed and switched to a static training plan thinking I'd be able to use it for free. I was wrong they want to charge you no matter what. The team seems short sighted in forcing out free users that would cost the company little and with ads could turn a profit. There are plenty of free apps that will do the same but free

I love their workouts and I love that premium it's free during these difficult times. However it's a deal breaker for me that it is not compatible with Chromecast. It's not easy to watch a little screen while working out. Tried on Apple and Android and it not only crashes the app, my phone would freeze afterwards.

This is a shocking app. My passes disappear, so if I cancel a class I have to book a replacement immediately or the next time I open the app the pass disappears. And then the app only shows the next week so if you can't find a slot in the next week you have to contact the gym to book further in advance...which defeats the purpose of the app! It's become apparent to me that the app was developed for the iPhone and not for android. I recommend you do more testing of the android version.
Easy to use. The challenge is to enter everything accurately so you can see where you need to tweak your eating - no fudging entries. Your body knows what you've eaten!

$70 a year, no monthly payment option, literally nothing for free. A 7 day trial, and then it will automatically charge my card $70. I'm not taking a chance on not being able to cancel before it charges my card, especially when I have to put in all the info before I can even see what you have to offer! there's plenty of other free apps out there for meditation 🙄

3/31/22 Echo others and say I miss the workout preview of the time and rep based videos. Really helps decide what I'm up for/getting into. And since they really aren't trainer led classes, you miss out on the 'interaction' and encouragement. Note to Nike, if you're going to keep it like this, at least add some more notes like joints worked or moves included in the workout 3/16/21 The recent update has left the app nearly unusable in a meaningful way. No premium videos will play.

App doesn't have a feature to track exercise or water like the website. Food only.  As far as food tracking, would be better if you could copy an entire meal from one day to another. Doesn't allow adding new foods from mobile. Needs functions added for the phone's "menu" button, such as returning to the home screen or other places (continuously clicking back or restarting the app is the only way to achieve this now) Overall, a huge disappointment of an app.

Long time user. I rarely complain about redesigns, but this new design update is horrendous. I just want to have some basic info on one screen: everything I ate that day, how many calories I've consumed, and how many I have remaining. Everything requires so many clicks now, it's so much harder to use for logging. I will likely switch to a competitor, even though I'm not happy to lose so much historical data.

I've loved using this app for years until just like everyone else, it's stopped tracking the distance and location of my walk. The first time I thought it was me then the second time I checked to make sure it was working before setting off but it didn't pick up more than the first 10m.
It tracks well. The automatic pause is a little irritating because it pauses on a slow turn. Other than that, I think it is a great app.

Used to be my go to workout app, but GPS issues have helped ween me off it. A former premium subscriber for a couple years & would run concurrently with Strava so I could keep using it whiley peers used Strava. However Endomondo no longer keeps accurate GPS readings & distance. Though it was a problem with my ohone but Strava, Garmin & Maps all work and my wife is having same issues on a different device.

1. Printing option is only for English supportable. Unicode Does not available. so that, Bengali, Arabic, Urdu, Does not able to print. Please correct the issue. 2. Please ad auto detect shopping list from current meal plan and filter all item which already been stored in pantry list.

I put it on a newer phone (the "old" phone isn't very old). So far it syncs great. 4 stars not 5, because I still don't trust it, also I'm bummed that I can't add the lost run and have it count (I have a screenshot of the time and distance, along with the neverending "syncing" circle). P.S. When I say sync, I'm referring to app syncing with the NRC site. I turn wifi back on when I get home. Turn it off before I leave to save battery. (No service on the phone). See my history for initial review.

Annoying Pop Ups. Been using this for 8 days straight. all I want is to input my food intakes without having to click hundreds of pop ups, doesn't even have a skip/close buttons on them. My friends already left and looked for another tracker app. I might leave too because this is really getting annoying ugh!!!
Has potential. when you first open the app it should go straight to the food entering page, the other part should be in the menu

With the latest update It's not as easy as it was to keep track of what you've selected . I've used this app for awhile and when it works it's great. They keep updating it and many of these cause more issues. The latest update does not correctly total calories. constantly not correctly adding calories to the total often just adding zero when the app correctly displays the calories for the food. After all that's the only reason I use the app is to keep track of calories.

Warning: latest update removes analysis section. Recommend disabling auto updates. IMO was a 5 star app until they added mascots, AI, game-ification, animations etc. With analysis removed I will finally have to stop my sub and find a new app, a pity because this one was so good before.

I have used this app for years and love it. It has been a great help in tracking how much sleep I am getting, what activities make me sleep worse, and so much more. Plus the multiple alarms makes it easier to set all my alarms for the full week. I have also always gotten help when dealing with an issue i couldn't figure out. Great app!

No ads! Favorite tracker for several years now for clean interface and customization options. Points: can add your own recipes, meals and new foods or products. You can also customize the display pretty fine-grained, including macros as numbers or as % of total. Helpful for hitting goals and seeing which meals tend to trip me up. You can also keep your posts, meals, diary etc. private (not on by default). I don't use the community feature but it seems supportive.
I downloaded this app to tic off Apple. Its actually a helpful app for a full time working mom.

The free version is more than adequate for my needs and I appreciate it tracks the macros. There is good community support. I intend to upgrade to the paid version when I get a little extra money. UPDATE: I did upgrade to the paid version and love the added functionality. It allows me to add professionals (dietitian) who get very detailed reports.

Something’s changed, and not for the better. When the “hold to finish” circle completes, it advances to another screen that still say “hold to finish,” but doing that just brings up a metrics screen. Completed mileage takes FOREVER to display so it can be saved. Google Pixel 8.

The first week is really misleading--ads get a LOT worse after. I like the big plus button at the bottom center and that it syncs with Samsung Health. it's a little frustrating that I can't fix incorrect nutrition myself, but they seem to be responsive when changes are requested so 🤷 I don't love how items appear in the search but I'm dealing with it.

Map my Fitness is my favorite activity tracker app, but it still is a little lacking for my purposes in 2 major ways. The first is that the workouts it lists first are the "top" workouts. It seems that with the number of years I've been using the app, these should be *my* top workouts, not the top workouts of the general population. Also, there are few generic workouts for what the latest science says we should be doing: HIIT, and resistance + cardio.

Displays spam push notifications - even when turned off in its settings.  However, besides that (and some annoying adverts), the app works well. Unlike google fit, there is a way to turn off tracking except when performing an activity, which is all I want, really.  Would be nice if it told me what units things were in though, during an exercise - We aren't all American!

If you are looking to track food for tomorrow on this app, be prepared to pay for a subscription now. I was enjoying the app until the recent update where they locked tracking food in advance behind a paywall. I like tracking in advance as meal prepping is an essential part of managing my diabetes and just a good tool for a healthy lifestyle. I do not see how tracking food for tomorrow is something that has to be locked behind a Pro subscription. In my opinion, it's a horrible change.

On this Moto g6 the map is no longer displayed after I drop and save the walking workout lately the GPS has been very unstable. Had trouble accessing it. Today was the last straw. Before I walk 1 mile. Endomondo had already 3 miles in. The GPS has been very bad . I use this program for several years. It was good.it has not been updated in over 14 months I have even sent a email about the problems. No response

Good app for tracking daily steps but it has no option of dowloading yearly stats. That feature should also be there in the app so as to measure annual progress. Update on 05 Apr 2026: App has some serious flaws. It increased minutes, steps and heart points in approximate manner. Like I took a short walk for 4 minutes but it increased minutes by 7 because just before this 4 min walk I was talking walk intermittently in 7 minutes slots. This gives incorrect assessment of minutes.

Works great, lots of options. I like the ability to adjust how often it updates one's progress. I changed mine for walking to every half mile. Oddly, on the main screen it shows miles, but to set the frequency, it is yards, minor nit.

it's only good because it has good functions but the amount of pressure the user is being put on just to purchase the premium version is borderline toxic. I am skipping the ads as soon as I can and then get back to the app to find another "inner ad" about their premium version.

Like the new app so far, only been active a day and a half. Some minor glitches like my map when I walk it isn't saved, however most of the info is. Not a nightmare like the Fitbit app ended up. Yes I've checked all my settings on my phone and Versa 4. I'll give it a chance and hopefully some of those minor glitches will be resolved. I'll give it a week. if corrected I'll increase my rating, if not I'll lower it..
Very frustrating.  Won't accept password on my android but will on my IPad. Will be uninstalling!
App won't open. Now considered useless and uninstalled. Resorted to creating a browser shortcut to my home screen since that is essentially what the app is anyways.

I've been using MyFitnessPal since 2020, and although the app did have some flaws, the new update makes it impossible to use. I keep accidentally adding meals I had previously into a the new logs, you can no longer move meals in between meal times, and the meal search button is way hidden. When I want to look at a meal I had last week, then move the bar back to this week, it immediately sets it at the day you were looking at last week. How annoying! I accidentally put all my meals on May 23rd.

I was loving this app until it crashed after my interval run. I went to keep the time going and it wouldn't pull back up. Tried restarting my phone and waiting 2 days. Finally just uninstalled/installed it again. Lost my data, huge bummer. Garmin never has this issue. 5/12- lost another interval run. Devs, please provide a fix.

I am new to using the app and I find that for tracking my cycling it works initially had issues with GPS connectivity which seems it's resolved. I have one problem though. For running I notice the app offers training planning but this isn't available for cycling. I have the premium option and I expected it will offer the full comprehensive features for all supported sporting categories.
Not a huge fan of the app. Is always a big somewhere that annoys you. And now there is AI, which adds no value, and I can't turn it off.

Since Google bought out Fitbit, I have had nothing but problems with my device staying synced. It will randomly connect and disconnect. I miss text messages and phone call because it won't stay connected. I won't even get started on the issues with trying to change devices. I'm not sure if they are trying to phase out the older models (Versa 2) and make you buy a newer one or if they just don't know what they're doing. Either way it's very irritating not having the functionality you paid for.

Edit - 16/04/25 The app takes a lot of time to load. Be it search, recently eaten, & etc. It's been happening for a few days. It's 50/50, sometimes it eventually loads the content, other times it just keeps loading.

The app gets worse with every update. Now I can't view my activities from a web browser. I've invested too many miles in the app, only to lose access to my data. The GPS tracking is flakey, I can't set a reasonable countdown timer, and the music controls aren't robust. Finally, it can't give me a partial split time for my last split. The only thing good left about the app is the free training plan.

I had a very hard time turning the app and the tracker off; I would have to force stop it several times before finally uninstalling it. Needless to say, it was not accurate and very frustrating to use...which sucks because it's one of only a few apps I can use with my smart watch. Also, I signed up for the 30 day trial, which forced me to select a subscription method and after cancelling the subscription (eleven days into the trial), I haven't seen any confirmation that I will not be charged for this failed experiment?

Love it, makes signing up for yoga class A breeze. I like that I can see all my favorite yoga studios with one click with the times lined out sequentially regardless of studio, and see what time works best for me. Also love that I can see how many people are signed up and how many spots are left.

this app is not user friendly, not accurate and not flexible to adapting to ones needs. the fasting timer is very hard to use. not flexible, and it disables eating moments like breakfast and doesn't let you customize it. It sets calorie and macros goals for you and it's not flexible. if you need to change the macros of a meal good luck. not easy at all. tries to do many things but actives none save your money!

I don't like the new design. It is not easy to find anything and my favorite part of the Fitbit calorie counting feature was being able to add custom foods. I used my custom list more than the public ones because they usually didn't scan. I almost want to quit counting calories because I don't want to download yet another app!!! Sometimes y'all make the wrong move for real.

im done. been using this app daily for nearly 10 years. maybe more. every so often, they decide to completely change the whole app, then I have to re learn it again. the original version was absolutely awesome. it gets worse and worse with every generation. wake up this morning and again, its a totally different app. I am NOT renewing my 50 plus dollar subscription anymore. if your goal was to ruin the app and drive people away, congratulations.
I haven't been using this app long, but it seems to have everything I have wanted in a prepping dinner app. I highly recommend it.
New to the app. not sure if it only maps walks and runs or counts steps.

Absolutely wonderful app!! I've had this life changing app since I started grad school and I would definitely recommend it to anyone that wants to have a more, calm, peaceful life overall. The app teaches you to show down and breathe. Truly Amazing!! 🤩

I've had issues getting the app to work since the first time i tried to use it (over a year). it NEVER syncs to my watch. in the last couple of days it stopped tracking anything, wouldn't register, just looked like it was trying to load. i uninstalled and reinstalled and now it won't let me log in, says doesn't recognize account. very disappointing.

such a good app and one of the best i tried so far , the only problem that it doesnt have that much products , as an arab i barely find anything related to our food or products there , but thats my only problem with it i hope you'll solve it , the rest is awesome!

The app works well when the GPS is working but for some reason the GPS system has a difficulty optainimg a GPS signal. In the past three weeks the app has only received a GPS signal on one of my walks. Even though other apps on my phone receive a gps signal just fine. Not very happy about this issue.
App is slower than website. Easier to use on the computer, but handy.
Great prices and good discounts through their emails. App seems ok so far, some pages took a bit to load after you chose that category, but could just have been the day.

I have previously been a Yazio “Pro” customer; their paid tier they themselves value at €239/year. For this I got an app that got worse over time, with very annoying gamification. I recently lost my 340-day streak to some bug that I still don't understand; support told me they wouldn't do anything about it and then stopped replying. The customer experience you get from Yazio for €239/year. There's a fasting feature, but they still try to heavily incentivize you to track food during your fast.

First, I love this app and I'm a premium user. However, there are a few things that need added or fixed. We need more challenges and a variety of challenges, not just one running challenge for the whole year. I loved the monthly challenges, but they've kind of gone away over the last year or so. Another problem is that this app doesn't connect with some other apps like Google fit and others. It needs to connect to many apps, platforms, and devices.

The device stopped working after a few days into the trial (wouldn't charge). Ended up returning the device during the trial period. 3/5 Unfortunately, I was still auto subscribed to the annual membership. 0/5 Fortunately, the support was quick to respond and fix the problem. I was able to get refunded for the membership. 5/5

There's some really good features but the serving sizes don't match correctly all the time and I would of expected a larger database of goods that can be scanned. A lot of items won't load with the barcode and entering the information isn't as easy to align with serving size (ie. grams, ml). I like that it integrates with Fitbit but would really like to see a bigger and better database of foods.

Only feature that isn't right is the announcement every mile. The app states the overall miles and overall time, but then states the last mile incorrectly... X minutes Y seconds per mile. That number is actually the last mile, but it infers the overall average time per mile. Surprised that it has been that way for many years.

TLDR: super great workout app Edit again: I guess I need to keep up to date on changes! I've been wanting to replace the core lifts with variants like front squats instead of back squats on B days and now I can! (Probably could for the last year or something but I didn't follow the changes with the updates.) I really love this app, it makes tracking workouts super easy and customer support has been outstanding the two times I've had (relatively minor) complaints.

I am in the same boat as a lot of people- the app has worked great for me for many years and then, recently, it isn't working correctly. Tells me GPS has been lost and tracks me in a straight line from point A to point B. I've reinstalled it several times and it still doesn't work. I hope someone at Endomondo is reading these reviews and fixes the problem. It's almost road biking season and I need this app to work!!

I have been using the app for a week now and I think it's amazing, I didn't give 5 starts because I sometimes get lost in the app and struggle to find stuff. Additionally it would be great to be able to edit the journal for the current day not yesterday... it's kind of difficult to remember specific times the day after.

Was nice app. Started having trouble with gaps tracking, like a lot of other people by the look of it. Just had a look at trouble shooting guide and everything seems to be in order. Trying a reinstall so will let you how this goes!!! Ok so check everything, still no joy! Uninstalling now and wont be using again as there are plenty of other apps that do the job better!!!

This app sucks on the phone. You can find everything you want but there is no way to add it to your cart. That's why I stopped ordering here for a while. Thought I would give it a try again and same thing. So I'll just get my supplements somewhere else.

update 5/15/26 8 years later. this app is still my go to for alarms because it has such great features. I love the slowly escalating volume and customizable snooze options. hope this works forever, as I would be lost without it and it's sleep alarm perfection. ------ So much depth of information and options! You can both find options to help you sleep and determine what is going on while you sleep. Comprehensive and solid app. Definitely worth the money and time to learn and use.
always never working. doesn't refresh availability by days. shows info for wrong days
Lots of great recipes that are easy to follow!!

After numerous attempts the app won't let me in and keeps telling me my password is wrong but I used the same password to log in online. This is frustrating and makes me want to shop elsewhere since I may not always want to go online I may just want to use the app for easier access. Uninstalled.

Recently (November 2025) this app has become very unreliable. It always drops out with a "something is missing message" at least once, and now it won't recognise that I'm zwifting at all for 90% of the ride, trying many restarts.

How do I turn off the audio coach or voice guidance or whatever they call then thing that says "go" and talks about pace etc?  Can't find this setting in either the app or the website.  Used to be able to turn this off.  I really don't like the verbal cues and notices.  I can well understand why others might like this feature, but there should be a setting to turn it off.   I'm using Android Oreo.  Other than this annoyance, I like Endomondo.
Used to be a decent app. Now it keeps telling me that I have incorrect username/password.

As per many other reviews for this app, it used to work fabulously, but for approx 12 months GPS tracking is unreliable for many different types of exercise. I have tried all of the suggestions on the support pages, but they provide temporary fixes at best. There is a fundamental issue with Endomondo's use of Android's location APIs

The app used to have some amount of use on the free version. Not anymore. All you can do is set a timed meditation with no words where it has a gong sound at the end. I found two "free" meditation series. For one, only the first session was free. The rest cost money. The other had a series of 4 brief 10 min meditations. You get a lot more value from looking up meditations on youtube. Very disappointed in what this app has become.

I originally gave 5 stars in 2018 but have to drop it to 1 star. This year it stopped syncing to the web so friends couldn't see my diary anymore. Then the new UI came out which made it less user friendly. Then it stopped updating with Garmin. Then recipes stopped saving. Today I noticed entire weeks of my logs from earlier this year have just disappeared. The company has turned a nice product into one that isn't even usable. I recommend looking into the many other options out there.

Love the app when it works, just can't seem to get it working at the first go... Updated rating because it seems like they have sorted out the issues. Would be great if you guys added a way to see who gives a thumbs up during the ride. Normally have to check after the ride. Keep up the good work team

No way to cancel free trial. The app seems nice and well designed, but after trying it out for the free trial, decided it wasn't for me (the ONLY way to get any idea of the value of the content, btw...there are no free options available unless you sign up for the trial). I am trying to cancel before I get charged $69 for an app I don't want, but it is impossible to cancel through Calm and when I try to manage my subscription through Google Play, it is not showing up. Feeling PLAYED.

The free version of the app is incredibly annoying. When I first log in, there's a very long survey that prevents me from using basic features. When I got to entering calories, I couldn't enter any food without the added benefit of protein, carbohydrates, and fats. Without a premium account, you can't sync the app with Health apps, but trying to enter steps manually also requires a premium account. I was ready to buy it, but the app simply stopped working with a connection error.
I've used it for years and it works reliably. Every once in awhile the watch decides to be difficult about syncing, but overall it does what I want it to.

1. The app logs out repeatedly, and wipes away settings, saved runs, and downloads! Really annoying to reset it again, especially if not running regularly 2. The voiceover is different now, and not in a good way. The earlier voice was much better because it had pauses in between groups of information. This one is a non-stop rattle of speech which makes it difficult to understand. Please fix ASAP 3. Add filters in the Guided Runs tab, instead of having an endless scroll + search bar 5⭐ if fixed

I've been using it for a while but then paused gym work for a while. The app has been updated, and initially; I found it more difficult to navigate. I will play around with it and edit my comment once I've used it a few more times. If it runs the same as the last version, then I am very happy with it and I would recommend the app to anyone wanting to start strength 5x5 training.
No yearly fee, I get all the stats, and now that I have given it a chance, I dont really miss other fitness apps where there is a subscription fee for premium.
Awesome app for mileage, time, and avg speed. Also, easy to use, even for an old geezer like me😉

Horrible!! Can no longer see everything on one page. Can't customize where to show items in pages. Fonts and charts are tiny. They have added an excessive amount of junk that I have no interest in seeing and can't remove. The new app was forced on us without an option to keep the old version. I will be looking for an alternative.

Edit from 5 star to 1 star, subscription creates a paywall. Now unable to view any recipe directions. Will just use pinterest and follow the link that way instead. Great app for planning meals, keeping track of household ingredients, and storing recipe collections. I've already recommended it to several friends!

App is great as a boxer training and doing road work. Only grievance is that the route tracking is wrong like 60% of the time.It will track the full distance and time,but it won't track the full running path. which is annoying cause a 3.5mile run will look like a 1.4 mile run on the map,but won't match your actual numbers.

This app works so well that I kinda feel like crying. I'm in graduate school and this is the perfect app to manage my diet. The ability to both add recipes and decide what to eat when, along with the grocery list will likely not only save me money but help stop me from either become hugely fat from eatting out or terribly thin from starving. Thank you so much for this app!
Came up when I searched for "sodium tracker" but doesn't allow you to view or keep track of sodium counts.

I'm only leaving the 2 stars because of basic functionality. The new layout with having everything on the home screen gives me a headache and isn't the big reveal you believe it is. Being able to directly access the diary and see the data right in front was much more user friendly without all the other distracting elements such as the graph at the top. I don't even recall there being this "home screen" that you're marketing. Also way too many clicks to be able to access what need.

I have a recent Motorola with Android 10 and use Musicolet for my music ap. This ap is rubbish with that combination. It drops out all the time, the voice doesn't work so you don't get distance or speed info and when you finish you discover it hasn't been working for most of the time so all your stats are missing. I have switched to Runkeeper (an Asics ap) and that actually does all the things endomondo is supposed to do. I will stick with Runkeeper and forget Endomondo.

You might want to be aware that this app now produces illogical and incorrect results on my Pixel 10 pro phone. It works fine on my wife's Samsung phone. It used to work on my phone, too, but it became erratic recently. That might be due to a Pixel update... I think I got one of those recently.
It is a really good 👏👍 had to lower the rating. Don't like that it logs me out if I don't use it for a few days and also changes the settings(since I usually set it with no oudio feedback)

The lack of even email support is very frustrating. I pay for this app. It has a cool feature that shows macros right on my Android home screen. I can't see the Macros because MyFitnessPal says I have to pay for the app even though I've already paid. Bugs happen. No problem. Poor/non-existent support is unacceptable for a paid product.

App is out of date. Click on the button to update. This takes you to Google Play Store. The only options available are 'uninstall' and 'open'. App can't be updated. I uninstalled, then re-installed the app. Still out of date! This is usually a very good app, unfortunately it is now unusable. EDIT: Zwift are aware if the problem and working on a fix. Hopefully it will be back up and running soon.

Had an issue with the latest update, but it seems to have resolved itself. Still have an issue with the app insisting on including "intensity" as a parameter, no matter what I've done to delete it. I did reach out to support and they offered several suggestions, but none have worked. So, every time I start a workout, I need to customize the data all over again. You can google the same workouts! I will be uninstalling and I see others doing the same...thats what happens when u get greedy.  I will be buying all my supplements from muscle and strength or amazon!

This is a terrible app. It's very difficult to search and find classes. Then when you find a class, good luck with trying to book. I would not recommend putting your card details into this app. I tried this app about a month ago and found it so awful that I uninstalled it before even booking a class. Order from the website directly THIS APP IS LOUSY. Every time I make an order with the app it freezes up and empties my card the moment I try to finalize the purchase. This has happened more than once and is INFURIATING.
customer support doesn't work. you open a ticket and it takes ages. The whoop itself looks like it has a glitch not capturing events

I’ve been a Whoop user for over two years, and I genuinely believe it's a great device. Unfortunately, my recent experience with the Whoop 5.0 has been very frustrating. After just two weeks of using the new device, it suddenly stopped working. The replacement device now frequently disconnects from my phone and has eventually stopped connecting altogether, leaving me unable to access any of my data through the app. The only solution offered was to unpair and reconnect the device each time.

Was really looking forward to Zwift: our area is under lockdown a few weeks + it is now freezing winter temperatures & icy roads. I love this app and Vitacost! So easy to use, I order once or twice a month from here it is so effortless! They have the best prices and brands from which to choose.
it's been a while since my friends used this app for running. Today, I decided to start running and installed to give it a try. I absolutely loved the experience, definitely a 5 star app.
This logo doesn't match with apple's logo. Not at all.. though this app is so useful for cooking and for new recipes.. Go Ahead.

It's good when it works. Today, it's not working. Best app for calorie tracking hands down, but there are many problems, mainly the aggressive marketing of the subscription. I finally gave in and subscribed so the app would stop brothering me, only to find out even this isn't enough. if you're pro, but have auto renewal off, the app will constantly have an alert on the bottom menu, asking you to "Stay Pro". Im a soon to be paying member and it won't allow me to log in

I'm an 18 year weekly customer, ordering thousands of dollars a year in products, and have been UNABLE TO SIGN IN AND ORDER SINCE EARLY AUGUST, and the US technicians have been UNABLE TO CORRECT THE PROBLEM despite trying every way around it!!!!

such an amazing experience NRC have me. I used strava before and shifted because of the subscription plans there and this app is really gives the far better service in free and the guided runs are just the cherry on top!!

Love the app! The UI is incredibly simple and easy to navigate. I use Nike Training for my home cardio workouts, and the HIIT workout is fantastic. It pushes my entire body to the max. It would be even better if Nike could organize the workouts step by step, as currently, I have to watch the entire video from A to Z every day. Overall, it's a great app.

when it works its brilliant. now that seems that saying that it doesn't work very often and you are thinking i won't use it. It works great the problem is Zwift has now got so popular it has latency and demand problems.

When I logged in again after years, I realized that my account had been hacked. My name had been changed to a website, etc... I asked for two factor authentication, or any other solution and they answered that they will get back to me later after some investigation. 1.5 months had passed, still no answer. Checked my ticket, it is marked as solved. tl;dr: Disappointing support.

Works well enough for a free app, it sometimes under or over estimates by a few miles. I only use it to track my runs, I don't use any of the preset programs. The latest update seems to have broken login for me and every few days I have to log in again which is very annoying.

This is not a good tracker at all. There isn't really a great way to connect the band to the device it's paired to. Next the heart rate is really off. I don't think it accurately tracks anything really, but I'm not 100% sure. I feel like I was trapped into a "6 month commitment" with this thing, but even though I still have a few more months remaining until I can cancel. I've taken the band off and thrown it in a drawer. Cannot sync to myfitnesspal. Everything set up correctly and now have to pay to unlock other challenges. shame on Garmin Connect. the price of the watch isn't enough????
so easy to use and find classes anywhere for great rates ! love it!

When creating an account on Android, selecting your birthday (a required step) doesn't do anything. It just pulls up a calendar, you click the date you were born, then nothing. If you click away, the calendar will go away, but it won't save your answer, so the process of creating an account is literally impossible to finish, and the app is unusable. I know this app used to be good, I used to use it all the time, but this version doesn't deserve anything more than 1 star. Go back.

I updated from the fitbit app to the health app (major downgrade) today. I hate it. the data is tiny at the top. My hourly step goals tracker is gone. My custom meals are gone. You cant add quick calories. AND YOU CAN'T PUT HALF OF SOMETHING DOWN FOR FOOD. I tried to add .5 table spoons of butter and its forcing me to put at least one. You can't eat half of anything. Calories tracking also took away the in and out comparison.

Everything is great except for the challenge update notifications that are sent after midnight. There is no option to schedule or change the notification time - the only option is to disable them completely. It makes no sense to receive notifications at midnight about someones activity in a challenge. Its annoying.

Was excellent for years. Now starting to get very slow to refresh feed, history and routes. Spent 15min waiting around this morning starting/restarting to bring up a route to track as I have been keen to track my progress. Lack of updates and responses to reviews does not bode well for this app and cant justify paying premium subscription. Its a pity but now actively looking at alternative apps. Under Armour have dropped the ball with this one

Love it as a calorie counter to help me stay on track. It allows you to search for ingredients or publish and save recipes or save meals. It calculates and tracks macros, nutrients and calories. It also gives you recommended caloric intake based on weight, height, goal weight and whether you're trying to lose weight or maintain. There's a pay option that also unlocks a water tracker and meal plans. I love the app, it's simple, yet tracks a decent amount of things, laid out well and easy to use.

I have found the app useful but poor tech support around trying to upgrade to a family subscription has meant that they cancelled my subscription and failed to solve the issue so I have been without it for several weeks involuntarily. the new rate offered for an individual subscription is higher than the one they cancelled on me. alot of my time has been wasted exchanging emails which give me instructions all leading to the higher subscription option and it's deeply frustrating.

This used to be more convenient. A lot of the great features that once were free and made using this app worth it is no longer free. I used to really like using this app to track but now its just extra time consuming and not very many features without paying $20 a month. Not worth it either way in my opinion. I used to use this all the time but has become a hassle without being able to scan the barcode to add it to my log.

I would love to explore my growth into this app if it wasn't a $70 lump sum investment. One weeks free trail is not enough time to judge the value this app will have in my life. I'd like to see monthly or even quarterly payment options. I'd happily pay for 3 months at a time. As a free user, I can't speak to its content. However, the sound scapes are great. Exactly similar to what you could get on any music streaming platform tho.

I switched from the Endomondo and I am very disappointed as others here. Endomondo was nice and it simply worked. This app stops tracking after some minutes without any obvious reason (OnePlus 3T with turned off battery optimization). Many adds, not responding, terrible UX design. Looking for usable replacement, but I expect the data collected over the years will not be transferred.

Almost essential for Zwifters. Map is useful, selecting events and sending multiple ride ons also. But, should have more functionality i.e. be able to see "garage etc." and change/select routes using this app rather than the main computer app (which is also an issue on that but l digress.)

So it's great when it works. But since the update I can't book some classes. Also my schedule starts in April 2021. As much as this year sucks, I kind of need to know what I have booked in it ... Seeing other reviews raised similar issues and got a reply asking the user to email an address I said great! I'll email that address! I sent an email withmyconcern. It was an automated reply that says "thanks for helping make the app better with your feedback." The "support" pages are useless. Argh.

i loved this app before they updated it to not just immediately displaying the steps for the recipe. now you have to load a webpage and scroll to find the recipe steps and many times it doesnt load properly so i cant make the recipe i had planned. i wish they would go back to the format prior to this one, it was much easier and more efficient

The most recent update took a great app and broke it, for no discernable reason. Videos that take forever to upload; random log-on requirements; removal of old formats that some might find easier to follow than a video. Been using this 5-7 days a week since they put it out. I know it's free, so I guess I can't complain. Was a wonderful partnership while it lasted.

Mainly using it to track my sleep. I love the journalling in the morning. But it never works on the first attempt. It always says there are network connection problems which is not the case on my side(anything else is working). Besides it would be great if u can save it Journal locally (when there is no network) and it will be send send it later automatically. It is super annoying especially in the morning when U need to get going. Please get this fixed.

I have used this app many times in the past on different phones with no problems. The app has worked well in the past on my current GS9 but the last few times out it stops and resets 8n less than a minute. When it did work, sort of, it tracked duration only. Had to find another app that might at least work. Disappointed.

With this latest update to center around step tracking, Google shot the knees out of the only thing that made this app truly unique. Finally, there was a solution out there that worked for people with long strides, people who bike more than they hike, and people who value anaerobic exercise. Instead, they went with what "users were more familiar with". Way to act like every other tracking app out there. The API will still be useful for moving health data between apps, but that's about it.

Way too expensive for what it is. I find it weird that slick apps like this are so heavily promoted. It is $80 a year for relatively minimal content. There is a 7 day free trial, but it requires n ahto-billing agreement for the full amount through the app store. This is handy for them because app store practices make it unlikely that people will get their money back. Insight timer has way more content available for free, with a much more useful, but not required, premium option.

Used to be fantastic... Now have to login twice per session and they moved to video format for exercises. You can't preview your workout to see if it's suitable and hence you can't prepare. Also you can't skip exercises, and you can't pause to see the form video - they removed those and when I did try pause I accidentally skipped to the end of the workout; I'm only given one view and the new view won't rotate soi was trying to Google the right form. Try Freeletics or 'Stretching Exercises'

The app definitely has a lot of positives for being free. Overall enjoy and recommend. However, I did a guided 30 minute interval, and it refused to let me stop the run after I finished. Only way to leave was to discard the run. It also should calculate the actual intervals, it was taking my pace during the rest times as the 1:30 and :45 timers all just ran consecutively.

I was really struggling to find an app that doesn't require a subscription... This app gives me everything I need for free!!! I wish apps would give you more payment options... I don't mind paying for a good app, just not subscriptions. Everything is a subscription now days, and leaves you poor. If anything, if they made this free for 14 days and then charged a $10 fee to own the basic one for life. I'd pay that $10 to support the devs. Sure, keep those premium features as a sub.

Yeah, no. This review isn't going to be long, because I won't be bothered to type out what I thought about an app that wouldn't open past the "Join/Log In" screen. I'm not installing this again.
First-time buying something from my phone, and it was as easy as on my computer. The app was very clean, clear, and efficient to use.
Bought the lifetime option a long time ago, now I can't use the app unless I spend money on a subscription. Welcome to the digital age where you own nothing and you'll like it.
With the pace shaming ads they have out right now (runners welcome, walkers tolerated), on top of the app crashes and incorrect data, I think I'll take my business elsewhere.

They moved free tools behind a firewall. The new UI is more difficult to use. They send reminders that are offputting: "Not logging meals is a choice, too :/" or "pls don't make us remind you again". This makes me want to use a different app that doesn't try to guilt me into using it. Quit trying to personify a tool. If you give a hammer a name and googly eyes, it's still just a hammer. MyFitnessPal is just a tool and so are the people who program it.

This app is absolutely useless! I tried to install and use it over a year ago and it was really buggy. Now it still doesn't work for class registration. It keeps showing a message that a user is outside of a registration window even when the registration begins. This sucks!

The App is good for home workouts, but it's not 5, because of the following: 1. I wish there were alerts on the main actions, like switching between the exercises. Too many times it's switched by accident, simply because you grabbed the phone and touched the screen by accident. 2. No option to choose UKRAINE as a main location. 3. No cast option. I love this app. It does let you know they have the item in stock or not.

Super disappointed. I have been using this app on and off for various years now. Nevertheless, the resent update was dissapointing. I think they shouldn't have pulled out all the content in the format they had it. I hope they bring it back in.

I switched from iPhone to Pixel and was surprised how much harder Google Fit/Health Connect makes everyday health tracking. I also wish I could choose which health tools appear directly from my login/lock screen instead of digging through menus every time. It should also be easier to quickly log symptoms that help predict periods, like migraines or cramps.

I can just use the Fitbit app instead (which always works for GPS tracking).

This is the best app Ive found for calorie counting, and food logging. Ive used other paid and free apps but nothing compares. There is an extensive list of foods with the option to add new items. You can adjust your macros and calorie goals easily. No intrusive ads and I use the free app. They do offer premium services though. There is a whole community to support you if you like. I really like that you can put in your own recipes and it will calculate nutritional values by portion size.

Even when outdoors there used to be the ability to show a larger display of numerical data without the map. # The app seems to report faster times for a measured activity than any other app I have tried. # The app and website are often unavailable without any warning. # Still no ability to export all one's data. The app has a lot of workouts, it's amazing and the couches are great. Especially if you try the premium version. However, the app has some problems with loading the workouts. I started using the app. 6 weeks ago and so far so good. I use it 3 times a week and the weight amount increase "accordingly" between session, I got a few solid pounds on my body as well! It's really simple to use and the additional exercices are perfect if you feel energized enough to do few of them. Speaking of wich, videos for the proposed extra exercices could be nice, had to go on another app to watch them but that's very minor considering how good 5x5 is.
After waiting so long, the app is almost useless. It needs to have more functionality. Add/modify account settings, view progress, etc.
😌 Works offline after setting route and turning on Location

So far, so good👍! Just started two weeks ago, but I find the logging in easy and even if sometimes you don't find your exact brand item to log, you can certainly find something comparable to add the calories in to the app. It does remind me a lot to do the logging in, which I appreciate because I always forget.

Everything was great with the 3.0. The 4.0, along with the app update, seems to have set everything back. I had more issues since having my 4.0 in the past month then I did with a 3.0 in the previous six. Data is missing; sleep was tracked but put on the previous day and listed as a 'nap', and I was unable to fix or erase this. Before the 4.0, I would say this is one of the best fitness trackers out there. With the 4.0 and app issues, I would say adequate on average; great when it works.

Been using this app for tracking road and mountain bike rides since 2015. Great way to track progress on identical rides, and to 'total up' mileage for the year. The free service provides everything I need; ave speed, speed at mile intervals, total miles, time and maps showing route. Can even switch to satellite view. Highly recommended for new or experienced riders.

I've been using this app to log kayak outings for over 10 years and have logged over 1000 miles. One of the great things about the app was that minutes after getting back onshore, I could text a link to my workout to anyone that I thought might be interested - usually my wife and a few friends. The feature has come to an end. Technically it is still there, but Map My Fitness now requires the link recipient to have a Map My Fitness account in order to view my workout. MMF, what were you thinking?

This app has gone rapidly downhill lately. it doesn't sync with the web app consistently. It "forgets" foods recently listed while remembering foods that haven't been consumed in years. I've been using it since 2014 and am strongly considering opting out at renewal time. Get your 💩 together, folks!!!

Just too flaky, doesn't deal well with treadmill based runs - told me i did 27km at an avg of 80+km per hour and then used that to adjust future workouts to be unusable. Workout plans are fixed date - they don't roll forward if you miss a date. When hrm connection drops it assumes you did the whole workout at the last rate it saw - doesnt warn you it has dropped. Fix some pretty basic stuff and it would be a nice app. Cancelling my subscription.

EDIT: wow. I wish I could leave zero stars. the response to my review was to go to their general FAQ page. no answer to my issue: being able to log custom food items, but maybe that feature is now behind a paywall. the update is not an improvement. used it to track my calorie intake/macros. due to the update, if the app doesn't have the item, there is no way to add a custom item. used fitbit since 2019 but this app update is the final nail in the coffin.

This app sucks! I simply don't understand why it's so hard to fix! Services are not categorized properly and searching for a service brings up businesses that don't even offer the searched service! Also, the few that are correctly categorized, now have all the services showing the same cost?! Seriously, this is worse than amateur hour! The businesses that use this service should move to a different platform, one that works and actually shows the client what is available, when, and how much!
Great for quickly placing orders. A little less navigation friendly than the website, but very convenient.

Beautiful sounds and songs. However you're locked out of almost all meaningful guided meditations, or relaxing stories unless you pay a subscription. The music is beautiful and I used it frequently, I just feel there's plenty of apps I can get the same from, for free. If this offered more free options then I might change my opinion, and rating.

I've been using Stronglifts 5x5 for close to 15 years now and had originally bought the lifetime membership. I'm sad to see the developer hasn't honored that lifetime tier. It has been a nice 15 years, but it's hard not to feel a bit betrayed. I would rather pay for an app that actually honors its commitments.

This app was great till it stopped working. Now I can't shop for anything at all, no products show up, so the app is useless :( not sure what happened or why. I tried to uninstall and reinstall but to no avail. Help!

Convoluted, but incredibly detailed. I still haven't figured out the many intricacies of the app, but it works reliably and has a couple of great features that make it more convenient than competitors. You can set automatic alarms to go off at a given time during the week like normal but it's also a great way to make sure you'll never miss a wake-up call. If you set sleep tracker but don't enough battery, the app will shut off tracking to make sure it saves enough power for the alarm.

I love this app, especially the feature where it estimates of you were asleep or not based on phone use and ambient lighting. since installing it I've actually used it every single day which is rare for me. normally the novelty wears off after a few days and I never touch it again but this app has helped me consistently log my sleep

It's only a good app if you can afford the annual subscription fee, otherwise you only have access to a tiny selection that is well hidden within "locked" content. I would give two more stars if there was at least an option to buy sessions you are interested in instead of the subscription. The way it is currently set up, it's an all or nothing situation. With the subscription, it's closer to a five star app. As a "free" app, two stars is generous.

I would have gave more stars if I could in fact edit workouts without having to redo it 6 times before it finally saves. But it also saves random workouts that I'm not sure how they're detected, I cant seem to turn it off, a 5 minute "general" workout is not really something I care to track.

Didn't work at all. Nice stories, but didn't work. i did not want to subscribe to this app; i thought i had unsubscribed after the trial period. I really like how simple and straight forward the app is to use. The database of foods is fantastic!

Love this app, but, received a coupon for 25% off premium subscription with 3 months free service, It's not stated, but the app offers additional free days, 7 after you sign up for the premium, I'm not sure if that free time is in additions, but it should be, as that is how it's advertised. I followed the link while using my desktop email program, followed the directions and ended up getting charged full amount, then told that I didn't follow the dir

Almost great - could do with a 'cancel' button for when you accidentally select a wrong turn, an option to see average power and speed and a better view of the upcoming gradient (possibly a lap button?) Then it would be perfect
Won't let me log in. Says that my username or password is wrong, but it isn't. I just logged into daily burn on my computer

as a free user there are limitations to what you can and cannot access but the free version is just as amazing. General feed very unreliable for the past few months Roughly since 2019 the general feed functionality does not work well. This app sucks! I had over 2 years of runs, and now they are all gone. I contacted support, but they just keep giving me different numbers to call. and this program like Garmin watches becomes unusable. nonsense data, erroneous measurements. In vain you report about the bug, Garmin is not interested. just buy.The maps are outdated, they haven't been updated in years, old programs, and they haven't been updated in 10 years.
Would be a great app if it worked. Last month I couldn't see pictures for couple weeks. Couple days ago I gave up trying to press the shop button. Now I had to login aprox 7 times to place the order.

I love this app. From the formats to the trainers and their programs. 👏🏽 On the other hand, lately it has been lagging pretty bad as of 7/25/23. I've loved this app since I got it. WAY better than LG health which is what my phone comes with. I love how it maps every run and I can upload a picture with my workout. I would like to be able to customize the workouts further instead of being locked into 2 main set ups of 5x5 with assistance exercises.
