This is a great app no doubt. The workouts are awesome. However the stated rating is given so that developers convert the application as it was prior to the update. Earlier it was allowed to download workouts so that even if we have trashy internet sometimes we can use it. I travel a lot and don't find good internet everywhere. So kindly make it as it was before update. Include a download option for the workouts. Also the routines earlier were better. Thanks.

this app was great, emphasis on was. I've had it for years, paid for it years ago. I got a new phone - now it wants me to pay for it again. I've followed how the app says to restore my purchase, but it hasn't worked yet. there's no point in only tracking my sleep every other night.
Easy to use to schedule classes.

Why does this app only cater for young women? Young refers to those who track their period. NO information exists for later stages in a woman's life, ie, peri menopausal, menopausal or post menopausal. I was shocked to discover that an app promoting itself aa being relatively advanced seemed to forget about this. We do exist, and I'm sure some of us use your app. FIX IT UP BEFORE I REMOVE IT! Incredibly disappointed!!

This app is really handy, have yet to scan anything that isn't in the database. Since they were purchased the ads have been a bit more intrusive but it's not too bad. For tracking calories this is the app you want. 2026 UPDATE: I hadn't used the app in years but wow, what a downgrade from before. The app is now cluttered with ads, has a constant push for premium tiers, even basic barcode scanning is now behind a paywall, and their paid tier is by far the priciest out there. No thanks.

Overall great app. I only had major problems with connectivity once, where I had to restart the PC app as well as the companion app and I just hope it never happens when racing. One thing I'd like to see improved: I dislike that it plays an uncontrollable sound when a new segment in a workout starts. I use my phone to listen to music during the workout and this sound is so much louder that it's just annoying. The app on the PC is already playing sounds, so why also play any sound on the phone?

Every time I open the app it says it crashed.  Calorie bar doesn't show correctly.  Says my goal is 1100 calories,  and I ate 1200.  The green line on the bar is only half way.  On a positive note, I love the scanner.  Wish I didn't have to go online to record my exercise.  This should be a "one stop shopping" app..  I don't know what happened but it seems the app is getting worse instead of better.

Great alarm app!! I mainly use it cuz you can decide to skip some alarms until a specific date which is very useful to me! (at least when you spam "Skip next" often enough) The only thing that I had to complain about is that it randomly switched the language from english to german without being able to change it back. But the developer answered my review and fixed the issue within a week! Great support and nothing to complain anymore!! 👌👌

UI is not as immediate as other apps I've been using, but good enough that it was easy to switch to Cronometer after years using the other one. Allows easily duplicating days and doesn't bombard with notifications or useless popups and gamification; and all this as a free version. Though I wish I could more easily copy foods from one meal to another on the same day, I really can't complain.

This app is phenomenal for many reasons, but what's kept me coming back for years is its built in library of foods (packaged and fresh), the customization of what nutrients or macros you're aiming for, and the developer customer service/communication. The free version is more than enough for what a typical user needs, especially compared to other nutrient tracking apps, but there's also a paid one if you want to get extremely specific with your tracking.

After many years of use I fear my time is done with Endomondo. They have now made basic graphs such as speed a premium feature.  I once paid for a premium version that was replaced by another premium version taking away features that I was paying for. Enough is enough.   The Garmin app is free and displays way more information than Endomondo does now.

I like the latest AI generated summaries of your sleep or your workouts; however, there is a bug on the "Run" interface, the average pace seems to be accurate, but the lap splits don't have anything to do with your run, for example I just completed a 3.48 mile run, but the splits it shows are 4'08", 2'57" and 5', with those splits I better quit my job and become an Olympian!. I googled this issue and it has been discussed in the Fitbit community, but the bug is still out there.

Used for 9-10 years for various activities and have been premium subscriber for several of those years. Recently noticed Inaccurate distance tracking of walking activity. Not as bad with running and cycling which reported accurately. Reported fault but received a general message back regarding correcting GPS problems. Premium trial ok but do not believe the extra features are worth the expense. There are now better tracking apps available.

last updated broke this appp?! can no longer see the map or workout details if your doing a workout. ride data is out of sync wiry your actual in progress ride and you can no longer message via the app. when it worked it was ok, only ok as the app will often drop out of the game and then reconnect shortly afterwards.. please fix this latest update

I was just forced to update from the original fit bit app. So far I'm not pleased with the UI. I used to have an easy way to track my calories and steps but now there are hoops I have to jump through in order to log my calories and see macros. The old app had a section for frequent and recent foods and that seems to be missing here. Again, not a fan of the forced update, hopefully they can make some meaningful changes

They offered me a Pro subscription right after the survey, even though I hadn’t really had a chance to try the app as a free user first. Have you heard about a trial period? Clickbait worked and I bought it, but today I couldn’t scan food from a picture at all — which was one of the main features advertised. It’s been less than a week since I subscribed, so now I’m considering requesting a refund. UPD: I’ve already requested a refund.

I love this App, like it's the best weight and calorie tracking app I've ever come across, it's very helpful and shows me my progress clearly and there are many varieties for the foods you eat you can put them and it will tell you how much of your daily calorie intake you are at already. I recommend it 🫶🫶🫶

5 stars. Good app and easy interface to learn. 3 small complaints. I think the weight chart should be easier to see and find certain days instead of just a straight line before changing settings just to see the dots. Another is that you should be able to add or remove sections like remove the uncategorized or to rename it. Last complaint Is the lack of food options say I try a new menu item but nobody has logged it yet there should be a way to import that foods nutrition label.

If you're after a way to track your workout route you probably want to give this one a miss. It's GPS functionality has deteriorated over the years to now be a well known problem with still no way of correcting the app's incorrect data. They've also removed ANT+ support so another star lost unfortunately :(

I loved this app, it was the best! I hadn't used it for a while – I had a premium for year, but then I couldn't pay because of my region. Recently I decided to return to at least a free version and it's almost impossible to use! after each tap in the app, you get the ad that you can't close for several seconds:( upd: got a paid version and now can't get rid of all the gamification things. would be great if I could turn off this duolingo in my yazio
App has gotten worse, its slow, switches over to browser app, and not user friendly.

3/17 Why does this app not incorporate heart rate data when I wear a chest strap HR monitor during any activity? it should be way more accurate than my vivosmart 5. My caloric figures are always inaccurate for this very reason. Recent update that separates connected devices was totally unnecessary. Now I can't find my sensor information on the app. Also, please make the alarm clock setting page more intuitive and accessible. it's buried under quite a few pages.

The sleep detection is massively unreliable. I have an autoimmune disease & I use Whoop for strain tracking &, most importantly, sleep analysis. I often stay in bed reading after I wake up. The app CHANGES my wake up time & totally alters my stats as a result (recovery, sleep performance, etc)... This makes the app maddeningly unreliable. Do I need to baby the app & correct it every single day? You know I woke up! Leave it alone!! Y'all need to add a button to lock the wake time after waking up.

Preferred the old version before the update, it was simple to track workouts and the feature/graph to compare week to week was good. It was also more visually pleasing. This version is frustrating, it does not seem to work properly, my dashboard does not always update workouts or is slow, and the days I ran some show on the dash but not all, and sometimes they disappear, although the total amount of km shown seems correct. It has more workout options which is good though.

It used to be an okay app although I didn't use it much bc I liked the fitbit app better. now that they've taken over the fitbit app, I'm not happy. The look of the dashboard when I open the app looks like it's from the 70s! I have an android. I have a friend who has an iPhone and her's looks much more like the old fitbit look, but this android look needs some work. Another thing, my fitbit battery is draining much faster now! It was fine before!

This app is useful as having a brick in your pocket when you go cycling. It used to be useful with the ability to sync the rides and runs from Garmin, but 1 week ago it stopped doing that. I used the app directly to track my ride and it tracked only the starting point and the end point I had a nice ride of 5 meters in 1 hour... even after following the FAQS for the GPS troubleshooting I didn't change... I recommend using any other app until they fix the sync and GPS bug...

The app is useful for monitoring calories and macronutrients. On the other hand, it is very annoying that every time you don't use it for a few days, it forces you to answer a long string of stupid questions. And I use the paid app! PS: I understand that the questions can be useful. But having to answer them over and over again just because you haven't used the app for a few days makes no sense, especially since you don't have the option to skip them. It seems more like a kind of 'punishment'.

The update is terrible. The ability to see the exercise list for each workout was one of the best features. With that gone there is no chance to avoid workouts with movements counter indicated to one's fitness level. I tried one of the new instructor led ones but gave up in frustration after just a few minutes as it went so fast I couldn't figure out what was going on. And the instructor repeatedly telling me how much fun we were supposed to have was really annoying.
Always a great experience. If I could give higher the five stars I would!!! Still using this great app five years and counting and still think it's great!!😊

the new user interface was not designed with the user in mind. We want to see a comparison of our macros, our meals laid out, and our water intake at least. The new UI is oversimplified so you can't really get any data out of the main view without clicking into each category. The designers should remember the "why" of the app when doing the design.

So far I like this app a lot. There are lots of options to choose from for help with sleep, anxiety, relationship. You name it its probably on here! However the yearly subscription is a pretty big dilemma for me. I know it equals out to around $6 a month (or less) however dropping $70 at one time seems like a lot for something I cant guarentee I'm going to want to use for a full year. So just my recommendation is to make a monthly subscription rather then yearly.

I bought a Fitbit sense 2 watch about a year ago and started using the Fitbit app. Last week they removed the app and replaced it with Google health. They erased a lot of my custom data. On the new app you cannot enter custom food and nutrition. You can only choose from their list. There is a bar code scan option but it's very limited. I wasn't even given the option to keep using the Fitbit app that was working fine. I do not recommend any of this at all.

Certain parts of the app give you a blank screen instead of loading, i.e. reset password. It's still easier to use the browser because you won't hit any roadblocks preventing you from placing your order. One star until you make a funtional app. It can't be that hard--pay a competent IT team. You've had years to work on this.
I have been a Garmin watch user for 20 years and love how far Garmin has come and with the app. the app makes looking at your progress so easy. just wish there was a ruck function.

After experiencing issues with the app (partially user error, partially not), I left a review and was contacted by the support staff who wanted to work to resolve the issue. After a couple of troubleshooting techniques, we were able to resolve the issue and the app works amazingly now! Great app and the best support staff I've ever encountered.

using galaxy watch I wanted to use this app for counting heart points, but it doesn't pick workouts from Samsung health app, and when I enter it manually it duplicates it to Samsung health. it doesn't have any other extra function that Samsung app doesn't have

I love this app and use it regularly but every once in a while it does something shockingly dumb, it allows me to something dumb. case in point today before saving my run I responded to a prompt to rate the app. I was all set to give it five stars but then it booted me out of my run and lost the data for duration and time. So my rating of 3 stars should be understood to be born of frustration in the moment.

The recipe sharing is not working in every version and I don't understand how is this acceptable in an app for which I have to pay. Plus the design is worse after every update. Now tracking the foods is a chore. :( I cannot understand why should they change the UI that the users have to click more and more. Now we have to use dropdown menus instead of icons. Very annoying.

I have used this app for close to 10 years, and as of late, it's been nothing but glitches. It keeps logging me out. It doesn't retain running preferences (shoes, treadmill or road, etc.). When running, it gets stuck in 0.12 miles and stops tracking, GPS is inaccurate, unable to pause a run, and one can't stop a run if it wasn't for 'force stop'. I hope they fix the bugs and glitches.

This is an excellent adjunct when you are riding in zwift. The virtual gps/bike computer features are awsome. As this app evolves I would like to see the developers Include a summary of your current challenges. I am Taking the Everest Challenge and the 20,000 ft climbed in one month challenge. Currently, I have to load up the game to see my results. Also I would like to see a more involved route overview to include the grades so I can select a route that will match my training goals.

App is full of bugs and basically unusable. Latest updates still have not fixed problems like blank pages, product quantities not updating in cart: Shows number of items on cart icon, but returns message that cart is empty. Feedback function doesn't work. So many functions produce blank pages. No chat function. Login issues... and so much more! I deleted app after one attempted use. I love Vitacost and their customer service, so am very surprised at such a poorly designed and implemented app.

My training plan sometimes doesn't load. I have to go into the app switcher then back in to make it load. The end workout button is too easily tapped. The navigation buttons disappear during workouts. I have to swipe up to reveal them, which has led to me ending my workouts accidentally. This might work better as a tap and hold action.

Good concept, good runs. This is my app for running since almost 10 years. I learned how to run with it and I still rely on it to improve. Please give us more coaching, more challenging long runs 🫂❤️ It needs an update: making folders for different runs.

I was just tricked into subscribing. Shortly after finishing a workout and stopping the timer as usual, I receive a thank you for subscribing notification. And then I promptly cancelled. The popups were already getting to me, and I'd been avoiding them routinely, so I don't even know what button I pressed to make this happen. This is not a way to do business. Constant popups and reminders to subscribe around events where fingers are slippery and the mind is elsewhere does not impress.

Have used this app since 2015 and it helped me lose weight (and keep it off) but the new update is horrible. I have to click twice as many times to get to the same screens as I did before the update. The "home" page does not show details of my diary at a glance like the old version did. My exercise from MapMyWalk does not transfer over like it used to and like it should. Please go back to the old version!

I'm do addicted to Zwift running. I very rarely run outside. Love the community and the different worlds and landscapes. Chatting, etc, so much easier using the app, which connects seemlessly with the main app.i use Android phone for comparison with an old ipad running Zwift displayed on a TV.

App is clean, easy to navigate, and provides an excellent database of foods. Knocking down to 3 stars for one glaring issue: the difficulty in changing portion size. Example: chicken breast is portioned at 4oz. Well, the piece I have is 5.5 oz. I should be able to just override the 4 with 5.5 to have it calculate the calories, but no. I have to do the calculation in my head (5.5 is 1.375 of 4) and enter a fraction of the 4oz portion size. Renders the app mostly useless.

The option for the total distance covered in a day (km) is currently only available for the present day. It is not showing for past days; instead, it shows the kilometers session-wise. If that is enabled, everything will be perfect.

Not as good as the browser version of the website. I've been shopping on vitacost a few years. I used the browser because my old phone didn't have the memory for the app. In the app I can't browse a general category like all food without selecting a brand or special feature. Also the thumbnail photos don't load more often than they do, no matter if I'm on data or WiFi with high speed internet. The check out seems easier to edit and adjust quantity and add or remove items.

This app was working well until recently. Now I find that I cannot book a class either without waiting for some time - 30 mins plus? or even at all on occasion. The venue concerned says there is nothing they can do - it is a fault with the app. What does appear to be happening is that the app does work for those customers who are booking on an iPhone but not for those using an Android like a Samsung Smartphone. This is a bug that needs fixing. I uninstalled/reinstalled but no improvement.
This is a great app made by Apple! You can tell that it's made by Apple because it has a fruit with a leaf as the logo. Thank you Tim Apple!
Not opening sence last update
Not accurate. I got over 9,000 steps when I synced, it only register 4000. I refreshed and it said I did 2000. Refreshed a few hundred. Refreshed, 197. How does it go down after refreshing?

They recently made it a forced subscription. It used to be free to use basic functionality to get your lift in, but now all my history, progress, and current lift values are locked behind a paywall. Terrible business move and I'm disappointed in the greed.

cannot even sign in, I sign in on the sign in page then it just takes me back to the first screen with "join us" or "sign in" and nothing else. no matter what I tried. you would think a company like this would have figured out how to make their app as user friendly as possible...

Full disclosure, this app has a significant glitch with respect to Android OS, whereby it does not sync with their web portal/cloud. This means that if you lost the data on your phone, or purchased a new phone, you would be SOL as none of your data would be able to sync because it does not exist anywhere else other than your phone! For me this is a big deal, but the developer is aware of it and is taking steps to remedy it. Other than this, I give this app a 4/5 (free version:)

1-needs an additional tab to "modify" servings beside the "details" tab as shortcut. 2-calories gained better summerised and written beside the title of each meal. 3-the favorites list should accomodate more entries (only 20 for now). 4-user's profile should add height and desired weight over chosen time period. 5-should say "if u continue with this way u will reach your target weight on.... date. 6- exercise entries?. 7-better save whole meal as favorite. 8-calendar  search is better than scrolling day by

I've been using this app for about a week now, maybe 2, for tracking walking. When I stop the tracker, it tells me the distance, time, and pacing/mile is one thing. However, when I go to check the journal, all the numbers have changed and cut about a third of a mile off, resulting in time changes as well. Why is the summary one thing, but the journal entry is completely different?!

I have tried several apps like this one. Fatsecret doesn't let you track certain nutrients in the free version (like iron), but it does let you adjust your daily goals for micros and macros, which is why I have stuck with it. I find the logging options to be somewhat lacking but you can add custom foods/meals in to make up for it.

I'm not happy with this app. It doesn't sync with the apps that it says it's connected to (under armour: Record & MyFitnessPal, Glow by Eve, MobileFit, LFconnect; it won't even connect to the Fitbit and MapMyRun apps. I don't even see how to connect my Fitbit watch to it). I have to log everything manually, which is time consuming. How is it supposed to track what I'm doing if it's not reading the apps' data? My phone is not always on my body. When it is, it's still saying that im not moving.

I rarely have issues with app and I've been using it almost 5 years, but since it updated the day before last, I can't use it now. The initial orange screen pops up w the logo, but then never actually opens. And the desktop version isn't really great. This is how I have to sign up for classes, so that kind of sucks. Please fix it soon!

I've used this app for a long time and now recently the mileage has become very variable and unreliable - maybe not by a huge amount, a few hundred metres here and there but when you are trying to improve and time how long it takes to run each mile it's very very frustrating and annoying. I paid for premium subscription which makes this very unsatisfactory when other apps are free!! If I change app I lose my running history 👎.

Being a beginner, I was in a decision paralysis for a few months as to what and how I should start. Stronglifts was recommended, and it's been great. The app makes it easy, plus it is full of information and videos showing you proper form. The daily emails give me inspiration. Stuart (age 55)
Nice app, would love to continue using but once I created an account I began being overwhelmed with marketing emails with a broken unsubscribe button.

Excellent app to help me keep track of the calories I take in. Turns out my meals aren't the problem as much as all the snacking and juices. Making yourself accountable through this app leads to healthy and natural weight loss that can also be supplemented with exercise, special diets, or other means. Back to the app itself; I particularly enjoy the daily diary of foods I eat, I love the shortcuts, I love that I can save recipes for quick entry later. This has been a great pairing to meal prep.

Good app to encourage people to cycle and run more. Has a range of activities from races, time trials, fitness training, badge/reward medal collecting, free style exploration. Good community both in-game and outside. There are a lot of routes and they are adding more on a regular basis. Some of the places need quite a lot of effort to get too, so joining random people on the map is a good way to have variety if your a regular and you may get to see new areas of the map.

Purely in Garmin ecosystem this works very well. Never needed to manually intervene for anything. missing some gym workouts is kind of a bummer though but that's been a thing with garmin for years apparently and has nothing to do with the app. Downside lately is that the sync isn't as flawless as it once used to be. I can't really say if it's the device or the app but sometimes it just has some hiccups and tells me to come back later... never had that last year so ist got to be somewhat recent..

Have used this app to track runs for awhile. After a recent update, the "average pace" is not being calculated correctly. You can find a thread about it on Reddit with many other users experiencing the same problem. It's frustrating.

I was experiencing problems placing my order online & with the app, until I realized that it was my debit card disallowing the transaction. I was over my max purchases for one day. I waited til the next day & the order went right through. The app works great & up to my expectations.
hey, what is this ? I have got opened box with fake supplement. Which not seems as you have shown on your site or app.I had no acceptation  from your site .waiting for your reply as soon as possible.

Disappointed with removal of the Plans feature. Plans was the one feature that kept me motivated to continue working out. It put together my workout schedule for me, helped me keep track of my progress against the goal of completing all the workouts, and adjusted the schedule each week to account for my progress. Now i don't even have the option to put together a plan in the app. collections are ok but it doesn't put them in a calendar or help me track progress. So very disappointed. Was 5 🌟.
(sleep) Accuracy is bad. The pricing is also way too high. If the subscription would be cheaper maybe I would become a member again.

Great app, but just one thing can make it perfect for me. I have a samsung phone, and was excited to see that NRC app now syncs with Health Connect. However, NRC doesn't send calories burned to Health Connect. Therefore, Health Connect doesn't record that activity in the Cronometer app that I use to track food intake. I can enter it manually, but defeats the whole purpose of connecting the apps. If that can be fixed, then I will change my rating to 5 stars.

The app was great before the latest update changed the interface. Your history section now has a list. The icon to get there has a calendar, why not keep the calendar view as default the list option is awful. At least give us options to choose. The timers are now broken. Every set now gives me 3 minutes of rest instead of 90 seconds. Sometimes the bell is inconsistent and doesn't ding for your next set. The graphs also changed. It all just looks bad. Please allow us to revert to the old look.

I've been using mfp for years to track calories and used to enjoy the simple layout and ease of use. The new UI has completely ruined my experience. Everything takes 1-3 more clicks than it used to which makes tracking take longer and it is less intuitive. It has seriously made it so that I would rather move to a different app if I don't see an improvement in UI within the immediate future.

I don't understand how they managed to make the app experience way worse than it was. If it's not broken don't fix it! We used to be able to download the routines and now we can't. We uaed to be able to select audio options for each routine (no audio, just drill timing, or full audio) and now we can't. Plus the app is way more unstable than before. I've edited my 5 star review to a 2 star review. Please change the app back to the way it was!

This is a really bad app, I honestly don't undarstand the 4.5 rating. I am so sorry I paid for 3 months because it is almost unusable. When I take a picture of the back of sth with nutritional facts and add how much I ate in grams it just misses the numbers by a lot. When I take a picture of a meal it missess by 200 kcal. Just so frustrating, in the end I have to input all of it manually.

What a ripoff. Beware of these thieves. I bought a "LIFETIME" pass a few years ago and have been happily rumbling along using it to log my lifts, without any interest in the extra features they developed and put behind a subscription. I logged in to discover they no longer support what I paid for, and if I want to get any functionality at all I have to pay them a subscription. stay away from dishonest business practices like this, who knows what other shady stuff they are pulling.

I love these workouts, I did these on Netflix before they were removed then had to get the app. The app itself could be better. You can save your favourite workouts but can't sort them into any particular order. It's also way too easy to accidentally delete saved workouts with one wayward swipe and no way to undo or know what you just deleted. Would be great if you could leave notes for yourself on workouts to help choose in the future and have a folder system to organise saved workouts

So much better than the last Fitbit app. The AI coach is the only ai that I have used so far. I am very pleased with it. Also so much to read and explore. Nicely organized. I can tell a lot went into this. Very nice.

After a month synchronisation with health application just stopped, even though all tickboxes are active. I emailed to help center and received auto message that they successfully received my email and forwarded me to online data base to read what I already know. I have filed for refund , let's see how it goes. update: no refund update 2: the problem is not resolved after 2 weeks. no information from developers, no emails, no official communication, web site keeps silence. developers do not care

this is a great app to use for following your diet. I am impressed by how many foods are catalogued through the bar code feature and those that aren't, can be easily created from the product label. it can keep track of macro and micronutrients and it is very easy to use. highly recommended.

Edited to reflect how terrible the free version is compared to the paid version, which seems to have been rising exponentially recently. When this app was new I paid for it, was meant to be a one off but then it turned to subscriptions payments, I bought a year even though I thought I'd paid for life, now it's just more expensive for the same thing. The free version sucks

Not sure what's going on with Google Health (RIP Fitbit), but it's not good. Sleep tracking has become unusable. The widgets are gone. When using the AI coach to log an activity, it looks like it got it right, but after reviewing the logged activity, it's something completely different. I'm all for improvement but Google is using a production app as a development environment and we're stuck with the bugs and missteps.

Seems Endomondo no longer cares about its customers. Service doesn't work recently. Sync is crappy. Their platform status report does not reflect the actual situation (Server error on main Endomondo page, but status page shows all Green). They are just lying to you. At least it feels so.

Great app for Zwift. Especially like the training mode that shows the dial with ranges when using ERG mode. Wish when you gave a thumbs up ride on by clicking the icon it would give it to the riders near by instead of having to select individuals from the other page.

I love this app! Works great for everything I do...gym workouts, hikes, races, bike rides, and even tracks recovery stuff like massages or family activities like dog walking, gardening, housework. I like the goal setting and tracking. I like seeing my achievements at the end of the week. It helps keep my on track and makes me feel good. And getting the goal achieved "star" is a plus...it's the small things that count! 🙂

Whoop is easily the best tracker. Because it's so in depth, the app can be a bit overwhelming at times, but their support features and podcast explain everything. They recently added personalization so I can put the health metrics I care about on the home page, and the stress tracker is phenomenal. I'm entirely sold on Whoop, my health has improved significantly since getting it (6 months now), and the app is great.
Please give us back the Yazio UI before the update. It looks so confusing now and I have to click around so much more!! It makes it frustrating and it looked a lot better before.

I'm going to miss Endomondo. Even though I'm a Garmin user all my activities sync with it and it's a great motivator. Underarmour sucks! Prior review... Love the service. Mostly sync it with my Garmin vs actually use the app to record my runs/walks/hikes/bikes since 2016. Good stat breakdown. Not much different than Garmin connect but some different features and data points.

I really have to suggest better usability of the Android app. There's a lot of smart stuff but a lot of things not smart also: the ease of configuring the "tiles" is not there, I want to move them around and set them. also I have preferred settings on setting alarms, but I can't make them permanent like don't stop tracking after alarm. How to set it? maybe the setting is there but it's not easy to find at all.

I've been using Sleep as Android as my primary alarm clock app for over 10 years now. I really like the ability to set specific days, to default alarm sounds, and to solve math problems to disable the alarm or go past your snooze limits to make sure you're actually (somewhat) awake and on time. Maybe it's because I'm 10+ years older, but it does seem to help me to not be late in the mornings.

It's pretty cool and it's full of fascinating features. But I struggle with the UI, and I don't really know how to gain useful knowledge from the data. Its sleep phase estimation seems fairly rough, at least when using smartwatch data. It has both movement and heart rate, but I can't just start it when I get into bed and expect it to tell when I fall asleep since I read in bed for a while. If I read for a while, it seems to use that as a baseline for sleeping. Still learning though.
